The final piece, Boots of the Unjust, drops from Broggok in Heroic Blood Furnace. This set does not include a visually matching waist or wrist piece from the same sources.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items are on a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, meaning each can be cleared once per day per character. All required dungeons are fully soloable by any class at current max level. Assuming an average drop chance of 20% per item, it will take approximately 5-10 daily clears of each specific dungeon to collect the full set. This is a direct-drop set and does not use a tier token system. The pieces are spread across five different dungeons, requiring travel between Hellfire Peninsula, Zangarmarsh, and Auchindoun.

### Farming Tips
Run these dungeons on Heroic difficulty, as this is the only mode where this specific recolor drops. Use a character with high mobility to minimize clear times; setting your Hearthstone to Shattrath City provides central access to all Outland zones. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be acquired from the Auction House. The key requirements to enter Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons were removed, allowing unrestricted access for any character level 70 or higher.

61.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the reward from the Classic-era Dungeon Set 2 upgrade questline. To begin, you must first collect the full 8-piece "Magister's Regalia" (Dungeon Set 1), which drops from various bosses in Stratholme, Scholomance, and Blackrock Spire. With the prerequisite set pieces, you start a quest chain from an NPC in either Ironforge or Orgrimmar. Each piece of the Arcane Regalia is a quest reward for turning in the corresponding Magister's piece, along with expensive trade goods like Arcane Crystals, Dark Iron Ore, and Flasks of Supreme Power. The quests also require you to venture back into these dungeons to kill specific bosses for quest items.

### Farming Logistics
The prerequisite set pieces are farmed from dungeons on a daily reset lockout. All required dungeons and quest objectives are easily soloable by a max-level character. Acquiring the full Tier 0 set can take 10-20 dungeon runs due to drop rate variance; the subsequent quest chain can be completed in a few hours once all materials are gathered. This set does not use a token system, and the classes that share this specific leather appearance are Rogue, Druid, Monk, and Demon Hunter. The prerequisite "Magister's Regalia" set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be acquired from any other source.

### Farming Tips
Run the classic dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this is the only difficulty available. It is most efficient to farm the complete 8-piece "Magister's Regalia" set before starting the upgrade questline. To save time, purchase all required crafting reagents from the Auction House before beginning the quests. Because the quest chain is long and requires significant travel, setting your Hearthstone to a major city like Orgrimmar or Stormwind is recommended.

62.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality Bind on Equip (BoE) world drop items found throughout Outland. The pieces do not drop from specific bosses but are random drops from trash mobs inside Burning Crusade dungeons and from creatures in outdoor zones. Effective farming locations include instances with high trash density like Mana-Tombs, Auchenai Crypts, and Sethekk Halls. The main cosmetic pieces are Nether-Stalker's Headpiece (Head), Pauldrons of the Thousand-Moons (Shoulder), Fel-Chain Hauberk (Chest), Greaves of the Iron-Guardian (Legs), Gauntlets of the Bold (Hands), and Boots of the Outlander (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout when killing mobs in open-world zones; when farming dungeons, Normal difficulty instances can be reset up to 10 times per hour. This set is easily soloable by any max-level character. Due to the extremely low and variable drop chance of specific world drops, collecting the full set via farming can take hundreds of dungeon runs or thousands of individual mob kills. This is not a tier set, so it does not use a token system and has no class restrictions on drops. All pieces come from the same source type but can also be purchased from the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House frequently, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and can be purchased directly from other players. If farming, run Normal difficulty dungeons like Mana-Tombs, as they can be reset multiple times per hour. A common strategy is to clear the initial trash packs in a loop, exit the instance, reset it, and repeat. Use a character with high mobility and large area-of-effect abilities to clear mobs as quickly as possible.

64.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of world drop items found within Level 70 Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The pieces are not tied to specific bosses and drop from non-boss enemies throughout the instances. The primary slots are the Helm, Mantle, Tunic, Leggings, Handguards, Boots, Belt, and Bracers of the Claw. Dungeons with high trash density, such as The Shattered Halls, The Steamvault, and Shadow Labyrinth, are efficient locations for farming these items.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, meaning you can clear each specific Heroic dungeon once per day per character. The entire set is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the low drop chance of world drops, collecting the full set can require dozens of runs across multiple dungeons and characters over many days. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces drop from the same general loot pool of Heroic trash mobs.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering. Since the items are Bind on Equip, check the Auction House frequently, as this is often the most reliable way to acquire specific missing pieces. For active farming, focus on clearing large groups of trash mobs quickly before exiting the instance and moving to the next Heroic dungeon on your daily lockout list. Using a high-mobility character will minimize time spent moving between mob packs.

65.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind-on-Equip world drops found throughout Outland. The pieces drop from level 65-70 mobs, making dungeons the most efficient farming source due to high mob density. The best dungeons to farm are The Mechanar, The Botanica, and The Arcatraz in Tempest Keep, as well as the Isle of Quel'Danas. All main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) drop from trash mobs within these instances and zones; no specific bosses need to be killed.

### Farming Logistics
Farming for this set is done in Normal difficulty dungeons, which are subject to the 10 instances per hour account-wide limit. The farm is easily soloable by any max-level character, as enemies will not pose a threat. Due to the extremely large loot table for Outland world drops, collecting the full set can take hundreds of runs and is heavily dependent on random luck. No tier token system is used for this set, and all pieces are found in the same manner from the same general loot tables.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by searching for the individual pieces on the Auction House, as they are all Bind-on-Equip. When farming manually, use a class with high mobility and AoE damage to clear trash quickly on Normal difficulty. A popular method is to clear the trash in The Mechanar up to the first boss, then run out, reset the instance, and repeat. Do not use Heroic difficulty, as it adds a daily lockout and does not increase the drop rate for these specific items.

66.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind on Equip world drops found throughout Burning Crusade content. The items are most efficiently farmed from trash mobs inside level 60-70 dungeons, not from specific bosses. All eight armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) share the same model name, such as "Nether-weave Robe," and drop randomly from mobs. Dungeons with high mob density, such as Hellfire Ramparts, The Slave Pens, Mana-Tombs, and The Underbog, are effective farming locations. The entire set can also be acquired by purchasing the individual BoE pieces from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
The primary farming method uses Normal difficulty dungeons, which have no lockout but are subject to the cap of 10 instances per hour per account. The farm can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. Due to the random nature of world drops, the number of runs required to collect all eight pieces is highly variable and can range from dozens to hundreds of clears. This set does not use a tier token system, as all pieces are direct drops. All pieces drop from the same general sources, and difficulty modes like Normal or Heroic do not affect the appearance of this specific world drop set.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as you can reset the instance immediately after clearing the trash. Classes with high mobility and strong AoE damage, such as Druids or Monks, can clear the instances in a few minutes. Because all pieces are Bind on Equip, the fastest way to complete the set is to check the Auction House regularly, though this can be expensive. Tailors can craft the "Imbued Netherweave" set, which uses the same models but is a different, purple-and-blue color scheme.

67.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind on Equip world drops found in high-level Classic dungeons and zones. The primary farming locations are the dungeons Stratholme, Scholomance, and both Lower and Upper Blackrock Spire, as they contain a high density of level 57-60 elite mobs. The individual pieces are the Sorcerer's Crown (Head), Sorcerer's Mantle (Shoulder), Sorcerer's Robes (Chest), Sorcerer's Leggings (Legs), Sorcerer's Gloves (Hands), Sorcerer's Boots (Feet), Sorcerer's Belt (Waist), and Sorcerer's Bindings (Wrist). These items can drop from any trash mob or boss within these instances.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons used for farming this set can be reset up to 10 times per hour and do not have a daily or weekly lockout on Normal difficulty. The entire farm is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low drop chance of specific world drops, collecting the full set through direct farming is inefficient and could require hundreds of instance resets. There is no tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general pool of high-level Classic elite mobs.

### Farming Tips
The most practical method to obtain this set is by purchasing the Bind on Equip pieces from the Auction House, which circumvents the low drop rate from farming. If farming directly, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty and focus on clearing only trash mobs, resetting the instance after each circuit. Use a character with high movement speed and area-of-effect abilities to maximize the number of mobs killed per hour. There are no skips, bonus rolls, or tier tokens applicable to this farm.

68.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from bosses and random enemies within the legacy Scholomance dungeon, located in the Western Plaguelands. The Necropile Helm drops from the final boss, Darkmaster Gandling. Specific pieces are tied to other bosses: the Necropile Mantle drops from Jandice Barov and the Necropile Leggings drop from Ras Frostwhisper. The Hands and Feet can drop from several of the mini-bosses in the final chambers, such as Doctor Theolen Krastinov and Hearthsinger Forresten. The Necropile Robe, Belt, and Cuffs are random zone drops that can be looted from any non-boss enemy inside the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The Scholomance dungeon operates on a daily reset, meaning you can clear it once per character per day for loot. The instance can be soloed by any class at current max level with no difficulty. Due to the multiple bosses and random zone drops required, collecting the full set typically takes between 10-20 full clears. There is no tier token system for this set, as all pieces are direct drops from enemies. This dungeon has only a single difficulty, so there is no need to manage different lockouts for Normal, Heroic, or Mythic modes.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter, to minimize clear time. A full clear of all trash mobs is recommended to maximize your chances at the random zone-drop pieces. The most time-consuming pieces to farm—the Robe, Belt, and Cuffs—are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can frequently be purchased directly from the Auction House, which is the most efficient way to acquire them. There are no shortcuts or skips available for this farm, as bosses are located throughout the dungeon and trash must be cleared for the BoE items.

69.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from Quartermaster Koro'lan in Lion's Watch, Tanaan Jungle, after reaching Exalted reputation with the Warlords of Draenor faction Laughing Skull Orcs. This faction is exclusive to Horde players. The entire set is sold as a single item, "Ensemble: Laughing Skull Regalia," for 5000 gold. The ensemble teaches the appearances for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Waist, Legs, Hands, and Feet slots. There is no matching wrist piece in this set.

### Farming Logistics
The set is obtained via a reputation grind which has no daily or weekly lockout. This farm can be fully soloed at max level by repeatedly killing specific mobs in Draenor. Reaching Exalted requires earning 42,000 reputation points through combat. While any Horde character can earn the reputation and purchase the ensemble, the appearances are Plate and can only be used for transmog by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The set is not obtained via tier tokens and all pieces are from the single vendor source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient reputation farm is killing the level 100 mobs in the Everbloom Wilds area of northern Gorgrond. Building a Level 2 or 3 Trading Post in your Garrison provides a 20% bonus to all Draenor reputation gains, significantly speeding up the process. Reputation-boosting items, such as a Darkmoon Top Hat, can also be used to accelerate the grind. The ensemble is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

71.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon world drops found primarily within the Temple of Atal'Hakkar dungeon, also known as Sunken Temple. While the pieces are not part of an official, named set, they share a distinct visual theme. The Head and Shoulder pieces drop from the final bosses, Jammal'an the Prophet and the Shade of Eranikus. The Chest and Legs are most commonly found on the six troll mini-bosses in the lower basin, such as Zolo and Loro. Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist items are random drops from any of the elite Atal'ai trolls populating the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The Temple of Atal'Hakkar is a dungeon and does not have a weekly lockout; it can be reset up to five times per hour per account. The instance is fully soloable by any class at current maximum level, with mobs posing no threat. Given an approximate 15-25% drop chance for each piece from its associated loot table, collecting the full set will take an average of 15-20 full clears. Classic dungeons do not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same location. The instance only has one difficulty, so multiple lockout types do not apply.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, enter the dungeon and proceed directly down into the circular lower basin, clearing the six troll bosses there first. Run a circular path around the basin to pull all elite troll packs to acquire the random drops, then proceed to the final boss area for Jammal'an and Eranikus. All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so check the Auction House regularly to purchase any missing items directly. There are no skips in this dungeon, and mechanics can be ignored entirely by a max-level character.

72.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Alliance appearance of the cloth armor from the Battle of Dazar'alor raid in Battle for Azeroth. The eight main pieces of the set drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Head drops from High Tinker Mekkatorque, Shoulders drop from the Conclave of the Chosen, and the Chest drops from the Jadefire Masters. The Legs are found on Opulence, while both the Hands and Waist are on Grong, the Revenant's loot table. Finally, both the Feet and Wrists drop from the Champion of the Light.

### Farming Logistics
The Battle of Dazar'alor raid has a weekly lockout per difficulty, per character. All bosses are easily soloable at the current maximum level. Collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 5-10 weekly resets on a single character, given a standard 15-25% drop chance per item. This set does not use a tier token system; loot drops directly for cloth-wearing classes: Mage, Priest, and Warlock. The Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts that can be completed once per week, each offering a different color variation of the set.

### Farming Tips
For the most efficient farm, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as it provides the fastest clears without the added complexity of Heroic or Mythic mechanics. You can significantly speed up acquisition by running the raid on multiple cloth-wearing characters each week. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The bonus roll system from this expansion, Seals of Wartorn Fate, is no longer functional and cannot be used to gain extra loot chances.

74.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon quality items found within the Classic 20-player raid, Zul'Gurub. The Grinning Skull Mask (Head) drops from Bloodlord Mandokir and the Grave-Dusted Pauldrons (Shoulder) are looted from High Priestess Jeklik. High Priest Thekal drops the Ribcage of Maniacal Laughter (Chest), while High Priestess Arlokk drops the Legguards of the Cackling Stalker (Legs). The Hex-Stained Handwraps (Hands) are a reward from Jin'do the Hexxer, and the Girdle of Unrestrained Glee (Waist) drops from any of the bosses summoned at the Edge of Madness event. The Muck-Caked Stompers (Feet) can be fished from the pools where Gahz'ranka is summoned, and the Bindings of the Mad God (Wrist) are a zone-wide drop from any non-boss enemy inside the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The raid instance of Zul'Gurub operates on a 3-day raid lockout, allowing for more frequent farming attempts than standard weekly raids. The entire instance is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, a player can expect to complete the 7 boss-drop pieces in approximately 10-15 lockouts. This set does not use a tier token system, so all pieces are direct drops. The wrist piece is a random drop from trash mobs rather than a specific boss. As a Classic-era raid, Zul'Gurub has only one difficulty and one loot table.

### Farming Tips
To speed up the farm, you can skip any bosses that have already dropped their respective set piece for you. The Bindings of the Mad God for the wrist slot are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be traded or purchased directly from the Auction House, which is often the fastest way to acquire them. Using a class with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, is recommended to navigate the large distances between bosses efficiently. While farming for this set, you also have a chance to loot the Swift Razzashi Raptor and Swift Zulian Tiger mounts from Bloodlord Mandokir and High Priest Thekal.

75.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased from Temel, the Ember Court Quartermaster in Revendreth. The currency required is Infused Ruby, which is earned by successfully hosting the weekly Ember Court scenario available to members of the Venthyr Covenant. Each piece has a specific cost: Head and Shoulders cost 150 rubies each, Chest and Legs cost 200 rubies each, and the Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist cost 100 rubies each. Unlocking the ability to purchase each piece is gated behind reaching "Best Friend" reputation status with specific guests of the Ember Court.

### Farming Logistics
The primary source of Infused Rubies, the Ember Court, is on a weekly lockout per character. This activity is a solo scenario and does not require a group. Acquiring the full set is a long-term goal, as it is gated by weekly reputation gains with multiple guests, which will take several months to complete. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces are obtained from the same vendor using the same currency, and the scenario has only one difficulty level.

### Farming Tips
The most effective way to accelerate this farm is to run the Ember Court on multiple Venthyr-aligned characters each week, as the currency is character-specific but the transmog appearances are account-wide once learned. Following an online guide for the Ember Court scenario will help maximize guest happiness, leading to faster reputation gains and higher Infused Ruby rewards per run. The items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Funneling anima to the Gilded Chalice location via the Anima Conductor can sometimes unlock a daily quest that rewards additional Infused Rubies.

76.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip items that drop from creatures throughout Pandaria. The best locations for farming are areas with high mob density, such as the frog ponds on the western side of the Timeless Isle, the Guo-Lai Halls in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms, or the dinosaur bone farming areas on the Isle of Giants. All pieces drop from the same general loot table of level 85-90 creatures in Mists of Pandaria zones. The specific items are Astralaan Headdress (Head), Astralaan Shoulderpads (Shoulder), Astralaan Tunic (Chest), Astralaan Leggings (Legs), Astralaan Gloves (Hands), Astralaan Belt (Waist), Astralaan Bracers (Wrist), and Astralaan Boots (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout; mobs can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with ease. Because drop rates are low and rely on random chance, collecting the full set through farming could take anywhere from a few hours to over a hundred, depending on luck. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces come from the same world drop loot table. There are no difficulty settings like LFR, Normal, or Heroic for this type of content.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by searching for individual pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip and are frequently listed by other players. For those who prefer to farm, using a character with high mobility and strong AoE capabilities, such as a Druid or Monk, will speed up clears. Farming in a 5-person group can force faster mob respawns, increasing your total number of kills per hour. Potions of luck, while discontinued, may still be in player inventories and can be used to generate additional loot containers from Pandaria mobs.

77.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted using the Protoform Synthesis system in Zereth Mortis. Each of the eight main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) is created using the same recipe type at the Protoform Synthesis forge. Crafting a piece requires one Regal Protoform Lattice, one Glimmer of Focus, and a quantity of Genesis Motes. Regal Protoform Lattices drop from rare creatures and treasures in Zereth Mortis. Glimmers of Focus are primarily looted from Devourer-type enemies throughout the zone. Genesis Motes are a common material dropped by nearly all enemies in Zereth Mortis.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as all components are obtained from repeatable activities and creatures in an open-world zone. The entire set can be farmed solo at the current maximum level. Collecting all the necessary Lattices and Glimmers can take several hours of dedicated farming, depending on drop rate luck. This is not a token-based set, and all pieces are created through the same crafting method. Protoform Synthesis must first be unlocked by completing the "Protoform Synthesis" quest, which becomes available during Chapter 3 of the "Secrets of the First Ones" campaign.

### Farming Tips
To begin, unlock flying in Zereth Mortis via the "Unlocking the Secrets" achievement to travel quickly between rare spawns. For Regal Protoform Lattices, use the in-game group finder to join "rare farm" groups that cycle through all active rare spawns efficiently. The best source for the Glimmer of Focus is farming Devourer enemies at the Spawning Grounds in the northwest or around the Path of Inception. None of the components or the final crafted items are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

78.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from level 52-60 mobs in the original Classic zones. The most effective farming locations include Felwood, Winterspring, Western Plaguelands, and Eastern Plaguelands. Mobs in the demon-heavy area of Jaedenar in northern Felwood are a thematically appropriate and efficient source. All major pieces, including the Shadow Council's Hood, Pauldrons, Tunic, Pants, Gloves, Boots, and Sash, drop randomly from this loot pool and are not tied to specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop farm, there is no instance lockout or daily reset; it can be farmed continuously. The set is fully soloable at the current max level, with all mobs being trivially easy to defeat. The number of kills required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to random drop chances and can range from hundreds to many thousands. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be freely traded and sold. This set does not use a tier token system and is not affected by raid or dungeon difficulties.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House, as direct farming is unreliable and time-consuming. For those who choose to farm, use a high-mobility class with instant area-of-effect abilities like a Druid or Monk to clear large groups of mobs quickly. You can create level 1 characters on other servers to check their Auction Houses for lower prices or better availability. Focusing on areas with high mob density and fast respawn rates will maximize your chances.

84.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces from multiple level 55-60 Classic dungeons. The primary instances required are Scholomance, Blackrock Depths, and Blackrock Spire. The Head (Spellweaver's Turban) drops from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire. The Chest (Robes of the Royal Crown) and Feet (Omnicast Boots) drop from Emperor Dagran Thaurissan and Golem Lord Argelmach respectively in Blackrock Depths. The Legs (Skyshroud Leggings) and Hands (Hands of Power) are found in Lower Blackrock Spire from Highlord Omokk and Quartermaster Zigris. The Shoulder (Kentic Amice) and Waist (Dust-Mite Cinch) drop from Darkmaster Gandling and Ras Frostwhisper in Scholomance, while the Wrist (Arcanist's Bindings) drops from The Unforgiven in Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items operate on a daily dungeon reset lockout. All four dungeons (Scholomance, Blackrock Depths, Blackrock Spire, Stratholme) can be fully soloed with ease by a maximum-level character. Because the pieces are spread across multiple instances, a complete collection will likely take several daily lockouts. This set does not use a tier token system; items drop directly as loot and can be equipped by any of the specified cloth-wearing classes. There are no alternate LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic difficulties for these dungeons; only one version exists.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to quickly traverse the instances. For Blackrock Depths, use the mole machine inside the entrance to teleport directly to the "Grim Guzzler" area, which provides a significant shortcut to the bosses who drop the Chest and Feet pieces. Be aware that the Wrist piece drops from The Unforgiven, a rare spawn in the Stratholme main gate entrance, which may require multiple resets to find. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

88.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces that drop from various bosses in Level 80 Heroic dungeons from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and drop directly from the specified bosses. There is no associated reputation or currency vendor for this set.

*   **Head:** Cowl of the Dire Wolf (Loken, Halls of Lightning) or Cowl of the Lithe (Grand Magus Telestra, The Nexus)
*   **Shoulder:** Mantle of the Woda (Skadi the Ruthless, Utgarde Pinnacle) or Mantle of Gnarled Stitching (Ormorok the Tree-Shaper, The Nexus)
*   **Chest:** Robes of Hoarse Cries (Anub'arak, Azjol-Nerub) or Robes of Crackling Flames (Keristrasza, The Nexus)
*   **Legs:** Breeches of the Caller (Jedoga Shadowseeker, Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom) or Leggings of the Ruins Dweller (Cyanigosa, The Violet Hold)
*   **Hands:** Gloves of Distorted Time (Chrono-Lord Epoch, The Culling of Stratholme) or Gloves of Glistening Runes (Ley-Guardian Eregos, The Oculus)
*   **Feet:** Footwraps of Teleportation (Lavanthor, The Violet Hold) or Boots of the Worshipper (Herald Volazj, Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom)
*   **Waist:** Sash of the Servant (Svala Sorrowgrave, Utgarde Pinnacle) or Belt of Unified Souls (Meathook, The Culling of Stratholme)
*   **Wrist:** Cuffs of the Shadow Ascendant (Volkhan, Halls of Lightning) or Azure-Lined Cuffs (Moragg, The Violet Hold)

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Heroic dungeons, which are subject to a daily dungeon reset. Each character can loot each specific boss once per day. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at current max level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all 8 pieces on a single character will likely take 1-2 weeks of clearing the relevant dungeons daily. There is no tier token system for this set, as the items drop directly. All pieces come from the same type of source (WotLK Heroic dungeons), though they are spread across many different instances.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering, as these pieces do not drop on Normal. Farming on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) is the most effective strategy, as each character has a separate daily lockout. To maximize efficiency, prioritize dungeons that drop multiple desired pieces, such as The Nexus, The Violet Hold, and Halls of Lightning. Use a high-mobility character to speed up clears and skip unnecessary trash packs between bosses. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

89.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from various bosses within level 85 Cataclysm Heroic dungeons. The Head, Helmet of the Inward Eye, drops from High Priestess Azil in The Stonecore. The Shoulders, Spaulders of the Endless Plains, drop from Asaad in The Vortex Pinnacle. The Chestguard of the Lasher drops from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides, and the Gloves of the Passing Night drop from Admiral Ripsnarl in The Deadmines. The Leggings of the Impenitent drop from Lord Godfrey in Shadowfang Keep, the Boots of the Predator drop from Forgemaster Throngus in Grim Batol, and the Belt of the Dim Forest drops from General Husam in The Lost City of the Tol'vir.

### Farming Logistics
The set pieces drop from dungeons that are on a daily heroic lockout, meaning each dungeon can only be looted for these items once per day per character. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with no difficulty. Given the seven different items dropping from seven different dungeons, collecting the full set will take a minimum of one day but will likely require multiple daily resets due to drop-rate variance. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are personal loot from the specified bosses. The required pieces only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set all dungeons to Heroic difficulty before entering to ensure the correct loot tables are active. Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear the instances as quickly as possible. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. There are no relevant skips needed as a max-level character can run straight to each boss, and no special items like bonus rolls apply to this content.

93.content:
### How to Obtain
This set was acquired from Quartermaster Rez'thol in Icecrown during the Shadowlands pre-patch event, "Death's Rising." The currency used was Argent Commendations, which were rewarded from the event-specific daily quests. The full set, covering Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots, was purchased directly from this vendor. As this was a limited-time event, both the vendor and the currency were removed from the game when the event concluded, making the set permanently unobtainable.

### Farming Logistics
Acquisition was originally tied to a daily quest reset during the pre-patch event, not a raid or dungeon lockout. The quests were designed to be completed solo by players at the appropriate level. The set did not use a token system; each piece was purchased individually for Argent Commendations. All pieces came exclusively from the event vendor. This farming method is no longer available in the game.

### Farming Tips
This set cannot be farmed, as the "Death's Rising" event is no longer active. The items were Bind-on-Pickup, so they are not available on the standard Auction House. This set is also not known to appear on the Black Market Auction House. Players who did not acquire this appearance during the event must seek alternative transmog sets.

94.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon quality items dropped in three separate Cataclysm dungeons. The majority of the set drops in The Stonecore: Helm of the Inward Eye (Corborus), Pauldrons of the High Chamberlain (High Priestess Azil), Breastplate of the Stilled Heart (Ozruk), Bracers of Crushing Resolution (Slabhide), and Legguards of the Unseeing (Corborus). Blackrock Caverns contains the Gloves of Brawn (Rom'og Bonecrusher) and Boots of Ferromancy (Karsh Steelbender). The final piece, Belt of the Still Stream, drops from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons can be farmed on Normal difficulty, which has no lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour. Alternatively, they can be run on Heroic difficulty, which has a daily lockout per character. The entire set is easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces could take between 5 to 15 total runs across the three dungeons. There is no tier token system for this set as the items drop directly from bosses.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty to bypass the daily lockout. A high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk can significantly reduce clear times. Since five of the eight pieces drop from The Stonecore, focus your farming efforts there first before moving to Blackrock Caverns and Throne of the Tides. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House. To reset Normal instances, step outside, right-click your character portrait, and select "Reset all instances."

99.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from the Shadowlands expansion, primarily obtained by killing random mobs in the four main leveling zones: Bastion, Maldraxxus, Ardenweald, and Revendreth. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be traded or sold on the Auction House. The set consists of the Deathsilk Cowl (Head), Deathsilk Mantle (Shoulder), Deathsilk Robes (Chest), Deathsilk Bracelets (Wrist), Deathsilk Gloves (Hands), Deathsilk Sash (Waist), Deathsilk Leggings (Legs), and Deathsilk Slippers (Feet). There is no specific boss or location for any individual piece; they are all part of the general zone-drop loot table.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it relies on killing respawning mobs in outdoor zones. The entire farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Due to the random nature of world drops and the large loot tables in Shadowlands zones, acquiring a full set through farming alone can take many hours. All pieces drop from the same sources and are not tied to any tier token system. Since the items are Bind on Equip, the most reliable acquisition method is often the Auction House, bypassing the need for farming entirely.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House first, as purchasing the full set is almost always faster than farming for it. If you choose to farm, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect (AoE) abilities to clear large groups of enemies quickly. Look for areas with high mob density, sometimes referred to as "hyperspawn" locations, to maximize kills per hour. Farming in a group can increase efficiency, allowing for a constant rotation of mob clearing.

100.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained by a cloth-wearing character aligned with the Necrolord Covenant in Shadowlands. The main armor pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Waist) are rewarded for completing chapters of the Necrolord Covenant Campaign, starting with the quest "A Purpose in Death". The remaining pieces (Hands, Feet, Wrist) are unlocked by channeling anima to specific locations using a fully upgraded Anima Conductor within the Seat of the Primus. The Deathsilk Gloves appearance is unlocked from the Construct's Custom Coffer in the House of Constructs. The Deathsilk Slippers appearance is unlocked by defeating the rare mob Gieger in the House of Plagues, and the Deathsilk Wristwraps appearance is unlocked by defeating the rare mob Sabriel the Bonecleaver in the House of Constructs. Once an appearance is unlocked, the cosmetic item can be purchased from Goreguts in the Seat of the Primus for Reservoir Anima, though this is not required to use the transmog.

### Farming Logistics
The campaign quest portion of this set has no lockout and can be completed once per character. The Anima Conductor portion operates on a daily reset, as the available rare mobs and treasures at a channeled location change each day. The entire farm is fully soloable at current max level. Collecting the campaign pieces takes a few hours, while the Anima Conductor pieces may take several days to a week, depending on the daily rotation RNG. This set does not use a tier token system. The main set pieces come from one-time quests, while the offset pieces come from a repeatable daily activity, making for two distinct sources.

### Farming Tips
To begin, join the Necrolord Covenant on any cloth character in Oribos and complete the nine chapters of the covenant campaign to acquire the main set pieces. To acquire the remaining pieces, you must upgrade your Anima Conductor sanctum feature, which requires a significant amount of Reservoir Anima. Prioritize channeling anima to the House of Constructs and House of Plagues each day and check for the required rare or treasure. Once an appearance is unlocked through its associated source, it is permanently added to your account-wide collection, and you do not need to spend anima to buy the physical item from the vendor.

103.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained by purchasing the "Ensemble: Deathsilk Regalia" from Temel, the quartermaster for The Ember Court in Revendreth. The Ember Court is the unique feature of the Venthyr Covenant from the Shadowlands expansion. The ensemble costs 3,000 Infused Ruby, the currency earned by hosting Ember Court scenarios. To unlock the ability to purchase the ensemble, you must first achieve the "Best Friend" reputation level with Polemarch Adrestes, one of the court's guests. The ensemble teaches the appearances for all armor slots simultaneously.

### Farming Logistics
The Ember Court scenario, the source of the required currency and reputation, is on a weekly lockout per character. This content is entirely soloable at current max level, as it was designed as single-player content during Shadowlands. Acquiring the necessary 3,000 Infused Ruby and reaching "Best Friend" status with Polemarch Adrestes will take a minimum of 5-10 weekly lockouts, depending on scenario performance. There is no tier token system; the ensemble is purchased directly with currency. All pieces are learned from the single ensemble item, with no parts coming from other sources.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate the farm, focus on inviting Polemarch Adrestes every week and selecting scenario events that cater to his preferences for combat and honor to maximize reputation gain. Using the "Wargames" RSVP item when inviting Adrestes provides a significant bonus to the reputation earned with him for that week's scenario. Maximize your weekly Infused Ruby gain by successfully catering to the preferences of all four guests, aiming for a final "Excellent" party mood. Your character must be a member of the Venthyr Covenant to host The Ember Court; you can switch covenants in Oribos.

104.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from mobs in the level 35-45 range, found primarily in Classic zones such as Stranglethorn Vale, Desolace, Arathi Highlands, and Dustwallow Marsh. The seven pieces that comprise this green recolor set are the Emblazoned Hat, Emblazoned Shoulders, Emblazoned Vest, Emblazoned Leggings, Emblazoned Gloves, Emblazoned Boots, and Emblazoned Belt. These items are not tied to specific bosses and can drop from any eligible mob in the appropriate level range. The dungeon Razorfen Kraul is also a concentrated source of mobs that can drop these pieces.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout, allowing for continuous farming. The farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level, as all enemies will be trivial. Collecting all pieces is entirely dependent on random drops, which can take anywhere from a few hours to dozens of hours of dedicated farming. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces come from the same general loot table of level 35-45 world mobs, though some pieces may be rarer than others.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and are frequently listed for sale. For self-farming, use a high-mobility class with instant area-of-effect abilities, like a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of humanoids or beasts in Stranglethorn Vale. Running the dungeon Razorfen Kraul provides a high density of mobs in an instanced environment, which can be reset up to five times per hour. Any class can farm the BoE items, but only leather-wearing classes can learn the appearance.

105.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon Bind on Equip items, primarily with the "Hibernal" prefix, that drop from various high-level Classic dungeons. The Head piece, Hibernal Helm, drops from Darkmaster Gandling in Scholomance. The Shoulder (Hibernal Mantle), Legs (Hibernal Pants), and Feet (Hibernal Boots) drop from War Master Voone, Mother Smolderweb, and Quartermaster Zigris respectively in Lower Blackrock Spire. The Chest, Hibernal Robe, drops from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire. The Hands (Hibernal Gloves) and Waist (Hibernal Belt) drop from High Interrogator Gerstahn and Fineous Darkvire in Blackrock Depths. The Wrist, Hibernal Bracers, is a zone drop from mobs within these same dungeons.

### Farming Logistics
These dungeons have no effective lockout, as they can be reset up to 10 times per hour on Normal difficulty, which is the only difficulty available. All dungeons required for the set (Scholomance, Blackrock Depths, Blackrock Spire) can be fully soloed at current max level. Due to the very large loot tables and low drop chance for specific green items, directly farming could take over 100 runs; purchasing from the Auction House is the more reliable method. This set does not use a token system, but its pieces are scattered across four different instances. There are no separate LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic lockouts to consider for Classic-era dungeons.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to obtain this set is to check the Auction House regularly for the "Hibernal" pieces, as all are Bind on Equip and can be bought and sold. When farming directly, use a high-mobility class like a Demon Hunter or Druid to speedrun to the specific bosses and ignore unnecessary trash. In Blackrock Depths, you can use the Mole Machine transport system to skip directly to the required areas. Since the pieces drop from specific bosses, you can kill the target, exit the dungeon, and reset the instance without completing the entire run.

108.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop, primarily found on humanoid and beast mobs between levels 30 and 40. All pieces, including the Gypsy Turban (Head), Gypsy Tunic (Chest), Gypsy Trousers (Legs), Gypsy Gloves (Hands), Gypsy Sandals (Feet), Gypsy Sash (Waist), and Gypsy Wraps (Wrist), are random drops from enemies in this level range. There are no shoulders for this set. Optimal farming zones with high mob density include Stranglethorn Vale, Arathi Highlands, and Desolace.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to consider, allowing for continuous farming. The entire farm is easily soloable at current max level. The number of hours required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to the random nature of world drops. All items in this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be traded or sold. There are no tier tokens, and all pieces come from the same general source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and are frequently listed by other players. If farming directly, use a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage to clear large groups of mobs quickly, such as a Druid or Hunter. Focus on areas with dense humanoid populations, like the troll ruins and pirate camps throughout Stranglethorn Vale. The item names will include random enchantments like "of the Eagle," but the appearance will be collected for your Wardrobe regardless of the suffix.

109.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from bosses within three separate Classic-era dungeons. Four pieces drop within Wailing Caverns: the Waist from Lady Anacondra, the Hands from Lord Cobrahn, the Wrist from Serpentis, and the Chest from Verdan the Everliving. The Legs drop from Ghamoo-ra in Blackfathom Deeps. The final piece, the Feet, drops from Cookie in The Deadmines. The set does not include a matching Head or Shoulder piece.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons do not have a daily or weekly lockout and can be reset manually after exiting. A single player is limited to entering a maximum of 10 instances per hour across their account. The entire set can be easily soloed by any character at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all pieces directly from bosses should take approximately 5-10 total dungeon runs. This set does not use a tier token system, and pieces drop from three different dungeons, requiring travel between The Cape of Stranglethorn (for The Deadmines), Northern Barrens (for Wailing Caverns), and Ashenvale (for Blackfathom Deeps).

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Grizzly Garb set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so check the Auction House first to purchase them directly, which is often faster than farming. When farming Wailing Caverns, you must defeat all four Serpent Lords (Anacondra, Cobrahn, Serpentis, and Pythas) to summon Verdan the Everliving for the chest piece. Use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to speed up clears between the geographically separated dungeons. There is only one difficulty level for these dungeons, so no specific setting is required.

111.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 20-30 zones and instances from the Classic expansion. There are no specific bosses that drop these items; they can drop from any eligible mob in the appropriate level range. The main slots are Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist. Prime farming zones include Duskwood, Hillsbrad Foothills, Ashenvale, and Stonetalon Mountains. Dungeons such as Razorfen Kraul, Blackfathom Deeps, and Gnomeregan also serve as excellent, high-density sources for these items.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming these pieces in the open world, but dungeon farming is subject to the 10 instances per hour limit. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with no difficulty. The number of runs required is entirely dependent on random chance, as world drop rates are low and inconsistent. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot pool of level 20-30 mobs.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by searching for "Ceremonial" on the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and are frequently listed by other players. If you prefer to farm it yourself, use a high-mobility class to clear large groups of enemies in the suggested zones or to quickly reset dungeons. For dungeon farming, running Normal Razorfen Kraul is effective due to its high mob density and linear layout. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not sources for these items.

112.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Uncommon quality Bind-on-Equip world drops from Classic-era zones. All pieces drop from level 20-30 humanoid mobs, with no specific bosses assigned to any piece. The primary farming zones include Duskwood, Redridge Mountains, Ashenvale, and Stonetalon Mountains. The set includes the Bandit Jerkin (Chest), Bandit Trousers (Legs), Bandit Gloves (Hands), Bandit Boots (Feet), Bandit Cinch (Waist), and Bandit Bracers (Wrist); no matching head or shoulder pieces exist for this specific recolor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as all pieces are farmed from repeatable open-world mobs. The entire farm is fully soloable at maximum level, often with one-shot mechanics against the low-level targets. Due to the extremely low and random drop chance of world drops, the number of "runs" or hours required is highly variable and unpredictable. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are obtained from the same world drop source pool.

### Farming Tips
The most time-efficient method for obtaining this set is to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House. Search for each item by name (e.g., "Bandit Jerkin") and be aware that prices can fluctuate significantly. If choosing to farm the items directly, use a max-level character with high AoE damage and movement speed to clear large packs of humanoids in the designated zones. Since the items are Bind-on-Equip, you can farm or purchase them on any character on your realm and mail them to the character who needs the appearance.

114.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop, primarily found on mobs between levels 48 and 60. The most effective farming locations are high-density Classic-era zones like Silithus, Winterspring, and the Eastern Plaguelands. The pieces can also drop from trash mobs inside level 55-60 dungeons such as Scholomance, Stratholme, and Blackrock Spire. As world drops, no specific boss is tied to any specific piece of the set. The set consists of Feral Cover, Feral Shoulderpads, Feral Harness, Feral Leggings, Feral Gloves, Feral Boots, Feral Cord, and Feral Bracers.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it drops from mobs in open-world zones or from dungeon trash. If farming inside dungeons, you are limited to the standard 10 instances per hour cap. The entire farm can be completed solo by any max-level character. Collection time is highly variable due to the random nature of world drops and can range from a few hours to hundreds of hours of dedicated farming. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces come from the same pool of world drops.

### Farming Tips
All pieces in the Feral Garb set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the most efficient way to acquire the set is to check the Auction House first. For active farming, create a dedicated route clearing trash mobs inside a Classic dungeon like Stratholme or Blackrock Spire, as the mob density is higher than in open-world zones. Use a character with high mobility and instant area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear trash packs quickly before exiting and resetting the instance.

115.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of uncommon-quality world drops found in the level 1-10 starting zones of the Classic expansion. The primary sources are low-level humanoid mobs such as Kobolds in Elwynn Forest, Defias in Westfall, or Quillboar in Durotar. The set pieces are Frayed Robe (Chest), Frayed Pants (Legs), Frayed Hat (Head), Frayed Mantle (Shoulder), Frayed Gloves (Hands), Frayed Shoes (Feet), Frayed Belt (Waist), and Frayed Bracers (Wrist). There are no specific bosses or dungeons that drop these items; they are random drops from the general mob population in these low-level zones.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from world mobs that can be killed repeatedly. It is fully soloable by any character, with max-level characters able to eliminate mobs instantly. Due to the low drop chance and large loot tables of these creatures, collecting all eight pieces through farming can take several hours. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces come from the same general source. Instance difficulties and lockouts are not a factor in this farm.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and are often available on the Auction House, which is the most time-efficient acquisition method. If farming directly, use a high-mobility class with area-of-effect abilities to clear densely populated mob areas like the Echoing Deeps mine in Elwynn Forest. Ensure your loot filter is not set to ignore green-quality items. The appearance is learned by any class upon equipping the item, as it is cosmetic cloth with no class restrictions.

116.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Seer's Garb," an uncommon quality world drop set from various dungeons in the Burning Crusade expansion. The pieces drop from specific bosses within four different instances located in Outland. The Head, Seer's Circlet, drops from Harbinger Skyriss in The Arcatraz. The Shoulder, Seer's Mantle, and Hands, Seer's Gloves, drop from Grand Warlock Nethekurse and Warchief Kargath Bladefist respectively in The Shattered Halls. The Chest, Seer's Robe, and Legs, Seer's Fine-Spun Kilt, drop from Mennu the Betrayer and Quagmirran respectively in Slave Pens. The Feet, Seer's Boots, drop from Hungarfen in The Underbog. No matching Waist or Wrist items exist for this particular set.

### Farming Logistics
The set pieces drop from dungeons that can be run on Normal or Heroic difficulty. Normal difficulty has no lockout and can be reset manually, though you are limited to 10 instance entries per hour across your account. Heroic difficulty has a daily lockout, resetting once per day. The entire set can be easily soloed by a current max-level character. Given the 15-25% drop chance per piece, collecting all six items will likely take 5-15 total runs distributed across the four required dungeons. This set does not use a token system, and Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same loot tables for these items.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to immediately reset the instance and try again for a specific piece without waiting for a daily lockout. The pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The four dungeons are located in three different zones: Slave Pens and The Underbog are in Coilfang Reservoir (Zangarmarsh), The Shattered Halls is in Hellfire Citadel (Hellfire Peninsula), and The Arcatraz is in Tempest Keep (Netherstorm). Using a high-mobility class will significantly speed up travel time within the linear instances.

117.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind on Equip world drops from level 15-25 humanoid mobs in Classic zones. The most concentrated farming location is The Deadmines dungeon in Westfall, as the Defias mobs inside have a high density and frequently drop the pieces. There are no specific bosses for any piece; they all drop from trash mobs. The set consists of Bandit Shoulders, Bandit Jerkin (Chest), Bandit Pants (Legs), Bandit Gloves (Hands), Bandit Cinch (Waist), and Bandit Boots (Feet), with no matching head or wrist items.

### Farming Logistics
Farming inside The Deadmines is subject to the standard dungeon lockout of 10 instances per hour, per account. The farm can be fully soloed at current max level. Due to the low, random drop chance of world drops, collecting the full set could take anywhere from 10 to 50+ dungeon clears or several hours of open-world farming. This set does not use a token system. All pieces are Bind on Equip and drop from the same pool of mobs, with no alternate sources or difficulty-specific lockouts.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the Normal difficulty version of The Deadmines. A max-level character can clear all trash mobs up to the first boss, exit, reset the instance, and repeat the process up to the hourly limit. Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), the fastest method to obtain the set is often to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House. Remember to equip the BoE items on a leather-wearing character to add their appearance to your collection.

118.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop, primarily farmed from humanoid mobs in the level 17-28 range. The best zone to farm all pieces is Duskwood. The Blackened Defias Mask, Gloves, and Boots drop from mobs like Nightbane Worgen, Skeletal Fiends, and Defias Enchanters around level 17-26. The Blackened Defias Armor and Leggings drop from slightly higher-level mobs in the same zone, such as Skeletal Horrors and Defias Night Blades, typically level 21-28. The set does not include matching Shoulders, Waist, or Wrists.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from world mobs, so there is no lockout or instance reset to consider. The farm can be done continuously and is fully soloable at max level for maximum efficiency. Due to the extremely low drop chance for uncommon world drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from a few hours to dozens of hours of dedicated farming. All pieces drop from humanoid mobs in the same general zones, so no travel between different instance types is required. There are no tier tokens or different difficulty versions for this set.

### Farming Tips
Use a max-level character with high mobility and area-of-effect abilities to clear mobs in Duskwood quickly. All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can frequently be found on the Auction House, which is the most time-efficient method of acquisition. If farming, focus on high-density humanoid areas like Raven Hill Cemetery or the southern Defias encampments. There are no special skips or lockouts to manage for this farm.

119.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained as a random world drop from humanoid and beast mobs, primarily in the level 20-30 range. All main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are part of this Bind-on-Equip world drop pool. The most efficient farming zones include Duskwood, the northern part of Stranglethorn Vale, Ashenvale, and Hillsbrad Foothills. There is no specific boss, dungeon, or raid that drops these items; they are found by killing common enemies in the open world.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set has no lockout, as it is composed of world drops that can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be soloed by any character and is trivial at maximum level. Since drop chances are low and depend on the specific mobs being farmed, collecting all pieces can take a significant amount of time, potentially requiring hundreds or thousands of mob kills. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces drop from the same general pool of level 20-30 enemies.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for individual pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind-on-Equip. For players who prefer to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities to clear large groups of mobs quickly in the target zones. Since the items are uncommon quality, consider using an addon that highlights or auto-loots items of a specific quality to avoid missing a drop. Characters with the Skinning profession can generate additional gold while farming beast-heavy areas for the set.

120.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip world drops found within the Classic dungeon, Wailing Caverns. The five core pieces drop from specific bosses located inside the instance. The chest, Armor of the Fang, drops from Lord Cobrahn. The legs, Leggings of the Fang, are looted from Lord Pythas. The hands, Gloves of the Fang, drop from Lady Anacondra, and the waist, Belt of the Fang, is found on Lord Serpentis. The final piece, the feet, Footpads of the Fang, is found in the chest that spawns after defeating Mutanus the Devourer.

### Farming Logistics
The instance has a normal dungeon lockout, which can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character, with a full clear taking only a few minutes. Given the number of pieces and their drop chances, a complete set can typically be farmed in 5-15 runs. There is no tier token system for this set; all loot drops directly from the bosses. Classic dungeons have only one difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), check the Auction House first, as you may be able to purchase the entire set cheaply and avoid farming altogether. When farming, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to navigate the winding caves of the instance quickly. You must defeat the four required Fang Lords before the final boss can be summoned. There is no way to skip these encounters, so a full clear of all relevant bosses is necessary on each run.

121.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from level 48-58 mobs throughout Classic Azeroth zones. There are no specific bosses that drop the pieces; they are found on the loot tables of creatures within the appropriate level range. Key farming zones include Western Plaguelands, Eastern Plaguelands, Felwood, and Burning Steppes. The complete set consists of the Emblazoned Hat, Emblazoned Shoulders, Emblazoned Robe, Emblazoned Pants, Emblazoned Gloves, Emblazoned Boots, Emblazoned Belt, and Emblazoned Bracers. Any mob within the level range in these zones has a small chance to drop a piece.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to consider, allowing for continuous farming. The set is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low drop chance for specific world drops, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can take many hours of farming. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces come from the same world drop loot pool. The concept of separate raid or dungeon difficulties does not apply to this farm.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip (BoE). Farming should be done with a high-mobility class capable of large area-of-effect pulls to maximize kills per hour. Focus on areas with high humanoid mob density, such as the undead scourge in the Plaguelands, for the best chance at cloth armor drops. Remember that you must equip a BoE item on a character that can wear that armor type for it to be added to your wardrobe.

123.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon quality Bind-on-Equip world drops, primarily found in The Deadmines dungeon and from Defias-affiliated mobs in the zones of Westfall and Duskwood. The chest piece, Blackened Defias Armor, drops from mobs in Duskwood and bosses like Cookie in The Deadmines. The legs, Blackened Defias Leggings, also drop in Duskwood from mobs such as Stitches. The hands, Blackened Defias Gloves, and feet, Blackened Defias Boots, are commonly found on mobs in Westfall and drop from The Deadmines bosses Miner Johnson and Sneed, respectively. The waist piece, Blackened Defias Belt, drops from mobs in Westfall and the boss Rhahk'Zor inside the dungeon. There are no matching head, shoulder, or wrist pieces for this specific recolor set.

### Farming Logistics
Farming inside The Deadmines is subject to the standard dungeon lockout: players below level 60 can reset the instance up to 10 times per hour, while higher-level characters are limited to a daily reset. The outdoor world drop farm has no lockout. The set can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Due to low drop chances, collecting the full set can take between 20 to 100 dungeon runs or equivalent time spent farming mobs. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same type of content. Only the "Classic" Normal difficulty version of The Deadmines contains these items, as the Cataclysm Heroic version has a different loot table.

### Farming Tips
For the most efficient farming, use a high-level character to clear the Normal difficulty "Classic" version of The Deadmines. Because all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), they can be purchased directly from the Auction House, which is often the fastest method of acquisition. Any class can farm the items, but only a Leather-wearing class (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) can equip them to add the appearances to the account-wide wardrobe. When farming, focus on full-clearing The Deadmines or using a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage to quickly eliminate mobs in Westfall or Duskwood.

124.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops primarily found in level 25-35 Classic dungeons. The best instances to farm for these pieces are Razorfen Kraul and Blackfathom Deeps due to the high density of mobs in the appropriate level range. All pieces share the "Headhunter's" name prefix and drop from trash mobs and bosses within these zones. The specific slots are Headhunter's Head-covering (Head), Headhunter's Shoulders (Shoulder), Headhunter's Tunic (Chest), Headhunter's Trousers (Legs), Headhunter's Mitts (Hands), Headhunter's Boots (Feet), Headhunter's Belt (Waist), and Headhunter's Bands (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout, but you are limited to 10 instance resets per hour on a single character. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the random nature of world drops, the number of runs required is highly variable and could range from a few dozen to several hundred dungeon clears. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot tables within the target zones. Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties do not apply to these Classic dungeons.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to move through the instance quickly. Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), the most efficient way to obtain the set is often to purchase the items directly from the Auction House. When farming inside dungeons, prioritize killing all trash mob packs, as they have the highest cumulative chance of dropping the pieces compared to the bosses. There are no skips required; a full clear of an instance like Razorfen Kraul is the most effective strategy.

125.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cloth world drop from Classic zones, primarily from humanoid, demon, and undead mobs in the level 35-45 range. The best zones for farming are Stranglethorn Vale, Dustwallow Marsh, Desolace, and Arathi Highlands. Unlike instance sets, specific pieces do not drop from specific bosses. The main armor pieces are Nocturnal Cap (Head), Nocturnal Shoulder Pads (Shoulder), Nocturnal Robe (Chest), Nocturnal Leggings (Legs), Nocturnal Gloves (Hands), and Nocturnal Slippers (Feet), all of which can drop from any eligible mob within the level range.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is farmed from respawning mobs in outdoor zones. The farm is fully soloable by any max-level character, and it is trivial in terms of difficulty. The time required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to the low, random nature of world drops, potentially taking dozens of hours. The set does not use a token system, and all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE). There are no different difficulties or lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
The fastest and most practical way to obtain this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip and commonly available. If you choose to farm it yourself, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs efficiently. Focus on high-density areas like the troll ruins in Stranglethorn Vale or the naga encampments along the coast of Desolace. No special skips, raid tools, or group mechanics are relevant to this farm.

126.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Looking for Raid difficulty plate armor appearance from the Sanctum of Domination raid in Shadowlands. The individual pieces are obtained from specific bosses within the LFR version of the instance. The Head drops from Painsmith Raznal, and the Shoulder drops from Kel'Thuzad. The Chest is looted from Fatescribe Roh-Kalo, while the Legs drop from the Guardian of the First Ones. The Hands are a drop from Soulrender Dormazain, the Feet from Remnant of Ner'zhul, and both the Waist and Wrist pieces drop from The Tarragrue.

### Farming Logistics
The Sanctum of Domination raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. The LFR difficulty required for this set has its own separate weekly lockout from Normal, Heroic, and Mythic. The entire raid is soloable by a max-level character. This set is comprised of leather armor pieces, so it must be farmed on a Demon Hunter, Druid, Monk, or Rogue to be equipped and added to your wardrobe. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all eight pieces may take between 5-10 full clears on a single character. This raid does not use a tier token system; gear drops directly as individual items from bosses.

### Farming Tips
To farm the LFR version specifically, visit the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos (in the Hall of Shapes) and choose the "Sanctum of Domination" LFR wings from his dialogue options. You can queue for the four wings individually, allowing you to target only the bosses you need if you have already collected some pieces. All items in this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Using a class with high mobility, such as a Demon Hunter or Monk, will speed up the process of moving between bosses within the raid wings.

128.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality world drops, primarily found on mobs between levels 42 and 52. The best locations for farming are the dungeons Zul'Farrak and Maraudon, or the open-world zones of Tanaris, Feralas, and The Hinterlands. The items are not tied to specific bosses and can drop from any eligible mob in these locations. The set pieces are: Head (Watcher's Cap), Shoulder (Watcher's Mantle), Chest (Watcher's Tunic), Legs (Watcher's Leggings), Hands (Watcher's Gloves), Feet (Watcher's Boots), Waist (Watcher's Cinch), and Wrist (Watcher's Bracers).

### Farming Logistics
This farm has no lockout; dungeons can be reset up to 10 times per hour, and open-world mobs can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be easily soloed by any character at current max level. Due to the random nature of world drops, the time required to collect all pieces can vary from a few hours to dozens of hours. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot pool. Classic-era zones and dungeons do not have different difficulties like LFR, Normal, or Heroic.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House first, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and can often be purchased directly, saving significant time. If farming manually, running the dungeon Zul'Farrak is highly efficient due to its high density of humanoid mobs in the required level range. A max-level character can clear the entire instance in minutes, exit, reset, and repeat. Since these are world drops, mechanics like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply.

129.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a random world drop from mobs and chests in the level 15-25 range. The best zones for farming include The Barrens, Darkshore, Silverpine Forest, and Westfall due to their high density of appropriate-level mobs. All pieces, including the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist, share this same drop source. They can also be found inside low-level dungeons such as Deadmines, Wailing Caverns, and Shadowfang Keep. The items do not drop from specific bosses but rather from any eligible mob within the level bracket.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it is a world drop. The farm is completely soloable by any character significantly above level 25. Due to the low and random nature of world drops, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can take many hours of dedicated farming. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are obtained from the same sources. There are no different difficulties or lockouts to consider for this type of farm.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for individual pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip (BoE). When searching, look for the base name (e.g., "Scouting Tunic") and ignore the random suffix ("of the Tiger," etc.), as the appearance is the same. If farming directly, use a high-mobility class with area-of-effect abilities to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Farming low-level dungeons can be more efficient than the open world because of the higher mob density in a confined area.

131.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained by purchasing the "Ensemble: Garb of the Twilight Grove" from the Night Fae Covenant vendor in Ardenweald. The vendor, Elwyn, is the Renown Quartermaster located in the Heart of the Forest. The ensemble costs 10,000 Reservoir Anima. Purchasing and learning the ensemble requires being a member of the Night Fae Covenant, reaching Renown 60, and completing Chapter 3 of the 9.1 Chains of Domination campaign ("Focusing the Eye"). The single ensemble item unlocks all eight appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Back slots.

### Farming Logistics
This set is purchased with currency, so it has no lockout and can be farmed continuously. All required content for farming Reservoir Anima is easily soloable at the current maximum level. The time needed to collect the 10,000 Anima can range from a few hours to a couple of days, depending on farming efficiency. Anima is rewarded from Shadowlands world quests, treasures, rare elites, dungeon quests, and the command table. Although you must be a member of the Night Fae covenant to purchase the item, once the ensemble is learned, the appearances are available account-wide to all characters of any class or covenant.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Reservoir Anima is by looting treasures and killing rare elites in Zereth Mortis, as they provide large amounts of currency. If you have a character with high Renown in a different covenant, you can farm the Anima on that character and then swap to the Night Fae covenant in Oribos to make the purchase, a process which is free and instant once you have reached Renown 80 on any character. The ensemble item is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Remember that the Chains of Domination campaign quest is a mandatory prerequisite and is started by accepting "The First Move" in Oribos.

132.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder difficulty tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. The five main tier pieces drop from specific bosses: the Hands drop from Lihuvim, Principal Architect; the Legs from Halondrus the Reclaimer; the Head from Anduin Wrynn; the Shoulders from the Lords of Dread; and the Chest from Rygelon. The non-tier lookalike Waist drops from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, the Feet drop from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener, and the Wrists drop from Anduin Wrynn. All pieces are from the same raid instance on Raid Finder difficulty.

### Farming Logistics
This set is on a weekly raid lockout per boss. At the current max level, the entire Raid Finder version of Sepulcher of the First Ones can be easily soloed by any of the eligible classes. Due to the weekly lockout and number of items, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 4-8 weeks. The tier pieces are obtained via the Mystic token, which is shared by Hunters, Mages, and Shamans; the appearance will also unlock for Evokers. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts, but this specific green recolor only drops in Raid Finder.

### Farming Tips
To access the Raid Finder version, speak to the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos, located at coordinates 64, 21. Tier tokens must be right-clicked in your inventory by an eligible class (Hunter or Shaman) to create the item and add the appearance to your collection. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The Great Vault and bonus roll systems from Shadowlands are no longer active for this content, so drops are entirely dependent on personal loot from boss kills.

133.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from level 47-57 mobs throughout Azeroth. All eight pieces—Insignia Cap (Head), Insignia Mantle (Shoulder), Insignia Robes (Chest), Insignia Leggings (Legs), Insignia Gloves (Hands), Insignia Boots (Feet), Insignia Sash (Waist), and Insignia Bracers (Wrist)—share the same random drop chance from this loot table. No specific boss drops any particular piece. The most effective farming locations are dungeons with high mob density, such as Blackrock Depths, Stratholme, and Scholomance, or open-world zones like Winterspring and the Eastern Plaguelands.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout when farming mobs in the open world. If farming inside dungeons, you are subject to the cap of 10 normal-difficulty instance resets per hour.
