This entire farm is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. This set does not use a tier token system, as it is a collection of random world drops. All pieces drop from the same mob level range, so no specific location needs to be targeted for an individual slot. Due to the extremely low probability of specific world drops, collecting the full set through targeted farming is highly variable and can take a significant amount of time.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House. All items in the set are Bind on Equip (BoE) and are frequently listed for sale by other players. If you choose to farm the set yourself, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect abilities to clear large packs of mobs quickly. Focus on high-density areas, such as the initial chambers of Blackrock Depths, and run the dungeon on Normal difficulty to allow for repeated resets.

134.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from the Kyrian Covenant vendor, Adjutant Galos, in Elysian Hold, Bastion. The full set costs 9,000 Reservoir Anima and requires reaching Renown 60 with the Kyrian Covenant. The pieces and their individual costs are: Ghostwalker's Diadem (Head, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Pauldrons (Shoulder, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Wraps (Chest, 1500 Anima), Ghostwalker's Leggings (Legs, 1500 Anima), Ghostwalker's Gauntlets (Hands, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Treads (Feet, 1000 Anima), Ghostwalker's Cord (Waist, 1000 Anima), and Ghostwalker's Armbands (Wrist, 1000 Anima). The back slot item, Ghostwalker's Drape, is a separate purchase for 1,250 Anima.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is purchased with a currency. The acquisition is gated by your ability to farm Reservoir Anima and the one-time requirement of reaching Renown 60 with the Kyrian Covenant. Farming the required 9,000 Anima can be done entirely solo and the time it takes depends on your farming efficiency. All pieces are from the same vendor and source. The appearance is cosmetic and can be learned by any class, but only a character aligned with the Kyrian Covenant can purchase the items.

### Farming Tips
To farm Reservoir Anima efficiently, focus on completing World Quests in all four Shadowlands zones, especially those that award 250 or 500 Anima. Daily Covenant Callings and weekly quests like "Replenish the Reservoir" provide large amounts of Anima. Farming rare mobs and treasures in Zereth Mortis is also a highly effective method. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Once purchased, the appearance is unlocked account-wide for all characters regardless of their armor type or Covenant allegiance.

135.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from world drops within the Shadowlands zone of Revendreth. All pieces of the set, including the Archer's Cowl (Head), Spaulders (Shoulder), Tunic (Chest), Leggings (Legs), Handwraps (Hands), Slippers (Feet), Cinch (Waist), and Bracers (Wrist), can drop from any standard, non-elite creature found throughout the zone. There are no specific bosses or locations tied to individual pieces. The entire set is Bind-on-Equip, meaning it can also be purchased from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it relies on killing respawning world mobs. The farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level, as Shadowlands creatures pose no threat. The time required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to random world drop chances; it can take anywhere from one hour to many hours of continuous farming. No tier token system is used for this set, and all pieces drop from the same type of source.

### Farming Tips
Before farming, check the Auction House, as individual pieces are often listed for sale and can be inexpensive. For manual farming, a good location is Wanecrypt Hill in northwestern Revendreth due to its high density of humanoid mobs that respawn quickly. Use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Hunter, to kill large groups of enemies efficiently. The set does not require a specific Covenant or questline to be eligible to drop.

137.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the base cosmetic armor from the Night Fae Covenant, awarded for completing chapters of the covenant's campaign in Shadowlands. The specific pieces are guaranteed rewards from the following quests which conclude each chapter.
*   **Head & Shoulder:** Awarded from "A New Beginning," the final quest of Chapter 8.
*   **Chest:** Awarded from "The Fading of the Vale," the final quest of Chapter 7.
*   **Legs:** Awarded from "The Night's Gambit," the final quest of Chapter 5.
*   **Hands & Feet:** Awarded from "The Horn of the Wild Hunt," the final quest of Chapter 3.
*   **Waist & Wrist:** Awarded from "Drust to Drust," the final quest of Chapter 2.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is obtained through a one-time questline. The entire Shadowlands covenant campaign is fully soloable at current max level. Obtaining the full set is guaranteed upon completion of the first eight chapters of the campaign and does not rely on random drops. This set does not use a token system. Once you collect all pieces on a single character, the full cosmetic set is added to your account-wide wardrobe, usable by any character regardless of armor type.

### Farming Tips
To begin the Night Fae campaign, you must first pledge allegiance to the covenant in Oribos. If you have already completed the main Shadowlands story on another character, you can use the Threads of Fate skip to immediately choose a covenant and begin its campaign. All pieces are soulbound quest rewards and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Should you delete a piece, it can be re-purchased from Elwyn, the Renown Quartermaster in the Heart of the Forest, Ardenweald.

139.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from the Classic expansion, found on mobs roughly between levels 40 and 50. The best zones for farming include Tanaris, Feralas, The Hinterlands, and the Searing Gorge. Specific dungeons such as Zul'Farrak, Maraudon, and the lower sections of Blackrock Depths are also excellent sources due to their high mob density. All main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are random drops from any humanoid, demon, or undead mob within this level range and are not tied to specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it relies on killing respawning world mobs or resetting dungeons. The dungeon instance limit is 10 instances per hour per account. The set is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to its nature as a random world drop, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can range from a few hours to several weeks of dedicated farming. No tier token system is used, and all pieces are obtained from the same pool of world drops.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and are frequently listed for sale. If you prefer to farm it directly, the dungeon Zul'Farrak in Tanaris is a superior location; pulling large groups of trolls, especially during the zombie graveyard event, maximizes kills per hour. A fast-moving class is recommended to clear the zone or instance quickly. Any character that can equip cloth armor can bind the items to their character to learn the appearance for all characters on the account.

140.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 35-45 mobs in original Classic zones. All pieces are Bind on Equip, making the Auction House the most reliable acquisition source. If farming directly, the best zones include Dustwallow Marsh, the Cape of Stranglethorn, Arathi Highlands, and the Scarlet Monastery dungeon. The core armor pieces are random drops from any mob within the appropriate level range, with no specific bosses assigned to any slot. The main armor slots are the Nocturnal Cap (Head), Nocturnal Shoulder Pads (Shoulder), Nocturnal Tunic (Chest), Nocturnal Wristbands (Wrist), Nocturnal Gloves (Hands), Nocturnal Sash (Waist), Nocturnal Leggings (Legs), and Nocturnal Moccasins (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is comprised of world drops from outdoor zones and dungeons that can be reset. It is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drops, the number of runs required is unpredictable and could range from dozens to thousands of hours of farming. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces originate from the same pool of world drops, though their individual drop rates may vary slightly.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House. Farming for these items directly is highly time-consuming and not recommended due to the low drop chance. If you choose to farm, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly in high-density areas. Running an instance like Scarlet Monastery repeatedly can be more effective than world farming due to the high number of enemies in a confined, instanced space that can be reset.

141.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is a random world drop obtained from mobs in the level 40-50 range, primarily within Classic dungeons. All pieces of the set, such as the Watcher's Cap, Robes, and Mantle, can drop from any non-boss creature in the appropriate level range. The most efficient farming locations are dungeons with high mob density, specifically Uldaman, Zul'Farrak, and Maraudon. No specific bosses are tied to these drops; the entire set comes from the shared loot table of trash mobs.

### Farming Logistics
The farm uses the standard dungeon system, which has no lockout and allows for up to 10 instance resets per hour on Normal difficulty. The set can be fully soloed at current max level, with each run taking only a few minutes to clear the relevant trash mobs. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance for any given piece per full clear, collecting all eight pieces would take approximately 30-50 runs. This set does not use a tier token system, and there are no alternate sources for any of the pieces. Classic dungeons only have a single difficulty, so multiple lockouts are not a factor.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the most reliable and fastest method of acquisition is often to purchase them from the Auction House. When farming in a dungeon like Uldaman, do not kill any bosses or complete the instance; focus solely on clearing large packs of trash mobs, then exit and reset. Use a character with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to maximize the number of mobs killed per hour. There are no skips or special mechanics involved beyond killing trash as quickly as possible.

145.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from mobs between levels 35 and 45. All eight pieces—Nocturnal Cap, Nocturnal Shoulder Pads, Nocturnal Tunic, Nocturnal Leggings, Nocturnal Gloves, Nocturnal Boots, Nocturnal Belt, and Nocturnal Wristbands—are random drops from this loot pool. The most effective zones for farming these mobs are Stranglethorn Vale, Arathi Highlands, and Desolace. There are no specific bosses or quests that award these items; they are found exclusively through killing creatures in the open world within the appropriate level range.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as it is composed entirely of world drops. The farm can be completed entirely solo at maximum level, as the mobs are low-level and pose no threat. Due to the extremely large loot tables and low drop chances for specific green items, directly farming the complete set could take dozens of hours of continuous mob grinding. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces drop from the same general source. All pieces are Bind-on-Equip, meaning they can be traded or sold on the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House; search for "Nocturnal" and filter by the appropriate armor type. If you choose to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high mob density to maximize kills per hour. Since the items are BoE, check the Auction House periodically even while farming, as individual pieces may be listed for a low price.

147.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon items found in level 35-45 Classic dungeons, primarily Scarlet Monastery, Uldaman, and Razorfen Downs. The chest piece, Watcher's Tunic, drops from Arcanist Doan in Scarlet Monastery: Library, while the wrist, Watcher's Bindings, drops from Houndmaster Loksey in Scarlet Monastery: Armory. The shoulder, hands, and feet pieces are zone drops primarily found by farming trash mobs within Uldaman. The waist is a zone drop from Scarlet Monastery, and the legs are a zone drop from Razorfen Downs. The head piece is the Shadoweave Mask, a crafted item from the Tailoring profession.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons for this set have no daily or weekly lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire farm is fully soloable at maximum level, with all bosses being defeated in a single hit. The boss-specific pieces will take approximately 4-7 runs per item to acquire, based on a 15-25% drop chance. The numerous zone-drop pieces have a significantly lower drop rate and may require many more full clears of their respective dungeons. No tier tokens are associated with this set, and the head piece is crafted by Tailors, not a dungeon drop. There are no separate LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic difficulties for these classic dungeon drops.

### Farming Tips
All of the zone-drop pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can frequently be purchased directly from the Auction House, which is the most efficient method of acquisition. For pieces that must be farmed, use a high-mobility class to clear the dungeons quickly; a character with a speed-increasing talent build is effective for trash farming. When farming boss-specific items in Scarlet Monastery, you can run directly to the final bosses of the Library and Armory wings to save time. The crafted head piece can either be made by a character with Classic Tailoring or purchased from another player or the Auction House.

151.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 51-60 zones and instances introduced in Classic. The most effective farming locations are the dungeons Scholomance and Stratholme due to their high density of elite mobs. All pieces are random drops from trash mobs and bosses within these zones. The main armor slots are the Cabalist Hood (Head), Cabalist Shoulders (Shoulder), Cabalist Robes (Chest), Cabalist Leggings (Legs), Cabalist Gloves (Hands), Cabalist Boots (Feet), Cabalist Belt (Waist), and Cabalist Bracers (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout, as you will run the Normal difficulty of the dungeons, which can be reset manually. However, you are limited by the cap of 10 instances per hour per account. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. The number of runs required is highly variable due to the low, random drop chance of world drops; it can take anywhere from a few dozen to several hundred clears. There is no tier token system for this set, and the pieces drop individually.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Cabalist Garb set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the fastest way to acquire the set is often by purchasing it from the Auction House. If farming manually, use a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to clear the instances quickly. To maximize your chances per run, kill all trash mobs in the dungeon rather than just rushing to the bosses. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault do not apply to loot from these dungeons.

152.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted by the Leatherworking profession using materials and patterns primarily found within the Wailing Caverns dungeon in The Barrens. The main reagent is Deviate Scale, which drops from Deviate Ravagers, Deviate Guardians, and other beasts inside the instance. The three patterns needed to craft the set also drop from various mobs within Wailing Caverns. The specific items that comprise this set are the Deviate Scale Breastplate, Deviate Scale Belt, and Deviate Scale Gloves. There are no associated head, shoulder, leg, feet, or wrist items in the named set.

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no daily or weekly lockout, but is subject to the retail client's limit of 10 instances per hour per account. The Wailing Caverns dungeon can be fully soloed at maximum level by any class. Acquiring all necessary patterns and materials typically takes 5-10 full clears of the dungeon's non-boss mobs. This set does not use a tier token system, as it is a crafted set rather than a raid tier set. All patterns and materials originate from the same source, Wailing Caverns, and there are no alternate difficulties for this classic dungeon.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House first, as both the patterns and the finished armor pieces are Bind-on-Equip and frequently available for purchase. If farming, focus on clearing the non-boss trash mobs in the main circular cavern, as they are the primary source of both Deviate Scales and the patterns. It is not necessary to kill any of the bosses. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault do not apply to this farm, as it is legacy dungeon content.

153.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth set is a world drop primarily found within the level 58-60 dungeons of Scholomance and Stratholme in the Eastern Plaguelands. While some pieces are random zone drops within these instances, others have a high chance to drop from specific bosses. Key drops include the Necropile Helm from Darkmaster Gandling in Scholomance, Necropile Robe from various bosses with a notable drop chance from Instructor Malicia in Scholomance, and Necropile Cuffs from The Unforgiven in Stratholme. The matching Shadoweave Shoulders are crafted by Tailors but the pattern is no longer obtainable; the closest visual match that drops is the Boreal Mantle from mobs in the dungeons. The remaining pieces (Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist) drop from various bosses and trash mobs throughout both Scholomance and Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to the standard normal dungeon lockout of 10 instances per hour, per account. The instances can be reset manually after each clear by right-clicking your character portrait. This entire set is easily soloable by any max-level character, with full clears of either dungeon taking only a few minutes. Due to the random nature of the drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from 5 to 20 runs, heavily dependent on luck with the zone-wide drops. There is no tier token system for this set, and the pieces are collected individually. The appearance is only collectable by cloth-wearing classes: Mage, Priest, and Warlock.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, perform full clears of Scholomance, killing all bosses and trash mobs to maximize your chances at the Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops. Since all pieces of this set are BoE, the fastest way to acquire them is often to check the Auction House, as other players may sell the pieces they find. There are no relevant skips for this farm, as a full clear is optimal. Running on Normal difficulty is sufficient, as Heroic difficulty is only available during Timewalking events and does not increase the drop chance for these specific items.

154.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set cloth items that drop from final bosses in various Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The Head, Cowl of the Grand Engineer, drops from Mechano-Lord Capacitus and the Legs, Trousers of the Astromancer, drop from Grand Astromancer Capernian in Heroic The Mechanar. The Shoulder, Mana-Sphere Shoulderguards, drops from High Botanist Freywinn and the Chest, Robe of the Great Dark Beyond, drops from Warp Splinter in Heroic The Botanica. The Hands, Gloves of the Fallen Wizard, drop from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in Heroic The Shattered Halls. The Feet, Boots of the Incorrupt, drop from Quagmirran in Heroic The Slave Pens, while the Waist and Wrist drop in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth: Evoker's Silk Cord from Grandmaster Vorpil and Cuffs of the Devastator from Murmur.

### Farming Logistics
The five required dungeons are on a daily dungeon reset for Heroic difficulty. All instances can be easily soloed by a current max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance, obtaining the full 8-piece set will likely require 5-10 resets per dungeon, spread across roughly one to two weeks of daily farming. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops from their respective bosses. All pieces drop exclusively from Heroic difficulty, which has a separate daily lockout from the repeatable Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance. Using a high-mobility class such as a Monk, Druid, or Demon Hunter will significantly speed up travel time within the instances. To minimize travel time between dungeons, set a hearthstone in Shattrath City for quick access to the Tempest Keep and Auchindoun instance portals. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

155.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon-quality world drops, primarily found on humanoid mobs between levels 41-51. The main pieces are the Cadaverous Headguard, Shoulderpads, Armor (Chest), Leggings, Gloves, and Walkers (Feet), with a matching Cadaverous Belt and Bracers (Wrist). The most concentrated source of eligible mobs is inside dungeons. The most effective dungeons for farming these drops are Zul'Farrak in Tanaris and Uldaman in the Badlands, due to their high density of humanoid targets within the correct level range.

### Farming Logistics
Dungeon farming is limited by the 10 instances per hour, per account cap; there is no daily or weekly lockout. The entire set is easily soloed by any class at current max level. Due to the large loot tables and low drop chances for any specific world drop, collecting the full set via farming can take hundreds of runs; there is no tier token system. All pieces drop from the same general loot tables and are not tied to specific difficulties, as these dungeons only have one mode.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, use a high-mobility class to clear large groups of non-elite mobs and reset the dungeon. The most practical way to acquire this set is by purchasing the Bind-on-Equip (BoE) pieces from the Auction House, as targeted farming is highly inefficient. Check the Auction House regularly for pieces, as prices can vary significantly. While any class can farm the BoE items to sell, only Leather-specialization classes (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) can learn the appearances.

159.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of cloth armor pieces that drop from various final bosses in Level 70 Heroic dungeons from The Burning Crusade. The Head, Cowl of the Grand Engineer, drops from Mekgineer Steamrigger in Heroic The Steamvault. The Shoulder, Mantle of the Mind Flayer, is from Murmur in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth. Find the Chest, Robe of the Great Dark Beyond, on Nexus-Prince Shaffar in Heroic Mana-Tombs, and the Legs, Leggings of the Ethereal, on Warp Splinter in Heroic The Botanica. The Hands, Gloves of the Nether-Stalker, drop from Pathaleon the Calculator in Heroic The Mechanar, while the Feet, Boots of the Incorporeal, are from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in Heroic The Shattered Halls. The Waist, Sash of the Ethereal, drops from The Black Stalker in Heroic The Underbog, and the Wrist, Bracers of the Incorporeal, drops from Harbinger Skyriss in Heroic The Arcatraz.

### Farming Logistics
The set is subject to a daily dungeon reset, as all pieces drop from Heroic difficulty dungeons which lock you out for 24 hours after completion. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level. Due to each piece dropping from a different instance, you have one chance per day for each specific slot. With an approximate 20% drop chance per item, acquiring the full 8-piece set can take several weeks of consistent daily runs across all eight dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces are direct boss drops and are not class-restricted for collection.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic before entering each instance. At max level, all trash and boss mechanics can be ignored, allowing you to run directly to the target boss for a quick kill. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. While most dungeons are straightforward, access to The Arcatraz requires either completing the lengthy attunement questline or having a character with high-level lockpicking or engineering to open the door.

161.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted through the Abomination Factory, the sanctum feature specific to the Necrolord Covenant in Shadowlands. Players must upgrade their Abomination Factory to unlock the patterns, which are then crafted at the Stitching Table. The primary reagent for all pieces is Malleable Flesh, a currency earned from the weekly Abomination Factory quest and other activities in Maldraxxus. The specific crafted items are: Visage of the Stitcher (Head), Pauldrons of the Stitcher (Shoulder), Jerkin of the Stitcher (Chest), Leggings of the Stitcher (Legs), Handwraps of the Stitcher (Hands), Boots of the Stitcher (Feet), Girdle of the Stitcher (Waist), and Bracers of the Stitcher (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The farm is not subject to a traditional raid or dungeon lockout but is time-gated by the acquisition of Malleable Flesh and Superior Parts from daily and weekly quests. The entire process is designed to be completed solo. Collecting the full set requires upgrading the Abomination Factory through multiple ranks, which can take several weeks of consistent play to gather the necessary Anima and Redeemed Souls. No tier tokens are involved, and all pieces are crafted from the same source. This is a covenant-specific activity, so the character crafting the set must be a member of the Necrolord Covenant.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate the process, prioritize upgrading the Abomination Factory to Rank 5 to unlock all patterns and quests. Focus on completing the weekly quest "Putting A Plan Together" from Abomination-Attendant Phrobus, as it is the largest source of Malleable Flesh. Additionally, complete the daily quests offered by your constructed abominations, as they also reward the necessary crafting materials. The crafted pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House, but once an appearance is learned, it becomes available account-wide for all characters.

162.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Uncommon (green) quality Bind-on-Equip world drops found in Burning Crusade content. All pieces are random drops from trash mobs inside dungeons intended for levels 60-65, with no specific boss dropping any particular piece. The eight items are the Scouting Turban (Head), Scouting Spaulders (Shoulder), Scouting Tunic (Chest), Scouting Trousers (Legs), Scouting Gloves (Hands), Scouting Boots (Feet), Scouting Belt (Waist), and Scouting Bracers (Wrist). The most efficient farming locations are the dense trash packs within Normal difficulty Hellfire Citadel dungeons like The Blood Furnace or Coilfang Reservoir dungeons like The Slave Pens.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed in Normal difficulty dungeons, which have no lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour per account. The farm is fully soloable at current max level, as all enemies will be defeated in a single hit. Due to the extremely low and random drop chance for specific world drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from 50 to over 200 instance clears. This set does not use a tier token system, as it is not a raid tier set. While pieces can drop from outdoor mobs in zones like Hellfire Peninsula, farming instanced trash is significantly more efficient.

### Farming Tips
Always run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to exit, reset the instance, and re-enter immediately. The optimal farming strategy is to clear all trash mobs up to the first boss in a dungeon like The Blood Furnace, then leave the instance and reset it without killing any bosses. All pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the fastest way to complete the set is to check the Auction House frequently, as other players may sell the pieces they find. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid with Feral Affinity or a Monk, will substantially decrease the time spent on each run.

163.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained from the Kyrian Covenant's Path of Ascension feature in Shadowlands. The appearance is purchased as two separate ensembles from Forgelite Neptira in Elysian Hold, Bastion, using Medallions of Service as currency. The "Ensemble: Steward's Attire" contains the Shoulder and Chest pieces, costs 1,500 Medallions of Service, and requires defeating the bosses Kalisthene and Echthra on Humility difficulty. The "Ensemble: Steward's Regalia" contains the Head, Hands, Waist, and Feet pieces, costs 2,500 Medallions of Service, and requires defeating Myn'ir, Alderyn & Myn'ir, Nuuminuuru, and Craven Corinth on Humility difficulty. Wrists and Legs are not part of this set, as the leg appearance is built into the robe.

### Farming Logistics
The Path of Ascension is a solo-player scenario and has no daily or weekly lockout; attempts are limited only by your ability to craft the required entry keys. The entire feature is easily soloed at current max level. The primary time investment comes from gathering the 4,000 total Medallions of Service, which can take several weeks of completing Bastion world quests and Covenant callings. This farm must be done on a character who is a member of the Kyrian Covenant. Unlocking the ensembles is character-specific, but once learned, the appearance is available account-wide.

### Farming Tips
All required boss victories are on the "Humility" difficulty, which is the easiest setting. To clear the trials faster, focus your Anima on upgrading the Soulbind Pelagos and his associated equipment, as he is the most effective combatant for the encounters. The ensembles are Bind-on-Account, meaning you only need to complete the farm on one Kyrian character to unlock the appearances for all characters on your account. Stockpile the key-crafting materials, such as Elysian Feathers, to ensure you can make attempts whenever needed without pausing to farm reagents.

164.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty cloth armor from Shadowlands dungeons. Each piece drops from a specific dungeon boss. The Head, Shoulder, and Wrist pieces drop from Grand Vizier in The Necrotic Wake. The Chest is from Dealer Xy'exa in De Other Side. The Legs and Feet are from Tred'ova in Mists of Tirna Scithe. Finally, the Hands and Waist drop from Executor Tarvold in Sanguine Depths.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Normal difficulty dungeons, which have no daily or weekly lockout but are subject to the account-wide limit of 10 instances per hour. At the current maximum level, all Shadowlands dungeons are easily soloable by any class. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per item, it may take 10-20 total targeted dungeon runs to collect all eight pieces. This set does not use a token system; loot drops directly for your armor type. Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate appearances and do not share lockouts.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Normal before entering to ensure you are farming the correct appearance. Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to run through the dungeons quickly, skipping all non-essential trash mobs. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. The Great Vault and bonus roll systems are not applicable for farming this specific Normal-difficulty dungeon set.

165.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted through the Leatherworking profession with Classic-era recipes requiring a skill of 205-250. The patterns are obtained from a mix of trainers, vendors, and world drops. The head, shoulders, hands, and feet patterns are sold by Leatherworking supply vendors like Worb Strongstitch in Ironforge and Gigget Zipcoil in Orgrimmar. The wrist and waist recipes are learned directly from profession trainers. The most difficult pieces to obtain are the Chest (Pattern: Nightscape Tunic) and Legs (Pattern: Nightscape Pants), as these patterns are world drops from level 40-55 humanoid mobs. Key reagents for the set include Thick Leather, Shadowcat Hide, and Black Dye.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is acquired via crafting and farming world drops. The entire process of farming the patterns and materials is fully soloable at the current max level. The time required to collect the set depends on the drop rate of the two world drop patterns, which can be very low; purchasing the items or patterns from the Auction House is significantly faster. No tier token system is used for this set. All eight pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), making them available for purchase from other players.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to buy the eight completed armor pieces directly from the Auction House. For players who wish to farm the patterns themselves, running level 40-55 dungeons with high humanoid density, such as Zul'Farrak or Sunken Temple, is the best approach. Alternatively, level a Leatherworker to craft the set, as six of the eight patterns are easily obtainable from vendors and trainers. The required crafting materials are common and can also be farmed or bought from the Auction House for a minimal cost.

166.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is a world drop from the Castle Nathria raid in the Shadowlands expansion. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the instance, and the appearance is the same across all difficulties (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic). The Head piece drops from Shriekwing, and the Shoulder piece drops from Huntsman Altimor. The Chest piece is from Artificer Xy'mox, the Feet are from the Sun King's Salvation encounter, and the Wrist is from the Hungering Destroyer. The remaining pieces are the Hands from Sludgefist, the Waist from the Council of Blood, and the Legs from the final boss, Sire Denathrius.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout, but LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing for four full clears per character each week. The entire raid is easily soloable by a max-level character. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance for a cosmetic item from each boss, the full 8-piece set can take approximately 3-6 weeks to collect if running multiple difficulties. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any class and are Bind-on-Pickup. All pieces for this set drop exclusively within Castle Nathria.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty. You can supplement this by also queuing for the LFR version via the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. There are no significant skips to bypass bosses that drop set pieces. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not systems that can be used to acquire these items from legacy content.

168.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops, primarily found on humanoid mobs between levels 40-50 in Classic zones. The pieces can be acquired by farming these zones or, more commonly, by purchasing them from the Auction House. No piece drops from a specific boss; they are all random zone drops. The full set consists of Dark Hood (Head), Inscribed Leather Spaulders (Shoulder), Inscribed Leather Breastplate (Chest), Inscribed Leather Pants (Legs), Inscribed Leather Gloves (Hands), Inscribed Leather Boots (Feet), Inscribed Leather Belt (Waist), and Inscribed Leather Bracers (Wrist). High-density dungeons such as Zul'Farrak or Maraudon are effective farming locations.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming these pieces in the open world, though dungeons are subject to the 10 instances per hour per account limit. The entire set is easily soloed by any max-level character. Due to the extremely low drop chance and large loot tables of the relevant mobs, targeted farming can take hundreds of runs or many hours with no guarantee of success. This set is for Leather-wearing classes only: Rogue, Druid, Monk, and Demon Hunter. No tier token system is involved, and all pieces are obtained from the same random drop pool.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House. Create a shopping list in the Auction House UI and check periodically for deals, as prices can vary significantly. If you choose to farm the set yourself, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk in a dungeon with high mob density like Zul'Farrak for the fastest clears. Since all items are BoE, you can farm them on any character and mail them to your intended Leather-wearer or sell duplicates on the Auction House.

169.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from pieces dropped in the level 60, 20-player raid Zul'Gurub, located in the northeast corner of Stranglethorn Vale. The main set pieces are obtained by turning in quest items dropped from specific bosses to Vinchaxa on Yojamba Isle. The Head slot is from the Primal Hakkari Kossack, a quest item from Jin'do the Hexxer. The Shoulder slot is from the Primal Hakkari Shawl from High Priestess Mar'li. The Chest slot is from the Primal Hakkari Tunic from Bloodlord Mandokir. The Legs slot is from the Primal Hakkari Leggings from High Priestess Arlokk. The Feet, Bloodtinged Boots, drop from High Priest Venoxis, while the Waist (Voodoo-woven Belt) and Hands (Grips of the Hidden Temple) are zone drops from trash mobs. The Wrist piece requires Exalted reputation with the Zandalar Tribe.

### Farming Logistics
The original Zul'Gurub instance has a weekly raid lockout, resetting with the weekly maintenance. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any class at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance for the boss items, collecting the four main token pieces will likely take 4-6 runs. The token-like quest items are class-specific and cannot be used by other classes; for example, the "Zandalar Madcap's" set is for Rogues, while Druids use the "Zandalar Haruspex's" set from the same tokens. Note that the wrist piece is a quest reward tied to Zandalar Tribe reputation, and the waist and hands are random drops from non-boss enemies within the raid. This raid only exists on one difficulty, so there are no LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic versions to farm for this specific appearance.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a high-mobility class and mount up between boss areas, as much of the instance is mount-friendly. You can speed up the reputation grind for the wrist piece by collecting coins and bijous from mobs inside the instance and turning them in. The waist and hands are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can often be purchased directly from the Auction House, which can save a significant amount of farming time. Since the main armor pieces come from quest turn-ins, you must loot the Primal Hakkari item from the boss and then travel to Yojamba Isle in Stranglethorn Vale to complete the quest and receive the item.

170.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon world drops found within the Wrath of the Lich King 5-player dungeon, Gundrak, located in Zul'Drak. The main armor pieces are looted from the instance's bosses. The Bonechewer Headcover and Leg-Gards drop from the final boss, Gal'darah. The Bonechewer Chestpiece drops from Moorabi, and the Bonechewer Spikeguards are looted from the Drakkari Colossus. Slád'ran drops the Bonechewer Handguards, while the optional boss Eck the Ferocious drops the Bonechewer Shredboots. The matching Bonechewer Pelt-Girdle and Bonechewer Armguards drop randomly from the troll and beast trash mobs throughout the dungeon.

### Farming Logistics
Gundrak can be farmed on Normal difficulty, which has no daily or weekly lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The instance is easily soloed by any character at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces spread across multiple bosses and random trash drops, collecting the full set typically takes 5-15 runs. Wrath of the Lich King dungeons do not use a tier token system, so all pieces are direct drops. Note that the Normal and Heroic difficulties share separate lockouts; Heroic has a daily lockout and is not necessary for this specific set's appearance.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the dungeon on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to reset it immediately after each clear. All pieces from this set are Bind-on-Equip, so check the Auction House frequently to buy missing items or sell duplicates. The fastest clear route is to head left to kill Slád'ran, proceed to Moorabi, then the Drakkari Colossus, before returning to the central area to defeat Eck the Ferocious and finally Gal'darah. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault do not apply to farming legacy dungeons.

171.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from the Shadowlands expansion, specifically from the Zereth Mortis zone. All eight pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are random drops obtained by killing mobs within the zone. The items have a higher chance to drop from Jiro mobs, who are the native inhabitants of the area. There are no specific bosses or quests that award these items; they are acquired purely through randomly-generated loot from enemies. The entire set must be farmed from mobs in this manner.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from standard enemies in an open-world zone. It is fully soloable at current max level, and farming consists of repeatedly killing mobs in a specific area. Due to the very low drop chance of world drops, the number of kills required can be in the thousands, making the time to collect all pieces highly variable. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces drop from the same mob types within Zereth Mortis.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Haal'eshi Garb are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be bought and sold on the Auction House. This is often the fastest way to complete the set, though it may be expensive. If you choose to farm it yourself, find an area with a high density of Jiro mobs, such as the areas surrounding Pilgrim's Grace. Using a class with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage will significantly speed up the process. Farming in a group can also increase the number of kills per hour.

172.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality Bind on Equip items that drop from trash mobs inside level 78-80 Wrath of the Lich King dungeons. The best instances to farm are those with high trash density near the entrance, such as Utgarde Pinnacle, The Culling of Stratholme, or the Icecrown Citadel 5-mans (Forge of Souls, Pit of Saron, Halls of Reflection). Since these are world drops, no specific boss is tied to a specific piece. The complete set consists of Cowl of the Dire Wolf (Head), Spaulders of the Scorned (Shoulder), Tunic of the Penitent (Chest), Leggings of the Sly (Legs), Gloves of the Vicious (Hands), Boots of the Wary (Feet), Belt of the Merciless (Waist), and Bracers of the Untold Tale (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no effective lockout if performed correctly. Set the dungeon difficulty to Normal, which allows you to reset the instance up to 10 times per hour. The entire farm can be easily soloed by any max-level character. Due to the low, random nature of world drops from trash, acquiring the full set can take dozens of instance resets. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces drop from the same pool of dungeon trash mobs. Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same loot table for these items, but Normal is superior for its reset capability.

### Farming Tips
Always check the Auction House before farming, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and can often be purchased directly. The most efficient farm method is to run a dungeon on Normal difficulty, clear the initial trash packs, and then exit and reset the instance. For example, in Utgarde Pinnacle, clear the trash up to the first boss, leave, reset, and repeat. Use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to speed up the process of moving between trash packs. These items cannot be supplemented by bonus rolls or the Great Vault.

174.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from bosses within the Scholomance dungeon. The headpiece, Dementia Cowl, drops from Darkmaster Gandling. Dementia Mantle drops from Ras Frostwhisper, and Dementia Robes drop from Rattlegore. The Dementia Leggings are looted from Baroness Anastari, while the Dementia Gloves drop from Jandice Barov. Dementia Boots are found on Instructor Malicia, and Dementia Bracers are a drop from Kirtonos the Herald. The Dementia Cord is a Bind on Equip world drop that can be found from any trash mob within the Western Plaguelands zone.

### Farming Logistics
Scholomance has no daily or weekly lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire dungeon is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming a 20% drop chance per item, a full set can be collected in approximately 5-10 full clears. This set does not use a token system and pieces are directly looted from their respective bosses. Note that the belt is a BoE world drop from the surrounding zone, not from within the dungeon itself.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeon on a high-mobility class like a Monk or Druid to clear it as quickly as possible. All boss-dropped items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The Dementia Cord, however, is a BoE and can often be purchased to complete the set faster. Ensure you have the Skeleton Key from the dungeon's questline, or a profession that can open locks, to access the final chamber where Darkmaster Gandling resides.

176.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from humanoid and beast mobs in the level 20-30 range across Kalimdor and the Eastern Kingdoms. All pieces, including the Bloodspattered Surcoat, Loincloth, Vizard, and Shoulderpads, share the same random drop chance from these creatures. A frequently recommended farming location is the Wetlands, targeting the high density of Bluegill Murlocs and Angerfang Gnolls. Other viable zones with similar level mobs include the northern Barrens, Stonetalon Mountains, and Ashenvale.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from world mobs that respawn continuously. The entire farm is easily soloable by a max-level character, with most mobs dying in a single hit. Due to the low drop chance of specific uncommon items, collecting the full eight-piece set can take several hours and require killing thousands of mobs. All pieces drop from the same general loot tables in the specified level range. There are no tokens, raid difficulties, or instance lockouts associated with this farm.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip, so the most efficient way to acquire them is often by checking the Auction House first. For farming, use a high-mobility class with instant, ranged area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid in Balance specialization, to clear large packs of mobs quickly. Farming in a small group can increase efficiency by covering more ground and forcing faster mob respawns. Since it is a world drop, there are no skips, lockouts, or specific instance mechanics to consider.

177.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from the Borean Tundra zone in Northrend. All pieces of the set, including the Daggerfen Cowl, Pauldrons, Tunic, Leggings, Gloves, Boots, Belt, and Bracers, are found on the loot tables of murloc-type mobs. The highest concentration of these mobs is in and around Daggerfen Village on the southern coast of the zone. Mobs such as the Daggerfen Muckdweller, Daggerfen Raider, and other nearby murlocs are the primary source for all items in the set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it is based on world drops from respawning mobs. The farm can be completed solo by any class at max level without any difficulty. Due to the low drop chance of specific green items, collecting the full set can take anywhere from one to four hours of continuous farming. The set does not use a token system, and all pieces drop from the same pool of enemies.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Daggerfen Garb are Bind on Equip (BoE), making the Auction House the most efficient way to acquire the set. For those who prefer to farm it directly, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect abilities to clear packs of murlocs quickly. A circular route through Daggerfen Village and along the southern coastline is effective for continuous farming while waiting for mobs to respawn. No special skips, raid difficulties, or bonus rolls apply to this farm.

178.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 51-61 mobs, primarily found in late-game Classic zones. The best zones for farming are the Eastern Plaguelands, Western Plaguelands, Winterspring, and Silithus. Dungeons with high mob density in this level range, such as Stratholme, Scholomance, and Blackrock Depths, are also excellent sources. All pieces of the set drop from the same loot tables within these zones: Sanguine Headband (Head), Sanguine Mantle (Shoulder), Sanguine Robe (Chest), Sanguine Trousers (Legs), Sanguine Gloves (Hands), Sanguine Sandals (Feet), Sanguine Belt (Waist), and Sanguine Cuffs (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to track. The farm can be repeated indefinitely and is easily soloed by any class at current max level. Due to the random nature of world drops, collecting all eight pieces can take anywhere from a few hours to dozens of hours of dedicated farming. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces are obtained from the same pool of enemies. Raid and dungeon difficulty settings do not apply to this farm.

### Farming Tips
Use a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Farming inside dungeons like Stratholme or Blackrock Depths can be more efficient than the open world due to higher mob density. All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so the fastest way to acquire them is often by searching for the individual item names on the Auction House. No bonus rolls, Great Vault rewards, or instance skips are relevant to this farm.

179.content:
### How to Obtain
This appearance is a recolor of the leather Bandit set and is sourced from the "Overlord's Plate" set, a collection of Bind-on-Equip world drops from Classic zones and dungeons. The primary farming locations are high-level dungeons with dense humanoid populations, such as Stratholme, Scholomance, and Blackrock Spire. All pieces, including the Head (Overlord's Helm), Shoulder (Overlord's Spaulders), Chest (Overlord's Chestguard), Legs (Overlord's Legplates), Hands (Overlord's Gauntlets), Feet (Overlord's Greaves), Waist (Overlord's Girdle), and Wrist (Overlord's Vambraces), drop randomly from level 55-60 mobs within these instances and corresponding open-world zones like Winterspring and the Plaguelands. As world drops, no specific boss is tied to any particular piece.

### Farming Logistics
Farming for this set has no lockout if done in the open world, but instances like Stratholme and Scholomance are subject to a daily reset. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character. Due to the extremely low drop chance of specific world drops from a large loot table, it is difficult to estimate the number of runs; however, one might expect to see a piece every 10-15 full dungeon clears. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same general pool of level 55-60 world mobs, so no alternate sources need to be farmed. Classic dungeon difficulties are not a factor, as there is only one version.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for the "Overlord's" pieces on the Auction House, as every item is Bind-on-Equip. For self-farming, the best strategy is to clear dungeons with high humanoid trash density; the living side of Stratholme is a common choice. Use a character with high movement speed and strong area-of-effect abilities to kill mobs as quickly as possible. Since the goal is to kill a high volume of trash mobs, there are no skips or shortcuts to utilize.

182.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained via the Leatherworking profession from the Classic era. The two pieces, Devilsaur Gauntlets (Hands) and Devilsaur Leggings (Legs), are crafted using patterns that are world drops. The primary materials required are Devilsaur Leather, Cured Rugged Hide, and Ironweb Spider Silk. The patterns themselves, "Pattern: Devilsaur Gauntlets" and "Pattern: Devilsaur Leggings," drop from level 50-60 mobs, with the highest drop rates found on humanoid mobs in late-game classic zones like Winterspring and Silithus. The Devilsaur Leather required for crafting is skinned from Devilsaurs found exclusively in Un'Goro Crater.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm as it involves crafting and farming open-world zones. The entire process is easily soloable by any max-level character. The time required depends heavily on the drop rate of the two patterns, which is very low; it is often more efficient to purchase the patterns or the finished items from the Auction House. No tier tokens are involved as this is a crafted set. Both pieces are created through the same profession, though the materials are gathered from different sources across multiple zones.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the completed Devilsaur Gauntlets and Devilsaur Leggings directly from the Auction House, as they are Bind-on-Equip. If you choose to craft it, note that while the patterns originally required Tribal Leatherworking, this specialization requirement has been removed, and any Leatherworker with sufficient Classic-era skill can learn them. To farm the Devilsaur Leather, circle the outer rim of Un'Goro Crater, as the Ironhide and Tyrant Devilsaurs patrol this area. You can also farm the patterns yourself, but their low drop rate makes this a time-consuming alternative to using the Auction House.

184.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the starting gear obtained from the Exile's Reach new player experience. All pieces are rewarded from quests within the zone and the appearance varies based on armor type (Cloth, Leather, Mail, Plate). The Chest (Expeditionary Tunic) is from "Down with the Quilboar" and the Legs (Expeditionary Trousers) are from "Forbidden Quilboar Necromancy." The Hands (Expeditionary Gloves) come from "An Explosive Distraction" while the Feet (Expeditionary Boots) are a reward from "The Harpy's Lair." Finally, the Waist (Expeditionary Sash) is from "WANTED: Gor'groth" and the Wrist (Expeditionary Bracers) is from "Hogger's Hoggers."

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is obtained by completing the one-time questline in Exile's Reach on a new character. The entire process is designed to be completed solo and takes approximately 45-60 minutes from character creation. You only need to complete the zone once on a character of the appropriate armor type to collect all appearances for that version. There is no tier token system, and all pieces come from the same quest chain. This set is also automatically granted to characters created using a Shadowlands-era character boost.

### Farming Tips
To collect all four armor-type variants, you must create and level a new character of each type (Cloth, Leather, Mail, Plate) through Exile's Reach. Choose a class with high mobility, such as a Monk or Demon Hunter, to complete the quests as quickly as possible. Once the quests are completed and the appearances have been added to your Wardrobe, the character can be deleted to free up a character slot. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

185.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon-quality world drops found in Northrend. The primary farming location is Borean Tundra, as the pieces drop most frequently from humanoid and undead mobs between levels 70-73. All items in the set are obtained this way, with no specific boss or container holding any particular piece. The main slots are the Cowl, Shoulders, Robe, Leggings, Gloves, Boots, Cord, and Cuffs of the Borean Tundra.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this set, allowing for continuous farming of Northrend zones. The entire farm can be completed solo by any max-level character, as the mobs pose no threat. Because the pieces are random world drops with a low drop chance, the number of mobs required to complete the set is highly variable and can range from hundreds to many thousands. No tier token system is used for this set, and all pieces are acquired from the same random drop pool.

### Farming Tips
Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), the fastest way to acquire this set is by searching for each piece on the Auction House. For those who prefer to farm it, choose a character with high mobility and area-of-effect damage to clear large groups of mobs in Borean Tundra quickly. Areas with high humanoid density, such as the cultist camps around the Temple City of En'kilah, are efficient farming spots. There are no skips or special mechanics involved in this farm.

187.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Necrolord Covenant cosmetic armor, purchased from the vendor Su Zettai in the Seat of the Primus in Maldraxxus. Acquiring the set requires being a member of the Necrolord Covenant and reaching Renown 60 on any character. The complete 8-piece ensemble, covering Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots, is purchased with a combination of Anima and Grateful Offerings. Individual pieces cost between 500-1500 Anima and 5-20 Grateful Offerings each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming the required currencies; Anima and Grateful Offerings can be acquired continuously. The entire farm is fully soloable at current max level, as it involves completing world quests and daily activities from the Shadowlands expansion. Assuming a focused effort, collecting the necessary currency takes approximately 3-5 days. This set does not use a tier token system. While the main armor pieces are from Su Zettai, a matching cloak and back attachment are purchased from Rendle at the Abomination Factory, requiring Abominable Stitching progress. Raid and dungeon difficulties are not relevant to this farm.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Anima quickly, farm rares and world quests in Zereth Mortis, as the rewards are substantial and can be deposited directly into your covenant reservoir. For Grateful Offerings, prioritize channeling anima to all nodes with the Anima Conductor in the Seat of the Primus each day to unlock the maximum number of daily quests that reward them. Players can now switch Covenants freely by speaking with the Covenant representatives in Oribos, so there is no permanent commitment required to access the Necrolord vendors. Once purchased, the appearance is unlocked account-wide for all characters of the same armor type (Plate, Mail, Leather, or Cloth).

190.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from the vendor Marasmius in Ardenweald (59.7, 32.5). To access this vendor, a character must be a member of the Night Fae Covenant and use the Anima Conductor to channel anima to Tirna Scithe. The full eight-piece set costs a total of 25,000 Reservoir Anima and 250 Grateful Offerings. Grateful Offerings are primarily obtained by completing daily Covenant Calling quests and the daily quest associated with your Anima Conductor channel. All main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are purchased directly from this vendor.

### Farming Logistics
This set is acquired through currency farming and has no daily or weekly lockout. The entire process is soloable at current max level. The primary currencies are Reservoir Anima and Grateful Offerings, with the latter being the significant time gate. Acquiring the required 250 Grateful Offerings will take several weeks of consistent play, as they are rewarded in small amounts from Night Fae Callings (3-5 per emissary chest) and Anima Conductor dailies. There is no tier token system, and all pieces are purchased directly from a single source.

### Farming Tips
Focus on completing your three daily Covenant Calling quests to maximize Grateful Offering acquisition, as these are the most reliable source. While you must be a member of the Night Fae Covenant on the character purchasing the set, the cosmetic appearance becomes account-wide once learned, allowing characters of any covenant or class to use it. The items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Reservoir Anima is no longer a limiting factor and can be farmed quickly in Zereth Mortis or other Shadowlands zones.

192.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier leather armor recolor from the 10-player version of the Ulduar raid, which is the Tier 8 instance of Wrath of the Lich King. The eight primary pieces drop directly from specific bosses within the instance. The Head slot piece drops from Thorim, the Shoulder from Yogg-Saron, and the Chest from Hodir. The Legs drop from Kologarn, Hands from Auriaya, Feet from General Vezax, Waist from XT-002 Deconstructor, and Wrists from Freya's Gift after defeating Freya.

### Farming Logistics
Ulduar operates on a weekly raid lockout, which is shared between the 10-player and 25-player difficulties. The instance can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level with trivial difficulty. Assuming an average drop chance for each piece, collecting the full 8-piece set will take approximately 4-8 weekly resets. This set does not use the tier token system; the pieces are direct boss drops, so you do not need to compete with other classes for a Vanquisher token. All pieces of this specific blue and grey colored set drop from within the 10-player version of the raid.

### Farming Tips
To acquire this specific color variation, ensure your Legacy Raid Size is set to "10 Player" before entering the instance. For the fastest clears, ignore all optional hard mode mechanics as they do not affect the drop of these specific items. After clearing a section of the raid, use the central teleporters in the Antechamber of Ulduar to quickly navigate between the different wings. The initial vehicle encounter with Flame Leviathan can be skipped by destroying the boss on foot before it engages.

194.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon-quality lookalike armor pieces that drop from bosses in the three Icecrown Citadel 5-man dungeons on Heroic difficulty: Forge of Souls, Pit of Saron, and Halls of Reflection. The plate version is the most recognized, but lookalikes for all armor types (Cloth, Leather, Mail, Plate) drop from the same sources. The plate items are: Head (Scourgeborne Warhelm) from Bronjahm in Forge of Souls; Shoulder (Pauldrons of Lost Hope) from Forgemaster Garfrost in Pit of Saron; and Chest (Weeping-Wound Chestguard) from Ick in Pit of Saron. The Legs (Legplates of Frozen Granite) drop from the Falric/Marwyn encounter in Halls of Reflection, while the Feet (Greaves of the Slaughter) drop from Scourgelord Tyrannus in Pit of Saron. Hands (Gauntlets of the Stouthearted Crusader) and Waist (Belt of the Lonely Noble) drop from the Captain's Chest after the Falric/Marwyn fight, and the Wrist (Bracers of Dark Blessings) drops from Bronjahm.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons are on a daily Heroic dungeon reset, meaning you can complete them once per day per character for a chance at the loot. The instances are trivially soloable by any class at the current maximum level. There is no tier token system; each piece of gear drops directly from a boss. Assuming a 20% drop chance per item, it may take an average of 5-15 daily lockouts to collect the full set, depending on luck. All pieces drop from within these three connected dungeons. The dungeons only have Normal and Heroic difficulty; the specific recolor pieces drop from Heroic.

### Farming Tips
You must run the dungeons on Heroic difficulty for the items to drop. The three instances form a linear progression and must be completed in order: Forge of Souls, then Pit of Saron, then Halls of Reflection. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The lengthy, unskippable roleplay event during the final waves of the Halls of Reflection instance is the primary time consumer of this farm. Modern loot systems such as bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to this legacy content.

195.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a collection of non-tier items that drop from the 10-player Normal difficulty of the Trial of the Crusader raid, the Tier 9 instance from Wrath of the Lich King. The head, waist, and wrist pieces are found in the Tribute Chest after defeating the Northrend Beasts encounter. The chest and feet drop from the Faction Champions encounter. Lord Jaraxxus drops both the legs and hands. No pieces from this specific set drop from Anub'arak, the final boss.

*   **Head:** Helm of the Silver-Blade (Gormok the Impaler)
*   **Shoulder:** Shoulderplates of the Eternal (Icehowl)
*   **Chest:** Breastplate of the Imperial Joust (Faction Champions)
*   **Legs:** Legplates of the Unthinking Strife (Lord Jaraxxus)
*   **Hands:** Gauntlets of the Stouthearted (Lord Jaraxxus)
*   **Feet:** Sabatons of the Courageous (Faction Champions)
*   **Waist:** Belt of the Chivalrous (Gormok the Impaler)
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of the Unholy Knight (Icehowl)

### Farming Logistics
Trial of the Crusader operates on a weekly raid lockout. The entire instance can be soloed easily at current max level. Because 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a single lockout, and Normal and Heroic difficulties also share a single lockout, you can only clear this raid once per character per week. You must set the raid difficulty to 10-player and Normal mode *before* entering the instance. This set is composed of direct boss drops and does not use a tier token system. Assuming a standard drop rate, collecting all eight pieces will likely take 4-8 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your raid settings are configured to 10-player Normal difficulty before zoning in, as this cannot be changed once inside for this specific raid. The raid takes place in a single arena, so there are no skips or shortcuts; simply defeat the bosses as they are announced. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Farming on a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) is most efficient, as they can loot and learn the appearances directly.

198.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is crafted using the Protoform Synthesis system in Zereth Mortis. To craft the full set, you must first find the "Schematic: Nimble-Knife Vestments," which is looted from the Stolen Protoform Synthesis Component treasure chest located at coordinates /way 51.5 22.8 in Zereth Mortis. Crafting each piece requires a specific amount of Genesis Motes, which drop from most non-automa creatures in the zone, and one Unformed Latticework per piece. The Unformed Latticework is a guaranteed drop from Mawsworn azerite-themed rares in Zereth Mortis, such as General Zarathura. The eight main pieces of the set are all crafted at the Protoform Synthesis forge in the southern part of the zone.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm as it is based on crafting materials from an open-world zone. The entire process is fully soloable at maximum level. Obtaining the set requires unlocking the Protoform Synthesis forge by completing the "Protoform Synthesis" quest, which becomes available during Chapter 5 of the Zereth Mortis campaign. The schematic is a one-time loot, while the Genesis Motes and Unformed Latticeworks must be farmed until you have enough for all eight pieces. Assuming average luck with lattice drops from rares, the entire set can be farmed in a few hours of dedicated play.

### Farming Tips
To farm Genesis Motes efficiently, find an area with high non-automa mob density, such as the Vombata and Devourer-filled caves in the western part of Zereth Mortis. For the Unformed Latticeworks, join or create groups to farm the specific Mawsworn rares as they spawn, as this is faster than waiting for them to respawn on your own. None of the components, including the schematic, lattices, or crafted cosmetic pieces, are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Ensure you have completed the prerequisite questline, as the forge and schematic will not be accessible otherwise.

205.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon quality (green) items that drop from bosses within various Burning Crusade dungeons on both Normal and Heroic difficulty. The primary sources are the dungeons located within Hellfire Citadel, Coilfang Reservoir, and Auchindoun. No crafting or vendors are involved in obtaining this specific recolor. The specific drop locations for each armor piece are as follows:

*   **Head:** Unyielding Helm drops from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in The Shattered Halls.
*   **Shoulder:** Unyielding Pauldrons drops from Grandmaster Vorpil in Shadow Labyrinth.
*   **Chest:** Unyielding Tunic drops from Murmur in Shadow Labyrinth.
*   **Wrist:** Unyielding Bracers drops from Broggok in The Blood Furnace.
*   **Hands:** Unyielding Gauntlets drops from Swamplord Musel'ek in The Underbog.
*   **Waist:** Unyielding Girdle drops from Hungarfen in The Underbog.
*   **Legs:** Unyielding Legguards drops from The Maker in The Blood Furnace.
*   **Feet:** Unyielding Boots drops from Quagmirran in The Slave Pens.

### Farming Logistics
The set can be farmed on Normal difficulty, which has no daily or weekly lockout but is subject to the account-wide limit of 10 instance resets per hour. Heroic difficulty versions of these dungeons have a daily lockout. The entire set can be easily soloed at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per boss, a player can expect to complete the set within 5-15 total runs of the relevant dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system and the pieces drop directly for the player. Normal and Heroic difficulties do not have separate loot tables for these specific items, making Normal the more efficient farming method.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, farm this set on Normal difficulty to bypass the daily Heroic lockout. Create a farming route starting in Hellfire Peninsula for The Blood Furnace and The Shattered Halls, then fly to Zangarmarsh for The Underbog and The Slave Pens, and finish in Terokkar Forest for Shadow Labyrinth. After clearing the relevant bosses, exit the dungeon, reset all instances via your character portrait, and repeat up to the hourly limit. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No special skips are required as a max-level character can clear to each boss in minutes.

206.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a Classic world drop, and its pieces are most commonly known as the "Glimmering Mail" set. The items are Bind on Equip and drop from humanoid, giant, and dragonkin mobs, primarily in the level 35-45 range. The best zones for farming are Arathi Highlands, Dustwallow Marsh, the southern portion of Stranglethorn Vale, and the Badlands. All pieces, including the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist, are random drops from mobs within these zones and do not come from specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it occurs in open-world zones and can be repeated indefinitely. The set is fully soloable by any max-level character, as the mobs are low-level. Because it is a random world drop with a very low drop chance, collecting all pieces through farming can take anywhere from a few hours to dozens of hours of mob grinding. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are sourced from the same world drop loot tables. As the pieces are Bind on Equip, they can be farmed by any class and then mailed to the appropriate character.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for the "Glimmering Mail" pieces on the Auction House, as all items are BoE. If you choose to farm it yourself, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities to clear large packs of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high humanoid density, such as Stromgarde Keep in Arathi Highlands or the Gurubashi troll ruins in Stranglethorn Vale. Since the items are uncommon quality world drops, they will not be supplemented by systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault.

207.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon mail armor pieces that drop from various bosses within Burning Crusade dungeons on both Normal and Heroic difficulty. The Head (Unyielding Helm) and Wrist (Unyielding Bracers) drop from The Black Stalker and Hungarfen in The Underbog. The Shoulders (Unyielding Pauldrons) drop from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in The Shattered Halls. The Chest (Unyielding Tunic) is found on Murmur in the Shadow Labyrinth, and the Hands (Unyielding Gauntlets) drop from Warlord Kalithresh in The Steamvault. Finally, the Waist (Unyielding Cinch) and Legs (Unyielding Greaves) drop from Broggok and The Maker in The Blood Furnace, while the Feet (Unyielding Boots) drop from Exarch Maladaar in Auchenai Crypts.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing this set have a daily lockout on Heroic difficulty, but can be reset up to 10 times per hour on Normal difficulty. All instances are fully soloable at the current maximum level with no special mechanics to consider. Due to the number of pieces spread across six different dungeons, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 10-20 total instance runs, assuming an average drop chance. This set does not use a tier token system, as all pieces are direct boss drops. Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same loot table for these items, so farming on Normal is the most efficient method.

### Farming Tips
Set all relevant dungeons to Normal difficulty to bypass the daily Heroic lockout, allowing for repeated runs in the same day. A character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, can significantly reduce the time spent running through each dungeon to reach the specific bosses. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House. No complex skips are necessary, as a max-level character can run directly to each boss, ignoring all other enemies along the way.

208.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a Bind-on-Equip world drop from mobs primarily located in Hellfire Peninsula. As a BoE set, all pieces can be purchased from the Auction House. The set is composed of the following items: Felstone Mail Helm (Head), Felstone Mail Pauldrons (Shoulder), Felstone Mail Vest (Chest), Felstone Mail Legguards (Legs), Felstone Mail Gauntlets (Hands), Felstone Mail Boots (Feet), Felstone Mail Belt (Waist), and Felstone Mail Bracers (Wrist). These items drop from creatures in the level 58-63 range, making the various fel orc and demon encampments throughout the zone the most common sources.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this farm; mobs in the open world can be killed repeatedly. The farm can be fully soloed at current max level, as all enemies will be trivial. The time required to collect all pieces via farming is highly variable due to random drop chances and could take anywhere from a few hours to over 50 hours. This set does not use a tier token system, and no pieces come from a different source. All items are from the same general world drop loot table.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for obtaining this set is to check the Auction House regularly, as buying the pieces is almost always faster than farming them directly. If you choose to farm, use a character with high mobility and strong instant-cast AoE abilities, such as a Druid in Balance specialization, to quickly clear large groups of enemies. High-density mob locations like the fel orc camps surrounding Hellfire Citadel are ideal for maximizing kills per hour. Since the items are BoE, you can farm them on any character and mail them to your main.

210.content:
### How to Obtain
This is a mail armor set comprised of Bind-on-Equip world drops from Burning Crusade content. All pieces drop randomly from level 60-70 mobs found within Outland zones and dungeons. While any Outland mob can potentially drop a piece, the most efficient sources are dungeons with high trash mob density. The optimal instances for farming this set are The Botanica and The Mechanar in Tempest Keep, or Hellfire Ramparts in Hellfire Peninsula, all run on Normal difficulty.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set has no daily or weekly lockout when done in Normal difficulty dungeons, which can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The set can be fully soloed at max level by a mail-wearing class (Hunter or Shaman) to learn the appearances. Due to the extremely low drop chance for specific world drops, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can take hundreds of instance runs. There is no tier token system, and all pieces come from the same general Outland loot table.

### Farming Tips
The most practical method to complete this set is to check the Auction House regularly, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and can be purchased directly. For self-farming, use a character with high speed and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk. Run Normal difficulty instances, pull as many trash mobs as possible in large groups, and ignore the bosses to save time. After clearing the trash, exit and reset the dungeon to repeat the process.

212.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 62-65 mobs, primarily located in Zangarmarsh. The most effective farming locations are the naga-infested areas surrounding Coilfang Reservoir, including Serpent Lake, The Lagoon, and the northern coastline.
