The Shoulders drop from Warbringer O'mrogg in The Shattered Halls. The Chest drops from Mekgineer Steamrigger in The Steamvault. The Legs drop from The Black Stalker in The Underbog, and both the Hands and Waist drop from bosses in Old Hillsbrad Foothills, Captain Skarloc and Lieutenant Drake respectively. The matching Feet and Wrists are primarily Bind on Equip world drops that can be found in the same dungeons or on the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons can be farmed on Normal difficulty, which has no lockout and can be reset up to 10 times per hour per account. Running on Heroic difficulty is not necessary for these items and imposes a daily lockout. The entire set is easily soloable by a single max-level character. Collection time varies greatly, as the pieces drop from six different bosses across four separate dungeons; expect to run each specific instance approximately 4-7 times to acquire its piece, assuming a 15-25% drop rate. This set does not use a token system and all pieces drop directly as equipment.

### Farming Tips
Always run the dungeons on Normal difficulty to enable unlimited farming without a lockout. To minimize travel time, use the portal to the Caverns of Time in your main city's portal room to access Old Hillsbrad and The Black Morass, then fly to the other instances in Hellfire Peninsula and Zangarmarsh. A character with high speed and mobility, like a Druid or Demon Hunter, will significantly reduce the clear time for each run. Check the Auction House regularly for the matching Bind on Equip pieces, as purchasing them can complete the set much faster than farming for random drops.

265.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from level 46-55 mobs in Classic-era zones. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE). The best zones for farming include Winterspring, Western Plaguelands, and Eastern Plaguelands due to their high density of mobs in the appropriate level range. Dungeons such as Blackrock Depths, Scholomance, and Stratholme are also effective sources. The specific pieces are the Magnificent Mail Helm, Pauldrons, Breastplate, Leggings, Gauntlets, Sabatons, Girdle, and Bracers, all of which drop from the same pool of enemies.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it relies on killing respawning mobs in the open world or resetting non-heroic dungeons. The set can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Collection time is highly variable and can range from a few hours to many weeks due to the extremely low, random drop chance from a large loot table. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same sources, though checking the Auction House is the most reliable acquisition method.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip. If you choose to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Hunter, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high mob density, like the undead-filled fields of Western Plaguelands or the caves in Winterspring. No skips or special mechanics are required for this type of farm.

268.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind-on-Equip world drops from mobs in The Burning Crusade zones, primarily those between levels 62 and 70. The best zones for farming are Hellfire Peninsula, Terokkar Forest, and Nagrand due to high mob density. The head slot is the Masterwork Helm, shoulders are Masterwork Pauldrons, and the chest is the Masterwork Breastplate. The remaining pieces are Masterwork Legplates, Masterwork Gauntlets (Hands), Masterwork Sabatons (Feet), Masterwork Girdle (Waist), and Masterwork Bracers (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop farm, there is no lockout or instance reset to track. The entire set can be fully soloed by any max-level character. The time required to collect all pieces through farming is extremely variable due to random drop chances and can take many hours. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general world loot table. All items are Bind-on-Equip, so they can be obtained by any class and then mailed to your mail-wearing character or sold on the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to purchase the individual "Masterwork" pieces from the Auction House. For those who prefer to farm, focus on areas with high mob density and fast respawn rates, such as the Bleeding Hollow orcs southeast of Zeth'Gor in Hellfire Peninsula. Use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage to clear mobs quickly. While any class can farm these BoE items, they can only be added to the wardrobe and used for transmogrification by mail-wearing classes: Hunter, Shaman, and Evoker.

269.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from high-level zones and dungeons within the Burning Crusade expansion. The items drop randomly from mobs level 67 and higher, with no specific boss assigned to any piece. The most effective farming locations are high-density dungeons such as The Botanica, The Mechanar, and The Arcatraz. Key zones for open-world farming include Netherstorm, Shadowmoon Valley, and the Isle of Quel'Danas. The eight pieces are: Mercurial Circlet (Head), Mercurial Pauldrons (Shoulder), Mercurial Mail (Chest), Mercurial Legguards (Legs), Mercurial Gauntlets (Hands), Mercurial Sabatons (Feet), Mercurial Girdle (Waist), and Mercurial Vambraces (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
Dungeon farming is subject to the 10 instances per hour, per account limit; open-world farming has no lockout. The entire farm can be easily soloed by any class at current maximum level. Due to the random nature of world drops, the number of runs required is highly variable and can range from a few dozen dungeon clears to hundreds. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same mob pool, and Normal difficulty dungeons are repeatable, while Heroic difficulty has a daily lockout, making Normal the optimal choice.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to run a high-density, linear dungeon like The Botanica on Normal difficulty, clearing all trash, exiting, resetting, and repeating. All pieces in this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), making the Auction House the fastest and often most reliable way to obtain the full set. Using a high-mobility character, such as a Druid with Feral Affinity or a Monk, will significantly reduce the time per dungeon clear. If you hit the hourly instance cap, you can switch to farming mobs in the open world, such as at the Mana-Forges in Netherstorm, until your lockouts reset.

270.content:
### How to Obtain
This set, known as the "Glimmering Mail" set, is a Bind-on-Equip world drop from Classic-era zones. All pieces are obtained as random drops from mobs primarily in the level 30-40 range. The most effective farming zones are Arathi Highlands, Desolace, the northern half of Stranglethorn Vale, and Thousand Needles. Dungeons with high mob density in this level range, such as Razorfen Kraul, Uldaman, and the Library and Armory wings of Scarlet Monastery, are also excellent sources. Each piece of the set (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) drops randomly from any eligible mob within this level bracket, with no specific boss assignments.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout; mobs in outdoor zones respawn continuously, and dungeons can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The farm is fully soloable at current max level, as all enemies will be trivial to defeat. The number of runs required to collect the full set is entirely dependent on random drop chance and cannot be reliably estimated. This set does not use a tier token system, and concepts such as separate lockouts for different difficulties (e.g., Normal/Heroic) are not applicable to this type of world drop content.

### Farming Tips
As all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), the most direct way to complete the set is to purchase the items from the Auction House. For self-farming, the most efficient method is to clear high-density dungeons like Razorfen Kraul repeatedly on a character with high movement speed. Any mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker) must be used to learn the appearances upon equipping the items. A Hunter is particularly effective for this farm due to their mobility and ranged attacks, which allow for pulling large groups of mobs at once.

271.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind-on-Equip world drops from level 77-80 mobs in the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The pieces drop most frequently in late-game zones with high mob density, such as Icecrown, The Storm Peaks, and Sholazar Basin. All main slots are obtained as random drops from creatures in these zones: Head (Unyielding Helm), Shoulder (Unyielding Pauldrons), Chest (Unyielding Tunic), Legs (Unyielding Legguards), Hands (Unyielding Gauntlets), Feet (Unyielding Sabatons), Waist (Unyielding Girdle), and Wrist (Unyielding Bracers). No pieces are tied to specific bosses, dungeons, or vendors.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to track. The farm can be done continuously and is easily soloed at current max level. The time required to collect the full set is entirely dependent on random drop chance and can range from a few hours to dozens of hours of dedicated farming. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same general pool of Northrend mobs, and instance difficulties like Normal or Heroic are not applicable to this farm.

### Farming Tips
The fastest and most reliable way to obtain this set is to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House, as they are all Bind-on-Equip. If you choose to farm it yourself, use a class with high mobility and strong AoE abilities to clear large packs of enemies efficiently. A popular farming location is the southeastern corner of Icecrown, targeting the Converted Hero mobs near the Argent Tournament Grounds. Any items you find can be mailed to the appropriate character or sold on the Auction House.

272.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from world drops, treasures, and quests within the Zereth Mortis zone. All pieces of the set are part of the general loot table for the zone and are not tied to specific bosses or dungeons. Key sources include one-time treasures scattered across the zone, drops from the various rare elites, and rewards for completing the main "Secrets of the First Ones" campaign. The main slots are the Felstone Headdress (Head), Felstone Spaulders (Shoulder), Felstone Tunic (Chest), Felstone Greaves (Legs), Felstone Gauntlets (Hands), Felstone Stompers (Feet), Felstone Girdle (Waist), and Felstone Vambraces (Wrist), all acquired through these zone-wide activities.

### Farming Logistics
There is no weekly or daily lockout for farming this set, as it comes from an open-world zone and repeatable mobs. The entire farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Rare elites, a primary source of drops, have a daily loot lockout for their unique items, but can still be farmed for cosmetic drops. Since the acquisition is based on random drops, it may take several hours of dedicated farming to collect all pieces. This set does not use a tier token system and there are no class restrictions on looting the items. All pieces originate from the same activities within Zereth Mortis.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire the set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can be purchased directly from other players. For self-farming, begin by completing the main Zereth Mortis quest campaign and looting all of the zone's one-time treasures to get a baseline collection. Use a zone map addon like HandyNotes to efficiently locate and kill all available rare elites each day. There are no skips or special mechanics involved, and the farm is entirely based on time investment and random luck.

273.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set mail pieces found in various level 70-80 dungeons from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The Head drops from Prince Keleseth in Utgarde Keep. The Shoulders drop from Elder Nadox in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom. The Chest is looted from Eck the Ferocious in Gundrak. The Legs drop from Herald Volazj in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom. The Hands are obtained from Meathook in The Culling of Stratholme, while the Feet drop from Sjonnir The Ironshaper in Halls of Stone. The Waist is looted from The Prophet Tharon'ja in Drak'Tharon Keep and the Wrists drop from Salramm the Fleshcrafter in The Culling of Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items are subject to a daily lockout on Heroic difficulty, but can be reset up to 10 times per hour on Normal difficulty. All listed dungeons and bosses can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 20%, it may take approximately 5-10 runs of each specific dungeon to acquire the desired piece, totaling around 30-50 instance clears for the full set. This set does not use a tier token system, as each piece drops directly from a boss. Since the loot tables for these items are shared between Normal and Heroic difficulty, farming on Normal is far more efficient.

### Farming Tips
For the most efficient farming, run all dungeons on Normal difficulty, which allows you to exit, reset the instance, and re-enter immediately. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, will significantly decrease the time spent running through the instances. Since all trash mobs can be skipped, you can run directly to the specific bosses that drop your desired pieces. Some pieces may have bind-on-equip lookalikes that can be purchased from the Auction House, so it is worth searching for them by appearance to potentially save time.

274.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Stitched Felstone" armor, a world drop from the Shadowlands expansion zone of Maldraxxus. All eight pieces are Bind-on-Equip and can drop from any creature or treasure container within the zone. The items are Stitched Felstone Helmet (Head), Stitched Felstone Pauldrons (Shoulder), Stitched Felstone Hauberk (Chest), Stitched Felstone Greaves (Legs), Stitched Felstone Gauntlets (Hands), Stitched Felstone Sabatons (Feet), Stitched Felstone Belt (Waist), and Stitched Felstone Bracers (Wrist). There are no specific bosses tied to individual pieces; the entire set is part of the zone's global loot table, with rare elites and treasures providing a higher drop chance than standard mobs.

### Farming Logistics
This farm has no lockout and can be repeated continuously. The set can be fully soloed by any class at the current maximum level, as Shadowlands-era creatures pose no threat. Due to the random nature of world drops, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can range from a few hours to dozens of hours of dedicated farming. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces drop from the same sources, so no alternative farming locations are necessary to complete the set.

### Farming Tips
Because all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), the most efficient way to acquire this set is often by purchasing it from the Auction House. Check the Auction House regularly for individual pieces, as this can save a significant amount of farming time. If you choose to farm it yourself, use a high-mobility character to quickly move between rare spawn points and treasure locations in Maldraxxus. Addons like HandyNotes can display the locations of all rares and treasures on your map to create an efficient farming route.

275.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind on Equip world drops found throughout Burning Crusade content, with the highest concentration of drops from creatures in Nagrand. The eight pieces of the set are the Clefthoof Hide Mask, Clefthoof Hide Shoulderpads, Clefthoof Hide Vest, Clefthoof Hide Leggings, Clefthoof Hide Gloves, Clefthoof Hide Boots, Clefthoof Hide Belt, and Clefthoof Hide Bracers. There are no specific bosses that drop these items; they are part of the global loot table for level 65-70 creatures. The most effective way to farm these pieces is by killing the dense populations of Clefthoof and Talbuk near the Throne of the Elements in Nagrand. Alternatively, all pieces can be purchased from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to consider, allowing for continuous farming. The set is fully soloable at maximum level, as the enemies in Nagrand pose no threat. Due to the low, random nature of world drop chances, obtaining the full set through farming could take anywhere from a few hours to dozens of hours, depending on luck. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same general loot tables, though the most reliable alternative source is the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to regularly check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and are frequently listed by other players. For those who prefer to farm it directly, use a class with high mobility and strong instant-cast area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high mob density to maximize kills per hour. Since the pieces are not soulbound on pickup, they can be farmed on any character and mailed to the character that needs the appearance.

276.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a Bind on Equip world drop, primarily found on mobs and in chests within level 40-50 zones and dungeons from the original game. The most effective farming locations are dungeons with a high density of mobs, such as Uldaman and Zul'Farrak. All pieces, including the Fenclaw Helm, Pauldrons, Robe, Leggings, Gloves, and Boots, drop randomly from any non-elite or elite mob within these instances. There are no specific bosses that have a higher chance to drop any particular piece; the entire set is part of a global loot table for that level range.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed by repeatedly clearing dungeons, which are subject to the 10-instances-per-hour account-wide lockout, not a daily or weekly lockout. It can be fully and easily soloed at current max level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drops, acquiring the full set through farming could take hundreds of runs. This set does not use a tier token system, and the pieces do not drop from raids, so different difficulty lockouts like Normal or Heroic do not apply. All pieces drop from the same pool of random mobs.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House, as direct farming is unreliable. If you choose to farm, use a high-mobility character to clear the trash mobs in Uldaman or Zul'Farrak up to the instance limit, then reset and repeat. Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), they can be farmed on any class and either mailed to the correct character or sold. There are no skips or special mechanics for this farm; the goal is simply to kill as many mobs in the appropriate level range as quickly as possible.

277.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of world drops from the Classic expansion, primarily found on creatures between levels 35 and 45. All pieces, including the Marshcreeper Cap, Pauldrons, Tunic, Leggings, Gloves, Boots, Belt, and Bracers, are random drops from mobs in this level range. The most thematically and efficiently farmed zones are Swamp of Sorrows and Dustwallow Marsh due to their high density of appropriate-level beasts and humanoids. Other viable zones include Desolace and Arathi Highlands. There are no specific bosses or guaranteed sources for any of the items.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance to reset, allowing for continuous farming. The entire farm is easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. The time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and depends entirely on random drop chance; it could take a few hours or dozens of hours. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces come from the same general source of level 35-45 world mobs. There are no different difficulties or lockouts to consider for this farm.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Marshcreeper Mail set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so the most efficient way to acquire the set is to first check the Auction House. Use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to kill large groups of enemies quickly and cover more ground. Farming in a group of five can increase the overall number of kills per hour, increasing your chances of a drop. Focus on areas with high mob density and fast respawn timers within the target zones to maximize farming efficiency.

278.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from The Burning Crusade zones of Eversong Woods and Ghostlands. All eight pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are random drops from humanoid and undead mobs between levels 1 and 20 within these two zones. No specific boss or location drops a particular piece; the entire set is found by killing common enemies throughout the areas. Some pieces may also be offered as quest rewards while leveling through these zones, but the only repeatable farming method is killing mobs.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from world mobs that respawn continuously. It can be fully soloed by a max-level character with trivial ease. Due to the extremely low and random drop chance of world drops, the number of hours required to collect all pieces is highly variable and could range from a few to dozens of hours. The set does not use a tier token system, and since it is not from a raid or dungeon, there are no difficulty settings like LFR, Normal, or Heroic to consider.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by purchasing the Bind on Equip (BoE) pieces from the Auction House. For farming, use a max-level character with high mobility and instant area-of-effect abilities to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on high-density areas, such as the troll camps in central Ghostlands or the Scourge-infested areas in the southern part of the zone. There are no skips, bonus rolls, or instance-specific mechanics relevant to this farm.

279.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is an Uncommon quality Mail world drop from The Burning Crusade expansion, available to all classes to farm but only wearable by Mail users. The pieces are found on level 65-70+ mobs in Outland zones, with the highest drop rates concentrated in Terokkar Forest, Netherstorm, and Blade's Edge Mountains. As world drops, no specific boss or location guarantees an item, and they instead drop randomly from a wide variety of enemies. The individual items are the Imbued Nether-weave Tunic (Chest), Imbued Nether-weave Pants (Legs), Imbued Nether-weave Shoulders, Imbued Nether-weave Gloves, Imbued Nether-weave Boots, Imbued Nether-weave Belt, and Imbued Nether-weave Bracers.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from open-world mobs and thus has no lockout; mobs can be killed continuously. The farm is easily soloable at maximum level by any class, especially those with strong area-of-effect damage. Due to the low drop chance of specific world BoE items, completing the set can take anywhere from hundreds to many thousands of mob kills. This is not a tier set, so it does not use a token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot pool.

### Farming Tips
All pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), making the Auction House the most reliable and fastest method to obtain the set. For self-farming, use a class with high mobility and instant AoE damage, such as a Druid or Hunter, to maximize efficiency. Target high-density mob areas like the arakkoa camps in Skettis (Terokkar Forest) or the Sunfury blood elf encampments in Netherstorm. Farming in a 2-5 player group can drastically increase kills per hour, yielding more opportunities for drops.

280.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of mail armor pieces that drop from bosses within the 10-player difficulty of the Ulduar raid, the Tier 8 instance from Wrath of the Lich King. The headpiece drops from Mimiron, and the shoulderpiece drops from Thorim. The chestpiece is looted from Yogg-Saron, the legs drop from Freya, and the hands come from General Vezax. The final pieces are the feet from Flame Leviathan, the waist from XT-002 Deconstructor, and the wrist from Kologarn.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed within Ulduar, which has a weekly raid lockout per character. You must set the instance to 10-player difficulty by speaking to the Lore Keeper of Norgannon at the entrance before you begin. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. This set does not use a tier token system; each piece is a direct boss drop that must be looted individually. Given the weekly lockout and the number of items, collecting the full set will likely take between 5 to 15 weeks, depending on drop luck.

### Farming Tips
To speed up the farm, set the raid difficulty to 10-player by speaking to the NPC at the instance entrance. Use the teleporters that become active after clearing the antechamber and the keepers' wings to move through the instance quickly. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Farming on a mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker) is required to learn the appearances for your collection.

282.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Stitchflesh's Misplaced" collection, a world drop from the Shadowlands zone of Maldraxxus. All pieces drop randomly from any non-elite mob within the zone. There are no specific bosses tied to individual armor slots. A commonly farmed location is the House of Constructs in the northwestern part of the zone due to its high mob density. The full set consists of the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout; mobs can be farmed continuously. The entire farm is easily soloable at the current maximum level. Due to the low drop chance of world BoE items, acquiring the full set through farming could take many hours, with completion time being entirely dependent on random luck. This is not a tier set, so there are no class tokens to collect. All pieces drop from the same source, and there are no alternate difficulties or lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House first, as every piece of this set is Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and can be purchased from other players. To farm it directly, form a 5-player group to significantly increase the number of mobs killed per hour. Use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage to clear packs of enemies efficiently. The items will be added to your account-wide wardrobe upon being equipped.

283.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality Bind on Equip items that drop from level 58-63 creatures throughout Outland. The primary farming zone is Hellfire Peninsula, which contains the highest density of mobs in the correct level range. The set consists of the Burnished Mail Helm, Pauldrons, Tunic, Leggings, Gloves, Boots, Belt, and Bracers. All pieces are random world drops and are not sourced from any specific boss, dungeon, or quest.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is farmed from open-world mobs that respawn continuously. The farm is fully soloable at the current maximum level by any class. Due to the extremely low drop chance for any specific piece, collecting the full set via farming is highly dependent on luck and can take dozens of hours. There is no tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general world loot table.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), making the Auction House the most efficient and recommended method for acquisition. If farming directly, target areas with high mob density in Hellfire Peninsula, such as the various demon encampments or the path from the Dark Portal. A character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage will significantly increase the number of mobs killed per hour. No other systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault can be used to acquire these items.

285.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from The Burning Crusade expansion. All pieces are obtained randomly from killing level 58-65 mobs and looting treasure chests throughout Outland. The most efficient farming zones are Hellfire Peninsula, Zangarmarsh, and Terokkar Forest due to their high density of appropriate-level creatures. The set consists of the Talhide Helm, Spaulders, Tunic, Leggings, Gloves, Boots, Belt, and Bracers, all of which drop from the same general sources.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it occurs in open-world zones and can be repeated indefinitely. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character. Collection time is highly variable due to the random nature of world drops, potentially taking many hours of continuous farming. This set does not use a token system and can only be added to the wardrobe by Mail-wearing classes: Hunter and Shaman. All pieces come from the same pool of world drops, with no items sourced differently.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Talhide Mail set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so the most efficient way to acquire them is often by searching for them on the Auction House. If you choose to farm the set yourself, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect (AoE) abilities to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with a high density of non-elite mobs, such as the northern parts of Hellfire Peninsula or the southern areas of Zangarmarsh. No instance mechanics like skips, lockouts, or bonus rolls apply to this farm.

298.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind on Equip world drops from the Shadowlands zone of Maldraxxus. All pieces of the set, including the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots, have a chance to drop from any creature killed within the zone. As these are Bind on Equip items, they are not soulbound upon being looted and can be freely traded or sold. The most direct alternative to farming the items is to purchase them from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as it comes from open-world mobs. The entire farm can be easily soloed by a single max-level character. Collection time is highly variable due to random drop chances; a targeted farm could take multiple hours, while purchasing the set from the Auction House can be instantaneous. This set does not use a tier token system, and since it does not drop from an instance, difficulty-based lockouts like Normal, Heroic, or Mythic do not apply.

### Farming Tips
Before farming, check the Auction House, as individual pieces are often listed for a low price, making it far more efficient to buy them than to farm them. For those who choose to farm, focus on areas with high mob density in Maldraxxus, such as the House of Constructs or the floating necropolis of an active world quest. All pieces are BoE, so you can farm them on any character and mail them to the appropriate mail-wearing character to learn the appearance, or simply equip and bind them on any character to add them to your account-wide collection.

305.content:
### How to Obtain
The Tsunami Mail set is a cosmetic ensemble purchased from the vendor Au'dara in the Hall of Curiosities in Oribos. The set becomes available for purchase after a character on the account reaches Renown 60 with any of the four Shadowlands Covenants. All eight pieces are sold by this single vendor: Visage of the Tsunami (Head), Pauldrons of the Tsunami (Shoulder), Cuirass of the Tsunami (Chest), Greaves of the Tsunami (Legs), Gauntlets of the Tsunami (Hands), Sabatons of the Tsunami (Feet), Girdle of the Tsunami (Waist), and Vambraces of the Tsunami (Wrist). Each piece costs 100 Reservoir Anima for a total of 800 Anima for the full set.

### Farming Logistics
As a vendor set purchased with currency, there are no lockouts or instance resets associated with this farm. The primary time investment is reaching Renown 60 on a single character, which can be done entirely solo through the Shadowlands campaign and by completing weekly quests for your chosen Covenant. Once the Renown requirement is met, the 800 Reservoir Anima can be farmed solo with no lockout from sources like world quests, treasures, and rare elites throughout the Shadowlands zones. No tier tokens are involved, and the set is available for purchase by any character on the account once unlocked.

### Farming Tips
Focus on reaching Renown 60 on just one character, as this will unlock the set for all characters on your account. The fastest way to gather the required 800 Anima is by completing world quests and looting treasures in Zereth Mortis, which award significantly more Anima than other zones. Once purchased, the items are Bind to Battle.net Account, and learning one piece adds the appearance to your collection for all characters. You can refund the items to the vendor within two hours of purchase to get your Anima back while keeping the transmog appearance.

307.content:
### How to Obtain
This set, commonly known as Overlord's Plate, is a world drop obtained from mobs within the level 40-50 range of Classic zones. All pieces are Bind on Equip and drop randomly from humanoid and beast mobs, not from specific bosses. The best zones for farming include Tanaris, Feralas, and The Hinterlands due to their high mob density. The set consists of Overlord's Chestplate, Overlord's Legplates, Overlord's Gauntlets, Overlord's Greaves, and Overlord's Girdle. There are no matching head, shoulder, or wrist items in the Overlord's set itself, though other items share the model.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it takes place in open-world zones. The farm is fully soloable at maximum level. Due to the extremely low drop chance for specific world drops, collecting the full set via farming can take dozens of hours or thousands of mob kills; there is no guaranteed number of runs. This set does not use a tier token system. The primary alternative source for every piece is the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip. Instance difficulty settings are not applicable to this world drop farm.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House; farming them directly is highly unreliable and time-consuming. If you choose to farm, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high mob density and fast respawn rates, like the pirate camps in southern Tanaris or the ogre mounds in Feralas. Since the items are BoE, any character can farm them and mail them to your plate-wearing character or sell them.

308.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality Bind on Equip plate items that drop from level 42-52 mobs in Classic-era zones. All pieces are random world drops and are not tied to specific bosses. The best zones for farming mobs in this level range include Tanaris, Feralas, and The Hinterlands. The specific items that comprise the set are the Emerald Helm, Emerald Pauldrons, Emerald Breastplate, Emerald Legplates, Emerald Gauntlets, Emerald Sabatons, Emerald Girdle, and Emerald Vambraces. Farming trash mobs within the dungeon Zul'Farrak is also an effective method for acquiring these pieces.

### Farming Logistics
As this is a world drop set, there is no lockout or instance reset to consider; mobs can be farmed continuously. The entire farm is easily soloable at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drop chances, the time required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can range from a few hours to hundreds of hours. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are obtained from the same random drop pool.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and are frequently listed by other players. If you choose to farm the set yourself, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with high mob density, like the Wastewander camps in Tanaris or the troll villages in The Hinterlands. Running the dungeon Zul'Farrak repeatedly and clearing only the trash mobs up to the first boss is a common farming strategy.

309.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind on Equip world drops primarily found on level 65-70 mobs in The Burning Crusade expansion zones. The optimal farming locations are areas with high humanoid and demon density in Shadowmoon Valley, such as Legion Hold or the Illidari Training Grounds, and Netherstorm, specifically around the Arklon Ruins. All eight pieces of this plate set drop from this shared world loot table. The main pieces are the Bloodscale Helm, Pauldrons, Chestpiece, Legguards, Gauntlets, and Sabatons, with the matching Bloodscale Belt and Bracers serving as the Waist and Wrist.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it involves killing respawning mobs in outdoor zones. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely large loot table for world drops and the low drop chance for specific items, acquiring a full set through farming can take dozens of hours and is highly dependent on luck. No tier token system is used, and because the items are Bind on Equip, they can be purchased from the Auction House, which is a separate acquisition source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by regularly checking the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can be traded or sold. For active farming, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of enemies quickly. While any class can loot these BoE items, only plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) can permanently add the appearances to their wardrobe collection.

312.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from Uncommon quality items dropped in the level 60 Classic dungeons, Scholomance and Stratholme. The majority of the pieces drop from specific bosses within these two instances located in the Western and Eastern Plaguelands, respectively. The Head drops from Darkmaster Gandling in Scholomance, and the Shoulders drop from Baron Rivendare in Stratholme. The Chest is found on Ras Frostwhisper and the Hands on Instructor Malicia, both in Scholomance. The Legs are looted from Maleki the Pallid and the Feet from Ramstein the Gorger, both in Stratholme. The Waist and Wrist are world drops from elite undead mobs throughout both zones.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons can be reset up to five times per hour, allowing for rapid farming sessions. Both Stratholme and Scholomance can be fully soloed by a current max-level character in a few minutes per run. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance, collecting the full set typically requires 15-25 total runs across both dungeons, though the world drop pieces may take longer. This set does not use a tier token system, as the items drop directly for any cloth-wearing class. There is only one difficulty for these Classic-era dungeons, and no LFR, Heroic, or Mythic versions exist. Note that the Waist and Wrist pieces must be farmed from outdoor mobs in the Plaguelands, separate from the instance runs.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a high-mobility class like a Monk or Druid. In Stratholme, you can enter through the service gate entrance to go directly to the undead section where most pieces drop. The waist and wrist pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they can often be found for purchase on the Auction House, which can save considerable time farming world mobs. No attunements or keys are required to enter these dungeons, and there are no raid-style mechanics to slow down the farm.

314.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind-on-Equip (BoE) world drops from The Burning Crusade content. The items are most frequently found by killing level 65-70 mobs in Outland zones and dungeons. High-density dungeons such as The Steamvault in Zangarmarsh or Mana-Tombs in Terokkar Forest are efficient farming locations. As the pieces are BoE, they can also be purchased directly from the Auction House. The set pieces are uncommon quality greens with the "of the Unbreakable" suffix, such as "Breastplate of the Unbreakable" and "Legplates of the Unbreakable," covering all eight armor slots.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set has no weekly lockout. If farming dungeons on Normal difficulty, you are limited to 10 instances per hour per account; Heroic difficulty imposes a daily lockout per character. The entire farm is easily soloable by any current max-level character. Because this is a random world drop set, the number of runs required is highly variable and can range from dozens to hundreds of hours; purchasing from the Auction House is a more predictable alternative. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same general loot pool of Outland mobs.

### Farming Tips
For dungeon farming, run on Normal difficulty to bypass the daily lockout and reset instances continuously up to the hourly cap. Use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear trash packs quickly. The most time-efficient method for acquiring this set is to regularly check the Auction House, as individual pieces are often listed for low prices. Since all items are BoE, you can farm them on any character and mail them to your intended plate-wearing character for transmog collection.

315.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from mobs between level 45 and 55. The most effective way to farm it is by clearing Classic-era dungeons with a high density of mobs in this level range. The primary sources are Stratholme and Scholomance. All pieces are random drops from any non-boss mob within these instances. The specific items that complete the set are the Symbolic Crown (Head), Symbolic Shoulders, Symbolic Breastplate (Chest), Symbolic Legplates (Legs), Symbolic Gauntlets (Hands), Symbolic Sabatons (Feet), Symbolic Girdle (Waist), and Symbolic Vambraces (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set in dungeons is subject to a daily reset for each character. Open-world farming has no lockout. The set can be fully soloed by any character at current max level. Due to being a random world drop with a very large loot table, the number of runs required can vary from under ten to several hundred; a specific estimate is unreliable. This set does not use a token system. All pieces drop from the same pool of mobs, and Classic dungeons do not have different difficulties like LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for the "Symbolic" pieces on the Auction House, as they are all Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and frequently listed. For self-farming, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Monk or Demon Hunter, to clear dungeons quickly. Focus on pulling as many trash mobs as possible in dungeons like Stratholme (the living side is popular) or Scholomance to maximize your chances per run. You can run these dungeons on multiple plate-wearing characters each day to bypass the daily lockout on a single character.

317.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality items that drop from various high-level Classic dungeons. The Head drops from Darkmaster Gandling in Scholomance. The Shoulders drop from Cannon Master Willey in the Live side of Stratholme. The Chest is looted from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire. The Legs drop from Baron Rivendare in the Undead side of Stratholme, and the Hands drop from Quartermaster Zigris in Lower Blackrock Spire. The Feet drop from Mother Smolderweb in Lower Blackrock Spire, the Waist from Maleki the Pallid in Stratholme, and the Wrists from The Unforgiven in Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
These dungeons are all subject to a daily reset, allowing one attempt per character per day. The entire set can be easily soloed at the current maximum level, with enemies posing no threat. Given the number of pieces spread across multiple instances, collecting the full set can take between 10-20 total runs, depending on drop luck. This set does not use a token system; each piece drops directly from a boss. Classic dungeons do not have different difficulties like Normal or Heroic, so there is only one loot table and one lockout per day.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, to navigate the dungeons as quickly as possible. Obtaining the Key to the City for Stratholme allows you to open the service gate, providing a significant shortcut. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Ensure you are farming on a Mail-wearing character (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker), as the appearances will not be collected by a character of a different armor type.

321.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality world drops primarily found in The Burning Crusade zones. The main pieces drop from humanoid and demon mobs between levels 58-63, with Hellfire Peninsula being the most common source. The Head slot is not part of this specific recolor set. The Shoulder is Felstone Pauldrons, the Chest is Fel-pitted Leather Tunic, and the Legs are Fel-pitted Leather Leggings. The Hands are Fel-pitted Leather Gloves, the Feet are Fel-pitted Leather Boots, the Waist is Fel-pitted Leather Belt, and there are no matching Wrists.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is farmed from world drops in open-world zones. The entire farm is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the random nature of world drops, collecting the full set can take anywhere from a couple of hours to dozens of hours, depending entirely on luck. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same source type and are Bind on Equip, meaning they can be traded or sold on the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
Farm in an area with high mob density in Hellfire Peninsula, such as the Bleeding Hollow Orcs around Zeth'Gor or the demons near the Pools of Aggonar. Use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Monk or Demon Hunter, to clear mobs quickly. Before starting the farm, check the Auction House for each piece, as they are often listed for a low price by players leveling through the zone. The entire set can sometimes be purchased for less gold than the time it would take to farm a single piece.

323.content:
### How to Obtain
This set and its lookalikes, such as the gold Hyperion Plate and the dark Revenant Plate, are Uncommon quality world drops from the Classic expansion. Pieces drop from humanoid and undead mobs between levels 45-60. All main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are part of this loot pool and can drop from any eligible creature. The most concentrated sources for farming are high-level dungeons, specifically the trash mobs in Stratholme, Scholomance, and Blackrock Spire. For open-world farming, the best zones are Winterspring, Eastern Plaguelands, and Silithus due to high mob density.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set has no weekly or daily lockout. When farming in the open world, mobs can be killed as they respawn. For dungeon farming, Normal difficulty instances can be entered and reset up to 10 times per hour, per account. The entire farm is fully soloable at the current max level, with mobs posing no threat. Because the items are random world drops from a very large loot table, the number of runs or hours needed is highly variable and can range from dozens to hundreds of clears. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning the fastest and most reliable way to complete it is by searching for individual items on the Auction House. For those who prefer to farm it directly, the most efficient method is to clear trash mobs in a dungeon like Stratholme (Living side). Use a high-mobility class to run through the instance, gather large groups of non-elite mobs, defeat them with area-of-effect abilities, and then exit and reset the instance. This method maximizes the number of potential drops per hour compared to open-world farming.

327.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from uncommon-quality leather pieces dropped by bosses within various Burning Crusade dungeons. The Head slot drops from Mechano-Lord Capacitus in The Mechanar and the Shoulders drop from Warlord Kalithresh in The Steamvault. Both the Chest and Legs are found in The Blood Furnace, dropping from The Maker and Broggok respectively. The Hands and Feet both drop in The Underbog from Swamplord Musel'ek and Hungarfen. Finally, the Waist piece drops from Quagmirran inside The Slave Pens.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed across multiple dungeons, each subject to a daily dungeon reset lockout. All encounters can be fully soloed at maximum level, with bosses being defeated in a single ability. Due to the pieces being spread across five different instances, collecting the full set will likely require 2-4 days of runs, assuming an average drop chance per piece. This set does not use a tier token system, as all items are direct boss drops that can be equipped by any leather-wearing class. Normal and Heroic difficulties are separate lockouts, but this specific green-quality set only drops on Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Run all dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this is the required setting for these specific items to drop. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid with Cat Form or a Monk, will significantly speed up the time it takes to clear each instance. Setting a character's hearthstone to Shattrath City provides the most central access to all the required dungeons in Outland. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

329.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon Bind-on-Equip world drops from The Burning Crusade content. The items drop from level 65-70 mobs, primarily found in zones like Netherstorm and Shadowmoon Valley, or within level-appropriate dungeons. The most efficient farming locations are high-density dungeons such as The Mana-Tombs or The Shadow Labyrinth in Auchindoun. The set consists of the Revenant Chestplate, Revenant Legguards, Revenant Gauntlets, Revenant Sabatons, Revenant Girdle, and Revenant Vambraces; no matching helm or shoulder pieces exist for this specific recolor.

### Farming Logistics
The most effective farming method uses Normal difficulty dungeons, which are subject to a lockout of 10 instances per hour, per account. The set is fully soloable by any max-level character. As random world drops from a large loot table, there is no predictable drop chance, and collecting the full set can require many hours of continuous farming. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces drop from the same random mob sources. Running on Heroic difficulty is not recommended as it imposes a daily lockout and does not increase the drop rate for these particular items.

### Farming Tips
Run dungeons on Normal difficulty to take advantage of the ability to reset the instance up to 10 times per hour. Focus on clearing large groups of trash mobs quickly, then exit the instance, reset it via your portrait, and repeat the process without killing any bosses. Because all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), check the Auction House regularly, as individual items can often be purchased to complete the set. Classes with high mobility and instant area-of-effect abilities, such as Druids or Monks, are optimal for this type of farm.

331.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop, randomly obtained from trash mobs within level 70 Heroic dungeons from The Burning Crusade. The entire set is Bind on Equip (BoE), making the Auction House a viable alternative source. While any BC Heroic dungeon can drop the pieces, instances with high trash density like The Shattered Halls, The Arcatraz, and The Botanica are the most efficient farming locations. The set pieces are not tied to specific bosses; they are random drops from any non-boss enemy within these zones.

*   **Head:** Heroic Plate Helm
*   **Shoulder:** Heroic Plate Pauldrons
*   **Chest:** Heroic Plate Breastplate
*   **Wrist:** Heroic Plate Vambraces
*   **Hands:** Heroic Plate Gauntlets
*   **Waist:** Heroic Plate Girdle
*   **Legs:** Heroic Plate Greaves
*   **Feet:** Heroic Plate Sabatons

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, meaning each specific dungeon can be fully cleared for loot once per day per character. The content is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low drop chance of each BoE piece, a targeted farm could require hundreds of runs; purchasing pieces from the Auction House is a more reliable acquisition method. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same trash mob loot tables. The distinction between Normal and Heroic difficulty is critical, as these items only drop in the Heroic versions of the dungeons.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to obtain this set is by regularly searching for the "Heroic Plate" pieces on the Auction House. For those who prefer to farm it directly, use a high-mobility character to clear large groups of trash mobs as quickly as possible, ignoring bosses entirely. You can run the daily lockout on multiple plate-wearing characters to increase your chances. After clearing the most dense trash packs near the entrance of an instance like The Shattered Halls, you can exit, reset all instances, and move to the next Heroic dungeon on your list for the day.

332.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind-on-Equip world drops from level 45-50 mobs in original Classic zones. There are no specific bosses that drop these items. The full eight-piece set consists of the Imbued Plate Helm, Pauldrons, Armor, Vambraces, Gauntlets, Girdle, Leggings, and Boots. The most effective zones for farming mobs in this level range are the Western Plaguelands, Eastern Plaguelands, and Burning Steppes. Dungeons such as Blackrock Depths, Scholomance, and Stratholme also contain a high density of mobs that can drop these pieces.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set has no lockout if done in open-world zones. If farming in dungeons, you are subject to the 10 instances per hour limit on Normal difficulty. The entire set can be easily soloed at current maximum level. Due to the items being random world drops with a very low drop chance, there is no approximate number of runs; acquisition is entirely dependent on random luck. This set does not use a token system and has no varying difficulty lockouts as it is not from a raid. The most reliable method of acquisition is the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House; farming them yourself can take an unpredictable and lengthy amount of time. If you choose to farm, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage to clear large groups of mobs quickly in zones like the Eastern Plaguelands. Running Normal Blackrock Depths and clearing all trash mobs is another viable, high-density farming strategy. The pieces are not class-specific drops, so any class can be used to farm them.

333.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind-on-Equip world drops from level 68-70 zones and instances in the Burning Crusade. The pieces do not drop from specific bosses but can drop from any eligible mob, making instance trash farming the most effective method. A commonly farmed dungeon is The Mechanar in Netherstorm due to its high density of humanoid mobs. The full set consists of the Helm of the Stalwart Defender (Head), Pauldrons of the Wardancer (Shoulder), Breastplate of the Righteous (Chest), Vambraces of the Bold (Wrist), Gauntlets of the Bold (Hands), Girdle of the Righteous (Waist), Greaves of the Martyr (Legs), and Boots of the Vindicator (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
The most efficient farming method uses a Normal difficulty dungeon, which has no daily or weekly lockout and can be reset manually. Players are, however, limited to 10 instance runs per hour per account. The set can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Due to the extremely low drop chance of world drops, collecting the full set through farming could take hundreds of runs. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same pool of world loot.

### Farming Tips
Farm The Mechanar on Normal difficulty to avoid the daily lockout associated with Heroic mode. The optimal strategy is to enter the instance, clear all trash mobs in a loop up to the first boss, exit the dungeon, and use the "Reset all instances" option by right-clicking your character portrait. Since every piece is a Bind-on-Equip (BoE) item, you can often find them for sale on the Auction House, which may be a faster alternative to farming. A character with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, will clear the trash packs most efficiently.

334.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon Bind-on-Equip world drops found within level 58-63 zones and dungeons from the late-vanilla and early Burning Crusade eras. The most effective farming locations are the Normal difficulty dungeons in Hellfire Peninsula, specifically Hellfire Ramparts and The Blood Furnace, due to their high density of humanoid mobs. The six core set pieces—Imbued Plate Helm, Imbued Plate Pauldrons, Imbued Plate Armor, Imbued Plate Gauntlets, Imbued Plate Leggings, and Imbued Plate Boots—drop randomly from any trash mob or boss inside these instances. No matching Waist or Wrist pieces exist for this specific recolor set.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to the standard 10-instances-per-hour, per-account lockout for Normal difficulty dungeons, which can be reset manually after each run. The content can be easily soloed by any max-level character in seconds. As the pieces are random world drops with a very large loot table, the number of runs required is highly variable and can take hundreds of resets to complete. There is no tier token system involved; all items are direct drops. You must run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as Heroic difficulty applies a separate daily lockout and contains a different loot table.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), meaning the fastest way to obtain the set is often by purchasing it from the Auction House. For direct farming, use a high-mobility class to clear only the initial trash packs of Hellfire Ramparts, then exit the instance, reset it, and repeat the process to maximize mobs killed per hour. Do not kill bosses, as this is inefficient for farming world drops. Since the items are BoE, any character can farm them, but the appearance will only be added to your wardrobe by a mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker) upon equipping the item.

335.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind-on-Equip world drops from level 60-70 mobs, primarily found throughout Outland. While the pieces can drop from any suitable mob in the open world, the most efficient farming locations are early Burning Crusade dungeons with high mob density, such as Hellfire Ramparts or The Blood Furnace on Normal difficulty. As these are not boss-specific drops, the entire set is obtained by killing trash mobs within these zones. Alternatively, all pieces can frequently be purchased directly from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no weekly or daily lockout; dungeons can be reset and farmed repeatedly, though you are limited to 10 instance runs per hour per account. The set can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Due to the extremely random nature of world drops, self-farming the complete set could take anywhere from a few dozen to hundreds of instance clears. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces drop from the same general loot table, so no part of the set requires farming a different location.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and can be bought and sold. For those who prefer to farm it themselves, use a high-movement speed character to clear all trash mobs in Normal Hellfire Ramparts, then exit, reset the instance, and repeat. Since the items are BoE, they must be equipped by a plate-wearing character (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) to be added to your account-wide wardrobe.

336.content:
### How to Obtain
This set, commonly known as the Saltstone armor, is a Bind-on-Equip world drop from level 48-58 mobs in original Classic zones. The best zones for farming include Silithus, Winterspring, the Blasted Lands, and both Western and Eastern Plaguelands. The pieces can also drop from trash mobs inside late-game Classic dungeons such as Scholomance, Stratholme, and Blackrock Depths. All main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are part of this world drop pool and are not tied to specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
This farm has no lockout, as it primarily involves killing mobs in outdoor zones; however, if farming dungeons, you are limited to 10 instances per hour across your account. The entire set can be easily soloed at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drops, acquiring the full set through farming could take hundreds of hours of dedicated effort. This set does not use a tier token system and all pieces drop from the same general loot table of high-level Classic mobs.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by searching for individual "Saltstone" pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind-on-Equip and can be traded. If farming manually, use a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage to clear large groups of mobs quickly in zones like Eastern Plaguelands or Winterspring. There are no skips or special mechanics for this farm; it is a straightforward grind of killing mobs in high-density areas.

337.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from mobs within the Burning Crusade expansion, primarily found in Zangarmarsh. The pieces are not tied to specific bosses and drop from the general population of creatures in the zone. The highest concentration of mobs that drop the set pieces are the various Naga and Murloc populations. The set consists of the Grimscale Helm, Pauldrons, Breastplate, Legplates, Gauntlets, Sabatons, Greatbelt, and Bracers, all of which drop from mobs in Zangarmarsh, with a higher chance from enemies around Coilfang Reservoir.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm as it consists entirely of world drops. The set can be fully soloed at maximum level with extreme ease. Due to the low drop chance of specific world drop items, collecting the full set could take anywhere from 5 to 50 hours of dedicated farming. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces drop from the same general sources, though some players report higher drop rates from the trash mobs inside the Coilfang Reservoir dungeons (The Slave Pens, The Underbog, The Steamvault).

### Farming Tips
Use a high-level character with significant area-of-effect damage and high mobility to clear large groups of enemies quickly. All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so the most efficient way to obtain it is to check the Auction House frequently. When searching, be sure to look under both the Mail and Plate categories, as the set has a plate appearance but is classified as Mail armor. A useful farming route involves circling Serpent Lake and the areas south of Telredor, killing all Naga and Murloc camps along the way.

338.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind-on-Equip world drops from level 51-60 mobs in Classic-era zones and dungeons. The most thematic and effective farming location is Blackrock Spire, due to its high density of appropriate-level elite mobs. All eight pieces—Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist—are random drops from any mob inside the dungeon. No specific boss drops any particular piece; the entire set is part of the global loot table for creatures in this level range. Other potential farming zones include the Eastern Plaguelands and Winterspring.

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout besides the standard limit of five dungeon instances per hour per account. A max-level character can solo Blackrock Spire without any difficulty. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drops, directly farming the full set is inefficient and could take hundreds of runs. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces drop from the same general sources, and there are no alternate difficulties (LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic) for this Classic dungeon.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House. All items are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), making them tradable and frequently listed for sale. If you choose to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class to clear the trash mobs in Normal Blackrock Spire as quickly as possible, ignoring bosses to maximize trash kills per hour. Reset the instance after each full clear, up to the hourly limit. There are no skips or bonus rolls relevant to this farm.

340.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted by Blacksmiths using patterns from The Burning Crusade. The primary reagents needed are Fel Iron Bars, Adamantite Bars, Khorium Bars, and various Primal elements like Primal Fire and Primal Earth. The patterns for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Gauntlets, and Wrist are world drops from level 65-70 mobs across Outland. The recipes for the Legs, Feet, and Waist are purchased from reputation vendors: Cenarion Expedition (Revered) for the Legs, Honor Hold/Thrallmar (Revered) for the Feet, and The Sha'tar (Honored) for the Waist.

### Farming Logistics
There is no instance lockout for this set, as it is acquired through crafting and farming world drops. All materials and patterns can be farmed solo at the current maximum level. The time required to collect the set depends entirely on farming the materials and world drop patterns, or the gold available to purchase them. Because this is a crafted set, there are no tier tokens, lockouts for different raid difficulties, or boss-specific drops to consider.

### Farming Tips
The most direct method to acquire this set is to purchase the finished armor pieces from the Auction House, as they are Bind-on-Equip. Alternatively, the patterns and materials can also be purchased from the Auction House for a Blacksmith to craft. For those who prefer to farm, high-density mob areas in Nagrand and Blade's Edge Mountains are efficient for the world drop patterns. The vendor-purchased patterns require targeted reputation grinding, which can be done by completing quests and running associated dungeons like The Underbog for Cenarion Expedition.

341.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is sourced from the three dungeons within Coilfang Reservoir in Zangarmarsh (The Burning Crusade): The Slave Pens, The Underbog, and The Steamvault. The Helm of the Bogslayer drops from Rokmar the Crackler and the Gauntlets drop from Quagmirran in The Slave Pens. The Pauldrons and Girdle both drop from Ghaz'an, while the Boots drop from Hungarfen, all located in The Underbog. To complete the set, the Breastplate and Bracers drop from Warlord Kalithresh and the Legplates drop from Mekgineer Steamrigger, both found inside The Steamvault.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from dungeons that can be run on Normal difficulty, which allows for up to 10 instance resets per hour. The dungeons also have a daily Heroic lockout, but Normal is more efficient for farming this specific set. All bosses and instances can be easily soloed by a current max-level character. Given the pieces are spread across three separate dungeons, expect to complete 15-30 total runs to acquire all eight items. This set does not use a token system and its appearance is the same on both Normal and Heroic difficulties.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, set the dungeon difficulty to Normal before entering to bypass any daily lockouts. All three dungeons are accessed from the central underwater pipe in Coilfang Reservoir, making it easy to cycle between them. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House. At max level, you can run directly to each required boss, ignoring all other enemies to speed up the process significantly.

343.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from plate armor pieces that drop in various Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The Head, Talonguard Helm, drops from Warlord Kalithresh in The Steamvault. The Shoulder, Talonguard Epaulets, drops from Vazruden in Hellfire Ramparts. The Chest, Talonguard Tunic, is from The Maker, and the Hands, Talonguard Gauntlets, are from Broggok, both within The Blood Furnace. The Legs, Talonguard Legplates, drop from Murmur in Shadow Labyrinth, and the Feet, Talonguard Boots, are looted from Keli'dan the Breaker, also in The Blood Furnace. The Waist, Talonguard Girdle, is obtained from Mennu the Betrayer in The Slave Pens, and the Wrist, Talonguard Bracers, drops from Lieutenant Drake in Old Hillsbrad Foothills.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items are all subject to a daily Heroic dungeon reset, allowing one attempt per boss each day. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level plate-wearing character (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per piece, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely require 5-10 days of clearing all relevant dungeons. There is no token system; the gear drops directly for plate wearers and is automatically added to the transmog collection. All pieces drop from different bosses across six separate instances, requiring significant travel between zones.

### Farming Tips
Set the instance difficulty to Heroic before entering each dungeon, as the set does not drop on Normal. Using a high-mobility class like a Paladin with speed talents or a Warrior with Heroic Leap will significantly reduce the time spent traveling within and between the instances. While some trash can be skipped, most bosses are on a linear path you must clear. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Having the permanent `Shadow Labyrinth Key` will save a few minutes in that instance, and a character with a portal to Shattrath simplifies travel to Outland's instance hubs.

345.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Overlord's Plate, a world drop from level 51-60 mobs found in late-game Classic zones and dungeons. The most efficient farming locations are dungeons with high mob density, such as Blackrock Depths, Stratholme, and Scholomance, as elites have a better chance of dropping uncommon items. The set consists of eight pieces: Overlord's Crown (Head), Overlord's Spaulders (Shoulder), Overlord's Chestplate (Chest), Overlord's Legplates (Legs), Overlord's Gauntlets (Hands), Overlord's Greaves (Feet), Overlord's Girdle (Waist), and Overlord's Vambraces (Wrist). All pieces share the same random drop source and are not tied to specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from zones with no lockout or from Normal difficulty dungeons, which can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire farm is easily soloable by a max-level character, with mobs posing no threat. Due to the low, random drop chance of world drops, collection time is highly variable and can take many hours of dedicated farming to acquire the full set. No tier token system is used, and different difficulties like Heroic or Mythic are not relevant to this Classic-era farm. All pieces drop from the same general loot table for mobs within the appropriate level range.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the Bind-on-Equip pieces from the Auction House, which bypasses the randomness of farming entirely. For those who prefer to self-farm, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to clear Normal difficulty Blackrock Depths or Stratholme, focusing on killing the maximum number of mobs per run. There are no required skips or special mechanics, and systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to this content. As the items are uncommon-quality greens, they can be easily overlooked, so be careful not to vendor them.

347.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of world drops found on creatures and in chests throughout level 25-35 zones in original Azeroth. The best zones for open-world farming are Thousand Needles, the southern portion of Hillsbrad Foothills, Arathi Highlands, and Desolace. Alternatively, the set pieces can drop from mobs inside dungeons of a similar level range, primarily Razorfen Kraul and the wings of Scarlet Monastery. All eight pieces—Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist—drop from the same pool of enemies and are not tied to specific bosses.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set in the open world, but Normal difficulty dungeons are subject to the 10-instances-per-hour limit. The entire set can be easily soloed by a max-level character. As a world drop BoE set with low drop chances, collecting all pieces through farming can take dozens of hours and is highly dependent on luck. This is not a tier set, so there are no tokens or class-sharing systems. All pieces come from the same general loot tables within the appropriate level range.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House first and foremost, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and can often be purchased for a reasonable price, saving significant farming time. If farming manually, focus on high-density mob areas or run Normal Scarlet Monastery (Armory and Cathedral wings) repeatedly, as the mob concentration is higher than in open-world zones. Use a high-mobility class with strong AoE abilities to clear packs quickly. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not applicable for obtaining this set.

349.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind on Equip items that drop randomly from level 67-70 creatures throughout The Burning Crusade expansion zones. There are no specific bosses that drop these items. The most effective farming locations are zones with a high density of appropriate-level mobs, such as Shadowmoon Valley, Netherstorm, and the Isle of Quel'Danas. All pieces, including the Conqueror's Plate Helm, Spaulders, Breastplate, Legplates, Gauntlets, Greaves, Girdle, and Vambraces, share this same random world drop source. The most efficient acquisition method is often purchasing the pieces from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from world mobs, so there is no lockout or instance reset to consider. The farm can be done continuously and is entirely soloable at current max level. Because the items are random world drops with a very low chance of appearing, it is not possible to estimate the number of runs required; collection time is highly variable and can take hundreds of hours of farming. This is not a tier set, so there is no token system, and no specific classes share loot. All pieces drop from the same general pool of mobs, with no alternate sources or difficulty settings involved.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by regularly checking the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can be traded or sold. If farming directly, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect abilities to kill large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on high-density areas like the Legion Hold in Shadowmoon Valley or the ethereals and demons in Netherstorm. Because it is a world drop, systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply.

351.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is an Uncommon quality world drop obtained by killing Murlocs in Classic-era zones. All pieces are part of the "Salt-Encrusted" set, which is the recolor of the original Grimscale armor. The most efficient farming locations are the large Murloc encampments along the southern coast of Hillsbrad Foothills and the western coast of Tirisfal Glades. The set consists of the Salt-Encrusted Breastplate (Chest), Salt-Encrusted Leggings (Legs), Salt-Encrusted Gauntlets (Hands), Salt-Encrusted Sabatons (Feet), and Salt-Encrusted Girdle (Waist). Head, Shoulder, and Wrist pieces do not exist for this specific recolor set.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop, this set has no lockout and can be farmed continuously by forcing mob respawns. The farm is trivial to solo at current max level, as the mobs are below level 30. Due to the low, random drop chance of specific Uncommon items, collecting all five pieces can take anywhere from one to five hours of dedicated farming, equating to several hundred Murloc kills. There is no tier token system, as this is not a raid set. All pieces drop from the same mob types in the specified zones.

### Farming Tips
Use a max-level character with high mobility and strong instant-cast area-of-effect (AoE) abilities to clear Murloc camps as quickly as possible. All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be traded and sold on the Auction House. Before starting the farm, it is highly recommended to check the Auction House, as you may be able to purchase the entire set or missing pieces for a relatively low amount of gold. Grouping with other players can increase kill speed, but loot will be personal, so it offers no specific advantage for collecting BoE appearances.

353.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of Bind-on-Equip items that drop randomly from level 55-60 mobs throughout Classic zones. All main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) share the same random world drop source and are not tied to specific bosses. The most effective farming locations are high-density mob areas in zones like Eastern Plaguelands, Western Plaguelands, and Silithus. Classic dungeons such as Stratholme, Scholomance, and Blackrock Spire also contain a high concentration of mobs that can drop these pieces.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout; it is farmed from world mobs or from dungeons that can be reset up to the hourly instance limit. The entire farm can be completed solo by any max-level character.
