As a world drop set with very low drop rates, collecting all pieces directly could take anywhere from dozens to hundreds of hours of farming. There is no tier token system for this set. All pieces come from the same loot pool of random world drops, but their primary acquisition method for many players will be the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient farming method is to repeatedly clear high-density Classic dungeons like Stratholme or Blackrock Spire on Normal difficulty, as difficulty does not affect the drop rate of these items. Because all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), check the Auction House regularly, as it is often faster to buy missing pieces than to farm them directly. Use a character with high mobility and area-of-effect abilities to maximize the number of mobs killed per hour. The appearance will be added to your Wardrobe once the BoE item is soulbound to you.

356.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon-quality quest rewards from the level 80-82 Cataclysm zones of Mount Hyjal and Vashj'ir. The pieces are guaranteed rewards for completing specific quests within each zone's main storyline. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, and Feet drop from Vashj'ir quests, while the Legs, Hands, and Waist are rewarded from Mount Hyjal quests. The set does not include a matching wrist piece with a unique appearance.

*   **Head:** Tsunami Helm - Quest reward from "All That Rises" in Vashj'ir.
*   **Shoulder:** Tsunami Shoulderguards - Quest reward from "Finders, Keepers" in Vashj'ir.
*   **Chest:** Tsunami Chestguard - Quest reward from "A Desperate Plea" in Vashj'ir.
*   **Legs:** Tsunami Leggings - Quest reward from "The Fires of Mount Hyjal" in Mount Hyjal.
*   **Hands:** Tsunami Gloves - Quest reward from "The Return of the Ancients" in Mount Hyjal.
*   **Feet:** Tsunami Boots - Quest reward from "Privilege of Rank" in Vashj'ir.
*   **Waist:** Tsunami Belt - Quest reward from "The Nordrassil Summit" in Mount Hyjal.

### Farming Logistics
This set is acquired via one-time quests and therefore has no lockout or reset timer. A character can only complete these quests once, but the appearances are unlocked account-wide for all characters of the appropriate armor type. The entire set can be collected in a single session by following the questlines and is fully soloable at max level. As these are guaranteed quest rewards, no repeat runs are necessary; completion of the specified quests guarantees acquisition. The set is split between two separate zones, requiring progression in both the Mount Hyjal and Vashj'ir storylines.

### Farming Tips
Use a max-level character to complete the required quests in Mount Hyjal and Vashj'ir for the fastest acquisition time. Start the Cataclysm questlines via the Hero's Call Board in Stormwind or the Warchief's Command Board in Orgrimmar. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. If you have already completed these quests on a character, the appearances may already be in your Wardrobe; if not, you must complete them on an alternate leather-wearing character to unlock the appearance account-wide.

357.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted by players with the Blacksmithing profession. The patterns for all eight pieces are learned directly from any Northrend Blacksmithing trainer, requiring a skill level between 415 and 440. Key reagents for the entire set are 96 Saronite Bars and 8 Eternal Earth. Each piece—Helm, Shoulders, Breastplate, Legplates, Gauntlets, Boots, Belt, and Bracers—is crafted individually using these materials. No raid or dungeon farming is required to obtain these specific patterns.

### Farming Logistics
There is no instance-based lockout for this set, as it is crafted. Farming is limited only by the time it takes to gather the raw materials, primarily Saronite Ore and Crystallized Earth. The material farming can be fully soloed at current max level. A character with Northrend Mining can gather the required ore and earth in approximately 1-2 hours of focused farming. All eight pieces are crafted and have the same source, with no tier tokens or raid difficulties involved.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is often to purchase the finished Bind on Equip pieces directly from the Auction House. Alternatively, buying the raw materials (Saronite Ore or Bars) from the Auction House and commissioning a Blacksmith via the Crafting Order system is also a fast option. For those who prefer to farm the materials, the best zones for Saronite Ore are Icecrown and Sholazar Basin. A character with both Mining and a flying mount is highly recommended to speed up the gathering process.

359.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from plate items that drop in Cataclysm dungeons, primarily Halls of Origination, The Stonecore, and The Lost City of the Tol'vir. No single instance contains all the pieces. Many items also share appearances with non-repeatable quest rewards from the Deepholm and Twilight Highlands zones. The primary farmable dungeon sources are listed below.
*   **Head:** Temple Guardian's Helm - Temple Guardian Anhuur, Halls of Origination
*   **Shoulder:** Raz's Pauldrons - General Husam, The Lost City of the Tol'vir
*   **Chest:** Breastplate of the Risen Land - Earthrager Ptah, Halls of Origination
*   **Legs:** Legplates of the Sepulcher - Rajh, Halls of Origination
*   **Hands:** Hematite Plate Gauntlets - Slabhide, The Stonecore
*   **Feet:** Clattering Skeletal Sabatons - Anraphet, Halls of Origination
*   **Waist:** Girdle of the Mountains - Slabhide, The Stonecore
*   **Wrist:** Alpha Bracers - Anraphet, Halls of Origination

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these items have a daily Heroic lockout and an hourly instance limit (10 per hour) for Normal difficulty. The entire set can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Given the 15-25% drop chance for each piece and their distribution across three separate dungeons, collecting the full set may take between 10-20 total instance runs. This set does not use a tier token system; all items are direct boss drops. Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same appearances and loot tables for these items, but they are on separate lockouts.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to reset the instance and re-clear it up to 10 times per hour, bypassing the daily Heroic lockout. Since the items are Bind-on-Pickup, they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. In Halls of Origination, use the teleporters that activate after defeating the first two bosses to navigate the instance more quickly. The set pieces will be added to your Wardrobe automatically upon being looted by a Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight.

365.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained by completing quests in the Mount Hyjal zone from the Cataclysm expansion. The majority of the set pieces are rewarded from the zone's final quest, "The Firelord." The shoulders are an exception, rewarded from the earlier quest "An Ancient Awakens." All other main armor slots are rewarded simultaneously upon completion of the final quest.

*   **Head, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist**: "The Firelord" quest
*   **Shoulder**: "An Ancient Awakens" quest

### Farming Logistics
This set is acquired via a one-time questline per character, so it does not have a weekly or daily lockout. The entire quest chain can be completed in a single session and is fully soloable by any max-level character. Collection is guaranteed upon one full completion of the required Mount Hyjal storyline. This is not a tier set, so there is no token system, and no pieces drop from other sources.

### Farming Tips
To collect this set's appearance, you must complete the questline on a Plate-wearing class: Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight. Even though the appearance is usable by all classes once collected, a Mage, Priest, or Warlock cannot equip the Plate-quality quest rewards to learn them. The quest items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House. Using a max-level character with flying will significantly speed up travel between quest objectives in the large zone.

370.content:
### How to Obtain
The Redsteel Plate set is a collection of Bind-on-Equip world drops from level 78-82 mobs in Cataclysm content. It can be farmed from enemies in the initial Cataclysm zones of Mount Hyjal, Vashj'ir, and Deepholm. A more targeted approach is farming trash mobs inside Cataclysm dungeons, with The Stonecore and Blackrock Caverns being common choices due to their mob density. All eight pieces (Helm, Shoulders, Breastplate, Bracers, Gauntlets, Belt, Legguards, Boots) drop from the same pool of enemies and can also be purchased from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
This farm is not subject to a weekly or daily lockout; Normal difficulty dungeons can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The set is fully soloable at the current maximum level by any class. The number of runs needed is highly variable due to the low, random drop chance of world BoEs, potentially taking hundreds of resets. No tier token system is used for this set. All pieces drop from the same sources, and farming on Normal difficulty is the most efficient method for repeated clears.

### Farming Tips
For the most efficient farm, enter The Stonecore on Normal difficulty and clear the dense packs of troggs and stone giants before the first boss, then leave and reset the instance. Before starting, check the Auction House thoroughly, as buying the pieces is almost always faster than farming them directly. Using a character with high mobility and area-of-effect damage will significantly speed up the trash clearing process. The set is a zone-wide drop, so any mob within the appropriate level range has a chance to drop a piece.

374.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from individual pieces that drop in various Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Head, `Cowl of the Crying Defender`, drops from Loken in Halls of Lightning. The Shoulder, `Mantle of the Unknowing`, drops from Sjonnir The Ironshaper in Halls of Stone. The Chest, `Robes of the Umbral Brute`, drops from Moorabi in Gundrak. The Legs, `Leggings of the Tortured Earth`, drop from Ley-Guardian Eregos in The Oculus. The Hands, `Gloves of the Cunning Schemer`, drop from Cyanigosa in The Violet Hold. The Feet, `Slippers of the Inevitable`, and Waist, `Belt of the Fated Score`, both drop in The Culling of Stratholme from Chrono-Lord Epoch and Salramm the Fleshcrafter respectively. Finally, the Wrist, `Bracers of the Divine Inferno`, drops from Keristrasza in The Nexus.

### Farming Logistics
The pieces are farmed from Heroic dungeons which are on a daily lockout, meaning you can attempt each boss once per day per character. The entire set can be soloed by any max-level character with trivial difficulty. Given the number of different dungeons and an approximate drop chance of 15-25% per piece, a player can expect to complete the set in 5-10 daily resets. There is no tier token system for this set; all pieces are direct boss drops. These specific appearances only drop on Heroic difficulty; Normal difficulty dungeons have a separate loot table and do not contain these items.

### Farming Tips
For fastest clears, use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, to move through the instances quickly. Be aware that The Culling of Stratholme is a timed escort instance that cannot be rushed, and The Violet Hold features random bosses, so you may need multiple daily attempts to get Cyanigosa to spawn. All instances must be set to Heroic difficulty before entering for the items to have a chance to drop. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

375.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is sourced from various Wrath of the Lich King dungeons set to Heroic difficulty. The Head, Cowl of Icy Breaths, drops from Scourgelord Tyrannus in Pit of Saron. The Shoulders, Mantle of the Wailing Banshee, drop from Echo of Sylvanas in Halls of Reflection. The Chest piece, Robes of the Cheating Heart, is looted from Prince Taldaram in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom. Both the Waist (Sash of the Servant) and Wrists (Cuffs of Devotion) drop in Utgarde Keep, from Ingvar the Plunderer and Prince Keleseth respectively. The Hands, Gloves of the Cunning, come from The Prophet Tharon'ja in Drak'Tharon Keep; the Feet, Sandals of the Atonement, from Gortok Palehoof in Utgarde Pinnacle; and the Legs, Leggings of the Soothing Touch, from Cyanigosa in The Violet Hold.

### Farming Logistics
Each piece is on a daily dungeon reset, tied to the Heroic difficulty lockout. The entire set can be farmed solo by any max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, collecting the full set may take between 5-10 days of clearing all seven required dungeons daily. This set does not use a token system; all items are direct boss drops. Note that the legs are from a randomly chosen final boss in The Violet Hold, which can significantly extend the farm time. Normal and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, but these items only drop in Heroic.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic via your portrait's right-click menu before entering each instance. Using a class with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, will significantly decrease the travel time across Northrend between the different dungeons. The farm's main obstacle is The Violet Hold, where the final boss is random; you must run the instance daily until Cyanigosa spawns. The Halls of Reflection boss is also gated behind a non-skippable roleplay event, so be prepared for a short wait. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

376.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from individual armor pieces that drop in various Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. Each piece drops from a specific end-of-dungeon boss, requiring you to clear multiple different instances. The Head drops from Loken in Halls of Lightning and the Shoulder from Sjonnir The Ironshaper in Halls of Stone. The Chest is looted from King Ymiron in Utgarde Pinnacle, while the Legs drop from Ley-Guardian Eregos in The Oculus. The Hands drop from Novos the Summoner in Drak'Tharon Keep, Feet from Keristrasza in The Nexus, Waist from Herald Volazj in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom, and Wrist from Moragg in The Violet Hold.

### Farming Logistics
The eight dungeons containing these set pieces are all on a daily Heroic lockout, meaning you can attempt each boss once per day per character. The entire set can be easily soloed at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 20%, you will likely need 1-5 runs of each specific dungeon to acquire its corresponding piece, leading to a total farm time of one to two weeks depending on luck. This set does not use a tier token system; items are direct boss drops shared among all cloth-wearing classes. No pieces drop from a different source, but they only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Before entering each dungeon, right-click your character portrait and set the Dungeon Difficulty to Heroic. The most significant obstacle is The Violet Hold, where bosses are summoned randomly; you may need several days of clears for Moragg to spawn as one of the two bosses. In The Oculus, you can immediately dismount from the drake during the final encounter and kill Ley-Guardian Eregos directly to save time. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

377.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set leather pieces from various Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Head, Mask of Lethal Intent, drops from Jedoga Shadowseeker in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom. The Shoulder, Spaulders of the Careless Thief, drops from Scourgelord Tyrannus in Pit of Saron. The Chest, Tunic of Indulgence, is found in the Champion's Purse rewarded for completing Trial of the Champion. The Legs, Leggings of the Insatiable, drop from Loken in Halls of Lightning. The Hands, Handgrips of the Savage Emissary, drop from Hadronox in Azjol-Nerub, and the Feet, Boots of the Never-Ending Murmur, drop from Eck the Ferocious in Gundrak. The matching Waist, Belt of the Merciless Killer, drops from Mal'Ganis in The Culling of Stratholme, while the Wrist drops from Salramm the Fleshcrafter in the same instance.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Wrath of the Lich King dungeons, which are on a daily lockout per character for Heroic difficulty. All encounters can be soloed by any max-level character with no difficulty. As the eight pieces drop from seven different dungeons, a dedicated player can farm every piece once per day. The total number of runs required will vary based on luck, but expect to clear the instances for several days to a week to collect the full set. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops wearable by any Rogue, Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering, as these items do not drop on Normal. Farming on multiple leather-wearing characters is the most effective way to bypass the daily lockout and increase your chances. For the Pit of Saron, you can mount up and ride past most trash directly to the final boss. Note that The Culling of Stratholme is a timed roleplaying event that cannot be significantly sped up, and Trial of the Champion is a scripted boss rush. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

378.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is a collection of non-tier pieces from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid. The headpiece drops from the final boss, Kel'Thuzad, in the Frostbrood Lair. The shoulders and legs drop from the final bosses of the Plague and Construct Quarters, Loatheb and Thaddius, respectively. The remaining pieces are scattered across the instance: hands from Heigan the Unclean, chest from Gothik the Harvester, feet from Anub'Rekhan, waist from Grobbulus, and wrists from Maexxna.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any max-level character, as all bosses and trash mobs pose no threat. With eight different pieces dropping from eight unique bosses, a full clear of the required bosses each week should yield the set in approximately 4-8 resets. The official tier version of this set is obtained via Protector tokens, which are shared by Hunters, Shamans, and Warriors. The 10-player and 25-player versions of Naxxramas have separate weekly lockouts and drop different color variations of the set; this blue recolor only drops from the 10-player version.

### Farming Tips
Clear the four outer wings (Arachnid, Plague, Construct, Military) in any order to unlock the teleporter to the final wing for the headpiece. Using a character with high movement speed will clear the instance significantly faster due to the large distances between boss rooms. The non-tier lookalike pieces that make up this set are Bind on Pickup and will be added to your wardrobe automatically upon looting. The actual tier tokens are not automatically collected and must be manually looted and right-clicked to generate the armor piece for your collection.

379.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of mail armor pieces sharing the same model and color scheme, acquired from various 5-player Normal difficulty dungeons in Wrath of the Lich King. The headpiece, Helmet of the Faceless, and wristguards, Bracers of the Herald, both drop from Herald Volazj in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom. The chest, Tunic of the Slinking Spirit, is from Novos the Summoner, and the hands, Handgrips of the Savage Emissary, are from The Prophet Tharon'ja, both located in Drak'Tharon Keep. The belt, Belt of the Impure, drops from Slad'ran, while the feet, Sabatons of the Unchanging, drop from Moorabi, with both bosses found in Gundrak. Finally, the legs, Leggings of the Tireless Sentry, drop from Loken in Halls of Lightning, and the shoulders, Spaulders of the Abomination, drop from Meathook in The Culling of Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Normal difficulty dungeons, which are subject to an instance lockout limit of 10 instances per hour per account. There is no daily or weekly lockout, allowing for repeated farming runs in a single session. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character, as all enemies will be defeated in a single hit. Assuming a standard 15-25% drop chance for each piece, collecting the full 8-piece set may take between 30 and 50 total dungeon clears. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are direct boss drops. Heroic difficulty versions of these dungeons have a separate daily lockout and do not drop the items required for this specific recolor.

### Farming Tips
Always set the dungeon difficulty to Normal, as these specific appearances do not drop on Heroic. To farm efficiently, focus on one dungeon at a time until you acquire all of its associated pieces before moving to the next location. For example, run Drak'Tharon Keep repeatedly until both the chest and hands drop. Use a class with high mobility, such as a Demon Hunter or Monk, to speed up clears. All items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House.

380.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of plate armor pieces that drop from various bosses across different Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Head piece, Faceguard of the Forward Commander, drops from King Ymiron in Utgarde Pinnacle, and the Shoulders, Pauldrons of the Colossus, drop from Sjonnir the Ironshaper in Halls of Stone. The Chestplate of the Solemn Council is obtained from Loken in Halls of Lightning. The Culling of Stratholme is a key location, providing the Gauntlets of the Culling from Mal'Ganis, the Sabatons of the Righteous Defender from Meathook, and the matching Waistguard of the Risen Knight from Salramm the Fleshcrafter. The Legplates of the Violet Champion drop from the final reward chest in Violet Hold, and matching Bracers of the Herald can be found on Keristrasza in The Nexus.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from WotLK Heroic dungeons, which are on a daily dungeon reset lockout. The entire farm is easily soloable at the current max level, with bosses posing no threat to a well-geared character. As the pieces are spread across seven separate instances, collecting the full set will likely take 5-10 days of targeted farming, assuming an average drop rate per piece. There is no tier token system; all pieces are direct drops from their respective bosses and must be looted by a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) to be added to the wardrobe. The Normal and Heroic versions of these dungeons have separate lockouts, but this specific recolor only drops on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance, as these items will not drop on Normal. Using a high-mobility class such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter can significantly reduce the time spent traveling through each dungeon. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House. In The Culling of Stratholme, the timed event can be expedited at max level by quickly clearing the required waves of undead for Arthas to progress.

381.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a plate lookalike of the Warrior Tier 7 armor, obtained from bosses within the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid, the first tier of Wrath of the Lich King. The Head piece drops from Kel'Thuzad and the Shoulder piece drops from Loatheb. The Chest drops from Gluth, the Legs from Thaddius, and the Hands drop from Maexxna. To complete the set, the Feet drop from Grobbulus, the Waist drops from Anub'Rekhan, and the Wrists drop from Gothik the Harvester.

### Farming Logistics
The raid instance Naxxramas operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any max-level plate-wearing class, with all bosses being defeated in a single hit. Due to the number of pieces required, expect to run the instance for 3-6 weeks on average to collect the full set. While this lookalike set drops as direct items, the actual Warrior Tier 7 uses the "Helm/Shoulders/Chest/Legs/Gloves of the Lost Protector" tokens, which are shared between Warriors, Hunters, and Shaman. The 10-player and 25-player versions of Naxxramas are separate lockouts, but this specific blue recolor only drops in the 10-player version.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your raid difficulty is set to 10-player before entering the instance to be eligible for this set's loot table. To clear the instance efficiently, use the teleporter in the central room to access the four main wings, clearing each one fully to unlock the final teleporter to the Frostbrood Lair. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be traded or purchased on the Auction House. No special mechanics or difficult encounters need to be managed when farming at a high level.

383.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike obtained from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid, the entry-level Tier 7 instance in Wrath of the Lich King. The eight pieces of the set drop from specific bosses spread across the four main wings of the instance. The Helm of the Vast Legions and Boots of the Worshiper both drop from Kel'Thuzad. The Pauldrons of Unnatural Death drop from Loatheb, Chestguard of the Exhausted from Gluth, Leggings of the Insatiable from Maexxna, and Gloves of Calculated Risk drop from Gothik the Harvester. The matching waist, Belt of the Tortured, and wrist, Bracers of the Unholy Knight, can drop from multiple bosses throughout the raid.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout. Naxxramas has separate lockouts for 10-player and 25-player modes, and this specific appearance only drops in the 10-player version. The instance is easily soloed by any class at current maximum level. As a non-tier set, the pieces drop directly from bosses and do not use a token system, so there is no need to worry about class token sharing. Given that most bosses drop multiple items, you can expect to collect the full set in approximately 2-4 clears, depending on luck.

### Farming Tips
To speed up the farm, use a character with high mobility to traverse the large wings of the instance. After clearing the first boss of each of the four main quarters, you can use the central teleportation brazier to move between them instantly. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Since this is legacy content, modern systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply. Simply clear all bosses that drop the desired pieces once per week on 10-player difficulty.

384.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Luminous Chevalier's Gallantry plate armor, a lookalike of the Paladin Tier 2 set, obtained from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid on Looking for Raid difficulty. The Head drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer, Shoulders from Lords of Dread, and Chest from Rygelon. Legs are obtained from Lihuvim, Principal Architect, Hands from Anduin Wrynn, and Feet from the Vigilant Guardian. The Waist drops from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, while the Wrists drop from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener.

### Farming Logistics
Sepulcher of the First Ones has a weekly raid lockout per character for each difficulty; LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic are separate lockouts. The entire raid is soloable at current max level on LFR difficulty. Given eight separate pieces, a complete collection will likely take 5-10 weeks of clearing the instance on a single character. This lookalike set drops as direct non-tier items and does not use the tier token system. All pieces drop from the same raid instance, but only on LFR difficulty for this specific golden color.

### Farming Tips
Queue for the four LFR wings of Sepulcher of the First Ones by speaking to the NPC Zo'sorg in Oribos. Since Shadowlands raids utilize personal loot, you must use a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight) for the items to be on your loot table. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Clearing bosses will add their loot to your Great Vault options for the week, providing a supplementary chance to obtain pieces you are missing.

385.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-tier leather armor pieces from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid, located in Dragonblight, Northrend. The head piece drops from the final boss, Kel'Thuzad, in the Frostwyrm Lair. The chest drops from Thaddius and the shoulders drop from Grobbulus, both located in the Construct Quarter. The legs drop from Gothik the Harvester in the Military Quarter, while the hands are sourced from Grand Widow Faerlina in the Arachnid Quarter. The remaining pieces are the waist from Maexxna, wrists from Anub'Rekhan, and feet from Noth the Plaguebringer.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American servers. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level, with all bosses posing no mechanical or damage threat. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per item, collecting all eight pieces will likely take 4-7 resets. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use the tier token system, making it obtainable by any eligible class. Note that 10-player and 25-player Naxxramas have separate lockouts and loot tables; this specific appearance only drops in the 10-player version.

### Farming Tips
You must set the raid difficulty to 10-player to be eligible for this set's appearance. The four main wings can be cleared in any order, but a common efficient path is Plague Quarter, Arachnid Quarter, Military Quarter, then Construct Quarter, which places you near the teleporter to the final bosses. No pieces are Bind on Equip, so the set cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Legacy raid features like bonus rolls or the Great Vault are not applicable to this content.

386.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of epic quality cloth armor pieces that drop from bosses within the Wrath of the Lich King raids Naxxramas (10-player) and The Obsidian Sanctum (10-player). The majority of the set comes from Naxxramas, with the hand slot piece dropping from the single boss in The Obsidian Sanctum. No tier tokens are involved; the items drop directly from the specified bosses and can be equipped by any cloth-wearing class.

*   **Head:** Cowl of the Damned (Kel'Thuzad, Naxxramas)
*   **Shoulder:** Spaulders of the Abomination (Loatheb, Naxxramas)
*   **Chest:** Robes of Mutation (Gluth, Naxxramas)
*   **Legs:** Leggings of Atrophy (Thaddius, Naxxramas)
*   **Hands:** Gloves of Gnarled Beams (Sartharion, The Obsidian Sanctum)
*   **Feet:** Boots of Forlorn Wishes (Maexxna, Naxxramas)
*   **Waist:** Sash of the Parlor (Heigan the Unclean, Naxxramas)
*   **Wrist:** Bindings of the Expansive Mind (Grobbulus, Naxxramas)

### Farming Logistics
Both Naxxramas and The Obsidian Sanctum operate on a weekly raid lockout that resets with the weekly maintenance. The set must be farmed on 10-player difficulty, as the 25-player difficulty drops a different (purple) color variation and the two difficulties have separate lockouts. Both raids can be fully soloed with ease by any class at the current maximum level. This set does not use a tier token system; the pieces drop directly from bosses. Due to the number of pieces spread across multiple bosses, acquiring the full set typically takes 2-6 weekly lockouts, depending on drop luck.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, use a high-mobility class like a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter. In Naxxramas, all four wings (Arachnid, Plague, Military, Construct) must be cleared before you can access the final boss, Kel'Thuzad. The order in which you clear the first four wings does not matter. The hand slot item drops from Sartharion in The Obsidian Sanctum; for the highest chance of receiving loot, defeat Sartharion without killing any of the three Twilight Drakes that are active in the encounter. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be acquired from the Auction House.

387.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific armor collection from the 25-player version of the Naxxramas raid, the entry-level raid of Tier 7. The pieces are direct boss drops and do not use a token system. The Head piece, Cowl of the Liberated, drops from Gluth. The Shoulder, Mantle of the Extensive Mind, drops from Noth the Plaguebringer, while the Chest, Sympathy, drops from Grobbulus. The Legs, Leggings of Mortal Arrogance, are from Sapphiron, and the Hands, Gloves of the Spell-Slinger, are from Gothik the Harvester. To complete the set, the Feet (Boots of the Escaped Captive) drop from Thaddius, the Waist (Leash of Heedless Magic) drops from Heigan the Unclean, and the Wrist (Bindings of the Hapless Prey) drops from Maexxna.

### Farming Logistics
The Naxxramas raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout. This lockout is shared between the 10-player and 25-player difficulties, meaning you can only defeat each boss once per week on a single character. The entire instance is fully soloable at the current maximum level. As the set is comprised of eight pieces from different bosses, collecting the full appearance will likely take between 5-10 weekly resets, depending on drop luck. This set does not use tier tokens, as it is the lookalike version; the actual Priest tier set uses the Protector token, shared with Paladins, Hunters, and Shaman.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your raid difficulty is set to 25-player before entering, as the 10-player version contains a different color scheme for this set. After clearing each of the four main wings (Arachnid, Plague, Military, Construct), use the teleporter in the center of the instance to quickly access the final bosses in the Frostwyrm Lair. To increase your weekly chances, you can run the raid on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock), as the appearances are account-wide once collected. No pieces of this specific set are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

388.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is obtained from non-tier mail armor pieces that drop in the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid on Raid Finder difficulty. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the instance. The Head drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Shoulder drops from The Jailer. The Chest is looted from Rygelon, while the Legs drop from Lihuvim, Principal Architect. The Hands are a drop from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener, the Feet from the Vigilant Guardian, the Waist from Anduin Wrynn, and the Wrists from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid is on a weekly lockout per difficulty, meaning you can clear the Raid Finder version once per character per week. At the current maximum level, the entire instance can be soloed by any class. As the items are direct boss drops from a large loot table, collecting all eight pieces may take 5-10 weekly clears. This set consists of non-tier lookalike pieces, so it does not use the tier token system; items drop directly for your class. Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts and reward different colorations of the set.

### Farming Tips
To farm this specific green coloration, you must run the raid on Raid Finder difficulty. To access past LFR content, speak to the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos, located at the back of the Enclave. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The Shadowlands Great Vault system is no longer active for awarding loot from this raid, so drops are limited to boss kills. The instance is mostly linear, but portals do open after key boss defeats to shorten the travel time back and forth.

389.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier mail lookalike armor from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid, a Tier 7 instance. The headpiece, Cowl of the Frightened, drops from Kel'Thuzad and the shoulders, Shoulderpads of the Fervent, drop from Loatheb. The chest, Tunic of Indulgence, is looted from Gluth, while the leggings, Leggings of the Honored, are obtained from Thaddius. The handguards, Gloves of the Fallen Keeper, drop from Heigan the Unclean, the belt, Belt of the Faint Hope, comes from Gothik the Harvester, and the boots, Boots of the Worshipper, drop from Noth the Plaguebringer.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas operates on a weekly raid lockout, with separate lockouts for the 10-player and 25-player versions, each dropping a different color variant. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level and a full clear can be done on a single character. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct boss drops with a fixed appearance. Assuming an average drop rate for each piece, collecting the full seven-item set will take approximately 3-6 weekly resets. No pieces for this specific blue color variant drop from any other source.

### Farming Tips
Before entering the instance in Dragonblight, Northrend, ensure your Legacy Raid Size is set to "10-Player". After entering, use the central teleporter to move between the four primary wings; clearing the wings in the order of Arachnid, Plague, Military, then Construct is an efficient route to unlock the final bosses. All armor pieces from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. There are no shortcuts that allow you to skip the required bosses, so a partial clear of the instance is necessary each week.

390.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is assembled from pieces that drop in various Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The World-Queller's Vest (Chest) drops from Warchief Kargath Bladefist in Heroic The Shattered Halls and the Helm of the Stalwart Defender (Head) drops from Pathaleon the Calculator in Heroic The Mechanar. The Pauldrons of the Furious Elements (Shoulder) drop from Murmur in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth. The Kilt of the Tides (Legs) and Tide-Stomper's Greaves (Feet) both drop in Heroic The Underbog, from Ghaz'an and Hungarfen respectively. The Grips of the Tides (Hands) drop from Warlord Kalithresh in Heroic The Steamvault, the Girdle of the Tides (Waist) from Mennu the Betrayer in Heroic The Slave Pens, and the World-Queller's Bracers (Wrist) from Broggok in Heroic The Blood Furnace.

### Farming Logistics
The set pieces drop from dungeons on a daily Heroic lockout, allowing one attempt per boss, per character, each day. All required dungeons can be fully soloed at maximum level with trivial difficulty. Since the eight pieces drop from seven different dungeons, collecting the full set requires visiting multiple instances daily. Assuming an average drop chance, expect to run each specific dungeon 10-15 times, which could take several weeks of consistent farming to complete. There is no token-sharing system; all items are direct drops and are bind-on-pickup.

### Farming Tips
All pieces for this set only drop on Heroic difficulty; running on Normal difficulty is ineffective. To enter Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons, you no longer need the original reputation requirements or keys. Many of the required instances are clustered in single locations, such as Coilfang Reservoir for Slave Pens, Underbog, and Steamvault, or Hellfire Citadel for Blood Furnace and Shattered Halls, allowing for efficient travel between runs. Set your Dungeon Difficulty to Heroic (5 player) before entering each instance to ensure you are in the correct mode.

391.content:
### How to Obtain
This Mail armor set is a lookalike of the Hunter tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Founders raid in Shadowlands. The pieces are not class-restricted beyond armor type and drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Head drops from Anduin Wrynn, and the Shoulders drop from the Lords of Dread. The Chest is looted from Rygelon, and the Legs drop from the Vigilant Guardian. To complete the set, the Hands drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer, the Feet from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener, the Waist from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, and the Wrists from Artificer Xy'mox.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout, with each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic) having a separate lockout and a different color tint of the gear. At the current maximum level, the raid is fully soloable on LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties. Mythic difficulty may require a small group for specific mechanics on later bosses like Anduin Wrynn or The Jailer. Assuming an average drop rate, a full set should take approximately 5-10 weekly clears to acquire. While the official Hunter tier set uses the Mystic token (shared with Druids and Mages), these lookalike pieces drop directly as items for Mail wearers.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest solo clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty. After killing the first three bosses, you can use a teleport pad near the entrance of the Dausegne encounter to skip directly to the Lords of Dread wing, and another later to reach Anduin Wrynn, saving significant time. Farm the set on a high-mobility Mail class like a Hunter or Shaman to be eligible for drops and to move through the large instance more quickly. None of the pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Legacy systems like the Great Vault or bonus rolls do not apply to this farm.

392.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate "Battlegear of the Holy Warrior" from the Hellfire Citadel raid in Warlords of Draenor. The gold and blue recolor drops from the Normal difficulty of the instance. Each piece drops from a specific boss: the Waist (Girdle of the Holy Warrior) from Hellfire High Council, the Feet (Sabatons of the Holy Warrior) from Iron Reaver, and the Wrists (Bracers of the Holy Warrior) from Kormrok. The Hands (Gauntlets of the Holy Warrior) drop from Socrethar the Eternal, the Legs (Legplates of the Holy Warrior) from Gorefiend, and the Head (Helm of the Holy Warrior) from Xhul'horac. Finally, the Shoulders (Pauldrons of the Holy Warrior) drop from Tyrant Velhari and the Chest (Breastplate of the Holy Warrior) drops from Mannoroth.

### Farming Logistics
Hellfire Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have a separate lockout, allowing a character to clear all four in one week for four separate chances at loot. The entire raid is fully soloable at maximum level. This set does not use a tier token system; the pieces drop directly as plate gear usable by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. With eight pieces to collect and a 15-25% drop chance, expect the full farm to take approximately 8-12 weeks per difficulty due to RNG.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for the gold and blue color variant. You must be on a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) to loot and learn the appearances. After defeating Gorefiend, a portal opens near the raid entrance that teleports you directly to the upper citadel, saving travel time. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. While the Warlords of Draenor bonus roll currency (Seal of Inevitable Fate) can be used for an extra loot chance if you have any remaining, this currency is no longer obtainable.

393.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of plate armor pieces that drop from various bosses across different Heroic-difficulty dungeons in Wrath of the Lich King. The Head drops from Loken in Halls of Lightning, the Shoulders from Moorabi in Gundrak, and the Hands from Meathook in The Culling of Stratholme. Three pieces are found in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom: the Chest from Prince Taldaram, the Legs from Jedoga Shadowseeker, and the Wrists from Herald Volazj. The Waist drops from Anomalus in The Nexus and the Feet drop from Krik'thir the Gatewatcher in Azjol-Nerub.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from Heroic dungeons, which are subject to a daily reset lockout. All pieces can be easily soloed by any max-level plate-wearing character (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight), and the appearance will be collected for all plate classes on the account. Since the items are spread across six different instances, collecting the full set will require several daily resets due to drop chance. There is no tier token system for this set; pieces are direct boss drops. All items drop from the same type of source, and only Heroic difficulty is relevant for this farm.

### Farming Tips
Set the instance difficulty to Heroic before entering, as these specific items will not drop on Normal. For maximum efficiency, use a high-mobility class to run past non-essential trash packs directly to the required bosses in dungeons like Halls of Lightning and Gundrak. The most time-consuming part of the farm is The Culling of Stratholme, which is a timed roleplaying event that cannot be rushed or skipped. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

394.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of look-alike pieces sourced from various Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Head slot, Cowl of the Dire Wolf, drops from Loken in Halls of Lightning. The Shoulders, Mantle of the Tribunal, drop from Sjonnir The Ironshaper in Halls of Stone. The Chest, Robes of Koegler, is found on Novos the Summoner in Drak'Tharon Keep, while the Legs, Breeches of the Caller, drop from Jedoga Shadowseeker in Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom. The Hands, Gloves of Glistening Runes, are looted from Ormorok the Tree-Shaper in The Nexus. The matching Waist, Plush Cinch of Swiftness, and Feet, Sandals of the Terrestrial Soul, drop from Salramm the Fleshcrafter in The Culling of Stratholme and Cyanigosa in The Violet Hold, respectively.

### Farming Logistics
Each piece is subject to a daily Heroic dungeon lockout per character. All required dungeons can be easily soloed by a max-level character. As the items are spread across seven different dungeons, collecting the full set can take one to three weeks, depending on drop-rate luck. This set does not use a tier token system; gear drops directly from the bosses for any class. All pieces are from separate instances, so you must farm each dungeon individually for its specific slot. Running the dungeon on Normal difficulty will not reward these items and shares the same daily lockout as Heroic.

### Farming Tips
You must run the dungeons on Heroic difficulty, as this is the only mode these specific items drop from. To maximize efficiency, you can run the required dungeons on multiple cloth-wearing characters daily, since appearances are account-wide. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. In The Violet Hold, the waves of enemies before the final bosses cannot be skipped, so be prepared for a 5-10 minute wait. Systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault are not applicable to legacy dungeon content.

395.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Mythic difficulty plate armor from the Sanctum of Domination raid, introduced in Shadowlands Tier 2. The headpiece, the Helm of the Dominated, drops from Kel'Thuzad. The Pauldrons of the Dominated drop from the final boss, Sylvanas Windrunner. The Breastplate of the Dominated drops from The Tarragrue, and the Legplates of the Dominated drop from Fatescribe Roh-Kalo. To complete the set, the Gauntlets of the Dominated drop from Soulrender Dormazain, the matching Girdle of Insatiable Greed drops from The Nine, the Greaves of Unending Agony drop from Painsmith Raznal, and the Bracers of Shared Pain drop from Soulrender Dormazain.

### Farming Logistics
The Sanctum of Domination raid has a weekly raid lockout for each difficulty. Due to boss mechanics and health pools, the Mythic difficulty is not soloable and requires a group of players, typically 5-10, at current max level. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per boss kill, it may take 10-20 weeks to collect all pieces. This set does not use a tier token system; armor drops directly, and Plate items can be traded among Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights in the group. Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the armor.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Mythic difficulty for this specific blue and silver recolor. The most effective method is to form or join a transmog-specific group in the Premade Groups finder. Running with other plate-wearing classes who can trade you items they do not need significantly increases your acquisition rate. There are no Bind on Equip pieces for this set, so it cannot be purchased from the Auction House. After defeating Fatescribe Roh-Kalo, a portal becomes available that allows you to skip directly to Kel'Thuzad.

396.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate lookalike from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid, which is a Tier 7 instance. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the raid's four main wings. The Head drops from Gothik the Harvester in the Military Quarter. The Shoulder drops from Grobbulus in the Construct Quarter. The Chest is sourced from Noth the Plaguebringer in the Plague Quarter, while the Legs drop from Instructor Razuvious in the Military Quarter. The Hands and Feet both drop in the Plague Quarter from Heigan the Unclean, the Waist is from Maexxna in the Arachnid Quarter, and the Wrist comes from Anub'Rekhan, also in the Arachnid Quarter.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday in North America and Wednesday in Europe. The instance is fully soloable by any max-level character, with bosses posing no threat. Collecting all eight pieces will likely take 4-8 runs due to drop rate variance. While the official tier set pieces are purchased with tokens, this lookalike set drops directly, allowing any plate class to collect it. The tier tokens are separated by class: Protector (Warrior, Hunter, Shaman), Conqueror (Paladin, Priest, Warlock), and Vanquisher (Death Knight, Rogue, Druid, Mage). Naxxramas has separate 10-player and 25-player lockouts which award different colored gear; this set requires farming the 10-player version.

### Farming Tips
Ensure you are running the 10-player version of the raid for this specific blue and silver coloration. You must clear all bosses in the Arachnid, Plague, Military, and Construct quarters to unlock the teleporter to the Frostwyrm Lair for the final two bosses, though no pieces for this specific set drop there. After clearing a wing, you can use the central teleporter pad to quickly return to the entrance or move to another wing. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

397.content:
### How to Obtain
This armor set is a collection of non-set pieces that drop from various bosses in Level 80 Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Head, Cowl of the Guiltless, drops from Salramm the Fleshcrafter in The Culling of Stratholme. The Shoulder, Spaulders of the Abomination, and Chest, Tunic of the Souldevourer, also drop in The Culling of Stratholme from Meathook and Mal'Ganis respectively. The Hands, Handgrips of the Foredoomed, are from Ingvar the Plunderer in Utgarde Keep. Loken in Halls of Lightning drops the Legs, Leggings of the Insatiable, while Volkhan in the same dungeon drops the Feet, Boots of the Worshiper. The Waist, Belt of the Tortured, is found on Eck the Ferocious in Gundrak, and the Wrist, Bracers of the Defiled, drops from the Maiden of Grief in Halls of Stone.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed across four separate dungeons, each subject to a daily Heroic lockout per character. All pieces can be easily soloed by a current max-level character. Given the 15-25% drop chance for each piece, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take between 5 to 15 daily clears of the relevant dungeons, depending on luck. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces are direct boss drops and can be learned by any class. The required items only drop from the Heroic difficulty version of these dungeons, not Normal.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance. Using a high-mobility class like a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter will significantly speed up the runs between bosses. The most efficient way to farm this set is to run all four required dungeons (The Culling of Stratholme, Utgarde Keep, Halls of Lightning, Halls of Stone) on multiple characters each day to bypass the daily lockout. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

398.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier leather lookalike from the Icecrown Citadel raid, specifically on 10-player Normal difficulty. All pieces are direct drops from bosses and do not use a token system. The Head, Sanctified Dark Coven Hood, drops from the Blood Prince Council. The Shoulder, Sanctified Dark Coven Shoulderpads, Legs, Sanctified Dark Coven Leggings, Feet, Returning Footfalls, and Wrist, Bracers of Dark Reckoning, all drop from Lord Marrowgar. The Chest, Sanctified Dark Coven Tunic, and Waist, Vengeful Noose, drop from Lady Deathwhisper. The Hands, Sanctified Dark Coven Handwraps, drop from Rotface.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed within Icecrown Citadel, which is on a weekly raid lockout. The instance is fully soloable at max level. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 4-8 weekly resets, as Lord Marrowgar is the source for half of the set. This is a lookalike set, so it does not use the tier token system; all items are direct drops. While Icecrown Citadel has separate lockouts for 10-player, 25-player, Normal, and Heroic difficulties, this specific blue-colored set only drops from 10-player Normal mode.

### Farming Tips
Before entering the instance, right-click your character portrait and set the Legacy Raid Difficulty to "10-Player Normal." To reach Rotface and the Blood Prince Council, you must defeat the first four bosses of the raid. There are no skips to access these wings earlier. All set pieces are Bind on Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter, located in the same wing as Rotface, can be skipped by non-healing classes by simply running past her to the teleporter.

399.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier cosmetic lookalike assembled from items that drop in the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid, with one piece from the 10-player Obsidian Sanctum raid. The Head, `Cowl of the Guiltless`, and Feet, `Boots of the Worshipper`, drop from Kel'Thuzad in Naxxramas. The Shoulder, `Spaulders of the Abomination`, drops from either Loatheb or Gluth, and the Chest, `Tunic of Indulgence`, drops from Gluth. The Legs, `Leggings of the Honored`, drop from Thaddius, while the Waist, `Torn Webbing Grip`, is from Maexxna and the Wrist, `Bindings of the Hapless`, is from Heigan the Unclean. The Hands, `Gloves of Calculated Risk`, are the only piece found elsewhere, dropping from Sartharion in the Obsidian Sanctum.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum each have a separate weekly raid lockout, allowing you to clear both instances once per character per week. The 10-player and 25-player versions of these raids share a single lockout, so you must specifically run the 10-player mode for these items. The entire set is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. Since this set consists of direct boss drops, it does not use a tier token system. Expect to collect all pieces within 1-4 weekly resets due to the large loot tables of the bosses.

### Farming Tips
To collect this set for your wardrobe, you must loot the pieces on a leather-wearing class: Rogue, Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter. In Obsidian Sanctum, the `Gloves of Calculated Risk` have a specific drop condition and only appear when Sartharion is defeated with exactly one of the three Twilight Drakes left alive. The Naxxramas wings can be cleared in any order to unlock the final bosses, and no skips are available. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

400.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased from the vendor Lhara at the Darkmoon Faire for a total of 475 Darkmoon Prize Tickets. The Faire is active for one week each month, beginning on the first Sunday. The individual pieces are Head (Fanciful Corsage), Shoulder (Fanciful Plumage), Chest (Fanciful Tunic), and Legs (Fanciful Leggings) for 75 tickets each. The Hands (Fanciful Gloves), Feet (Fanciful Slippers), and Waist (Fanciful Sash) cost 50 tickets each, while the Wrist (Fanciful Bracers) costs 25 tickets.

### Farming Logistics
Farming is time-gated by the Darkmoon Faire's monthly one-week-long availability. All activities to earn Darkmoon Prize Tickets are fully soloable by any character. Collecting the full 475 tickets will likely take 2-3 separate visits to the Faire for a player completing the daily mini-games and monthly profession quests on a single character. This set does not use a tier token system as it is purchased directly with currency. The appearance is the same regardless of your character's class or armor type.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate ticket acquisition, complete all daily mini-games and monthly profession quests. The most efficient method is using multiple alternate characters to farm the Darkmoon Adventurer's Guide quest items (e.g., Soothsayer's Runes, A Treatise on Strategy) from dungeons, raids, and battlegrounds. These quest items are Bind on Account and can be mailed to a single character for turn-in, making it possible to acquire all necessary tickets in one Faire week. All set pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

401.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is obtained from activities related to the Battle for Stromgarde Warfront in Arathi Highlands. Individual pieces are not tied to specific bosses, but are instead awarded randomly from several sources. The main methods are completing the Warfront scenario itself for a guaranteed item reward per cycle and defeating the rare elite creatures located throughout the Arathi Highlands zone when your faction has control. The zone's world boss (Doom's Howl for Alliance, The Lion's Roar for Horde) also has a chance to drop pieces, as does the one-time introductory quest for the Warfront. All main armor slots are available: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist.

### Farming Logistics
The acquisition of this set is governed by multiple, non-standard lockouts. The Warfront scenario reward is available once per cycle (roughly every two weeks). The world boss is on a weekly raid lockout, and the rare elites in the Arathi Highlands zone are on a daily lockout. The entire process, including the scenario and outdoor elites, can be fully soloed at maximum level. Due to the various lockouts and random nature of drops, collecting all eight pieces can take several Warfront cycles. There is no tier token system, and the Warfront has only a single difficulty, separate from standard raid lockouts.

### Farming Tips
For fastest results, kill every rare elite in Arathi Highlands once per day whenever your faction controls the zone; addons like HandyNotes can map their locations. You can queue for the Warfront scenario from the War Table in Boralus (Alliance) or Port of Zandalar (Horde) when the battle is active for your faction. Since the appearances are account-wide, you can farm the rares and complete the scenario on multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) to accelerate collection. All items from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

402.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from pieces that drop in various Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The Head drops from Warlord Kalithresh in Heroic The Steamvault. The Shoulders are dropped by Grandmaster Vorpil and the Chest is dropped by Murmur, both located in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth. The Legs drop from Hungarfen and the Hands drop from The Black Stalker, both bosses within Heroic The Underbog. The set is incomplete and does not include matching Waist, Wrist, or Feet pieces, which must be sourced from other locations to complete the look.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these set pieces are all on a daily Heroic lockout, meaning you can attempt to loot each boss once per day per character. The content is from the Burning Crusade expansion and can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Due to the daily lockout and drop chances, expect to spend 5-10 daily resets farming the necessary instances to collect all five core pieces. There is no tier token system for this set; pieces drop directly from the bosses. Normal and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, but these specific items only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering to be eligible for the loot. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Monk or Demon Hunter, will significantly speed up clears as you can run directly to the target bosses. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House. Since the instances are trivial in difficulty, the main limiting factor is the once-per-day lockout for each required dungeon.

403.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of non-tier plate armor pieces from the Naxxramas raid in its 10-player Normal difficulty setting. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the instance. The Head, `Plated Facemask of Hope`, drops from Gluth. The Shoulders, `Shoulderplates of the Inevitable`, are from Loatheb. The Chest, `Breastplate of the Piercing Abyss`, is looted from The Four Horsemen. The Legs, `Legplates of Inescapable Death`, drop from Thaddius, while the Hands, `Gauntlets of the Master's Command`, come from Maexxna. The Feet, `Boots of the Worshipper`, are from Anub'Rekhan; the Waist, `Belt of the Impaler`, drops from Grobbulus; and the Wrist, `Bracers of the Unholy Knight`, is from Instructor Razuvious.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms. The entire instance is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, a full collection could take between 5 and 10 weekly resets, though this is subject to random luck. This set does not use a tier token system; the pieces drop directly as items usable by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The 10-player and 25-player versions of Naxxramas share a lockout but have separate loot tables with different colorations, so you must clear the 10-player version for this specific set.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your raid difficulty is set to "Legacy 10-Player Normal" before entering the instance to be eligible for this specific blue recolor. To speed up farming, use multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight), as each one has its own separate weekly lockout. After clearing the first boss of each wing, you can use the central teleportation orb to quickly move between the different wings of the raid. All items in this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

404.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set plate items that drop from various bosses inside Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Head, Faceguard of the Hammer Clan, drops from Krystallus in Heroic Halls of Stone. Shoulders, Pauldrons of the Colossus, drop from Skadi the Ruthless and Hands, Gauntlets of the Stouthearted Crusader, drop from Gortok Palehoof, both in Heroic Utgarde Pinnacle. The Chest, Breastplate of the Solace, drops from Loken in Heroic Halls of Lightning, and the Legs, Legplates of the Immortal Spider, drop from Moorabi in Heroic Gundrak. The Feet, Sabaton of the Watcher, drop from Cyanigosa, and the Waist, Girdle of Ripped Space, drops from Xevozz, both in Heroic The Violet Hold. Finally, the Wrists, Bracers of the Lonesome Road, are in the chest after defeating Mal'Ganis in Heroic Culling of Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
The instances are all subject to a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, meaning you can attempt each boss once per day per character. The entire set can be easily soloed by a max-level character, with bosses dying in one hit. There is no tier token system; all pieces are direct drops from specific bosses. Because the pieces drop from seven different dungeons, collecting the full set requires visiting each instance daily. Assuming a 20% drop rate, a player could expect to complete the set in 4-7 days of targeted farming, though this is subject to random luck.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering, as these pieces do not drop on Normal. Using a class with high mobility, such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter, will significantly speed up the process of moving between and through the dungeons. All the pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No special skips are required, as a max-level character can run straight to each boss, ignoring or quickly eliminating all trash mobs.

405.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-tier cloth armor pieces from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas raid, introduced in Tier 7 of the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The Head drops from Grobbulus, Shoulders from Instructor Razuvious, and Chest from Noth the Plaguebringer. The Legs are obtained from Heigan the Unclean, while the Hands drop from Gothik the Harvester. To complete the set, the matching Waist drops from Maexxna, Feet from Anub'Rekhan, and Wrists from Grand Widow Faerlina.

### Farming Logistics
The Naxxramas raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The instance can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 2-4 full clears. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use the tier token system. The 10-player and 25-player versions of Naxxramas share a single lockout, so you must ensure your raid difficulty is set to 10-player before your first kill of the week to be eligible for this specific gear's appearance.

### Farming Tips
Set your raid difficulty to "Legacy 10-Player" by right-clicking your character portrait before entering the instance. After defeating the final boss of each of the four main wings, a teleporter pad in the central chamber will activate, allowing for quick travel back to the entrance of that wing. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House. No bonus rolls or other modern systems are available to supplement loot drops from this content.

406.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is the Look-Alike version of the Warlock Tier 15 LFR set, obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid instance from Tier 15. The pieces drop from specific bosses within the Looking For Raid difficulty. The Head (Hood of the Final Banishment) drops from the Twin Consorts. The Shoulders (Shoulders of the Haunted Priestess) drop from Iron Qon. The Chest (Robes of the Exorcist) drops from Dark Animus. The Hands (Handwraps of the Risen Soul) drop from the Council of Elders, the Legs (Leggings of the Undulating Brood) drop from Ji-Kun, and the Feet (Sandals of Leaping Puritain) drop from Tortos. The matching Waist (Girdle of the Foaming Flea) drops from Megaera, and the Wrist (Cuffs of the In-Between) drops from Durumu the Forgotten.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout. You can kill each boss once per week per character for a chance at loot. The entire raid can be soloed by any class at maximum level. To access the LFR version of Throne of Thunder, speak to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above the Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. As the set is comprised of eight separate pieces, expect the full farm to take approximately 5-10 weekly resets per character. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility to speed up the run between bosses. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls can be used on bosses for an extra chance at loot; the currency is Elder Charm of Good Fortune, which can be farmed on the Timeless Isle. The instance is split into four wings when queuing via Lorewalker Han, so you can target specific wings if you only need items from certain bosses. No pieces are Bind on Equip, so the set cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

407.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Tier 7 Mage "Frostfire Garb" and drops from bosses within the 25-player version of the Naxxramas raid, a Tier 7 instance. The purple and red pieces drop from specific bosses spread across the instance's five wings. Head: Cowl of the Absurd (Gothik the Harvester) or Cowl of Vanity (Grobbulus). Shoulder: Mantle of the Corrupted (Loatheb). Chest: The Sanctimonious Finery (Maexxna). Legs: Leggings of the Instructor (Instructor Razuvious). Hands: Handgrips of Turmoil (Maexxna). Feet: Boots of Forlorn Wishes (Gothik the Harvester). Waist: Leash of Heedless Magic (Heigan the Unclean). Wrist: Bracers of the Tyrant (Noth the Plaguebringer).

### Farming Logistics
The Naxxramas raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout. Both the 10-player and 25-player versions can be completed once per character per week, and they have separate lockouts with different colored loot. This specific set is entirely farmable solo by any class at maximum level. While these lookalike pieces are direct boss drops, the actual Mage tier set uses the Vanquisher token system, shared with Death Knights, Druids, and Rogues. With multiple bosses dropping pieces for the head, legs, and hands slots, collecting the full eight-piece set should take approximately 2-4 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, run the instance on 25-player Normal difficulty, as there is no Heroic mode for Naxxramas. Use a high-mobility class like a Demon Hunter or Druid to navigate the large wings of the instance more quickly. There are no skips; the first four wings must be fully cleared to access the Frostwyrm Lair. These items are Bind on Pickup and not available on the Auction House. Unlike tier tokens, these specific items must be of your character's primary armor type (cloth) to be automatically added to your wardrobe upon looting.

408.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate set is obtained from the 5-player dungeon, Trial of the Champion, on Heroic difficulty, located in the Crusaders' Coliseum in northeastern Icecrown. The Head and Legs drop from the final boss, The Black Knight. The Shoulders drop from the second boss, which can be either Eadric the Pure or Argent Confessor Paletress. The Chest, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist pieces all drop from the Grand Champions' Cache that appears after defeating the first encounter.

### Farming Logistics
The instance has a daily dungeon reset for Heroic difficulty. It can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Given the multiple pieces that can drop from the first encounter's chest, a full set collection can take between 5 to 15 daily runs, assuming an average drop chance per piece. This set does not use a tier token system; all items are direct boss drops. Normal and Heroic difficulties have different loot tables, and these specific items only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
You must run the dungeon on Heroic difficulty as the Normal difficulty version does not contain this set. For the initial jousting event, target the enemy NPCs' mounts to dismount them quickly, then defeat the riders on foot. All items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. There are no skips available in this linear dungeon, requiring all three encounters to be defeated for a chance at all pieces.

409.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Looking For Raid recolor of Shaman Tier 13 armor, obtained from the Dragon Soul raid instance. The primary set pieces are acquired using tier tokens that drop from bosses on LFR difficulty. The Helm token drops from Warmaster Blackhorn, the Shoulder token from Hagara the Stormbinder, and the Chest token from Ultraxion. The Legs token is looted from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, and the Hands token comes from Warlord Zon'ozz. Matching non-set Feet, the Treads of Dormant Dreams, also drop from Hagara the Stormbinder, while a matching Waist, the Girdle of the Indomitable, drops from the Madness of Deathwing encounter.

### Farming Logistics
Dragon Soul has a weekly raid lockout per character, meaning you can attempt to get each piece once per week. The LFR difficulty has its own distinct lockout, separate from Normal, Heroic, or Mythic difficulties, and this specific color only drops in LFR. The raid is fully soloable at the current maximum level. The set uses the "Corrupted Protector" tier token, which is shared among Hunter, Shaman, and Mage classes. With an average drop rate, expect to spend 5-10 weeks farming the five core token pieces.

### Farming Tips
To access the Looking For Raid version, you must speak to the NPC Auridormi in the Caverns of Time in Tanaris. After killing the first boss, you can use the portals at the base of Wyrmrest Temple to teleport directly to the later wings of the raid to save time. Tier tokens must be right-clicked in your inventory to create the actual armor item before the appearance will be added to your collection. No bonus roll system is active for this content, and the main set pieces are Bind on Pickup, so they cannot be traded or bought on the Auction House.

410.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty cloth armor from the Hellfire Citadel raid in Tanaan Jungle. The Head, Shadow-Grynne Hood, and Feet, Dessicated Soulrender Slippers, both drop from Gorefiend. The Shoulder, Amice of the Convoker, and Waist, Belt of Arcane Storms, drop from Xhul'horac. The Chest, Demon-Etched Robes, drops from Mannoroth, and the Legs, Leggings of the Iron Summoner, drop from Tyrant Velhari. Finally, the Hands, Gloves of the Void-Gazer, drop from Socrethar the Eternal, and the Wrist, Bracers of the Banished, drop from Kormrok.

### Farming Logistics
Hellfire Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout per difficulty, meaning you can clear Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic once per character each week for four separate chances at loot. The entire raid is fully soloable at maximum level on all difficulties. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces drop directly as cloth items usable by Mages, Priests, and Warlocks. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 4-8 weekly clears on a single character. All pieces drop within the Hellfire Citadel raid instance.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty specifically for this purple and blue recolor. After defeating the first three bosses, use the portals that appear in the central area to quickly navigate to the later wings of the raid to save time. The initial Hellfire Assault encounter can be expedited by using the cannons to destroy demolishers and large groups of enemies. Warlords of Draenor bonus rolls are no longer available for this content, and none of these specific set pieces are Bind on Equip to be purchased from the Auction House.

411.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather lookalike set is obtained from bosses within the Throne of Thunder raid, introduced in Tier 15. The specific green color version of this set drops from the Normal difficulty of the instance. The set consists of eight main pieces, each dropping from a specific boss encounter within the raid.

*   **Head:** Hood of the Blind Seer, drops from Jin'rokh the Breaker.
*   **Shoulder:** Spaulders of the Unblinking Eye, drops from Durumu the Forgotten.
*   **Chest:** Chestguard of the Unblinking Eye, also drops from Durumu the Forgotten.
*   **Hands:** Gloves of the Unblinking Eye, drops from Megaera.
*   **Legs:** Leggings of the Unblinking Eye, drops from Iron Qon.
*   **Feet:** Boots of the Still Breath, drops from Tortos.
*   **Waist:** Waistguard of the All-Consuming Maw, drops from Horridon.
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of the Midnight Comet, drops from the Twin Consorts.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder is on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for NA realms and Wednesday for EU realms. The raid is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single weekly lockout, but the Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty has its own separate lockout, allowing for an additional chance at different-colored pieces each week. This set is comprised of direct item drops and does not use a tier token system. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per item, a full set will take approximately 5-10 weekly clears to collect. These items are leather and can only be collected and transmogged by Druids, Rogues, Monks, and Demon Hunters.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific green color variant; LFR and Heroic difficulties award different color palettes. Players can use an Elder Charm of Good Fortune for a bonus roll on each boss, providing an extra chance at loot. These charms can be acquired by turning in Lesser Charms of Good Fortune at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms or on the Isle of Thunder. To speed up clears, use a high-mobility class and follow the intended boss order, as there are no major sequence skips. None of the pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

412.content:
### How to Obtain
This set, named the "Regalia of the Dashing Scoundrel," was awarded from the Shadowlands meta-achievement "Fates of the Shadowlands Raids." Earning this required completing all three Shadowlands raids—Castle Nathria, Sanctum of Domination, and Sepulcher of the First Ones—on at least Normal difficulty during Season 4 while they were "Fated." The reward was a single ensemble item that taught the entire appearance at once. This achievement and the associated Fated raid system were exclusive to the end of the Shadowlands expansion and are no longer available in the game.

### Farming Logistics
This cosmetic set is no longer obtainable and cannot be farmed. When it was available, acquisition was tied to a one-time achievement completion, not a recurring farm with a lockout. It required a full raid group to complete the necessary raids on Normal difficulty or higher. The set was a guaranteed reward from the achievement and was not subject to drop-chance RNG or a token system. Since the Fated system has been removed, there is no way to complete the prerequisite achievement.

### Farming Tips
As this set is unobtainable, no farming tips apply. The ensemble and its appearances are Bind-on-Pickup and have never been available on the Auction House.
