Players seeking a similar appearance can farm the original red Skyshatter Regalia (Shaman-only) from Black Temple and the Battle for Mount Hyjal raids. A blue recolor, also Shaman-only, can be acquired from the 10-player version of the Trial of the Crusader raid.

413.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Sunwell Plateau Tier 6 armor and is obtained from the Ulduar raid in 10-player difficulty. The Head, `Helm of the Furnace Master`, and Hands, `Gloves of the Stonemason`, drop from Mimiron. The Shoulder, `Shoulderpads of the Monolith`, drops from Thorim, while the Legs, `Leggings of the Lifetender`, drop from Freya. The Chest, `Chestguard of the Fallen God`, is looted from the final boss, Yogg-Saron. The matching Waist, `Belt of the Tenebrous Mist`, drops from General Vezax; the Feet, `Boots of the Petrified Forest`, also drop from Freya; and the Wrist, `Bracers of the Smothering Inferno`, drop from Ignis the Furnace Master.

### Farming Logistics
Ulduar operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms. The instance is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Given the number of pieces spread across different bosses, it will likely take 3-7 weekly resets to collect the full set, depending on drop luck. This set does not use the tier token system; the items drop directly as named gear for any class to loot. The 10-player and 25-player versions of Ulduar have separate lockouts, but you must run the 10-player difficulty for these specific appearance pieces.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as this is the only source for this specific color variation. Use the teleporters that become active after defeating bosses in each quadrant to navigate the instance quickly. For the fastest clear, mount up and use the vehicle section to bypass trash and travel directly to the Antechamber of Ulduar. None of the pieces are Bind-on-Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Do not activate any "hard modes," such as engaging Freya with her three Elders alive, as this unnecessarily complicates the encounters.

414.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a combination of tier pieces and non-tier items from three distinct raids in The Burning Crusade: Hyjal Summit, Black Temple, and Sunwell Plateau. The five main tier pieces drop as tokens which can be turned in at vendors in Hyjal Summit or on the Isle of Quel'Danas. The Head token drops from Archimonde in Hyjal Summit and the Hands token drops from Azgalor in the same raid. The Shoulder token drops from Mother Shahraz, the Legs token from The Illidari Council, and the Chest token from Illidan Stormrage, all within the Black Temple. The remaining pieces are direct drops from Sunwell Plateau: Wrist from Kalecgos, Waist from Brutallus, and Feet from Felmyst.

### Farming Logistics
The three source raids—Hyjal Summit, Black Temple, and Sunwell Plateau—each have a separate weekly raid lockout. All three raids are easily soloable by any class at current maximum level. Collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 4-8 weeks, as you must clear specific bosses across three different instances each week. The five main armor pieces use the "Fallen Defender" tier token, which is shared by Priests and Warriors. The off-set pieces from Sunwell Plateau are cloth items that can be looted directly by any class but can only be learned by cloth wearers (Mage, Priest, Warlock).

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear of Black Temple, use the teleporter behind the main entrance after defeating Supremus to skip directly to the Temple Summit. Farming on a Priest is most efficient as you are eligible for the tier tokens and all direct cloth drops, but a Warrior can also collect the tier tokens to unlock the appearance account-wide. The tier tokens themselves are not automatically added to your wardrobe; you must take them to a vendor in Hyjal Summit or on the Isle of Quel'Danas and purchase the actual gear piece to learn the appearance.

415.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cloth lookalike of the Mage Tier 6 "Tempest Regalia" and is obtained from bosses within the two Cataclysm-era 5-player Heroic dungeons, Zul'Gurub and Zul'Aman. The Head ("Head-Dresser's Mask") drops from Bloodlord Mandokir in Zul'Gurub. The Shoulders ("Ritual-Leader's Shoulderguards") drop from High Priestess Kilnara in Zul'Gurub. The Chest ("Robes of the Unraveler") drops from Daakara in Zul'Aman. The Legs ("Leggings of the Betrayed") drop from Hex Lord Malacrass in Zul'Aman, and the Hands ("Soul-Strung Gloves") drop from Zanzil in Zul'Gurub. The matching Feet ("Slippers of the Betrayed") drop from Halazzi in Zul'Aman, the Waist ("Voodoo-Laced Girdle") from Zanzil, and the Wrist ("Spirit-Bindings") from Jan'alaj in Zul'Aman.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, meaning you can complete each dungeon for a chance at loot once per day per character. The dungeons can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance for each item, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely require 10-20 total runs across both dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops from their respective bosses. The Cataclysm versions of these dungeons only have a Heroic difficulty, which is the required setting to obtain this gear.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering, as Normal difficulty does not exist for the level 85 versions of these instances. At max level, all bosses and trash can be defeated in seconds, making clears very fast. You can use mounts for a significant portion of Zul'Gurub to travel between bosses quickly. The timed bear mount event in Zul'Aman is not required for farming these armor pieces and can be ignored. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

416.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Castle Nathria raid, the first raid tier of the Shadowlands expansion. The eight primary pieces of the set drop as direct, personal loot from specific bosses within the instance. The Helm is dropped by Sire Denathrius, and the Shoulders are dropped by the Stone Legion Generals. The Chest armor drops from Sludgefist, while the Leggings drop from Artificer Xy'mox. The Gloves can be looted from the Hungering Destroyer, the Boots from Huntsman Altimor, the Waist from Shriekwing, and the Wrists from Sun King's Salvation.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms and Wednesday for European realms. At the current maximum level, the entire raid is soloable on LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, a player can expect to complete the set in approximately 10-15 weekly clears, though this can vary significantly with luck. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces are direct drops for cloth-wearing classes. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout and loot tables, allowing up to four chances per boss each week.

### Farming Tips
For the most efficient farming, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as it provides the fastest clear time for a solo player with a high drop rate. There are no major skips available in Castle Nathria, so you must defeat the bosses in a linear order. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Mage, can speed up the time spent moving between boss encounters. Some non-set cloth items with a similar appearance may drop from trash mobs as Bind-on-Equip items, which can occasionally be found for sale on the Auction House.

417.content:
### How to Obtain
This set drops from the Sanctum of Domination raid, a tier in the Shadowlands expansion. Pieces are obtained as personal loot drops from specific bosses within the instance. The Head token drops from Kel'Thuzad, and the Shoulder token drops from Fatescribe Roh-Kalo. The Chest piece is looted from Soulrender Dormazain, while the Legs are a reward from The Nine. Hands drop from the Remnant of Ner'zhul, and the Feet are found on Painsmith Raznal. The matching Waist drops from the Guardian of the First Ones and the Wrists can be looted from The Tarragrue.

### Farming Logistics
The Sanctum of Domination raid is on a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can attempt to get loot from each boss once per week per difficulty. The instance can be fully soloed by a max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-20% per boss, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 8-12 weekly resets, though this is subject to random luck. This set does not use a tier token system; instead, class-specific armor pieces drop directly from bosses. Since LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate weekly lockouts, you can run the raid up to four times a week for more chances at the set's different color variations.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as bosses have low health and mechanics can be ignored by a high-level character. Since the set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Shadowlands raids do not feature a bonus roll system, but completing bosses can provide options in the Great Vault the following week, which may include pieces you are missing. There are no major skips in the instance, but a high-mobility class can significantly speed up the transit time between the large boss rooms.

418.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased as a single item, the "Ensemble: Gronnstalker's Sunwell Battlegear", from the Trading Post vendors. The vendors are T&W (Tawney and Wilder) in Stormwind and the Zen'shiri Trading Post in Orgrimmar. The ensemble was available for 850 Trader's Tender during the month of May 2023. Trader's Tender is earned by completing monthly activities in the Traveler's Log or as a monthly grant for maintaining an active subscription. The ensemble teaches the appearances for all eight armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Waist, Legs, Feet, Hands) and a matching Cloak.

### Farming Logistics
The Trading Post vendor's stock rotates on a monthly basis, so there is no traditional raid or dungeon lockout. Earning the required Trader's Tender is an account-wide, solo activity that does not require a group. Since the set was only available during a specific month, players who missed it must wait for the ensemble to return to the rotating monthly stock. There are no tier tokens involved as this is a cosmetic set available to all classes and armor types. The entire appearance is learned from a single ensemble item, so there is no need to collect individual pieces.

### Farming Tips
To acquire the necessary Trader's Tender, focus on completing the easiest and fastest objectives in the Traveler's Log each month. The ensemble item and Trader's Tender currency are account-bound and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. If the set returns to the Trading Post and you lack the currency, you can "freeze" the item, which keeps it available for purchase in a special slot in subsequent months until you buy it. Check the Trading Post inventory at the start of each calendar month to see if the ensemble has returned.

419.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set was a reward from the Mage Tower, which was active during The Burning Crusade Timewalking event weeks in the Shadowlands expansion. To earn the ensemble, a player had to complete any of the seven available Mage Tower challenges on a Shaman character. Upon successfully finishing the challenge, the entire eight-piece cosmetic set was awarded at once via an achievement. The set includes cosmetic appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Cloak, rather than individual gear pieces that drop from bosses.

### Farming Logistics
The Mage Tower had no lockout and could be attempted an unlimited number of times, but it was only available for the one-week duration of the TBC Timewalking event. This was a mandatory solo challenge; groups were not permitted to enter or assist. Only one successful run on a Shaman was needed to unlock the set for the entire account. This set was a direct cosmetic reward and did not use the tier token system, so no other classes shared a token. All pieces of the set were awarded from this single achievement source.

### Farming Tips
This specific cosmetic armor set is no longer obtainable, as it was a reward exclusive to the Shadowlands version of the Mage Tower. During the event, players often assembled specialized Timewalking gear sets with pieces from older expansions that had many gem sockets to gain an advantage. Because it was a cosmetic reward tied to an achievement, the appearances were automatically added to the Wardrobe upon completion. The set is not composed of Bind on Equip items and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

420.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a plate armor lookalike of the Paladin Tier 6 Lightbringer Armor, obtained from bosses in Heroic-difficulty Cataclysm dungeons. The Head, Helm of the Proud, drops from High Priestess Azil in The Stonecore. The Shoulders, Pauldrons of the Forlorn, are looted from General Umbriss in Grim Batol. The Chest, Breastplate of the Witness, drops from Siamat in The Lost City of the Tol'vir. The Legs, Greaves of Splendor, are from Rajh in Halls of Origination. The Hands, Gauntlets of the Disturbed, also drop in The Lost City of the Tol'vir from the boss Augh. The Feet, Boots of the Sullen Hart, come from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides, the Waist, Belt of the Ferocious Wolf, is from Drahga Shadowburner in Grim Batol, and the Wrists, Bracers of the Dark Mother, are from Corla, Herald of Twilight in Blackrock Caverns.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed across multiple instances, each with a daily Heroic dungeon lockout. All required dungeons can be fully soloed at maximum level. Due to the pieces being spread across six different daily lockouts, collecting the full eight-piece set will likely take 7-14 days of consistent farming, assuming a 15-25% drop chance per item. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct boss drops that can be looted by any plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). The items for this appearance only drop on Heroic difficulty, which is a separate lockout from the Normal difficulty version of the dungeons.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic before entering each instance to be eligible for the loot. Using a high-mobility class like a Demon Hunter, Monk, or Druid will significantly speed up the time it takes to travel through the dungeons to each boss. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House. There are no major skips required, as all relevant bosses are on the main path of each linear dungeon.

421.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a look-alike of the Warrior Tier 6 Onslaught Battlegear and is obtained from the Tier 15 raid, Throne of Thunder. All pieces drop from bosses within the Normal difficulty of the instance. The Head (Crown of the Golden Golem) and Shoulders (Pauldrons of the Golden Golem) drop from the Twin Consorts. The Chest (Carapace of the Golden Golem) and Legs (Legplates of the Golden Golem) drop from Iron Qon. The Hands (Gauntlets of the Golden Golem) are dropped by Ji-Kun, the Feet (Sabatons of the Golden Golem) drop from Tortos, the Waist (Girdle of the Golden Golem) drops from Durumu the Forgotten, and the Wrists (Bracers of the Golden Golem) drop from Megaera.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces and bosses, expect to make 4-8 runs on average to collect the full set. This set does not use a tier token system; each piece drops directly as an item. Note that while any class can collect the appearance, it is a Plate set and can only be transmogged by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The LFR (green), Normal (gold), and Heroic (purple) versions of this set are different colors and must be collected from their respective difficulties; Normal and Heroic difficulties share a lockout, while LFR has its own separate lockout.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear times, use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter, to navigate the large instance. You can use Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) on bosses for an additional chance at loot. The raid has a linear progression with no major skips, but teleporters unlock within the instance as you defeat key bosses. To increase your weekly chances, farm the instance on multiple plate-wearing characters, as the appearances are account-wide. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

422.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is acquired from the Tier 3 Arathi Warfront armor. The primary source for all eight pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) is the cache awarded for completing the quest "Warfront: The Battle for Stromgarde" when the Arathi Highlands Warfront is active for your faction. Each quest completion rewards a cache containing a single, random piece of gear from this set. Additionally, the Arathi Highlands world boss (Doom's Howl for Alliance, The Lion's Roar for Horde) has a chance to drop pieces, but only when your faction controls the zone. This set is a recolor of the no-longer-obtainable BFA S1 Elite Dread Gladiator's PvP armor.

### Farming Logistics
The Warfront quest is on a cyclical lockout, becoming available once per character every few weeks, rather than a standard weekly raid or daily dungeon reset. The associated world boss is on a weekly lockout. The Warfront scenario itself is not soloable and requires a group, which is formed automatically via the queue system; at max level, the content is trivial. Due to receiving only one guaranteed random piece from the quest per cycle, collecting the full 8-piece set can take a minimum of 8 cycles, but expect 10-20 cycles due to duplicate drops. This set does not use a token system, and there are no separate LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic versions of this armor source.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate the collection process, complete the Warfront quest on multiple leather-wearing characters (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) during each active cycle, as the appearances are account-wide. There are no skips or shortcuts within the Warfront scenario. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Monitor the War Table in Boralus or Dazar'alor to know when the contribution phase or active queue for the Arathi Warfront begins for your faction.

423.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic plate collection "Regalia of the Sanctified Mawsworn" from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. Each piece drops directly from a specific boss within the instance. The Head drops from Anduin Wrynn, and the Shoulders drop from Rygelon. The Chest is looted from The Jailer, while the Legs come from Lihuvim, Principal Architect. The Hands are a drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer, the Feet drop from Vigilant Guardian, the Waist comes from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, and the Wrist is obtained from Artificer Xy'mox.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per difficulty for each character. The Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts and drop different color variations of the set, allowing for four attempts per character per week. At the current maximum level, the instance is fully soloable on all difficulties up to and including Heroic. This set is a direct cosmetic drop and does not use the tier token system, so the items can be looted and learned by any class. Expect to complete 10-20 runs per difficulty to acquire the full 8-piece set due to random drop chances.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as it provides a straightforward solo experience without the need for LFR queues. The raid layout is linear, and there are no significant skips to bypass bosses required for the set pieces. These cosmetic items are Bind-on-Pickup and will be automatically added to your account-wide Wardrobe regardless of your character's armor proficiency. The Great Vault and bonus roll systems from Shadowlands are no longer active for this content, so drops come exclusively from boss kills.

424.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set, the Ensemble: Devotion's Rites, is purchased from Vindicator Jaela, the Enlightened Quartermaster located in Haven, Zereth Mortis. The ensemble becomes available for purchase upon reaching Exalted reputation with The Enlightened faction. There is no currency cost; it is a direct reward for achieving the maximum reputation level. Purchasing and using the ensemble grants the appearances for all armor slots: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set is not subject to a lockout but is time-gated by reputation gains. All content required to farm reputation with The Enlightened can be fully soloed at current max level. Reaching Exalted from Neutral will take approximately three to four weeks of consistent play, as the largest sources of reputation are from daily and weekly quests. There are no tier tokens involved, and the entire set comes from a single source. No part of the set drops from a different location or raid difficulty.

### Farming Tips
To gain reputation efficiently, complete the entire Zereth Mortis campaign first, as this unlocks all daily and world quests. Prioritize the weekly quest "Patterns Within Patterns," which provides the largest single gain of reputation. Additionally, defeat rare mobs and loot one-time treasures throughout Zereth Mortis each day, as they also grant reputation. A Contract: The Enlightened can be used to gain a small amount of reputation while completing world quests in other Shadowlands zones, but focusing on Zereth Mortis activities is faster. The ensemble is Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

425.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather set is a non-class lookalike of the Druid Tier 14 set, obtained from the Heart of Fear raid in Mists of Pandaria. All pieces drop directly from bosses on Normal difficulty. The Head drops from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, Shoulders from Blade Lord Ta'yak, and Hands from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok. The Chest drops from Garalon, the Legs drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, and the Feet drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The matching Waist is Girdle of the Mutilated from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, and the matching Wrists are Bracers of the Sudden Reaction from Garalon.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear raid has a weekly lockout, resetting with the weekly maintenance. The instance is fully soloable at max level. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item, collecting the full 6-piece set will take approximately 5-10 weekly clears depending on luck. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use the tier token system. Looking For Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout and drop different color variations of this armor model; this specific version drops from Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for this specific blue coloration; this is also faster than Heroic due to lower boss health. The raid is linear with no significant skips, but you must defeat the three minibosses in the large central chamber to activate the encounter with Wind Lord Mel'jarak. Bonus rolls (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) are not relevant for farming transmog from this content. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

426.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather armor set is a non-tier recolor obtained from the Heart of Fear raid, the Tier 14 instance in Mists of Pandaria. The Head, Crown of the Humbled, drops from the final boss, Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs, and Wrist pieces drop from specific bosses: Shoulderpads of the Unseen (Wind Lord Mel'jarak), Vest of the Unseen (Amber-Shaper Un'sok), Gloves of the Unseen (Blade Lord Ta'yak), Leggings of the Unseen (Garalon), and Bracers of the Unseen Path (Imperial Vizier Zor'lok). The Waist (Belt of the Unseen) and Feet (Boots of the Unseeing) are Bind-on-Equip items that drop from trash mobs within the raid instance.

### Farming Logistics
Heart of Fear operates on a weekly raid lockout per character. However, Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have a separate lockout, allowing for up to three full clears per week on a single character. The entire raid is fully soloable at current maximum levels. This is a non-tier lookalike set, so it does not use the tier token system; pieces drop directly as lootable items. Due to RNG, collecting the six boss-dropped pieces can take an average of 5-10 weekly clears. The Waist and Feet pieces drop from trash mobs, not bosses, and may require separate farming runs dedicated to clearing trash.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for the fastest clears, as this color variant drops from both Normal and Heroic modes. After defeating the first boss, Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, you can clear the left and right wings in any order to reach the final bosses more efficiently. Because the Waist and Feet pieces are Bind-on-Equip, they can be bought and sold on the Auction House, which can significantly speed up the collection process. You can use Mogu Runes of Fate, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an additional chance at loot from bosses.

427.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the mail armor lookalike from the Castle Nathria raid in Shadowlands. The main armor pieces drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Head drops from Huntsman Altimor, the Shoulder from Artificer Xy'mox, and the Chest from Hungering Destroyer. The Hands drop from Sludgefist, the Legs from Stone Legion Generals, and the Feet from Sire Denathrius. The Waist piece drops from the first boss, Shriekwing, and the Wrist from Sun King's Salvation.

### Farming Logistics
Castle Nathria has a weekly raid lockout. Each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic) has a separate lockout, allowing you to clear the raid up to four times per week on a single character for different color variations. The raid is fully soloable by a max-level character on all difficulties. Due to legacy loot rules being active, each boss drops multiple items, meaning the full set can typically be collected in 2-4 runs. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces drop directly as loot from bosses.

### Farming Tips
For the quickest farm, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as it provides the fastest and easiest boss kills. There are no significant skips or shortcuts in Castle Nathria for a solo player, so you must defeat the bosses in their intended order. The Great Vault and bonus roll systems are not applicable for farming this set. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House, although some non-set BoE items with similar appearances may be available.

428.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the leather lookalike of the Paladin Tier 14 set, and all pieces drop from the Mogu'shan Vaults raid in Pandaria. The Head, Crown of the Doomed, drops from The Spirit Kings. The Shoulders, Shoulders of the Roaring Tiger, drop from Feng the Accursed. The Chest, Robes of the Raging Tempest, drops from Elegon, and the Hands, Gloves of the Raging Tempests, drop from Gara'jal the Spiritbinder. The Legs, Leggings of the Raging Tempest, drop from the Will of the Emperor, while the Feet, Treads of the Raging Tempest, also drop from The Spirit Kings. The matching Waist, Ghost-Forged Belt, drops from Gara'jal the Spiritbinder, and the Wrist, Bracers of the Raging Tempests, drops from The Stone Guard.

### Farming Logistics
The Mogu'shan Vaults raid has a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The instance is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per item, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take between 4-8 weekly resets due to the possibility of duplicate drops. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any class able to loot them. The Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout and drop different color variations of this set, allowing you to farm all three simultaneously on the same character each week.

### Farming Tips
To speed up clears, use the teleporter pads in the main antechamber after defeating the first four bosses to instantly travel to Elegon's platform and then to the final boss. Mists of Pandaria raids feature a bonus roll system; you can acquire Elder Charms of Good Fortune from various Mists of Pandaria content (such as Timeless Isle) to use on bosses for an extra chance at loot. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Running the raid on multiple leather-wearing classes (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) each week will increase your total chances of acquiring the set.

429.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the mail armor recolor from the Looking For Raid difficulty of the Dragon Soul raid, released in the Cataclysm expansion. The tier pieces drop directly as loot from specific bosses for mail-wearing classes. The head slot drops from Warmaster Blackhorn, shoulders from Hagara the Stormbinder, chest from Ultraxion, legs from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, and hands from Warlord Zon'ozz. The matching non-tier waist and feet drop from Warlord Zon'ozz and Hagara the Stormbinder respectively, while the wrists drop from the first boss, Morchok.

### Farming Logistics
The Dragon Soul raid instance has a weekly raid lockout. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties are on separate lockouts, but this specific color variant only drops from the LFR version. The entire instance is fully soloable at the current maximum level. There is no tier token system; gear drops directly for mail-wearing classes, so there are no shared tokens. Given the number of pieces and drop chance RNG, expect to complete 4-8 weekly runs to acquire the full set.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version of Dragon Soul, you must speak to the NPC Auridormi located in the Caverns of Time in Tanaris. The raid is linear, so using a character with high movement speed will reduce clear time. The Spine of Deathwing encounter has a mandatory mechanic requiring you to manage Corrupted Bloods and Amalgamations to break Deathwing's armor plates, which cannot be skipped even at high levels. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and are not available on the Auction House.

430.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from individual non-set pieces that drop from various bosses across several Heroic Cataclysm dungeons. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and must be farmed directly. The Head drops from Lord Godfrey in Shadowfang Keep and the Waist and Wrist drop from Rom'ogg Bonecrusher and Karsh Steelbender respectively in Blackrock Caverns. The Chest and Hands drop from Earthrager Ptah and Anraphet in Halls of Origination. The Legs drop from Asaad in The Vortex Pinnacle, while the Shoulders and Feet drop from Echo of Jaina and Echo of Tyrande in the End Time dungeon.

- **Head:** Helm of the Grave (Lord Godfrey, Shadowfang Keep)
- **Shoulder:** Pauldrons of the High Requiem (Echo of Jaina, End Time)
- **Chest:** Breastplate of the Risen Land (Earthrager Ptah, Halls of Origination)
- **Legs:** Greaves of Gallantry (Asaad, The Vortex Pinnacle)
- **Hands:** Gauntlets of the Disturbed (Anraphet, Halls of Origination)
- **Feet:** Greaves of Wu the Elder (Echo of Tyrande, End Time)
- **Waist:** Belt of the Ferocious Wolf (Rom'ogg Bonecrusher, Blackrock Caverns)
- **Wrist:** Bracers of Cooled Anger (Karsh Steelbender, Blackrock Caverns)

### Farming Logistics
The items for this set drop from Heroic Cataclysm dungeons, which are on a daily lockout per character. The entire set can be easily soloed at current maximum level. As the pieces are spread across five different dungeons, collecting the full set can take anywhere from 8 to 30+ total runs, depending on drop luck. This set does not use a tier token system; items are direct boss drops and must be looted by a plate-wearing class. Normal and Heroic difficulties have separate loot tables and lockouts; these specific pieces only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Before entering each dungeon, ensure your difficulty is set to Heroic. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, will significantly speed up clears. In The Vortex Pinnacle, you can use a mount to travel between boss platforms. The End Time dungeon presents a unique challenge: the first two bosses are randomized from a pool of four, so you may need to run the dungeon multiple times just for the correct bosses (Echo of Jaina and Echo of Tyrande) to spawn. No pieces are Bind-on-Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

431.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Warrior Tier 16 set, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Mists of Pandaria. The pieces drop from specific bosses within the Normal and Heroic difficulties, which share the same color scheme. The head, chest, shoulder, and leg pieces are direct lookalikes of the tier set, while the other pieces are matching non-set items from the same raid. The full list of items and their boss sources is as follows: Head (Demolisher's Reinforced Helmet - Malkorok), Shoulder (Shoulderguards of the Cursed Raider - Siegecrafter Blackfuse), Chest (Breastplate of the Cursed Raider - Sha of Pride), Legs (Greaves of the Cursed Raider - General Nazgrim), Hands (Handguards of the Cursed Raider - Spoils of Pandaria), Feet (Greatboots of the Cursed Raider - Immerseus), Waist (Demolisher's Wrecking-Ball Girdle - Garrosh Hellscream), and Wrist (Bracers of Destructive Strength - Iron Juggernaut).

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American servers. A max-level character can easily solo the raid on all difficulties, including Mythic. Due to having eight distinct pieces dropping from eight different bosses, collecting the full set will likely require 5-10 weekly clears, depending on drop luck. While this set is composed of non-tier lookalike pieces, Warriors, Rogues, Monks, and Death Knights can also obtain the same appearance using the "Zenith" tier tokens that drop from different bosses within the raid. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout, allowing you to clear all four for different color variations of the set each week.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as enemies have less health than on Heroic or Mythic. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) can be used on bosses for an additional chance at loot; these can be purchased with Lesser Charms of Good Fortune at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. After defeating the first three bosses past the entrance (Fallen Protectors, Norushen, Sha of Pride), a rope will appear near the Sha of Pride's room that provides a shortcut directly to the Galakras encounter. Note that tier tokens themselves do not grant an appearance and must be turned in to a vendor to receive the item.

432.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-tier cloth lookalike pieces that drop from the Cataclysm raids Bastion of Twilight, Blackwing Descent, and Throne of the Four Winds on 10-player Heroic difficulty. The Head, Cowl of the Unseen World, drops from the Ascendant Council in Bastion of Twilight. The Shoulders, Mantle of the Unseen World, drop from Cho'gall in Bastion of Twilight. The Chest, Shadow-Gouged Tunic, drops from Nefarian in Blackwing Descent. The Hands, Gloves of the Forlorn, drop from Halfus Wyrmbreaker in Bastion of Twilight, while the Legs, Leggings of the Forlorn, drop from Al'Akir in Throne of the Four Winds. The matching Feet, Einhorn's Galoshes, drop from Chimaeron in Blackwing Descent and the matching Waist, X-Treme-Soak Belt, drops from Valiona & Theralion in Bastion of Twilight.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed across three raids, each with a separate weekly lockout per character. All encounters can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all pieces may take 3-6 weekly resets. This is a lookalike set, so it does not use the tier token system; the specific items must drop directly from the bosses. Because pieces drop from three separate raid instances, you must clear all three each week for a chance at the full set. Remember to set the difficulty to 10-player Heroic before entering, as 25-player difficulty drops a different color variation and shares a lockout with the 10-player version for this expansion.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a high-mobility class such as a Druid or Demon Hunter to move quickly through the instances. All raid bosses can be defeated in seconds, so there is no need to learn mechanics. While the core set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, it is worth checking the Auction House for the matching belt or other non-set filler items, as some Cataclysm-era items with the same appearance are Bind-on-Equip world drops. There are no major skips in these raids; you must clear the bosses in a mostly linear fashion to reach the ones you need.

433.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Warlock Tier 10 Heroic set, obtained from the Icecrown Citadel raid in the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces drop from bosses on 25-player Heroic difficulty. The Head, Anachronistic Hood, drops from Lady Deathwhisper. The Shoulder, Cultist's Bloodsoaked Spaulders, drops from Lord Marrowgar. The Chest, Robes of the Waking Nightmare, and Legs, Leggings of the Refracted Mind, both drop from Valithria Dreamwalker. The Hands, San'layn Ritualist Gloves, drop from Blood-Queen Lana'thel, while the Feet, Plague-Soaked Treads, drop from Festergut. The Waist, Crushing Coldwraith Belt, drops from the Twin Val'kyr, and the Wrist, Bracers of Dark Blessings, drops from The Lich King.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout, giving you one chance per character per week to obtain the desired pieces. The raid can be fully soloed at maximum level with ease. Collecting all eight pieces will likely take several weekly resets, ranging from 4 to 10 runs depending on drop luck. This set does not use the tier token system; the items drop directly as loot, bypassing the Vanquisher token used by the actual Warlock set. For Icecrown Citadel, 10-player and 25-player difficulties have separate lockouts, but Normal and Heroic difficulties share one; you must clear it on 25-player Heroic to be eligible for these specific items.

### Farming Tips
Before entering, right-click your character portrait and set the legacy raid difficulty to 25-player and the difficulty to Heroic. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter requires healing the friendly dragon to full health to complete, so characters without healing abilities may need to use bandages or healing items, though most classes have sufficient self-healing to manage it. After defeating Deathbringer Saurfang, a teleporter becomes active in the central ring, allowing you to move between cleared wings more quickly. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

434.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Mage Tier 12 armor, with all pieces dropping from bosses within the Firelands raid, the Tier 12 instance from Cataclysm. The Head, Cowl of the Cleansing Flame, drops from the final boss, Ragnaros. The Incineration Robes (Chest) and Majordomo's Chain of Office (Waist) are looted from Majordomo Fandral Staghelm. Lord Rhyolith drops both the Flame-Ascended Leggings (Legs) and the Boots of the Black Flame (Feet). The remaining pieces are the Flickering Shoulders from Baleroc, Gloves of the Raging Conflagration from Shannox, and Bracers of the Black Dream from Alysrazor.

### Farming Logistics
The Firelands raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets with the weekly server maintenance. The instance is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level, with bosses posing no threat. Given the number of pieces required and their individual drop chances, collecting the full set will take an average of 4-8 weekly clears per character. This set is a direct-drop lookalike and does not use the tier token system. The Normal and Heroic difficulties have separate weekly lockouts, but this specific red color variant only drops from Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal before entering to ensure you are targeting the correct red-colored version of the set. After defeating Alysrazor, you can loot a feather that grants temporary flight, which significantly speeds up travel between the remaining bosses. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Farming on multiple cloth-wearing characters can accelerate the acquisition of the set, as each character has a separate weekly lockout.

435.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a mail lookalike of the Mage Tier 10 armor, obtained from bosses within the Icecrown Citadel raid (25-player Normal difficulty) from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The Head, Ahn'kahar Blood Hunter's Headpiece, drops from Blood-Queen Lana'thel. The Shoulders, Shoulderguards of Cautious Calculation, and Legs, Legplates of Aetheric Strife, both drop from Festergut. The Chest, Engraved Saronite Breastplate, is from Lady Deathwhisper, and the Hands, Scourge-Hunter's Vambraces, drop from the Blood-Prince Council. Matching non-set pieces include the Waist, Belt of the Tenebrous Mist, from Valithria Dreamwalker; the Feet, Boots of Unnatural Growth, from Rotface; and the Wrist, Bracers of Dark Blessings, from Lord Marrowgar.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, reset every Tuesday. It can be fully soloed at maximum level by any class with ease. Since the items are direct boss drops and not from a vendor, completing the 8-piece set will likely take 2-4 runs, depending on drop luck. This lookalike set does not use the tier token system; the items are direct drops that can be equipped by any mail-wearing class. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties, as well as Normal and Heroic modes, all have separate weekly lockouts, but this specific color variant only drops from 25-player Normal.

### Farming Tips
You must set the raid difficulty to 25-player Normal to be eligible for these specific items. After clearing the first four bosses, an arcane teleporter near the entrance can be used to quickly access the upper wings. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter for the belt requires healing the boss to full health, which can be difficult for pure DPS classes; it can be skipped by simply running past her if the belt is not needed. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

436.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike that drops from the Firelands raid, the Tier 12 instance from the Cataclysm expansion. All pieces are obtained by defeating bosses within the 10-player Normal version of the instance. The Head slot, Cowl of the Clicking Menace, drops from Shannox. The Shoulder slot, Spaulders of Recurring Flame, drops from Majordomo Fandral Staghelm. The Chest and Hands, Robes of the Burning Council and Flickering Handguards, both drop from Baleroc. Leggings of the Incomprehensible drop from Alysrazor, while the Embereye Belt drops from Beth'tilac. Finally, both the Feet (Boots of the Black Flame) and Wrist (Firescribe Wristbands) drop from Lord Rhyolith.

### Farming Logistics
The Firelands raid is on a weekly raid lockout. For Cataclysm raids, the 10-player and 25-player versions share a lockout, as do the Normal and Heroic difficulties, meaning you can only kill each boss once per character per week. The instance is fully soloable by any class at current max level. As this is a lookalike set, it does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any class. Assuming a standard 15-25% drop chance per item, it will likely take 4-8 weeks to collect the full 8-piece set on a single character.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal before entering to ensure you are targeting the correct items. A high-mobility class such as a Druid or Demon Hunter can clear the instance in under 15 minutes. After defeating Alysrazor, you can use the feathers she drops to fly directly to Majordomo Staghelm's platform, saving time. All items in this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Bonus rolls are not available for this content.

437.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-class-specific lookalike of the Warlock Tier 10 armor, obtained from bosses in the Icecrown Citadel raid from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces drop from the 25-player Normal difficulty. The Head, Wrist, and Feet drop from Lord Marrowgar. The Shoulder and Chest drop from Lady Deathwhisper. The Hands and Waist drop from the Gunship Battle encounter. The Legs drop from the boss Festergut in the Plagueworks wing of the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The instance has a weekly raid lockout per character. Icecrown Citadel can be fully soloed at maximum level. Collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 3-6 weekly resets, as multiple required items drop from the same bosses. This lookalike set drops as direct gear and does not use a tier token system; the actual Warlock tier set uses the Vanquisher token (Death Knight, Druid, Mage, Rogue), but these lookalike pieces can be collected and worn by any class. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties have separate lockouts, as do Normal and Heroic, but this specific red color variant only drops from 25-player Normal mode.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your raid difficulty is set to 25-player and Normal before entering the instance. To speed up the run, you can skip healing the boss Valithria Dreamwalker by running past her to access the teleporter to the upper spire. During the Gunship Battle, immediately board the enemy ship with your jetpack and defeat the enemy commander to end the encounter quickly. Check the Auction House for Bind on Equip items from Icecrown Citadel trash mobs, as some pieces like the "Belt of the Biting Cold" share the same appearance as set pieces and can complete the look without relying on a boss drop.

438.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces from various Cataclysm Heroic dungeons. The Head, Cowl of the Unseen World, and Wrist, Armbands of the Crying Void, both drop from The Stonecore from bosses High Priestess Azil and Corborus respectively. The Shoulder, Meadow-Tinged Shoulderpads, drops from Lord Godfrey in Shadowfang Keep, while the Chest, Robes of Orsis, is from Rajh in Halls of Origination. The Legs, Pensive Legwraps, are looted from General Umbriss in Grim Batol, and the Hands, Gloves of the Painless Midnight, come from Admiral Ripsnarl in The Deadmines. The Waist, Belt of the Depths, drops from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides, and the Feet, Slippers of the Twilight Prophet, are from Archbishop Benedictus in the Hour of Twilight dungeon.

### Farming Logistics
These items are farmed from Cataclysm dungeons set to Heroic difficulty, which operate on a daily reset lockout per character. The entire set can be easily soloed at maximum level. As this is a lookalike set, it does not use a tier token system and the pieces can be equipped by any cloth-wearing class. The feet, Slippers of the Twilight Prophet, drop from a different dungeon (Hour of Twilight) than the rest of the pieces, which are from launch-era Cataclysm dungeons. With an estimated 20% drop chance per piece, collecting the full set may take 7-10 days of targeted farming on a single character.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeons on Heroic difficulty, as the items do not drop on Normal. Farming on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) is the most efficient method due to the daily lockout. In The Stonecore, use the Rock Borers after the second boss to fly directly to the platform of High Priestess Azil to save time. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

439.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike of Priest Tier 13 armor and is obtained from the Dragon Soul raid on 10-player Heroic difficulty. The hands, Colossal Dragonplate Gauntlets, drop from Morchok. Warlord Zon'ozz drops both the legs, Greaves of the Shattered Abyss, and the wrist, Graveheart Bracers. The shoulders, Shoulderguards of the Unflanked, drop from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, while the feet, Treads of Crushed Flesh, are from Hagara the Stormbinder. The chest piece, Chestplate of the Fading Light, drops from Ultraxion, and the head, Faceguard of the All-Consuming Maw, drops from Warmaster Blackhorn. The waist, Goriona's Collar, is found in the Cache of the Dragon Queen after defeating Deathwing.

### Farming Logistics
The Dragon Soul raid has a weekly lockout per character. Under the Cataclysm loot system, you can defeat each boss only once per week, and this lockout is shared between 10-player, 25-player, Normal, and Heroic difficulties, so you must run it exclusively on 10-player Heroic. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level. As this is a non-tier lookalike set, the pieces drop directly and do not use a token system. With eight total pieces to collect across seven different bosses, a player can expect to complete the set in 4-8 weeks on a single character, depending on luck. All pieces drop from within the Dragon Soul raid.

### Farming Tips
To get this specific red recolor, you must set the raid difficulty to Heroic and the Legacy Raid Size to 10 Player before entering the instance. After the first boss, portals will appear that allow you to skip ahead, but do not use them, as Warlord Zon'ozz and Yor'sahj drop pieces essential to this set. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House. During the Spine of Deathwing encounter, be sure to stand inside the glowing craters left by destroyed Amalgamations to avoid being rolled off his back.

440.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces primarily sourced from various Cataclysm Heroic dungeons, with matching non-set items from Cataclysm raids. The Head, Helm of the Inward Eye, drops from High Priestess Azil in The Stonecore. The Shoulders, Bile-O-Tron Nut-Guards, drop from Foe Reaper 5000 in Deadmines. The Chest, Tunic of Sinking Envy, drops from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides, and the Hands, Gloves of Biting Chill, drop from Altairus in The Vortex Pinnacle. The Legs, Leggings of the Path, drop from Anraphet in Halls of Origination, while the matching Wrist, Poison-Binder's Bracers, drop from Corla, Herald of Twilight in Blackrock Caverns. The matching Waist, Belt of the Fallen Brood, drops from Halfus Wyrmbreaker in The Bastion of Twilight, and the Feet, Fungus-Stained Treads, drop from Warlord Zon'ozz in Dragon Soul.

### Farming Logistics
The five core dungeon pieces are on a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, allowing one attempt per boss per day. The matching raid pieces from The Bastion of Twilight and Dragon Soul are on a weekly raid lockout. The entire set can be easily soloed at current maximum level. Due to RNG, collecting all dungeon pieces may take several weeks of daily clears, while the raid pieces can be farmed on both 10-player and 25-player Normal difficulty each week for two chances per piece. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any class and are added to your appearance library upon looting.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering, as the pieces do not drop on Normal. For the raid items, run The Bastion of Twilight and Dragon Soul on Normal difficulty (either 10- or 25-player). Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter, will significantly speed up the time it takes to travel through the instances. In The Vortex Pinnacle, you can use mounts between boss platforms to clear the instance faster. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

441.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces from multiple Cataclysm instances, including six Heroic dungeons and The Bastion of Twilight raid. The Head drops from High Prophet Barim in Lost City of the Tol'vir and the Hands from Asaad in The Vortex Pinnacle. Shoulders and Waist drop from Echo of Sylvanas and Echo of Baine, respectively, in End Time. The Legs drop from Forgemaster Throngus in Grim Batol, the Wrist from Corla, Herald of Twilight in Blackrock Caverns, and the Chest and Feet drop from Halfus Wyrmbreaker and Cho'gall in The Bastion of Twilight on Normal difficulty.

### Farming Logistics
The farm involves two different lockout types: a daily lockout for the six Heroic dungeons and a weekly raid lockout for The Bastion of Twilight. All instances can be easily soloed by a max-level character. Due to the weekly lockout on the Chest and Feet, collecting the full set will take a minimum of two weeks but can average 4-7 weeks depending on drop luck. This set is composed of individual lookalike items and does not use a tier token system. For The Bastion of Twilight, the 10-player and 25-player Normal modes share a single weekly lockout.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeons on Heroic difficulty and The Bastion of Twilight on Normal difficulty, as this is where the specific appearances drop. To manage the multiple daily lockouts, it can be efficient to park different characters outside the entrances to the various dungeons. There are no significant skips in these instances, so using a high-mobility class will speed up clears. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

442.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor lookalike set is obtained from bosses within the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty. The Head drops from Lady Deathwhisper, Shoulders from Festergut, Chest from Lord Marrowgar, and Hands from Rotface. The Waist drops from the Blood Prince Council and the Wrist from Scourgelord Tyrannus. Both the Legs (Cold-Forged Legplates) and Feet (Sabatons of the Lingering Vortex) are found on the final boss, The Lich King.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel has a weekly raid lockout, and you only have one chance per character per week to get these specific items. The raid is easily soloable at current max level. As a lookalike set, the items drop directly from bosses and do not use a tier token system. Be aware that 10-player and 25-player difficulties, as well as Normal and Heroic, share the same lockout, so you must run it on 25-player Heroic for these specific appearances. A complete set can take 5-15 runs depending on drop luck.

### Farming Tips
Ensure you set the raid difficulty to 25-player and the difficulty to Heroic from your character portrait before entering the instance. You can skip the Valithria Dreamwalker encounter by running past the boss, saving time on each clear. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought from the Auction House; there are no alternative acquisition methods like bonus rolls. Farming on a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) is necessary, as the armor type cannot be collected by leather, mail, or cloth classes.

443.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a lookalike of the Normal difficulty Tier 12 Shaman set and is obtained from bosses within The Nighthold, a Legion raid. The Head slot, Helm of the Blind Seer, drops from Trilliax. The Shoulder slot, Spaulders of the First Vindicator, drops from High Botanist Tel'arn, and the Chest, Breastplate of the Remembered King, drops from Krosus. The Legguards of Abhorrent Verdure drop from Skorpyron, the Grips of Vengeful Purpose drop from Tichondrius, and the Boots of Renewed Flight drop from Chronomatic Anomaly. The Waistguard of Profane Duplicity drops from the final boss, Gul'dan, while the Vambraces of the Ebon-Hand drop from Star Augur Etraeus.

### Farming Logistics
The Nighthold raid has a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American realms. The instance can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, a full collection will likely require 5-10 weekly clears. This set does not use a tier token system; the armor pieces drop directly and are shared by all Mail-specialization classes: Hunter, Shaman, and Evoker. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout, but this specific appearance only drops from the Normal difficulty version of the raid.

### Farming Tips
To acquire this specific color variation, you must run the raid on Normal difficulty. After killing initial bosses like Skorpyron and Chronomatic Anomaly, teleporters unlock in the main hub, significantly speeding up travel back to the central chamber on subsequent runs. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. You must be on a Mail-wearing character (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker) for the items to be added to your loot table. Legion Bonus Rolls (Seal of Broken Fate) are no longer obtainable and cannot be used to supplement drops.

444.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Normal Rogue Tier 16 set, obtained from bosses within the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Pandaria. The Head, Darkfang Mask, drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty. The Shoulders, Shoulderpads of the Unblinking Eye, drop from Malkorok. The Chest, Tunic of the Unblinking Vigil, drops from the Sha of Pride, while the Legs, Leggings of the Unblinking Vigil, drop from General Nazgrim. The Hands, Gloves of the Unblinking Vigil, are looted from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman, and the Feet, Boots of the Unblinking Vigil, drop from Immerseus. The Waist, Belt of the Unblinking Vigil, drops from Norushen, and the Wrists, Bracers of the Unblinking Vigil, are from Galakras.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar operates on a weekly raid lockout per difficulty, meaning you can clear Normal, Heroic, and Mythic separately each week for more chances at loot. The raid is fully soloable at max level by any class that can wear leather armor. Expect to run the instance for 5-10 weeks on a single character to collect all eight pieces, depending on luck. This lookalike set drops as direct items and does not use a token system. The actual Rogue tier set uses the "Cursed Protector" token, shared by Druids, Mages, Monks, and Rogues, which you may loot in addition to these specific pieces.

### Farming Tips
Set the instance difficulty to "Normal Raid" before entering to ensure you are farming the correct color variation. You can use Warforged Seals, a Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an extra chance at loot from each boss; these can be acquired on the Timeless Isle. While there are skips available in the raid, several required pieces drop from the initial bosses (Immerseus, Norushen, Sha of Pride), so a full clear of at least the first eight bosses is necessary. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

445.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier plate lookalike obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid, which was the Tier 15 instance in Mists of Pandaria. All pieces drop from bosses within the Normal difficulty version of the raid. The headpiece, Crown of the Golden Golem, drops from Megaera. The shoulder piece, Pauldrons of the Golden Golem, drops from the Twin Consorts. The chest, Breastplate of the Golden Golem, is looted from Dark Animus, while the legplates, Legplates of the Golden Golem, are on Horridon's loot table. The hand piece, Gauntlets of the Golden Golem, drops from the Council of Elders; the feet, Greaves of the Golden Golem, drop from Tortos; the waist, Waistplate of the Golden Golem, drops from Ji-Kun; and the wrists, Bracers of the Golden Golem, drop from Iron Qon.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday in North America. The entire raid can be soloed by any max-level plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item, a full set can be collected in approximately 4-7 weekly clears, subject to luck. This lookalike set drops as direct items and does not use the tier token system. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout, and you must run the Normal version to obtain this specific coloration.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a max-level character with high mobility. Set the raid difficulty to Normal before entering; this can be done by right-clicking your character portrait. As you defeat bosses, portals will activate throughout the instance, allowing you to quickly travel back to earlier sections. There are no major skips to bypass the specific bosses that drop the set pieces, so you will need to clear most of the instance up to the Twin Consorts. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are a possible but inefficient way to gain extra loot, as the currency is no longer easily farmed.

446.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a non-tier lookalike of the Warrior's Tier 14 Normal set, and all pieces are found within the Heart of Fear raid from Tier 14. The headpiece, Plate Helm of the Maelstrom, and feet, Treads of the Star-Strider, both drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok. The shoulders, Shoulderguards of the Unflanked, and chest, Chestplate of the All-Consuming Maw, drop from Garalon. The legs, Legplates of Whipping Reeds, are looted from Amber-Shaper Un'sok. Blade Lord Ta'yak drops the hands, Gauntlets of the Clogged Arteries. The waist, Klaxxi Lash of the Orator, and wrists, Bracers of Destructive Strength, both drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear raid instance is on a weekly raid lockout. This means you can kill each boss for a chance at loot once per character per week. The instance can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per item, it may take approximately 5-10 weekly clears to collect the full 8-piece set. This is a lookalike set, so it does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any class. The Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single weekly lockout, while the LFR version has its own separate lockout.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears and to maximize loot drops, run the raid on Normal 25-player difficulty, which drops more items per boss than the 10-player version. There are no significant skips in this linear raid, so you must clear the bosses in order. Items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Since these are direct boss drops and not tokens, their appearances are automatically added to your wardrobe upon looting, provided your character can equip plate armor.

447.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cloth lookalike of the Death Knight Tier 15 set, with all pieces dropping from bosses within the Throne of Thunder raid instance from Mists of Pandaria. All pieces drop from the Normal (10 or 25-player) difficulty of the raid. The specific source for each armor slot is as follows:

*   **Head:** Cowl of the Exorcist (Lei Shen)
*   **Shoulder:** Shoulderguards of the Spirit-Forge (Iron Qon)
*   **Chest:** Lifekeeper's Robe (Council of Elders)
*   **Legs:** Leggings of the Poisoned Soul (Megaera)
*   **Hands:** Gloves of the Inconspicuous Bear (Tortos)
*   **Feet:** Bone-Inlaid Sandals (Primordius)
*   **Waist:** Girdle of the Mogu-Slayer (Ji-Kun)
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of the Mogu-Slayer (Ji-Kun)

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms and Wednesday for European realms. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, a player can expect to complete the set in 5-10 weekly clears per character, though this can vary with luck. These lookalike pieces are direct boss drops and do not come from a tier token system. The Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout, but this specific color tint is only available from Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as enemies have significantly less health than their 25-player counterparts. After clearing the first four bosses, use the teleport pad near the instance entrance to skip directly to Durumu the Forgotten, which saves considerable travel time. Players can acquire Elder Charms of Good Fortune from the Timeless Isle or Lesser Charms of Good Fortune turn-ins to use as bonus rolls on bosses for an extra chance at loot. All pieces from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

448.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cosmetic collection obtained from the Castle Nathria raid in Shadowlands. All pieces drop from bosses on Normal difficulty. The Head slot, Visage of the Loa-Speaker, drops from Shriekwing. The Shoulder slot, Shawl of the Penitent, drops from Sludgefist. The Chest piece, Soul-Igniter's Tunic, is from Huntsman Altimor, and the Hands, Handwraps of Inevitable Doom, are from the Stone Legion Generals. The Waist, Spell-Woven Tourniquet, drops from the Council of Blood; the Legs, Leggings of Lingering Infusion, drop from Lady Inerva Darkvein; the Feet, Courtier's Costume Slippers, are from Artificer Xy'mox; and the Wrists, Cuffs of Obscured Dignity, drop from Sire Denathrius.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday in North America. Castle Nathria is fully soloable on Normal difficulty by a max-level character from the Dragonflight expansion. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per item, collecting the full 8-piece set can take approximately 5-10 weekly clears, subject to random luck. This set does not use a tier token system; loot drops directly for your class specialization, or as generic pieces usable by all cloth wearers. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate weekly lockouts and reward different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific color tint. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. There are no major skips to bypass bosses, so a full clear up to the required bosses is necessary. The Great Vault system from Shadowlands is no longer active for this content and cannot be used to supplement drops. After defeating the Council of Blood, you can choose the order in which you engage Sludgefist and the Stone Legion Generals.

449.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Normal difficulty Druid tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Founders raid in Shadowlands. The eight individual pieces are direct drops from bosses within the raid on Normal difficulty. The Head, Cowl of the Dashing Scoundrel, drops from Anduin Wrynn. The Shoulders, Pauldrons of the Dashing Scoundrel, drop from Lords of Dread. The Chest, Jerkin of the Unseen Path, drops from Rygelon. The Legs, Leggings of the Dashing Scoundrel, drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer, while the Hands, Gloves of the Dashing Scoundrel, drop from Lihuvim. The Feet, Stalkers of the Dashing Scoundrel, drop from The Jailer; the Waist, Taut Girdle of the Unseen Path, drops from Vigilant Guardian; and the Wrist, Wristwraps of the Unseen Path, drops from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Founders raid operates on a weekly raid lockout per character per difficulty. At the current maximum level, the Normal difficulty version of the raid is fully soloable by a well-geared character. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces may take approximately 8-12 weekly resets on a single character due to the random nature of drops. These lookalike items are direct drops and do not come from a tier token system. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate weekly lockouts, but this specific color variant only drops from Normal.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty, as this is the only source for this particular color variant. After defeating key bosses, you can use the teleporters within the raid to navigate between the different wings more quickly. Pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Running with a group of other leather-wearers (Demon Hunter, Monk, Rogue) can significantly speed up collection, as players can trade items they do not need. Killing raid bosses will also grant progress toward your weekly Great Vault, which provides an additional chance to receive a piece from your loot table.

450.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a lookalike assembled from epic-quality cloth armor pieces that drop in Mists of Pandaria Heroic dungeons. The Head, Cowl of the Fire-Gizzard, drops from Flameweaver Koegler in Scarlet Halls. The Shoulders, Spaulders of the Divided Mind, drop from High Inquisitor Whitemane in Scarlet Monastery. The Chest, Robes of the Burning Cougar, drops from Houndmaster Braun in Scarlet Halls. The Legs, Leggings of the Frenzy, drop from Master Snowdrift in Shado-Pan Monastery. The Hands, Gloves of the Raging River, drop from Wise Mari in Temple of the Jade Serpent. The Feet, Sandals of the Blackest Night, drop from Sha of Violence in Shado-Pan Monastery, and the Waist, Incineration Belt, drops from General Pa'valak in Siege of Niuzao Temple.

### Farming Logistics
The five dungeons containing these items are on a daily Heroic lockout, meaning you can attempt to get each piece once per day per character. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with no difficulty. Given an approximate 20% drop chance per piece spread across five instances, collecting all seven items may take an average of 5-10 daily resets. This set does not use a tier token system; all items are direct boss drops. Normal and Heroic difficulties for Mists of Pandaria dungeons share a lockout, so you must run them on Heroic to be eligible for this specific epic-quality set.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance. Use a high-mobility class like a Monk or Demon Hunter to move through the dungeons quickly. You can set your hearthstone to the Shrine of Seven Stars or Shrine of Two Moons in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms for convenient portal access to the zones containing the dungeons. All items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls and the Great Vault system do not apply to these drops.

451.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier plate lookalike of the Normal difficulty Warrior Tier 16 set, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Pandaria. All pieces drop directly from bosses within the raid on Normal or Heroic difficulty, which share the same appearance. The Head drops from Immerseus, Shoulders from Malkorok, and Chest from Garrosh Hellscream. The Legs are looted from The Sha of Pride, Hands from General Nazgrim, Feet from Iron Juggernaut, Waist from Norushen, and Wrists from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout, and Siege of Orgrimmar has separate lockouts for LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties, allowing four attempts per week for different colorations. The entire instance is fully soloable by a max-level character on all difficulties up to and including Mythic. This is a direct-drop set and does not use the tier token system, so the items can be looted by any plate-wearing class. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all eight pieces can take approximately 8-12 weeks of clearing the instance.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty, as it is the fastest to clear and drops the correct appearance. Players can use Mists of Pandaria bonus roll seals, called Warforged Seals, for an extra chance at loot from each boss; these seals can be purchased from an NPC on the Timeless Isle. As you defeat bosses, teleporters will unlock near the entrance of each major wing, allowing you to skip cleared sections on subsequent runs. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

452.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike obtained from various bosses within Mists of Pandaria Heroic dungeons. The Head, Chitinous Greathelm, drops from Raigonn in Gate of the Setting Sun. The Shoulders, Shoulderguards of the Unflanked, drop from Wing Leader Ner'onok in Siege of Niuzao Temple. The Chest and Waist, Breastplate of the Risen Land and Waistplate of the Risen Land, both drop from Xin the Weaponmaster in Mogu'shan Palace. The Hands, Gauntlets of the Disturbed Giant, drop from Gekkan, also in Mogu'shan Palace. The Legs, Foundry-Fired Legplates, drop from Commander Ri'mok in Gate of the Setting Sun, while the Feet, Treads of Crispin, drop from Striker Ga'dok in Siege of Niuzao Temple. Finally, the Wrists, Bracers of Inlaid Jade, drop from Liu Flameheart in the Temple of the Jade Serpent.

### Farming Logistics
The Mists of Pandaria Heroic dungeons where this set drops are on a daily lockout, meaning each character can complete each specific dungeon once per day. The dungeons are fully soloable at maximum level, with all bosses being trivial encounters. Given the 15-25% drop chance for each piece and the daily lockout, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 1-3 weeks depending on luck. This set does not use a token system; pieces drop directly for plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). Normal and Heroic difficulties share a daily lockout, so you must run them on Heroic difficulty to be eligible for this gear.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic before entering each instance to be eligible for the loot. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Demon Hunter or Monk, will significantly reduce clear times. Most non-essential trash mobs in these dungeons can be skipped by running straight to the required boss. The items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No legacy loot systems like bonus rolls apply to this content.

453.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a non-tier lookalike of the Warrior Tier 16 set, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Mists of Pandaria on Normal difficulty. The Head, `Hood of Perpetual Conflict`, drops from Immerseus. The Shoulders, `Pauldrons of the Souleater`, are from Malkorok, and the Chest, `Battleplate of the All-Consuming Maw`, is from the Sha of Pride. The Legs, `Legguards of the Unseeing`, drop from General Nazgrim, while the Hands, `Blood-Soaked Saronite Plated Gauntlets`, are found in the Spoils of Pandaria encounter. The Feet, `Sabatons of the Graceful Spirit`, drop from The Fallen Protectors, the Waist, `Girdle of the Cursed Vanquisher`, is from Thok the Bloodthirsty, and the Wrist, `Bracers of Ultimate Agony`, is from the Iron Juggernaut.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid has a weekly raid lockout per character for each difficulty. At max level, the entire raid is easily soloable by any plate-wearing class. Due to the number of pieces and drop chance variance, expect to complete between 5-15 runs per character to acquire the full set. This lookalike set drops as individual items and does not use the tier token system; however, the actual Warrior tier set uses the "Vanquisher" token, which is shared with Death Knights, Hunters, and Mages. All pieces for this lookalike set drop within Siege of Orgrimmar. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the armor.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty, as this is the only source for this specific color tint. After defeating General Nazgrim for the first time, a scroll will appear near the entrance on all subsequent runs for that character, allowing you to teleport directly to the start of his encounter and skip the first eight bosses. You can use Warforged Seals, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an extra chance at loot from bosses. The Galakras encounter requires you to use tower cannons to shoot down the boss, so be prepared to run between the two towers to manage the encounter solo.

454.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Paladin "Battleplate of Immolation" tier set, obtained from the Sepulcher of the First Vigilant raid on Normal difficulty. The Head (Helm of the Fallen Lord) drops from Anduin Wrynn. The Shoulders (Pauldrons of the Fallen Lord) drop from Rygelon. The Chest (Breastplate of the Fallen Lord) is from the Lords of Dread encounter. The Legs (Legplates of the Fallen Lord) are looted from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Hands (Gauntlets of the Fallen Lord) drop from Lihuvim, Principal Architect. The matching Waist (Girdle of the Deplorable) drops from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener, the Wrist (Bracers of the Deplorable) from the Prototype Pantheon, and the Feet (Stompers of the Deplorable) from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Vigilant raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday in North America. Due to being a Shadowlands raid, it cannot be fully soloed at current max level and requires a small group of 2-5 players. With personal loot rules and an approximate 15-25% drop chance, collecting all eight pieces could take between 5-10 weekly clears, subject to RNG. This set's pieces drop directly as items and do not use a tier token system. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate weekly lockouts, allowing you to farm each difficulty once per week for different color variations of the raid's gear.

### Farming Tips
To obtain this specific coloration, you must run the raid on Normal difficulty. The most efficient method is to form or join a transmog group using the in-game Premade Groups finder, specifically looking for Sepulcher of the First Vigilant runs. Running with other plate-wearing classes (Warriors, Paladins, Death Knights) allows for item trading if a player receives a piece they already own or do not need. There are no major boss skips, so a full clear is required to reach all relevant bosses. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not systems that can be used to retroactively acquire these appearances.

455.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic armor awarded by the Venthyr Covenant in Shadowlands, purchased with Reservoir Anima from Mar'lan, the Renown Quartermaster in Sinfall, Revendreth.
