Unlocking the pieces requires reaching specific Renown levels with the covenant. The Hands and Feet become available at Renown 26, the Chest, Legs, Waist, and Wrist unlock at Renown 32, and the Head and Shoulders are unlocked at Renown 37. Major slots like the Head, Chest, and Legs cost 1,500 Anima each, while Shoulders, Hands, and Feet cost 1,200 Anima each, and the Waist and Wrist cost 1,000 Anima each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as both Renown and Reservoir Anima can be farmed continuously. The entire process can be fully soloed at current max level. The primary time investment is farming Renown up to level 37; collecting the necessary 10,300 Reservoir Anima is secondary. This set does not use a tier token system, as all pieces are purchased directly from the vendor. Once a character on your account reaches Renown 80 with the Venthyr, the appearances are unlocked for all other characters on the account to use, regardless of their own covenant allegiance.

### Farming Tips
To gain Renown quickly, focus on completing the Venthyr campaign questline and the weekly "Replenish the Reservoir" quest. The fastest way to farm Reservoir Anima is by completing world quests across all Shadowlands zones and by looting treasures and killing rare mobs in Zereth Mortis. Reaching Renown 80 on a single character is the most efficient long-term goal, as it permanently unlocks all Venthyr cosmetic rewards account-wide. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

456.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set epic items assembled from multiple Cataclysm Heroic dungeons. The Head, Shoulders, and Wrists drop from Lord Godfrey, Lord Walden, and Baron Ashbury respectively in Heroic Shadowfang Keep. The Legs and Feet drop from High Prophet Barim and Siamat in Heroic Lost City of the Tol'vir. The Chest is from High Priestess Kilnara in Heroic Zul'Gurub, the Hands from Hex Lord Malacrass in Heroic Zul'Aman, and the Waist from Helix Gearbreaker in Heroic Deadmines. All items are personal loot from the final boss of their respective encounter.

### Farming Logistics
The set pieces drop from Cataclysm Heroic dungeons, which are on a daily reset lockout, allowing one attempt per character per day for each dungeon. The entire farm is easily soloable by a max-level character. Given the pieces are spread across five separate dungeons, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 5-10 runs of each relevant instance, averaging 1-2 weeks of daily farming on a single character. There is no tier token system; all items are direct boss drops. The Heroic difficulty required for these drops shares a daily lockout with the Normal difficulty version of these instances.

### Farming Tips
Set your dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering, as the items do not drop on Normal. You can speed up runs by using a high-mobility class and mounting where possible, such as in Zul'Aman and Zul'Gurub. In Shadowfang Keep, Deadmines, and Lost City of the Tol'vir, you can run directly to the required bosses, skipping most trash and any optional encounters. In Zul'Aman, you must defeat Halazzi before Hex Lord Malacrass will spawn, and in Zul'Gurub you must defeat High Priest Venoxis before High Priestess Kilnara becomes accessible.

457.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic plate lookalike of the Death Knight "Terrorblade Battlegear" and is named the "Warplate of the Cryptic Rune." It is obtained by crafting via the Protoform Synthesis system in Zereth Mortis and is usable by all Plate classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). Each piece requires a specific schematic, a base plate item from Zereth Mortis, and crafting reagents including Genesis Motes. Schematics for the set drop from rares in Zereth Mortis and bosses within the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid. The Head schematic drops from General Zarathura, the Shoulder schematic from Xy'mox's Cache, and the Chest schematic from Lord Artificer Xy'mox, all in the raid. The Hands, Waist, Legs, and Feet schematics drop from various rare mobs and treasures within the Zereth Mortis zone.

### Farming Logistics
The three raid-based schematics are on a weekly raid lockout per difficulty. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are separate lockouts and all can drop the schematics. The Zereth Mortis rares and treasures that drop the other schematics are on a daily lockout. Sepulcher of the First Ones is not fully soloable at max level and requires a small group of 2-5 players. The main crafting reagent, Genesis Motes, can be farmed infinitely from wildlife in Zereth Mortis. Collection time varies based on raid RNG, but the material grind can take 5-10 hours. This set does not use a tier token system.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest raid clears, form a group for Normal difficulty Sepulcher of the First Ones, as drop chances for the schematics are consistent across difficulties. Having flying unlocked in Zereth Mortis is critical for efficiently farming the outdoor rares, treasures, and Genesis Motes. Genesis Motes can be farmed effectively from wildlife mobs around the Genesis Fields or at the Arrangement Index. The crafted cosmetic items are soulbound and cannot be traded or sold on the Auction House, but the base plate items needed for the craft can be.

458.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate set is the Looking For Raid (LFR) difficulty recolor from the Throne of Thunder, the Tier 15 raid instance. To access this specific difficulty, you must speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. The items drop directly from bosses within the four wings of the raid. The Headpiece drops from Twin Consorts, the Shoulderguards from Iron Qon, the Chestpiece from Dark Animus, and the Legplates from Ji-Kun. The Handguards are dropped by the Council of Elders, while matching non-set Feet, Waist, and Wrist pieces drop from Megaera, Tortos, and Iron Qon respectively.

### Farming Logistics
This appearance set is on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms. Each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic) has a separate lockout, allowing you to farm each color variant once per week. The instance is fully soloable at the current maximum level with no group required. This LFR version does not use the tier token system found in Normal and Heroic difficulties; instead, pieces drop directly for your character based on a personal loot system. Given the large LFR loot tables, expect an average of 5-10 full clears to collect all eight pieces.

### Farming Tips
To begin the farm, travel to the Seat of Knowledge above Mogu'shan Palace in Pandaria and speak to Lorewalker Han to queue for the specific Throne of Thunder LFR wings. For maximum efficiency, use a high-mobility class to move between bosses quickly. To increase your chances of receiving loot per boss, you can use a bonus roll currency called Elder Charm of Good Fortune. These can be acquired by turning in Lesser Charms of Good Fortune, which drop from various Mists of Pandaria mobs. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

459.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of epic leather armor pieces that drop from bosses in the level 85 Cataclysm heroic dungeons, Zul'Aman and Zul'Gurub. The Head, Skull-Shredder's Face-guard, drops from Bloodlord Mandokir in Zul'Gurub. The Shoulder, Flickering Shoulderpads, drops from Daakara in Zul'Aman, while the Chest, Shadow-Binder's Tunic, comes from Halazzi in the same instance. The Legs, Leggings of the Pride, and Hands, Fingers of the Spore, are from Gri'lek and Zanzil respectively in Zul'Gurub. The Feet, Treads of the Green-Tower, drop from High Priestess Kilnara in Zul'Gurub; the Waist, Belt of the Untamed, drops from Hex Lord Malacrass in Zul'Aman; and the Wrist, Amani'shi Bracers, is a world drop from trash mobs inside Zul'Aman.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from two dungeons subject to a daily heroic dungeon reset, allowing one attempt at each boss per character per day. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Due to the pieces being split between two instances and subject to RNG, expect to run both dungeons for approximately 10-20 days to collect all eight pieces. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops for leather-wearing classes (Druid, Rogue, Monk, Demon Hunter). As Cataclysm dungeons, they only have a Heroic difficulty setting, so there are no separate lockouts to manage for different difficulties.

### Farming Tips
To maximize efficiency, use a high-mobility class like a Demon Hunter or Druid to clear the dungeons quickly. Before entering, ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic, as this is the only mode these specific dungeons can be completed in for this loot. The bracers, Amani'shi Bracers, are Bind-on-Equip and can often be purchased from the Auction House to save time farming trash mobs. There are no relevant bonus roll systems or instance-skipping shortcuts for this legacy content.

460.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces from multiple Heroic Cataclysm dungeons. The Head, Shoulder, and Feet pieces drop in The Stonecore: Helmet of the Inward Eye from High Priestess Azil, Somber Shawl from Slabhide, and Boots of Hasty Reversal from Corborus. The chest piece, Kamaei's Ceremonial Robes, drops from General Husam in Lost City of the Tol'vir. The hands, Liar's Handwraps, drop from General Umbriss in Grim Batol, while the legs, Leggings of the Path, drop from Anraphet in Halls of Origination. The waist, Belt of the Dim Forest, is obtained from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides; there is no matching wrist piece from these sources.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from five dungeons on Heroic difficulty, which are subject to a daily lockout per character. All bosses can be fully soloed at current max level with no difficulty. Given the number of separate pieces and dungeons, collecting the full set will likely take 3-5 days of clearing all relevant instances. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct drops from their respective bosses. Normal and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, but these specific items only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering to be eligible for the loot. For efficiency, use a high-mobility class like a Monk, Demon Hunter, or Druid to move through the instances quickly. In Grim Batol and Throne of the Tides, the bosses holding your desired loot are the very first encounters, allowing for a rapid in-and-out farm. Conversely, the leggings from Halls of Origination drop from the final boss, requiring a full clear of the instance. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

461.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class lookalike of the Druid Tier 11 armor, dropping from the Bastion of Twilight and Blackwing Descent raids on 10-player Normal difficulty. The head, chest, legs, hands, and wrist pieces are found in Blackwing Descent, while the shoulder, feet, and waist drop in Bastion of Twilight. The Head slot drops from Atramedes, the Chest and Legs drop from Maloriak, and the Hands also drop from Atramedes. The matching Wrist drops from Magmaw, the Shoulders drop from Cho'gall, the Feet from The Ascendant Council, and the Waist from Valiona & Theralion.

### Farming Logistics
Both Blackwing Descent and Bastion of Twilight operate on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The instances can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level with no difficulty. Given that multiple pieces drop from the same boss, it will likely take between 5-10 weekly clears to acquire the full 8-piece set. This lookalike set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly from bosses. You must set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal to be eligible for this specific color variation's loot table.

### Farming Tips
Before entering either raid, right-click your character portrait and set the "Legacy Raid Size" to 10-player and "Raid Difficulty" to Normal. In Blackwing Descent, after defeating Magmaw and the Omnotron Defense System, a teleporter activates in the central chamber, allowing you to skip directly to later bosses like Maloriak and Atramedes. The Bastion of Twilight raid is linear with no major skips. All pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House.

462.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a leather lookalike of the Sanctified Shadowblade's Battlegear, with pieces dropping from bosses in the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Normal difficulty. The Head, Geistlord's Punishment Sack, drops from Lord Marrowgar. The Shoulders, Cultist's Bloodsoaked Spaulders, are from Festergut. The Chest, Ikfirus's Sack of Wonders, is contained in the cache after the Gunship Battle. The Legs, Leggings of Broken Magic, drop from Lady Deathwhisper, and the Hands, Aldriana's Gloves of Secrecy, drop from Rotface. The Waist, Astrylian's Sutured Cinch, is looted from Professor Putricide, and the Wrists, Toskk's Maximized Wristguards, are from Deathbringer Saurfang. There are no directly matching boots within Icecrown Citadel; a suitable alternative is Boots of the Never-Ending Path from Herald Volazj in the Heroic dungeon Ahn'kahet: The Old Kingdom.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout, which is shared between 10-player/25-player and Normal/Heroic difficulties once a boss is defeated. The entire instance can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. As a lookalike set, these pieces drop directly and do not use the tier token system. Assuming a standard 15-25% drop chance for each of the seven items from the raid, a complete collection can be expected within 4-8 weekly resets. Remember that the boots must be farmed separately from a dungeon source which has a daily lockout. You must set the raid difficulty to 25-player and raid difficulty to Normal before entering to be eligible for this specific color variant.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Normal before the first boss kill to lock yourself into the correct loot table for the week. To speed up travel within the raid, use the teleporter in the center of the main ring which activates after you have cleared the bosses in the upper wings. For the Gunship Battle, immediately take control of a cannon, build its heat to 90+ with the first ability, then fire the second ability to damage the enemy ship; use the jetpack to board the enemy ship to kill the adds and Battle-Mage when your cannons are frozen. None of the set pieces are Bind-on-Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

463.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather armor set is a lookalike of the Rogue Tier 13 set and is obtained from the Dragon Soul raid on 10-player Normal difficulty. The Head piece, Hood of Unknowable Horrors, drops from Hagara the Stormbinder and the Shoulders, Underdweller's Spaulders, drop from Warmaster Blackhorn. Ultraxion drops the Chest, Shadowbinder's Tunic; Warlord Zon'ozz drops the Hands, Gloves of the Ranging Roc; and Yor'sahj the Unsleeping drops the Legs, Leggings of the Unseen. The Feet, Interrogator's Bloody Footpads, drop from the first boss, Morchok, while the Waist (Belt of the Merciless) and Wrist (Bracers of the Banished) pieces are Bind on Equip items that can drop from any trash mob inside the raid.

### Farming Logistics
Dragon Soul operates on a weekly raid lockout, but 10-player and 25-player difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing two chances per week at the Normal mode appearance if you run both. The entire instance is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. As this is a lookalike set, the items drop directly from bosses and are not obtained via a tier token system. The Waist and Wrist pieces are the exception, dropping from trash mobs within the raid. Assuming an average drop rate, it may take 4-7 clears to acquire all boss-related pieces. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest farm, check the Auction House for the Bind on Equip waist and wrist pieces to complete the set without relying on random trash drops. Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal before entering, as this specific color tint does not drop on other difficulties. During the Spine of Deathwing encounter, kill three tentacles at a time to spawn the necessary adds, then DPS the exposed tendon to proceed. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not applicable for this legacy content.

464.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific leather lookalike of the Heroic Rogue Tier 10 set, obtained from the 25-player Heroic difficulty of the Icecrown Citadel raid from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The head piece, Gangrenous Head-Phylactery, and legs, Plague-Soaked Leather Leggings, both drop from Festergut. The Chestguard of the Frigid Noose and Aldriana's Gloves of Secrecy are looted from Lady Deathwhisper. The Shoulderpads of the Bitten drop from Blood-Queen Lana'thel, the Frostbitten Fur Boots from Lord Marrowgar, the Astrylian's Sutured Cinch from Professor Putricide, and the Toskk's Maximized Wristguards from Deathbringer Saurfang.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel has a weekly raid lockout, shared between Normal and Heroic difficulties for each raid size (10- and 25-player). To farm this specific set, you must set the instance difficulty to 25-player and toggle to Heroic before engaging each boss. The raid is fully soloable at max level by any class. As this is a lookalike set, the items drop directly from bosses and do not use the tier token system that the class-specific version uses. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per piece, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 5-10 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Normal before entering, then right-click your portrait to set the raid difficulty to Heroic once inside. You must re-check this setting if you leave and re-enter the instance, as it can sometimes reset. To speed up the run, you can skip the Valithria Dreamwalker encounter, as she is not required to progress and drops no pieces for this set. All pieces for this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

466.content:
### How to Obtain
This lookalike set is acquired from bosses within the Hellfire Citadel raid on Normal difficulty. The main pieces drop from specific encounters: the Helmet of the Desecrated Soul drops from Kormrok, the Mantle of the Desecrated Soul drops from Xhul'horac, the Robes of the Desecrated Soul drop from Mannoroth, the Leggings of the Desecrated Soul drop from Gorefiend, and the Gloves of the Desecrated Soul drop from Socrethar the Eternal. The matching off-set pieces are the Sash of Unhallowed Anima from the Soulbound Construct during the Gorefiend encounter, the Pious Cenobite's Slippers from Tyrant Velhari, and the Manacles of the Multitudes from Kilrogg Deadeye. All items are personal loot and are awarded directly upon defeating the boss.

### Farming Logistics
Hellfire Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can clear it once per character per week for a chance at the loot. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-20% per item, it may take between 5 to 10 weekly resets to collect all eight pieces. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any cloth-wearing class. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout and drop different color variations of this set, so you can run all four difficulties each week.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, set the raid difficulty to Normal and use a high-mobility class. After defeating Gorefiend, use the portal in his room to skip a significant portion of the raid. You can use a Seal of Inevitable Fate for a bonus roll on bosses if you have any, though acquiring this currency now is impractical. The items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No encounters require a group, though the Hellfire Assault at the beginning requires you to destroy siege engines and gates before your cannons are overwhelmed.

467.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is a lookalike of the Shaman Tier 10 set from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces are direct drops from bosses within the Icecrown Citadel raid instance set to 25-player Heroic difficulty. The head piece, Broken Ram Skull Helm, drops from Lord Marrowgar. Shoulders, Pauldrons of the Deadliest Scourge, drop from Rotface, while the chest, Ikfirus's Sack of Wonders, drops from Festergut. The hands, Flesh-Shaper's Gory Grips, and wrists, The Lady's Brittle Bracers, both drop from Lady Deathwhisper. Finally, the legs, Leggings of the Snowy Bramble, are found in Valithria Dreamwalker's chest; the feet, Rock-Steady Treads, drop from Festergut; and the waist, Belt of the Forgotten Martyr, drops from the Gunship Battle.

### Farming Logistics
The instance has a weekly raid lockout, allowing one full clear per character each week. You must set the raid difficulty to 25-player and Heroic mode before entering to be eligible for these specific items. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. As this is a lookalike set, pieces are direct boss drops and do not use the tier token system. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 weekly clears. All raid difficulties (10 Normal, 10 Heroic, 25 Normal, 25 Heroic) share a single raid ID, so you can only kill each boss once per week per character.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, use a character with high mobility. After clearing the first four bosses, a central teleporter will activate, allowing you to quickly access the Plagueworks, Crimson Hall, and Frostwing Halls. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter, which is required for the leg slot, can be completed instantly by using any high-level healing spell or bandage on the boss. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No bonus rolls or other modern systems can be used to acquire this gear.

468.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Horde-exclusive plate armor from the Battle for Azeroth Arathi Highlands Warfront. The specific appearance, which matches the Tier 10 Normal Shaman set, is the "Tier 2" Warfront reward. All pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are obtained from the cache awarded by the quest "Warfront: The Battle for Stromgarde." This quest is picked up at the War Table in Dazar'alor when the Horde is contributing to or attacking the Arathi Highlands. A different, lower-tier appearance is rewarded for completing the Warfront scenario itself.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is tied to the region-wide Warfront cycle, not a standard weekly raid lockout; you can complete the quest once per cycle, which typically lasts a few weeks. The entire process, including the Warfront scenario and the associated world boss Doom's Howl, can be fully soloed at max level. Given the quest is the only source and there are eight armor slots, expect to complete 5-10 Warfront cycles (several months) to collect the full set due to RNG. No tier tokens are involved, as pieces are awarded directly. The world boss, Doom's Howl, drops pieces of a separate, higher-tier appearance and is not a source for this specific set.

### Farming Tips
Check the War Table in the Port of Zuldazar frequently to track when the Arathi Highlands cycle is active for the Horde. Completing the end-of-scenario objective is required to turn in the quest, so focus on quickly capturing bases and gathering resources to speed up the run, which takes about 15-20 minutes. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House. Ignore the world boss if you are only farming this specific "Normal" lookalike appearance.

469.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Hunter Tier 10 armor, obtained from bosses in the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Normal difficulty. All pieces are direct boss drops. The Head, `Geist-Lord's Gaze`, drops from Lord Marrowgar. Shoulders, `Shoulder-Swords of the Blood-Troll`, drop from Festergut. The Chest, `Stalking-Viper's Tunic`, is looted from Lady Deathwhisper, and the Hands, `Scourge-Hunter's Gauntlets`, are from Rotface. Legs, `Leggings of the Broken Beast`, drop from The Lich King. The matching non-tier Waist, `Scourge-Strung Belt`, drops from the Icecrown Gunship Battle, the Wrist, `Bracers of the Glade-Stalker`, comes from the Blood Prince Council, and the Feet, `Footpads of Impending Death`, drop from Valithria Dreamwalker.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout, which is shared between 10-player and 25-player difficulties as well as Normal and Heroic modes for each boss. This means you can kill each boss only once per week per character. The entire instance is fully soloable at maximum level with trivial difficulty. Since the pieces drop directly and not from a shared token, you are competing only against drop chance. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item, a full set can be collected in approximately 4-8 weekly resets on a single character. All pieces for this lookalike set drop from the same raid instance and difficulty, 25-player Normal.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player and the mode to Normal before entering to ensure the correct items can drop. After reaching Friendly reputation with The Ashen Verdict, you can use a teleporter near the entrance to skip directly to the upper spire, saving significant time. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter is not required for progression to the final bosses and can be skipped by walking around the room if you have already obtained the feet. Note that the roleplay sequences during the final encounter with The Lich King cannot be skipped and will add several minutes to each run.

471.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-tier mail items from the Tier 11 Cataclysm raids: Blackwing Descent, The Bastion of Twilight, and Throne of the Four Winds. The Head, Helm of the Blind Seer, and Hands, Gloves of Cacophony, both drop from Atramedes in Blackwing Descent. The Shoulders, Spaulders of the Scarred Lady, drop from Chimaeron, and the Legs, Leggings of the Brood-Lord, drop from Nefarian, both also in Blackwing Descent. The Chest, Tunic of the Raging Elements, drops from Halfus Wyrmbreaker in The Bastion of Twilight. The Feet, Storm-Rider's Boots, drop from the Conclave of Wind in Throne of the Four Winds, while the Waist, Wind-Stalker's Belt, drops from Al'Akir's cache in the same raid. The Wrists, Parasitic Bands, drop from Magmaw in Blackwing Descent.

### Farming Logistics
The three raids containing this set—Blackwing Descent, The Bastion of Twilight, and Throne of the Four Winds—are on a weekly raid lockout. All three instances can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. As a lookalike set, these pieces drop directly as items and do not use a tier token system. Since the 8 required pieces drop from 8 different boss encounters spread across three separate raids, expect to need 4-6 weekly resets to collect the full set. For this raid tier, the 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a single weekly lockout, so you must choose one to complete per character each week.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raids on 10-player Normal difficulty to obtain the specific color variant for this set. In Blackwing Descent, after defeating Magmaw and the Omnotron Defense System, use the teleporter in the central chamber to quickly access Atramedes and Nefarian's Lair. The Bastion of Twilight is a linear instance with no major skips. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House, and legacy systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to these raids.

472.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Hunter Tier 10 Heroic armor, obtained from bosses in the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty. The Head, Rusted-Linked Faceguard, drops from Lord Marrowgar. The Shoulder, Taldron's Short-Sighted Pauldrons, drops from the Gunship Battle. The Chest, Cuirass of the Universal Soldier, drops from Lady Deathwhisper. The Hands, Gloves of the Blind Stalker, drop from Rotface, while the Legs, Leggings of the Northern Lights, drop from Festergut. The Feet, Treads of the Wasteland, drop from the Blood Prince Council and the Waist, Belt of the Merciless Killer, drops from the Gunship Battle.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout. The raid can be fully soloed by any class at max level. Because the pieces drop from six different encounters, collecting the full set will likely take 4-8 weekly resets, depending on luck. This lookalike set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly from bosses and can be looted by any mail-wearing class. Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single lockout, so you must clear the instance on 25-player Heroic to be eligible for these specific items.

### Farming Tips
Before entering the instance, right-click your character portrait and set the legacy raid difficulty to "25 Player (Heroic)". The Gunship Battle can be completed almost instantly by speaking to the goblin NPC on the deck to start and then immediately finish the encounter. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter is not required and can be skipped by running past the boss. No bonus rolls or other modern systems are available to supplement drops in this legacy raid.

473.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a lookalike of Paladin Tier 10 and drops from the three Icecrown Citadel 5-man dungeons: Forge of Souls, Pit of Saron, and Halls of Reflection. All pieces drop on both Normal and Heroic difficulty. The Head drops from Scourgelord Tyrannus in Pit of Saron, and the Shoulders drop from Falric in Halls of Reflection. The Chest and Feet both drop from Bronjahm in Forge of Souls. The Legs drop from Marwyn in Halls of Reflection, while the Hands drop from Forgemaster Garfrost and the Waist from Ick, both in Pit of Saron. Finally, the Wrists can be found in the Captain's Chest after the gauntlet event in Halls of Reflection.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons can be farmed on Normal difficulty, which has no lockout and allows you to reset the instance up to 10 times per hour. Alternatively, Heroic difficulty has a daily lockout per character. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character. Expect to complete 20-40 total dungeon runs to acquire all eight pieces, depending on luck. This set does not use a token system; all pieces are direct boss drops for plate-wearing classes. The appearances are shared between Normal and Heroic difficulty, so farming Normal is the most efficient method.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the three dungeons on Normal difficulty. After completing all three, exit the instance, right-click your character portrait, and select "Reset all instances" to farm again. Be prepared for the Halls of Reflection instance, which contains a long and unskippable roleplay gauntlet that significantly increases its clear time compared to the other two dungeons. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. You must be on a plate class (Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight) for the items to be added to your transmog collection.

475.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a lookalike of the Paladin Tier 10 set and is obtained from bosses within the Icecrown Citadel raid from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces drop exclusively on 25-player Normal difficulty. The Head (Broken Ram Skull Helm), Shoulders (Boneguard Commander's Pauldrons), and Wrists (Bracers of Dark Reckoning) all drop from Lord Marrowgar. Lady Deathwhisper drops both the Hands (Gauntlets of the Kraken) and Legs (Scourge Reaver's Legplates). The Chest (Blight-Interred Tunic) drops from Festergut, the Feet (Boots of the Funeral March) drop from the Icecrown Gunship Battle, and the Waist (Belt of the Lonely Noble) is looted from the Blood Prince Council.

### Farming Logistics
The raid has a weekly lockout, but the 10-player and 25-player versions are on separate lockouts, allowing two full clears per character each week. However, this specific color variation only drops from 25-player Normal difficulty. The instance can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Collecting the full 8-piece set typically takes 4-8 resets, as multiple desired items drop from the same few bosses. These lookalike pieces are direct drops and are not obtained via the Tier 10 "Conqueror" token system used by the official Paladin set.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your raid settings are configured to 25-player and Normal difficulty before entering the instance. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter is a healing-based fight that can be completely skipped by running around the edge of her room to the next area. As you progress, teleporters will activate in the entrance hall, allowing you to quickly move back to later wings. For the Gunship Battle, stay in a cannon until it overheats, then use the jetpack to board the enemy ship and defeat the enemy battle-mage to make the fight progress faster.

476.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier lookalike of the Paladin Tier 10 armor, obtained from bosses and trash mobs in the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty. The Head (Broken Ram Skull Helm) drops from Lord Marrowgar. The Chest (Cataclysmic Chestguard) drops from Lady Deathwhisper. The Hands (Gauntlets of the Kraken) are looted from Rotface. The Legs (Scourge Reaver's Legplates) and Wrist (Bracers of the Heir) both drop from the final boss, The Lich King. Several pieces, including the Shoulder (Boneguard's Pauldrons), Waist (Verdigris Chain Belt), and Feet (Greaves of the Saronite Citadel) are Bind-on-Equip items that drop from trash mobs within the instance.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel is on a weekly raid lockout, shared between 10/25-player and Normal/Heroic difficulties. You must set the difficulty to 25-player and Heroic before entering to be eligible for this set's color variant. The raid is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces required from specific bosses, a full set collection can take 5-10 weeks on average, subject to drop-rate luck. This lookalike set consists of direct-drop items and does not use the tier token system, so the pieces can be equipped and learned by any Plate class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight).

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, use a high-mobility class. Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic outside the instance; you cannot switch to Heroic once inside if you started on Normal. Several key pieces for this set are BoE drops from trash mobs, including the shoulders, waist, and feet. Always check the Auction House for these items first, as purchasing them can significantly shorten the farm. The entrance to Icecrown Citadel is in the southern part of Icecrown in Northrend, and players with Ashen Verdict reputation can use a ring to teleport directly to the instance.

477.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained by purchasing the "Ensemble: Regalia of the Eternal Lord" in Torghast, Tower of the Damned. The ensemble is sold by the vendors at the entrance to Torghast or can be purchased directly from the Box of Many Things interface. It costs 3,500 Tower Knowledge, a currency earned by completing Torghast runs. Purchasing the single ensemble item instantly adds all eight armor appearances (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) to your collection.

### Farming Logistics
Torghast has no weekly or daily lockout and can be farmed repeatedly for Tower Knowledge. The instance is designed for solo play and can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Collecting the required 3,500 Tower Knowledge will take approximately 4-7 full, high-scoring runs of the upper layers (Layer 12+). This set does not use a tier token system and is purchased directly with currency. The entire set is from this single source, and there are no separate lockouts to manage.

### Farming Tips
To farm Tower Knowledge most efficiently, complete the highest layer you can with a perfect score, which maximizes currency rewards. Before purchasing the ensemble, invest your initial Tower Knowledge into the Box of Many Things talent tree to unlock permanent upgrades that speed up future runs. The ensemble and its appearances are bind-on-pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House. Focus on choosing anima powers that grant significant movement speed or damage to clear floors as quickly as possible.

478.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a lookalike of the Death Knight Tier 10 Normal set and is obtained from the Sanctum of Domination raid in Shadowlands. All pieces drop from bosses on Normal difficulty. The Head, Helm of the Fallen Lord, drops from Kel'Thuzad. The Shoulder, Pauldrons of the Fallen Lord, drops from Sylvanas Windrunner. The Chest, Cuirass of the Fallen Lord, is from Fatescribe Roh-Kalo, and the Legs, Legplates of the Fallen Lord, are from Remnant of Ner'zhul. The Hands, Gauntlets of the Fallen Lord, drop from The Nine, while matching lookalike feet, waist, and wrist pieces drop from The Tarragrue, Soulrender Dormazain, and the Guardian of the First Ones, respectively.

### Farming Logistics
The Sanctum of Domination raid has a weekly lockout per character, per difficulty. The Normal difficulty version of this instance is fully soloable by a max-level character. Given the eight different item slots and an approximate 15-20% drop chance per piece, a player can expect to take between 5-10 weekly clears to collect the full set. This raid does not use a shared tier token system; loot drops directly for your armor type, so it must be farmed on a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts and reward different color variations of this set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty with a high-mobility plate class like a Warrior or Paladin. There are no major skips available to bypass the bosses that drop the required set pieces. All major set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Since this is legacy content, bonus rolls and the Great Vault are no longer sources for obtaining these items. You must defeat each specific boss for a chance at their designated piece of the set.

479.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Heroic plate lookalike armor from The Nighthold raid, released in the Legion expansion. The Head (Helm of the Foreseen) and Hands (Grips of the Foreseen) drop from Krosus. The Shoulders (Spaulders of the Unseen Path) and Feet (Sabatons of the Unseen Path) are looted from Tichondrius. The Chest (Breastplate of the Fiery Demise) and Waist (Waistplate of the Fiery Demise) can be obtained from the first boss, Skorpyron. The final piece, the Legs (Legguards of the Unseeing Eye), drops from Star Augur Etraeus.

### Farming Logistics
The Nighthold has a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for NA realms and Wednesday for EU realms. This set is fully soloable by any max-level character on Heroic difficulty. Assuming an average drop rate, expect to complete 5-10 weekly clears to acquire all seven pieces of the set. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces are direct drops that can be equipped by any Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight. Note that Normal, Heroic, and Mythic Nighthold are separate lockouts, but this specific red and orange color variant only drops from Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Heroic difficulty, as this specific color tint does not drop on other difficulties. Use a class with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, to move between boss rooms more quickly. Legion Bonus Rolls, purchased with a Seal of Broken Fate, can be used on each boss for an extra chance at loot per week. None of the set pieces are Bind-on-Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. To enter the raid, fly to the main Nighthold structure in Suramar and find the instance portal on a lower balcony on the western side of the building.

480.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-tier plate armor pieces from the Heroic difficulty of the Throne of Thunder raid in Mists of Pandaria. The Head (Crown of the Golden Golem) drops from Durumu the Forgotten, and the Shoulder (Shoulderguards of the Unflanked) drops from the Twin Consorts. The Chest (Battleplate of the Last Mogu) is from Iron Qon, while the Legs (Legguards of the Unforgiven) are looted from Dark Animus. The Hands (Gauntlets of the Golem-Lord) drop from Primordius, Feet (Sabatons of the Cursed Protector) from Tortos, Waist (Greatbelt of the Crendor) from Horridon, and Wrist (Bracers of Constant Implosion) from Ji-Kun.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder operates on a weekly raid lockout. The instance is fully soloable at current maximum level on all difficulties. This is a lookalike set, so the plate items drop directly and do not use a tier token system. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties of this raid have separate weekly lockouts, but this specific coloration only drops from Heroic difficulty. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per item, expect to complete 5-15 runs to acquire the full 8-piece set.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Heroic for the fastest clear, as this reduces boss health without affecting transmog drop chances. Mounts can be used to speed up travel between the first several bosses after defeating Jin'rokh the Breaker. Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currencies (Mogu Runes of Fate) are no longer obtainable or usable for this raid. All pieces in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

481.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a lookalike of the Normal Monk tier set and drops from the Sepulcher of the First Founders raid in Shadowlands. All pieces are non-tier leather items that drop from bosses on Normal difficulty. The Headcover of Ambiguous Reality drops from Anduin Wrynn, and the Pauldrons of the Predatory Pack drop from Lords of Dread. The Chestwrap of Vexing Visions drops from Rygelon, the Clandestine Leggings of the Riven drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Gloves of Obfuscated Plots drop from Lihuvim. To complete the set, the Boots of the Fleeting Stampede drop from Skolex, the Bindings of the Shadow-Swarm from Vigilant Guardian, and the Wristwraps of the First Movement from Artificer Xy'mox.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Founders raid has a weekly raid lockout per character for each difficulty. At the current maximum level, the entire raid can be soloed on Normal difficulty without issue. Given an approximate 15-25% drop chance per item, expect to complete 5-10 weekly runs to collect all eight pieces. This lookalike set drops as direct items and does not use a tier token system. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts, allowing you to farm different colorations of the set in the same week.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific green and brown color variant. Due to the age of the content, a max-level character can clear the bosses very quickly, and no group is required. The items are Bind on Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Farming cannot be supplemented with bonus rolls or the Great Vault, as those systems do not apply to legacy Shadowlands raids.

482.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather set is a lookalike of the Monk tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands, and all pieces drop on Heroic difficulty. The Head, Cowl of the Dashing Scoundrel, and Waist, Waistguard of the Dashing Scoundrel, both drop from Anduin Wrynn. The Shoulder, Spaulders of the Relentless Tracker, drops from Lihuvim, Principal Architect, while the Legs, Legguards of the Unshakable Pursuer, drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer. The Chest, Breastplate of the Stalking Devourer, and Hands, Gauntlets of the Stalking Devourer, both drop from Rygelon. Finally, the Feet, Stalkers of the Devious Schemer, and Wrist, Bracers of the Devious Schemer, drop from the Lords of Dread encounter.

### Farming Logistics
This set is subject to a weekly raid lockout per character. Sepulcher of the First Ones cannot be fully soloed at current max level due to boss mechanics on encounters like Halondrus and Anduin Wrynn, requiring a small group. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance and group loot competition, collecting the full set could take between 5-15 clears. This is a lookalike set and does not use the tier token system; the pieces drop as direct leather items usable by Druids, Rogues, Monks, and Demon Hunters. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate weekly lockouts and drop different color variations of this gear.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Heroic difficulty to obtain this specific color tint. Use the in-game Premade Group Finder to join or create transmog runs for "Sepulcher of the First Ones Heroic." There are no significant boss skips available to reach the necessary encounters; the raid must be cleared linearly up to each required boss. All pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The Great Vault and bonus roll systems from Shadowlands are no longer active sources for this gear.

483.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is a non-class lookalike of the Shaman Tier 14 set, obtained from the Normal difficulty versions of the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. The majority of the set drops in Heart of Fear: Imperial Vizier Zor'lok drops Gloves of the Unflayed, Blade Lord Ta'yak drops Shoulders of the Unflayed, Garalon drops Tunic of the Unflayed, Wind Lord Mel'jarak drops Leggings of the Unflayed and the Klaxxi Lash of the Orator belt, and Grand Empress Shek'zeer drops the Crown of the Doomed Empress. The remaining pieces are from Terrace of Endless Spring: Lei Shi drops Bracers of the Still Breath, and Tsulong drops Boots of the Still Breath.

### Farming Logistics
Both Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring operate on a weekly raid lockout per character. At the current maximum level, both instances can be fully soloed with ease by any class. As this is a lookalike set, it does not use the tier token system; the pieces are direct drops that can be looted and equipped by any mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker). Most of the set drops from Heart of Fear, but the Feet and Wrist pieces require a separate run of Terrace of Endless Spring. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing you to farm each difficulty on the same character each week for different colorations. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item, a full set could be collected in 4-8 lockouts, subject to random luck.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raids on 10-player Normal difficulty, as this has the lowest boss health without affecting the drop chance of transmog items. The most effective way to speed up collection is to run the raids on multiple mail-wearing characters each week. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Elder Charm of Good Fortune) can grant an extra chance at loot, but the currency is time-consuming to acquire and using alts is more efficient. There are no major skips in either raid; Heart of Fear is completely linear, and you must defeat the first three bosses in Terrace of Endless Spring to access the final boss.

484.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is a non-tier lookalike obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids on Heroic difficulty. The head, Horns of the Betrayer, drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The shoulders, Shoulders of the Unflanked, drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak. The chest, Chestguard of the All-Consuming Maw, drops from Garalon, and the hands, Gloves of the Blind Stalker, drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok. The legs, Legguards of the Unseeing, drop from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, while both the feet (Boots of the Still Breath) and waist (Klaxxi Lash of the Orator) drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The wrist piece, Bracers of the Deadly Flower, is the only item from Terrace of Endless Spring, dropping from the Protectors of the Endless encounter.

### Farming Logistics
Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring both operate on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. Both raids are fully soloable at the current maximum level by any class, though only Mail-wearing classes can learn the appearances. With a 15-25% drop chance per item, collecting the full set typically takes 4-8 weeks, subject to random luck. These are direct boss drops and do not use the tier token system. Note that LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, but this specific coloration only drops from Heroic. The wrist piece requires running a separate raid from the rest of the set.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Heroic for the fastest clear time, as it has the lowest mob health. In Heart of Fear, after defeating Blade Lord Ta'yak, you can use the wind gust to fly across the chasm, skipping a significant amount of trash before the Garalon encounter. Farming this set must be done on a Mail character (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker) to add the items to your wardrobe. The main set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House.

485.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Shaman Tier 15 set, obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid in Mists of Pandaria. All pieces drop from individual bosses on Normal difficulty. The Helm of the Haunted Forest drops from Jin'rokh the Breaker, and the Spaulders of the Scorned drop from Iron Qon. The Carapace of Segmented Scale drops from Tortos, while the Legguards of the Unforgiven drop from Durumu the Forgotten. The Gloves of the Thousandfold Blades drop from Council of Elders, the Abyssal Leather Treads drop from Ji-Kun, the Abandonment-Wrought Waistguard drops from Dark Animus, and the Wraps of the Unsoiled drop from Primordius.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid has a weekly lockout per character per difficulty. The instance is fully soloable at max level. As these are non-tier lookalike items, they drop directly from bosses and do not use the tier token system. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts and reward different color variants, but you can only clear Normal once per week per character for this specific appearance. With eight required pieces, collecting the full set will likely take 4-8 weekly clears on a single character, depending on drop luck.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal, as boss health is lower, making for faster kills. You can use Mists of Pandaria Bonus Rolls (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) on bosses for an additional chance at loot for a specific slot. After defeating a boss, a teleport pad will activate at the zone's entrance, allowing you to quickly return to your progress point on subsequent runs. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

486.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is a lookalike of the Shaman Tier 15 set, obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid on Heroic difficulty. The hands, Gauntlets of the Distant Sea, drop from Jin'rokh the Breaker, while the feet, Stonetoe's Tormented Treads, drop from the Council of Elders. Tortos drops the chest piece, Carapace of Segmented Scale, and Ji-Kun drops the Leggings of the Unrepentant. The shoulders, Spaulders of the Unblinking Gaze, drop from Iron Qon, the headpiece, Crown of the Doomed Empress, drops from Twin Consorts, and the Bracers of Constant Implosion drop from Primordius. The waist piece, Abandoned Zandalari Goreplate, is a zone-wide drop from trash mobs within the instance.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder has a weekly raid lockout, which is separate for LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties, allowing three chances at different colorations per character per week. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level by any class capable of wearing mail armor. Since these are direct boss drops and not tier tokens, any mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker) can loot and learn the appearance. Due to the number of items and RNG, expect the full farm to take between 5-10 weeks on a single character. The belt is the only piece not from a boss, dropping from any trash mob inside the raid.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic, as this increases the quantity of loot dropped per boss, raising your chances of obtaining a piece. The waist piece, Abandoned Zandalari Goreplate, is Bind on Equip (BoE), so check the Auction House to potentially save significant farming time. To speed up the acquisition process, run the raid on multiple mail-wearing characters each week. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are no longer functional for legacy loot and will not help you acquire these items.

487.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Shaman Tier 21 set, obtained from the Legion raid Antorus, the Burning Throne on Normal difficulty. The eight pieces of the set drop directly from specific bosses within the instance. The Head, Cowl of the Ceaseless Vigil, drops from Aggramar. The Shoulder, Pauldrons of the Unstoppable Advance, and the Hands, Gauntlets of the Blazing Bastion, both drop from Kin'garoth. The Chest, Breastplate of the Highest Truth, is a drop from the Eonar the Life-Binder encounter. The Legs, Legplates of the Ashen Path, drop from Imonar the Soulhunter, while the Waist, Waistguard of Interminable Unity, drops from Portal Keeper Hasabel. Finally, the Feet, Sabatons of the Obstinate, drop from Varimathras and the Wrist, Bracers of the Lucent Aerie, drop from the Felhounds of Sargeras.

### Farming Logistics
The raid has a weekly lockout, meaning you can kill each boss for a chance at loot once per week per character on Normal difficulty. The entire raid is easily soloable by characters at the current maximum level. As these are direct drops and not part of the tier token system, no other classes compete for the items. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate weekly lockouts, but this specific colorway only drops from Normal. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
To significantly speed up farming, complete the "Seat of the Pantheon" questline on at least one character on your account; this unlocks a teleport option allowing you to skip directly to Aggramar after killing the first boss. For the Eonar the Life-Binder encounter, use the jump pad to quickly move between platforms to intercept enemy ships and adds before they reach her. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Legion bonus rolls (Seal of Broken Fate) no longer function, so there is no way to get an extra chance at loot.

488.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set was the reward for completing the achievement "Shadowlands Keystone Master: Season Three," which is no longer obtainable. The achievement required players to attain a Mythic+ Rating of at least 2000 during Shadowlands Season 3. Upon earning the achievement, players were mailed the "Ensemble: Lightning-Charged Battlegear," which taught the appearance for all armor slots simultaneously. The set is a white and gold recolor of the Mythic-difficulty Paladin/Priest/Shaman tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid. As the season and its associated achievement have been retired, there is no current method to acquire this set.

### Farming Logistics
The set was not farmed via a traditional lockout system like a raid or dungeon. It was earned once per account by reaching the seasonal Mythic+ rating threshold, which required running numerous Mythic+ dungeons. Obtaining it required a 5-player group and could not be soloed. The number of runs needed was highly variable, but generally involved successfully completing all Shadowlands dungeons at the +15 key level or higher on both Tyrannical and Fortified weeks. There was no tier token system for this reward, and different difficulties were not a factor beyond the key level itself.

### Farming Tips
As this set can no longer be obtained, current farming methods are not applicable. During Shadowlands Season 3, players focused on forming meta group compositions with strong area-of-effect damage and survivability to handle high-level keys. Using addons to plan routes and coordinate interrupts was essential. The most efficient strategy was to complete every dungeon on both affix weeks (Tyrannical and Fortified) at a consistent key level, as rating was awarded for the best run on each. Acquiring 4-piece tier set bonuses from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid was considered a significant power increase that simplified the process.

489.content:
### How to Obtain
This armor set is a non-class lookalike of the Priest Tier 11 set, obtained from the Heroic difficulty of two Cataclysm raids: Bastion of Twilight and Blackwing Descent. The Head, Cowl of the Unseen World, drops from Atramedes in Blackwing Descent. The Shoulder, Mantle of the Elusive Knack, is from Chimaeron in Blackwing Descent. The Chest, Robes of the Burning Acolyte, drops from Halfus Wyrmbreaker in Bastion of Twilight, while the Hands, Gloves of the Raging Elements, come from the Twilight Ascendant Council in the same raid. The Legs, Leggings of the Malformed Sapling, drop from Maloriak in Blackwing Descent, and matching Feet, Melodious Slippers, drop from Atramedes. The matching Waist, Belt of the Depths, is from Valiona & Theralion, and the Wrist, Bracers of the Dark Mother, drops from the heroic-only boss Sinestra in Bastion of Twilight.

### Farming Logistics
Both Bastion of Twilight and Blackwing Descent operate on a weekly raid lockout per character. You can only clear either Normal or Heroic difficulty each week, not both, as they share a lockout. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. As the pieces are spread across two raids, collection can take between 5-15 weeks depending on drop luck. This lookalike set consists of direct boss drops and does not use the tier token system. The actual class set uses the "Conqueror" token, shared by Paladins and Warlocks, but those pieces are class-restricted.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raids on Heroic difficulty to obtain this specific red color variant of the set. In Blackwing Descent, use the teleporter in the main chamber after defeating the first two bosses to access later encounters more quickly. Since all pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Cataclysm raids are not eligible for bonus rolls or Great Vault rewards. Note that the bracers drop from Sinestra, a bonus boss who only appears after you have defeated Cho'gall on Heroic difficulty within the same lockout.

490.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from the Night Fae Covenant vendor Choofa in Ardenweald for a total of 140 Grateful Offerings and 14,000 Reservoir Anima. All eight pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are sold by this vendor. To make the items available for purchase, you must unlock and upgrade your covenant's Anima Conductor to Rank 3. Grateful Offerings are rewarded for completing the daily quests and killing the rares unlocked by channeling anima through the Anima Conductor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it is based on currency acquisition which is primarily time-gated by daily quests. The entire process is soloable at current max level. Obtaining the full set requires channeling anima to a location each day and completing the associated daily quest, which rewards 3-5 Grateful Offerings. Assuming an average of four offerings per day, collecting the 140 needed for the full set will take approximately 35 days of consistent play. This set does not use a token system and has no raid or dungeon lockout to consider.

### Farming Tips
Prioritize upgrading the Night Fae Anima Conductor to Rank 3 to unlock access to the vendor's full inventory. Focus on completing the Anima Conductor daily quests and your covenant callings to maximize Grateful Offering and Reservoir Anima gains. You can send Reservoir Anima to your Night Fae character from other characters via the Broker vendor next to the flight master in Oribos. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

491.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cloth lookalike armor from the Throne of Thunder raid, specifically from the Looking for Raid difficulty. The pieces are direct boss drops and are not acquired via tokens. The Head, Crown of the World-Breaker, drops from Twin Consorts. The Shoulders, Shoulders of the Roaring Flame, drop from Iron Qon. The Chest, Robes of the Burning Scroll, drops from Dark Animus. The Legs, Leggings of the Burning Scroll, drop from Ji-Kun. The Hands, Gloves of the Burning Scroll, drop from the Council of Elders. The Feet, Fire-Gizzard Greaves, drop from Megaera. The Waist, Girdle of the Mogu-Slayer, drops from Tortos, and the Wrists, Bracers of the Banished, drop from Horridon.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly raid lockout per character. To access the Looking for Raid version, speak to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. This instance can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will take approximately 5-10 weekly clears. This set's pieces are direct drops with unique names and do not use the tier token system. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties of Throne of Thunder are all on separate weekly lockouts and each contains a different color variation of this armor set.

### Farming Tips
The farm must be done on the Looking for Raid difficulty to obtain this specific red and orange coloration; select "The Last Stand of the Zandalari" through "Downfall of Lei Shen" from Lorewalker Han's dialogue options. Only cloth-wearing classes (Mage, Priest, Warlock) can learn these appearances upon pickup. While any class can clear the raid, using a cloth-wearer is mandatory for collection. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Using a high-mobility class like a Druid or Demon Hunter can slightly speed up the time it takes to travel between boss rooms.

492.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate lookalike set is assembled from individual epic-quality items that drop from bosses in the Dragon Soul raid on Heroic difficulty. The Waist, Girdle of Shattered Stone, drops from Morchok, while both the Hands (Gauntlets of the Corrupted Protector) and Feet (Treads of Crushed Flesh) drop from Warlord Zon'ozz. Yor'sahj the Unsleeping drops the Legs (Greaves of the Corrupted Protector), Ultraxion drops the Chest (Chestplate of the Corrupted Protector), and Hagara the Stormbinder drops the Head (Helm of Blazing Glory). Finally, both the Shoulders (Pauldrons of the Great Ettin) and Wrist (Bracers of Destructive Strength) are found on Warmaster Blackhorn's loot table.

### Farming Logistics
Dragon Soul operates on a weekly raid lockout, which is shared between the 10- and 25-player versions of Heroic difficulty. The entire instance is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. This lookalike set does not use the tier token system; the items drop directly for any plate-wearing class, unlike the class-specific tier set which uses the Conqueror token (Paladin, Priest, Warlock). Since the Looking For Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate weekly lockouts, you can farm all three color variations of the set each week on the same character. Expect to run the raid for 8-12 weeks to collect the full set due to random drop chances.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering to maximize the number of items dropped per boss. After killing the first boss, use the teleporters near the instance entrance to skip directly to later boss areas, which significantly speeds up the run. The Ultraxion encounter includes a one-minute unskippable roleplay timer, but the boss can be killed in a single hit once he becomes active. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip and cannot be purchased from the Auction House, nor are bonus rolls usable in this Cataclysm-era raid.

493.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike obtained from the Throne of Thunder (Tier 15) raid in Mists of Pandaria on Heroic difficulty. All pieces drop directly from specific bosses within the instance. The Head slot, Hood of Cursed Dreams, drops from Durumu the Forgotten. Shoulderpads of Cursed Dreams drop from Tortos, and Chestguard of Cursed Dreams drops from Megaera. Legs, Leggings of Cursed Dreams, are looted from Ji-Kun, while both the Hands, Gloves of Cursed Dreams, and Feet, Spire-Stalker's Treads, drop from Iron Qon. The matching Waist, Brutal Carnage Belt, is a drop from Horridon.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. At the current maximum level, the entire instance is easily soloable by any class. Collecting the full 7-piece set will likely take between 4-8 weekly clears, depending on drop luck. This set consists of direct item drops and does not use the tier token system. Note that the 10-player and 25-player versions of Normal and Heroic difficulty share a single weekly lockout, meaning you can only defeat each boss once per week on one of those settings. The Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty has its own separate lockout and drops a different color variation of this set.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of obtaining loot, set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering, as this increases the number of items dropped per boss. You can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune for bonus rolls on bosses, which are purchasable on the Timeless Isle. Once a wing is cleared, teleport pads at the entrance of the raid can be used to quickly travel to different sections. All items from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

494.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is the heroic-difficulty, non-tier lookalike of the Warrior Tier 16 set, obtained from the Mists of Pandaria raid, Siege of Orgrimmar. The "Crystalline Observer" pieces drop from specific bosses within the Heroic version of the instance. The Head drops from Malkorok, Shoulders from Siegecrafter Blackfuse, Chest from Garrosh Hellscream, Legs from General Nazgrim, Hands from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman, and Waist from The Fallen Protectors. The matching Wrist piece drops from Immerseus, and the matching Feet drop from Malkorok.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to a weekly raid lockout. Siege of Orgrimmar's LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing you to run each version once per week per character. The raid is fully soloable at maximum level. This set is comprised of direct drops and does not use the tier token system. Given the number of pieces required and standard drop rates, collecting the full set can take 8-12 weeks or more.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Heroic difficulty for this specific color variant. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) can be used on bosses for an extra chance at loot. The Galakras encounter is soloable by firing a cannon at the dragon, which will stun the enemies in the opposite tower, giving you time to run over and use the second cannon. After clearing the raid once on a character, teleporter scrolls become available near the start of each wing, allowing you to skip directly to later sections on subsequent runs.

495.content:
### How to Obtain
This armor set is a non-tier plate lookalike obtained from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands on Heroic difficulty. The head piece drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the shoulders drop from the Lords of Dread. The chest piece is looted from Rygelon, while the legs drop from Anduin Wrynn. The hands also drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer, the feet drop from the Vigilant Guardian, the waist is from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, and the wrists are from Artificer Xy'mox.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly raid lockout. Each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic) has a separate lockout, but this specific red and gold coloration only drops from Heroic. The raid is not soloable at current max level and requires a small group of 2-5 players or a larger organized transmog run. Given the number of pieces and the weekly lockout, expect the farm to take 4-8 resets or more. This set is comprised of direct boss drops and does not use a tier token system.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Heroic difficulty, as it is the only source for this specific appearance. Use the in-game Group Finder to search for or create "Sepulcher Transmog Run" groups. Communicate with other plate wearers in your group about trading pieces they do not need, as all items are Bind-on-Pickup. There are no major skips in the instance, so a full clear is required to reach all the bosses that drop the set pieces.

496.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor obtained from Battle for Azeroth's Arathi Highlands Warfront and world zone. It shares its appearance with the Normal difficulty plate set from the Uldir raid. Pieces are acquired from multiple sources: the quest to complete the Arathi Warfront, a random drop upon successful completion of the Warfront scenario, killing the zone's world boss (Doom's Howl for Horde, The Lion's Roar for Alliance), and from the various rare elite NPCs found throughout the Arathi Highlands. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, and Feet can drop from all sources, while the Waist and Wrist pieces are most commonly found on the rare elites.

### Farming Logistics
The Arathi Warfront scenario is not on a weekly lockout and can be queued for at any time, solo, by speaking with the Warfront NPC in Boralus or Dazar'alor. The rare elites that drop set pieces in the Arathi Highlands zone are on a daily lockout per character. The world boss can only be killed for loot once per character during its active cycle. The entire farm is easily soloable at current max level, and collecting the full set can take anywhere from 10 to 40+ rare kills due to RNG. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are Bind-on-Pickup.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient strategy is to farm the Arathi Highlands rare elites daily on multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) to maximize your chances. Always kill the world boss on all available plate alts when it is active, as it provides a guaranteed piece of loot from the Warfront table. Queuing for the Warfront scenario itself is less time-efficient for farming than clearing the rares. The set pieces cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House.

497.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a recolor of tier gear found in the Shadowlands raid, Sepulcher of the First Ones. The main set pieces are obtained by looting tier tokens from specific bosses, while lookalike off-pieces drop from other bosses within the same raid. The Head token drops from Anduin Wrynn, the Shoulder token from the Lords of Dread, and the Chest token from Rygelon. The Hands token is looted from Lihuvim, Principal Architect, and the Legs token drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer. The matching Waist can be found on the Vigilant Guardian, the Feet from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, and the Wrists from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout for each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic), and each difficulty rewards a different coloration of the set and must be cleared separately. The raid is fully soloable on Normal difficulty at current max level, though some mechanics on Heroic or Mythic bosses like Halondrus or Anduin may require a small group. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all 8 pieces will likely take 5-10 weekly clears due to the random nature of drops. This set uses a tier token system shared among specific classes; the token for this set is redeemable by Hunters, Shamans, and Evokers.

### Farming Tips
For the quickest farm, clear the raid on Normal difficulty, as this is the easiest difficulty to solo and provides the target appearance. There are no significant skips in Sepulcher of the First Ones, so you must defeat the bosses in a mostly linear order to reach the ones that drop the tokens. When a tier token drops, you must right-click it in your bags to learn the appearance, as it is not automatically added to the Wardrobe on loot. The Great Vault system from Shadowlands is no longer active for this content, and the pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

498.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a lookalike of Death Knight Tier 7 and is obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid (Tier 15). The pieces drop directly from bosses within the Normal and Heroic difficulties. The Head, Crown of the Doomed Empress, drops from Twin Consorts. Pauldrons of the Last Mogu drop from Iron Qon, and the Chestplate of the Dead-Eyed Seer drops from Durumu the Forgotten. The Legs, Legplates of the Disgraced Sage, are from Ji-Kun, while the Hands, Gauntlets of the Corrupted Conqueror, are from the final boss, Lei Shen. Matching Feet, Stompers of the Buried Giant, drop from Primordius.

### Farming Logistics
This raid operates on a weekly raid lockout per difficulty and size combination. A character can clear 10-player Normal, 25-player Normal, 10-player Heroic, and 25-player Heroic each week, providing four separate chances at this specific appearance. The entire raid is easily soloable by any max-level character. This set does not use a tier token system; plate items drop directly and can be looted by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. Assuming average luck, collecting the full six-piece set will take approximately 5-10 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
Set the difficulty to 25-player Heroic to maximize the amount of loot dropped per boss, which increases the probability of obtaining set pieces. After defeating the first six bosses, you can use the teleport pad at the start of the instance to skip directly to the second half, saving time on subsequent clears. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Farming on multiple plate-wearing characters is the most effective way to increase your weekly attempts.

499.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the 25-player version of Ulduar, a Wrath of the Lich King raid. The five main tier pieces are purchased using tokens that drop from specific bosses, while matching non-set pieces drop directly. The blue and gold lookalike items, usable by any plate class, also drop directly from bosses within the same instance.

*   **Head:** Drops from Mimiron (lookalike) or bought with a token from Thorim.
*   **Shoulder:** Drops from General Vezax (lookalike) or bought with a token from Yogg-Saron.
*   **Chest:** Drops from Yogg-Saron (lookalike) or bought with a token from Hodir.
*   **Legs:** Drops from General Vezax (lookalike) or bought with a token from Freya.
*   **Hands:** Drops from Ignis the Furnace Master (lookalike) or bought with a token from Mimiron.
*   **Waist:** `Belt of the Betrayed` drops from Auriaya.
*   **Feet:** `Greaves of the Iron Army` drops from XT-002 Deconstructor.
*   **Wrist:** `Bracers of the Untold Story` drops from The Assembly of Iron.

### Farming Logistics
Ulduar operates on a weekly raid lockout, with 10-player and 25-player difficulties having separate lockouts and different color gear. To obtain this specific set, you must clear the 25-player version. The entire raid is fully soloable by any class at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all pieces may take 5-10 weekly clears due to loot randomization. The tier set itself is obtained via the "Vanquisher" token, which is shared by Death Knights, Druids, Mages, and Rogues. Warriors and Paladins must collect the non-tier lookalike pieces that drop directly from bosses.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, set the raid difficulty to 25-player before entering. Using the teleporters inside the instance allows you to move quickly between the four main wings after clearing the initial bosses. Tier tokens do not automatically grant the appearance upon looting; you must take the physical token to a tier set vendor, such as Dubin Clay in Dalaran (Northrend), to exchange it for the armor piece. The non-tier lookalike items and weapons are added to your collection immediately upon being looted and are not class-restricted beyond wearing plate armor.

500.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a lookalike of the Death Knight Tier 14 and is obtained from bosses within the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. The specific items drop from bosses across both instances on Normal or Heroic difficulty. The LFR difficulty provides a different color variation.

- **Head:** Drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear.
- **Shoulder:** Drops from Blade Lord Ta'yak in Heart of Fear.
- **Chest:** Drops from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring.
- **Legs:** Drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak in Heart of Fear.
- **Hands:** Drops from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok in Heart of Fear.
- **Waist:** Drops from Amber-Shaper Un'sok in Heart of Fear.
- **Wrist:** Drops from Tsulong in Terrace of Endless Spring.
- **Feet:** Matching boots drop from Garalon in Heart of Fear.

### Farming Logistics
Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring are subject to a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at max level. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic versions of this set have separate lockouts, allowing you to run each difficulty once per week per character. Since the lookalike pieces are direct drops, this set does not use the tier token system. Based on an approximate 15-25% drop chance per item, expect to complete the set in 4-8 weekly resets, depending on luck.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raids on Normal difficulty, as enemies have the lowest health while still dropping the epic version of the set. You must be on a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight) for the items to be added to your appearance collection. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or bought on the Auction House. There are no skips in Terrace of Endless Spring, and the route through Heart of Fear is mostly linear after the first boss. Bonus rolls are no longer available for Mists of Pandaria content.

501.content:
### How to Obtain
