This set is the non-class plate lookalike of the Death Knight Tier 15 armor, obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid, which was the Tier 15 instance in Mists of Pandaria. The pieces drop directly from individual bosses within the raid. The Head drops from Dark Animus, and the Shoulders drop from Iron Qon. The Chest is looted from Ji-Kun, while the Legs are a drop from Horridon. The Hands can be obtained from the Council of Elders, the Feet from Megaera, the Waist from Tortos, and the Wrists from the first boss, Jin'rokh the Breaker.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder operates on a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces and random drop chances, expect to run the raid for 4-8 weeks per character to collect the full set. This set does not use a tier token system; the armor pieces drop directly and are only usable by plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have separate lockouts and drop different color variations of the set, allowing for three full clears per week on a single character for three chances at the appearance.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as LFR requires queuing via Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge and Heroic offers no speed advantage. A significant shortcut exists after defeating Tortos; a door to the left of the boss's cavern leads directly to Megaera's platform, skipping a large trash pack. When fighting Dark Animus, focus all damage on the large Anima Golem in the center of the room to prevent the boss from activating and wiping you with its interrupt ability. The set pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

502.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set epic items that drop from various level 85 Cataclysm Heroic dungeons. The Head slot, Cowl of the Unseen, drops from Siamat in Lost City of the Tol'vir. The Shoulder slot, Mantle of the Spiders, and the Legs, Leggings of the Path, also drop in Lost City of the Tol'vir from General Husam and Lockmaw, respectively. The Chest, Karoma's Robes, drops from Anraphet in Halls of Origination, and the Hands, Gloves of the Greymane Wall, drop from Lord Godfrey in Heroic Shadowfang Keep. The Feet, Awakening Footfalls, come from Corborus in The Stonecore, while the Waist, Stratosphere Belt, drops from Altairus in The Vortex Pinnacle; there is no matching wrist piece for this set.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from multiple instances, each on a daily Heroic dungeon lockout per character. All Cataclysm dungeons can be fully soloed by a maximum-level character, with bosses and trash mobs being trivial encounters. Given the number of pieces spread across different dungeons and a standard 15-25% drop chance, collecting the full set may take 1-2 weeks of daily clears. There is no tier token system; all items are direct boss drops that can be looted by any class. You must run each instance on Heroic difficulty, as the items do not drop on Normal.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic before entering each instance. The Vortex Pinnacle allows the use of mounts throughout most of the instance, making it the fastest to clear. In The Stonecore, use the rock worms after the second boss to quickly travel to Corborus. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Characters with high mobility, such as Druids or Demon Hunters, can significantly reduce the time spent running between bosses.

503.content:
### How to Obtain
This armor set is a non-class plate lookalike of the Death Knight Tier 10 set, acquired from the Icecrown Citadel raid in the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces drop from the 25-player Normal difficulty setting. The Head, `Broken Ram Skull Helm`, drops from Lord Marrowgar. The Shoulders, `Pauldrons of Lost Hope`, drop from Lady Deathwhisper. The Chest, `Blightborne Warplate`, drops from Rotface. The Legs, `Legplates of Bloody Reprisal`, drop from Deathbringer Saurfang. The Hands, `Gauntlets of the Kraken`, drop from Festergut. Matching non-set pieces are the Waist (`Belt of the Lonely Noble` from the Gunship Battle), Feet (`Boots of the Frozen Seed` from Valithria Dreamwalker), and Wrists (`Bracers of the Heir` from Professor Putricide).

### Farming Logistics
The raid instance Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout. You can clear the 25-player Normal version once per character per week. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level with trivial difficulty. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance per item, a full set could be collected in approximately 5-10 resets, subject to luck. Death Knights can also obtain this appearance by collecting the Vanquisher's Mark of Conquest tier tokens (shared with Rogues, Mages, and Druids) and upgrading them with a Mark of Sanctification from The Lich King, but farming the direct-drop lookalike items is more efficient for all plate classes. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties are separate lockouts, but this specific color only drops in 25-player Normal.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player before entering the instance to ensure the correct loot table. For speed, use the teleporter in the central spire to quickly navigate between the different wings of the raid after the first four bosses are defeated. The Gunship Battle can be completed in under a minute by using your character's cannon to launch onto the enemy ship and defeating the enemy commander directly. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter can be skipped entirely by running past the boss if you do not need the boots from her loot table.

504.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Death Knight Tier 10 armor, obtained from the Icecrown Citadel raid in the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The appearance requires two components: a base tier token and a Heroic-difficulty upgrade token called Conqueror's Mark of Sanctification (Heroic). Both tier tokens and non-set matching pieces drop from bosses in the 25-player Heroic version of the raid. The upgrade token drops from Professor Putricide, Blood-Queen Lana'thel, Sindragosa, and The Lich King.

*   **Head:** Token drops from The Lich King.
*   **Shoulder:** Token drops from Lady Deathwhisper.
*   **Chest:** Token drops from Lady Deathwhisper.
*   **Legs:** Token drops from Lord Marrowgar.
*   **Hands:** Token drops from Lord Marrowgar.
*   **Waist:** Belt of Broken Bones drops from Lord Marrowgar.
*   **Feet:** Boneshatter Greaves drop from Lord Marrowgar.
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of Dark Reckoning drop from Lord Marrowgar.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel has a weekly raid lockout, shared between 10/25 player modes and Normal/Heroic difficulties, so each boss can be looted once per week per character. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to the multiple tokens and specific off-set pieces required, collecting the full set can take 5-10 runs on average. The base tier token is the "Protector's Mark of Conquest", shared by Death Knight, Rogue, Druid, and Mage. You must loot both the base tier token and the Heroic Mark of Sanctification to unlock the appearance. The matching feet, Greaves of the Saronite Citadel, are a popular alternative that drop from the final boss of the Pit of Saron dungeon on a separate daily lockout.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-Player Heroic before entering the instance to ensure the correct items drop. You can skip healing the boss Valithria Dreamwalker by walking past her encounter, which saves several minutes. The Gunship Battle can be completed in under a minute by immediately using your cannon's secondary ability to build heat, firing once, then boarding the enemy ship to defeat their leader. Upon looting a tier token, the base appearance is automatically added to your wardrobe; looting the Conqueror's Mark of Sanctification (Heroic) while having the base appearance collected will unlock the Sanctified version. Farming on a Death Knight is required to wear and transmog the final plate set.

505.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty plate armor from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Mists of Pandaria. The five main tier pieces are obtained by looting tokens from specific bosses and exchanging them with an armor vendor. The matching non-tier waist, feet, and wrist pieces drop directly from other bosses within the same raid.

*   **Head:** Token (Helm of the Cursed Protector) from Thok the Bloodthirsty
*   **Shoulder:** Token (Pauldrons of the Cursed Protector) from Siegecrafter Blackfuse
*   **Chest:** Token (Chest of the Cursed Protector) from Sha of Pride
*   **Legs:** Token (Leggings of the Cursed Protector) from General Nazgrim
*   **Hands:** Token (Gauntlets of the Cursed Protector) from Malkorok
*   **Waist:** Greatbelt of the Cursed Protector from Immerseus
*   **Feet:** Sabatons of the Cursed Protector from Malkorok
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of the Cursed Protector from Kor'kron Dark Shaman

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American servers. The entire instance can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. Given there are eight unique pieces dropping from eight different encounters, collecting the full set will likely take 4-8 weekly clears depending on drop luck. The tier tokens used for this set are "Protector" tokens, which are shared by Warrior, Hunter, Shaman, and Monk classes. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts and loot tables, so you must run on Normal to obtain this specific color variation.

### Farming Tips
To farm this set, run the raid on Normal difficulty. After defeating Galakras, use the portal in the tower to skip directly to the Iron Juggernaut area, saving significant time. You can further optimize by farming the set on any of the four "Protector" token classes (Warrior, Hunter, Shaman, Monk), as collecting the token and turning it in will unlock the appearance account-wide for all plate-wearing characters. Players can use Mogu Runes of Fate, purchased with Lesser Charms of Good Fortune, for a bonus roll on bosses for an extra chance at loot. None of the pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

506.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate armor from the Tier 16 raid, Siege of Orgrimmar, on Heroic difficulty. Each piece is a direct drop from a specific boss within the instance. The Head, Greathelm of the Last Mogu, drops from Iron Juggernaut. The Shoulders, Pauldrons of the Last Mogu, are from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman. The Chest, Breastplate of the Last Mogu, is looted from the He Softfoot, Rook Stonetoe, & Sun Tenderheart council encounter. The Legs, Legplates of the Last Mogu, come from General Nazgrim, while the Hands, Gauntlets of the Last Mogu, drop from Malkorok. To complete the set, the Feet, Greaves of the Last Mogu, are from Sha of Pride; the Waist, Waistplate of the Last Mogu, is from the Spoils of Pandaria event; and the Wrist, Bracers of the Last Mogu, drops from Galakras.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid has a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic versions of the raid are all on separate lockouts, allowing you to run each one once per week for different color variations. This specific red set only drops from Heroic difficulty and can be fully soloed at current max level by any plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). This set does not use the tier token system; the items drop directly from bosses. Assuming a standard drop rate, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 weeks of clearing the instance.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering to maximize the amount of loot dropped per boss under legacy loot rules. You can use Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) for an extra chance at loot from bosses; these can be purchased from the Ethereal vendor on the main floor of your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. After you have cleared the raid once on a character, you can use the raid teleporter scroll near the entrance to skip directly to later wings on subsequent weeks. For the Galakras encounter, you must use the tower cannons to shoot down drakes before the boss will land, which can be time-consuming when solo.

507.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Death Knight Tier 13 armor, obtained from the Dragon Soul raid in Cataclysm on Normal difficulty. The five main tier pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses, while the matching waist, feet, and wrist are non-tier boss drops. The Head token drops from Warmaster Blackhorn, Shoulder from Hagara the Stormbinder, Chest from Ultraxion, Legs from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, and Hands from Warlord Zon'ozz. For the remaining slots, the Waist drops from Warlord Zon'ozz, the Feet from Ultraxion, and the Wrist from the Madness of Deathwing encounter. Several bosses also drop non-tier lookalike items for the Head, Chest, and Leg slots which can be used to complete the set.

### Farming Logistics
The Dragon Soul raid has a weekly raid lockout, and the Normal and Heroic difficulties share this lockout. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces and reliance on both token and direct drops, expect to run the raid for 4-8 weeks on a single character to collect the full set. The tier pieces use the Vanquisher token, which is shared by Death Knights, Druids, Mages, and Rogues. To collect the appearance on a Warrior or Paladin, you must loot the non-tier lookalike pieces, as they cannot use the Death Knight's token.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Normal to maximize the amount of loot dropped per boss, increasing your chances of getting the non-tier pieces. On the Spine of Deathwing encounter, stay in one of the glowing craters left by a destroyed Amalgamation to avoid being rolled off his back. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your collection; you must take the "Gauntlets of the Vanquished Vanquisher" (or equivalent token) to a Tier 13 vendor in Stormwind or Orgrimmar to exchange it for the actual armor piece. None of the pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

508.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Heroic difficulty leather tier armor from the Tier 14 Mists of Pandaria raids: Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring. The five main tier pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses, while lookalike non-tier pieces for the Waist, Feet, and Wrist slots drop directly from other bosses.

*   **Head:** Token of the Shadowy Conqueror from Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear)
*   **Shoulder:** Token of the Shadowy Conqueror from Lei Shi (Terrace of Endless Spring)
*   **Chest:** Token of the Shadowy Conqueror from Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear)
*   **Legs:** Token of the Shadowy Conqueror from Wind Lord Mel'jarak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Hands:** Token of the Shadowy Conqueror from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok (Heart of Fear)
*   **Waist:** Lock-Twist Waistguard from Amber-Shaper Un'sok (Heart of Fear)
*   **Feet:** Boots of the Still Breath from Blade Lord Ta'yak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of Violent Meditation from Garalon (Heart of Fear)

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids are on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday in North America. For Mists of Pandaria raids, Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same lockout, meaning you can only kill each boss once per week on either Normal or Heroic. The set is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces may take between 5 to 15 weekly clears due to the random nature of drops. The tier pieces use the Protector token, which is shared by Monk, Druid, Rogue, and Warrior classes. Only the specified leather-wearing classes will receive this set's appearance from the token.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering to maximize the amount of loot per boss, which increases the chances of an item dropping. Tier tokens are automatically added to your wardrobe upon being looted; you do not need to find a vendor to turn them in. There are no major skips in Heart of Fear, but you can use mounts in some outdoor sections to travel faster between bosses. All items from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House.

509.content:
### How to Obtain
The set is obtained from three Tier 4 raids: Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair. The five main set pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses. The Head token drops from Prince Malchezaar in Karazhan, the Shoulder token drops from High King Maulgar in Gruul's Lair, and the Chest token drops from Magtheridon in Magtheridon's Lair. The Legs token drops from Gruul the Dragonkiller in Gruul's Lair, and the Hands token drops from The Curator in Karazhan. Matching non-set Waist and Feet pieces drop from Moroes in Karazhan, and matching Wrist pieces drop from Maiden of Virtue in Karazhan.

### Farming Logistics
The three source instances (Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, Magtheridon's Lair) are all on a weekly raid lockout that resets on Tuesdays. The entire set can be fully soloed at current maximum level. Expect to take 3-6 weeks to collect all pieces, depending on token drop luck across the three separate lockouts. This set uses the "Fallen Defender" tier token, which is shared by Paladins, Shamans, and Druids; only a Druid can use this token to create the Cenarion Raiment pieces. The Waist, Wrist, and Feet are direct drops from Karazhan and do not use the token system. Burning Crusade raids only have a single difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts to manage.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter, to clear Karazhan quickly as it is a large instance. Gruul's Lair and Magtheridon's Lair are very short, linear raids with no available skips. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your wardrobe; a Druid must loot the token, take it to a vendor in Shattrath City to purchase the gear piece, and then the appearance will be learned account-wide for all leather-wearing classes. No pieces from this set are Bind-on-Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

510.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Druid Tier 4 armor, obtained from three raids in The Burning Crusade: Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair. The armor is acquired by looting tokens from specific bosses, which are then turned in for the gear piece. The Head token drops from Prince Malchezaar in Karazhan. The Shoulder token drops from High King Maulgar in Gruul's Lair. The Chest token drops from Magtheridon in Magtheridon's Lair. The Legs token drops from Gruul the Dragonkiller in Gruul's Lair. The Hands token drops from The Curator in Karazhan. The original set does not include matching Waist, Wrist, or Feet pieces.

### Farming Logistics
All three raids operate on a separate weekly raid lockout. These instances can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. Given that each relevant boss guarantees a token drop and the token is shared by only three classes, a player can expect to collect the full five-piece set in approximately 2-4 weekly resets. The set uses the "Fallen Hero" tier token, which is shared by Druids, Mages, and Shamans. While any of these classes can loot the token, only a Druid can use it to create a piece of the Stormrage Raiment. These raids only have a single difficulty, so there are no separate LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic lockouts.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, mount up inside the instances where possible and focus only on the required bosses. In Karazhan, you can head directly to the Opera Event and then The Curator, followed by Prince Malchezaar, skipping most of the instance. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your collection upon looting; you must right-click the token item in your inventory on a Druid character to create the armor piece and learn its appearance. All pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House.

511.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Mythic-difficulty cloth lookalike version of the Priest Tier 17 armor, obtained from the Blackrock Foundry raid in Warlords of Draenor. The eight pieces of the set drop from specific bosses within the Mythic version of the instance. The Genesis Helm drops from Operator Thogar, Genesis Shoulderpads from the Iron Maidens, and Genesis Robes from The Blast Furnace. Genesis Leggings are looted from Flamebender Ka'graz, Genesis Handwraps from Gruul, Genesis Boots from Beastlord Darmac, Genesis Cord from Blackhand, and the Genesis Bracers from Oregorger.

### Farming Logistics
The Blackrock Foundry raid has a weekly raid lockout, and the Mythic difficulty has a separate lockout from Normal and Heroic. The instance is fully soloable by a max-level character, with all boss mechanics being trivialized by modern damage output. Since this is a lookalike set, it drops as direct loot and does not use a tier token system. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full 8-piece set can take approximately 8-16 weekly resets, though this is subject to random luck. All pieces drop from within the same instance and difficulty.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Mythic difficulty, as this specific coloration does not drop on any other setting. Characters with high mobility, such as Monks or Demon Hunters, can clear the instance faster due to the significant travel time between bosses. After defeating the first three wings, a train in the main hall provides a shortcut to the Iron Maidens' platform, which then grants access to the final boss, Blackhand. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

512.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is obtained from non-tier mail armor pieces dropped in the Sepulcher of the First Founders raid from Shadowlands. The Head piece drops from Lihuvim, Principal Architect, and the Shoulder piece drops from the Lords of Dread. The Chest piece is looted from Rygelon, while the Leg piece drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer. The Hands drop from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, the Feet from Artificer Xy'mox, the Waist from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Wrist from the Prototype Pantheon. Each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic) drops a different color variation of the set.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Founders raid operates on a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic are all separate lockouts, allowing for four chances per week per character to obtain pieces. At the current maximum level, the raid can be fully soloed on all difficulties. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per boss, collecting the full 8-piece set may take between 5 to 10 weekly clears, though this is subject to random luck. The Shaman tier set pieces, which share this appearance, are created from Zenith tokens (shared with Hunter and Monk), but this guide focuses on the direct-drop lookalike pieces.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as it presents minimal challenge to a max-level character and has a more straightforward loot system than LFR. To access the LFR version for its specific color tint, speak to Zo'sorg in Oribos. There are no significant boss skips, but using a class with high mobility will speed up the time spent moving between encounters. Neither bonus rolls nor the Great Vault are available or relevant for farming this older content. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

513.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the mail covenant armor for the Night Fae and is purchased from a vendor after reaching specific Renown levels. The vendor is Mar'lan, the Renown Quartermaster, located in the Heart of the Forest in Ardenweald. The full set is unlocked for purchase with Reservoir Anima by progressing the Night Fae Covenant campaign and reaching Renown level 37. The individual pieces are unlocked incrementally: the Cloak at Renown 26; the Wrists, Hands, Waist, and Feet at Renown 32; and the Head, Shoulders, Chest, and Legs at Renown 37.

### Farming Logistics
There is no weekly or daily lockout for obtaining this set; progress is gated by acquiring Renown and Anima. The entire process can be completed solo at the current maximum level. From a starting point of zero, a player can expect to spend several hours farming the required Renown by completing Shadowlands campaign quests, world quests, and dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system. The appearance is account-wide for all mail-wearing characters once unlocked, but the character purchasing the items must be a member of the Night Fae covenant.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to gain Renown is to complete the entire Night Fae covenant campaign storyline, as each chapter awards a large amount. Supplement this by completing World Quests in Shadowlands zones and running Shadowlands dungeons, which have a chance to award Renown tokens upon completion. The set pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Once the set is purchased and the appearances are learned, you can switch covenants without losing access to the transmog.

514.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier, leather lookalike of the Druid Tier 6 armor, available from the Throne of Thunder raid (Tier 15). The specific green recolor only drops from the Raid Finder (LFR) difficulty. The head piece, Poison-Tipped Headguard, drops from Ji-Kun. The shoulder piece, Spaulders of the Unblinking Gaze, and matching feet, Boots of the Still Breath, drop from Tortos. The chest, Living Fluid Chestguard, and matching wrists, Bracers of the Buried Room, drop from Primordius. The leg piece, Leggings of the Poisoned Soul, drops from Megaera. The hands, Poison-Binder's Gloves, and a matching waist, Seal of the Shaman-King, drop from the Council of Elders.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder is on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday in North America. The entire instance can be soloed easily at maximum level. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties share one loot-based lockout per week, while Mythic has a separate lockout; however, this specific green set only drops from LFR. Given an approximate 15-25% drop rate per item, collecting all eight pieces may take 4-7 weekly clears. The main tier set from this raid uses the Vanquisher token (shared by Rogue, Death Knight, Mage, Druid), but this guide's lookalike pieces drop directly from bosses and do not use the token system.

### Farming Tips
To farm this set's specific color, you must run the Throne of Thunder raid on Raid Finder difficulty. This version is accessed by speaking to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located high up in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms in Pandaria. You can queue for the specific wings of the raid: "The Forgotten Depths" for Council of Elders and Tortos, "Halls of Flesh-Shaping" for Primordius and Megaera, and "Pinnacle of Storms" for Ji-Kun. All listed pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House. No major skips are available for these bosses, so a clear of each relevant wing is required.

515.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid, the final raid tier of Shadowlands. The main set pieces (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands) are obtained via tier tokens or as direct drops from specific bosses. The Headpiece token drops from Anduin Wrynn, the Shoulder token drops from Lords of Dread, the Chest token drops from Rygelon, the Legs token drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Hands token drops from Lihuvim. The matching non-set pieces drop from other bosses: the Waist from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener, the Feet from the Vigilant Guardian, and the Wrists from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic), and all four can be completed each week for separate chances at loot. The entire raid is soloable by a max-level character on all difficulties. Due to the number of pieces and drop chance, expect an average of 4-7 full clears to collect the set. Mage, Priest, and Warlock do not share a single tier token; Mage uses the Mystic token (shared with Hunter and Druid), Priest uses the Venerated token (shared with Paladin and Shaman), and Warlock uses the Dreadful token (shared with Death Knight and Demon Hunter).

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by using the Creation Catalyst in Zereth Mortis. You can obtain eligible gear from any boss in the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid and convert it into a tier set piece, bypassing token RNG. Running the raid on Normal difficulty provides the fastest clears while still yielding gear that can be converted. No tier pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

516.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-elite cosmetic appearance of the Shadowlands Season 3 PvP armor, purchased as an armor-type-specific ensemble. The full set is sold by Zo'kuul, the PvP Vendor in Oribos, located in The Enclave at coordinates (34.8, 56.6). You must purchase the ensemble corresponding to your character's primary armor type: Cosmic Gladiator's Cloth, Leather, Mail, or Plate Ensemble. Each ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor and instantly teaches the appearances for all armor slots, including Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the Marks of Honor currency can be obtained continuously from participating in PvP activities. The entire set can be farmed solo by queuing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and brawls. Obtaining the 12 Marks of Honor required for the ensemble will take approximately 6-12 hours of active play, depending on your win/loss ratio. This set is purchased directly from a vendor and does not use a tier token system. The Elite color variation of this set was a seasonal reward and is no longer obtainable.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl whenever it is active, as it offers fast, guaranteed wins against AI opponents. Additionally, check for the weekly quests in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Arenas" or "Observing Battlegrounds", which reward Marks of Honor for minimal participation. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Ensure you purchase the correct ensemble for your desired armor type, as they are named similarly.

517.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty cosmetic "Garb of the First Eidolon" armor from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. The Head, Garb of the First Eidolon's Headdress, drops from Anduin Wrynn. The Shoulders, Garb of the First Eidolon's Spaulders, drop from the Lords of Dread, while the Chest, Garb of the First Eidolon's Tunic, drops from Rygelon. The Legs, Garb of the First Eidolon's Leggings, drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Hands, Garb of the First Eidolon's Handwraps, drop from Lihuvim, Principal Architect. The Feet, Waist, and Wrist items (Treads, Cord, and Vambraces) all drop from the final boss, The Jailer.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly raid lockout per difficulty. While most bosses are soloable by a geared max-level character, mechanics on bosses like Halondrus and Anduin Wrynn can be difficult, and a small group is recommended for a guaranteed clear. This set is a cosmetic collection that drops as direct items; it does not use the raid's tier token system and the pieces can be looted by any class. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are separate lockouts that award different color versions of the set. Expect to complete 5-10 full clears to collect all eight pieces due to RNG.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific color variant; using the in-game group finder for "transmog runs" can simplify the process. After defeating key bosses, teleporters activate at the raid's entrance, allowing you to skip directly to later sections of the instance on subsequent visits. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. This cosmetic set must be looted directly from bosses and cannot be created using the Creation Catalyst, as that system only creates class-specific tier set appearances.

518.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike armor from the Heart of Fear raid on Looking for Raid difficulty. The items are not part of a tier set and drop directly from bosses. The Head and Legs drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, the Shoulder drops from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, and the Chest drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The Hands drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak, the Feet from Garalon, and both the Waist and Wrist pieces drop from the first boss, Imperial Vizier Zor'lok.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear raid has a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can loot each boss once per character per week for this specific appearance. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level. Due to the random nature of drops, collecting all eight pieces can take approximately 5-15 weekly clears. This set does not use the tier token system; plate items drop directly and can be looted by any Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight. Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate weekly lockouts and reward different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
To access the Looking for Raid version of Heart of Fear, speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms and queue for the "The Dread Approach" and "Nightmare of Shek'zeer" wings. No skips are necessary as a max-level character can defeat bosses in seconds. The Mists of Pandaria bonus roll system is no longer active for this content, and all items are Bind on Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

520.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-class lookalike version of the Mage tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. The pieces drop directly from specific bosses within the raid and can be looted by any Mage, Priest, or Warlock. The Head piece drops from Anduin Wrynn, and the Shoulder piece drops from Lords of Dread. The Chest piece is looted from Rygelon, while the Legs and Waist both drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer. The Hands can be found on Lihuvim, Principal Architect, the Feet on Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, and the Wrists on the Vigilant Guardian.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per character, per difficulty. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts and award different color variations of the set. At current max level, a well-geared character can solo the Normal difficulty, but Heroic and Mythic difficulties will require a group due to boss mechanics on encounters like Anduin Wrynn and Halondrus. While Priests and Warlocks must farm the specific lookalike pieces, Mages can also obtain this appearance by looting Mystic tier tokens (shared with Druids and Hunters) and converting them into their class set pieces. The average farm will take 5-10 weeks per difficulty due to the nature of random drops.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty, which is largely soloable. For higher difficulties and their unique color tints, use the Premade Group Finder to join "transmog runs." Mages can use the Creation Catalyst in Zereth Mortis to convert non-set raid, M+, or PvP items from that season into tier set pieces, which provides a powerful bad-luck protection system for acquiring the appearance. The main armor pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, but lookalike belts, bracers, and boots can sometimes be found as Bind-on-Equip world drops within the raid, so check the Auction House.

521.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor lookalike from the Throne of Thunder raid on Looking For Raid (LFR) difficulty. The pieces are direct drops from specific bosses within the instance. The Head, "Runetotem's Skullcap," drops from the Twin Consorts, and the Shoulders, "Runetotem's Mantle," drop from Iron Qon. The Chest, "Runetotem's Hauberk," is from Dark Animus, while the Legs, "Runetotem's Legguards," come from Ji-Kun. The Hands, "Runetotem's Gauntlets," drop from the Council of Elders, the Waist, "Abandoned Zandalari Bucklebreach," is from Tortos, the Feet, "Treads of the Wandering Nomad," drop from Megaera, and the Wrists, "Bracers of the Gilded Fan," are from Primordius.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can kill each boss for a chance at loot once per week per character. The entire instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Given the eight pieces required and standard drop rates, a complete set can take approximately 4-8 weeks to farm on a single character. This set does not use a tier token system; each armor piece drops directly for Plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). The LFR difficulty has a separate lockout from Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties, and this specific color variation of the set is exclusive to LFR.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version of Throne of Thunder, speak to Lorewalker Han inside the Seat of Knowledge in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms in Pandaria. Players can use a Mogu Rune of Fate for a bonus roll on bosses, providing an extra chance at loot each week. These runes can be obtained on the Timeless Isle or by trading in Lesser Charms of Good Fortune. To speed up the run, use a high-mobility class and after defeating Tortos, jump directly down into Megaera's pit to skip the connecting hallway. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

522.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier leather lookalike found in the Firelands raid, which is the Tier 12 instance. Most pieces drop from specific bosses within the 10-player Normal difficulty of the raid. The Head, [Cowl of the Clicking Queen], and Legs, [Cinderweb Leggings], both drop from Beth'tilac. The Shoulders, [Flickering Shoulderpads], are a drop from Lord Rhyolith, while the Chest, [Carapace of Imbibed Flame], drops from Baleroc. The Hands, [Clutches of the Ripening Inferno], drop from Majordomo Staghelm, the Waist, [Firearrow Belt], drops from Shannox, and the Wrist, [Chains of the Roaring Dragon], drops from Alysrazor. The Feet, [Flippers of Both Ponderous and Swift], are an exception, as they are a quest reward from "The Forlorn Spire" in the separate Molten Front daily questing zone.

### Farming Logistics
The Firelands raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday in North America. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level with trivial difficulty. Since the set does not use a tier token system, each piece drops directly from its respective boss. Collecting the seven raid-dropped pieces will likely take 5-10 weeks, subject to random drop chance. The feet require unlocking and progressing through the Molten Front daily quests, which can be done concurrently with the raid farming. The green appearance of this set is exclusive to the 10-player Normal difficulty; 25-player Normal and Heroic difficulties award different color variations and share a lockout with their 10-player counterparts.

### Farming Tips
To acquire this specific color, you must set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal before entering. To speed up the clear, kill the two trash packs immediately inside the entrance to force Shannox to spawn and engage you early. After defeating Alysrazor, you can use the flight buff she provides to fly directly across the lava to Baleroc's platform, saving significant travel time. While most pieces are boss drops, some wrist slot items with the same appearance, such as the [Flickering Wristbands], are Bind-on-Equip drops from trash mobs and may be available for purchase on the Auction House.

523.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather armor set is a lookalike of the Tier 10 Druid set and is obtained from bosses in the Tier 11 Cataclysm raids: Blackwing Descent and Throne of the Four Winds. All pieces drop from the 25-player Normal versions of these instances. The Head slot, Helm of the Blind Seer, and Hands slot, Gloves of Cacophonous Combustion, both drop from Atramedes in Blackwing Descent. The Shoulders, Poison-Drenched Spaulders, drop from Chimaeron, while the Chest, Tunic of Failed Experiments, drops from Maloriak, both also in Blackwing Descent. The Legs, Leggings of the Treacherous Path, are acquired from the Conclave of Wind in Throne of the Four Winds. Matching non-set pieces include the Waist (Belt of Nefarious Machinations from Omnotron Defense System), Feet (Boots of the Predator from Magmaw), and Wrist (Parasitic Bands from Magmaw).

### Farming Logistics
The source raids operate on a weekly raid lockout, resetting each Tuesday for NA realms and Wednesday for EU realms. All encounters can be easily soloed by a max-level character. Given an average drop rate, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 5-10 weekly resets. This is a "lookalike" set, meaning the items drop directly and do not use a tier token system for acquisition. Note that for Cataclysm raids, the 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a lockout, so you can only complete one version per character each week; you must run the 25-player Normal mode for these specific items.

### Farming Tips
Before entering, ensure your raid difficulty is set to "25 Player (Normal)" via your character portrait's right-click menu, as this provides the largest loot table per boss. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, will significantly decrease the time spent running through the instances. After defeating the Omnotron Defense System in Blackwing Descent, use the teleporter in the center of the room to quickly reach Atramedes and Maloriak. Some off-set pieces that match this set, particularly for the Waist and Feet slots, are Bind-on-Equip and can sometimes be purchased directly from the Auction House.

524.content:
### How to Obtain
This set appearance is a collection of leather armor pieces that drop from the Icecrown Citadel raid, a Wrath of the Lich King instance, on 25-player Heroic difficulty. The Head and Waist pieces drop from Professor Putricide, and the Shoulders drop from Lady Deathwhisper. The Feet are looted from Lord Marrowgar, while the Wrists come from the chest after the Gunship Battle. The Chest piece is found in the cache that appears after completing the Valithria Dreamwalker encounter. Both the Hands and Legs drop directly from the final boss, The Lich King.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout per character. The 10-player and 25-player versions have separate lockouts, but Normal and Heroic difficulties share a lockout within each size, meaning you must clear it on 25-player Heroic from the start. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any max-level character. As the appearance is sourced from direct boss drops, you do not need to use the tier token system; the actual Druid tier set uses the Vanquisher token shared with Rogues, Death Knights, and Mages. With eight unique pieces dropping from multiple bosses, expect an average of 5-10 weekly clears to complete the full set.

### Farming Tips
Before entering, set your raid difficulty to 25-Player and Heroic via your character portrait's right-click menu. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter does not need to be fought; healing classes can heal the dragon to full to complete it, while pure damage classes can simply wait for the event to time out and the reward chest to spawn. There are no major skips in the raid, as most bosses must be defeated to progress. None of the pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

525.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier mail lookalike for the Shaman Tier 14 armor, collected from the Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring raids on Normal difficulty. The Head (Ghost-Seer's Crown) drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer and Legs (Leggings of the Poisoned Soul) drop from Garalon, both in Heart of Fear. The Shoulder (Spaulders of the Divided Mind) drops from Lei Shi and Feet (Boots of the Still Breath) drop from Tsulong, both in Terrace of Endless Spring. The remaining pieces are from Mogu'shan Vaults: Chest (Robes of the Burning August) from Will of the Emperor, Hands (Gloves of the Raging Giant) from Feng the Accursed, Waist (Belt of the Malleable Clay) from The Stone Guard, and Wrist (Bracers of the Banished) from Elegon.

### Farming Logistics
The three source raids—Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring—are all on separate weekly raid lockouts per character. The instances are fully soloable at max level, with bosses posing no threat. Given the number of items spread across different instances, collecting the full set can take an average of 5-10 weekly clears, depending on drop luck. This set does not use a tier token system; the pieces are direct boss drops and can be looted by any mail-wearing class. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties are separate lockouts, but this specific blue and gold colorway only drops from Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal via your character portrait's context menu before entering. There are no major boss skips in these raids; bosses in Mogu'shan Vaults and Heart of Fear must be defeated in a mostly linear order to progress. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are no longer available for this content, so farming is reliant on direct boss loot.

526.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier mail lookalike of the Shaman Tier 16 set, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid on Heroic difficulty. The Head, "Hood of the Tidal-Forged," drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty, and the Shoulders, "Spaulders of the Tidal-Forged," drop from Malkorok. The Chest, "Chestguard of the Tidal-Forged," is found on the final boss, Garrosh Hellscream, while the Legs, "Legguards of the Tidal-Forged," drop from the Spoils of Pandaria encounter. General Nazgrim drops the Hands ("Gloves of the Tidal-Forged"), and the Iron Juggernaut drops the Feet ("Greaves of the Tidal-Forged"). The matching Waist, "Waistguard of the Tidal-Forged," and Wrist, "Bubble-Smothered Bracers," both drop from the first boss, Immerseus.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar operates on a weekly raid lockout, allowing one chance per boss each week on a given difficulty. The entire raid is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces and reliance on drop chance, collecting the full 8-piece set typically takes 6-12 weeks. These items are direct boss drops and do not come from a tier token; the class-specific version uses the Protector token (Shaman, Hunter, Monk), but that is not required for this lookalike set. While the LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, this specific blue recolor is exclusive to the Heroic difficulty setting.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest and easiest clears, set the raid difficulty to 10-player Heroic before entering the instance. You can use Warforged Seals, a Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, on bosses for an additional chance at loot. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Because this is a mail armor set, the appearances can only be collected and used for transmog by mail-wearing classes: Hunter, Shaman, and Evoker. Upon entering the raid, click the scroll near Lorewalker Cho to skip the introductory roleplay sequence and begin the first encounter immediately.

527.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a vendor and requires membership in the Night Fae Covenant. The vendor is Mar'lan, located in the Heart of the Forest in Ardenweald at coordinates (41.8, 29.8). The currency required is Reservoir Anima, and the character must have achieved Renown 60 with the Night Fae to purchase the pieces. The total cost for all eight pieces is 10,000 Reservoir Anima. Individual pieces are priced as follows: Head (1500), Shoulder (1500), Chest (2500), Legs (2000), Hands (1000), Feet (500), Waist (500), and Wrist (500).

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as it is based on currency acquisition. The entire process is soloable at current max level by farming Reservoir Anima from Shadowlands content. A focused player can farm the required 10,000 Anima in a few hours by completing world quests, killing rare mobs, and looting treasures across the four main Shadowlands zones and Zereth Mortis. This is not a tier set, so there is no token system. All pieces are from the same vendor and require the same currency and reputation level.

### Farming Tips
To farm Reservoir Anima efficiently, focus on completing world quests that offer it as a reward, especially the weekly "Replenish the Reservoir" quest in Oribos. Flying in Shadowlands is essential for speeding up travel between objectives. You must be a member of the Night Fae covenant on the character you use to purchase the set; the appearances are Bind on Pickup and will not be added to your collection otherwise. Once learned, the cosmetic set can be used by any character on your account regardless of their class or covenant affiliation.

528.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Heroic difficulty cloth tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. The appearance is shared by the Priest, Mage, and Warlock class sets. The main armor pieces drop from specific bosses on Heroic difficulty. Head drops from Anduin Wrynn, Shoulder from Lords of Dread, Chest from Rygelon, and Legs from Halondrus the Reclaimer. The Hands token drops from Lihuvim, Principal Architect, while the remaining non-tier lookalike pieces (Waist, Feet, Wrist) can drop from The Jailer.

### Farming Logistics
This raid is on a weekly lockout per character, per difficulty. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic are separate lockouts that can be completed once per week. At the current maximum level, this raid cannot be fully soloed on Heroic due to boss mechanics on encounters like Halondrus and Anduin, requiring a group of at least 2-5 players. The set uses a tier token system: Mages use the Mystic token, Priests use the Venerated token, and Warlocks use the Dreadful token. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance, collecting the full set via direct boss drops could take 5-15 weeks, depending on luck.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid specifically on Heroic difficulty for this purple and blue recolor. Non-tier cloth pieces that drop from the raid can be converted into tier set pieces with the identical appearance at the Creation Catalyst in Zereth Mortis, which greatly accelerates acquisition. Forming a group with other cloth wearers allows for loot trading to fill in missing slots. Tier tokens are automatically added to your collection upon being looted, so you do not need to use them on a specific character to collect the appearance.

529.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "7th Legionnaire's/Honorbound Centurion's Dragonhide" armor, a recolor of the classic Wildheart Raiment, obtained from the Battle for Azeroth Darkshore Warfront. All pieces drop from the 20+ rare elite creatures scattered throughout the Darkshore zone when your faction is in the "Patrol" phase of the warfront cycle. The world boss of the zone, Ivus the Forest Lord (Alliance) or Ivus the Decayed (Horde), also has a chance to drop pieces. A guaranteed piece is awarded upon completion of the one-time quest "Warfront: The Battle for Darkshore" for completing the scenario. The main armor slots—Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist—are all part of the same large loot table shared by these rares and the world boss.

### Farming Logistics
The primary loot sources for this set, the rare elites in Darkshore, operate on a per-character, per-cycle lockout. This means you can kill each rare for a chance at loot once on every character during each warfront cycle your faction controls the zone. The world boss is on a standard weekly raid lockout. The entire farm is easily soloable at current max level. Due to the large loot pool and the nature of the warfront cycle, which can take weeks to return to your faction, collecting all pieces can take 4-10+ cycles. There is no tier token system for this set, and there is only one difficulty for the warfront and its associated outdoor content.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to park multiple leather-wearing characters in Darkshore and log onto each one to kill all available rares when your faction controls the zone. Use an addon like TomCat's Tours or Rarescanner to display rare locations on your map and track which ones you have already killed on that character for the current cycle. All pieces from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased on the Auction House. Focus farming during the "Patrol" phase of the warfront cycle, as this is when your faction has control of the zone and all rares are available to be killed.

530.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Druid Tier 1 armor, obtained from the Molten Core raid, originally from the Classic expansion. The Feralheart Cowl (Head) drops from Garr and the Feralheart Kilt (Legs) drops from Magmadar. The Feralheart Spaulders (Shoulder) are looted from Baron Geddon, while the Feralheart Gloves (Hands) come from Shazzrah. Golemagg the Incinerator drops the Feralheart Vest (Chest), and Lucifron drops the Feralheart Boots (Feet). The Feralheart Belt (Waist) and Feralheart Bindings (Wrist) are Bind-on-Equip items that drop from trash mobs throughout the instance.

### Farming Logistics
Molten Core operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets with the server's weekly maintenance. The instance can be easily and fully soloed by any class at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop rate for boss items, collecting the six boss-dropped pieces will take approximately 3-5 weekly resets. This set predates the tier token system, so the items drop directly for the Druid class only. The raid has only one difficulty, meaning there is only one version of this set and a single loot lockout per character each week.

### Farming Tips
To enter Molten Core, speak to Lothos Riftwaker inside Blackrock Mountain to be teleported directly to the raid instance, bypassing the need to run through Blackrock Depths. Since the Feralheart Belt and Feralheart Bindings are Bind-on-Equip, check the Auction House to purchase them and potentially complete the set much faster. Use a character with high movement speed to clear the large raid zone more efficiently, as there are no shortcuts. A visually identical but non-tier lookalike set for all leather-wearing classes also drops from the same bosses and trash mobs.

531.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a look-alike of the classic Giantstalker armor and is obtained from the Throne of Thunder, the Tier 15 raid instance in Mists of Pandaria. The individual mail armor pieces drop directly from specific bosses within the raid. The sources for each slot are as follows: Head drops from Twin Consorts, Shoulder drops from Iron Qon, Chest and Feet drop from Tortos, Legs and Waist drop from Horridon, Hands drop from Lei Shen, and Wrist drops from Megaera. All pieces are personal loot and are automatically added to your collection upon being looted.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid has a weekly raid lockout for Normal and Heroic difficulties, which are shared. The Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty has a separate weekly lockout, allowing for multiple chances at the gear per character each week. The entire raid is fully soloable by a max-level character on all available difficulties. As this is not a tier set, it does not use a token system; mail pieces drop directly for any class that can clear the instance. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance, collecting all eight pieces could take between 4-10 clears of the instance.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the LFR version of the raid by speaking to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. After defeating the Council of Elders, a teleporter appears that will return you to the instance entrance, saving time. You can use an Elder Charm of Good Fortune for a bonus roll on bosses for an additional chance at loot. These charms can be purchased with Lesser Charms of Good Fortune, which drop from various Mists of Pandaria content. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

532.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained by collecting tier tokens from the 25-player raids Serpentshrine Cavern and Tempest Keep: The Eye. The Head, Shoulder, and Chest tokens drop in Tempest Keep, while the Legs and Hands tokens drop in Serpentshrine Cavern. Matching non-set Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces also drop from bosses within Serpentshrine Cavern. The tokens are exchanged for gear with vendors in Shattrath City.

*   **Head:** Kael'thas Sunstrider (Tempest Keep: The Eye)
*   **Shoulder:** Void Reaver (Tempest Keep: The Eye)
*   **Chest:** Kael'thas Sunstrider (Tempest Keep: The Eye)
*   **Legs:** Fathom-Lord Karathress (Serpentshrine Cavern)
*   **Hands:** Leotheras the Blind (Serpentshrine Cavern)
*   **Waist:** Hydross the Unstable (Serpentshrine Cavern)
*   **Wrist:** Tidewalker (Serpentshrine Cavern)
*   **Feet:** The Lurker Below (Serpentshrine Cavern)

### Farming Logistics
Both Serpentshrine Cavern and Tempest Keep are subject to a weekly raid lockout that resets with the weekly maintenance. The instances can be fully soloed at current maximum level with no difficulty. Given the number of pieces and drop chances, a dedicated player can expect to complete the set within 4-8 weekly resets. The set uses the "Fallen Champion" tier token, which is shared between Mage, Priest, and Warlock classes. As these raids predate the modern difficulty system, they have only one lockout and loot table per week.

### Farming Tips
Farm the raids on multiple eligible characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) to increase your weekly chances, as looting a tier token will unlock the appearance for all three classes simultaneously. The tier tokens are automatically added to your wardrobe upon being looted, so you do not need to turn them in to a vendor to collect the appearance. In Tempest Keep, you must defeat Al'ar, Void Reaver, and High Astromancer Solarian to access Kael'thas Sunstrider. All pieces for this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House.

533.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor appearance from the Castle Nathria raid on Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty. The Head drops from the Stone Legion Generals, and the Shoulders drop from Sire Denathrius. The Chest is looted from Sludgefist, while the Legs drop from Huntsman Altimor. The Hands come from Artificer Xy'mox, the Feet from Hungering Destroyer, the Waist from Shriekwing, and the Wrists from Lady Inerva Darkvein.

### Farming Logistics
This set is subject to a weekly raid lockout, specific to the LFR difficulty. Each of the four wings of Castle Nathria LFR can be completed once per character per week. The raid can be fully soloed by a max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5 and 15 weekly clears due to the possibility of duplicate drops. Castle Nathria does not use a tier token system; all gear drops directly for your armor type. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version of Castle Nathria, speak to the NPC Ta'hsup in the Enclave in Oribos to queue for specific wings. This allows you to target only the bosses you need for missing pieces, speeding up the process significantly. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Past systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault cannot be used to acquire these appearances.

534.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is obtained by crafting the "Ensemble: Cryptstalker's Guise" via the Abomination Factory, a feature of the Necrolord Covenant in Shadowlands. To craft the ensemble, you must first upgrade the Abomination Factory to Tier 5. The crafting process requires you to have completed the quest "Putting A Plan Together" from Abominable Sewing. The primary reagents needed are 500 Malleable Flesh and 25 Superior Parts, which are acquired by completing quests and activities associated with the Abomination Factory. The single crafted ensemble contains all nine pieces of the set: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist, and Cloak.

### Farming Logistics
The acquisition of this set is not subject to a traditional lockout but is time-gated by the process of upgrading the Abomination Factory and gathering the required materials. The entire process is designed to be completed solo and can take several weeks of consistent effort to unlock Tier 5 and accumulate the necessary reagents. There is no token system, as this is a cosmetic ensemble available to all classes and armor types. The farm is entirely self-contained within the Necrolord Covenant's activities and does not involve raid or dungeon drops. The difficulties of LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic are not applicable to this set.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate the process, focus on completing the main Necrolord Covenant campaign to unlock the Abomination Factory and its upgrades. Prioritize the weekly quest "Putting It All Together" which rewards a large amount of Malleable Flesh and Superior Parts. Complete daily quests offered by your abominable creations and use your covenant's Anima Conductor to channel anima to the House of Constructs for additional quests and reagent sources. The ensemble and its components are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

535.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike version of Paladin Tier 14 armor, obtained from the Heart of Fear raid in Mists of Pandaria. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the instance on Normal or Heroic difficulty. The Head drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, Shoulders and Wrists drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak, and the Chest drops from Amber-Shaper Un'sok. The Legs and Waist are both rewards from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, the Hands drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, and the Feet drop from Garalon.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear raid has a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties are on separate lockouts, allowing for three attempts per week on a single character for different color variations of the set. The instance can be fully soloed by any max-level character due to the legacy damage multiplier. This set does not use a tier token system; the armor pieces drop directly as loot. Expect to complete 5-10 full clears to obtain all eight pieces, depending on drop luck.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as boss health is at its lowest. There are no significant skips; you must kill the first three bosses to unlock access to the final three. After defeating Garalon, remember to click the pheromone vial in the center of the room to break the amber door blocking the path forward. All items are Bind on Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House, and Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are no longer functional for this content.

536.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is a lookalike of the Hunter Tier 5 armor and is obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid, the Tier 15 instance from Mists of Pandaria. All main armor pieces drop directly from individual bosses within the raid. The Headpiece of the Rushing Fissure drops from the Twin Consorts, and the Spaulders of the Unblinking Vigil drop from Iron Qon. The Chestguard of the Frilled Mystic is looted from Dark Animus, while the Legguards of the Unforgiving Path are from Ji-Kun. The Gloves of the Unblinking Vigil drop from the Council of Elders, the Troll-Burner's Trekkers (Feet) drop from Tortos, the Abandoned Zandalari Buckleboard (Waist) is from Megaera, and the Cuffs of the Frilled Mystic (Wrist) come from Primordius.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid operates on a weekly raid lockout per character. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per item, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take between 8 to 12 weekly clears on a single character, subject to random luck. This set does not use a tier token system; gear drops directly from bosses for your character's armor type. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockouts and reward a different color variation of the set, allowing you three chances per week per mail-wearing character.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as boss health is lowest. After defeating the first boss, Jin'rokh the Breaker, you can jump directly off the side of his platform into the water below to skip a significant amount of trash leading to Horridon. Players can use Mogu Runes of Fate for a bonus roll on bosses for an extra chance at loot; these can be acquired on the Timeless Isle. The main set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

537.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Tier 15 raid, Throne of Thunder. The five main pieces are acquired via tier tokens, while the matching waist, feet, and wrist are non-tier lookalike items from the same raid. Head (Gronnstalker's Headpiece) is from the Twin Consorts. Shoulders (Gronnstalker's Spaulders) are from Iron Qon. Chest (Gronnstalker's Chestguard) is from Dark Animus. Legs (Gronnstalker's Legguards) are from Ji-Kun. Hands (Gronnstalker's Gloves) are from the Council of Elders. The matching waist (Arrow-Lined Quiver) drops from Jin'rokh the Breaker, the feet (Treads of the Unchained) drop from Megaera, and the wrists (Bracers of Constant Implosion) drop from Horridon.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid has a weekly raid lockout per character. The raid is fully soloable by any class at current maximum level. Collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 runs, depending on drop luck. This set uses the "Vanquisher" tier token system, which is shared by Hunters, Druids, Mages, and Shamans. The set's different color variations drop from Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties, which all have separate weekly lockouts, allowing three chances per character per week. Note that the belt, boots, and bracers are not from tokens and drop directly as items from their respective bosses.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run on Normal difficulty, as enemies have significantly less health than on Heroic. After defeating the first six bosses, teleporters unlock in the central hub, allowing you to quickly move between the raid's different wings. Tier tokens are automatically added to your appearance collection upon being looted, so there is no need to visit a vendor. You can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune for a bonus roll on each boss, providing an extra chance at loot; these can be acquired on the Isle of Thunder. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

538.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased as a complete ensemble from the Black Market Auction House (BMAH). The item required is the "Ensemble: Cryptstalker's Battlegear," which teaches the appearances for all nine pieces of the blue recolor set. When used, the ensemble adds the appearances for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist, and Cloak slots to your account-wide Wardrobe. These appearances can be used by any Mail-wearing character (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker). The BMAH is a special auction house where players bid against each other using Gold.

### Farming Logistics
The Black Market Auction House has no character lockout, but its inventory rotates daily on a server-by-server basis. Obtaining this set is not a farm in the traditional sense; it requires winning a competitive auction with gold. The ensemble may not appear for months or even years, and winning it requires outbidding all other players on the server. The set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are contained within the single ensemble item. This process is entirely solo but is a test of wealth and patience rather than combat.

### Farming Tips
Park a character at the BMAH location in Valdrakken to check the listings immediately after the daily reset. The ensemble can be a rare drop from the "Unclaimed Black Market Container," also sold on the BMAH, but this is highly random. The final bid price for this ensemble often reaches millions of gold, so it is critical to have a large amount of capital saved. Because BMAH inventory is server-specific, checking low-population servers may result in less bidding competition, though the item may also appear less frequently.

539.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is obtained from the non-tier "Firstborn" plate-armor-type items that drop in the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid from the Shadowlands expansion. The Head drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Shoulder drops from the Lords of Dread. The Chest is looted from Rygelon, and the Legs drop from Lihuvim, Principal Architect. The Hands can drop from either Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle or Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener. The Feet drop from Artificer Xy'mox, the Waist drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Wrist is looted from the Vigilant Guardian.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a standard weekly raid lockout per character for each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic). At current max level, the entire raid is fully soloable on all difficulties. Collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 8-12 weeks per difficulty, depending on drop luck. These lookalike pieces are direct boss drops and do not use a tier token system. Each difficulty has its own separate lockout and loot table, allowing you to farm each color variation simultaneously on the same character each week.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty, as boss health and mechanics are trivialized by current player power. There are no major skips available, so you must defeat the bosses in a linear order to reach the ones you need. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House. During the Halondrus encounter, be prepared for the knockback mechanics on the bridges to avoid being knocked off and having to run back.

540.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier mail lookalike that drops within the Throne of Thunder raid, introduced in the Mists of Pandaria expansion. The pieces drop from specific bosses on both Normal and Heroic difficulty. The Head slot (Helm of the Forgotten Protector) drops from Jin'rokh the Breaker, and the Shoulder slot (Shoulderguards of the Unflanked) drops from the Twin Consorts. The Chest (Spirit-Talon Carapace) is looted from Tortos, while the Legs (Leggings of the Unseen Path) come from Megaera. The Hands (Gauntlets of the Shadow's Clutch) drop from the Council of Elders, Feet (Spiked-Toe Spaulders) from Iron Qon, the Waist (Abandoned Zandalari Bucklebraid) from Primordius, and the Wrists (Bracers of the Midnight Comet) from Durumu the Forgotten.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American realms. The entire instance is easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, a complete set can typically be collected in 5-10 full clears. This set does not use a tier token system; each piece drops directly as an item. The Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate weekly lockouts, but this specific green coloration only drops from Normal and Heroic difficulties.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the instance on 10-player Normal difficulty, as it has the lowest boss health. After defeating the first boss, Jin'rokh, you can jump off the side of the bridge to the water below to skip directly to the central part of the instance, avoiding several trash packs. Players can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune, obtained on the Timeless Isle, for a bonus roll on bosses for an extra chance at loot. Some matching green-colored mail items can drop from trash mobs within the raid; these are Bind-on-Equip and may be available for purchase on the Auction House.

541.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from the Ulduar raid, the Tier 8 instance from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The five main armor pieces are not direct drops but are created from tokens found in the 25-player version of the raid. The Head token drops from Mimiron, the Shoulder token from Thorim, the Chest token from Yogg-Saron, the Legs token from Hodir, and the Hands token from Freya. Matching non-tier pieces also drop within Ulduar (25): the Waist (Belt of the Ardent Marksman) from Flame Leviathan, the Feet (Boots of the Treacherous Path) from XT-002 Deconstructor, and the Wrist (Bracers of the Untold Story) from Yogg-Saron.

### Farming Logistics
Ulduar operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms. The entire instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. To get this specific blue and gold recolor, you must set the Legacy Raid Size to "25 Player" via your portrait's right-click menu before entering the instance. The five main armor pieces use the "Protector" tier token, which is shared by Hunters, Shamans, and Warriors. Because eight unique items are required from specific bosses, expect to run the raid for 4-8 weeks to collect the full set. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a single weekly lockout.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run on 25-player normal difficulty, as activating boss hard modes is not necessary for this appearance. After clearing the initial bosses, use the teleporters in the center of the instance to quickly travel to the four keeper wings. The tier tokens themselves do not grant the appearance; you must right-click the token in your bags to create the corresponding armor piece, which then adds it to your wardrobe. Only a Hunter or a Shaman can use the Protector token to create pieces of the Scourgestalker set; Evokers can collect the non-token mail pieces, but cannot use the tokens for the five primary slots.

542.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from the Firelands raid, the Tier 12 instance from the Cataclysm expansion. The primary armor pieces are obtained via tier tokens which must be used by a Hunter: the Head token drops from Ragnaros, Shoulders from Majordomo Staghelm, Chest from Alysrazor, Legs from Shannox, and Hands from Baleroc. Non-tier mail lookalike items drop from various bosses in the raid, allowing Shamans and Evokers to collect the appearance. The matching lookalike Feet are purchased from vendors at the Molten Front for Marks of the World Tree, and the Wrists are a world drop from trash mobs within the instance.

### Farming Logistics
Firelands operates on a weekly raid lockout, which is shared between Normal and Heroic difficulties, allowing you to loot each boss once per character per week. The instance can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. The tier tokens for this set are named "of the Fiery Protector" and are shared by Hunters, Shamans, and Warriors. Obtaining all boss-drop pieces will likely require 4-8 weekly runs, depending on luck. Note that the lookalike Feet are purchased with a separate currency from daily quests, and the lookalike Wrists can be farmed from trash mobs without a lockout.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance at loot, set the raid difficulty to 25-man Heroic, as this setting causes bosses to drop a larger quantity of items. Upon looting a tier token, the appearance is automatically added to your Wardrobe, so there is no need to find a vendor to turn it in. The lookalike bracers, Unrelenting Flame Bracers, are Bind-on-Equip and can frequently be found on the Auction House, allowing you to complete that slot without relying on a random drop. A max-level character with a movement speed set can clear the entire raid in 10-15 minutes.

543.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate lookalike from the Dragon Soul raid, the final raid of Tier 13. The pieces drop as individual items directly from specific bosses within the instance. The Headpiece drops from Warmaster Blackhorn, and the Shoulders drop from Hagara the Stormbinder. The Chest is looted from Ultraxion, the Legs from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, and the Hands from General Zon'ozz. The Feet drop from Warlord Zon'ozz, the Wrist from Morchok, and the Waist is a BoE item that drops from trash mobs throughout the raid or from the Goriona's Collar item during the Warmaster Blackhorn encounter.

### Farming Logistics
Dragon Soul has a weekly raid lockout, shared between 10- and 25-player versions of the same difficulty, but LFR, Normal, and Heroic are separate lockouts. The entire raid is fully soloable by any class at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full set may take approximately 5-10 runs. This set does not use the tier token system; plate items drop directly and are usable by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. Note that the waist and a lookalike version of the feet are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can drop from any trash mob inside the raid.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of loot per run, clear the instance on 25-player Normal or Heroic difficulty, as more items drop per boss. The waist and feet pieces are BoE, so check the Auction House to potentially purchase them and complete the set faster. The Spine of Deathwing encounter requires a specific solo strategy: stand near one of the wound plates, kill exactly three tentacles to spawn a Corruption, kill it, and let the resulting Amalgamation absorb nine bloods before killing it on the plate. Repeat this process three times to complete the encounter.

544.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier mail Agility lookalike from the Cataclysm Tier 11 raids, specifically from 10-player Normal difficulty. The pieces drop from bosses in Blackwing Descent and The Bastion of Twilight. The Head drops from Atramedes and the Chest from Maloriak, both in Blackwing Descent.
