Shoulders drop from Halfus Wyrmbreaker and Hands from Cho'gall in The Bastion of Twilight. The Legs are from Chimaeron and the Feet from Magmaw, both in Blackwing Descent. The Waist drops from Valiona & Theralion in The Bastion of Twilight, and matching Wrists drop from Magmaw as well.

### Farming Logistics
The raids Blackwing Descent and The Bastion of Twilight operate on a weekly raid lockout, which is shared between 10-player and 25-player difficulties as well as Normal and Heroic modes. You must set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal before entering to be eligible for this specific color tint. The instances are fully soloable by any class at maximum level. As these are direct item drops and not tier tokens, no other classes compete for the appearance. Assuming a standard drop rate, collecting the full seven-piece set will likely take 5-10 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, farm this set on a mail-wearing class like a Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker to learn the appearance directly upon looting. In Blackwing Descent, you must defeat Magmaw and the Omnotron Defense System to activate the elevator to the upper labs containing Maloriak, Atramedes, and Chimaeron. In The Bastion of Twilight, the instance is linear, requiring you to clear all bosses in order to reach the final boss, Cho'gall. Systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to Cataclysm content.

545.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from the Trial of the Crusader (25-player Normal) raid, located in the northeastern part of Icecrown. The five main armor pieces are not direct boss drops but are purchased from Highlord Tirion Fordring or a faction-specific vendor inside the raid instance using the currency `Trophy of the Crusade`. This currency is awarded from the Tribute Chest that appears after defeating the final boss, Anub'arak. The matching non-set pieces are dropped by specific bosses within the same instance.

*   **Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands:** Purchased with `Trophy of the Crusade`.
*   **Waist:** Drops from Lord Jaraxxus.
*   **Feet:** Drops from the Faction Champions encounter.
*   **Wrist:** Drops from Anub'arak.

### Farming Logistics
The Trial of the Crusader raid has a weekly raid lockout. The instance is easily soloed by any class at current max level. Collecting the full set will take approximately 4-6 runs, as the main pieces are bought with a currency that is awarded in fixed amounts each week, while the off-set pieces are subject to standard drop chance RNG. Hunters purchase their tier set directly with `Trophy of the Crusade` and do not share a tier token with other classes in this raid. Be aware that the 25-player Normal and 25-player Heroic difficulties share a single weekly lockout, so you can only complete one of them per character each week.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on 25-player Normal difficulty to obtain this specific recolor. The instance is a single arena with no trash, making clears extremely fast, typically under 10 minutes. After completing the initial jousting event once on a character, you can speak to an NPC to skip it on all subsequent runs on that character. Since the main pieces are purchased from vendors located within the raid instance itself, it is efficient to buy any affordable pieces immediately after looting your currency from the final chest.

546.content:
### How to Obtain
The set pieces are acquired from the Icecrown Citadel raid (25-player Heroic) from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. Tier tokens for the Head and Shoulders drop from The Lich King. The Chest token drops from Lady Deathwhisper, the Legs token from Lord Marrowgar, and the Hands token from Deathbringer Saurfang. The matching Waist, the Belt of the Merciless Killer, drops from Festergut. The matching Feet, Boots of the Frozen Seed, drop from Rotface, and the matching Wrist, Bracers of the Heir, drops from the Icecrown Gunship Battle.

### Farming Logistics
The raid has a weekly raid lockout, which is shared between 10/25-player and Normal/Heroic difficulties, so you must set the instance to 25-player Heroic before entering. It is fully soloable at max level. The tier tokens are named "...of the Grand Protector (Heroic)" and are shared by Hunters, Paladins, Shamans, and Warriors, which can increase farming time. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all eight pieces could take between 5-10 weekly clears. All required pieces drop within this single raid instance and difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic via your portrait's right-click menu before entering the instance. Use the teleporter in the central spire after defeating Deathbringer Saurfang to quickly access the upper floors. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter is skippable by running past the boss if you do not need loot from her chest. The tier tokens automatically grant the appearance upon being looted, so there is no need to visit a vendor to turn them in.

547.content:
### How to Obtain
This set appearance is obtained from the Normal difficulty of the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. The main set pieces are acquired from tier tokens dropped by the later bosses, while the matching non-set pieces drop from earlier bosses. The specific drop locations for each slot are as follows: Head from Rygelon, Shoulder from The Lords of Dread, Chest from Rygelon, Legs from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and Hands from Lihuvim, Principal Architect. The matching Waist drops from the Vigilant Guardian, Feet drop from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, and Wrist drops from Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts and can be cleared independently each week. At current max level, the Normal version of the raid is fully soloable by any class. The set uses a tier token system for the five main slots; the "Dreadful" tokens for Normal difficulty are shared between specific classes. The Zenith token is for Druid, Monk, Rogue, and Warrior; the Venerated token is for Paladin, Priest, and Shaman; the Mystic token is for Hunter, Mage, and Warlock; and the Dreadful token is for Death Knight, Demon Hunter, and Evoker. Given the number of pieces and drop chances, expect the full farm to take 4-8 weeks per character.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for this specific recolor. Any non-tier gear pieces of the appropriate slot and armor type that drop from the raid can be converted into tier set appearances using the Creation Catalyst in Zereth Mortis, providing significant bad luck protection. Looting a tier token automatically adds the appearance to your collection for your class, so you do not need to use the token to create the item. There are no major skips, but using a high-mobility class will speed up the time spent moving between bosses.

548.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Heroic difficulty mail armor tier set from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid. The five main armor pieces are acquired by obtaining class-agnostic tier tokens that drop from specific bosses. The Head token drops from Anduin Wrynn, the Shoulder token from Lords of Dread, the Chest token from Rygelon, the Hands token from Lihuvim, and the Legs token from Halondrus. Matching non-tier Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces drop directly from the final boss, The Jailer. This appearance is shared between the Shaman set "Lightning-Charged Battlegear" and the Hunter set "Venerated Serpentstalker's Guise."

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly raid lockout per character. The LFR difficulty has a separate lockout from Normal, Heroic, and Mythic, which all share one lockout. This set is not soloable at current max level and requires a small group to clear on Heroic difficulty. Assuming a 20% drop chance for each token, collecting the full set will likely take 5-10 weekly clears, depending on group loot competition and luck. The tier tokens for this set are the Zenith Anima Spherule, which is shared between Hunters, Paladins, Shamans, and Warriors.

### Farming Tips
To acquire this specific recolor, the raid must be run on Heroic difficulty. Forming a dedicated transmog group with other mail-wearers can increase the chances of someone getting a piece and being able to trade it. There are no significant skips to reach the required bosses faster; the raid must be cleared in a linear fashion. Using a tier token on a relevant class (Hunter or Shaman) will automatically add the appearance to your wardrobe. The matching non-tier pieces from The Jailer are bind-on-pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House.

549.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike gear from the Tier 14 Mists of Pandaria raids: Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring. The headpiece, Flamewaker's Headguard, drops from Will of the Emperor in Mogu'shan Vaults. The chest and legs, Flamewaker's Tunic and Flamewaker's Legguards, both drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear. The hands, Flamewaker's Handguards, drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok in Heart of Fear, while the shoulders, Flamewaker's Spaulders, drop from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring. Matching non-set feet (Ghost-Forged Greaves) and a matching waist (Un'sok's Abyssal Girdle) drop from The Stone Guard (Mogu'shan Vaults) and Un'sok (Heart of Fear) respectively.

### Farming Logistics
The three raids—Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring—are all on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. The entire set can be fully soloed at max level by any of the eligible plate-wearing classes. Given the number of pieces spread across three raids, collecting the full set will likely take 4-8 weekly clears per character, depending on drop luck. The set pieces are direct drops and do not use a token system; they can be looted by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties for these raids have separate lockouts and contain different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
Run the raids on Normal difficulty for this specific red and gold color tint. You can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an extra chance at loot from each boss. These charms can be acquired in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms by turning in Lesser Charms of Good Fortune, which drop from enemies throughout Pandaria. All three raids are linear with no major skips, and bosses can be defeated in seconds at max level, making clears very fast. The appearances are automatically added to your collection upon looting, as long as you are on a plate-wearing character.

550.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is the heroic plate lookalike from the Firelands raid, a Tier 12 instance from the Cataclysm expansion. All pieces drop exclusively on Heroic difficulty. The Head, Jaw of Repudiation, and Wrist, Bracers of the Fiery Path, drop from Baleroc. The Shoulder, Spaulders of Recurring Flame, drops from Majordomo Fandral Staghelm. The Chest, Breastplate of the Raging Heart, is from Alysrazor, and the Legs, Legguards of the Molten Lord, drop from Beth'tilac. The Hands, Flickering Handguards, are looted from Shannox, while both the Waist, Cinch of the Flaming Ember, and Feet, Sabatons of the Raging Volcano, drop from Lord Rhyolith.

### Farming Logistics
The Firelands raid has a weekly lockout for each difficulty and raid size, meaning you can clear both 10-player Heroic and 25-player Heroic on the same character each week for two chances at loot. The instance is easily soloed at current maximum level. As this is a lookalike set, the items drop directly from bosses and do not use the tier token system. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full 8-piece set can take anywhere from 4 to 10 weekly clears per character, though this is highly dependent on luck. No pieces of this specific recolor set drop from any other source.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of obtaining gear, run the raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty, as it drops a larger quantity of loot per boss compared to 10-player. All bosses required for this set must be defeated, but Ragnaros can be skipped as he does not drop any of the set pieces. After killing the initial bosses, use the unlocked teleporters near the instance entrance to quickly move between boss platforms. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

551.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Hunter Tier 4 armor, acquired from three separate raids from The Burning Crusade: Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair. Three pieces are obtained by turning in tokens, while the other five are direct boss drops.

*   **Head:** Token (Helm of the Fallen Defender) drops from Prince Malchezaar in Karazhan.
*   **Shoulder:** Direct drop from High King Maulgar in Gruul's Lair.
*   **Chest:** Token (Tunic of the Fallen Defender) drops from Magtheridon in Magtheridon's Lair.
*   **Legs:** Token (Leggings of the Fallen Defender) drops from Gruul the Dragonkiller in Gruul's Lair.
*   **Hands:** Direct drop from The Curator in Karazhan.
*   **Waist:** Matching non-set piece (Gronn-Stitched Girdle) drops from Gruul the Dragonkiller in Gruul's Lair.
*   **Feet:** Matching non-set piece (Fiend-Slayer Boots) drops from Netherspite in Karazhan.
*   **Wrist:** Matching non-set piece (Vambraces of the Hunt) drops from Moroes in Karazhan.

### Farming Logistics
All three raids (Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, Magtheridon's Lair) operate on a weekly raid lockout that resets every Tuesday. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces spread across three lockouts, expect farming to take approximately 3-5 weekly resets to collect all items. The Head, Chest, and Legs use the "Fallen Defender" token, which is shared by Hunters, Shamans, and Warriors. The Shoulder and Hand pieces are direct drops, not tokens, as are the matching non-set pieces for the waist, feet, and wrists. These legacy raids only have one difficulty and do not have separate lockouts for different modes.

### Farming Tips
You must turn in the tokens for the Head, Chest, and Legs to a vendor to receive the gear and unlock the appearance; they are not automatically added to your wardrobe. Tier 4 vendors are located in Shattrath City (Kelara) or on the Isle of Quel'Danas (Arodis Sunblade). Karazhan requires you to complete the Opera event to access the upper levels where The Curator and Prince Malchezaar are located. The non-set matching pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can often be purchased from the Auction House to speed up completion of the set.

552.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from pieces found in the Tier 4 Burning Crusade raids: Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair. The five main set pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses, while the remaining pieces are direct drops. The Head token drops from Prince Malchezaar in Karazhan and the Hands token drops from The Curator in Karazhan. The Shoulder token drops from High King Maulgar in Gruul's Lair, while the Legs token drops from Gruul the Dragonkiller in the same raid. The Chest token drops from Magtheridon in Magtheridon's Lair, with the matching non-set Waist dropping from Gruul, the Feet from Maiden of Virtue in Karazhan, and the Wrist from Netherspite in Karazhan.

### Farming Logistics
All three source raids—Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair—are subject to a weekly raid lockout. These raids can be fully and easily soloed at maximum level. The primary set pieces use the "Fallen Champion" tier token, which is shared by Warrior, Paladin, and Death Knight classes. Assuming an average drop chance, it may take 4-8 weekly resets to collect all eight pieces due to the multiple raids and random nature of drops. The waist, feet, and wrist pieces are non-token drops from different bosses than the main set. These legacy raids only have one difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts for Normal or Heroic.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, only kill the bosses required for your desired pieces. In Karazhan, you can skip several optional bosses if you only need drops from The Curator, Maiden of Virtue, Netherspite, and Prince Malchezaar. Gruul's Lair and Magtheridon's Lair are linear and require no special skips. Tier tokens are physical items that must be looted and taken to a vendor in Shattrath City to be exchanged for the gear; they do not automatically grant the appearance. The most effective strategy is to clear the raids on multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) each week to increase your chances of obtaining the shared tokens.

553.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of epic-quality items that drop from various bosses in Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The Arcanist Headpiece drops from Nexus-King Salhadaar in The Mana-Tombs. The Arcanist Mantle and Arcanist Robes both drop in The Shadow Labyrinth, from Grandmaster Vorpil and Murmur respectively. Hungarfen in The Underbog drops the Arcanist Leggings, while The Black Stalker in the same instance drops the Arcanist Belt. The Arcanist Gloves drop from Nazan in Hellfire Ramparts, the Arcanist Boots from Keli'dan the Breaker in The Blood Furnace, and the Arcanist Bindings from Pathaleon the Calculator in The Mechanar.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from multiple instances, each with a daily Heroic dungeon reset. The entire farm is fully soloable by any class at current max level. Due to the daily lockout and the number of different dungeons required, collecting all eight pieces can take between one to three weeks, depending on drop luck. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any class and are not restricted by armor type for transmog collection. Each dungeon's Normal and Heroic lockouts are separate, but this specific epic set only drops from Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
You must run the dungeons on Heroic difficulty to be eligible for these specific purple epic items; Normal difficulty drops a different, blue-quality set. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter, will significantly speed up the time spent running between bosses and traveling between dungeons. All pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. No complex skips are necessary as a max-level character can run straight through each dungeon to the relevant bosses in just a few minutes.

554.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Tier 2 armor for the Mage class, primarily obtained from the Blackwing Lair raid, with one piece dropping from Molten Core. The specific source for each armor slot is a boss within these two raids. The Head and Chest drop from Nefarian, the Shoulder drops from Chromaggus, and the Hands can drop from Flamegor, Ebonroc, or Firemaw. The Feet drop from Broodlord Lashlayer, the Waist from Vaelastrasz the Corrupt, the Wrists from Razorgore the Untamed, and the Legs drop from Ragnaros in the separate Molten Core raid instance.

### Farming Logistics
Both Blackwing Lair and Molten Core use a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American servers. Both raids can be easily soloed by any class at max level, but the appearances can only be collected by Mages. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full set could take between 5-10 weekly clears of both instances. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces drop directly as class-specific items. The raids do not have modern LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic difficulties and have only one standard legacy lockout.

### Farming Tips
To enter Blackwing Lair, click the orb located inside the Upper Blackrock Spire instance hallway. The first boss, Razorgore, requires you to mind control the orb, use the controller to mind control the boss, and then use his abilities to destroy all the eggs in the room. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Note that while other classes can loot the items, the appearances will only be added to the wardrobe collection for the Mage class.

555.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Tier 14 Priest armor, obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids in Pandaria. The five main tier pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses. The Head token drops from Sha of Fear in Terrace of Endless Spring, and the Shoulder token drops from Lei Shi in the same instance. The Chest token drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, the Hands token drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, and the Legs token drops from Blade Lord Ta'yak, all within Heart of Fear. The matching Waist drops from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, the Feet from Tsulong, and the Wrist from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids are on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. Each difficulty (LFR, 10-player Normal, 25-player Normal, 10-player Heroic, 25-player Heroic) has a separate lockout, allowing multiple chances per week across different color variations. The instances are fully soloable at maximum level. The tier pieces use the "Conqueror" token, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks. Due to the number of pieces being split across two raids, expect to spend 5-10 weekly clears to collect the full set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest farming, run the raids on 25-player difficulty (Normal or Heroic), as this setting causes bosses to drop a higher quantity of loot, increasing your chances per run. You can farm the tier tokens on a Paladin, Priest, or Warlock, but the token must be used on a Priest character to learn the "Enigma Vestments" appearance. The non-tier lookalike pieces are cloth armor and can be collected by any cloth-wearing class. The set pieces are bind-on-pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

556.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Looking For Raid difficulty of the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids, released as Tier 14. The five main set pieces are purchased with tier tokens that drop from specific bosses. The Head and Chest tokens drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear. The Shoulder token drops from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring. The Legs token drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak in Heart of Fear, and the Hands token drops from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok in Heart of Fear. The matching Waist, "Girdle of Passing Mists," drops from Tsulong in Terrace of Endless Spring, the Feet, "Sandals of the Starving," drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak in Heart of Fear, and the Wrist, "Spinescale Bracers," drop from Garalon in Heart of Fear.

### Farming Logistics
The instances operate on a weekly raid lockout per character. LFR difficulty has its own lockout, separate from Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties, which each drop a different color variation of this set. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level. The tier pieces use the "Crown of the Shadowy Conqueror" token, which is shared by Hunters, Shamans, and Monks. Evokers can also equip the armor and collect the appearance. Given the number of pieces spread across two raids, expect an average of 4-7 weekly clears to collect the full 8-piece appearance.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version of these raids, speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. He will allow you to queue for the specific wings of Heart of Fear ("The Dread Approach") and Terrace of Endless Spring ("Terrace of Endless Spring"). Upon looting a tier token, it will automatically be converted into the set piece for your class and added to your collection. Running the instances on multiple eligible characters (Hunter, Shaman, Monk, or Evoker) each week is the most effective way to speed up the collection process.

557.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike collection from The Burning Crusade raids Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye (Tempest Keep). The Head, Cowl of the Guiltless, drops from The Lurker Below in Serpentshrine Cavern. Pauldrons of the Feral Lord drop from Leotheras the Blind, Vest of Living Lightning drops from Fathom-Lord Karathress, and Kilt of the Fading Nature drops from Morogrim Tidewalker, all within Serpentshrine Cavern. The remaining main pieces, Gloves of the Searing Grip and Robe of the Elder Scribes, drop from Al'ar and High Astromancer Solarian respectively in The Eye. Matching non-set pieces are Girdle of the Prowler (Hydross the Unstable, SSC), Bracers of the Forest Stalker (Void Reaver, The Eye), and Boots of the Prowler (Fathom-Lord Karathress, SSC).

### Farming Logistics
Both Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye are subject to a weekly raid lockout that resets with the server's weekly reset. These raids only have a single difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts for different modes. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. As the set pieces drop from two different instances and eight different bosses, it may take 4-8 weekly clears to collect every piece, assuming an average drop rate. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use a tier token system, so there are no class-sharing restrictions beyond the leather armor type.

### Farming Tips
Clear both raids in their entirety each week, as pieces are spread across most bosses. In The Eye, you must defeat Al'ar, Void Reaver, and High Astromancer Solarian before Kael'thas Sunstrider becomes active. Similarly, in Serpentshrine Cavern, you must defeat the other five bosses before Lady Vashj can be engaged, so no boss skips are possible or recommended. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No special mechanics, bonus rolls, or grouping are required for this farm.

558.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Tier 9 Warlock set, purchased with currency from the Trial of the Crusader raid. The main pieces are bought from vendors inside the instance (Aspirant Forudir for Horde, Champion Brienna for Alliance) using Trophy of the Crusade, a currency that drops from bosses on Heroic difficulty. The matching waist and feet are not sold by the vendor and instead drop directly from bosses within the raid on 10 or 25-player difficulty.

*   **Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands:** Purchased for 1 Trophy of the Crusade each.
*   **Waist:** Drops from the Faction Champions encounter.
*   **Wrist:** Does not have a unique model; any simple bracer will suffice.
*   **Feet:** Drops from Anub'arak.

### Farming Logistics
The Trial of the Crusader raid has a weekly raid lockout. Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same lockout, so you can only complete one version per character per week. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. The primary currency, Trophy of the Crusade, only drops from the 10-player Heroic and 25-player Heroic versions. A single clear on 25-player Heroic awards 5 Trophies from Anub'arak, enough to purchase all five vendor pieces in one run. The actual Warlock tier set uses the Vanquisher token, shared by Death Knight, Rogue, and Mage, but this lookalike set does not use tokens.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest farm, run the instance on 25-player Heroic difficulty. This guarantees enough Trophy of the Crusade currency in a single run to buy the five main armor pieces and provides the highest drop chance for the matching belt and boots. There are no skips; all encounters must be completed in order, but a max-level character can clear the entire raid in 5-10 minutes. The vendors are inside the instance, allowing you to purchase the gear immediately after defeating Anub'arak. The belt and boots are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought on the Auction House.

559.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired by purchasing the `Ensemble: Tempest Regalia` from Chachi the Artiste, the Venthyr Covenant quartermaster located in Sinfall, Revendreth at coordinates 44.0, 48.0. The ensemble costs 10,000 Reservoir Anima. Purchasing this item requires being a member of the Venthyr Covenant and having reached Renown 80 with them. Using the ensemble teaches the appearances for all eight armor slots: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming Reservoir Anima, which is acquired from most Shadowlands activities, including world quests, treasures, rare elites, dungeons, and raids. The entire farm is soloable at maximum level. Reaching the Renown 80 requirement can be accelerated by purchasing the "Broker Mark of Distinction" from Au'dara in Oribos (coordinates 63.1, 37.5) to instantly reach Renown 60. The remaining Renown and the Anima can be farmed in a few days of dedicated play. This set does not use a token system and is purchased directly.

### Farming Tips
To gather Reservoir Anima quickly, prioritize completing activities in Zereth Mortis, as the rares and treasures there provide large amounts. Also complete your covenant's daily Calling quests and the Shadowlands world boss for substantial weekly Anima rewards. Players not aligned with the Venthyr can switch covenants at any time in Oribos to begin the process. Once the ensemble is purchased and used, the appearance is added to the account-wide Wardrobe and can be used by any character, regardless of their armor type or current covenant affiliation.

560.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Tier 7 armor for Mages, obtained from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum raids in the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The main set pieces are acquired by looting tokens from specific bosses. The Head token drops from Kel'Thuzad, the Shoulder token from Loatheb, the Chest token from The Four Horsemen, and the Legs token from Thaddius. The Hands token drops from Sartharion in the Obsidian Sanctum (10-player). Matching non-set pieces include the Cincture of Polarity (Waist) from Thaddius, the Bracers of Unleashed Magic (Wrist) from The Four Horsemen, and the Boots of the Impaler (Feet) from Kel'Thuzad, all within Naxxramas.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum are on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. Both raids can be fully soloed at maximum level with ease. Collecting the five main token pieces will take approximately 4-8 weekly resets, depending on drop luck. The set pieces are obtained from the "Vanquisher" tier token, which is shared by Death Knight, Druid, Mage, and Rogue. The Hands drop from a separate raid instance, Obsidian Sanctum, which must be cleared in addition to Naxxramas each week. Note that the 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a single lockout; you cannot run both on the same character in the same week.

### Farming Tips
Ensure the raid difficulty is set to 10-player, as the 25-player version drops a different color of the set. After clearing a wing in Naxxramas, a teleporter opens at the wing's entrance, allowing for a quick return to the central platform. Tier tokens from this expansion do not automatically grant the appearance; you must loot the token and turn it in to the appropriate vendor in Northrend's Dalaran, such as Magistrix Lambriesse for Horde or Arcanist Firael for Alliance. No set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

561.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased with gold from the Shado-Pan Assault quartermaster, Ao Pye. He is located at the Shado-Pan Garrison on the Isle of Thunder. The pieces require specific reputation levels to purchase: the waist and wrists are available at Neutral, the hands and feet unlock at Friendly, the head and legs unlock at Honored, and the chest and shoulders unlock at Revered. Reputation is gained by killing bosses inside the Throne of Thunder raid instance.

### Farming Logistics
The reputation needed to purchase this set is earned on a weekly raid lockout. You can only gain reputation from each Throne of Thunder boss once per week per character, regardless of the difficulty chosen. The raid is fully soloable at the current maximum level. It takes approximately 10-14 weekly full clears of the raid to reach Revered reputation on a single character. This set is a cosmetic lookalike and does not use the tier token system. All pieces are acquired from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
To gain reputation, clear all twelve bosses in Throne of Thunder on Normal 10-player difficulty each week, as it is the fastest and easiest version. Once one character reaches Revered with the Shado-Pan Assault, purchase the Grand Commendation of the Shado-Pan Assault from the vendor. Using this item permanently doubles the reputation gained with that faction for all characters on your account, significantly speeding up the farm for alts. The armor pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

562.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the leather armor appearance from the Uldir raid in Battle for Azeroth. The pieces drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Head drops from G'huun, and the Shoulders drop from Mythrax the Unraveler. The Chest and Wrists are sourced from Zek'voz, Herald of N'zoth, while the Legs are from Fetid Devourer. The Hands drop from Vectis, the Feet from Taloc, and the Waist from MOTHER.

### Farming Logistics
Uldir has a weekly raid lockout, but the LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts, allowing for four chances per character each week. The entire raid is soloable by a max-level character. Expect to run the raid for 5-10 weeks per difficulty to collect all eight pieces, depending on drop luck. Battle for Azeroth raids do not use a tier token system; loot drops directly for the four eligible leather classes: Rogue, Druid, Monk, and Demon Hunter. All pieces of this set are found within the Uldir raid.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for the fastest full clear. To collect the complete set, you must defeat all eight bosses and should not use the skip that allows you to jump down the central pit after the third boss. If you have any leftover Seals of Wartorn Fate from the Battle for Azeroth expansion, they can still be used on bosses for a bonus roll and an extra chance at loot. The main set pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

563.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is a non-class lookalike of the Mage Tier 8 armor, obtained from bosses in two Cataclysm dungeons located in Uldum. Most pieces drop within the Halls of Origination: the Head drops from Anraphet, Shoulders from Setesh, Legs from Earthrager Ptah, and Waist from Isiset. The remaining items are found in The Lost City of the Tol'vir: the Chest drops from Siamat, Hands from General Husam, and Feet from High Prophet Barim. There is no matching Wrist piece from this specific source.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from dungeons that have a daily Heroic lockout and a repeatable Normal difficulty lockout, which is capped at 10 instances per hour. The farm is easily soloed by any class at maximum level. As the set pieces are split between two different dungeons, collecting all seven items will likely require 15-20 total runs, assuming average luck. The items are direct drops from bosses and do not use a tier token system. Both Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same loot table and appearances for this set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as you can reset and re-enter immediately up to the hourly cap. In Halls of Origination, you can run directly to the four bosses that drop the set pieces and ignore the others. In The Lost City of the Tol'vir, you must clear the initial trash and activate the Siamat encounter, which involves a multi-wave gauntlet that takes a few minutes. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

565.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Blood Elf Heritage Armor, obtained by completing the questline "Heritage of the Sin'dorei". To be eligible, you must have a Blood Elf character that is level 50 or higher and has achieved Exalted reputation with Silvermoon City. The questline begins with "The Pride of the Sin'dorei," offered by Ambassador Dawnsworn at the Orgrimmar Embassy. The entire eight-piece cosmetic ensemble is rewarded upon completion of the final quest, "The Sunstrider's Legacy."

### Farming Logistics
This set is acquired through a one-time questline and cannot be repeatedly farmed. The quest chain is designed to be completed entirely solo and takes approximately 30-45 minutes once the prerequisites are met. Acquisition is guaranteed upon quest completion. The set does not use a tier token system, as all pieces are awarded together in a single ensemble. All items come from this single source, and there are no different difficulties or lockouts involved.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to earn the required Silvermoon City reputation is to purchase a Silvermoon City Tabard from the vendor in Eversong Woods and run dungeons; reputation is awarded for mob kills. Note that newly created Blood Elf characters who do not level through the Exiles' Reach starting zone will begin with Exalted reputation automatically. Once unlocked, the appearance is added to the wardrobe for all Blood Elf characters on your account. The questline involves significant travel, so having flight paths in the Eastern Kingdoms unlocked will speed up the process.

566.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cloth lookalike collection from the Icecrown Citadel raid in the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The pieces are direct drops from bosses in the 25-player Normal difficulty version of the instance. The head, Cowl of Malefic Repose, drops from Lord Marrowgar. Shoulders of Ruinous Senility and Gloves of False Gestures both drop from Lady Deathwhisper. The chest, Robes of Rascality, is also from Lord Marrowgar, while the waist, Crushing Coldwraith Belt, can drop from him as well. The legs, Leggings of the Soothing Touch, are obtained from the Valithria Dreamwalker encounter, and the feet, Boots of Unnatural Growth, drop from Rotface.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout, and you can only clear it once per character per week. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties share this lockout, as do the Normal and Heroic modes, so you must choose to run 25-player Normal specifically for this appearance. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. As these pieces are direct drops and not from a tier token, there are no class restrictions on looting beyond the armor type. Given the number of pieces and drop chances, a dedicated character can expect to complete the set in 4-8 weeks.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player and the legacy raid difficulty to Normal before entering. After clearing the lower wings, use the central teleporter to quickly access the upper spire. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter requires you to heal the friendly dragon to full health to complete it; modern self-healing abilities, potions, or bandages are sufficient. A bind-on-equip version of the helmet, Thaumaturge's Crackling Cowl, drops from trash mobs within the raid and may be available for purchase on the Auction House.

568.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is acquired by collecting non-tier cloth items from the 25-player Heroic version of Icecrown Citadel, a Wrath of the Lich King raid. These items share the model and color of the Mage Tier 10 set but can be collected and worn by any cloth class. The Head, `Cowled Lich-Lord's Headwrap`, drops from Valithria Dreamwalker, and the Chest, `Vestments of the Soul-Flayer`, drops from Blood-Queen Lana'thel. Deathbringer Saurfang drops the Legs, `Legwraps of the Resurgent Phantom`, while Lady Deathwhisper drops the Wrist, `The Lady's Brittle Bracers`. The Hands, `Gloves of the Frozen Glade`, drop from Rotface, and the Waist, `Crushing Coldwraith Belt`, comes from the Gunship Battle. Lord Marrowgar drops both the Shoulder, `Shoulderpads of Frozen Stones`, and the Feet, `Boots of the Icy Floe`.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms and Wednesday for European realms. The entire instance is fully soloable on 25-player Heroic difficulty by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, collecting the full 8-piece set will take approximately 5 to 8 weekly clears. Since you are farming direct boss drops rather than tier tokens, the appearances can be collected by any cloth class (Mage, Priest, Warlock). While 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a lockout, Normal and Heroic difficulties do not, allowing you to clear the raid on both 25 Normal and 25 Heroic each week, though this specific recolor only drops from 25 Heroic.

### Farming Tips
You must set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering to be eligible for this specific red and gold color variant. To speed up the run, use the teleporter at the instance entrance to travel directly to the Upper Spire once the initial four bosses are defeated. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter requires healing the boss to full health, so characters without a healing specialization should bring a healing ability, consumable, or the `Bandage Bazooka` toy. For the Gunship Battle, immediately use your cannon to launch yourself to the enemy ship and defeat the enemy Battle-Mage to prevent your own cannons from being frozen.

569.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty mail tier set from The Nighthold raid. The five tier pieces are obtained by looting class-specific tokens from bosses, while the lookalike non-set pieces drop directly. The Head token drops from Gul'dan, the Shoulder token from Tichondrius, and the Chest token from Krosus. The Legs token is looted from Grand Magistrix Elisande, and the Hands token drops from Star Augur Etraeus. For the non-set matching pieces, the Waist drops from Skorpyron, the Feet drop from Krosus, and the Wrist drops from Spellblade Aluriel.

### Farming Logistics
The Nighthold raid operates on a weekly raid lockout. The instance can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces and drop chance, expect to run the raid for 5-10 weekly resets to collect the full set. The tier pieces use the "Venerated" token system, which is shared between Hunter, Shaman, Monk, and Druid classes; any of these four classes can loot the token. The non-set pieces (Waist, Feet, Wrist) drop as direct items only for mail-wearing classes. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are separate lockouts and can each be completed once per week.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific green and orange recolor. You can speed up the run by using the teleporter in the main chamber after defeating the first three required bosses in the lower wings to access the upper levels directly. Farming on a Hunter, Shaman, Monk, or Druid will allow you to collect the tier tokens, which can then be used to create the appearance for your Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker. Remember that tier tokens do not automatically grant the appearance; you must right-click the token in your inventory to create the armor piece.

570.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor from The Nighthold raid on Mythic difficulty. The Head slot appearance drops from Trilliax (Hood of Uncouth Servitude) and the Legs drop from Gul'dan (Legguards of the Uncrowned). The Shoulders are from Krosus (Shoulderguards of Indomitable Purpose) and the Hands are from Tichondrius (Grips of Astounding Potency). The Chest piece is obtained from Star Augur Etraeus (Chestplate of Impenetrable Fervor). The remaining pieces are the Waist from Spellblade Aluriel (Girdle of Nefarious Stratagem), and both the Feet (Stonestep Boots) and Wrist (Blood-Scoured Wristguards) from the first boss, Skorpyron.

### Farming Logistics
The Nighthold raid operates on a weekly raid lockout for Mythic difficulty, resetting every Tuesday. The instance can be fully soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Expect to take approximately 4-8 weekly resets to collect the full set, depending on drop-rate luck. This raid uses a tier token system, but the three plate classes are on different tokens: Warrior (Zenith), Paladin (Venerated), and Death Knight (Dreadful). Because of this, collecting the non-tier lookalike pieces is the primary method for completing the set across all three classes. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts and reward different colorations of the set.

### Farming Tips
Farm the set on any of your plate-wearing characters, as the non-tier lookalike pieces that drop from bosses will unlock the appearance for your Warrior, Paladin, and Death Knight simultaneously. Before entering, ensure your raid difficulty is set to Mythic for this specific red and orange color variant. There are no significant skips available for solo farming; you must clear bosses in a linear fashion to unlock the path to later bosses like Gul'dan. Legion-era bonus rolls (Seal of Broken Fate) are no longer obtainable or usable to increase your chances of loot.

571.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased from the Trading Post vendors: T&W (Tawney and Wilder) at the Mage District in Stormwind or the Zen'shiri Trading Post next to Grommash Hold in Orgrimmar. The set is sold as a single item, "Ensemble: Firehawk Robes of Conflagration," which grants all eight armor appearances upon use: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist. It was available during the May 2023 Trading Post rotation for 750 Trader's Tender. As the vendor's stock rotates monthly, this set is only obtainable when it returns to the active catalog.

### Farming Logistics
The acquisition of this set is tied to a monthly currency lockout, not a traditional raid or dungeon lockout. Players earn a maximum of 1,000 Trader's Tender per month by completing activities in the Traveler's Log, with an additional 500 awarded just for owning the Dragonflight expansion. Earning the currency is a solo activity and does not require a group. Since this is a cosmetic set purchased directly, there is no tier token system and no different versions for LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic difficulties. All pieces are acquired simultaneously from the single ensemble purchase.

### Farming Tips
To prepare for the set's potential return, consistently complete the monthly Traveler's Log to bank Trader's Tender. If the set reappears in the Trading Post catalog, you can purchase it directly or use the "freeze" option to hold the item in the vendor's inventory, allowing you to buy it in a subsequent month. This set is Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House or traded between players. The ensemble automatically adds all appearances to your Wardrobe upon use.

572.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Heroic difficulty cloth armor from the Firelands raid, released in the Cataclysm expansion. The pieces are a mix of boss drops, tier tokens, and Bind on Equip items that drop from trash mobs within the instance. You must set the raid difficulty to Heroic to obtain this specific red and orange recolor. The following bosses drop the corresponding non-tier lookalike pieces:

*   **Head:** `Occultist's Fire-Stitched Hood` (Alysrazor)
*   **Shoulder:** `Flickering Shoulderpads` (Majordomo Fandral Staghelm)
*   **Chest:** `Cinderweb Robes` (Alysrazor)
*   **Legs:** `Cinderweb Leggings` (Beth'tilac)
*   **Hands:** `Firescruff Gauntlets` (Lord Rhyolith)
*   **Feet:** `Fire-Scorched Greaves` (BoE trash drop)
*   **Waist:** `Cinderweb Cinch` (BoE trash drop)
*   **Wrist:** `Bracers of the Black Dream` (Lord Rhyolith)

### Farming Logistics
Firelands operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms. Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single lockout, so you can only complete one version per character each week. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. The tier pieces for this set use the "Vanquisher" token, which is shared by the Mage, Rogue, Death Knight, and Druid classes. Most pieces drop from specific bosses, but the Waist and Feet are Bind on Equip items that drop from any trash mob and can be traded or sold. A complete set can be reasonably farmed in 5-10 weekly resets, though this is subject to random chance.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance at loot, set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering the instance; this increases the number of items dropped by each boss. Because the waist and feet are BoE, check the Auction House first, as they can often be purchased to speed up collection. To farm these BoE items directly, you can clear the trash mobs up to the first boss, exit the instance, reset it, and repeat. Farming on a Mage is most efficient, as they can use both the lookalike drops and the tier tokens for the appearance.

573.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is a collection of lookalike pieces that drop from bosses in various Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The headpiece drops from Keli'dan the Breaker in Heroic Blood Furnace, and the shoulders drop from Hungarfen in Heroic Underbog. The chest is looted from Murmur in Heroic Shadow Labyrinth, while the legs drop from Warlord Kalithresh in Heroic Steamvault. The hands are obtained from Vazruden & Nazan in Heroic Hellfire Ramparts, and the feet come from Captain Skarloc in Heroic Old Hillsbrad Foothills. The waist drops from The Black Stalker in Heroic Underbog, and the wrists are found on Broggok in Heroic Blood Furnace.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed across multiple dungeons, each with a daily Heroic dungeon reset. The instances can be fully soloed at maximum level with no difficulty. As each piece drops from a different boss, collection time is entirely dependent on drop-rate luck across several lockouts; expect to run each specific dungeon multiple times. This set does not use a tier token system and drops as direct loot for any class able to loot it, though only cloth-wearers can add the appearance to the wardrobe. The items only drop on Heroic difficulty; Normal difficulty has a different loot table.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeons on Heroic difficulty, as this is the only mode where the set pieces drop. Use a high-mobility class like a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter to clear the instances as quickly as possible. Since each dungeon has a daily lockout, you can increase your chances by farming on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) each day. Plan an efficient route by clearing dungeons in the same zone consecutively, such as running Hellfire Ramparts and Blood Furnace back-to-back in Hellfire Peninsula. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

574.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike that drops from the Tier 5 raids, Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye (Tempest Keep). The headpiece, Cowl of the Guileful, drops from Lady Vashj in Serpentshrine Cavern. The chest, Chestguard of the Conniver, drops from Kael'thas Sunstrider in The Eye. The shoulders, Pauldrons of the Drowned, drop from High Warlord Naj'entus, while the legs, Leggings of the Seventh Circle, drop from Fathom-Lord Karathress. The hands, Gloves of the Unforgiving, drop from Leotheras the Blind, and the feet, Boots of the Shifting Nightmare, drop from The Lurker Below, all within Serpentshrine Cavern. The matching waist, Belt of One-Hundred Deaths, is a world drop from trash mobs inside Serpentshrine Cavern, while the wrists, Vambraces of the Vile, drop from Al'ar in The Eye.

### Farming Logistics
Both Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye are subject to a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. The instances can be fully soloed at maximum level with any class, as all bosses will be trivial encounters. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces can take approximately 5-15 weeks, depending on luck, as you can only loot each boss once per week per character. This set is a direct drop lookalike and does not use a tier token system; the gear pieces drop directly from bosses. Burning Crusade raids only have a single 25-player difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts for Normal, Heroic, or Mythic versions to farm.

### Farming Tips
To access the final bosses in each raid, you must defeat all preceding bosses. In Serpentshrine Cavern, you must kill the first six bosses to make Lady Vashj attackable. In The Eye, you must defeat all four of Kael'thas Sunstrider's advisors to spawn him. The matching waist piece, Belt of One-Hundred Deaths, is Bind-on-Equip and can often be purchased from the Auction House to save time farming trash. Use a high-mobility character like a Demon Hunter or Monk to speed up movement through these large raid instances.

575.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a recolor of the Lawbringer Armor and drops from various bosses across Cataclysm-era Heroic dungeons. The Head drops from High Priestess Azil in The Stonecore, and the Hands drop from Rom'ogg Bonecrusher in Blackrock Caverns. The Shoulders and Legs both drop in Shadowfang Keep, from Commander Springvale and Lord Godfrey respectively. The Chest and Feet are found in Grim Batol, dropping from General Umbriss and Forgemaster Throngus. A matching Waist can be acquired from Corla, Herald of Twilight in Blackrock Caverns, while matching Wrists drop from Lockmaw in Lost City of the Tol'vir.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed across multiple dungeons that are on a daily Heroic dungeon reset, meaning each boss can be looted once per day per character. The entire set can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. Due to the pieces being spread across five different dungeons, collecting the full set will likely take 5-10 days of targeted farming, assuming an average drop chance per piece. This set does not use a tier token system; all items are direct boss drops for Plate-wearing classes (Paladin, Warrior, Death Knight). Each piece comes from a unique source, requiring you to run all specified dungeons daily for maximum efficiency.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic, as this gear does not drop on Normal difficulty. Max-level characters can clear these dungeons in minutes, so no group is needed. There are no relevant skips or shortcuts required, as you can simply run directly from boss to boss. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House, and older content systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to these drops.

576.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor lookalike from the 10-player version of the Ulduar raid, released in Tier 8. The pieces are not class-specific and drop directly from bosses, separate from the tier token system. The Head slot, Helm of the Furnace Master, and Hands slot, Gauntlets of the Iron Furnace, both drop from Ignis the Furnace Master. The Shoulder slot, Shoulderplates of the Deconstructor, drops from XT-002 Deconstructor, while the Chestpiece of the Tower of Strength drops from Freya. The Legs, Plated Leggings of the Colossus, are looted from Kologarn, and the matching off-set Feet, Sabatons of the Iron Guardian, drop from the Assembly of Iron. The Waist, Belt of the Ringworm, is from Hodir, and the Bracers of the Smothering Inferno drop from Flame Leviathan.

### Farming Logistics
The Ulduar raid instance has a weekly raid lockout that resets every Tuesday for North American realms. The entire instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will take approximately 8-12 weekly resets. This guide focuses on the lookalike pieces that drop directly; the official class tier sets use the Protector token (Warrior), Conqueror token (Paladin), and Vanquisher token (Death Knight), which is a more complex farm. The 10-player and 25-player versions of Ulduar share a single lockout, meaning you can only kill each boss once per week on either difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player before entering the instance to ensure the correct red and gold color variant drops. Activating any boss's "hard mode" will change its loot table to the 25-player version, which awards a different, blue and purple colored set, so all encounters should be completed on their normal settings. To speed up the first encounter, speak to the Lore Keeper of Norgannon at the entrance to skip the vehicle portion of the Flame Leviathan fight. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or bought from the Auction House.

577.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Plate armor from the Castle Nathria raid on Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty, available only to Warrior, Paladin, and Death Knight characters. The individual pieces drop directly from specific bosses within the instance. The Head is from Sire Denathrius, the Shoulder is from Stone Legion Generals, and the Chest is from Sludgefist. The Legs drop from Hungering Destroyer, Hands from Artificer Xy'mox, and Feet from Huntsman Altimor. Finally, the Waist is from Shriekwing and the Wrist is from the Council of Blood.

### Farming Logistics
The instance has a weekly raid lockout per character. The Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts, each awarding a different color variation of the set. At current max level, the entire raid is fully soloable on all difficulties. Castle Nathria does not use a tier token system; armor pieces drop directly from bosses for eligible classes. Assuming a 15-20% drop chance per boss, collecting the full 8-piece set can take approximately 6-10 full clears, subject to random luck.

### Farming Tips
To farm this specific LFR coloration, speak to the NPC Ta'elfar in Oribos to queue for the solo version of Castle Nathria LFR wings. Running the instance on multiple plate-wearing characters each week (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) will increase your chances, as transmog appearances are account-wide once an item is collected. There are no significant skips within the raid, requiring you to clear bosses in a linear path through each wing. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

579.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal/Heroic difficulty leather lookalike set from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid, released in Tier 16 of the Mists of Pandaria expansion. Five pieces are obtained by exchanging tier tokens, while the Feet, Waist, and Wrists are direct boss drops.
*   **Head:** Token of the Cursed Vanquisher, dropped by Thok the Bloodthirsty.
*   **Shoulder:** Token of the Cursed Vanquisher, dropped by Siegecrafter Blackfuse.
*   **Chest:** Token of the Cursed Vanquisher, dropped by Sha of Pride.
*   **Legs:** Token of the Cursed Vanquisher, dropped by General Nazgrim.
*   **Hands:** Token of the Cursed Vanquisher, dropped by Garrosh Hellscream.
*   **Feet:** Dark-Eye Boots, dropped by Immerseus.
*   **Waist:** Rope-Girdle of Forseen Death, dropped by Norushen.
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of Purified Spirit, dropped by Malkorok.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar has a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing up to four attempts per week on a single character for the desired appearance. The entire raid is fully soloable at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces spread across different bosses and the weekly lockout, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 4-8 weeks. The Token of the Cursed Vanquisher is shared by Rogue, Druid, Death Knight, and Mage; however, the appearance can only be obtained by using the token on a Rogue or Druid. Once learned, the appearance is available for all Leather-wearing classes.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for the fastest and easiest clears, as it shares its appearance with the Heroic version. You must right-click the tier tokens in your inventory on a Rogue or Druid to create the armor piece and add its appearance to your collection; simply looting the token does not unlock the transmog. Use the teleporters located after the Iron Juggernaut and Malkorok to navigate the instance more quickly. It is possible to use Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) on bosses for an extra chance at loot; these can be acquired from the Elder Liao on the Timeless Isle.

580.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Tier 5 Paladin armor, acquired by redeeming tokens from the Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye (Tempest Keep) raids from The Burning Crusade. The Head token drops from Lady Vashj in Serpentshrine Cavern, and the Chest token drops from Kael'thas Sunstrider in The Eye. The Shoulder token is from Void Reaver in The Eye, the Leg token is from Fathom-Lord Karathress in Serpentshrine Cavern, and the Hands token is from Leotheras the Blind in Serpentshrine Cavern. Matching non-set pieces drop directly from bosses: the Waist (Girdle of the Invulnerable) is from Morogrim Tidewalker in Serpentshrine Cavern, the Feet (Tide-Stomper's Greaves) are from Hydross the Unstable in Serpentshrine Cavern, and the Wrists (World-ender's Wristguards) are from Kael'thas Sunstrider in The Eye.

### Farming Logistics
Both Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye operate on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. Both raids can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level with no difficulty. Given the eight total pieces drop from eight different bosses, a full collection can be expected in 5-10 weekly clears, assuming a 20-25% drop chance per item. The five main set pieces use the "Vanquisher" token, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks; a Paladin looting the token will unlock the Crystalforge appearance. These raids have only one difficulty, so you cannot run them multiple times per week on the same character.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, mount up and run past all non-essential trash mobs directly to the bosses. In Serpentshrine Cavern, after defeating Hydross the Unstable, you can swim across the central body of water directly to Morogrim Tidewalker's platform to save time. When you loot a tier token, the appearance is automatically added to your Wardrobe for all eligible classes, meaning you do not need to travel to Shattrath City to turn the physical token in. You can farm the raids on a Priest or Warlock alt to collect the token appearances, which will also unlock them for your Paladin.

581.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Paladin Tier 6 armor, obtained from the Black Temple and Hyjal Summit raids in The Burning Crusade expansion. The Head and Hands tokens drop in Hyjal Summit, while the Shoulder, Chest, and Legs tokens drop in Black Temple. The matching non-set Waist, Feet, and Wrist pieces drop from trash mobs within Black Temple.

*   **Head:** Token drops from Archimonde in Hyjal Summit.
*   **Shoulder:** Token drops from Mother Shahraz in Black Temple.
*   **Chest:** Token drops from Illidan Stormrage in Black Temple.
*   **Legs:** Token drops from Illidan Stormrage in Black Temple.
*   **Hands:** Token drops from Azgalor in Hyjal Summit.
*   **Waist:** The lookalike Girdle of Hope drops from the trash mob Essence of Anger in Black Temple.
*   **Feet:** The lookalike Boots of the Divine Light drop from various trash mobs in Black Temple.
*   **Wrist:** The lookalike Bracers of the Divine Light drop from various trash mobs in Black Temple.

### Farming Logistics
Both Black Temple and Hyjal Summit operate on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The entire set can be fully soloed at maximum level with ease. Collecting all five tier tokens from bosses will likely take 4-8 weeks, depending on drop luck. The set uses the "Vanquisher" token for the Helm, Chest, and Legs, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Hunters, while the Shoulder and Hand tokens are Paladin-specific. The non-set pieces drop from different sources (trash mobs) than the main set pieces (bosses). These raids only have a single difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts to farm.

### Farming Tips
Use a high-mobility class like a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter to clear the instances faster. The tier tokens themselves are not automatically added to your wardrobe; you must take them to a vendor in Shattrath City or on the Isle of Quel'Danas to exchange them for the gear pieces. The matching non-set Waist, Feet, and Wrist pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can often be purchased from the Auction House to complete the set faster. To fight Illidan in Black Temple, you must first defeat Supremus, Shade of Akama, Teron Gorefiend, Gurtogg Bloodboil, Reliquary of Souls, and Mother Shahraz.

582.content:
### How to Obtain
This set appearance is obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids on Normal difficulty. The primary armor pieces are non-tier lookalikes of the Paladin Tier 14 set, which can be looted and equipped by any plate-wearing class. The matching non-set boots, belt, and bracers also drop from bosses within these two raids.

*   **Head:** Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear)
*   **Shoulder:** Lei Shi (Terrace of Endless Spring)
*   **Chest:** Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear)
*   **Legs:** Wind Lord Mel'jarak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Hands:** Blade Lord Ta'yak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Feet:** Garalon (Heart of Fear)
*   **Waist:** Imperial Vizier Zor'lok (Heart of Fear)
*   **Wrist:** Protectors of the Endless (Terrace of Endless Spring)

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids operate on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The instances can be fully soloed at maximum level with trivial difficulty. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-8 weekly clears across both raids. While the actual tier pieces use the "Shadowy Conqueror" token shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks, the lookalike plate items listed drop directly and can be obtained by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic versions of these raids have separate lockouts, allowing for multiple attempts per week if you are also collecting the different color variants of the set.

### Farming Tips
For this specific set's coloration, ensure your raid difficulty is set to Normal before entering. In Heart of Fear, you must defeat the first three bosses (Zor'lok, Ta'yak, Garalon) before the gate to the lower levels opens. In Terrace of Endless Spring, the instance is linear and requires defeating the bosses in order. Any tier tokens you loot will automatically grant the appearance to your collection, so there is no need to find a vendor. The bonus roll system from Mists of Pandaria is no longer active for these raids, and none of the set pieces are Bind on Equip to be purchased from the Auction House.

583.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the 10-player version of the Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum raids from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The five main tier pieces are sourced from specific bosses within these two instances. The Head token drops from Kel'Thuzad, the Shoulder token from Loatheb, the Chest token from The Four Horsemen, and the Legs token from Thaddius. The Hands drop directly from Sartharion in the Obsidian Sanctum, not as a token. Matching non-set pieces drop from various other Naxxramas bosses: the Waist from Grobbulus or Heigan the Unclean, the Feet from Gluth or Gothik the Harvester, and the Wrist from Maexxna.

### Farming Logistics
The farm involves two separate weekly raid lockouts for Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum, both of which can be completed in the same week. Both raids are easily soloable by any class at current maximum level. The tier tokens for Head, Shoulders, Chest, and Legs are guaranteed drops from their respective bosses, meaning all four can be acquired in a single run. The Hands have a high drop rate from Sartharion. This set uses the "Valorous" token system: the single "Token of the Valorous" is shared by and can be used to create tier pieces for Mage, Priest, and Warlock characters. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties for Naxxramas have separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your raid difficulty is set to "10-Player (Normal)" before entering each instance to get the correct version. Inside Naxxramas, use the teleporter in the central room after clearing each wing to quickly access later bosses and save time. For the hand slot piece from Obsidian Sanctum, defeating Sartharion with the three Twilight Drakes alive increases the amount of loot, but is not required for the tier gloves to drop. Upon looting a tier token, the appearance is automatically added to your collection for all eligible classes, so you do not need to take it to a vendor. The non-set Waist, Feet, and Wrist pieces are Bind on Equip, so they can sometimes be purchased directly from the Auction House.

584.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Plate of the Last Mogu" lookalike, which drops from the Terrace of Endless Spring raid in Mists of Pandaria. The main set pieces are direct boss drops from the Normal difficulty version of the instance. The Helm and Legplates of the Last Mogu drop from the final boss, Sha of Fear. The Spaulders of the Last Mogu drop from Lei Shi, the Chestplate of the Last Mogu drops from Tsulong, and the Gauntlets of the Last Mogu drop from the Protectors of the Endless. Matching non-set belts and boots, such as the Girdle of Crushing Strength and Flesh-Shredding Sabatons, also drop from bosses within the same raid.

### Farming Logistics
The instance has a weekly raid lockout, allowing one chance at loot per boss each week. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces and drop chance, expect to complete between 4-8 weekly resets to collect the full set. This set is a non-tier lookalike, so its pieces drop directly and do not use a tier token system. The Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single weekly loot lockout, while the LFR difficulty has its own separate lockout and drops a different green-colored version of the set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear and the correct blue and bronze color variant, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty. Terrace of Endless Spring is a linear, four-boss instance with no available skips or shortcuts. The bonus roll system (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) from Mists of Pandaria is no longer functional for acquiring loot from these bosses. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House, so they must be farmed directly.

585.content:
### How to Obtain
This lookalike plate set is assembled from pieces found in the Mists of Pandaria raids Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring on Heroic difficulty. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Hands, Legs, and Feet drop from bosses within Heart of Fear. The Crown of the Doomed Empress (Head) drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, the Shoulderguards of the Unflanked (Shoulder) drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak, and the Breastplate of the Fallen Emperor (Chest) drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The Gauntlets of the Warlord's Face (Hands) drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, the Legplates of the Unthinking (Legs) from Garalon, and the Treads of the Unchained (Feet) from Amber-Shaper Un'sok. The final pieces, the Waistplate of the Chosen Dead (Waist) and Bracers of the Banished (Wrist), are obtained from Tsulong and Protectors of the Endless in Terrace of Endless Spring, respectively.

### Farming Logistics
Both Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring have a weekly raid lockout, shared between 10- and 25-player sizes for Heroic difficulty. The instances can be fully soloed at maximum level. Due to the number of items split between two raids, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 5-10 lockouts, subject to drop chance RNG. While this guide covers the direct-drop lookalike pieces for all plate classes, Paladins can also obtain the tier set versions using the "Chest of the Shadowy Conqueror" and "Shoulders of the Shadowy Conqueror" tokens, which they share with Priests and Warlocks. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout and drop different color variations of this set.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to Heroic by right-clicking your character portrait before entering each instance. The raid automatically scales to a flexible size, but Heroic difficulty ensures the correct item appearances will drop. There are no major skips in either raid, requiring a linear clear of all bosses up to the one that drops your desired piece. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls, using an Elder Charm of Good Fortune, can be used on bosses for an additional chance at loot. Tier tokens do not automatically add to your wardrobe; they must be looted and then right-clicked to create the gear piece, which then grants the appearance.

586.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate lookalike from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid, released in Tier 16 of Mists of Pandaria. The pieces are direct drops that can be equipped by any plate-wearing class. The headpiece, Immerseus's Headplate, drops from Immerseus. The shoulders, Shoulderguards of the Cursed Protector, drop from Malkorok. The chest, Carapace of the Cursed Conqueror, is a drop from Garrosh Hellscream. The legplates, Legplates of the Cursed Vanquisher, drop from the Paragons of the Klaxxi. The hands, Gauntlets of the Cursed Protector, drop from General Nazgrim, and the feet, Sabatons of the Cursed Vanquisher, drop from Thok the Bloodthirsty. The matching waist and wrist pieces are Greatbelt of the Cursed Vanquisher from Kor'kron Dark Shaman and Bracers of the Cursed Vanquisher from Norushen, respectively.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid operates on a weekly raid lockout per difficulty. This means you can clear Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties once per week on each character for four separate chances at loot. The entire raid is fully soloable at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, it may take 5-15 weekly clears to collect the full set on a single character. While these lookalike pieces drop directly, the official Paladin tier set from this raid uses the Conqueror token, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks. The different raid difficulties drop different color variations of the set, and their lockouts are completely independent.

### Farming Tips
For the golden color scheme, run the instance on Normal or Heroic difficulty, with Normal being faster to clear. To speed up the farm, use multiple plate-wearing alts (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight), as each can clear all four difficulties weekly. After defeating the first boss, Immerseus, a portal will open that allows you to skip directly to the second major wing of the raid. An additional skip portal becomes available after defeating Malkorok. You can use Warforged Seals, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an extra chance at loot from bosses.

587.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from individual plate armor pieces dropped in the Throne of Thunder raid (Tier 15). The items that drop are Plate, but collecting their appearances unlocks this set for all classes. The specific pieces and their boss sources are as follows: Head (Helm of the Golden Vindicator) from Dark Animus, Shoulder (Pauldrons of the Sunlit Champion) from Twin Consorts, and Chest (Breastplate of Resolute Devotion) from Ji-Kun. The Legs (Legplates of the Relentless Crusader) drop from Iron Qon, Hands (Gauntlets of the Argent Dawn) drop from Horridon, and Feet (Sabatons of Consecrated Ground) drop from Tortos. The Waist (Girdle of the Penitent Knight) is found on the Council of Elders, and the Wrist (Bracers of the Last Stand) drops from Primordius.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid is on a weekly lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American realms. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-20% per item, a full set collection can take approximately 5-10 weeks per character running the instance. This set is a lookalike collection of direct drops and does not use a tier token system; the actual Paladin tier set from this raid uses the Vanquisher token (Paladin, Death Knight, Monk, Druid), which is not relevant for this specific appearance. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout, allowing up to four chances per week at most pieces if you run all versions.

### Farming Tips
For the quickest farm, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as it provides the lowest boss health pools. After killing the first boss, Jin'rokh the Breaker, you can use a major instance skip by jumping down from the left side of the bridge to land directly in the nesting pit area, skipping to Tortos. All items for this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought from the Auction House. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Elder Charms of Good Fortune and Warforged Seals) can be used for an extra chance at loot if you still have the currency, but it is no longer obtainable.

588.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-set plate items from various Cataclysm Heroic dungeons that share the same appearance. The Head, `Greathelm of the Sullen Initiate`, drops from Lord Godfrey in Heroic Shadowfang Keep, which is also where the Shoulders, `Pauldrons of the Forlorn`, drop from Commander Springvale. The Chest, `Breastplate of the Stolen Prince`, is looted from High Priestess Azil in Heroic Stonecore, and the Hands, `Gauntlets of the Disturbed`, come from Corla, Herald of Twilight in Heroic Blackrock Caverns. The Legs, `Legplates of Bloody Reprisal`, and Feet, `Boots of the Incorrupt`, both drop in Heroic Grim Batol from General Umbriss and Forgemaster Throngus respectively. The Waist, `Belt of the Hateful`, drops from Glubtok in Heroic Deadmines, and the Wrist, `Alpha-Warden's Bracers`, is from Anraphet in Heroic Halls of Origination.

### Farming Logistics
The instances containing these items are all subject to a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, allowing one attempt at each boss per character per day. All dungeons and bosses can be fully soloed at maximum level with no difficulty. Given eight separate pieces from different dungeons, collecting the full set will likely take between 5-10 days of targeted runs, assuming an average drop rate of 20% per piece. This is not a tier set, so it does not use a token system, and all pieces are individual drops. Normal and Heroic difficulties are separate lockouts, but these specific appearance items only drop in Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to create a daily checklist of the required dungeons and run each of them once on a plate-wearing character. Before entering each instance, right-click your character portrait and ensure the Dungeon Difficulty is set to Heroic. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Monk, can significantly reduce the time spent running through the dungeons to reach each boss.
