The Feet drop from The Conclave of Wind in the Throne of the Four Winds, and a matching Wrist piece drops from Magmaw in Blackwing Descent.

### Farming Logistics
The three raids operate on separate weekly raid lockouts. All bosses are easily soloable by a single max-level character. In Cataclysm raids, the 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a single lockout, as do Normal and Heroic modes. This set consists of direct-drop items available to any cloth class and does not use the tier token system. Assuming a standard drop rate, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 4-8 weekly resets per character.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering to maximize the amount of loot dropped per boss. Running the raids on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) each week is the most effective way to speed up acquisition, as appearances are account-wide. There are no combat skips in these raids; bosses must be defeated in a mostly linear order to progress. These items are bind-on-pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

647.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from The Burning Crusade raids Hyjal Summit and Black Temple. The five main set pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses. The Head token drops from Archimonde in Hyjal Summit. The Shoulder token drops from Mother Shahraz in Black Temple. The Chest token drops from Illidan Stormrage in Black Temple. The Legs token drops from The Illidari Council in Black Temple. The Hands token drops from Azgalor in Hyjal Summit. The matching Waist, "Belt of the Forgotten Conqueror," drops from Gurtogg Bloodboil in Black Temple, while the Feet, "Boots of the Shifting Nightmare," drop from Teron Gorefiend in Black Temple.

### Farming Logistics
The sources are subject to a weekly raid lockout, with Hyjal Summit and Black Temple having separate lockouts. Both raids can be easily soloed by any class at current max level. The "Forgotten Protector" tier token is used for this set, which is shared by Hunter, Shaman, and Warrior classes. The waist and feet are not part of the token system and drop as direct items. These raids only have a single difficulty, so you only have one chance per boss per character each week. On average, expect 5-10 weekly clears to collect all pieces due to token and item drop rates.

### Farming Tips
For Black Temple, a significant skip exists: after killing Supremus, go to the right wall and climb the chains to bypass several bosses and access Mother Shahraz and the Illidari Council directly. You must take the tier tokens to the vendor Tydormu, located just inside the Caverns of Time instance hub, to turn them in for the gear; the appearance is not automatically added to your Wardrobe. Hyjal Summit is a linear, wave-based encounter with no skips, so bring a class with strong area-of-effect abilities to clear waves quickly. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

648.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a mail armor look-alike of the Shaman Tier 7 set and drops from bosses within the Naxxramas (10-player) raid from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The set pieces are direct boss drops, not token-based items. The Head piece drops from Heigan the Unclean, Shoulders from Gluth, and the Chest from Maexxna. The Legs are found on Gothik the Harvester, Hands drop from Sapphiron, and Feet from Noth the Plaguebringer. The matching Waist drops from Anub'Rekhan, while the Wrists drop from Grobbulus.

### Farming Logistics
The Naxxramas raid has a weekly lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The 10-player and 25-player versions share a single lockout, so you can only kill each boss once per week on a given character; ensure your raid difficulty is set to "Legacy 10-Player" before entering. The entire instance is easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will take approximately 5-10 weekly clears. The actual tier set uses the "Valorous Frostfire" token system for Mages, Warlocks, and Priests, and the "Valorous Dreamwalker" token system for Druids, Rogues, and Death Knights, but this specific look-alike set drops as complete items for any mail-wearing class.

### Farming Tips
To speed up the farm, clear the four main wings (Arachnid, Plague, Military, Construct) in any order to unlock the final bosses. Using the central portal after clearing a wing will teleport you back to the entrance for faster navigation. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. This raid is legacy content, so features like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply. The appearances are automatically added to your wardrobe upon looting, provided your character can equip mail armor.

650.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty leather armor from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid. The main set pieces are obtained via Zenith tier tokens (for Druid, Monk, Rogue) or Dreadful tier tokens (for Demon Hunter), while other matching lookalike pieces drop directly from bosses. The head token drops from Anduin Wrynn, shoulder token from Rygelon, chest token from The Jailer, hands token from Lords of Dread, and legs token from Lihuvim. Matching lookalike feet drop from Halondrus the Reclaimer, the waist drops from Artificer Xy'mox, and the wrists drop from the Prototype Pantheon.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly raid lockout, with LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each having a separate lockout and loot table. At max level, Normal difficulty can be soloed by a well-geared player, though a small group of 2-3 will make mechanical checks on bosses like Halondrus and Anduin significantly easier and faster. Expect 5-15 weekly clears to collect the full set due to drop rate variance. The Zenith tier token is shared by Druids, Monks, and Rogues, while Demon Hunters share the Dreadful tier token with Death Knights and Warlocks; all four leather classes can collect the final appearance.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for this specific brown and bronze color scheme; other difficulties award different color variants. Tier tokens are automatically added to your collection upon being looted, so you do not need to find a vendor to convert them. Some non-set lookalike pieces, particularly from trash mobs, are Bind on Equip and may be available for purchase on the Auction House. There are no major boss skips, so the instance must be cleared in a linear fashion to reach the later bosses who drop the tier tokens.

651.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Heroic leather lookalike version of the Warrior Tier 13 set, obtained from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. All pieces drop from bosses on Heroic difficulty. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Hands, and Legs can be obtained either as direct drops or by acquiring a tier token from specific bosses. The remaining pieces are non-tier drops with a matching appearance.

*   **Head:** Lihuvim, Principal Architect
*   **Shoulder:** Lords of Dread
*   **Chest:** Rygelon
*   **Legs:** Anduin Wrynn
*   **Hands:** Halondrus the Reclaimer
*   **Waist:** Vigilant Guardian
*   **Feet:** Skolex, the Insatiable Ravener
*   **Wrist:** The Jailer

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. The Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing for three chances at loot per week if you clear different versions, though only Heroic drops this specific color. This raid is not fully soloable on Heroic difficulty due to boss mechanics, requiring a small group of 2-5 max-level players. The tier set uses a token system: the Venerated token is shared by Rogue, Druid, and Monk, while the Zenith token is used by Demon Hunters. Non-tier pieces with the same appearance can be looted directly by any leather-wearing class. Collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 8-16 weekly clears due to the drop chances and potential for duplicates.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest farm, create or join a "transmog run" group in the Premade Groups finder, specifically for Heroic Sepulcher of the First Ones. Having more players increases the amount of loot that drops and allows for item trading between players who can wear leather. Tier tokens are automatically added to your transmog collection when looted, so you do not need to turn them in. Additionally, the Creation Catalyst in Zereth Mortis (Coordinates 47, 85) can be used to convert non-set Heroic items from Sepulcher into tier pieces, providing an alternative way to complete the set. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

652.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Tier 11 Cataclysm raids: Blackwing Descent and The Bastion of Twilight. The main set pieces are acquired via tier tokens, which are looted from specific bosses and can be turned in for the gear. The Helm token drops from Nefarian in Blackwing Descent, and the Shoulder token drops from Cho'gall in The Bastion of Twilight. The Chest token is from Halfus Wyrmbreaker in The Bastion of Twilight, while the Leg token drops from Maloriak and the Hand token drops from Magmaw, both in Blackwing Descent. The matching waist, Belt of the Forgemaster, drops from Halfus Wyrmbreaker, while the matching feet, Boots of the Predator, and wrists, Parasitic Bands, both drop from Magmaw.

### Farming Logistics
The raids for this set operate on a weekly raid lockout. Both Blackwing Descent and The Bastion of Twilight can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop rate of 15-25% per item, it will take approximately 4-6 weekly clears to collect the full set. The tier pieces use the Protector token, which is shared by Warriors, Hunters, and Shaman. Normal (10- and 25-player) and Heroic (10- and 25-player) difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing you to clear each version once per week for more opportunities at loot.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raids on 10-player Normal difficulty, as enemy health is significantly lower. Tier tokens are automatically added to your transmog collection upon being looted, eliminating the need to visit a vendor to redeem them. In Blackwing Descent, after defeating Magmaw and the Omnotron Defense System, you can use the central elevator and a slow fall effect to drop down directly to the lower levels, skipping the need to run back. The specific non-set lookalike pieces for this set are Bind on Pickup drops from bosses and cannot be purchased on the Auction House.

653.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Dragon Soul raid, the Tier 13 instance of Cataclysm. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Hands, and Legs pieces are acquired by trading in the "Token of the Forsaken Protector" at vendors in Orgrimmar or Stormwind. The Head token drops from Warmaster Blackhorn, Shoulder from Hagara the Stormbinder, Chest from Ultraxion, Hands from Warlord Zon'ozz, and Legs from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping. The lookalike non-tier Waist, Girdle of the Grotesque, drops from Morchok. The Feet, Treads of Dormant Dreams, drop from Hagara the Stormbinder, and the Wrist, Bracers of Looming Darkness, drop from Warlord Zon'ozz.

### Farming Logistics
Dragon Soul has a weekly raid lockout per character, but the Looking For Raid, 10-player, and 25-player versions are all on separate lockouts. The Normal and Heroic difficulties share a lockout but offer different color variations of the set. The entire instance is fully soloable at current maximum level. The "Token of the Forsaken Protector" is shared by Hunter, Shaman, and Warrior classes. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate, expect an average of 4-6 weekly clears to collect all eight pieces.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic, as this setting drops the highest quantity of loot per boss, increasing your chances of getting a token or off-set piece. There are no major skips, but you can use the teleporters that activate after each boss to move through the instance faster. On the Spine of Deathwing encounter, stand inside the fiery craters left by destroyed Amalgamations to anchor yourself and avoid being rolled off. Tier tokens are automatically added to your appearance collection when looted, eliminating the need to turn them in at a vendor.

654.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor lookalike from the Uldir raid in Battle for Azeroth, obtained on Normal difficulty. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the instance. The Helm of the Gilded Serpent drops from G'huun, and the Spaulders of the Gilded Serpent are from Zek'voz, Herald of N'zoth. The Chestguard of the Gilded Serpent drops from Vectis, while the Legplates of the Gilded Serpent are looted from Fetid Devourer. The Gauntlets of the Gilded Serpent drop from Mythrax the Unraveler, the Sabatons of the Gilded Serpent from Taloc, the Girdle of the Gilded Serpent from MOTHER, and the Vambraces of the Gilded Serpent from Zul, Reborn.

### Farming Logistics
Uldir is on a weekly raid lockout. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout, allowing you to clear each one for different color variations of the set. This specific blue recolor only drops from Normal difficulty. The entire raid can be soloed easily at current maximum level by any of the specified plate classes. As Battle for Azeroth raids do not use a tier token system, loot is personal, with each of the eight bosses having a chance to drop a piece directly; expect 5-10 runs to collect the full set.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty, as this is the only source for this specific color variant. At max level, all bosses can be defeated in a few seconds, making the run very fast. After defeating MOTHER, a teleporter becomes active near the raid's entrance that takes you directly to the final wing, saving travel time. Battle for Azeroth bonus rolls (Seals of Wartorn Fate) can be used on bosses for an extra loot chance if you still have them. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

655.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-tier cloth armor pieces from the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Normal difficulty. The head, Cowl of Dark Whispers, and wrists, The Lady's Brittle Bracers, drop from Lady Deathwhisper. Lord Marrowgar drops both the shoulders, Shoulders of Ruinous Senility, and the waist, Crushing Coldwraith Belt. The remaining pieces are the chest, Robes of Creation from Festergut; the hands, Gloves of False Gestures from the Icecrown Gunship Battle; the legs, Leggings of the Refracted Mind from Valithria Dreamwalker; and the feet, Boots of Unnatural Growth from Rotface.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout. This specific blue recolor only drops from the 25-player Normal difficulty mode. While 10-player Normal, 10-player Heroic, and 25-player Heroic are separate lockouts that can be cleared each week, they do not award this appearance. The entire raid is fully soloable at maximum level. These items are direct boss drops and do not use a tier token system. Expect to complete 3-5 full clears to acquire the entire set.

### Farming Tips
Set your raid difficulty to 25-player Normal before entering the instance. After clearing the first four bosses, you can use the teleporter at the start of the instance to skip directly to the Upper Spire. For the Gunship Battle, you must jump to the enemy ship and use their cannons to prevent your own from freezing. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter requires healing the friendly dragon to full health, which can be accomplished with class healing spells or potent bandages; you may also bypass the encounter entirely by walking along the wall of her chamber.

656.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Tier 10 Shaman lookalike armor, obtained from the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty. The five main set pieces are acquired via a two-step process: first, loot a "Protector's" tier token from a boss, then use that token along with a Mark of Sanctification (Heroic) from The Lich King to purchase the final item from a vendor in Dalaran (Northrend) or at the raid's entrance. The Head token drops from The Lich King, Shoulder from Rotface or Festergut, Chest from Saurfang the Younger, Legs from Lady Deathwhisper, and Hands from Lord Marrowgar. The matching non-tier Waist, the Belt of the Biting Cold, and Feet, the Boots of the Frozen Seed, drop from Valithria Dreamwalker, while the Wrists, Bracers of the Frozen Night, drop from Lord Marrowgar.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is on a weekly raid lockout per character for the 25-player size. The instance can be fully and easily soloed at current max level. Collecting the full set typically takes 4-8 weeks, as you must obtain five separate tier tokens, five Marks of Sanctification from the final boss, and three non-tier drops. The tier pieces use the "Protector" token, which is shared by Shaman, Hunter, and Warrior, meaning you may receive a token for a different class. All pieces for this appearance drop exclusively within the 25-player Heroic version of Icecrown Citadel. You can only defeat each boss once per week per character, so you must have the raid set to 25-player Heroic for every boss kill.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a high-mobility class and set the difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering. After clearing the first four bosses, use the central teleporter to go directly to Sindragosa's antechamber to save time. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter requires you to heal the friendly dragon to full health, so bring a class with a targeted healing spell. Tier tokens themselves do not grant the appearance; you must loot the base token, purchase the Normal-difficulty item from a vendor, and then use the Mark of Sanctification (Heroic) to upgrade it to the Heroic version to collect this specific recolor.

657.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is comprised of non-tier leather pieces from the Mists of Pandaria raid, Throne of Thunder. The Head (Darkfang Mask) drops from Twin Consorts and the Shoulders (Living Wood Spaulders) drop from Ji-Kun. The Chest (Carapace of Segmented Bone) is obtained from Tortos, while the Legs (Leggings of the Final Stand) drop from Iron Qon. Hands (Viseclaw Gauntlets) are looted from Horridon, and the Feet (Stonetoe's Tormented Treads) come from Megaera. The Wrist piece (Plated Vise-Claw Bracers) drops from Durumu the Forgotten, and the matching Waist (Abandoned Zandalari Gore-Sash) is a zone drop from trash mobs within the raid.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American servers. The entire instance is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the six main armor pieces may take 5-10 weekly clears per character. This set does not use the tier token system; each piece drops directly as an item for any class to loot. Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout and drop different color variations of this gear, so you must clear the Normal difficulty for this specific appearance.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, set the raid difficulty to Normal and utilize a character with high mobility. The most critical time-saver is that the belt, Abandoned Zandalari Gore-Sash, is a Bind-on-Equip item and can frequently be purchased from the Auction House, bypassing the need to farm trash mobs. On the Ji-Kun encounter, be sure to click one of the feathers on the ground to gain the ability to fly up to the boss's platform. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) can be used on bosses, but are not efficient to farm for today.

658.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is a non-class-specific recolor obtained from bosses within the Siege of Orgrimmar raid on Heroic difficulty. The Head drops from Malkorok, and the Shoulders are from Siegecrafter Blackfuse. The Chest is looted from the Spoils of Pandaria, while the Legs drop from General Nazgrim. The Hands are from Thok the Bloodthirsty, Feet are from Galakras, the Waist is from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman, and the Wrists drop from the final boss, Garrosh Hellscream.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid operates on a weekly raid lockout. At the current maximum level, the entire instance can be easily soloed by any mail-wearing class. This lookalike set drops as direct, non-token mail pieces, so class token sharing does not apply to this specific farm. Because LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, you can farm each one weekly for different colorations, though this specific set only drops from Heroic. Expect to run the instance for 5-10 weeks to collect all eight pieces, depending on drop luck.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Heroic before entering; loot is personal, so raid size does not matter for solo farming. Use Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) on bosses for an additional chance at loot per kill. After clearing the first few bosses on your initial run, you can use the Ancient Crate and scrolls found throughout the instance to teleport directly to later wings on subsequent weekly clears. During the Galakras encounter, stand near the top of the southern tower to shoot down all drakes with the cannons without needing to move between them.

659.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of non-tier cloth lookalike pieces that drop from the Throne of Thunder raid, which was the Tier 15 instance. The Head, Helm of the Haunted Forest, drops from Horridon, while an alternate, Crown of the World-Breaker, drops from Twin Consorts. The Shoulders of the Haunted Forest drop from Jin'rokh the Breaker, and the Robes of the Haunted Forest drop from Ji-Kun. The Gloves of the Haunted Forest drop from the Council of Elders, and the Leggings of the Haunted Forest drop from Dark Animus. The off-set pieces are the Cord of the Exalted Light (Waist) from Iron Qon, Cuffs of the Exalted Light (Wrist) from Megaera, and Sandals of the Starving Eye (Feet) from Durumu the Forgotten.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder operates on a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can loot each boss once per week per character. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 weekly clears due to the possibility of duplicate drops. This set is comprised of direct boss drops and does not use the tier token system. The Looking For Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockouts and reward different color variations of the set; you must run the raid on Normal difficulty for this specific appearance.

### Farming Tips
You must run this raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific tint. While any class can clear the instance, only cloth-wearing classes (Mage, Priest, Warlock) can add these appearances to their Wardrobe upon looting them. After defeating the first boss, you can use the central dais to teleport to the different wings of the raid to reach bosses more quickly. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Mists of Pandaria's bonus roll currency, Elder Charms of Good Fortune, can grant an extra chance at loot but is no longer obtainable.

660.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cloth lookalike of the Mage Tier 13 Volcanic Regalia, obtained from the Dragon Soul raid in the Cataclysm expansion on Heroic difficulty. The pieces drop directly from bosses throughout the instance. The Head, `Cowl of the Cleansing Flame`, drops from Hagara the Stormbinder. The Shoulders, `Mantle of the Cleansing Flame`, drop from Warmaster Blackhorn. The Chest, `Robes of the Cleansing Flame`, is looted from Ultraxion. The Legs, `Leggings of the Cleansing Flame`, are from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, while the Hands, `Gloves of the Cleansing Flame`, drop from Warlord Zon'ozz. The Feet, `Fungus-Stained Slippers`, drop from Morchok, the Waist, `Cord of the Slain Champion`, is from the Spine of Deathwing encounter, and the Wrists, `Bracers of the Banished`, also drop from Hagara the Stormbinder.

### Farming Logistics
The Dragon Soul raid instance has a weekly raid lockout. Normal and Heroic difficulties share a lockout, while the LFR version has a separate lockout; only Heroic difficulty drops this specific red color tint. The entire raid is easily soloed at current maximum levels. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, a player can expect to complete the full 8-piece set in approximately 5-8 weekly resets. The actual Mage tier set uses the Vanquisher token (shared by Death Knights, Druids, Mages, and Rogues), but this guide's lookalike set pieces drop directly and can be equipped by any cloth-wearing class.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of loot per boss, run the raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty, as this setting drops a larger quantity of items compared to 10-player. There are no major skips, but you can skip the roleplaying dialogue before the Spine of Deathwing encounter by speaking to Sky Captain Swayze. Some non-set lookalike pieces, such as alternate boots or bracers, are Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops from within the instance and can occasionally be found on the Auction House. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault are not applicable to this legacy content.

661.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Tier 5 armor pieces obtained by exchanging tokens that drop in the Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye raids from The Burning Crusade. The non-set matching pieces (Waist, Wrist, Feet) drop directly from bosses in the same raids. The Head token drops from Lady Vashj in Serpentshrine Cavern, and the Legs token drops from Fathom-Lord Karathress in the same instance. The Hands token drops from Leotheras the Blind, also in Serpentshrine Cavern. The Shoulder token drops from Void Reaver in The Eye, while the Chest token drops from the final boss, Kael'thas Sunstrider. The most common matching belt is Belt of the Long Road from Al'ar in The Eye, and matching boots are Boots of Foretelling from High Astromancer Solarian in The Eye.

### Farming Logistics
Both Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye operate on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The instances can be fully soloed with ease by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop rate for each of the five required tokens, a player can expect to complete the set in 8-12 weekly clears. The tier tokens are called "of the Vanquished Champion" (e.g., "Helm of the Vanquished Champion") and are shared by Mage, Priest, and Warlock characters. The Waist, Feet, and Wrist pieces do not come from tokens and drop as regular loot. These raids only have one difficulty (25-player), so there are no separate lockouts to manage.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, use a class with high movement speed. In Serpentshrine Cavern, you can jump from the entrance bridge down into the water to skip the initial trash and go straight to Hydross the Unstable. In The Eye, after defeating Void Reaver, the next three wings can be cleared in any order. Remember that tier tokens are not automatically added to your collection; you must right-click the token in your inventory to create the class-specific armor piece, which is then added to your wardrobe. All pieces from this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

662.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 65-67 elemental mobs in the Burning Crusade zone of Nagrand. The primary farming location is the Throne of the Elements in the northeastern part of the zone. The set consists of eight Bind on Equip pieces that drop from any of the four elemental types (fire, water, earth, air) in this area. Specific pieces include the Helm of the Gathering Storm, Pauldrons of the Sundered Sky, Breastplate of the Rolling Thunder, and Legguards of the Earthen Tremor. The remaining pieces are Gauntlets of the Crashing Tide, Sabatons of the Raging Inferno, Girdle of the Whirling Tempest, and Bracers of the Elemental Fury.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is farmed from mobs in an open-world zone. The farm is trivial to solo at current max level. Due to the low drop chance of world epics, collecting the full set via farming could take 10-20 hours of dedicated mob killing. This is not a tier set, so there are no tokens, and the set pieces are not shared between specific classes, only by armor type. All pieces drop from the same mob pool, though anecdotal evidence suggests a slightly higher drop rate for specific pieces from their corresponding elemental type (e.g., Sabatons of the Raging Inferno from fire elementals).

### Farming Tips
As all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), check the Auction House first to buy any missing items, which is often faster than farming them directly. For active farming, create a 5-player group to increase kill speed and potentially force hyperspawns, dramatically increasing the number of mobs killed per hour. Use a character with high mobility and strong instant-cast area-of-effect abilities to clear packs of elementals quickly. Potions that increase movement speed can also reduce downtime between mob packs.

663.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased as the "Ensemble: Fel-Hearted Raiment" from the Trading Post vendors: Tawney and Wilder in Stormwind or Zen'shiri in Orgrimmar. The ensemble costs 750 Trader's Tender and is available during months it is featured in the rotating stock. Purchasing the ensemble grants the appearances for all eight pieces of the set. The items granted are: Felheart Horns (Head), Felheart Shoulder Pads (Shoulder), Felheart Robes (Chest), Felheart Belt (Waist), Felheart Bracers (Wrist), Felheart Gloves (Hands), Felheart Pants (Legs), and Felheart Slippers (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
The acquisition of this set is tied to the monthly reset of the Trading Post. Trader's Tender currency is earned by completing activities in the Traveler's Log, which also resets monthly. Earning the required currency is fully soloable and can be done through a wide variety of in-game activities. Assuming the ensemble is available, it can be obtained in a single month by earning the necessary currency. There is no tier token system for this set. The entire set comes from the single ensemble purchase and is not found in different difficulties or sources.

### Farming Tips
To acquire the set, players should focus on completing objectives in the Traveler's Log to earn Trader's Tender. If the "Ensemble: Fel-Hearted Raiment" appears in the Trading Post but you lack the currency, you can drag the item into your "frozen" slot to hold it for purchase in a future month. This ensures it will not rotate out of your personal vendor stock. The ensemble and its appearances are bind-on-pickup and cannot be purchased or sold on the Auction House. No in-game systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault can be used to acquire this set.

665.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cloth lookalike of the Warlock Tier 8 armor, obtained from the Ulduar raid (Tier 8) on 10-player difficulty. The pieces are direct boss drops and are not acquired through tier tokens. Each main armor slot drops from a specific boss within the instance.

*   **Head:** Cowl of the Absolute drops from Mimiron.
*   **Shoulder:** Mantle of the Unknowing drops from Thorim.
*   **Chest:** Robes of the Umbral Brute drops from Yogg-Saron.
*   **Hands:** Handwraps of the Roving Pianist drop from XT-002 Deconstructor.
*   **Legs:** Leggings of the Enslaved Idol drops from Freya.
*   **Waist:** Sash of Potent Incantations drops from The Assembly of Iron.
*   **Feet:** Boots of the Mourning Widow drop from Auriaya.
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of Unleashed Magic drop from Kologarn.

### Farming Logistics
Ulduar operates on a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can kill each boss for a chance at loot once per week per character. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at current maximum level. Ulduar has a shared lockout between 10-player and 25-player modes; you cannot clear both in the same week on the same character. This particular set does not use the tier token system; the pieces drop directly as items and can be looted by any class. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take between 5 and 10 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal before entering to ensure you are in the correct loot table for this set. You must defeat all four Keepers (Freya, Hodir, Mimiron, and Thorim) to unlock access to the final boss, Yogg-Saron, who drops the chest piece. After defeating the keepers, use the teleporters in the central Antechamber of Ulduar to quickly navigate between the different wings of the instance. All items in this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

666.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is obtained from the 10-player version of Naxxramas, the Tier 7 raid located in Dragonblight, Northrend. The main set pieces are purchased with tokens: Head from Kel'Thuzad, Shoulders from Loatheb, Chest from The Four Horsemen, and Legs from Thaddius. The Hands token is an exception, dropping from Sartharion in the Obsidian Sanctum raid. Alternatively, non-tier lookalike pieces drop directly from bosses: Chest from Noth the Plaguebringer, Shoulders and Feet from Grobbulus, Legs from Instructor Razuvious, and Hands from Maexxna. Matching non-set Waist and Wrists drop from Gothik the Harvester and Sapphiron, respectively.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum both operate on a weekly raid lockout and are fully soloable by any max-level character. The raid has separate 10-player and 25-player versions, each with its own weekly lockout and distinct color variant of the set; both can be farmed each week for more chances at lookalike items. The set uses the "Lost Conqueror" tier token, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks. Due to the guaranteed drop of tier tokens for solo players and the high number of bosses that can drop lookalike pieces, the full set can typically be collected in 2-4 weekly clears per difficulty.

### Farming Tips
It is most efficient to farm this set on a cloth-wearing class (Mage, Priest, or Warlock) to ensure you can equip and learn the appearance from every piece that drops. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your collection; you must take them to the appropriate tier set vendor in Dalaran (Northrend) and exchange them for the actual armor piece to learn its appearance. After clearing each of the four main wings in Naxxramas, use the teleporter in the central room to quickly return and access the next quarter. All pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

667.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cosmetic replica of the Warlock Tier 4 armor, obtained from Mists of Pandaria raid content. The main set pieces drop from the Throne of Thunder raid. The Head drops from an Anima Golem, the Shoulder from Iron Qon, and the Chest from Dark Animus. The Legs drop from Ji-Kun, while the Hands drop from Horridon. Matching non-set items for the Waist, Feet, and Wrist slots are also found within the Throne of Thunder and the preceding raid, Terrace of Endless Spring.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder and Terrace of Endless Spring are on a weekly raid lockout. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties share a lockout, but Mythic (Heroic in MoP's original naming) is a separate lockout, allowing two full clears per week per character. The entire set can be easily soloed at current maximum level. The armor pieces are obtained by turning in a "Silent" tier token, which is shared by Hunters, Mages, and Rogues, allowing you to farm the tokens on any of those three classes. Given the number of pieces and shared tokens, expect the farm to take 4-7 weekly resets.

### Farming Tips
You must take the tokens to a vendor to purchase the gear; the appearance is not automatically added to your Wardrobe. The vendors are located at Niuzao Temple in Townlong Steppes. To speed up farming, run both the Normal and Mythic (25-player Heroic) difficulties each week. In Throne of Thunder, you can use the teleport pads throughout the instance to skip directly to later wings after defeating the first boss. Bonus rolls (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) can be used on bosses for an extra chance at loot.

668.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier lookalike of the Warlock Tier 14 set, obtained from the Heart of Fear raid in the Dread Wastes. The Head, `Cowl of the Tempest`, drops from Blade Lord Ta'yak. The Shoulders, `Mantle of the Tempest`, drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The Chest, `Robes of the Tempest`, drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Legs, `Leggings of the Tempest`, drop from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, while the Hands, `Gloves of the Tempest`, drop from Garalon. The matching Waist, `Belt of the Night Sky`, drops from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok. A matching pair of Feet, `Sandals of the Blackest Night`, can be found by defeating Tsulong in the Terrace of Endless Spring raid.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids are on a weekly raid lockout. Both instances can be fully soloed by a max-level character. Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same lockout, meaning you can only complete one of them per character each week; the LFR difficulty has its own separate lockout and drops a different color variant. Assuming a standard drop rate, collecting the full set may take 4-8 weekly resets. Warlocks can also obtain the original tier set pieces using the "Conqueror" token, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks; other cloth classes must farm the specific lookalike items.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as it shares a loot table with Heroic but has lower boss health. Heart of Fear is a linear instance with no available skips. You can use an Elder Charm of Good Fortune for a bonus roll on each boss, giving you an extra chance at loot. The items for this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. If you receive a tier token, you must take it to Commander Oxheart at the Niuzao Temple in Townlong Steppes to exchange it for the gear piece to unlock the appearance.

669.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Warlock Tier 4 armor from The Burning Crusade expansion, obtained from three different raids. The Head token (Mask of the Corruptor) drops from Prince Malchezaar in Karazhan. The Shoulder token (Pauldrons of the Corruptor) drops from High King Maulgar in Gruul's Lair. The Chest token (Chestguard of the Corruptor) drops from Magtheridon in Magtheridon's Lair. The Legs token (Leggings of the Corruptor) drops from Gruul the Dragonkiller in Gruul's Lair. The Hands token (Gloves of the Corruptor) drops from The Curator in Karazhan. Matching non-set pieces include the Bracers of Havok from Maiden of Virtue in Karazhan, Cord of Screaming Terrors from The Curator in Karazhan, and Ruby Slippers from the Opera Event in Karazhan.

### Farming Logistics
The three source raids—Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair—are all on a weekly raid lockout. All three instances can be easily soloed by a single player at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, acquiring the full five-piece tier set typically takes 5-10 weeks of clearing all three raids. This set uses a token system; the "Fallen" protector token is shared by Hunter, Mage, and Warlock classes. These raids only have a single difficulty level, so there are no separate lockouts for Normal, Heroic, or Mythic modes.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to speed up clears, especially in the larger Karazhan instance. Karazhan has several side entrances that open up as you progress, allowing for shortcuts back to the main hall after clearing wings like the Opera Hall or the Menagerie. None of the tier tokens or matching non-set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Legacy systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to this content, so drops are entirely dependent on weekly boss kills.

670.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather armor set is a recolor of the Warlock Tier 7 set and drops from the Cataclysm raid, Bastion of Twilight. The individual pieces are direct lookalike drops from specific bosses within the Normal difficulty version of the raid. The Head ("Cowl of the Unseen World"), Chest ("Shadow of the Unblinking Eye"), Legs ("Aberration's Leggings"), and Waist ("Belt of the Depths") all drop from the final boss, Cho'gall. The Shoulder ("Underdweller's Spaulders") and Wrist ("Parasitic Bands") drop from Halfus Wyrmbreaker, while the Hands ("Gloves of the Unblinking Eye") drop from Valiona & Theralion, and the Feet ("Boots of the Stalking Night") drop from The Ascendant Council.

### Farming Logistics
The Bastion of Twilight raid has a weekly raid lockout, shared between 10-man and 25-man Normal difficulties, allowing one full clear per character per week for this appearance. The raid is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to personal loot RNG, collecting all eight pieces can take between 4-10 weeks. An alternative acquisition method is via tier tokens ("Vanquisher" tokens), which are shared by Rogue, Death Knight, Mage, and Druid classes and can be exchanged for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands pieces. All other pieces, such as the belt and boots, must be obtained as direct drops.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on 10-man Normal difficulty for the lowest boss health and fastest clear time, as the desired blue/purple color scheme drops here. The raid is linear, requiring each boss to be defeated in sequence to progress, offering no skips. Note that tier tokens are not automatically added to your collection and must be taken to a Cataclysm tier vendor in Stormwind or Orgrimmar to be converted into an item. The direct-drop lookalike pieces, however, are added immediately upon being looted.

671.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the legacy Wrathful Gladiator's Felweave Armor, a red recolor of the Tier 7 Plagueheart set, purchased from PvP vendors. The five main pieces are sold by Legacy Armor Quartermasters found in Dalaran (Northrend), Gadgetzan (Tanaris), and Area 52 (Netherstorm) for Marks of Honor. The Head, Chest, and Legs each cost 12 Marks of Honor, while the Shoulders and Hands each cost 8 Marks of Honor. The set does not include a matching Waist, Wrist, or Feet piece from the vendor; these must be obtained from other sources.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. The full 5-piece set costs a total of 52 Marks of Honor, which can be farmed entirely solo by participating in PvP activities like Battlegrounds, Arenas, and weekly PvP Brawls. This set is purchased directly and does not use a tier token system. Since the matching belt and boots are not sold by the vendor, they must be farmed separately from Wrath of the Lich King raids like Trial of the Crusader or Ruby Sanctum.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly PvP Brawl, particularly "Comp Stomp" when it is active, as it offers fast wins against AI opponents. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, but you can purchase BoE containers of Marks of Honor from the Auction House to bypass the PvP requirement entirely. Although the original set was designed for Warlocks, the appearance can be purchased and learned by any cloth-wearing class: Mage, Priest, or Warlock.

672.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Warlock Tier 7 set, obtained from the Tier 14 raids Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring. All pieces are direct boss drops. Matching boots and wrists are not part of this specific set and must be obtained from other sources.
*   **Head:** Hood of the Unblinking Eye — Garalon (Heart of Fear)
*   **Shoulder:** Shoulders of the Unblinking Eye — Wind Lord Mel'jarak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Chest:** Robes of the Unblinking Eye — Blade Lord Ta'yak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Legs:** Leggings of the Unblinking Eye — Protectors of the Endless (Terrace of Endless Spring)
*   **Hands:** Gloves of the Unblinking Eye — Imperial Vizier Zor'lok (Heart of Fear)
*   **Waist:** Girdle of the Unblinking Eye — Lei Shi (Terrace of Endless Spring)

### Farming Logistics
The raids Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring operate on a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The entire set can be fully soloed at current maximum level. Collecting all six pieces can take approximately 4-8 weeks, depending on drop chance luck. This set does not use a tier token system; all pieces are direct equipment drops from their respective bosses. The Looking For Raid, Normal, and Heroic versions of the raids are on separate lockouts, allowing you to clear each difficulty once per week for more chances at the gear. Most of the set drops in Heart of Fear, with two pieces located in Terrace of Endless Spring.

### Farming Tips
For the quickest clears, run both raids on 10-player Normal difficulty. In Heart of Fear, the first four bosses must be defeated to reach Wind Lord Mel'jarak. In Terrace of Endless Spring, the first three bosses can be killed in any order to unlock the final encounter. Legacy Loot Mode is automatically active, ensuring bosses drop a full quantity of items from their loot table. Since these are generic armor pieces, you can farm them on any class that can equip them to unlock the appearance account-wide. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

673.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cosmetic recolor obtained from the Looking For Raid difficulty of the Throne of Thunder, a Tier 15 raid from Mists of Pandaria. The cloth armor pieces drop directly from bosses and can be collected by any cloth-wearing class. The Head drops from Twin Consorts, Shoulders from Iron Qon, Chest from Dark Animus, Hands from Council of Elders, and Legs from Ji-Kun. Matching non-set Feet drop from Tortos, the Waist drops from Primordius, and the Wrists drop from Horridon.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder LFR has a weekly lockout for each of its four wings, which can be cleared in any order. The entire raid is easily soloable by a max-level character using legacy loot rules. This set does not use a tier token system; gear drops directly from bosses and is soulbound on pickup. Given the multiple items that drop per boss, a player can reasonably expect to complete the set in 2-4 weekly clears, though this is subject to random chance. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all on separate lockouts and award different color variations of this armor model.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version, speak to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms, and select the specific Throne of Thunder wings. Queueing for each of the four wings is the most direct way to target all the required bosses. Running the raid on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) each week will increase your chances, as the appearances are account-wide. The pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

674.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from the Black Market Auction House (BMAH), currently located with Madam Goya in the Little Scales Daycare in Valdrakken. The individual pieces appear randomly as daily listings and must be won through a competitive gold auction against other players. The set consists of eight pieces: Crown of the Lich King (Head), Pauldrons of the Lich King (Shoulder), Robes of the Lich King (Chest), Leggings of the Lich King (Legs), Gauntlets of the Lich King (Hands), Slippers of the Lich King (Feet), Girdle of the Lich King (Waist), and Bracers of the Lich King (Wrist). There is no guaranteed vendor; acquisition relies entirely on the BMAH's randomized inventory and having sufficient gold to outbid competitors for each piece.

### Farming Logistics
The Black Market Auction House has a daily reset schedule, with new items appearing each day. Acquiring the set is a solo activity based on gold accumulation and bidding, though you are competing directly with other players on your server cluster. The time required to collect all pieces is entirely dependent on random item appearances and can range from weeks to years. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces come exclusively from the BMAH. The main prerequisite for this farm is having a significant amount of gold, often millions, to secure bids on all eight items.

### Farming Tips
To increase your chances and lower costs, position a character on a low-population realm, as fewer players lead to less bidding competition. Check the BMAH listings daily, immediately after the server's reset time, to plan your bids for the day. Bidding the maximum gold cap (10,000,000) on a piece will guarantee a win if no other player does the same. Pieces can also be a rare drop from the Unclaimed Black Market Container, which is a high-risk gamble also purchased from the BMAH.

675.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is a non-tier lookalike of the Warlock Tier 14 set, assembled from pieces dropped in the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. The headpiece, Hood of the Shadow's Caress, drops from Sha of Fear in Terrace of Endless Spring. The shoulders, Mantle of the Shadow's Caress, drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak in Heart of Fear. The chest, Robes of the Shadow's Caress, drops from Garalon, and the hands, Gloves of the Shadow's Caress, drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, both in Heart of Fear. The legs, Leggings of the Shadow's Caress, drop from Amber-Shaper Un'sok in Heart of Fear. Matching waist, feet, and wrist pieces drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear), Lei Shi (Terrace of Endless Spring), and the Protectors of the Endless (Terrace of Endless Spring) respectively.

### Farming Logistics
Both Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring are on a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. All encounters can be fully soloed at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all core pieces may take 4-8 lockouts. This set is composed of direct boss drops and does not use a tier token system, so there are no token-sharing classes to consider. The set pieces are split between two different raid instances, requiring you to clear both each week. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout, allowing up to three chances per boss each week on a single character if you farm all color variants.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raids on 10-player Normal difficulty, which has the lowest boss health. The most efficient farming method is to run both raids weekly on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock), as any collected piece is added to your account-wide wardrobe. In Heart of Fear, you can use the wind gusts after the first boss to fly across the chasm, skipping some trash packs. The Mists of Pandaria bonus roll system (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) technically still functions but is inefficient to farm for; using alts is a better use of time.

676.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Warlock Tier 13 Normal recolor, obtained from the Dragon Soul raid. The five main tier pieces are acquired by looting tokens from specific bosses: the Helm token drops from Warmaster Blackhorn, the Shoulder token from Hagara the Stormbinder, the Chest token from Ultraxion, the Leg token from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, and the Hand token from Warlord Zon'ozz. Three matching non-tier pieces also drop from bosses in the same instance. The Bracers of the Banished drop from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, the Cord of the Slain Champion drops from Warlord Zon'ozz, and the Boots of the Corrupted Tides drop from Hagara the Stormbinder.

### Farming Logistics
Dragon Soul operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American realms. The instance is fully soloable by any class at current maximum level, with all mechanics being trivial. Due to the weekly lockout and specific boss drops, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 3-6 runs on average. This set uses the "Corrupted Conqueror" tier token, which is shared by Paladin, Priest, and Warlock classes. While any of those three classes can loot the token, only cloth-wearing classes can learn the appearance once the item is created. Raid Finder, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout and drop a different color variation of this set.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal before entering to ensure you are farming the correct color variation. The non-tier lookalike pieces (Waist, Wrist, Feet) are Bind on Equip and can often be purchased from the Auction House to accelerate collection. During the Spine of Deathwing encounter, you must strafe left and right to stay within one of the fiery holes left by a destroyed tentacle to avoid being rolled off his back. Tier tokens must be right-clicked in your inventory to create the armor piece before its appearance is added to your collection.

677.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike of the Warlock Tier 10 (25-player Normal) armor, obtained from bosses in the three Cataclysm dungeons located in the Caverns of Time: End Time, Well of Eternity, and Hour of Twilight. The head piece, Cowl of the Unseen, drops from Murozond in End Time. The shoulder piece, Mantle of Doubt, drops from Asira Dawnslayer in Hour of Twilight. The chest, Robes of Rampant Growth, drops from the Echo of Tyrande in End Time. The legs, Leggings of the Betrayed, drop from Queen Azshara in Well of Eternity, and the hands, Gloves of the Unforgiven, are looted from the chest after defeating Mannoroth and Varo'then in Well of Eternity. Matching waist, wrist, and feet pieces are not part of this specific collection and must be sourced elsewhere.

### Farming Logistics
These dungeons can be farmed on Normal difficulty, which has no lockout and can be reset manually after each run. Heroic difficulty contains the same loot but is on a daily dungeon reset, making it inefficient for farming. The entire set can be easily soloed at current maximum level. As the five pieces drop from specific bosses across three different dungeons, collecting the full set will likely require 10-15 total dungeon clears, assuming an average drop chance. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use a tier token system. The items are cloth armor, available for transmog to Mages, Priests, and Warlocks.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty to bypass the daily lockout. To reset the instance, exit the dungeon, right-click your character portrait, and select "Reset all instances." To access these dungeons, travel to the Caverns of Time in Tanaris and enter the portal area for the End Time, Well of Eternity, and Hour of Twilight instances. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No special skips are needed as the dungeons are linear and can be cleared in minutes by a high-level character.

678.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired by collecting tier tokens and non-set items from the Icecrown Citadel raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty. The five main set pieces are purchased using tokens called Regalia of the Grand Conqueror (Heroic), which drop from specific bosses. The Head token drops from The Lich King, the Shoulder token from Rotface, the Chest token from Lady Deathwhisper, the Legs token from Festergut, and the Hands token from Deathbringer Saurfang. The matching Waist, the Crushing Coldwraith Belt, drops from Lord Marrowgar. The matching Feet, Plague Scientist's Boots, drop from Festergut. The matching Wrists, Bracers of Fiery Night, drop from the loot chest after the Valithria Dreamwalker encounter.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel operates on a weekly raid lockout. Normal and Heroic difficulties share a lockout, so you must clear the instance on 25-player Heroic to be eligible for this set's appearance. The entire raid is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. The tier tokens required for this set are the Conqueror tokens, which can be looted by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks. Due to the random nature of which of the three token types drops from a boss, collecting the full 5-piece set may take 10-15 runs. The matching non-set belt, boots, and bracers drop as direct items rather than tokens.

### Farming Tips
You must set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering to farm this specific recolor. To speed up the clear, you can skip the Valithria Dreamwalker encounter by walking around the sides of her room if you do not need the wrist item. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your wardrobe upon looting; you must take the token to a vendor, such as Plate-Purveyor Arlian inside the instance entrance, and purchase the actual armor piece to unlock the appearance. None of the set pieces or their matching lookalikes are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

679.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Trading Post vendor as a cosmetic ensemble available to all classes. It is purchased as a single item, the "Ensemble: Shadowflame Regalia," which unlocks the full appearance set for the account. The ensemble costs 850 Trader's Tender, the currency earned by completing monthly activities in the Traveler's Log. This set was first available in May 2023 and can return to the vendor's rotating stock in a future month. The ensemble provides cosmetic appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Waist, Legs, Feet, Hands, and Cloak slots.

### Farming Logistics
The set is not subject to a raid or dungeon lockout; its availability is tied to the monthly vendor rotation of the Trading Post. The currency, Trader's Tender, is earned by completing objectives in the Traveler's Log to fill a progress bar each month, which awards a total of 500 Tender. An additional 500 Tender is granted at the start of each month to players who own the current expansion and have active game time. As a cosmetic ensemble, there are no tier tokens or class restrictions for purchasing or learning the appearances. All pieces are obtained from the single ensemble purchase.

### Farming Tips
To acquire the necessary currency, focus on completing the easiest and fastest objectives in the Traveler's Log each month. If the ensemble appears on the Trading Post and you do not have enough Trader's Tender, you can freeze the item by dragging it to the dedicated slot in the vendor interface. Freezing an item prevents it from rotating out at the end of the month, guaranteeing you can purchase it later once you have enough currency. The ensemble and its appearances are bind-on-pickup and cannot be sold or purchased on the Auction House.

680.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid on Heroic difficulty. The five main set pieces are acquired via tier tokens that drop from specific bosses. The Head token drops from The Jailer, the Shoulder token from Halondrus, the Chest token from Anduin Wrynn, the Hands token from Lords of Dread, and the Legs token from Rygelon. Matching non-tier pieces for the remaining slots also drop within the raid. The Waist can be found on Lihuvim, the Feet on Artificer Xy'mox, and the Wrists on the Vigilant Guardian.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. Due to the mechanics and health of bosses like Anduin and The Jailer, this instance is not soloable and requires a group, typically found in the Premade Groups finder under "Legacy Raids." Acquiring the full 8-piece set will likely take 5-10 weekly clears per character due to drop rate RNG. The raid uses a tier token system: Dreadful (Death Knight, Demon Hunter, Warlock), Mystic (Druid, Hunter, Mage), Venerated (Monk, Paladin, Priest), and Zenith (Rogue, Shaman, Warrior). LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are on separate lockouts and each award a different color variation of the set.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Heroic difficulty to obtain this specific color variant. There are no significant skips to reach the required bosses; the raid must be cleared in a linear fashion. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your wardrobe upon looting; you must either right-click the token on an appropriate class to create the item or take it to the Revival Catalyst in Zereth Mortis to convert it. While the main set pieces are Bind on Pickup, the matching non-tier items (Waist, Feet, Wrist) have a chance to be Bind on Equip world drops from trash mobs, which can occasionally be purchased from the Auction House.

681.content:
### How to Obtain
This cloth armor set is a non-tier look-alike obtained from the Tier 12 raid, Firelands, from the Cataclysm expansion. All pieces drop from bosses within the Normal difficulty version of the instance. The Head, Cowl of the Clicking Queen, and Hands, Widow's Clutches, both drop from Beth'tilac. The Shoulders, Mantle of the Unheeded, drop from the final boss, Ragnaros. The Chest, Robes of the Burning Acolyte, is looted from Majordomo Staghelm, and the Legs, Leggings of the Fiery Protector, drop from Baleroc. The Feet, Boots of the Black Flame, are from Lord Rhyolith; the Waist, Belt of the Seven Seals, is from Alysrazor; and the Wrists, Cuffs of the Fiery Disciple, are from Shannox.

### Farming Logistics
The Firelands raid has a weekly raid lockout that resets with the weekly maintenance. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level, with all bosses posing no significant threat. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per piece, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take between 5 to 10 weekly clears, depending on luck. This set does not use the tier token system; the pieces are direct boss drops. The Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single weekly lockout, but the Looking For Raid (LFR) version has its own separate lockout, though it drops a different color variation of this set.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of loot per boss, run the instance on 25-player Normal difficulty, as this increases the number of items that drop from each boss. After defeating Alysrazor, you can use the flight mechanic in her area to quickly travel to other parts of the instance, such as the platforms for Baleroc and Majordomo Staghelm. High-level characters can ignore most boss mechanics; for example, Lord Rhyolith can be defeated by attacking one of his legs with high damage, and Shannox's dogs can be killed instantly. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

682.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Heroic difficulty Warlock tier set from the Tier 14 raids, Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring. The main tier pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from bosses, while lookalike pieces for non-tier slots drop directly. The Head and Chest tokens drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear. The Leg token drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, and the Hand token drops from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, both also in Heart of Fear. The Shoulder token drops from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring. Lookalike feet, the Sandals of the Unbidden, drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak (Heart of Fear), and a matching waist, Vestments of the Blind Denizen, drops from Garalon (Heart of Fear).

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids are on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. Both raids are easily soloable by any class at maximum level. The tier pieces are obtained via the "Sha-Touched" Conqueror token, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks. A character of any of these three classes can loot the token and exchange it for the gear. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting the five token-based pieces may take 4-8 weeks per character. Normal and Heroic difficulties share a lockout, but the LFR version is a separate lockout, allowing an additional attempt at a different color of the set each week.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Heroic for the fastest clears, as boss health is lower than on 25-player difficulty with no change to the item appearances. In Heart of Fear, after defeating the first boss, use the wind gusts to travel between the platforms for the next two bosses. Tier tokens do not automatically grant the appearance; you must take them to Commander Lo Ping at Niuzao Temple in Townlong Steppes to exchange them for the actual armor piece. Remember that Elder Charms of Good Fortune can be used for a bonus roll on bosses for an additional chance at loot.

683.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from pieces found in level 60 dungeons from the original game. The majority of the set drops in Scholomance: the Head from Darkmaster Gandling, Shoulders from Jandice Barov, Hands from Ras Frostwhisper, and Feet from Kirtonos the Herald. The Chest piece drops from General Drakkisath in Upper Blackrock Spire, and the Legs drop from Baron Rivendare in Stratholme. The Waist and Wrist pieces are Bind on Pickup zone drops from trash mobs within Scholomance and Stratholme.

### Farming Logistics
These dungeons are subject to the standard hourly instance limit, allowing up to 10 runs per hour, and have no daily or weekly lockout. The entire set can be easily soloed at max level by any class that can equip the armor: Mage, Priest, or Warlock. There is no tier token system for this set; pieces drop directly from bosses and are automatically added to your collection upon pickup. The set is split between three separate instances: Scholomance, Stratholme, and Upper Blackrock Spire.

### Farming Tips
Set your Dungeon Difficulty to Normal, as these instances do not have Heroic or Mythic modes. For the Waist and Wrist pieces, clear all available trash mobs in Scholomance and Stratholme, as they do not drop from a specific boss. To farm the legs in Stratholme, use the main gate entrance and clear the ziggurats to spawn the bosses that open the path to Baron Rivendare. Since all pieces are Bind on Pickup, they cannot be purchased from the Auction House and must be farmed directly.

684.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from bosses and trash mobs within the Tier 5 raids, Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye (Tempest Keep). The Headpiece drops from Lady Vashj, Hands from Leotheras the Blind, and Legs from Fathom-Lord Karathress, all located in Serpentshrine Cavern. The Shoulder piece drops from Void Reaver and the Chest from Kael'thas Sunstrider in The Eye. The Waist and Wrist pieces are Bind-on-Equip items that drop from trash mobs in Serpentshrine Cavern, while the Feet are a BoE drop from trash in The Eye.

### Farming Logistics
Both Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye operate on a weekly raid lockout, shared across all difficulties as only one exists. The entire farm is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Collecting the five boss-dropped pieces may take 4-7 weeks per character due to drop rate variance. This lookalike set drops as direct, named items; it does not use the tier token system of the original Warlock set, which used the "Fallen Hero" token shared by Paladins, Warlocks, and Priests. The Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces can be farmed from trash mobs without a lockout by repeatedly clearing and resetting the instance.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House for the Bind-on-Equip Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces first, as purchasing them is often faster than farming trash. In The Eye, you can kill Void Reaver for the shoulders and then leave the instance without clearing further on characters that do not need the chest piece. To access the bosses in Serpentshrine Cavern, you must kill them in a specific progression, meaning you cannot skip directly to later bosses. Because the full set is split between two separate raids, running the instances on multiple cloth-wearing characters is the most efficient way to increase your weekly chances.

685.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from bosses within Molten Core, a raid from the Classic expansion. The Helm of Might and Breastplate of Might are both dropped by Golemagg the Incinerator. Magmadar drops the Legplates of Might, Baron Geddon drops the Spaulders of Might, Lucifron drops the Gauntlets of Might, and Gehennas drops the Sabatons of Might. The remaining pieces, the Girdle of Might (Waist) and Bracers of Might (Wrist), are Bind-on-Equip items that can drop from any of the non-boss trash mobs inside the instance.

### Farming Logistics
Molten Core operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets with the weekly maintenance. The raid is fully soloable at maximum level, with all encounters posing no threat. As this set does not use a tier token system, the items drop directly and can be looted by any class, but the appearance can only be collected by Plate armor classes: Warrior, Paladin, and Death Knight. Given the number of pieces and standard drop rates, acquiring the full set from boss drops typically takes 5-10 weekly clears. The instance has only a single difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts to manage.

### Farming Tips
To enter the raid quickly, speak with Lothos Riftwaker inside Blackrock Mountain, who can teleport you directly to the instance entrance. Since the Girdle of Might and Bracers of Might are Bind-on-Equip, check the Auction House to potentially purchase them and save time on trash farming. All other set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and must be acquired directly from their respective bosses. Modern systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply to this legacy content.

686.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from tier tokens and non-set matching pieces found in the Burning Crusade raids Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair. The Head token drops from Prince Malchezaar and the Hands token drops from The Curator, both in Karazhan. The Shoulder token drops from High King Maulgar and the Legs token drops from Gruul the Dragonkiller, both in Gruul's Lair. The Chest token drops from Magtheridon in Magtheridon's Lair. The matching Waist, "Girdle of the Pious," drops from Attumen the Huntsman; the Wrist, "Vambraces of the Virtuous," drops from Maiden of Virtue; and the Feet, "Boots of the Protector," drop from the optional boss Nightbane, all within Karazhan.

### Farming Logistics
The three source raids—Karazhan, Gruul's Lair, and Magtheridon's Lair—are all on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. All three instances can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Due to the pieces being spread across seven different bosses in three separate raids, collecting the full 8-piece set will likely take 4-6 weekly resets. The five main set pieces are obtained by looting a "Fallen Champion" token, which is shared by Warriors, Paladins, and Hunters. These raids only have a single difficulty, so there are no separate lockouts for different modes.

### Farming Tips
To obtain the boots from Nightbane in Karazhan, you must first clear the Opera Event and then go to the Master's Terrace balcony to interact with the Smoldering Urn, which summons the boss. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your collection; you must take the token to a vendor, such as G'eras in Shattrath City, and exchange it for the armor piece to unlock the appearance. There are no skips that help with this specific farm, as the required bosses are located along the normal clear path of each instance. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

687.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased from the Necrolord Covenant vendor Su Zettai, the Renown Quartermaster, located in the Seat of the Primus in Maldraxxus. Acquiring the set requires reaching Renown level 60 with the Necrolord Covenant. The complete set, covering the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots, is sold as a single item called "Ensemble: Conqueror's Battlegear". The cost for this ensemble is 10,000 Reservoir Anima.

### Farming Logistics
There is no weekly or daily lockout for farming this set, as its acquisition is based on currency and reputation grinding. The entire process of farming both the required Renown and Reservoir Anima is fully soloable at the current maximum level. The time needed to collect the set can range from a few days to a week of focused play, depending on your efficiency in farming Shadowlands content. This set does not use a tier token system and is purchased directly. Once the ensemble is used, the appearance is learned account-wide for all characters, regardless of their class, armor type, or covenant allegiance.

### Farming Tips
To farm Reservoir Anima quickly, focus on completing World Quests in Zereth Mortis and looting treasures, as they provide high amounts of the currency. For accelerated Renown gains, complete chapters of your covenant campaign or repeatedly run high layers of Torghast, as these activities offer guaranteed Renown until you reach the cap. After completing Chapter 3 of the Korthia campaign, you can swap to the Necrolord covenant instantly in Oribos, removing the original quest-based cooldown. The set pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

688.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from tier tokens and off-set pieces dropped in the Throne of Thunder raid, the Tier 15 instance from Mists of Pandaria. Tier tokens can be exchanged for gear at the Niuzao Temple in Townlong Steppes. The Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, and Legs drop as tokens from specific bosses, while the Waist, Feet, and Wrists are non-token drops with a matching appearance.

*   **Head:** Token drops from Twin Consorts.
*   **Shoulder:** Token drops from Iron Qon.
*   **Chest:** Token drops from Dark Animus.
*   **Legs:** Token drops from Ji-Kun.
*   **Hands:** Token drops from Council of Elders.
*   **Waist:** Drops from Tortos.
*   **Feet:** Drops from Megaera.
*   **Wrist:** Drops from Jin'rokh the Breaker.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The entire instance is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting the full set typically takes 3-5 clears due to the reliance on five separate token drops and three specific boss drops. The tier tokens for this set are "Crown of the Cursed Protector" (Head), "Mantle of the Cursed Protector" (Shoulder), "Chestguard of the Cursed Protector" (Chest), "Leggings of the Cursed Protector" (Legs), and "Gauntlets of the Cursed Protector" (Hands). These tokens are shared by Hunters, Shamans, and Monks; Evokers can collect the appearance by farming on a Hunter or Shaman. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout and award a different color variation of the set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as it has the same drop chance as 25-player but enemies have significantly less health. After defeating the first six bosses, you can use the teleport pad in the central chamber to go directly to Durumu's area, skipping several trash packs. Tier tokens do not automatically grant the appearance to your wardrobe; you must take the token to either the Shado-Pan Quartermaster at the Shado-Pan Garrison in Townlong Steppes or your faction's vendor on the Isle of Thunder to purchase the actual gear piece. All pieces from this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

689.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Tier 16 armor from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid. The five main tier pieces are obtained by looting tokens from specific bosses, while the matching lookalike pieces (Waist, Feet, Wrist) drop directly from other bosses within the same raid. The Head token drops from Garrosh Hellscream, the Shoulder token from The Paragons of the Klaxxi, and the Chest token from the Sha of Pride. The Legs token is looted from Malkorok and the Hands token comes from General Nazgrim. The matching waist piece, Cord of Cacophonous Cawing, drops from Immerseus, the matching feet, Unspeakable Secret, drop from the Spoils of Pandaria, and the matching wrists, Bracers of Purified Spirit, drop from the Sha of Pride.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid has a separate weekly lockout for each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic), allowing you to clear all four difficulties each week for more chances at the set's different color variations. The raid is fully soloable at max level on all difficulties. Due to the number of pieces and reliance on token drops, a full set can take approximately 5-10 weeks to complete. Priest and Warlock characters share the "Conqueror" tier token, while Mages use the "Vanquisher" tier token, which also drops for Rogues, Death Knights, and Druids. Collecting the full set appearance may require running the raid on multiple cloth-wearing characters if you do not have a Priest, Warlock, and Mage.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal difficulty. After defeating Galakras, a Gnomish or Goblin engineer creates a teleporter that allows you to skip directly to the second-to-last wing of the raid, saving significant time. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) can be used on bosses for an extra chance at loot. The lookalike waist, feet, and wrist pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can often be purchased directly from the Auction House. Remember that tier tokens are not automatically added to your wardrobe; you must right-click the token on a character of the appropriate class to create the item and learn the appearance.

690.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is the non-class-specific lookalike of the Death Knight Tier 14 set, obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. The majority of the set drops in Heart of Fear: the Hands drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, Feet from Blade Lord Ta'yak, Waist from Garalon, Legs from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, Wrists from Vizier Jin'bak, and Chest from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The final two pieces are found in Terrace of Endless Spring: the Shoulders drop from Tsulong and the Head drops from Lei Shi. All pieces drop directly as loot and are not acquired via tier tokens.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids are on a weekly loot lockout per character, per difficulty. This means you can clear the LFR, 10-player Normal, 25-player Normal, 10-player Heroic, and 25-player Heroic versions each week for five separate chances at the loot. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character. As this is a lookalike set, the pieces are direct drops and do not use the tier token system.
