Collecting all eight pieces can take several weeks, depending on drop-rate luck.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raids on 10-player Normal difficulty, as enemies have the lowest health pools. The LFR version of this set is a different color and can be accessed by speaking to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. Using a character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, will significantly speed up the time it takes to travel between bosses within the instances. No pieces from this set are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

691.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike version of Warrior Tier 6, obtained from the Throne of Thunder raid in Mists of Pandaria. While the model is the same, the coloration is different from the original set. This is a plate set, available to Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The individual pieces drop from specific bosses within the raid: the Head drops from Twin Consorts, the Shoulders from Iron Qon, and the Chest from Dark Animus. The Legs drop from Ji-Kun, Hands from the Council of Elders, Feet from Tortos, Waist from Horridon, and Wrists from Primordius.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid has a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The instance is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Collecting all eight pieces can take anywhere from 4 to 12 runs on average, heavily dependent on luck, as each piece has an independent drop chance. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for plate-wearing classes. Raid Finder, Normal, and Heroic difficulties are separate lockouts, each rewarding a different color variation of the set (green, blue, and purple, respectively).

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal or Heroic difficulty, as mob health is lower and the loot table is the same as 25-player for legacy content. After defeating the first boss, you can use a skip by heading up the stairs to the left (west) to bypass several early bosses and go directly to the latter part of the instance where most pieces drop. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Bonus roll currency from Mists of Pandaria is no longer obtainable or usable in this raid, so you cannot supplement your drops.

692.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Mists of Pandaria Challenge Mode reward for Warriors and is now exclusively available from the Black Market Auction House (BMAH). It is not obtained from bosses but purchased as a single container item called "Arsenal: Greatleader's Battlegear" which contains all eight armor pieces. The BMAH is currently located in Valdrakken, but a permanent location exists with Madam Goya in the Veiled Stair, Pandaria. The currency required to bid is gold. The container awards the Greatleader's Helm, Spaulders, Tunic, Legguards, Gauntlets, Stompers, Waistguard, and Bracers.

### Farming Logistics
The BMAH has no lockout; availability depends entirely on when the "Arsenal: Greatleader's Battlegear" appears in the daily rotation of items. Farming is a solo endeavor focused on gold acquisition, as the item is purchased, not looted. Only one successful purchase is required to collect all eight pieces. This is a Plate armor set, and its appearance can be used by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The original source, completing all Mists of Pandaria dungeons on Challenge Mode with a Gold rating, was permanently removed at the end of the expansion.

### Farming Tips
The primary strategy is to accumulate a significant amount of gold, as bids on this set regularly reach the 10,000,000 gold cap. To improve your chances, consider creating or transferring a character to a low-population realm where BMAH competition may be less intense. The container and the items within are bind-on-pickup and cannot be sold on the regular Auction House. Use third-party websites or Discord communities that track BMAH listings to monitor when the Arsenal is available on any server. When bidding, it is advisable to place your maximum intended bid in the final moments to prevent being outbid.

693.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is assembled from non-tier lookalike pieces found in the 25-man versions of the Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum raids. The headpiece, `Platehelm of the Great Wyrm`, drops from Sartharion in Obsidian Sanctum. The `Bone-Polished Iceplate Shoulders` drop from Gothik the Harvester, the `Chestplate of the Risen Soldier` from Anub'Rekhan, the `Legplates of Double Strikes` from Maexxna, and the `Gloves of the Steady Hand` from Noth the Plaguebringer in Naxxramas. The matching `Boots of the Neverending Path` drop from Patchwerk, the `Belt of the Forgotten Martyr` from Grobbulus, and the `Bracers of Unrelenting Attack` from Grand Widow Faerlina, all within Naxxramas.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas and Obsidian Sanctum operate on separate weekly raid lockouts, and you must run the 25-player version for this specific color tint. The raids are easily soloed by any max-level character, with a full clear of Naxxramas taking approximately 45-60 minutes. Because the set pieces drop from eight different bosses across two raids, expect to spend 5-10 weeks farming to collect the full appearance. While the official Warrior tier set uses the Vanquisher token (shared with Death Knights, Rogues, and Mages), this lookalike set consists of pieces that drop directly from bosses for any plate-wearing class. The 10-man and 25-man versions of these raids have separate lockouts, but only the 25-man version drops this set.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player before entering each instance. For the helmet farm in Obsidian Sanctum, you can ignore the three Twilight Drakes and engage Sartharion directly for a much faster kill. In Naxxramas, there are no major skips; you must clear the four main wings to unlock the final bosses in the Frostwyrm Lair. One lookalike piece, the `Broken Ram Skull Helm`, is a bind-on-equip world drop and may be available on the Auction House, allowing you to skip the Obsidian Sanctum farm.

694.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Normal difficulty plate lookalike of the Warrior Tier 16 armor, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid. All pieces drop directly from bosses within the instance for any plate-wearing class. The waist, Chain of Unleashed Rage, drops from Immerseus, and the chest, Battleplate of the Last Mogu, drops from Sha of Pride. The feet, Demolisher's Reinforced Stompers, are found on Iron Juggernaut, while the hands, Korgra's Pummelling Fists, drop from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman. General Nazgrim drops both the legs, Legguards of the Unseeing, and the wrists, Bracers of Direct Command. Finally, the shoulders, Shoulderguards of Guttering Light, drop from Malkorok, and the head, Xarantil's Cursed Visage, drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar has a weekly raid lockout for each difficulty, resetting every Tuesday. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level on Normal difficulty. Because there are eight individual pieces subject to personal drop chance, expect to complete approximately 8-12 weekly runs per character to finish the set. This set is comprised of non-tier lookalike pieces, so it does not use the tier token system; however, the official Warrior set uses the Cursed Vanquisher token, shared by Warriors, Rogues, Death Knights, and Monks. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts and reward different color variations of this armor.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific red recolor. After defeating the first boss and unlocking later wings, you can use the teleport scroll near the raid entrance to skip directly to later boss areas on subsequent runs. You can use Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) on each boss for an additional chance at loot. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Farming on multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) each week is the most efficient method to collect the set.

695.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate look-alike of the Warrior Tier 16 set, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid on Normal difficulty. The pieces are direct boss drops and are not acquired via tier tokens. The Head (Helm of the Cursed Protector) drops from Immerseus, the Shoulder (Pauldrons of the Cursed Protector) drops from Malkorok, and the Chest (Chestplate of the Cursed Protector) drops from the Spoils of Pandaria encounter. The Legs (Legplates of the Cursed Protector) drop from The Blackfuse Company, Hands (Gauntlets of the Cursed Protector) drop from General Nazgrim, and Feet (Greaves of the Cursed Vanquisher) drop from Thok the Bloodthirsty. Finally, the Waist (Greatbelt of the Cursed Vanquisher) drops from the Paragons of the Klaxxi and the Wrist (Bracers of the Cursed Protector) drops from Galakras.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid has a weekly raid lockout that resets every Tuesday for NA realms and Wednesday for EU realms. The entire instance is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance per piece, a full set could take between 5 to 15 weekly clears, though this is subject to random luck. The look-alike pieces that form this set are direct drops; they do not use the tier token system. The actual Warrior tier set from this raid uses the Vanquisher token, shared by Warrior, Rogue, Death Knight, and Monk. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all on separate lockouts, but this specific color scheme only drops from Normal difficulty.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, set the raid difficulty to Normal before entering. After defeating the first boss, Immerseus, you can speak with Lorewalker Cho to skip ahead to the second half of the raid if you have completed the instance before on that character. This is useful if you only need drops from later bosses like Malkorok or Garrosh. You can use Mists of Pandaria bonus roll seals, called Warforged Seals, for an extra chance at loot from a boss; these can be obtained from vendors on the Isle of Thunder. None of the pieces are Bind on Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

696.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty variant from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Mists of Pandaria. Individual pieces drop directly from specific bosses within the raid instance. The Head drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty, Shoulders and Feet from Malkorok, and the Chest from the Spoils of Pandaria encounter. The Legs are obtained from General Nazgrim, the Hands from Garrosh Hellscream, the Waist from Immerseus, and the Wrists from Norushen.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. You can clear LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic on the same character in one week as they have separate lockouts. The entire raid is easily soloed at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 5-10 weekly clears per character. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight).

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version of Siege of Orgrimmar, you must speak to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. He allows you to queue for specific wings of the raid, which speeds up targeting specific bosses. Since you queue for each wing separately, there are no raid-wide skips to utilize. Bonus rolls from Mists of Pandaria (Warforged Seals) are no longer functional for this content, and all set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

697.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid, the final raid of Tier 16. All pieces drop exclusively on Mythic difficulty. The Head slot, Hellscream's Helmet, drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty. The Shoulder slot, Hellscream's Shoulderguards, drops from Siegecrafter Blackfuse. The Chest, Hellscream's Chestguard, is from the final boss, Garrosh Hellscream. The Legs, Hellscream's Legplates, drop from the Klaxxi Paragons. For the remaining pieces, the Hands drop from General Nazgrim, the Feet from Malkorok, the Waist from Norushen, and the Wrist from the Iron Juggernaut.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout, specific to the Mythic difficulty. The entire instance can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming a 15-25% chance for your specific piece to be among a boss's multiple drops, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 4-8 weekly clears. This set does not use a tier token system; the armor pieces drop directly as named items. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts, but this specific red and black coloration is only available from Mythic.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Mythic difficulty to obtain this set's appearance. Using a high-mobility class like a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter will significantly speed up the time it takes to travel between bosses. Do not use the Lorewalker Cho skip at the entrance if you need pieces from the first half of the raid, as it will teleport you past those bosses. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are no longer available for this content, and all set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be bought from the Auction House.

698.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from the Ulduar Timewalking raid, which is only active during the Wrath of the Lich King Timewalking world event. The armor pieces drop from specific bosses within the single-difficulty instance. The Head piece drops from Mimiron, the Shoulders from Thorim, and the Chest from Yogg-Saron. The Legs are looted from Freya, while the Hands drop from XT-002 Deconstructor. Matching non-set pieces include the Waist from General Vezax, Feet from Ignis the Furnace Master, and Wrists from Kologarn.

### Farming Logistics
The Ulduar Timewalking raid has a weekly lockout, meaning it can only be completed for loot once per character per event week. The raid cannot be soloed and requires a pre-made group of 10-30 players, typically formed through the Group Finder tool. Due to the event's limited-time nature and personal loot system, collecting all eight pieces may take 3-5 separate Timewalking events. This version of the raid uses personal loot, so there is no tier token system; loot is awarded directly to players. There is only one difficulty for the Timewalking raid, and its lockout is separate from the original Ulduar legacy raid.

### Farming Tips
To maximize your chances, run the raid on multiple Plate-wearing characters during the event week. Form your own group in the Premade Groups finder, as this is often faster than waiting for an invite. Inside the instance, use the teleporters that activate in the central Antechamber of Ulduar to quickly move between the different wings after defeating the initial bosses. The gear is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House, and neither bonus rolls nor the Great Vault can be used to acquire pieces.

699.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the upgraded Tier 10 for Death Knights from the Icecrown Citadel raid in Wrath of the Lich King. The five main set pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands) are obtained by purchasing the base item level 251 gear from a vendor (e.g., Bragon Bronzebeard in Icecrown Citadel) and upgrading it with a Protector's Mark of Sanctification, which drops from The Lich King on 25-player Normal difficulty. You will need one mark for each of the five slots. The matching non-tier pieces drop from specific bosses within the same raid and difficulty: Cold-Blooded Girdle (Waist) from Lord Marrowgar, Greaves of the Bloody Pinion (Feet) from The Blood Princes, and Bracers of the Heir (Wrist) from Festergut.

### Farming Logistics
Icecrown Citadel has a weekly raid lockout, and all four difficulty/size combinations (10-Normal, 10-Heroic, 25-Normal, 25-Heroic) are separate lockouts. This entire farm can be easily soloed by any class at current maximum level. Collecting the five required Protector's Marks of Sanctification should take 2-3 weekly runs, as The Lich King drops multiple marks per kill. The base tier set used the Vanquisher token, shared by Death Knights, Druids, Mages, and Rogues, but this system is obsolete as the base pieces are now purchased directly for gold. The waist, feet, and wrist are separate drops from different bosses within the same instance.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Normal before entering to ensure you get the correct item appearances and marks of sanctification. To speed up the clear, use the teleporters that become active after killing a wing's final boss to quickly return to the central chamber. The Valithria Dreamwalker encounter is a healing check and can be skipped by any non-healing class by simply running past her on either side of the room. The Gunship Battle can be completed in under a minute by immediately using your cannon to launch onto the enemy ship and defeating the enemy commander.

700.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Cataclysm Season 10 Savage Gladiator's PvP armor, purchased from a vendor. Alliance players can find Captain Dirgehammer in the Old Town of Stormwind, while Horde players can find Sergeant Thunderhorn in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar. The entire 8-piece set is available from these vendors. Each piece costs one Mark of Honor, for a total of eight Marks of Honor for the complete set. While designated as a Druid set, its appearance is unlocked account-wide for all leather-wearing classes (Rogue, Monk, Demon Hunter) upon purchase.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming the currency needed to purchase this set. Marks of Honor are primarily obtained by winning PvP battlegrounds and brawls, which can be queued for solo at any time. The full set requires 8 Marks of Honor, which can realistically be farmed in a single day. This set does not use a tier token system and all pieces are purchased directly from the vendor. There are no different difficulties or lockouts to consider as it is a PvP set.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl whenever it is active, as it offers quick and consistent wins against AI opponents. When this brawl is unavailable, simply winning random battlegrounds is the primary method. Additionally, the Great Vault can sometimes offer Marks of Honor as a reward for PvP participation if you choose not to take a gear piece. The set items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

701.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Wrath of the Lich King Season 7 PvP armor, purchased from vendors in Dalaran (Northrend). Horde players can find Trapjaw Rix in Sunreaver's Sanctuary, while Alliance players can find Lieutenant Karter in the Silver Enclave. The currency used is Marks of Honor, obtained from participating in PvP activities like Battlegrounds and Brawls. The five main set pieces (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands) cost a total of 52 Marks of Honor, with the Head, Chest, and Legs costing 12 Marks each and the Shoulders and Hands costing 8 Marks each. Matching non-set pieces for the Waist, Feet, and Wrist slots are also available from the same vendors.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. The entire process is soloable, as players can queue for PvP content alone. The time required to collect the full 52 Marks of Honor for the main set varies based on PvP win rates and queue times, typically taking several hours of focused gameplay. This set is purchased directly and does not use a token system, meaning there are no class-sharing restrictions beyond the armor type. All pieces are sold by the same legacy armor quartermaster for the same currency.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is during the "Comp Stomp" weekly PvP Brawl, which involves fighting AI opponents and offers very fast victories. When this brawl is not active, participating in Random Battleground Blitz or winning standard Random Battlegrounds are the next best sources. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. To reach the vendors, use the portal to Crystalsong Forest in your faction's main portal room in Orgrimmar or Stormwind and fly to Dalaran (Northrend).

702.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Druid PvP armor from Wrath of the Lich King Arena Season 7. It is purchased from a Legacy Armor Quartermaster in Dalaran (Northrend). The Alliance vendor is Zom Bocom and the Horde vendor is Xazi Smolderpipe; both are located in The Underbelly. The currency used is Marks of Honor. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each, while the Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist cost 2 Marks of Honor each, for a total of 20 Marks to complete the set.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is purchased from a vendor. Farming the required currency, Marks of Honor, can be done entirely solo through the PvP queue system and has no daily or weekly limitations. Collecting the 20 Marks of Honor needed for all eight pieces will require approximately 20 random battleground wins. This is a class-specific set for Druids and does not use a shared token system. All pieces are available from the same vendor, so no other sources need to be farmed.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire Marks of Honor is to queue for and win Random Battlegrounds. Participating in the weekly rotating PvP Brawls or queuing for specific battlegrounds during their bonus weekend event can also provide a high rate of return. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House. Ensure you are purchasing the items with "Relentless Gladiator" in the name, as the vendor sells multiple visually similar sets from different PvP seasons.

703.content:
### How to Obtain
This is a replica PvP set purchased from a vendor. Alliance players can buy the set from Leeni "Smiley" Smalls, and Horde players can buy it from Kezzik the Striker; both vendors are located in Area 52 in Netherstorm. The currency used is Marks of Honor. The Head, Chest, and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each. The Shoulder, Hands, and Feet cost 2 Marks of Honor each. The Waist and Wrist cost 1 Mark of Honor each, for a total of 17 Marks for the full 8-piece set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily earned by participating in and winning PvP battlegrounds, which can be queued for solo at max level. The full set requires 17 Marks of Honor, which would take approximately 6 to 12 random battleground wins to collect. This set is purchased directly and does not use a tier token system. All pieces are available from the same vendor for the same currency.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by winning Random Battlegrounds, especially during a specific battleground's "Call to Arms" bonus event. When the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl is active, it provides the most efficient farm, as it involves fighting predictable AI opponents. The weekly PvP quests offered in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Battle," are another reliable source of Marks. The pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

704.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased as an ensemble from a vendor. The ensemble, "Ensemble: Elite Vicious Gladiator's Dreadplate Armor", is sold by Theozhaklos the Curious in the Gladiator's Refuge in Valdrakken. The required currency is one Obsidian Aspectral Earthstone. This token is earned once per character per Dragonflight PvP season by reaching a rating of 1800 in any rated bracket (Rated Battlegrounds, 2v2 Arena, 3v3 Arena, or Solo Shuffle). The ensemble instantly teaches the appearances for all armor slots, including the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, and Waist. This is a Plate set available to Death Knights, Paladins, and Warriors.

### Farming Logistics
There is no traditional lockout for this farm; the acquisition is tied to a seasonal PvP achievement. A character can earn one Obsidian Aspectral Earthstone per season, allowing the purchase of one elite set. Obtaining the 1800 rating can be done entirely solo by queuing for the Rated Solo Shuffle bracket, or in groups for other rated modes. The time required is entirely dependent on player skill and can range from a few hours to many weeks of play. Since the entire set is purchased as a single ensemble, there is no RNG involved with individual pieces. The system is separate from any raid or dungeon content and their respective lockouts.

### Farming Tips
For solo players, Rated Solo Shuffle is the most direct path to obtaining the required 1800 rating. To increase your win rate, focus on learning your class's burst damage windows, defensive cooldown usage, and crowd control chains. Utilizing PvP addons to track enemy cooldowns and diminishing returns is highly recommended. The set ensemble and the Obsidian Aspectral Earthstone token are soulbound and cannot be traded or purchased on the Auction House. No part of this set is obtained through PvE content, so bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply.

705.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the replica Cataclysm Season 10 PvP armor, purchased from a vendor using Marks of Honor. Alliance players must find Sergeant Thunderhorn in Champion's Hall in Old Town, Stormwind. Horde players must visit Captain Dirgehammer in the Hall of Legends, located in Orgrimmar's Valley of Strength. The most direct way to acquire the set is by purchasing the "Ensemble: Ruthless Gladiator's Dragonhide Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor. This single item unlocks the appearances for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously through PvP activities. The currency farming can be done entirely solo by queuing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and brawls. Obtaining the 12 Marks of Honor needed for the ensemble can be accomplished in a single play session, typically requiring between 6 to 12 battleground wins. This is not a token-based set; the ensemble is a direct purchase that instantly adds all the set's appearances to your account-wide wardrobe.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to farm Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP brawl when it is available, as it provides quick, nearly guaranteed wins against AI. Also, complete the weekly PvP quests available in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Battle," for bonus Marks of Honor. Since the appearances are learned from a single ensemble, any class can purchase it from the vendor to unlock the set for all Leather-wearing characters on the account. The individual pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

706.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the elite recolor of the Cataclysm Season 10 PvP gear and is no longer obtainable. It was originally awarded to players who achieved a personal rating of 2200 or higher in Arena or Rated Battlegrounds during that specific season. Upon earning the required achievement, players could purchase the individual armor pieces from the legacy PvP vendors in Stormwind or Orgrimmar. As the acquisition method was tied to a limited-time seasonal achievement, players who did not earn it in Season 10 cannot acquire this specific appearance.

### Farming Logistics
There are no farming logistics for this set as the source has been removed from the game. Acquisition was not based on a lockout, drop chance, or token system, but rather a direct purchase unlocked by a seasonal PvP achievement. The classes that share this leather armor type are Rogue, Druid, Monk, and Demon Hunter, although Monks and Demon Hunters were not in the game when the set was current. Unlike modern PvP rewards, this appearance cannot be obtained from the Great Vault or any other current source.

### Farming Tips
Since this specific elite set cannot be farmed, the best alternative is to collect the non-elite version, "Ruthless Gladiator's Dragonhide Armor." This set shares the same model but has a different color scheme and is readily available. It can be purchased from legacy vendors Apothecary Frye in Orgrimmar or Lieutenant Jackspring in Stormwind. The entire ensemble can be bought for a total of 12 Marks of Honor, which are earned by participating in various forms of PvP content.

707.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the replica of the Alliance Season 2 Gladiator's armor and is purchased from a vendor in Netherstorm. The vendor is Leeni "Smiley" Smalls, located in Area 52 at coordinates (32.8, 64.6). The currency used for purchase is Marks of Honor. The head, chest, and leg pieces each cost 12 Marks of Honor, while the shoulder and hand pieces each cost 8 Marks of Honor. Matching non-set pieces for the waist, wrist, and feet are available from the same vendor for 4 Marks of Honor each, completing the appearance.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily earned by participating in PvP activities such as Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, and Brawls, which can be queued for solo. A complete 8-piece appearance costs a total of 64 Marks of Honor. Earning this will require approximately 30-60 Battleground completions, depending on win/loss rates. This set is purchased directly and does not use a tier token system. All pieces of the appearance are sold by the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor more efficiently, complete the first daily win for each type of PvP queue (Random Battleground, Epic Battleground, weekly Brawl) for bonus rewards. This set's appearance is exclusive to Alliance characters; a Horde character purchasing the gear will unlock the Horde-equivalent "General's" appearance instead. The items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be traded or found on the Auction House. Check current expansion world quests, as they sometimes offer Marks of Honor as a reward.

708.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is acquired by progressing through the Kyrian Covenant system in Shadowlands. The main armor pieces are awarded directly by completing specific chapters of the Kyrian campaign: the Head from Chapter 8, Shoulders from Chapter 9, Chest from Chapter 7, and Legs from Chapter 5. The remaining pieces (Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist) become available for purchase once you reach Renown 26 with the Kyrian. These items are sold by the Renown Quartermaster, Forgelite Neptira, in Elysian Hold for Reservoir Anima.

### Farming Logistics
There is no weekly or daily lockout for this set, as it is tied to quest and reputation progression. The entire acquisition process can be completed solo at the current maximum level by finishing the Kyrian Covenant campaign and earning Renown. Earning all pieces is guaranteed through this progression and should take a few hours of dedicated questing. The set is cosmetic and not tied to a token system, making it available to all armor types once collected. The main armor pieces are rewarded from the campaign, while the minor pieces must be purchased from a vendor.

### Farming Tips
To begin the Kyrian Covenant campaign, you must first complete the main Shadowlands story quests through Oribos on at least one character. Renown, required to unlock the vendor pieces and progress the campaign, can now be rapidly farmed by completing Shadowlands content such as dungeons, raids, or world quests. The required Reservoir Anima is best farmed via world quests and by looting treasures in Shadowlands zones, with Zereth Mortis being a particularly efficient source. You must be a member of the Kyrian Covenant to complete the quests and purchase the items, but once the appearances are collected, they are available account-wide for all classes.

709.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Grand Marshal's Regalia," the replica version of the original level 70 PvP gear, available to players of any PvP rank. It is purchased from Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is Marks of Honor. The total cost for the eight-piece set is 18 Marks of Honor: Head, Shoulders, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist pieces cost 2 Marks each, while the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks each. All pieces are sold by the same vendor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it is purchased with currency. The primary method for obtaining Marks of Honor is by participating in and winning Player vs. Player activities such as Random Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, and Brawls. The set can be farmed entirely solo by queueing for these activities. The number of runs required depends entirely on your win rate, with a single victory typically rewarding one Mark of Honor. This set is purchased directly and does not use a token system.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, as it involves fighting predictable AI opponents and results in quick, consistent wins. You can check the in-game calendar for the Brawl schedule. Otherwise, queueing for Random Battlegrounds during a Battleground Bonus Event will yield the most currency over time. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

710.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Mythic difficulty mail lookalike from the Blackrock Foundry raid in Warlords of Draenor. The pieces are direct drops from specific bosses within the raid, not from tier tokens. Each piece drops from a different boss on Mythic difficulty only.
*   **Head**: Drops from The Iron Maidens.
*   **Shoulder**: Drops from Operator Thogar.
*   **Chest**: Drops from Beastlord Darmac.
*   **Legs**: Drops from The Blast Furnace.
*   **Hands**: Drops from Gruul.
*   **Feet**: Drops from Flamebender Ka'graz.
*   **Waist**: Drops from Hans'gar and Franzok.
*   **Wrist**: Drops from Kromog.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Blackrock Foundry, which has a weekly raid lockout. Mythic difficulty has a single, instance-based lockout, meaning once you kill a boss, you cannot kill it again on that character until the weekly reset, even in a different group. The entire raid is fully soloable by a max-level character. As each piece drops from a different boss, collecting the full 8-piece set will take an average of 4-8 weekly resets, depending on drop luck. The pieces are direct mail armor drops and do not use a tier token system. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts and reward different colorations of the set; this specific appearance is exclusive to Mythic.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Mythic difficulty to obtain this set's appearance. Warlords of Draenor bonus rolls, using the Seal of Tempered Fate, can be used on each boss for an additional chance at loot. After clearing the first wing, an NPC near the entrance can teleport you to unlocked sections to save time on subsequent runs. For a faster descent to the Iron Maidens after Operator Thogar, a Goblin Glider or slow fall effect can be used to jump from the platform. Farming with other mail-wearing classes (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker) allows for loot trading if a player receives an item they already have.

711.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a PvP vendor. Alliance players can buy the "Ensemble: Grand Marshal's Pursuit" from Lieutenant Karter in the Champion's Hall in Old Town, Stormwind City. The complete ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor. Purchasing and using the ensemble item will teach your character the appearances for all eight armor slots: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist. This is a cosmetic replica of the original level 60 Grand Marshal plate armor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily farmed by winning instanced PvP content such as Random Battlegrounds and Brawls, which can be queued for solo. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor needed will take approximately 12 battleground wins, as a victory typically awards one Mark. This is a plate armor set; only Death Knights, Paladins, and Warriors can collect and use this appearance. All pieces are contained within the single ensemble item, so no parts come from a different source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring Marks of Honor is to participate in PvP during the Battleground Bonus Event, which grants extra Marks. Completing the weekly "Observing War" quest by earning honor in Epic Battlegrounds also awards Marks of Honor. The ensemble item is soulbound and cannot be traded or purchased on the Auction House. Horde players cannot purchase this Alliance-specific set but can acquire its counterpart, the "High Warlord's" set, from a vendor in Orgrimmar.

712.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor in Warlords of Draenor. The vendor, Cladd Dawnbringer, is located in Warspear, Ashran, at coordinates (55.4, 35.8). The entire set is bought with Marks of Honor. The main pieces (High Warlord's Headguard, Spaulders, Tunic, Legguards) cost 12 Marks of Honor each. The minor pieces (High Warlord's Gauntlets, Sabatons, Waistguard, Bracers) cost 8 Marks of Honor each, for a total of 80 Marks of Honor for the complete eight-piece set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency (Marks of Honor) can be acquired continuously through PvP activities or other means. The farm can be done solo by queuing for random battlegrounds or by farming certain legacy content. Acquiring the 80 Marks of Honor required for the full set may take between 25 to 50 battleground matches, depending on win/loss rates. This is a direct-purchase set and does not use a tier token system; all pieces are bought from the same vendor. The appearance is distinct from raid difficulty color variants.

### Farming Tips
An efficient alternative to queuing for PvP is farming Legion-era raids on any difficulty, as bosses can drop items containing multiple Marks of Honor. Purchasing any piece of the set on one character will add the appearance to your account-wide Wardrobe for all mail-wearing classes to use. The items themselves are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Before farming, check your Appearances tab, as you may have already collected some pieces if you participated in PvP during Warlords of Draenor.

713.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier lookalike that drops from bosses within the Trial of the Crusader raid, the Tier 9 instance from Wrath of the Lich King. The individual pieces are direct drops from specific encounters. The Helm of the Swift Fangs drops from Anub'arak, and Spaulders of the Snow-Leopard, Leggings of the Tireless Sentry, and Treads of the Ice-Walker all drop from Lord Jaraxxus. The Cuirass of the Post-Apocalypse and Bindings of the Ice-bound Pinion drop from the Faction Champions encounter, while Gloves of the Raging Giant and Belt of the Merciless Killer drop from the Northrend Beasts encounter.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout. The entire instance can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take 3-6 weekly resets. This is a lookalike set composed of direct boss drops and does not use the tier token system; the class-specific Hunter set uses the Protector token (shared with Shaman, Paladin, and Warrior), but those pieces are not required for this appearance. The 10-player and 25-player versions of the raid have separate lockouts, allowing two full clears per character per week, each with a chance at different-colored gear.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal. The Trial of the Crusader instance is a single-room "boss rush" with no trash mobs, making it one of the quickest raids to farm. To begin an encounter, simply speak to the Argent Crusade NPC in the center of the arena. Note that the appearance of the gear from this raid is faction-specific; the blue/gold "Stalker's" appearance drops for Alliance characters on 10-player Normal and for Horde characters on 10-player Heroic. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

714.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Deadly Gladiator's Plate Armor, a Wrath of the Lich King Season 5 PvP reward. The entire ensemble is purchased from Legacy Armor Quartermasters in Dalaran (Northrend). For the Horde, visit Trapjaw Rix in the Underbelly, and for the Alliance, find Kylo Kelwin, also in the Underbelly. Each of the five main set pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands) costs 12 Marks of Honor. Matching non-set pieces, such as the Waist and Feet, are available from the same vendors for the same currency and cost. The items are class-specific, so you must purchase them on a Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously. The currency is obtained through instanced PvP, which can be queued for solo. The full 5-piece set costs 60 Marks of Honor. This is not a token-based set; each class purchases its own specific armor pieces directly, and no tokens are shared between Warriors, Paladins, or Death Knights. All pieces are acquired from the same vendor source, eliminating the need to farm different locations for specific slots.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when the event is active, as this provides quick and consistent wins. When Comp Stomp is unavailable, queue for random battlegrounds, especially during the PvP bonus week for increased rewards. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Ensure you travel to the Dalaran located in Northrend, not the Broken Isles version from the Legion expansion.

715.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Arena Season 2 gear from The Burning Crusade expansion, not Wrath of the Lich King. It is purchased as a full set from a vendor. The vendor is Kezzik the Striker, located in Area 52 in Netherstorm. You must purchase the "Ensemble: Merciless Gladiator's Chain Armor" which costs 12 Marks of Honor. This single item will teach your character the appearances for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, and Waist slots.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously. The entire appearance is taught by a single ensemble, so it is a one-time purchase that can be completed solo. The 12 Marks of Honor required can be obtained in a single day by participating in PvP activities like random battlegrounds, brawls, or epic battlegrounds. This is not a tier token set; the ensemble teaches the appearance directly to all Mail-wearing classes: Hunter, Shaman, and Evoker.

### Farming Tips
Purchase the ensemble on any character, as Marks of Honor are a bind-on-account currency and can be mailed between your characters. The fastest way to farm Marks of Honor is during the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl event, where you face AI opponents for quick, guaranteed wins. When this event is not active, focus on winning your first random battleground and epic battleground of the day for bonus currency. The ensemble is not available on the Auction House, and its appearance cannot be supplemented by other sources.

716.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor. The primary vendor for this set is Blizzik Firewhistle, located in the Underbelly of Dalaran (Northrend). The currency required is Marks of Honor. The five main set pieces (Vengeful Gladiator's Chain Helm, Spaulders, Tunic, Leggings, and Gauntlets) cost 2-3 Marks of Honor each. The matching belt and boots are not part of the core set and must be purchased separately from the same vendor; these are the Vindicator's Chain Girdle and Vindicator's Chain Sabatons. The total cost for all seven pieces is 16 Marks of Honor. Alternative vendors are located in Area 52 in Netherstorm.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously from PvP activities. The entire set can be acquired solo by queueing for random Battlegrounds, Brawls, or Arenas. A full set can be obtained in a single day of focused farming, requiring approximately 6-10 battleground wins. The appearance is for the Mail armor type, usable by Hunters, Shaman, and Evokers; the original items were restricted to Hunter and Shaman. The matching belt and boots are from the previous PvP season but are sold by the same vendor to complete the visual set.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, as it involves fighting predictable AI opponents for quick victories. Winning your first random Battleground of the day also awards a large bonus, accelerating the process. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased on the Auction House. This set is a recolor of the Tier 5 raid armor from Serpentshrine Cavern and The Eye (Tempest Keep), providing a PvE alternative for farming the same model.

717.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Brutal Gladiator (Season 4) PvP armor from The Burning Crusade expansion. It is purchased from legacy PvP vendors Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm or Vixton Pinchwhistle in Gadgetzan, Tanaris. The entire set, including Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, and Feet, can be bought as a single item called "Ensemble: Brutal Gladiator's Chain Armor" for 80 Marks of Honor. Individual pieces can also be purchased for 12 Marks of Honor each. This is a chain set, and the appearance can only be learned and used by Hunter and Shaman characters.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming Marks of Honor; they can be acquired continuously by participating in PvP activities. The farm can be done solo by queuing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, or weekly brawls. To acquire the 80 Marks of Honor needed for the ensemble, a player would need approximately 27 wins in random battlegrounds, which award 3 Marks per win. This set does not use a tier token system. The main armor pieces are all purchased from the same vendor with the same currency.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate during the weekly Battleground Bonus Event, which increases Mark gains. Additionally, certain weekly PvP Brawls, such as "Comp Stomp vs AI," allow for extremely fast and consistent wins, making them the best source when available. Always purchase the Ensemble for 80 Marks of Honor, as buying the pieces individually would cost more. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

718.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from legacy PvP vendors for Marks of Honor. The primary vendor for both factions is Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. Alternatively, players can visit Zom Bocom (Horde) or Kylo Kelwin (Alliance) in the Underbelly of Northrend's Dalaran. The full set costs 12 Marks of Honor. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands pieces cost 2 Marks each, while the Waist and Feet cost 1 Mark each. The Wrist slot is not part of the Deadly Gladiator's set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is purchased with currency, not obtained via drops. The entire set can be acquired solo in a single visit to the vendor once you have the required 12 Marks of Honor. Farming the Marks is done through instanced PvP and can take a few hours of gameplay. This is not a tier set, so there are no class tokens to consider. All listed pieces come from the same vendor source, and there are no different difficulty versions.

### Farming Tips
To farm Marks of Honor efficiently, complete the weekly quests for random battlegrounds and brawls, which provide a significant number of Marks. The single fastest method is to queue for the "Comp Stomp" PvP brawl whenever it is available, as it allows for quick and consistent wins against AI opponents. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased on the Auction House. Ensure you are visiting the original Dalaran in Northrend, not the Broken Isles version from the Legion expansion.

719.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Wrath of the Lich King Season 5 PvP mail armor, purchased from vendors in Dalaran (Northrend). Alliance players can find Arcanist Ivrenne in the Magus Commerce Exchange, while Horde players can find Trapjaw Rix in the Underbelly. The currency required is Marks of Honor, with the full 8-piece set costing 17 Marks. The five main set pieces are Hateful Gladiator's Chain Helm (3 Marks), Spaulders (2 Marks), Armor (3 Marks), Gauntlets (2 Marks), and Leggings (3 Marks). The three matching non-set offset pieces are the Hateful Gladiator's Waistguard of Triumph (1 Mark), Sabatons of Triumph (2 Marks), and Wristguards of Triumph (1 Mark).

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this set, as the Marks of Honor currency can be farmed continuously through PvP activities. The set is acquired entirely solo by purchasing the items from a vendor once the required currency has been obtained. Assuming a player has no Marks of Honor, the full set can be farmed in a single day, often in just a few hours of dedicated play. This set does not use a token system; pieces are purchased directly. This is a mail armor set, and the appearance can be collected and used by both Hunters and Shamans.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, which allows for fast, consistent wins against AI opponents. When this brawl is unavailable, the fastest method is winning Random Battlegrounds, particularly during a Battleground Bonus Event weekend. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Ensure you are visiting the correct "Legacy Armor Quartermaster" vendor, as other nearby vendors sell different PvP seasons.

720.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a Cataclysm PvP vendor for Marks of Honor. Alliance players can find Captain Dirgehammer in Old Town, Stormwind, while Horde players can find Blood Guard Zar'shit in the Valley of Strength, Orgrimmar. The five main pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands) are available together as "Ensemble: Savage Gladiator's Chain Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor. The matching non-set pieces, Savage Gladiator's Waistguard and Savage Gladiator's Sabatons, are sold separately by the same vendor for 1-2 Marks of Honor each. There are no matching bracers that are part of the set's appearance.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as it is a vendor purchase limited only by how quickly you can farm the currency. Marks of Honor are primarily earned from participating in and winning PvP activities like random battlegrounds, and the entire farm can be done solo. Acquiring the 12-16 Marks of Honor needed for the full appearance typically takes between 5 to 10 battleground wins. This set does not use a tier token system; it is a direct purchase for any mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker, Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). All pieces are available from the same vendor, ensuring a consistent farming source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly PvP Brawls, especially fast-paced ones like "Comp Stomp vs. AI" when available. Always purchase the "Ensemble: Savage Gladiator's Chain Armor" for 12 Marks, as it is cheaper than buying the five main armor pieces individually. Queuing with a group can increase your win rate in battlegrounds, accelerating currency gain. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

721.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Wrath of the Lich King Season 7 PvP armor, purchased from vendors in Dalaran (Northrend). Horde players must visit Zom Bocom and Alliance players must visit Kylo Kelwin, both located in the Underbelly sewers of Dalaran. The currency is Marks of Honor. The main set pieces are purchased from the "Legacy Armor Quartermaster" vendor, while the matching non-set belt and boots are purchased from the adjacent "Legacy Justice Quartermaster" vendor.

*   **Head:** Furious Gladiator's Chain Helm (3 Marks of Honor)
*   **Shoulder:** Furious Gladiator's Chain Spaulders (3 Marks of Honor)
*   **Chest:** Furious Gladiator's Chain Armor (3 Marks of Honor)
*   **Hands:** Furious Gladiator's Chain Gauntlets (2 Marks of Honor)
*   **Legs:** Furious Gladiator's Chain Leggings (3 Marks of Honor)
*   **Waist:** Furious Gladiator's Waistguard of Triumph (2 Marks of Honor)
*   **Feet:** Furious Gladiator's Sabatons of Triumph (2 Marks of Honor)

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it is purchased with a currency. The entire set can be obtained solo by farming 18 Marks of Honor. The time required depends entirely on your efficiency at earning Marks, which are primarily rewarded for winning PvP Battlegrounds, Brawls, and Arenas. This is a cosmetic PvP set and does not use the raid tier token system; the appearance is specific to Hunter and Shaman classes, who must purchase the items to learn the transmog. The set has only one appearance and is not affected by difficulty settings like LFR, Normal, or Heroic, as those are raid-specific.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire Marks of Honor is by winning PvP activities, especially during a Battleground-specific holiday event like Arathi Basin Comp Stomp, which allows for fast and consistent victories. Participating in current expansion PvP world quests may also reward Marks of Honor. You must purchase the armor on a character that can equip mail (Hunter or Shaman) to add the appearance to your wardrobe. Although any character can purchase the items, only mail-wearers can learn the appearance upon purchase. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

722.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased as an ensemble from the Battle for Azeroth PvP vendors. For Alliance, visit Captain Dirgehammer at the Saltwater Tavern in Boralus. For Horde, visit Sergeant Severe in the Mugambala in Dazar'alor. The "Ensemble: Relentless Gladiator's Chain Armor" costs 12 Marks of Honor. Purchasing this single item will grant the appearances for all eight armor slots: Head, Shoulders, Chest, Wrists, Hands, Waist, Legs, and Feet.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is farmed by participating in PvP, which can be done entirely solo by queuing for Random Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, or Brawls. Acquiring the required 12 Marks of Honor typically takes 12-20 battleground matches, depending on win rate. This set does not use a class token system. The Elite appearance associated with this set's season is no longer obtainable.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor quickly, participate in the weekly PvP Brawl event when it is active, as the first win often awards multiple Marks of Honor. The set is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Note that the Alliance and Horde versions of this armor have entirely different appearances and color schemes, requiring you to collect them on characters of each faction. While the ensemble can be purchased by any class, the appearances will only unlock for Chain armor wearers: Hunters, Shaman, and Evokers.

723.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cloth Wrathful Gladiator's PvP armor from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion, purchased with Marks of Honor. The most efficient method is to buy the "Ensemble: Wrathful Gladiator's Silk Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor from a Legacy Armor Quartermaster such as Lieutenant Jackspring in Stormwind's Old Town or Galra in Orgrimmar's Hall of Legends. Alternatively, individual pieces can be purchased from legacy vendors in Dalaran (Underbelly) or Gadgetzan. The ensemble includes the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands slots, while the matching Waist and Feet must be purchased separately for 1 Mark of Honor each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as Marks of Honor can be farmed continuously from PvP activities. The entire farm is soloable by queueing for random battlegrounds, and collecting the 12 Marks required for the ensemble takes approximately 10-15 battleground wins. This set does not use a tier token system; purchasing the gear on a Mage, Priest, or Warlock unlocks the appearance account-wide for all other cloth-wearing characters. All pieces are purchased from the same vendors, and there are no raid or dungeon lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor quickly, participate in the weekly PvP brawl or queue for the specific battleground featured in the "Call to Arms" event, as these often provide bonus rewards. Purchasing the ensemble for 12 Marks is cheaper than buying the five main armor pieces individually, which would cost a total of 13 Marks. While queueing solo is effective, joining a premade group for random battlegrounds will increase your win rate and accelerate currency acquisition. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

724.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of replica PvP gear purchased from a vendor. The primary source for Horde players is Kylo Kelwin, located in Orgrimmar's northern gatehouse (63.8, 14.6). For Alliance players, the vendor is Starlight Tressa in Old Town, Stormwind City (76.8, 66.8). The currency required is Marks of Honor. While the prompt names the "Chain Armor" set, there are corresponding Plate, Leather, and Mail versions available, and a character can only purchase the set matching their primary armor type. The most efficient way to purchase the set is to buy the "Ensemble: Bloodthirsty Gladiator's Chain Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor, which instantly teaches the appearances for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as Marks of Honor can be farmed continuously through PvP activities. The entire farm can be completed solo by queuing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, or weekly PvP brawls. Obtaining the 12 Marks of Honor needed for the ensemble can typically be done in a single gameplay session, requiring approximately 4-8 battleground matches depending on win/loss rates. There is no tier token system; pieces are purchased directly. All pieces are from the same vendor and there are no alternate sources or lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
Always purchase the Ensemble for 12 Marks of Honor rather than buying pieces individually, as this is more cost-effective. The fastest method for farming Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP brawl when it is active, as it involves fighting easy AI opponents for quick victories. You must purchase the set on a character that wears Mail armor (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker), though the appearance will become available account-wide for all other Mail-wearing characters once learned. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be sold or traded on the Auction House.

725.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Cataclysm Season 10 PvP armor, purchased for Marks of Honor. The vendors are located on the Serpent's Spine in Pandaria: Armsmaster Holinka for Alliance in Valley of the Four Winds, and Roo Desvin for Horde in Kun-Lai Summit. The main set pieces are Ruthless Gladiator's Plate Helm (2 Marks), Shoulders (2 Marks), Chestguard (3 Marks), Legguards (3 Marks), and Gauntlets (2 Marks). The matching non-set pieces are the Girdle of Unconquered Glory (1 Mark), Sabatons of Unconquered Glory (2 Marks), and Bracers of Unconquered Glory (1 Mark), all purchased from the same vendors. The total cost for the full 8-piece appearance is 16 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming the currency required for this set. Marks of Honor are primarily acquired by participating in and winning PvP activities such as random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and brawls, which can be queued for solo. The time required to collect the 16 Marks of Honor can vary, but generally takes a few hours of consistent PvP participation, depending on win rates and Strongbox rewards. This set does not use a token system; the appearances are automatically shared among all plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) on your account once purchased. All pieces come from the same vendor source.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor efficiently, focus on winning random battlegrounds, especially during a holiday event like the Battleground Bonus Event. Completing the weekly PvP quests, such as "The Arena Calls" or "Something Different," provides a substantial amount of Marks. Additionally, check for active World PvP quests in Dragonflight zones, as the "Compendium of Battle" reward frequently contains Marks of Honor. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

726.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Prideful Gladiator's Leather Armor, a Mists of Pandaria PvP set purchased from a vendor. The original vendors are located on the Serpent's Spine wall: Shonn Su for Horde and Amelia Clarke for Alliance. Modern, more accessible vendors include Seltherex in Valdrakken or legacy vendors in Orgrimmar's Strength and Honor block and Stormwind's Champion's Hall. The set is purchased with Marks of Honor. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands cost 2 Marks of Honor each, while the Waist, Wrist, and Feet cost 1 Mark each, for a total of 13 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. Farming is fully soloable by queueing for PvP content like random battlegrounds, arenas, or brawls. Assuming a 50% win rate in random battlegrounds, collecting the required 13 Marks of Honor should take approximately 3-5 hours of gameplay. This set is purchased directly and does not use a tier token system, so there are no class-sharing restrictions beyond the armor type. All pieces are from the same vendors, and raid/dungeon difficulties are not applicable.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to farm Marks of Honor is to queue for the weekly "PvP Brawl" event, which often provides faster queues and higher rewards per win. Marks of Honor are account-bound, so you can farm them on any character and mail them to the character you wish to purchase the set on. Once purchased, you can learn the appearance by equipping the item, then sell it back to the vendor within the two-hour refund window to get your Marks of Honor back. The items are not Bind on Equip, so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

727.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is purchased from Leeni "Smiley" Smallpockets in Area 52, Netherstorm for the Alliance faction. The vendor requires the player to have achieved an 1800 rating in Rated Battlegrounds on any character on the account to make the items purchasable. The set costs a total of 18 Marks of Honor, a PvP currency. The individual piece costs are: Head (2), Shoulder (2), Chest (3), Legs (3), Hands (2), Feet (2), Waist (2), and Wrist (2). Although available for purchase by any class that meets the rating requirement, this is a cloth armor set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming the required Marks of Honor currency. The main requirement, achieving an 1800 rating in Rated Battlegrounds, must be completed in a coordinated 10-player group and cannot be soloed. Once the rating requirement is met, the 18 Marks of Honor can be farmed by queuing for various PvP activities like random battlegrounds, brawls, and arenas. Marks of Honor are primarily awarded for winning these events and from reward chests. All pieces are from the same vendor and have no separate sources.

### Farming Tips
To acquire the necessary 1800 Rated Battleground rating, join PvP communities or guilds that actively form RBG teams, as this cannot be achieved solo. To farm Marks of Honor efficiently, participate in the weekly Battleground Bonus Event which rewards extra Marks. Once the pieces are purchased, the appearances are permanently added to your account-wide wardrobe for all cloth-wearing characters of the appropriate faction. These items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

728.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from drops within the two Heroic Caverns of Time dungeons from the Burning Crusade. The Head, Chest, and Hands drop in Heroic Old Hillsbrad Foothills, while the Shoulder, Legs, and Feet drop from Heroic The Black Morass. The Waist is crafted by Leatherworkers using Pattern: Champion's Arcanum Belt, a rare drop from Infinite Defilers in the Caverns of Time. The Wrist is a world drop from the same Infinite Defiler mobs located in the main cavern before entering either instance.

-   **Head:** Epoch Hunter (Heroic Old Hillsbrad Foothills)
-   **Shoulder:** Aeonus (Heroic The Black Morass)
-   **Chest:** Captain Skarloc (Heroic Old Hillsbrad Foothills)
-   **Legs:** Temporus (Heroic The Black Morass)
-   **Hands:** Lieutenant Drake (Heroic Old Hillsbrad Foothills)
-   **Feet:** Chrono Lord Deja (Heroic The Black Morass)
-   **Waist:** Crafted (Leatherworking)
-   **Wrist:** World Drop (Infinite Defilers)

### Farming Logistics
The two primary dungeons, Old Hillsbrad Foothills and The Black Morass, are on a daily Heroic lockout, meaning you can clear each one for loot once per day per character. The entire set is easily soloable by any class at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 20%, it will take approximately 5-10 days of clearing both instances to acquire the six dungeon pieces. This set does not use a tier token system; gear drops directly from bosses. The Normal difficulty versions of these dungeons do not drop the set pieces, and there are no LFR, Mythic, or Timewalking variants that award these specific appearances.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeons on Heroic difficulty, as it is the only source for the main set pieces. The crafted Waist and world drop Wrist are Bind on Equip, so check the Auction House to potentially purchase them and save time. The Black Morass dungeon requires defending against 18 waves of mobs, a process that cannot be skipped but is trivial at high levels. Use a character with high mobility to speed up the roleplaying event in Old Hillsbrad Foothills and travel between portal spawns in The Black Morass.

729.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the original level 60 Grand Marshal PvP gear, purchased from the vendor Kezzik the Striker located in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is the Mark of Honor, which is earned from participating in player-versus-player activities. The eight main armor pieces are purchased individually: Head (Grand Marshal's Coronet), Shoulder (Grand Marshal's Epaulets), Chest (Grand Marshal's Archmage's Robes), Legs (Grand Marshal's Legguards), Hands (Grand Marshal's Gloves), Feet (Grand Marshal's Boots), Waist (Grand Marshal's Cord), and Wrist (Grand Marshal's Cuffs). Each piece costs exactly 1 Mark of Honor, for a total of 8 Marks to complete the set.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as its acquisition is tied to currency rather than instance drops. Marks of Honor can be farmed solo by any class through PvP activities like random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, brawls, and skirmishes. A single battleground win can award one or more Marks, meaning the full set can be acquired in under a dozen wins. This set does not use a tier token system; the pieces are purchased directly by Mages, Priests, or Warlocks. The appearance is learned account-wide upon purchase, allowing any cloth-wearing character on the account to use the transmog.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by queuing for random battlegrounds, particularly during the weekly Battleground Bonus Event which increases honor gains and often rewards extra Marks. Completing the weekly PvP quests available in the current expansion's capital city, such as "Observing Battle," also provides a consistent source of Marks. Since the items are purchased directly from a vendor, there are no shortcuts, and the pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

730.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Season 2 Gladiator PvP mail armor, purchased from vendors in Outland or Tanaris. The primary vendor is Kezzik the Striker, located in Area 52, Netherstorm. Each piece is bought with Marks of Honor, with the five main set items costing 12 Marks each. The specific pieces are Warlord's Mail Helm (Head), Warlord's Mail Spaulders (Shoulder), Warlord's Mail Armor (Chest), Warlord's Mail Leggings (Legs), and Warlord's Mail Gauntlets (Hands). Matching non-set pieces, Veteran's Mail Girdle (Waist), Veteran's Mail Sabatons (Feet), and Veteran's Mail Bracers (Wrist), are also sold by the same vendor for 12 Marks each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously through PvP activities. The entire process can be completed solo by queuing for random battlegrounds and brawls. To collect the full 8-piece appearance, a total of 96 Marks of Honor is required, which typically takes between 30-50 battleground wins to acquire from the reward chests. This is not a raid tier set and therefore does not use a token system; items are purchased directly. All pieces are from the same vendor source, and difficulty settings do not apply.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition of Marks of Honor, participate in the weekly PvP Brawls, especially fast-paced ones like "Arathi Blizzard" or "Comp Stomp vs. AI". Monitor the in-game calendar for the Battleground Bonus Event, which significantly increases honor gains and the rate at which you earn reward boxes containing Marks. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. The appearances are added to your collection immediately upon purchase.

733.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is obtained from bosses within the Ulduar raid in The Storm Peaks, Northrend, on 25-player difficulty. The five main armor pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses, while the matching non-tier pieces drop directly. The tokens are redeemed for gear with Paldesse (Alliance) or Magistrix Lambriesse (Horde) in Dalaran.

*   **Head:** Token (Helm of the Wayward Vanquisher) drops from Thorim.
*   **Shoulder:** Token (Spaulders of the Wayward Vanquisher) drops from Hodir.
*   **Chest:** Token (Chestguard of the Wayward Vanquisher) drops from Yogg-Saron.
*   **Legs:** Token (Leggings of the Wayward Vanquisher) drops from Freya.
*   **Hands:** Token (Gauntlets of the Wayward Vanquisher) drops from Mimiron.
*   **Waist:** Sash of Ancient Power drops from General Vezax.
*   **Feet:** Boots of Fiery Resolution drop from Ignis the Furnace Master.
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of Unleashed Magic drop from Yogg-Saron.

### Farming Logistics
Ulduar operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms. The instance is fully soloable at maximum level, with all bosses being trivial encounters. Due to the number of individual pieces and reliance on specific token drops, a player can expect to complete the set in 5-10 weekly clears. The tier tokens used for this set are "Wayward Vanquisher" tokens, which are shared loot for Death Knight, Druid, Mage, and Rogue classes; however, under legacy loot rules, they can drop for any class in the raid. Ulduar's 10-player and 25-player difficulties share a single weekly lockout, so you must run the 25-player version to get the correct items.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player before entering to ensure the correct loot table is active. To reach all required bosses, the entire instance must be cleared up to the final encounter; there are no major skips for the bosses that drop the set pieces. Use the central tram system to quickly travel between the different wings of the raid. The tier tokens themselves do not grant the appearance; you must take them to the legacy armor vendors in Northrend's Dalaran to exchange them for the gear, at which point the appearance is added to your collection. The matching belt, boots, and bracers are Bind on Pickup and cannot be acquired from the Auction House.

734.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Wrathful Gladiator's Mail Armor, a Player vs. Player set from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. All pieces are purchased from a vendor for the Marks of Honor currency. The primary modern vendor is Seltherex, located in the Gladiator's Refuge in Valdrakken. Each main armor piece (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) costs 12 Marks of Honor. A full 8-piece set requires a total of 96 Marks of Honor. Legacy vendors in Dalaran (Northrend) also sell these items.

### Farming Logistics
There is no weekly or daily lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily earned by participating in and winning instanced PvP content like random battlegrounds, arenas, and weekly brawls, all of which can be queued for solo. The time needed to acquire the full set is variable and depends entirely on PvP win rates, but typically takes 20-30 hours of dedicated farming. This set is purchased directly and does not use a class-based token system like raid gear. There are no different difficulty-based versions of this set.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor efficiently, participate in the weekly "Call to Arms" bonus event, which grants extra marks for wins in a specific battleground. The "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl, when active, offers the fastest source of marks due to its quick and reliable wins against AI opponents. Also, complete the weekly PvP quests available in Valdrakken for a consistent, low-effort supply of the currency. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

735.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the replica of the The Burning Crusade Season 2 PvP armor, purchased from a vendor. All pieces are sold by Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is Marks of Honor, earned from participating in various PvP activities. The Head, Shoulder, Hands, Waist, and Feet pieces each cost 2 Marks of Honor. The Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each, and the Wrist costs 1 Mark of Honor. The entire 8-piece set costs a total of 17 Marks of Honor and is available to cloth-wearing classes: Mage, Priest, and Warlock.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is rewarded through instanced PvP content like Battlegrounds and Arenas, which can be queued for solo but are group-based activities. The time required to farm the 17 Marks of Honor for the full set can range from a few hours to several days depending on your win rate and participation. This set is purchased directly and does not use a tier token system. All pieces are sold by the same vendor, and there are no difficulty-based variations like LFR, Normal, or Heroic.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, which pits players against AI opponents for fast and consistent wins. Completing the weekly PvP quests, such as "Observing Battle" in Valdrakken, and achieving the first random battleground win of the day also provide a significant number of Marks. Once purchased, the gear is Bind on Pickup and its appearance is automatically added to your account-wide Wardrobe. These pieces cannot be sold on the Auction House.

736.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a legacy PvP armor set purchased from vendors in Area 52, Netherstorm. The primary vendor is Kezzik the Striker (Alliance) or Leeni "Smiley" Smalls (Horde). The main set pieces are purchased with Marks of Honor: Head, Chest, and Legs cost 2 Marks of Honor each, while the Shoulders and Hands cost 1 Mark of Honor each. Matching non-set pieces for the Waist, Feet, and Wrist slots are also sold by these vendors for 1 Mark of Honor each. The entire appearance can be collected for a total of 10 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously from PvP activities. Marks are primarily earned by participating in instanced PvP such as Battlegrounds, Brawls, and Arenas. The farm can be done entirely solo by queuing for PvP content, and the full set can be obtained in a single day of focused farming. This set does not use a token system; Mages, Priests, and Warlocks purchase their own class-specific versions, but learning one unlocks the appearance for all other cloth-wearing classes on the account. All pieces are from the same source.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by winning battlegrounds, especially during weekly PvP quests like "The Call of Battle" that award extra currency. When the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl is active, it offers an extremely efficient method for farming marks against predictable AI opponents. Marks of Honor are account-bound, so you can farm them on any character and mail them to the character purchasing the set. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

737.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from the Legacy PvP vendor Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is Marks of Honor, which are earned from participating in player-versus-player activities like Battlegrounds. The main set pieces cost 2-3 Marks of Honor each: the Brutal Gladiator's Plate Chestpiece and Legguards cost 3 Marks, while the Helm, Shoulders, and Gauntlets cost 2 Marks. The matching off-set Vindicator's Lamellar Sabatons (Feet) and Belt cost 2 Marks, and the Bracers cost 1 Mark, for a total of 17 Marks of Honor for the full cosmetic set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. Farming is done by queuing for PvP Battlegrounds, which can be done solo but involves group content. The entire set can be acquired in a single day of farming; assuming an average of 2 Marks of Honor per Battleground, collecting the 17 marks needed would take approximately 9-10 completed matches.
