This is not a tier token set, so each class purchases its own armor directly without sharing tokens. All pieces are sold by the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor more quickly, focus on winning Battlegrounds, as a win rewards more marks than a loss. Check the Group Finder for the daily "Call to Arms" Battleground, which often provides bonus rewards upon completion. Participating in weekly PvP Brawls when they are active can also be a lucrative source of Marks of Honor via associated quests. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House.

738.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor. The primary vendor is Zom Bocom, located in the Underbelly of Dalaran (Northrend). The currency required is Marks of Honor. The main set pieces cost 2-3 Marks each: Head and Shoulders cost 2 Marks of Honor, the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor, and the Hands cost 2 Marks of Honor. Matching non-set pieces for the Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots are also available from the same vendor for 1 Mark of Honor each. The entire 8-piece collection costs a total of 15 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout as it is purchased from a vendor. The acquisition is limited only by how quickly you can farm Marks of Honor, which are awarded for participating in and winning PvP activities like Battlegrounds, Arenas, and Brawls. The set can be farmed entirely solo. Assuming a 50% win rate in random battlegrounds, you can expect to earn the required 15 Marks of Honor in approximately 25-30 matches. This is a direct purchase system; there are no raid tokens or class-sharing mechanics for the vendor version. A full lookalike set with the same appearance also drops from 10-player Naxxramas, but this is subject to a weekly raid lockout.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by completing the weekly PvP quests, such as "Observing Battle," which often reward multiple Marks. Winning your first random battleground of the day provides a significant bonus to Honor, which speeds up progress towards the Great Vault PvP track, a passive source of Marks. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. When purchasing the items, ensure you are on a Mail-wearing character (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker) to correctly add the appearance to your collection.

739.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the epic Primal Gladiator's Mail armor, purchased from a Warlords of Draenor PvP vendor. Alliance players can find Amelia Clarke in Stormshield, while Horde players can find Blood Guard Axelash in Warspear. The entire set can be purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Primal Gladiator's Mail Armor," for 12 Marks of Honor. Purchasing pieces individually costs more: Head, Chest, and Legs cost 3 Marks each; Shoulders, Hands, and Feet cost 2 Marks each; and the Waist and Wrist cost 1 Mark each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming the required currency, Marks of Honor. The currency can be farmed entirely solo by queueing for PvP activities like random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and brawls. A random battleground win typically awards one Mark of Honor, meaning the full ensemble can be acquired after approximately 12 wins. There is no tier token system for this set, as all pieces are purchased directly from the vendor for any eligible class. All pieces come from the same source, and there are no different lockouts or difficulties to consider.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by completing the first win of the day in a random battleground or by participating in the weekly PvP brawl when it is active, as these often have quests that reward multiple Marks. The "Comp Stomp" brawl, which pits players against AI opponents, is an especially fast farm when available. It is significantly cheaper to purchase the ensemble for 12 Marks of Honor rather than buying each piece individually. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

740.content:

Horde players can purchase the set from Blood Guard Zar'shi, the Legacy Armor Quartermaster in the Hall of Legends in the Valley of Strength, Orgrimmar. There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as it is purchased with a currency. The currency, Marks of Honor, is primarily obtained by participating in and winning instanced PvP such as Random Battlegrounds, Brawls, and Arenas. The entire farm can be done solo, and the Marks of Honor are Bind-on-Account, so they can be farmed on any character and spent on another. There is no tier token system for this set. The set is purchased directly, and all pieces come from the same vendor source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly PvP Brawls or during weeks with a Battleground bonus event, which often yield currency faster than standard play. Since Marks of Honor are account-bound, you can farm them on your most effective PvP character regardless of their armor type. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House. The appearance is added to your collection immediately upon purchase from the vendor.

741.content:

The total cost for the five main pieces is 13 Marks of Honor: Head (3), Shoulder (2), Chest (3), Legs (3), and Hands (2). There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency is obtained through repeatable PvP content. The acquisition is entirely soloable by queueing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, or weekly brawls at max level. The time required to collect the set depends entirely on PvP participation and win rates; however, obtaining the necessary 13 Marks of Honor can typically be done in a single session of 2-4 hours. Marks of Honor are an account-wide currency, so they can be farmed on any character and spent on the character you wish to unlock the appearance for. The vendor sells distinct versions for Mages, Priests, and Warlocks, so you must purchase the items on the correct class to add them to your wardrobe.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition of Marks of Honor, participate in the "Something Different" weekly PvP brawl whenever it is active, as these often have faster queues and quicker match completion times. 743.content:

744.content:

745.content:

746.content:

748.content:

757.content:

The five main pieces are purchased from a Legacy Armor Quartermaster, such as Blazik Fireclaw in Gadgetzan, Tanaris, using Marks of Honor. The "Hateful Gladiator's Scaled" set consists of the Helm, Shoulders, Chestpiece, Legguards, and Gauntlets. 762.content:

764.content:

This specific blue and gold elite recolor is no longer obtainable in World of Warcraft. It was the reward for achieving a 2200 rating in Rated Battlegrounds or Arena during Cataclysm PvP Season 10. After the season concluded, the set was permanently retired as a reward and has not been available since. The standard, red-colored version of the "Ruthless Gladiator's Scaled Armor" can be purchased for Marks of Honor. The vendor for this non-elite set is Armorer Furos in Old Town, Stormwind or Blazik Fireclaw in the Valley of Strength, Orgrimmar, with the full ensemble costing 12 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There are no farming logistics for this elite set as it is not available from any repeatable source. It is not tied to a raid lockout, dungeon reset, or any specific drop chance from an enemy. The acquisition method was a time-limited Feat of Strength achievement earned during a specific PvP season. Consequently, there is no number of runs to estimate, no bosses to kill, and no class-specific tokens involved in its acquisition. The appearance is not available through any modern game systems like the Great Vault or bonus rolls.

### Farming Tips
As the elite set cannot be farmed, players seeking similar models should focus on obtainable recolors. The fastest alternative is to purchase the non-elite red version from the legacy PvP vendors for Marks of Honor. For different color schemes using the same armor model, players should farm the Dragon Soul raid instance for the Tier 13 "Colossal Dragonplate Battlegear." Dragon Soul offers separate weekly lockouts for LFR (green), Normal (purple), and Heroic (red-orange) difficulties, each providing a unique tint of this armor design.
