### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor efficiently, participate in the weekly PvP Brawls, as they often have faster queue times and completion rates. The "Arathi Basin Comp Stomp" event is a particularly fast source of marks due to the high win chance against AI opponents. Check for PvP-flagged World Quests in Dragonflight zones, as they can also reward Marks of Honor. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. To reach the vendor, take the portal to Shattrath from the Stormwind mage tower and fly north.

766.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired by collecting tier tokens from two Burning Crusade raids: Hyjal Summit and Black Temple. The Head token drops from Archimonde and the Hands token drops from Azgalor, both in Hyjal Summit. The Shoulder token drops from Mother Shahraz, the Legs token drops from The Illidari Council, and the Chest token drops from Illidan Stormrage, all in Black Temple. Matching non-set pieces also drop in these raids: the Waist (Belt of One-Hundred Deaths) drops from Illidan Stormrage, the Feet (Shadowmaster's Boots) drop from Kaz'rogal in Hyjal Summit, and the Wrist (Vambraces of Ending) drop from Teron Gorefiend in Black Temple.

### Farming Logistics
Hyjal Summit and Black Temple have separate weekly raid lockouts per character. Both raids can be fully soloed at max level by any class. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per piece, collecting the five main tier tokens typically takes 4-7 runs on average. The tier tokens are "of the Forgotten Vanquisher" and are shared by Rogue, Druid, and Mage; however, the appearance can be collected by any leather-wearing class. The non-set matching pieces drop directly from bosses and do not use a token system. These raids only have a single 25-player difficulty, so there are no LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic variants to track.

### Farming Tips
For Hyjal Summit, speak to the faction leaders at each camp to skip the lengthy trash waves and summon the bosses directly. In Black Temple, there are no significant skips, and you must clear bosses in a specific order to reach Illidan. The tier tokens themselves do not grant the appearance; you must right-click the token in your inventory to create the armor piece, which is then added to your collection. None of the set pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they cannot be found on the Auction House. Use a high-mobility class like a Demon Hunter or Druid to reduce travel time within the instances.

768.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor in Area 52, Netherstorm. Horde players purchase the "Ensemble: Warlord's Raiment" from Oluros, while Alliance players purchase it from Leeni "Smiley" Smalls. The entire set costs 12 Marks of Honor and is sold as a single ensemble item that contains all eight pieces. The ensemble includes the following slots: Warlord's Silk Cowl (Head), Warlord's Silk Amice (Shoulder), Warlod's Silk Raiment (Chest), Warlord's Silk Trousers (Legs), Warlord's Silk Handguards (Hands), Warlord's Silk Footguards (Feet), Warlord's Silk Belt (Waist), and Warlord's Silk Cuffs (Wrist). No pieces are obtained from any other source.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency is acquired through repeatable PvP content. The required Marks of Honor are earned by winning Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, Arenas, and certain weekly Brawls. Farming is entirely soloable by queueing for these activities. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor for the full set can take approximately 4-12 battleground wins, which can be achieved in a single day of focused play. This set is a direct currency purchase and does not use a tier token system.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by participating in the weekly PvP Brawls, such as "Comp Stomp" or "Arathi Blizzard," which often have faster queue times and completion rates. Completing the weekly PvP quests, like "Observing War," also provides a consistent source of Marks. The ensemble and the Marks of Honor currency are Bind-on-Account and cannot be traded or sold on the Auction House. While any class can purchase the ensemble, the appearances can only be learned and used for transmog by cloth-wearing classes: Mage, Priest, and Warlock.

770.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the level 60 Horde PvP armor, available for purchase from Leeka Smazz, the Legacy Armor Quartermaster in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency used is Marks of Honor. The full set can be bought as an ensemble for 12 Marks of Honor, which is the most efficient method. To purchase the set, a player must have earned a rating of 1800 in Rated Battlegrounds on at least one character on their account. The set consists of Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, and Feet, but does not include a matching Waist or Wrist piece.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously through PvP activities. The process is entirely soloable by queuing for Random Battlegrounds, Arenas, or Brawls. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor for the full ensemble can be done in a single day, typically requiring 4-6 battleground wins. This is not a token-based set; the appearance is learned directly upon purchase and is available account-wide to all characters of the appropriate armor type (Plate, Mail, Leather, or Cloth).

### Farming Tips
The primary requirement is achieving an 1800 rating in Rated Battlegrounds; players should focus on this prerequisite before farming Marks of Honor. The weekly PvP Brawl quest, when available, rewards 5 Marks of Honor for a single win and is the most time-efficient source. This set is faction-specific; only Horde characters can purchase and learn the "High Warlord's" appearances. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

771.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted using the Northrend Tailoring profession, and all patterns are learned directly from any Northrend Tailoring trainer. The primary reagent for all pieces is Moonshroud, a specialized cloth created by tailors on a multi-day cooldown from Imbued Frostweave. The Chest and Legs also require one Frozen Orb each, which can be purchased from vendors in Dalaran (Northrend). All eight main slots—Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist—are created through this crafting process.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no instance lockout; the primary limiting factor is the 3-day, 20-hour character-specific crafting cooldown on Moonshroud. The entire process of gathering materials and crafting is fully soloable. Crafting the 40 Moonshroud required for the full set on a single character would take several months. Since this is not a tier set, there are no raid tokens or class restrictions to consider. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE) and can be acquired from other players, which is the most common method of completion.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to purchase the finished pieces directly from the Auction House, as the crafting process is lengthy. To speed up self-crafting, use multiple characters with Northrend Tailoring to produce Moonshroud simultaneously, bypassing the individual cooldown. If farming the base materials, the best source for Frostweave Cloth is the trash mobs in the Icecrown Citadel raid instance. Set the raid to 25-player Heroic difficulty to maximize the number of enemies in the initial areas before the first boss.

772.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of Season 7 PvP gear and is purchased from a vendor in Northrend Dalaran. Horde players buy it from Herwin Steampop and Alliance players from Kylo Kelwin; both are located in the Underbelly. The currency is Marks of Honor, obtained from participating in PvP battlegrounds and brawls. The specific pieces and their costs are: Replica Relentless Gladiator's Satin Hood (3 Marks), Replica Relentless Gladiator's Satin Mantle (2 Marks), Replica Relentless Gladiator's Satin Tunic (3 Marks), Replica Relentless Gladiator's Satin Leggings (3 Marks), Replica Relentless Gladiator's Satin Gloves (2 Marks), Replica Relentless Gladiator's Cord of Dominance (1 Mark), and Replica Relentless Gladiator's Treads of Dominance (1 Mark). The full set costs 15 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously from PvP content. The currency can be farmed solo by queueing for Battlegrounds, although the content itself is group-based. The time required to collect the 15 Marks of Honor depends entirely on PvP win rates but can typically be completed in a few hours. This is a cosmetic cloth set shared by Mage, Priest, and Warlock. All pieces are purchased directly from the same vendor with the same currency.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to complete the quest associated with the weekly rotating PvP Brawl. When the "Comp Stomp" brawl is active, marks can be farmed quickly and reliably against AI opponents. Ensure you are in the Northrend version of Dalaran, accessible via the main portal rooms, as the vendors are not present in the Legion version of the city. The purchased replica items are soulbound and cannot be found on the Auction House.

773.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the unranked Merciless Gladiator's gear from Shadowlands Season 3, purchased from a vendor. The vendor is Zo'sorg, located in The Enclave in Oribos (coordinates 35.8, 57.6). The currency required is Honor, which is earned through any form of PvP combat. The Head, Chest, and Legs cost 875 Honor each; the Shoulders, Hands, and Feet cost 700 Honor each; and the Waist and Wrist cost 525 Honor each. This "Satin" version is the cloth set intended for Mages, Priests, and Warlocks.

### Farming Logistics
There is no daily or weekly lockout associated with farming Honor currency. The entire set can be farmed solo by queuing for PvP activities such as Random Battlegrounds, Skirmishes, or Brawls. The total cost for all eight main armor pieces is 5,775 Honor. Assuming an average of 400-500 Honor per battleground win, a player can expect to complete the set in 12-15 wins. All pieces are purchased directly, so there is no tier token system or shared loot with other classes.

### Farming Tips
To accelerate Honor gain, focus on the daily first win bonuses for Random Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, and Arena Skirmishes. Completing the weekly PvP quests available in Oribos also provides a significant amount of Honor. The weekly PvP Brawl event, when active, often provides one of the fastest Honor-per-hour farming methods. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

774.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of gear from The Burning Crusade's Season 3, purchased from a vendor. All pieces are sold by Blazik Fireclaw in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency used is the Mark of Honor, earned from participating in PvP activities. The main 5-piece set can be bought as "Ensemble: Vengeful Gladiator's Satin Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor. Individual pieces can also be purchased: Head and Shoulders cost 2 Marks each; Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks each; Hands cost 2 Marks. The matching non-set pieces are the Waist, Feet, and Wrist, which cost 1 Mark of Honor each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the Mark of Honor currency can be acquired continuously. The entire process is soloable by queuing for various PvP instances like battlegrounds, arenas, or brawls. Collecting the full 8-piece set requires a total of 15 Marks of Honor. This armor set does not use a token system. Although the items can be purchased by any class, the appearances can only be learned and transmogged by cloth wearers: Mages, Priests, and Warlocks. All pieces are obtained from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly-rotating PvP Brawl, which often has faster match times and objectives. When the "Comp Stomp" brawl against AI opponents is active, it provides the fastest and most reliable source of Marks. Purchasing the ensemble for 12 Marks is cheaper than buying the five corresponding pieces individually for 12 Marks. The matching belt, boots, and bracers must always be purchased separately and are not included in the ensemble.

775.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the Season 4 PvP armor from The Burning Crusade and is purchased from a vendor. All pieces are sold by Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is the Mark of Honor, obtained from participating in PvP activities. The full set costs a total of 17 Marks of Honor: Head (2), Shoulders (3), Chest (3), Legs (3), Hands (2), Feet (2), and Waist (2). There are no wrist pieces that are part of this specific visual set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it is based on currency acquisition. The currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed entirely solo by queuing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and PvP brawls. The time it takes to collect the required 17 Marks of Honor is dependent on win rates and queue times, but can typically be done in one or two play sessions. This set is not based on a tier token system; each piece is purchased directly by any of the eligible plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). All pieces are from the same vendor and source.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by winning PvP matches, particularly during the weekly "Call to Arms" bonus event for random battlegrounds. When the "Comp Stomp" PvP brawl is active, it offers an exceptionally fast and reliable method for farming Marks, as it pits players against AI opponents. The items purchased are cosmetic replicas, are Bind-on-Pickup, and cannot be traded or sold on the Auction House. The appearance is added to your Wardrobe immediately upon purchase, so there is no need to equip the item.

779.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set is a complete ensemble purchased from a PvP vendor. The "Ensemble: Furious Gladiator's Satin Armor" is sold by Zo'sorg, the PvP vendor located in The Enclave in Oribos at coordinates (35.6, 57.6). The ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor. Purchasing this single item grants all eight pieces of the set: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist. No individual pieces are sold separately from this Shadowlands vendor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency can be obtained continuously. Marks of Honor are primarily acquired from participating in and winning instanced PvP content such as Random Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, and Arenas. While PvP is group content, Marks can also be obtained through soloable activities like PvP World Quests in Battle for Azeroth and Legion. Obtaining the required 12 Marks of Honor may take between 4 to 12 battleground wins, depending on daily win bonuses. All pieces are obtained from the single ensemble, so there are no differing sources or tier tokens to consider.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to farm Marks of Honor is by completing the weekly "Call to Arms" battleground quests, which award additional Marks upon completion. If you prefer solo farming, check Battle for Azeroth zones for PvP World Quests that reward Marks of Honor, which can be completed quickly on a max-level character. Purchasing the ensemble item will automatically add all eight appearances to your character's wardrobe. The ensemble and the appearances it grants are Bind-on-Account.

780.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Wrath of the Lich King PvP Season 7 armor, purchased from a vendor. The full set is sold by Zom Bocom in the Underbelly of Dalaran (Northrend). The currency required is Marks of Honor. The individual pieces cost: Head (2), Shoulder (2), Chest (3), Legs (3), Hands (2), Feet (2), Waist (2), and Wrist (1). The total cost to acquire all eight pieces of the "Relentless Gladiator's Satin Armor" is 17 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for purchasing this set, as it is bought directly from a vendor. The acquisition is limited only by the amount of Marks of Honor you can farm. Marks of Honor are primarily earned through group-based instanced PvP like Battlegrounds and Arenas, though some PvP World Quests and Brawls can be completed solo. The entire set can be farmed in a single day of focused PvP. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces are bought directly for the cloth armor type. All pieces are sold by the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
Participate in the "Battleground Bonus Event" when it is active to earn extra Marks of Honor from objectives and victories. The weekly PvP Brawl, particularly "Comp Stomp vs. AI", often provides the fastest and most consistent source of Marks due to high win rates and quick match times. The items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. Ensure you are in Dalaran located in Northrend, not the Broken Isles, to find the correct legacy vendor.

781.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the Season 8 PvP armor and is purchased for Marks of Honor. The vendor is Leeni "Smiley" Smalls, located in Gadgetzan, Tanaris at coordinates (52.6, 27.6). The full ensemble requires a total of 13 Marks of Honor. The individual pieces are the Replica Wrathful Gladiator's Leather Helm (3 Marks), Replica Wrathful Gladiator's Leather Spaulders (2 Marks), Replica Wrathful Gladiator's Leather Tunic (3 Marks), Replica Wrathful Gladiator's Leather Legguards (3 Marks), and Replica Wrathful Gladiator's Leather Gloves (2 Marks). The feet, waist, and wrist pieces are not sold by this vendor and must be sourced from other sets.

### Farming Logistics
The currency required, Mark of Honor, has no lockout and can be farmed continuously. The farm is fully soloable by queueing for instanced PvP activities such as Random Battlegrounds, Arenas, and Brawls. Marks of Honor are typically rewarded upon winning a match, with a small chance to be found in the reward lockboxes from losing. Assuming a 50% win rate in Random Battlegrounds where a win awards one Mark, it would take approximately 26 matches to acquire the 13 Marks needed. This set is purchased directly and does not use a class-based token system.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to win Random Battlegrounds or participate in the weekly "Brawl" event when available. Check for the weekly PvP quests in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Battle," which often reward additional Marks of Honor for completing objectives. Winning your first Random Epic Battleground of the day also awards a bonus Mark. These pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased on the Auction House.

782.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a PvP vendor for the Mists of Pandaria Season 13. The Alliance vendor is Starlight, Armsmaster of the Alliance, located on the Serpent's Spine wall in the Valley of the Four Winds (coordinates 89.8, 16.8). The Horde vendor is Roo Desvin, located on the Serpent's Spine wall in Kun-Lai Summit (coordinates 55.0, 93.6). The entire 8-piece set (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) is sold as a single item called "Ensemble: Vicious Gladiator's Satin Armor". This ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously through PvP activities. The entire process can be completed solo by queuing for random battlegrounds, arenas, or PvP brawls. Since the set is purchased directly for a fixed currency cost, there is no drop chance RNG; acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor required typically takes 1-3 hours of focused gameplay. This is a mail armor set, and purchasing the ensemble on a Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker will unlock the appearance for all of them. All eight armor pieces are obtained from the single ensemble item.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, as this game mode pits players against AI and results in fast, consistent wins. When this brawl is unavailable, queueing for random battlegrounds during a Battleground Bonus Event will increase honor gains, which in turn awards Marks of Honor. The ensemble item is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House. To learn the appearances, you must purchase and use the ensemble on a mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker).

783.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Cataclysm PvP Season 10 armor, purchased from a vendor. It is not found in Shadowlands content. The complete eight-piece set can be purchased as an ensemble for 12 Marks of Honor, or individual pieces can be bought for 2-3 Marks of Honor each. For the Alliance, visit Captain Dirgehammer in the Champion's Hall in Stormwind City. For the Horde, visit Sergeant Thunderhorn in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar. All main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are available from these vendors.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the Marks of Honor currency can be earned continuously. Marks are primarily awarded by participating in and winning Player vs. Player battlegrounds, arenas, and brawls, which can be queued for solo or with a group. A full set costs 12 Marks of Honor via the ensemble, which would require approximately 6-12 battleground wins to acquire. This is not a tier token set; the Satin appearance is directly purchasable by Mage, Priest, and Warlock characters. All pieces are from the same vendor source, and difficulty modes are not applicable.

### Farming Tips
Purchase the "Ensemble: Ruthless Gladiator's Satin Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor, as this is cheaper than buying the eight pieces individually for a total of 18 Marks. The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is during the weekly Battleground Bonus Event, which rewards extra marks upon completion of battlegrounds. Additionally, the weekly "Observing Battle" PvP quests in Valdrakken provide a reliable source of Marks. The ensemble and individual pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased on the Auction House.

784.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Elite recolor of the Cataclysm Season 10 PvP armor. It was not obtained from its original expansion. The full set was purchased as "Ensemble: Ruthless Gladiator's Satin Armor" from the vendor Zo'kuul in The Enclave in Oribos. This purchase required a special token earned by reaching an 1800+ rating in any rated PvP bracket during Shadowlands PvP Season 3 or Season 4. As these seasons have concluded, the token and the ensemble are no longer obtainable.

### Farming Logistics
The acquisition of this set was tied to a seasonal PvP reward, not a farmable drop, so it had no lockout. Obtaining it required group-based rated PvP (2v2 Arena, 3v3 Arena, or Rated Battlegrounds) and could not be done solo. The "farm" consisted of winning enough rated matches to achieve the 1800 rating threshold, which granted the single token needed to purchase the entire ensemble. The ensemble unlocked the appearances for all three cloth classes that could wear it: Mage, Priest, and Warlock. No pieces came from a different source.

### Farming Tips
This set is currently unobtainable, but when it was available, the fastest method was to achieve the 1800 rating requirement in the 2v2 or 3v3 arena brackets. This required finding consistent partners through the in-game group finder or with friends. Upon purchase, the ensemble item was consumed and immediately taught all pieces of the appearance set to the Wardrobe for all eligible classes account-wide. There is no alternative way to acquire this specific elite recolor at this time.

785.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the replica of the level 60 Alliance PvP plate armor. It is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor, Leeni "Smiley" Smalls, who is located in Area 52 in Netherstorm at coordinates (32.8, 64.4). The currency required is Marks of Honor, which are obtained by participating in PvP battlegrounds and arenas. The costs are as follows: Lieutenant Commander's Lamellar Headguard (2 Marks), Lieutenant Commander's Lamellar Shoulders (2 Marks), Lieutenant Commander's Lamellar Chestpiece (3 Marks), Lieutenant Commander's Lamellar Legguards (3 Marks), Lieutenant Commander's Lamellar Gauntlets (2 Marks), and Lieutenant Commander's Lamellar Sabatons (2 Marks). This set does not include matching waist or wrist pieces from this vendor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as it is purchased with a currency that can be earned continuously. The entire process of earning Marks of Honor and purchasing the set can be completed solo by queueing for PvP activities. The full 6-piece set costs a total of 14 Marks of Honor, which would require approximately 14 battleground wins to acquire. This is not a tier set and does not use a token system, so there are no class-sharing considerations. All pieces are purchased directly from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by completing the weekly PvP brawl quest, "Something Different," which typically awards 5 Marks of Honor for a single victory. When the "Comp Stomp" brawl is active, it provides the most efficient farm, as it involves a quick PvE encounter against AI opponents. These armor pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House. No legacy rank or rating is required to purchase this replica version from the vendor.

786.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Horde version of the Season 2 "Merciless Gladiator" PvP plate armor, purchased from a vendor in Netherstorm. Travel to Area 52 and find the vendor Vixton Pinchwhistle inside the southern building at coordinates (32.8, 64.0). The set is sold under the "Legacy Armor" tab and requires the currency Mark of Honor, which is earned from participating in player-vs-player activities. The main pieces cost between 2 and 3 Marks of Honor each: the Head, Shoulders, Hands, and Feet cost 2 Marks, while the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks. While the "Champion's Guard" set is specific to Warriors, equivalent Merciless Gladiator sets for all other classes and armor types are available from the same vendor.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as the pieces are purchased with Marks of Honor farmed on-demand from PvP. The entire set can be acquired solo by queuing for random or epic battlegrounds, and a player with enough currency can purchase all pieces instantly. Assuming a 50% win rate in random battlegrounds where wins reward one Mark of Honor, it would take approximately 12 wins to earn the 12 Marks needed for the six core armor pieces. This is a vendor purchase, so there is no tier token system or differing loot sources for set pieces. The set appearances are not tied to any difficulty level like LFR, Normal, or Heroic.

### Farming Tips
The fastest method for acquiring Marks of Honor is to consistently win PvP matches, so joining a pre-made group for battlegrounds can improve your win rate. Check the in-game calendar for weekly PvP brawls, as some offer quicker matches and faster rewards than standard battlegrounds. Current expansion world quests that reward Marks of Honor are also an excellent, non-instanced source of the currency. The vendor sells the entire set, so there is no randomness involved, and the pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

787.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the original level 60 Grand Marshal PvP gear, purchased from vendors in Area 52, Netherstorm. Alliance players can buy the full set as "Ensemble: Field Marshal's Vestments" for 12 Marks of Honor from Leeni "Smiley" Smalls. The main pieces (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet) can also be bought individually from her. The matching Replica Field Marshal's Girdle (Waist) and Replica Field Marshal's Bracers (Wrist) are sold by the adjacent vendor, Trixie Majoria, for 1 Mark of Honor each, but are included if you purchase the ensemble. The currency, Marks of Honor, is awarded for participating in and winning various PvP activities.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set; Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously from PvP queues. The farm can be done entirely solo by queuing for battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and brawls. Obtaining the 12 Marks of Honor required for the full ensemble typically takes 2-4 hours of active PvP, with the speed depending on match length and win rate. This set is a direct purchase and does not use a tier token system, so there are no class-sharing restrictions beyond the plate armor type. A critical requirement is that a character on your account must have earned the legacy PvP rank of Knight-Captain or higher from the original pre-TBC honor system.

### Farming Tips
The fastest method for farming Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP brawl when it is active, as it provides quick, consistent wins against AI opponents. Additionally, look for the weekly quest "Observing War" in Valdrakken, which rewards Marks of Honor for earning honor in PvP. The items themselves are cosmetic and are not available on the Auction House. Before traveling to the vendor, verify that you have the required legacy Feat of Strength achievement on your account, as you cannot purchase or learn the appearance without it.

788.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Mythic difficulty Cloth armor from the Castle Nathria raid, the first tier of the Shadowlands expansion. The five core set pieces drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Warlord's Soul-Stitched Handguards drop from Artificer Xy'mox, the Warlord's Soul-Torn Leggings drop from the Council of Blood, the Warlord's Soul-Piercing Tunic drops from Sludgefist, the Warlord's Soul-Sheening Mantle drops from the Stone Legion Generals, and the Warlord's Soul-Glimmering Hood drops from the final boss, Sire Denathrius. The matching Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces do not share the "Warlord's" name and drop from Sun King's Salvation, Shriekwing, and Huntsman Altimor, respectively.

### Farming Logistics
Castle Nathria has a weekly raid lockout, and the Mythic difficulty version has a single, fixed ID that is shared across all characters on your account for the week. While many bosses can be soloed at current maximum level, encounters like Sludgefist and Stone Legion Generals have mechanics that require multiple players, making a group of 2-5 players necessary for a full clear. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the five main pieces will likely take 6-10 weekly resets. Shadowlands raids did not use a tier token system; loot drops directly for a specific armor type, so you will be competing with other cloth-wearers. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts but award different colorations of the set; this specific appearance is exclusive to Mythic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Forming a group in the custom Group Finder is the most effective method for this farm, as key bosses cannot be reliably soloed. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. There are no bonus rolls or other legacy systems like the Great Vault that can be used to acquire these appearances retroactively. After killing Shriekwing, you can use the teleporter in the antechamber to skip directly to the second wing of the raid. To save time, a high-mobility class can pull Sludgefist and run back to the entrance pillar to avoid the charge mechanic, though this may require practice.

790.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a PvP vendor, Kezzik the Striker, located in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is Marks of Honor, obtained from participating in PvP activities. The entire set costs a total of 16 Marks of Honor. The specific costs are: Head (High Warlord's Coronet) for 2 Marks, Shoulder (High Warlord's Epaulets) for 2 Marks, Chest (High Warlord's Robe) for 3 Marks, Legs (High Warlord's Leggings) for 3 Marks, Hands (High Warlord's Gloves) for 2 Marks, Feet (High Warlord's Slippers) for 2 Marks, Waist (High Warlord's Cord) for 1 Mark, and Wrist (High Warlord's Bracers) for 1 Mark.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is earned through group-based PvP such as Battlegrounds, Brawls, and Arenas, so the farm cannot be completed solo. A victory in a random battleground typically awards between one and three Marks of Honor, meaning the full set can be acquired after approximately 6 to 16 wins. This set does not use a token-sharing system; all pieces are purchased directly from the vendor. All pieces of the set are obtained from the same source.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor more quickly, participate in PvP during the weekly Battleground Bonus Event, which rewards extra marks for victories. Completing PvP World Quests can also award a Compendium of Battlegrounds, which contains Marks of Honor. These items are Bind on Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. The original level 60 PvP rank requirement to purchase this gear was removed in a previous expansion, and there are no prerequisites to buy it.

791.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a Player versus PvP vendor using Marks of Honor. Alliance players can buy the set from Arcanist Uovril in The Silver Enclave in Dalaran (Northrend), while Horde players can purchase it from Magistrix Lambriesse in Sunreaver's Sanctuary. The set consists of five pieces: Head and Shoulders cost 2 Marks of Honor each, Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each, and Hands cost 2 Marks of Honor. There are no matching Waist, Feet, or Wrist items with the "Opportunist's" name.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the Marks of Honor currency can be obtained continuously. The currency is primarily earned by participating in and winning PvP Battlegrounds, Brawls, and Arenas, which can be done solo or in a group. The full five-piece set costs a total of 12 Marks of Honor. As this is a vendor set, it does not use a tier token system and there are no raid or dungeon lockouts to consider. Marks of Honor can also be acquired from PvP-related World Quests in current content when they are available.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly "Call to Arms" Battleground bonus event, which rewards extra currency. Additionally, the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl, when active, offers a very fast and reliable way to farm Marks by repeatedly defeating AI opponents. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The set can be purchased by any class, but the appearance will only be added to the wardrobe for Mage, Priest, and Warlock characters.

792.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Wrathful Gladiator's Leather Armor, the PvP armor from Wrath of the Lich King Season 8. The five main set pieces (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, Legs) are purchased from Legacy Armor Quartermasters in Dalaran (Northrend). For the Alliance, visit Herwin Steampop in the Silver Enclave; for the Horde, find Xazi Smolderpipe in Sunreaver's Sanctuary. The currency is Marks of Honor: the Chest and Legs each cost 3 Marks, while the Head, Shoulders, and Hands each cost 2 Marks. The matching non-set belt and boots are purchased for gold from the Legacy Justice Quartermaster located nearby in the same city zones.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily earned by participating in and winning PvP activities like Battlegrounds, Arenas, and Brawls, all of which can be queued for solo. The full 5-piece set costs a total of 12 Marks of Honor, which can be farmed in a single day. This is a direct currency purchase and does not use a token system. The main set pieces are from the PvP Armor vendor, while the matching belt and boots are from the separate Justice Quartermaster vendor.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire Marks of Honor is to participate in PvP during the weekly Battleground Bonus Event, which increases honor gains that contribute to Mark acquisition. Additionally, weekly PvP quests in the current expansion's capital city often reward Marks of Honor. Ensure you are in Dalaran located in Northrend, not the Broken Isles version, to find the correct legacy vendors. While the individual pieces are class-specific to Druids and Rogues, collecting the appearance on one of those classes will unlock it for all leather-wearing classes on your account (Monk, Demon Hunter).

793.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased as an "Ensemble: Merciless Gladiator's Leather Armor" from a vendor in Legion Dalaran (Broken Isles). For Horde players, the vendor is Apothecary Lee in Windrunner's Sanctuary. For Alliance players, the vendor is Lieutenant Surtees in Greyfang Enclave. The set is not obtained from individual boss drops but is bought as a single item that unlocks the appearances for all 8 armor slots. The purchase price for the complete ensemble is 12 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
The currency, Marks of Honor, has no weekly or daily lockout and can be farmed continuously through PvP activities. The farm is fully soloable at max level by queuing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, arenas, or brawls. Collecting the 12 marks required can take a few hours, as they are primarily rewarded from strongboxes for winning matches. This set does not use a tier token system; the ensemble teaches the appearance to all eligible leather-wearing classes on your account (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter).

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor faster, participate in the "Call to Arms" weekly bonus event for specific battlegrounds, which increases honor gains and reward frequency. Winning battlegrounds provides the most consistent source, so consider grouping with other players to improve your win rate. You can also obtain Marks of Honor from PvP world quests in Legion, Battle for Azeroth, and Dragonflight zones. Since the ensemble is account-bound, you can farm the currency on any character, purchase the item, and use it to unlock the appearance account-wide.

794.content:
### How to Obtain
The ensemble for this set is purchased from a PvP vendor in Outland. The vendor is Kezzik the Striker, located in Area 52 in the Netherstorm zone. The complete set is sold as a single item, "Ensemble: Vengeful Gladiator's Leather Armor," which costs 12 Marks of Honor. This ensemble grants the appearances for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, and Waist items.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily earned by participating in PvP activities such as random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and arenas, which can be queued for solo. Acquiring the required 12 Marks of Honor can take between 4 battleground wins and 12 losses, as wins typically award more marks than losses. This set is not obtained via a token system and does not drop from raids or dungeons, so class-based token sharing is not a factor.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor efficiently, complete the weekly PvP quests in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Battle" and "Sparks of Life," which provide a substantial amount of the currency. Participating in the weekly "Battleground Bonus Event" when it is active will also accelerate farming by rewarding extra Marks of Honor upon completion of battlegrounds. The ensemble and the Marks of Honor are soulbound and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Ensure you are purchasing the "Leather Armor" version of the ensemble, as plate, mail, and cloth versions are also available.

795.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of a legacy PvP armor set, purchased from a vendor in Shadowlands. The vendor is Leatrix, located in The Enclave in Oribos. The entire set is purchased using the currency Marks of Honor, which are earned from participating in various Player vs. Player activities. The individual pieces are sold as cosmetic items: the Head, Shoulders, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrists each cost 2 Marks of Honor, while the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each. The full set costs a total of 18 Marks of Honor if purchased piece by piece.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency is obtained from repeatable PvP content. The primary source, random battlegrounds, can be queued for continuously and can be farmed entirely solo. Assuming a 50% win rate, it will take approximately 15-20 completed battlegrounds to acquire the necessary Marks of Honor, as wins award one Mark directly. This set is a direct purchase and does not use a tier token system, so no other classes compete for the same items. The items are cosmetic replicas and have no connection to raid or dungeon difficulties.

### Farming Tips
For efficiency, purchase the "Ensemble: Brutal Gladiator's Felweave Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor, which is cheaper than buying the pieces individually and instantly teaches all appearances. Farming during the weekly Battleground Bonus Event significantly speeds up acquisition by rewarding extra Honor, which can be exchanged for reward boxes containing Marks of Honor. Marks of Honor are account-bound, allowing you to farm them on any character and send them to the character you wish to buy the set on. Joining pre-made battleground groups can increase your win rate, leading to faster currency gain.

796.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a legacy PvP armor set from Wrath of the Lich King, purchased with Marks of Honor. The primary vendor for both factions is Zom Bocom in the Underbelly of Dalaran (Northrend), who sells the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Hands, and Legs. The matching non-set pieces (Waist, Feet, and Wrist) are sold by the adjacent vendor, Herwin Steampop. The Head, Chest, and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each; the Shoulders and Hands cost 2 Marks of Honor each; and the Waist, Feet, and Wrist cost 1 Mark of Honor each, for a total of 16 Marks of Honor for the full 8-piece appearance.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is purchased with currency, not obtained from drops. The entire farm is soloable and depends on acquiring Marks of Honor through PvP activities. The 16 Marks of Honor required can be obtained in a single day by completing PvP quests and winning battlegrounds. This set does not use a tier token system. The main set pieces and the offset pieces are from two different, but adjacent, vendors in the same location. Purchasing an item adds its appearance to the wardrobe for all leather-wearing classes on your account: Druid, Rogue, Monk, and Demon Hunter.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring Marks of Honor is to complete the weekly PvP quests, such as "Something Different" for PvP Brawls, which awards 5 Marks of Honor. Participating in the daily "Call to Arms" battleground provides bonus honor and a chance at a victory satchel containing Marks of Honor. Epic Battlegrounds like Alterac Valley and Wintergrasp can also be a reliable source. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Since you only need to purchase each piece once, you can buy it on any character capable of equipping leather to unlock the appearance account-wide.

797.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Season 5 Arena PvP armor from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. It is purchased from Trapjaw Rix in the Underbelly of Dalaran (Northrend) for Marks of Honor. The Head, Chest, and Legs each cost 3 Marks of Honor. The Shoulders and Hands each cost 2 Marks of Honor. The matching non-set Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces are also available from the same vendor for 1 Mark of Honor each. The entire 8-piece collection costs a total of 17 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The farm is entirely solo, as players can queue for PvP Battlegrounds and Arenas alone. Acquiring the 17 Marks of Honor required for the full set can be achieved in a single day of focused PvP, typically requiring 12-17 Battleground wins. This leather set can be purchased and its appearance learned by Rogues, Druids, Monks, and Demon Hunters. All pieces are from the same vendor and use the same currency.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by participating in the weekly PvP Brawl "Comp Stomp" when it is active, as it provides fast, consistent wins against AI opponents. Completing the weekly "Observing Battle" quests in Valdrakken provides a large quantity of Marks of Honor. Winning your first random battleground of the day also awards a bonus. The set pieces are bind-on-pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House.

798.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Cataclysm Season 9 PvP armor, purchased as a cosmetic ensemble. Horde players can buy the "Ensemble: Savage Gladiator's Leather Armor" from Armorer Leth'var in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar. Alliance players can purchase the same ensemble from Captain Dirgehammer in the Champion's Hall in Stormwind. The full ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor. Individual pieces are not sold separately for transmog purposes; you must purchase the entire set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is purchased from a vendor with currency. The currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed entirely solo by participating in PvP activities like random battlegrounds. The entire ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor, which can be acquired in a few hours of focused farming, depending on your win rate. Marks of Honor are an account-bound currency, so you can farm them on any character and use them to purchase the ensemble on your desired leather-wearing character (Druid, Rogue, Monk, Demon Hunter).

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by winning random battlegrounds, especially during the weekly Battleground Bonus Event which rewards extra Marks. Check for weekly PvP quests in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Battle," which often reward a coffer containing multiple Marks of Honor. This set is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Remember to also check your mission tables from previous expansions like Battle for Azeroth and Shadowlands, as they can sometimes offer missions that reward Marks of Honor.

799.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Season 7 PvP leather armor from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. It is purchased from a Legacy Armor Quartermaster for Marks of Honor. These vendors are located in Orgrimmar (Hall of Legends) for the Horde and Stormwind City (Champion's Hall) for the Alliance. The entire set can be purchased as an "Ensemble: Furious Gladiator's Leather Armor" for 80 Marks of Honor. Individual pieces for the head, shoulder, chest, hands, and legs slots can also be purchased for 12 Marks of Honor each, though the ensemble is more cost-effective.

### Farming Logistics
This set is for leather-wearing classes: Druid, Rogue, Monk, and Demon Hunter. There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency (Marks of Honor) can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be obtained instantly by a solo player, provided they have the required 80 Marks of Honor. All pieces are from the same vendor source, and there is no tier token system or raid difficulty lockout to consider. The time required to collect the set is dependent entirely on the player's speed at acquiring Marks of Honor.

### Farming Tips
Purchase the "Ensemble: Furious Gladiator's Leather Armor" for 80 Marks of Honor, as this is cheaper than buying the main five pieces individually. The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by winning Random Battlegrounds or Epic Battlegrounds. Participating in the weekly PvP Brawl quest when available also provides a significant number of Marks. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

800.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Wrath of the Lich King Season 7 PvP armor, purchased from vendors in Dalaran (Northrend) using Marks of Honor. Horde players must visit Trapjaw Rix in the Underbelly, while Alliance players can find Blazik Fireclaw in the same area. The Head, Shoulder, and Hands pieces each cost 2 Marks of Honor, and the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each. The matching belt and feet are sold separately by the same vendors for 2 Marks of Honor each. The exact matching wrists ("Armbands of the Wary") are not sold by the vendor and instead drop from the boss Koralon the Flame Watcher in the 10-player version of the Vault of Archavon raid in Wintergrasp.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming the main armor pieces, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously from PvP activities. The bracers, however, are on a weekly raid lockout from the Vault of Archavon. The raid can be fully soloed at maximum level. Assuming consistent wins in PvP, acquiring the 16 Marks of Honor needed for the vendor set can be done in a single play session. The 10-player and 25-player versions of Vault of Archavon have separate lockouts, allowing two attempts per week for a wrist item, though the specified lookalike drops in the 10-player version. This set does not use a class-based tier token system.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is by winning Random Battlegrounds or participating in the weekly PvP brawl event, especially "Comp Stomp" when it is available. For the wrists, set your raid difficulty to 10-Player Normal before entering the Vault of Archavon. The instance is a single room, so you can go directly to Koralon the Flame Watcher if he is the active boss for the week. All pieces for this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

801.content:
### How to Obtain
This is a replica PvP set from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion, purchased from vendors using Marks of Honor. Alliance players can buy the set from Kylo Kelwin in Dalaran (Northrend) at coordinates (36.8, 54.0), while Horde players purchase it from Blazik Fireclaw at coordinates (63.0, 46.2). The eight-piece ensemble costs a total of 17 Marks of Honor. The Head, Shoulders, Hands, Waist, and Feet pieces each cost 2 Marks of Honor; the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor; and the Wrists cost 1 Mark of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is purchased with a currency farmed from PvP. Marks of Honor are primarily acquired from winning player-vs-player battlegrounds, brawls, and arenas, which can be queued for solo. Obtaining the full set requires 17 Marks of Honor, which would take approximately 10-17 battleground wins to acquire. The original items were class-specific for Druids and Rogues, but any leather-wearing class (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) can purchase and learn the replica appearance. All pieces, including the "offset" non-set items for Waist, Feet, and Wrist, are sold by the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP brawl whenever it is active, as it offers fast and guaranteed wins against AI opponents. Players should also complete the weekly PvP quests, like "Observing Battle," which award additional Marks of Honor. The replica items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. Ensure you are visiting the Northrend version of Dalaran, accessible via the portal rooms in Stormwind or Orgrimmar, as the Legion version of the city does not have these vendors.

803.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor version purchased from legacy PvP vendors, as the classes listed cannot equip leather. The full set is sold by Apothecary Furrows in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar for Horde players, or Captain Dirgehammer in the Champion's Hall in Stormwind City for Alliance players. All pieces are purchased with Mark of Honor. The chest and legs each cost 3 Marks of Honor, the head, shoulders, hands, feet, and waist each cost 2 Marks of Honor, and the wrists cost 1 Mark of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously from PvP content. The entire collection can be farmed solo by queuing for battlegrounds, arenas, or brawls. A total of 17 Marks of Honor are needed to purchase the complete eight-piece set. This is a vendor set, so there is no token-sharing system; all pieces are bought directly. Note that only the standard version of this set is available, as the original Elite version from Cataclysm Season 10 is no longer obtainable.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to farm Marks of Honor is by consistently winning Random Battlegrounds and completing the weekly "Observing War" PvP quests available in Valdrakken. Participating in the active weekly PvP Brawl is also an efficient source of the currency. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. Ensure you are speaking to the correct "Legacy Armor" or "Legacy Weapons" vendor in the capital cities to find Cataclysm-era gear.

804.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Elite PvP armor from Cataclysm Season 10 and is no longer obtainable. It was originally awarded to players who achieved a 2200 rating in Arenas or Rated Battlegrounds during that specific season. After reaching the rating requirement, pieces could be purchased from PvP vendors in Stormwind or Orgrimmar. The ensemble included the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots. This specific recolor was exclusive to that season's high-rated players and has not been made available again through any other source.

### Farming Logistics
The acquisition of this set was not based on a farmable currency or drop chance, but on personal rating achieved during a limited-time PvP season. It required a dedicated group for 2v2, 3v3, 5v5 arenas or 10-person Rated Battlegrounds. There was no lockout system; players could queue for rated PvP continuously. As the method of acquisition has been permanently retired from the game, there are no logistics for farming it today. The set is not tied to a token system, as it was purchased directly by eligible leather-wearing classes.

### Farming Tips
There are no tips or shortcuts to obtain this specific Elite set, as the means to earn it were removed at the end of Cataclysm Season 10. However, the non-elite version, "Ruthless Gladiator's Leather Armor," which shares the same model with a different color scheme, is still available. This replica set can be purchased for 12 Marks of Honor from vendors located on the Serpent's Spine wall in Kun-Lai Summit, Pandaria: Armsmaster Holinka (Alliance) or Roo Desin (Horde). No pieces of the Elite set are Bind on Equip or available on the Auction House.

805.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic appearance of the unrated Shadowlands Season 3/4 mail PvP gear. The full appearance is purchased as a single item, the "Ensemble: Cosmic Gladiator's Mail Armor", which costs 12 Marks of Honor. It is sold by Zo'sorg, the Legacy PvP Vendor located in The Enclave in Oribos at coordinates 35.6, 57.4. Purchasing and using the ensemble will grant the appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Hands, Waist, Legs, Feet, and Wrist slots. The cloak is a separate purchase, "Cosmic Gladiator's Drape," also from Zo'sorg for 12 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously. The entire farm is soloable by queuing for PvP instances such as random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, arenas, or PvP brawls. Since the set is purchased directly via an ensemble, there is no random drop chance for individual pieces. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor for the main armor ensemble typically requires between 4 to 12 battleground victories. This is not a tier set, so there is no token system.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in PvP during the weekly "Battleground Bonus Event," which increases honor gains and thus the rate at which you acquire Marks. When the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl is active, it provides the fastest and most predictable source of Marks of Honor due to its quick matches against AI opponents. The ensemble and the Marks of Honor are soulbound and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Ensure you are purchasing the "Cosmic Gladiator's Mail Armor" ensemble, as the vendor also sells separate ensembles for cloth, leather, and plate armor types.

806.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Blackrock Foundry raid in Warlords of Draenor. The primary pieces are acquired via tier tokens, while lookalike pieces for the remaining slots drop directly from bosses. The Head token drops from Blackhand, the Shoulder token drops from The Iron Maidens, the Chest token drops from Flamebender Ka'graz, the Legs token drops from The Blast Furnace, and the Hands token drops from Operator Thogar. Lookalike Feet (Flamewrought Cinderboots) drop from Flamebender Ka'graz, lookalike Waist (Belt of Misconceived Loyalty) drops from Oregorger, and lookalike Wrist (Furnace-Tender's Bracers) drops from Gruul.

### Farming Logistics
This set is subject to a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have their own separate lockout and reward a different color variation of the set. The entire instance can be easily soloed at current maximum level. The primary set pieces use the "Protector" tier token, which is shared by Hunters, Shamans, Monks, and Warriors. Assuming an average drop rate, it may take 5-10 clears to collect a full set on one difficulty, though this can vary significantly with luck.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on Normal or Heroic difficulty, as Mythic has some mechanics that can slow down a solo player. Tier tokens are automatically added to your transmog collection upon being looted, so you do not need to turn them in at a vendor. To reach the final boss, Blackhand, you must defeat the three main wing bosses: The Blast Furnace, The Iron Maidens, and Operator Thogar. The lookalike off-set pieces (Waist, Feet, Wrist) are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

809.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Trial of the Crusader 10-player Normal raid, which is the Tier 9 raid instance from Wrath of the Lich King. The five main set pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands) are purchased from Aylara inside the Crusader's Coliseum for one Trophy of the Crusade each, a currency which drops from Anub'arak. The tokens to create these items, called Regalia of the Grand Vanquisher, also drop from the Argent Crusade Tribute Chest after defeating Anub'arak. The matching non-tier pieces drop from specific bosses within the same raid: Bejeweled Wizard's Bracers (Wrist) from The Twin Val'kyr, and both Belt of the Tenebrous Mist (Waist) and Sandals of the Mourning Widow (Feet) from Anub'arak.

### Farming Logistics
The raid has a weekly lockout, but the 10-player and 25-player versions share a lockout, while Normal and Heroic difficulties are separate. You can run 10-player Normal and 10-player Heroic in the same week, but not 10-player Normal and 25-player Normal. The instance is fully soloable at max level. The tier token, Regalia of the Grand Vanquisher, is shared between Mage, Priest, and Warlock characters. Given that multiple Trophies of the Crusade drop from the final boss, it is possible to acquire the full five-piece set in a single run.

### Farming Tips
Run the instance on 10-player Normal difficulty, as this is the only source for this specific blue-quality set. The raid is linear and very short with no available skips; you must complete all encounters. Tier tokens from this expansion are not automatically added to your wardrobe and must be taken to the vendor, Aylara (Horde), at the instance entrance to be exchanged for the actual armor piece. The non-tier lookalike pieces are not BoE and cannot be purchased on the Auction House.

810.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic Warlords of Draenor Season 1 PvP gear, purchased as a single item. The "Ensemble: Primal Gladiator's Ringmail Armor" is sold by PvP vendors in Ashran for 12 Marks of Honor. For the Alliance, visit Amelia Clarke in Stormshield at coordinates (72, 32). For the Horde, visit Ingrid Blackingot in Warspear at coordinates (55, 34). Purchasing and using the ensemble instantly teaches all eight appearances (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) for your character's Wardrobe.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is purchased with a currency that can be farmed continuously. The entire farm can be completed solo. Acquiring the required 12 Marks of Honor can typically be done in a single play session, taking anywhere from 3 to 12 random battleground matches depending on win/loss rate. This set does not use a token system and is purchased directly. The ensemble provides all pieces from a single source, and difficulty settings are not applicable to this PvP acquisition method.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by winning Random Battlegrounds or participating in the weekly PvP Brawl, especially "Comp Stomp" which offers fast, guaranteed wins against AI opponents. Check your capital city for the weekly "Observing War" quests which also reward Marks of Honor for participating in battlegrounds. The ensemble itself is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Using the ensemble item automatically adds all associated appearances to your collection.

811.content:
### How to Obtain
This is a replica PvP armor set from The Burning Crusade expansion, available to Hunter and Shaman characters. The full 8-piece set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor for the Mark of Honor currency. Horde players can purchase the set from Apothecary Lee, while Alliance players can purchase it from Arcanist Rocolon, both located in the Underbelly of Dalaran (Broken Isles). The five main set pieces are the Merciless Gladiator's Ringmail Helm, Spaulders, Tunic, Leggings, and Gauntlets. The three matching non-set pieces are the Vindicator's Ringmail Belt, Boots, and Bracers, which are sold by the same vendor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. Marks are rewarded from participating in PvP activities, and players can queue solo for Random Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, and Arena Skirmishes to earn them. A total of 96 Marks of Honor is required to purchase all eight pieces, as each piece costs 12 Marks. The time required to farm the set depends entirely on your PvP win rate, with wins rewarding more Marks than losses. The weekly PvP quests in Valdrakken also provide a reliable source of Marks.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition of Marks of Honor, participate in the "Comp Stomp" Battleground Brawl whenever it is active, as it allows for quick and consistent wins against AI opponents. Your first win in a Random Battleground and a Random Epic Battleground each day awards a significant bonus to Marks of Honor earned. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Ensure you are purchasing the "Merciless Gladiator" items, as the vendor also sells the identically shaped but differently colored Season 1 "Gladiator's" set.

812.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a cosmetic replica of the The Burning Crusade PvP Season 3 armor, available to Mail-wearing classes. The five main pieces are sold by Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is Marks of Honor. Each of the five purchasable pieces—Replica Vengeful Gladiator's Ringmail Helm, Spaulders, Tunic, Leggings, and Gauntlets—costs 12 Marks of Honor. The total cost for the set is 60 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously through PvP. The currency is primarily earned by participating in random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and arenas, all of which can be queued for solo. Assuming an average of 2 Marks of Honor per battleground, collecting the required 60 Marks will take approximately 30 battleground completions. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are purchased directly from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor efficiently, prioritize winning the first random battleground and epic battleground of the day for bonus rewards. Participate in the weekly PvP Brawl events when available, as they often offer faster matches and rewards than standard battlegrounds. Purchasing the five main armor pieces (Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands) will also automatically grant the appearances for the matching Waist, Wrist, and Feet items. While any class can purchase these replica pieces, only Hunters and Shamans can use them for transmog.

813.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the replica of the Brutal Gladiator's PvP armor from Arena Season 4. It is purchased from legacy PvP vendors using Marks of Honor as currency. The vendors are Leeni "Smiley" Smalls in Gadgetzan, Tanaris or Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. The Head, Shoulders, Hands, Waist, and Feet cost 2 Marks of Honor each, while the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each. The complete 7-piece set costs a total of 17 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency is acquired through repeatable PvP content. Marks of Honor are primarily earned by participating in and winning Battlegrounds, Arenas, and Brawls, which can be queued for solo or in a group. The time to collect the set depends entirely on PvP win rates; it takes approximately 9-17 random battleground wins to acquire the necessary currency. This set is purchased directly and does not use a tier token system. All pieces are available from the same vendors.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly "Call to Arms" PvP quests, which reward extra currency upon completion. When the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl is active, it offers a fast and reliable farming method against AI opponents. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Once purchased, the appearance is permanently added to the account-wide wardrobe for all plate-wearing classes to use.

814.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Arena Season 5 armor from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The entire set is purchased from a PvP vendor using the Mark of Honor currency. Alliance players can find the vendor Kylo Kelwin in the Dalaran Underbelly (Northrend), while Horde players can visit Zom Bocom in the same location. The main armor pieces are purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Deadly Gladiator's Ringmail Armor," for 12 Marks of Honor. This ensemble unlocks the appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands slots. The matching Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces must be purchased individually from the same vendor for 1 Mark of Honor each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be earned continuously. The currency can be farmed entirely solo by queuing for various PvP activities like random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and brawls. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor needed for the main ensemble can typically be done in one to three hours of focused play. This set does not use a tier token system; it is a direct purchase for Mail armor classes (Hunter and Shaman). All pieces come from the same vendor, with no parts dropping from alternate sources.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly rotating PvP brawls, particularly "Comp Stomp," which offers fast and consistent wins against AI opponents. Check the in-game calendar to see when specific brawls are active. Completing the weekly PvP quests in Valdrakken, which require earning honor, also provides a significant amount of Marks of Honor. The set ensemble and individual pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

815.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor. The primary vendor is Blazik Fireclaw, located in the Underbelly of Dalaran (Northrend). The set is classified as "Hateful Gladiator's Mail Armor" and is purchased with Marks of Honor. The total cost for all eight pieces is 15 Marks of Honor. The Head, Shoulders, and Hands cost 2 Marks each; the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks each; and the Waist, Wrist, and Feet cost 1 Mark each.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously. Acquiring the currency is a solo activity that involves queuing for PvP battlegrounds or completing specific PvP world quests from recent expansions. The time required to collect the full set depends entirely on the speed of currency acquisition, typically taking a few hours of dedicated PvP. No tier token system is used for this set, as each piece is purchased directly from the vendor. All pieces come from this single source and do not have different appearances based on difficulty.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by completing the weekly PvP quests in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Battle," which award several Marks upon completion. Winning a random battleground also guarantees one Mark of Honor. You must purchase the set on a Mail-wearing character (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker) to unlock the appearance in your wardrobe. The pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House.

816.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the unrated mail PvP armor from Shadowlands Season 1 and is purchased from a vendor. The full appearance is acquired by buying the "Ensemble: Savage Gladiator's Ringmail Armor" from Purveyor Zo'kuul in The Enclave in Oribos. The ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor and instantly teaches the appearances for all eight armor slots: Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist. Individual pieces can also be purchased from the same vendor, with major slots costing 2 Marks of Honor and minor slots costing 1 Mark of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be earned continuously. The currency is farmed solo by participating in PvP activities such as random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and brawls. Obtaining the 12 Marks of Honor required for the full set ensemble can take approximately 4-8 hours of PvP, depending on queue times and win rates. This set does not use a tier token system. The appearance is distinct from the rated PvP set of the same season, which has a different coloration and is purchased separately.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, which provides consistent and rapid wins against AI opponents. Marks of Honor are Bind-on-Account, allowing you to farm them on any character and send them to the character making the purchase. Although any character can purchase the ensemble, it is recommended to buy it on a mail-wearing class (Hunter or Shaman) to ensure the appearances are correctly added to your collection. The set pieces are not Bind-on-Equip and cannot be found on the Auction House.

817.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from PvP vendors using Marks of Honor. The most efficient method is to buy the "Ensemble: Furious Gladiator's Ringmail Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor from Apothecary Lee (Horde) or Sergeant Major Clate (Alliance) in Dalaran (Broken Isles). This ensemble unlocks the appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Hands, and Legs slots. The matching off-set pieces are purchased separately from vendors in Dalaran (Northrend): Zom Bocom (Horde) or Herwin Steampop (Alliance). The Furious Gladiator's Waistguard of Salvation (Waist) and Bracers of Salvation (Wrist) each cost 1 Mark of Honor, while the Furious Gladiator's Sabatons of Salvation (Feet) cost 2 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously from PvP activities. The currency can be farmed entirely solo by queuing for Random Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, and weekly Brawls. The full set costs a total of 16 Marks of Honor, which can typically be farmed in a single day, requiring approximately 5-10 battleground wins. This set does not use a tier token system and is purchased directly. The main set is acquired via an ensemble in Broken Isles Dalaran, while the off-set pieces must be purchased individually from vendors in Northrend Dalaran.

### Farming Tips
To speed up the farm, focus on winning PvP matches and completing the weekly battleground quests like "Observing Battle," which award bonus Marks of Honor. Always purchase the ensemble first, as it grants the five main set pieces for a discounted price compared to buying them individually. These items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Purchasing either the ensemble or the individual pieces will automatically add the appearances to your account-wide Wardrobe for all mail-wearing characters.

818.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Relentless Gladiator mail armor from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion, purchased from a Legacy Armor Quartermaster. For Alliance, the vendor is Arcanist Ivrenne in the Silver Enclave of Dalaran (Northrend). For Horde, the vendor is Magistrix Lambriesse in Sunreaver's Sanctuary in Dalaran (Northrend). All pieces are purchased with Marks of Honor. The main set pieces are Relentless Gladiator's Ringmail Helm (3 Marks), Spaulders (2 Marks), Armor (3 Marks), Leggings (3 Marks), and Gauntlets (2 Marks). The matching non-set pieces are Relentless Gladiator's Waistguard of Triumph (1 Mark), Sabatons of Triumph (2 Marks), and Armbands of Triumph (1 Mark).

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. Farming the currency requires group PvP activities like Battlegrounds or Arenas, but the purchase itself is a solo activity. The full 8-piece appearance costs a total of 17 Marks of Honor, which would require approximately 10-20 battleground wins to accumulate. This is not a token-based set; pieces are purchased directly. The "Ringmail" set is for Mail armor classes: Hunter and Shaman. A character must be able to equip Mail armor to purchase and learn the appearance.

### Farming Tips
The most consistent method for acquiring Marks of Honor is by participating in and winning Random Battlegrounds. The weekly PvP quests, such as "Observing War" in Valdrakken, also provide a steady source of Marks. Since Marks of Honor are Bind-on-Account, you can farm them on any character and mail them to your desired Hunter or Shaman to purchase the set. The set pieces themselves are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

819.content:

The time required to collect the 15 Marks for the full set depends entirely on PvP queue times and win rates. All pieces are from the same vendor and use the same currency.

Certain weekly PvP Brawls, when active, can also offer a fast way to acquire marks. Keep an eye on World Quests in the current expansion zones, as some may offer Marks of Honor as a direct reward. 820.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a PvP vendor using Marks of Honor. The five main set pieces (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands) cost a total of 14 Marks of Honor. The non-set matching pieces for the Waist, Feet, and Wrist slots are also sold by the same vendor for 2 Marks of Honor each, bringing the total cost for the complete 8-piece appearance to 20 Marks of Honor.
