There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the Marks of Honor currency can be acquired continuously. The farm is fully soloable through participation in player-vs-player activities. The time required to collect the full set depends entirely on PvP win rates, but a player can acquire the necessary 20 Marks of Honor in a single day of focused gameplay. This set does not use a token system; the gear is purchased directly, and the appearance is learned upon purchase. All pieces come from the same vendor, and raid or dungeon difficulties are not applicable to this PvP set.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for acquiring Marks of Honor is to participate in and win Random Battlegrounds or Brawls, particularly during the weekly bonus events for those activities which grant additional currency. Pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House. Crates awarded for winning battlegrounds, such as the Steel Strongbox, have a small chance to contain Marks of Honor, supplementing what you earn directly. Additionally, keep an eye on Mists of Pandaria Timewalking events, as the event vendor often sells a bag containing Marks of Honor in exchange for Timewarped Badges.

821.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic appearance of a previous season's PvP gear and is purchased from a vendor. The complete 8-piece set, covering Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist slots, is sold as an ensemble. The vendor is Zo'sorg, located in The Enclave in Oribos. The ensemble is named "Ensemble: Ruthless Gladiator's Ringmail Armor" and costs 12 Marks of Honor. Individual pieces are not sold separately.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency can be obtained continuously. Marks of Honor are awarded from participating in and winning various Player vs. Player activities, and the farm can be completed entirely solo. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor required for the ensemble can take a few hours of dedicated PvP. This set is not obtained via a token system and has no connection to raid or dungeon lockouts. The appearance is singular and not tied to different difficulties.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly rotating PvP brawls, which often have quests that reward the currency. Winning random battlegrounds also provides a reliable source of Marks. The ensemble is Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Upon purchasing and using the ensemble item, all eight appearances are added directly to your wardrobe collection.

822.content:
### How to Obtain
This specific elite armor set is not obtainable through any in-game activity in Shadowlands or any other expansion. The "Ruthless Gladiator's Ringmail Armor (Elite Recolor)" was the reward for achieving a 2200 rating in Rated Arenas or Rated Battlegrounds during Cataclysm PvP Season 10. While Shadowlands introduced vendors that sold cosmetic weapon ensembles from past elite sets, this system never included the corresponding armor appearances. As a result, there are no bosses, vendors, or crafting patterns in Shadowlands that can be used to acquire this set.

### Farming Logistics
The set is not farmable, and therefore has no associated lockout type. Its acquisition was permanently tied to a time-sensitive achievement during its relevant PvP season and it was not made available again. The original requirement was to form a highly-skilled group for 2v2/3v3 Arenas or Rated Battlegrounds and maintain a high win rate to reach the 2200 rating threshold. This set did not use a tier token system; eligibility to purchase the pieces was granted directly upon reaching the required rating.

### Farming Tips
As the elite version is unobtainable, the best alternative is to collect the non-elite "Ruthless Gladiator's Ringmail Armor" set, which uses the same 3D models with a different color scheme. This set can be purchased for a total of 12 Marks of Honor from specific legacy vendors. Alliance players can find Captain Dirgehammer in the Old Town of Stormwind, while Horde players can visit Blood Guard Zar'shi in Orgrimmar's Valley of Strength. The appearances are purchased as a complete cosmetic ensemble called "Ensemble: Ruthless Gladiator's Ringmail Armor."

823.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor. The full six-piece set is sold by Leeni "Smiley" Smallfish in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is Marks of Honor, which are earned from participating in PvP activities like battlegrounds and arenas. The total cost for the six main pieces is 14 Marks of Honor: Head (2), Shoulders (2), Chest (3), Legs (3), Hands (2), and Feet (2). This set does not include a matching Waist or Wrist piece from the vendor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is purchased with currency. The farm is entirely soloable and depends on acquiring Marks of Honor, which are account-bound. A single character can earn all the Marks needed. Assuming an average of one Mark awarded per battleground win, collecting the full set requires approximately 10-15 battleground completions. This is a direct purchase set and does not use a token-sharing system. The appearance is not tied to a specific difficulty, and all pieces come from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor quickly, complete the weekly PvP quests that reward them, such as "Observing Battle." The fastest method is often participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, as it provides rapid wins against AI opponents. While this is the Alliance version of the set, purchasing the Horde equivalent ("Champion's Dreadgear") on a Horde character will unlock this appearance for Alliance characters on the same account. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

824.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the Season 2 Gladiator PvP armor, purchased from Kezzik the Striker in Area 52, Netherstorm. The currency required is the Mark of Honor, obtained from participating in PvP activities. The five main armor pieces are Replica Grand Marshal's Dreadgear (Head, 2 Marks), Replica Grand Marshal's Lamellar (Shoulders, 2 Marks), Replica Grand Marshal's Dragonhide Tunic (Chest, 3 Marks), Replica Grand Marshal's Dragonhide Legguards (Legs, 3 Marks), and Replica Grand Marshal's Dragonhide Gloves (Hands, 2 Marks). The vendor does not sell matching Feet, Waist, or Wrist pieces for this specific replica set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the Marks of Honor currency can be earned continuously. The purchase is a solo activity, but earning the currency requires participating in group PvP battlegrounds or arenas. The full 5-piece set costs a total of 12 Marks of Honor. This set is not obtained via a token system and all pieces are purchased directly from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by winning Player vs. Player battlegrounds, particularly during a holiday event like the Battleground Bonus Event or when the "Comp Stomp" brawl against AI is active. Instead of buying pieces individually, you can purchase the "Ensemble: Champion's Dreadgear" for 12 Marks of Honor, which instantly adds the entire 5-piece appearance to your collection. All purchased pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or sold on the Auction House.

825.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the original level 60 PvP gear and is purchased from a vendor. The complete 8-piece set can be bought from Leeni "Smiley" Smalls at Area 52 in Netherstorm for Alliance players, or from Kezzik the Striker at the same location for Horde players. The currency used for purchase is the Mark of Honor, which is earned from participating in player-versus-player activities. The entire set costs a total of 18 Marks of Honor: Head, Shoulders, Hands, Waist, Feet, and Wrists each cost 2 Marks, while the Chest and Legs each cost 3 Marks.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency can be acquired continuously. Marks of Honor are primarily obtained from strongboxes awarded for winning Random Battlegrounds, Epic Battlegrounds, and Arenas. The farm is done solo by queuing for PvP content, and the time required depends entirely on your win rate, as a victory is usually required to earn a strongbox containing the Marks. There is no tier token system for this set; all pieces are purchased directly with the same currency from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire Marks of Honor is by participating in special event battlegrounds like Korrak's Revenge (during the WoW Anniversary event) or by completing the weekly PvP quests offered in the current capital city. Queuing for Random Battlegrounds is the standard, consistent method outside of these events. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Unlike some other replica PvP sets, there is no legacy PvP rank requirement to purchase or transmogrify the Field Marshal's Threads.

826.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of the original level 60 PvP gear, purchased from vendors in Area 52, Netherstorm. Horde players speak to Leeni "Smiley" Smalls, while Alliance players speak to Kezzik the Striker. The currency is Mark of Honor, obtained from participating in PvP battlegrounds and brawls. The Head, Shoulders, Hands, and Feet cost 2 Marks of Honor each; the Chest and Legs cost 3 Marks each; and the Waist and Wrist cost 1 Mark each, for a total of 16 Marks of Honor. Purchasing these replicas requires the "Veteran of the Alliance" or "Veteran of the Horde" achievement, earned by reaching an 1800 rating in Rated Battlegrounds on any character on the account.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming Marks of Honor; they can be acquired continuously by queuing for PvP battlegrounds. While battlegrounds require a group, you can queue for them solo at max level. The number of runs needed depends entirely on your win rate, but collecting the 16 Marks of Honor for the full set may take between 5 to 10 battleground victories. This is not a tier token set, and all pieces are purchased directly from the vendor with the same currency. The appearance is only purchasable and learnable by cloth-wearing classes (Mage, Priest, Warlock), and the specific set names are "Replica Warlord's Silk," "Vestments," or "Regalia."

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor quickly, focus on winning battlegrounds during events like the Battleground Bonus Event for extra rewards. Complete the weekly PvP quests, such as "Something Different" for the active Brawl, which awards a bonus of 5 Marks of Honor upon completion. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. Ensure your account has the required 1800 RBG rating achievement before traveling to the vendor, as the items will not be visible for purchase otherwise.

829.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Naxxramas (Tier 7) raid instance on 10-player difficulty. The eight pieces are direct drops from specific bosses scattered throughout the raid's five wings. The Head, Helm of the Unsubmissive, drops from Gothik the Harvester. The Shoulders, Mantle of the Unrestrained, drop from Gluth. The Chest, Robes of the Umbral Brute, drops from Maexxna. The Legs, Leggings of the Instructor, drop from Instructor Razuvious, while the Hands, Gloves of the Fallen Wizard, drop from Sapphiron. The Feet, Boots of Forlorn wishes, drop from Loatheb; the Waist, Sash of the Parlor, drops from Heigan the Unclean; and the Wrists, Bindings of the Hapless Prey, drop from Anub'Rekhan.

### Farming Logistics
Naxxramas operates on a weekly raid lockout, shared between the 10-player and 25-player versions. The instance is fully soloable by any class at max level. As this is not a tier set, it does not use a token system and all pieces are direct drops. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take 3-5 weekly clears, though bad luck can extend this significantly. You must run the 10-player version of the raid, as these specific items do not drop in the 25-player version. All pieces drop from the same source, the 10-player Naxxramas raid.

### Farming Tips
To farm this set, you must set the legacy raid difficulty to 10-player before entering. The four main wings can be completed in any order to unlock the final two bosses. After entering a wing, a teleporter pad near the instance entrance can be used to quickly travel between cleared wings. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. While this is a cloth set, the items have no class restrictions, allowing any class to loot them and learn the appearance.

830.content:

The matching wrist piece is not available from the PvP vendor and must be obtained from a different source, such as the "Bracers of the Unholy Knight" from Gothik the Harvester in the Naxxramas raid.

To farm Marks of Honor quickly, participate in PvP during the weekly "The Call to Arms" battleground bonus event, which often rewards extra currency. Ensure you are visiting the Dalaran in the Northrend continent, not the versions in the Broken Isles or Dragon Isles. The set pieces are bind-on-pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

834.content:

The most efficient method to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is available, as it provides quick and guaranteed victories against AI opponents. Also, take advantage of the weekly battleground bonus event, which rewards extra Marks for completing objectives. The ensemble item itself is Bind-on-Account, allowing you to farm the Marks on any character and then send the ensemble to the character you wish to have the appearance. These items are not BoE and cannot be found on the Auction House.

836.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the replica of The Burning Crusade's Season 4 PvP armor, purchased from vendors for Marks of Honor. Alliance players can find Lieutenant Karter in the Champion's Hall in Stormwind City, while Horde players can find First Sergeant Holrem in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar. A neutral vendor, Kezzik the Striker, is also located in Area 52 in Netherstorm. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, and Legs cost 3 Marks of Honor each. The Hands, Feet, and Waist cost 2 Marks of Honor each, while the Wrists cost 1 Mark of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily earned by participating in and winning PvP Battlegrounds, Arenas, and Brawls, making it fully farmable through queued content. A full set (excluding the wrist slot) costs 18 Marks of Honor; the number of matches required varies based on win rate but can be completed in a single day of focused play. This set is purchased directly and does not use a token system, so there are no class-sharing restrictions beyond the armor type.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to accumulate Marks of Honor is by winning the PvP Brawl of the week, as they often have quicker objectives than standard battlegrounds. Your first random battleground win of the day also provides bonus currency. Check Dragonflight and older expansion zones for World Quests that may reward Marks of Honor for a non-battleground alternative. The purchased items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

1101.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic Cloth armor from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands on Normal difficulty. The eight armor pieces drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Wristwraps drop from Vigilant Guardian, the Belt from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle, and the Robe from Halondrus the Reclaimer. The Gloves are obtained from Lihuvim, Principal Architect, while the Slippers can drop from either Skolex or Artificer Xy'mox. The final three pieces, the Crown, Mantle, and Leggings, drop from Anduin Wrynn, Rygelon, and The Jailer respectively.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per difficulty. While early bosses may be soloable at maximum level, later encounters such as Halondrus and Anduin require a small group of 2-5 players. Collecting all eight pieces can take approximately 5-10 weekly clears due to RNG, assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item. This set's pieces drop directly and do not use the tier token system. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties each have their own separate weekly lockout and reward a different color variation of the set.

### Farming Tips
For this specific gold and brown appearance, you must run the raid on Normal difficulty. The most efficient method is to create or join a transmog group using the in-game Group Finder tool. There are no significant skips available, so all requisite bosses must be defeated in order to reach the later ones. Pieces from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House, though visually similar BoE items from the raid's trash mobs may exist. The Great Vault system from Shadowlands is no longer active for acquiring this gear.

1105.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Mythic difficulty cloth armor from the Blackrock Foundry raid, a tier 17 instance. The main armor pieces drop from specific bosses within the raid on Mythic difficulty only. The Head slot, Hood of the Chromatic Hydra, drops from Kromog. The Shoulder slot, Mantle of the Chromatic Hydra, drops from The Iron Maidens. The Chest, Robes of the Chromatic Hydra, is from the final boss, Blackhand. The Legs, Leggings of the Chromatic Hydra, are obtained from The Blast Furnace, while the Hands, Gloves of the Chromatic Hydra, drop from Operator Thogar. The Feet, Sandals of the Chromatic Hydra, drop from Flamebender Ka'graz, the Waist, Sash of the Unclaimed, is from Gruul, and the Wrist, Bracers of Spattered Steel, is from Hans'gar and Franzok.

### Farming Logistics
This set is subject to a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can attempt to get loot from each boss once per character per week. The entire instance is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Warlords of Draenor raids do not use a shared tier token system for Mythic difficulty; instead, the specific cloth items drop directly from the bosses. Because there are eight separate pieces dropping from eight different bosses, collecting the full set will likely require several months of consistent weekly clears. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts but award different color variations of the set and will not count towards this appearance.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Mythic difficulty, as this color variant is exclusive to it. There are no Bind on Equip pieces for this set, so it cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Warlords of Draenor bonus rolls (Seal of Tempered Fate) are no longer obtainable and cannot be used to supplement drops. The most efficient way to farm is to clear the entire instance weekly on a cloth-wearing character, as all pieces are Bind on Pickup and will be added to your wardrobe immediately upon looting.

1106.content:
### How to Obtain
This lookalike set is obtained from bosses within the Throne of Thunder, the Tier 15 raid from Mists of Pandaria. The Head, Shoulders, Chest, and Hands items all drop from the Twin Consorts encounter. The Waist (Girdle of the Unconquered) and Legs (Leggings of the Unstable Discharge) drop from Iron Qon. The Feet (Sandals of the Unbidden) are looted from Tortos, and the Wrist (Bracers of the Banished) comes from Horridon.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder operates on a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. The raid is fully soloable by any class at current max level. Because multiple key pieces drop from a single boss (Twin Consorts), collecting the full set can take approximately 5-10 weeks per character due to drop-rate variance. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use the tier token system, so no classes share loot. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties are on separate lockouts, allowing three attempts per week on a single character for different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty. To speed up navigation, use the teleport orb that appears after defeating Megaera to go directly to Iron Qon's platform. Players can also use Elder Charms of Good Fortune, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an additional chance at loot from each boss. These charms can be acquired by trading Lesser Charms of Good Fortune at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1107.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Lookalike (LFR) version of the Shaman Tier 16 set, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid. All pieces drop from specific bosses within the raid on Looking For Raid difficulty. The Head drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty, the Shoulder drops from Malkorok, and the Chest drops from Sha of Pride. The Legs are looted from General Nazgrim, the Hands from Immerseus, and the Feet from the Iron Juggernaut. The Waist drops from Garrosh Hellscream, and the Wrist can be obtained from the Spoils of Pandaria encounter.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid has a weekly lockout for each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic) per character. The LFR difficulty required for this set is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the number of pieces and personal loot RNG, expect to run the full raid 5-10 times on average to collect the complete set. This set is obtained via direct boss drops through the personal loot system; it does not use the tier token system found in Normal and Heroic difficulties. While only Mail-wearing classes (Shaman, Hunter, Evoker) will receive the actual items, the appearances are awarded directly to the wardrobe for all other armor types upon looting.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version of Siege of Orgrimmar, speak with Lorewalker Han, Lorewalker Cho, or Lorewalker Fu in the Seat of Knowledge, located above the Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. The raid is divided into four separate wings, allowing you to queue for only the sections containing the bosses you need. You can use Warforged Seals for a bonus roll on bosses for an extra chance at loot each week. All items from this set are Bind on Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House.

1117.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier mail armor from the Heroic version of the Throne of Thunder raid, the Tier 15 instance from Mists of Pandaria. All eight pieces drop from specific bosses within this raid when set to Heroic difficulty. The Head, Crown of the Doomed Empress, drops from Twin Consorts. The Shoulders, Spaulders of the Scorned, drop from Tortos. The Chest, Carapace of Segmented Scale, is looted from Megaera, and the Legs, Legplates of the Unthinking, come from Iron Qon. The remaining pieces are the Hands, Gauntlets of the Wretched, from Ji-Kun; the Feet, Sabatons of the Cursed Protector, from Jin'rokh the Breaker; the Waist, Plated Tooth-Breaker Girdle, from Horridon; and the Wrists, Bracers of Constant Implosion, from Dark Animus.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder operates on a weekly raid lockout. For this instance, Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single lockout, meaning you can only defeat each boss on one of those two difficulties per character each week. The Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty has a separate lockout and drops a different color version of this armor. The entire instance is fully soloable by a single player at current maximum level. Because this set is not a tier set, it does not use a token system and all pieces drop directly for your class. Given the number of pieces and standard drop rates, acquiring the full set will likely take several weekly resets.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of loot, set the raid difficulty to 25-player Heroic before entering, as this makes bosses drop a larger quantity of items. You can use Mogu Runes of Fate, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an extra chance at loot from a boss you have defeated. These runes can be acquired on the Isle of Thunder. All boss mechanics are trivialized by max-level character power, allowing for a fast clear. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1118.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate lookalike from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid on Normal difficulty. The pieces drop directly from specific bosses throughout the instance. The Head slot, Hood of Cursed Victories, drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty. The Shoulderguards of Cursed Victories drop from Malkorok, and the Legguards of Cursed Victories drop from General Nazgrim. The Chestplate of Cursed Victories drops from the final boss, Garrosh Hellscream. The Gauntlets of Cursed Victories are looted from the Spoils of Pandaria encounter, the Greaves of Cursed Victories drop from Immerseus, the Waistplate of Cursed Victories drops from the Paragons of the Klaxxi, and the Bracers of Cursed Victories drop from Galakras.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday in North America. The entire instance can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance per piece, collecting the full 8-piece set may take between 4-8 weekly resets per character. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop as direct loot usable by any Plate class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). Note that the LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties of Siege of Orgrimmar are all separate lockouts, allowing you to clear each one once per week for different-colored versions of the set.

### Farming Tips
Run the instance on Normal difficulty, as this is the only source for this specific brown and bronze color variant. After you have cleared the raid once on a character, an NPC will appear near the entrance on subsequent runs to teleport you directly to the second wing, skipping the first four bosses. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls, using Warforged Seals, can be used on bosses for an extra chance at loot. These seals can be obtained from an NPC on the Timeless Isle or in your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. For the Galakras encounter, you must use the anti-air cannons in the two towers to shoot down the boss before you can engage him on the ground.

1119.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate armor lookalike from the Dragon Soul raid, specifically the Looking For Raid (LFR) difficulty. The Head (Greathelm of the Lifeless) and Hands (Gauntlets of the Lifeless) drop from Warlord Zon'ozz. The Shoulder piece (Shoulderplates of the Lifeless) is from Hagara the Stormbinder. The Chest (Battleplate of the Lifeless) drops from Ultraxion, Legs (Legguards of the Lifeless) drop from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping, and Feet (Sabatons of the Lifeless) drop from Morchok. The Waist (Waistplate of the Lifeless) and Wrist (Bracers of the Lifeless) are Bind on Equip items that drop from trash mobs within the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The raid has a weekly lockout per character. It is fully soloable by any class at current max level. Since this is a lookalike set, it does not use the tier token system. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties for Dragon Soul are on separate lockouts, but this specific color tint only drops from the LFR version. Expect to complete the full set in approximately 4-8 runs, depending on drop luck.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR version of Dragon Soul, speak to the NPC Auridormi in the Caverns of Time. The Waist and Wrist pieces are BoE, so they can often be purchased from the Auction House to speed up completion. On the Spine of Deathwing encounter, kill three Corruption tentacles, wait for Deathwing to roll, then kill the final tentacle to expose the Amalgamation and avoid bugging the fight. There are no skips in this linear raid, but you can use your mount in the outdoor areas to travel faster.

1123.content:
### How to Obtain
This set was a world drop from rare elite mobs during the Shadowlands pre-patch event, "Death's Rising," which took place in Icecrown. The specific rare mobs that dropped each piece were located throughout the zone. The Frostsavage Helm dropped from Patchwerk, the Pauldrons from Bronjahm, the Breastplate from Blood Queen Lana'thel, and the Legplates from Skadi the Ruthless. The Gauntlets dropped from Prince Keleseth, the Sabatons from Ingvar the Plunderer, the Belt from Noth the Plaguebringer, and the Bracers from Lord Marrowgar. This pre-patch event and its associated rare mobs are no longer available in the game, making the set unobtainable through its original source.

### Farming Logistics
The event rares spawned on a 20-minute rotation in Icecrown, with no personal or daily lockout, allowing for continuous farming. The farm was fully soloable by a max-level character at the time of the event. Due to relatively high drop chances, a player could typically collect the full set within a few hours of dedicated farming by rotating between the rare spawn points. This set did not use a tier token system and was a direct drop for any character class that could kill the mobs. There were no difficulty settings such as LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic for this world event.

### Farming Tips
As this set is no longer obtainable from its original source, it cannot be actively farmed. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the regular Auction House. The only potential way to acquire pieces of this set is by purchasing an Unclaimed Black Market Container from the Black Market Auction House and hoping a piece is inside. There are no skips, shortcuts, or alternative farming methods for this appearance.

1125.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained as Bind-on-Equip world drops from trash mobs inside level 80-85 Cataclysm dungeons. The full set consists of eight pieces: Frostwoven Cowl, Frostwoven Shoulders, Frostwoven Breastplate, Frostwoven Leggings, Frostwoven Gloves, Frostwoven Boots, Frostwoven Belt, and Frostwoven Bracers. No specific piece drops from a particular boss; all are random drops from non-boss enemies. The most efficient dungeons to farm for these drops are those with high mob density, such as Grim Batol, Halls of Origination, and The Stonecore.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed in Cataclysm dungeons, which have a 10-instance-per-hour account-wide limit on Normal difficulty and a daily lockout on Heroic difficulty. The farm is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. This is not a tier set and does not use a token system, so no classes share tokens for its acquisition. Due to the random nature of BoE drops, collecting the full set from drops alone can take anywhere from 20 to over 100 instance clears. All pieces drop from the same trash mob loot tables within the specified dungeons.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Normal, as this allows you to exit and reset the instance up to 10 times per hour. The most direct method for acquiring this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind-on-Equip and can be purchased from other players. If farming directly, focus on clearing large packs of trash mobs near the entrance of an instance like Grim Batol or Halls of Origination, then leave and reset without completing the dungeon. Bonus rolls and the Great Vault system do not apply to loot from Cataclysm dungeons.

1126.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased as a single item, the "Ensemble: Swashbuckling Buccaneer's Slops," which teaches all armor appearances. It is sold by Au'Dara, the Quartermaster for The Archivists' Codex, located in Exile's Hollow in Zereth Mortis. The cost is 5,000 Cataloged Research. Purchase requires reaching Honored reputation with The Archivists' Codex. The ensemble grants the appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Waist, Legs, Feet, Wrist, and Hands slots.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming the required currency; Cataloged Research can be obtained continuously by killing rare mobs and looting treasures in Zereth Mortis. The content is fully soloable at current max level. Reaching Honored reputation and farming the 5,000 Cataloged Research will likely take 5-10 hours of dedicated farming. This set does not use a tier token system, as all pieces are learned from one item. The currency and reputation are account-wide, allowing you to farm on multiple characters.

### Farming Tips
Unlocking flying in Zereth Mortis by completing the "Unlocking the Secrets" achievement is the most significant way to speed up this farm. Use addons like HandyNotes with the Zereth Mortis plugin to display rare and treasure locations on the map. Joining a group to kill rares makes the process faster and more efficient. The ensemble is Bind on Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

1127.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is created with the Tailoring profession, requiring a skill of at least 245. The patterns for the core pieces—Red Mageweave Headband, Red Mageweave Shoulders, Red Mageweave Vest, and Red Mageweave Pants—are sold by Tailoring vendors in capital cities. The primary reagent is Bolt of Mageweave, which is crafted from Mageweave Cloth. Mageweave Cloth drops from level 40-50 humanoid mobs in zones such as Tanaris, Feralas, and the classic dungeon Zul'Farrak. The set does not have its own hands, feet, or waist slots; these are commonly filled with pieces from the crafted Black Mageweave set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as its materials are obtained from repeatable world or dungeon content. The process of farming the required Mageweave Cloth is fully soloable at the current maximum level. The number of runs required to gather materials is low; a dedicated player can farm enough cloth in under an hour. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces share the same acquisition method (crafting), but the raw materials must be farmed or purchased separately.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so the fastest method of acquisition is often to buy them directly from the Auction House. For those who prefer to self-farm the materials, repeatedly clearing the Zul'Farrak dungeon is the most efficient method for gathering large quantities of Mageweave Cloth. Before crafting, compare the Auction House cost of raw materials like Mageweave Cloth and Red Dye against the price of the finished armor pieces to find the most economical option.

1128.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind-on-Equip world drops from level 51-60 mobs found in late-game Classic zones and dungeons. The eight pieces, such as the Sorcerer's Robes and Sorcerer's Mantle, do not drop from specific bosses but are random drops from trash mobs. The most efficient farming locations are high-density dungeons like Blackrock Depths, Stratholme, and Scholomance. Outdoor zones including Winterspring, Eastern Plaguelands, and Silithus are also viable sources.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from world drops, which have no lockout and can be farmed continuously in outdoor zones. When farming inside dungeons, you are limited to the 10 instances per hour cap for Normal difficulty resets. The entire farm is easily soloable by any max-level character. There is no tier token system; each piece is an individual random drop. The number of mob kills required to collect all pieces is highly variable and can take many hours due to the large loot tables of the mobs.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of this set are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), so check the Auction House first, as you can often purchase the entire set or specific missing pieces. For active farming, use a character with high mobility and instant area-of-effect damage to quickly clear large groups of trash mobs in dungeons. The goal is to maximize kills per hour, so focus on clearing dense packs of non-elite mobs and ignore bosses. This set is not obtainable through bonus rolls or the Great Vault.

1129.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Swashbuckler's" armor, a collection of Bind on Equip world drops primarily found in Classic zones. All pieces drop from humanoid mobs between level 25 and 45. The best farming locations are the pirate encampments in The Cape of Stranglethorn and the Syndicate camps in Arathi Highlands due to high mob density. The eight pieces that comprise the set are: Swashbuckler's Eyepatch (Head), Swashbuckler's Shoulderpads (Shoulder), Swashbuckler's Shirt (Chest), Swashbuckler's Leggings (Legs), Swashbuckler's Gloves (Hands), Swashbuckler's Boots (Feet), Swashbuckler's Belt (Waist), and Swashbuckler's Bracers (Wrist). There are no specific bosses or quests associated with these items.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this set, as it is farmed from repeatable world mobs. The entire farm is fully soloable at maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random drop chance for specific world drops, acquiring the full set through direct farming can take anywhere from a few hours to hundreds of hours. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), meaning they can be freely traded and purchased from the Auction House. This set does not use a token system and has no connection to raid or dungeon lockouts of any difficulty.

### Farming Tips
The most time-efficient method to acquire this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House. Farming gold and buying the items will almost always be faster than farming the items directly. If you choose to farm, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Focus on areas with rapid respawn rates and high mob density to maximize your number of kills per hour. You can also check the Auction Houses of different servers, as prices for BoE items can vary significantly.

1130.content:
### How to Obtain
The Felcloth Regalia is an uncommon quality Bind on Equip set that drops from enemies in The Maw and Korthia zones from the Shadowlands expansion. All eight pieces—Felcloth Hood, Felcloth Mantle, Felcloth Robes, Felcloth Trousers, Felcloth Gloves, Felcloth Slippers, Felcloth Belt, and Felcloth Bindings—can drop from any Mawsworn creature in these zones. There are no specific bosses or locations tied to individual pieces. As the items are Bind on Equip, the entire set can also be purchased directly from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed in open-world zones, so there is no lockout or reset timer to consider. The entire farm can be completed solo at the current maximum level, as the enemies are from a previous expansion. Due to the very low drop chance for specific world BoEs, acquiring the full set through farming could take an extensive number of hours defeating Mawsworn mobs. There is no tier token system for this set, and all pieces drop from the same general pool of enemies and are not tied to a specific difficulty.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by monitoring the Auction House, as all pieces are Bind on Equip and can be bought and sold. For players who prefer to farm the items directly, focus on areas with high Mawsworn enemy density, such as the Beastwarrens in the Maw or the main questing areas of Korthia. Using a class with high mobility and area-of-effect damage will speed up the process of clearing large groups of enemies. Creating a group with other players in the zone can also increase the number of total kills per hour.

1135.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon quality lookalike pieces that drop from various bosses across four different Burning Crusade dungeons. The head, waist, and chest pieces are found within The Steamvault: Cowl of the Grand Engineer and Sash of the Grand Engineer both drop from Mekgineer Steamrigger, while the Nether-Stitched Tunic drops from Warlord Kalithresh. The hands, legs, and feet—Gloves, Kilt, and Boots of the Fallen Martyr—all drop from the boss Broggok in The Blood Furnace. The Mana-Etched Spaulders drop from Tavarok in Mana-Tombs. Finally, the Bands of the Exorcist drop from Exarch Maladaar in Auchenai Crypts.

### Farming Logistics
The dungeons containing these pieces operate on a daily reset for Normal difficulty and a weekly lockout for Heroic difficulty. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. As the pieces are spread across four dungeons, collecting the full set may require between 5-10 runs of each relevant instance, assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item. There is no tier token system for this set. Since Normal and Heroic difficulties share the same loot tables for this appearance and have separate lockouts, you can farm both in the same week for more chances, though Normal is more efficient due to its daily reset.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to reset the instance daily for more frequent farming attempts compared to the weekly Heroic lockout. Use a high-mobility class like a Druid, Demon Hunter, or Monk to significantly reduce the time spent running through each dungeon. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House. Focus your efforts on The Blood Furnace and The Steamvault first, as these two dungeons contain six of the eight required pieces.

1138.content:
### How to Obtain
The Borean Embrace set is obtained from world drops in the starting zones of Northrend. All pieces, including the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist, have a chance to drop from any creature within the Borean Tundra and Howling Fjord zones. Mobs in the level 68-72 range are the most common source for these items. There are no specific bosses or vendors for this set, as the pieces are random Bind on Equip drops from general mob farming.

### Farming Logistics
This farm has no lockout and can be repeated indefinitely by killing mobs in the designated zones. It is fully soloable at the current maximum level. The number of kills required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to the low, random drop chance of specific items, potentially taking several hours of farming. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces come from the same general loot pool of Northrend world drops.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by searching for individual "Borean" pieces on the Auction House, as all items are Bind on Equip. For self-farming, use a character with high mobility and area-of-effect damage to clear large groups of mobs quickly in high-density areas like the Geyser Fields in Borean Tundra. Since the items are BoE, they must be equipped by a Leather-wearing character to be added to your appearance collection. There are no instance skips or special mechanics for this type of world farm.

1139.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from pieces that drop from various final bosses in level 80 Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Head drops from Loken in Halls of Lightning, and the Shoulders drop from Sjonnir The Ironshaper in Halls of Stone. The Chest is looted from King Ymiron in Utgarde Pinnacle, while the Legs drop from Moorabi in Gundrak. The Hands are obtained from Ingvar the Plunderer in Utgarde Keep, the Feet from Cyanigosa in The Violet Hold, the Waist from Gal'darah in Gundrak, and the Wrist from Keristrasza in The Nexus.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed across multiple instances, each on a daily Heroic dungeon reset. The entire set can be easily soloed at current max level by any of the eligible plate-wearing classes. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate, collecting all eight pieces will likely require 4-7 daily resets per specific dungeon, totaling one to two weeks depending on luck. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces drop directly for Warrior, Paladin, and Death Knight characters. Note that the Feet are from The Violet Hold, where the bosses are random, which may require extra runs just for the correct boss to spawn.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance. The farm can be expedited by using a high-mobility class like a Warrior or Paladin to move through the linear instances quickly. For Gundrak, you can defeat both Moorabi and Gal'darah in a single lockout to farm the Legs and Waist simultaneously. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House. The most time-consuming piece will be the Feet, as you must wait for waves of enemies in The Violet Hold and hope Cyanigosa is the final boss that spawns.

1141.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the three Cataclysm Heroic dungeons located in the Caverns of Time: End Time, Well of Eternity, and Hour of Twilight. The Helm of the Eviscerator drops from Murozond, the Chestplate from Echo of Baine, the Belt from Echo of Jaina, and the Bracers from Echo of Sylvanas, all within End Time. The Pauldrons of the Eviscerator drop from Asira Dawnslayer and the Legplates of the Eviscerator drop from Archbishop Benedictus in Hour of Twilight. Finally, the Gauntlets of the Eviscerator drop from Peroth'arn and the Sabatons of the Eviscerator drop from Queen Azshara in the Well of Eternity.

### Farming Logistics
The three required dungeons each have a daily Heroic lockout, allowing one full clear per character, per day. The instances can be easily soloed by any max-level plate-wearing class. Assuming an average drop chance of 20% per item, collecting all eight pieces can take between 5 to 15 daily lockouts, depending on luck. There is no tier token system; the gear drops directly as individual items for Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. Only the Heroic difficulty version of these dungeons drops this specific set appearance.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic before entering, as the Normal mode versions do not contain this gear. To increase the number of attempts per day, run the three dungeons on multiple plate-wearing characters, as the transmog appearances are account-wide. A class with high mobility, such as a Warrior with Heroic Leap or a Paladin with Divine Steed, can clear the dungeons slightly faster. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

1142.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the mail armor recolor of the Rogue Tier 13 set, obtained from the Dragon Soul raid in the Looking for Raid difficulty. The pieces are direct drops from bosses and do not use a tier token system. The matching belt and wrist pieces drop from different bosses within the same raid, while the feet are a bind-on-equip drop from trash mobs. The specific drop locations for each armor slot are as follows:

*   **Head:** Mask of the Corruptor - Drops from Warmaster Blackhorn.
*   **Shoulder:** Spaulders of the Corruptor - Drops from Hagara the Stormbinder.
*   **Chest:** Breastplate of the Corruptor - Drops from Ultraxion.
*   **Legs:** Legplates of the Corruptor - Drops from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping.
*   **Hands:** Gauntlets of the Corruptor - Drops from Warlord Zon'ozz.
*   **Feet:** Treads of Corrupted Water - Drops from trash mobs throughout the instance.
*   **Waist:** Belt of the Seven Seals - Drops from the chest after defeating Madness of Deathwing.
*   **Wrist:** Fossil-Inlaid Bracers - Drops from Morchok.

### Farming Logistics
The Dragon Soul raid has a weekly raid lockout per character per difficulty. To access the Looking for Raid version, you must speak to the NPC Auridormi, located outside the raid entrance in the Caverns of Time. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing a character to clear all three each week for more chances at different recolors. The entire raid is fully soloable at current max level. As this set does not use tier tokens, no other classes compete for the same drops. Given the number of pieces and drop rates, expect to complete 5-10 full runs to collect the entire set.

### Farming Tips
To begin the farm, travel to the Caverns of Time in Tanaris and speak with Auridormi to queue for the LFR version; select the "Fall of Deathwing" scenario. During the Spine of Deathwing encounter, stand on one side of the boss to trigger a barrel roll, which is necessary to remove tentacles. Kill three tentacles, then kill the resulting Corrupted Amalgamation near the head armor plate to destroy it, and repeat this process once more to complete the fight. The feet, Treads of Corrupted Water, are Bind-on-Equip and can frequently be found on the Auction House, allowing you to bypass farming them.

1145.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind on Equip items that drop from trash mobs inside three separate Cataclysm dungeons. The pieces do not drop from bosses. The most reliable sources for each piece are specific instances: Throne of the Tides for the chest, shoulder, and head; Blackrock Caverns for the hands and legs; and The Stonecore for the feet, waist, and wrist.

*   **Head:** Glyphed Hood (Throne of the Tides trash)
*   **Shoulder:** Glyphed Epaulets (Throne of the Tides trash)
*   **Chest:** Glyphed Robe (Throne of the Tides trash)
*   **Wrist:** Glyphed Bracers (The Stonecore trash)
*   **Hands:** Glyphed Gloves (Blackrock Caverns trash)
*   **Waist:** Glyphed Sash (The Stonecore trash)
*   **Legs:** Glyphed Leggings (Blackrock Caverns trash)
*   **Feet:** Glyphed Boots (The Stonecore trash)

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout if performed on Normal difficulty, which allows for infinite instance resets. The set can be fully soloed at current max level. Because the pieces are BoE world drops from high-density trash packs, collection time is highly variable but can be fast with focused farming of the first few pulls in each instance. This is not a tier set, so there is no token system. The three dungeons (Throne of the Tides, Blackrock Caverns, The Stonecore) must be farmed separately for their respective pieces. Running on Heroic difficulty will subject you to a daily dungeon lockout with no increased drop chance for these specific items.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, set the dungeon difficulty to Normal. Enter the instance, kill the first one or two groups of non-boss enemies, and then exit and reset the dungeon. Repeat this process until you acquire the desired pieces from that specific instance. Since all items in this set are Bind on Equip (BoE), the fastest method of acquisition is often to check the Auction House, where they can frequently be purchased. This avoids the randomness of drop rates entirely.

1146.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind-on-Equip world drops primarily found in the Swamp of Sorrows. The complete set includes the Daggerfen Hood, Daggerfen Pauldrons, Daggerfen Tunic, Daggerfen Leggings, Daggerfen Gloves, Daggerfen Boots, Daggerfen Belt, and Daggerfen Bracers. There are no specific bosses that drop these items. The most effective mobs to farm are the Daggerfen murlocs located in the western portion of the zone, specifically within and around the Daggerfen Village. Any mob within the zone's approximate 35-45 level range has a small chance to drop a piece.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it takes place in an open world zone and relies on respawning mobs. The farm can be fully soloed by any character, with a max-level character being able to defeat enemies instantly. Unlike raid or dungeon items with a 15-25% drop chance, specific world drops have a very low probability, meaning collection time is based on total kills rather than a number of runs. Because the items are Bind-on-Equip, any class can farm them, but only leather-wearing classes (Druid, Rogue, Monk, Demon Hunter) can add the appearances to their collection upon equipping them.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Daggerfen Garb are Bind-on-Equip and can be traded or sold, making the Auction House the most reliable and fastest way to acquire the full set. For those who prefer to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class with large, instant area-of-effect spells to clear the murloc villages efficiently. Create a circular route through the camps in western Swamp of Sorrows to allow for mob respawns between loops. Since this is a Classic-era farm, systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault do not apply.

1147.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Bind on Equip items that drop randomly from creatures and treasure chests in level 15-25 zones. The primary method of acquisition is farming world mobs or purchasing from the Auction House. The best zones for farming include Duskwood, Redridge Mountains, Ashenvale, and the Stonetalon Mountains. Low-level dungeons such as The Deadmines and Wailing Caverns also have a chance to drop pieces. The set consists of a Chest (Fine Leather Tunic), Legs (Fine Leather Pants), Hands (Fine Leather Gloves), Feet (Fine Leather Boots), Waist (Fine Leather Belt), and Back (Fine Leather Cloak).

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with this set, allowing for continuous farming of world mobs. It can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. The number of kills required to obtain all pieces is highly variable due to the large loot tables of world mobs and is subject to random chance. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces come from the same general world drop pool. There are no raid or dungeon difficulties like Normal or Heroic to consider for these items.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House, as they are frequently listed for sale. For active farming, use a high-level character with strong AoE abilities and high mobility to clear large packs of mobs in the target zones. The chest piece, Fine Leather Tunic, is also a guaranteed reward from the Alliance quest "Selling Fish" in the Redridge Mountains, making it the easiest piece to acquire for that faction.

1149.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Uncommon quality Bind on Equip items that drop from level 51-60 enemies throughout Classic zones and dungeons. There are no specific bosses that drop these pieces; they are part of a global loot table. High-density level 60 dungeons such as Stratholme, Scholomance, and Blackrock Spire are the most efficient sources for farming. Alternatively, mobs in outdoor zones like Winterspring, Eastern Plaguelands, and Silithus can also drop the pieces. All main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are obtained from this same random world drop source.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming these items in the open world, but farming inside dungeons is subject to the retail limit of 10 instances per hour, per account. The set can be soloed by any max-level character with trivial difficulty. Because the items are random world drops with a very low drop chance, directly farming the full set could take hundreds of dungeon runs or thousands of individual mob kills. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces are Bind on Equip, meaning the most reliable way to acquire them is often the Auction House.

### Farming Tips
The most time-efficient method to acquire this set is to purchase the pieces from the Auction House. For those who prefer to farm it directly, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage to clear dungeons like Stratholme or Scholomance quickly. Running the dungeon on Normal difficulty is sufficient as difficulty does not affect the drop chance of these specific world drops. Since the items are Bind on Equip, you can farm them on any character and mail them to the character you want to learn the appearance.

1151.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted with the Leatherworking profession. The patterns for all pieces are learned directly from Expert and Artisan Leatherworking trainers at skill levels 205-250. Key materials required for crafting the full set are Thick Leather, Shadowcat Hide, and Silken Thread. The specific crafted items are Nightscape Headband (Head), Nightscape Shoulders (Shoulder), Nightscape Tunic (Chest), Nightscape Pants (Legs), Nightscape Gloves (Hands), and Nightscape Boots (Feet). There are no matching waist or wrist items for this specific set.

### Farming Logistics
As a crafted set, there is no lockout; materials can be farmed continuously. The mobs that drop the required leather and hides are low-level and can be soloed by any character. The entire set can be acquired immediately by purchasing the finished pieces or the raw materials from the Auction House. All pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), allowing them to be traded or sold. Crafting the set requires a character with a Leatherworking skill of at least 250.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to buy the pieces directly from the Auction House. If farming materials yourself, the best locations for Thick Leather are Uldaman or the yeti cave in Feralas. Shadowcat Hides are farmed from Shadowmaw Panthers located in the northern area of Stranglethorn Vale. Since the patterns are taught by trainers, there is no need to farm for rare recipe drops.

1154.content:
### How to Obtain
This world drop set is obtained from random mobs in Classic zones with a level range of approximately 43-51. This set is for Leather classes (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter). The best farming locations are high-density mob areas in Tanaris, Feralas, and Searing Gorge, or within dungeons like Zul'Farrak and Maraudon. Individual pieces do not have a specific boss source and can drop from any eligible mob within these locations.
*   **Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist:** All pieces drop from the same random monster loot tables in the specified zones and dungeons.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm; open-world zones can be farmed indefinitely, and normal dungeons can be reset up to 10 times per hour. The entire set is easily soloable by any class at current max level. The time required to collect all pieces is highly variable due to random drop chances and can take anywhere from a few hours to many weeks of dedicated farming. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces share the same world drop source. Difficulty modes like LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic do not apply to this Classic set farm.

### Farming Tips
All pieces of the Wicked Leather Armor set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so the most efficient way to acquire them is by searching for them on the Auction House. If you prefer to farm them yourself, target areas with high mob density, such as the Wastewander mobs in southern Tanaris or by repeatedly clearing the Zul'Farrak dungeon. Use a high-mobility class with strong, instant area-of-effect abilities to clear mobs as quickly as possible. There are no skips, bonus rolls, or special mechanics involved in this farm.

1155.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a Bind-on-Equip collection that drops from trash mobs inside level 80 Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. There are no specific bosses that drop these pieces; they are random world drops from non-boss enemies within the instances. The complete set consists of the Ahn'kahar Watcher's Hood (Head), Ahn'kahar Crypt Lord's Pauldrons (Shoulder), Ahn'kahar Blood-Hunter's Tunic (Chest), Ahn'kahar Web-Spinner's Legguards (Legs), Ahn'kahar Handwraps (Hands), Ahn'kahar Tunneler's Boots (Feet), Ahn'kahar Onyx-Claw Clasp (Waist), and Ahn'kahar Swarm-Caller's Cuffs (Wrist). Dungeons with high trash density, such as Halls of Lightning, The Culling of Stratholme, or Utgarde Pinnacle, are efficient farming locations. As all pieces are BoE, they can also be purchased from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is limited by a daily Heroic dungeon lockout, allowing you to clear and loot each unique WotLK Heroic dungeon once per day per character. All WotLK dungeons are trivially soloable at current maximum level by any class. Due to the very low and random nature of world drop BoE items, collecting the full set through farming can take an unpredictable number of runs, potentially hundreds of dungeon clears. This set does not use a tier token system. The only alternative source for these pieces is the Auction House, which is often a more reliable way to obtain them.

### Farming Tips
The most practical way to acquire this set is by regularly checking the Auction House, as direct farming is highly dependent on luck. If you choose to farm, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Monk or Demon Hunter, to clear trash packs quickly. The optimal strategy is to run a circuit of all WotLK Heroic dungeons daily, focusing on clearing all trash mobs. Make sure to set your dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance.

1157.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from Atticus within the Necrolord Covenant's Abomination Factory feature in Shadowlands. Players must have unlocked the Abomination Factory and crafted Atticus to access his vendor inventory. The set is purchased using the Malleable Flesh currency, which is earned primarily from World Quests in Maldraxxus and quests within the Abomination Factory itself. All eight main armor pieces are available from this single source: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Wrist, Hands, Waist, Legs, and Feet. The total cost for the complete eight-piece set is 325 Malleable Flesh.

### Farming Logistics
There is no instance lockout for this set; acquisition is gated by the collection of Malleable Flesh currency from daily and weekly quests. The entire process is solo content designed for a single player. Obtaining the full set can take one to two weeks, depending on the availability of relevant quests and the player's focus on farming them. This set is purchased directly and does not use a tier token system. All pieces come from the same vendor, and there are no alternate difficulties or loot tables to consider.

### Farming Tips
To acquire the set faster, prioritize completing the Abomination Factory's weekly quest, "Putting A Plan Together," which provides a large amount of Malleable Flesh. Also, focus on completing all available World Quests in Maldraxxus that reward the currency. The set pieces are Bind on Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Once a character in the Necrolord Covenant has purchased the items, the appearances are added to the account-wide Wardrobe for any eligible Leather-wearing class to use.

1158.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Korthian Cloth Armor, a world drop and quest reward appearance from the Korthia zone. The primary acquisition methods are opening Korthian Armaments tokens, receiving pieces as drops from rare elites and treasures within Korthia, or completing local quests. All main armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) can be obtained through these random sources. Alternatively, specific slots can be purchased directly from the vendor Duchess Mynx in Keeper's Respite for Cataloged Research, the zone's currency.

### Farming Logistics
The farm has no lockout, as Korthia is an open zone with daily quests and respawning rare mobs and treasures. The entire set is fully soloable by any max-level character. Collecting the full set via random drops and tokens is highly variable and can take several hours of dedicated farming, while purchasing pieces with Cataloged Research offers a deterministic path. The Korthian Armaments tokens are armor-type specific, creating a random cloth piece for Mage, Priest, or Warlock characters when opened. All pieces of the set come from the same Korthia zone ecosystem.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient strategy is to join "Korthia Rares" groups in the Premade Groups finder to quickly kill all active rare elites in the zone. Korthian Armaments tokens are Bind-on-Account, so they can be farmed on any character and mailed to a cloth-wearer to be opened. You must open the token on a Mage, Priest, or Warlock to learn the appearance; it does not auto-collect. Use the vendor Duchess Mynx to purchase your final missing pieces with Cataloged Research to avoid prolonged RNG.

1159.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike pieces from multiple Heroic difficulty Cataclysm dungeons. The Head, Cowl of the Guiltless, drops from Anraphet in Halls of Origination. The Shoulders, Mantle of the Preserver, drop from High Priestess Azil in The Stonecore. The Chest, Robes of the Umbral Brute, is from Forgemaster Throngus in Grim Batol. The Legs, Leggings of the Path, drop from Siamat in The Lost City of the Tol'vir. The Hands, Grips of the Raging Giant, and Feet, Boots of the Sullen Rock, both drop in The Stonecore from Slabhide and Ozruk respectively. The Waist, Belt of the Still Stream, drops from Lady Naz'jar in Throne of the Tides, and the Wrists, Bracers of the Dark Mother, drop from Erudax in Grim Batol.

### Farming Logistics
The five dungeons containing these pieces are on a daily Heroic dungeon reset, meaning you can loot each specific boss once per day per character. The entire set can be soloed by any character at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 20% per piece, a player can expect to collect the full set within 5-15 daily clears across the relevant dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system; pieces drop directly from bosses. The set only drops on Heroic difficulty; Normal difficulty does not contain this gear and has its own separate (or no) lockout.

### Farming Tips
You must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering to be eligible for the loot. For efficiency, park characters outside each of the five dungeons to quickly cycle through them daily. In The Stonecore, you can jump down the central chasm after the first boss to skip trash and land directly near Slabhide. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Characters with high mobility, such as Druids or Monks, can clear these dungeons in under five minutes each.

1160.content:
### How to Obtain
This Mail set is acquired from the Trial of the Crusader raid in Icecrown. The five main armor pieces are purchased with Trophy of the Crusade currency from Valis Windrunner in the Alliance tent at the Argent Tournament grounds. The matching off-set pieces are not purchased and drop directly from bosses within the raid.

*   **Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands:** Purchased with 1 Trophy of the Crusade each.
*   **Waist:** Belt of the Forgotten Martyr (Faction Champions)
*   **Feet:** Treads of the Icewalker (Lord Jaraxxus)
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of the Untold Massacre (Faction Champions)

### Farming Logistics
Trial of the Crusader has a weekly raid lockout, but the 10-player Normal, 10-player Heroic, 25-player Normal, and 25-player Heroic difficulties are all separate lockouts, allowing for four full clears per character each week. The entire instance is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. A single clear on 25-player Heroic difficulty will award enough Trophies of the Crusade to purchase the entire 5-piece set. This set does not use a tier token system; the currency is used to buy class-specific pieces directly, with the Mail appearance being collectible by Hunters and Shamans. The off-set belt, boots, and bracers must be farmed from their specific bosses.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the raid on 25-player Heroic difficulty to guarantee enough currency for the full set in one run. The raid instance is a single-room "boss rush" with no trash to clear or skips required. The Trophies of the Crusade currency must be taken to the vendor outside the instance to be exchanged for the gear; the set pieces do not drop directly from bosses and are not automatically added to your collection. None of the set pieces or their matching lookalikes are Bind-on-Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1162.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained via the Leatherworking profession. The key reagents for all pieces are Thick Scorpid Scale and Cured Thick Hide. Most of the required patterns are sold by Blixrez Goodstich in Winterspring for Horde and Qia in Winterspring for Alliance, which include the Bracers, Belt, Gauntlets, Leggings, and Boots. The Pattern: Heavy Scorpid Helm is sold by Zannok Hidepiercer in Cenarion Hold, Silithus, but requires a Friendly reputation with the Cenarion Circle. The final piece, the Pattern: Heavy Scorpid Breastplate, is a world drop from Hederine Slayers, found in Silithus.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is crafted and its materials are gathered from the open world. The entire process of farming materials and patterns can be fully soloed at current max level. The main time investment is not based on resets, but on the drop chance of the Pattern: Heavy Scorpid Breastplate, which can take a significant amount of time to acquire from Hederine Slayers. The only other gate is farming Cenarion Circle reputation to Friendly to purchase the helm pattern. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are acquired through crafting.

### Farming Tips
The most direct method to obtain this set is by purchasing the finished Bind-on-Equip armor pieces directly from the Auction House. The Pattern: Heavy Scorpid Breastplate is also Bind-on-Equip and may be available on the Auction House, allowing you to bypass farming it. To gather the materials yourself, a character with the Skinning profession can farm Thick Scorpid Scales from scorpids in Tanaris or Silithus. Reputation for the helm pattern is quickly earned by completing the main Silithus questlines or turning in Encrypted Twilight Texts.

1163.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop mail recolor primarily obtained from the level 60 dungeons Upper Blackrock Spire, Stratholme, and Scholomance. Most pieces are found in Upper Blackrock Spire: the War-strung Headgear (Warchief Rend Blackhand), War-strung Shoulders (The Beast), War-strung Chestpiece (General Drakkisath), and War-strung Gauntlets (Pyroguard Emberseer). The War-strung Belt and War-strung Bracers are zone drops from non-boss enemies within Upper Blackrock Spire. The War-strung Leggings drop from Baron Rivendare in Stratholme, and the War-strung Sabatons drop from Kirtonos the Herald in Scholomance.

### Farming Logistics
The three relevant dungeons operate on a daily dungeon reset lockout. The entire set can be farmed solo by any max-level character with no difficulty. As the pieces are split across three instances, plan for approximately 5-10 runs of each dungeon to collect the full set. This set does not use a token system and all items are direct drops. Classic-era dungeons only have a single difficulty, so there are no separate LFR/Normal/Heroic/Mythic lockouts to manage.

### Farming Tips
The War-strung Belt and War-strung Bracers are Bind on Equip, so check the Auction House to potentially complete the set faster. To farm the feet in Scholomance, you must summon Kirtonos the Herald by using the Brazier of Beckoning on his balcony after defeating Instructor Chillheart. To access the boss for the legs in Stratholme, Baron Rivendare, you must first defeat the enemies at the three ziggurats in the Slaughter Square to open his gate. Using a class with high mobility will significantly reduce the time spent running through these instances.

1164.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Glimmering Mail armor, a world drop from level 49-59 mobs throughout original Classic zones. Each piece drops from a shared global loot table, meaning there are no specific bosses to target for individual items. The best zones for farming include Winterspring, Western Plaguelands, Eastern Plaguelands, and Burning Steppes due to their high density of mobs in the correct level range. The complete set consists of the Glimmering Mail Coif, Pauldrons, Breastplate, Legguards, Gauntlets, Girdle, Bracers, and Greaves. Dungeons like Blackrock Depths, Stratholme, and Scholomance also contain many mobs that can drop these pieces.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from world mobs and dungeons, so there is no weekly or daily lockout. Non-heroic dungeons can be reset up to 10 times per hour, allowing for continuous farming sessions. The entire farm is fully soloable at current max level, with mobs dying instantly to any AoE ability. Due to the extremely low drop chance of specific world drop items, collecting the full set through farming alone can take hundreds of hours and thousands of mob kills. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces come from the same world drop source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire this set is by purchasing the Bind on Equip (BoE) pieces from the Auction House. For those who prefer to farm, use a high-mobility class with strong AoE abilities, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large areas or dungeons quickly. Farming high-density dungeons like Stratholme (living side) can be more efficient than open-world farming due to the sheer number of mobs. Check the Auction House periodically even while farming, as individual pieces may be listed for a reasonable price, allowing you to complete the set faster.

1165.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted with the Leatherworking profession and can be worn by Mail classes. The primary reagents are Turtle Scales, farmed from turtles in zones like Thousand Needles or Tanaris, and Heavy Leather. The patterns have multiple sources: the recipes for the Turtle Scale Gloves, Leggings, and Bracers are taught by Expert Leatherworking trainers. The Pattern: Turtle Scale Breastplate is sold by vendors like Warg Deepwater in Arathi Highlands for the Alliance. The Pattern: Turtle Scale Helm and Pattern: Turtle Scale Boots are uncommon world drops from mobs in the level 35-45 range, particularly turtles and basilisks in Thousand Needles. The original set does not include shoulder or waist pieces.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is created from farmable materials and patterns. All components can be farmed solo by a max-level character. The primary time investment is farming the world-drop patterns for the Helm and Boots, which have a low drop chance and can take many mob kills to acquire. This set does not use a token system. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), allowing them to be crafted by a Leatherworker and then traded or mailed to the character that will use the transmog.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is often to purchase the finished BoE pieces or the patterns directly from the Auction House. If farming the materials, the highest density of turtles for Turtle Scales is on the small islands of southern Tanaris or the Shimmering Flats in Thousand Needles. While any character can farm the materials, a character with Leatherworking (skill 150+) is required to craft the items. Since the pieces are BoE, any Mail-wearing class (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker) can equip them to learn the appearance.

1166.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of uncommon Bind-on-Pickup mail items from the Classic dungeons Stratholme and Scholomance. The Blood-Spattered Breastplate drops from Jandice Barov in Scholomance. Most other pieces drop from bosses within Stratholme: the Headdress from Hearthsinger Forresten, the Shoulder-Pads from The Unforgiven, the Gauntlets from Timmy the Cruel, and the Sabatons from Maleki the Pallid. The Blood-Spattered Greaves drop from Baroness Anastari on the Undead side of Stratholme. The matching Blood-Spattered Sash (Waist) and Blood-Spattered Bracers (Wrist) are world drops from trash mobs within these same dungeons.

### Farming Logistics
The instances can be reset up to 10 times per hour, as there is no daily or weekly lockout for these classic dungeons. The entire set is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Acquiring all boss-specific pieces will likely require 10-20 full clears of the relevant dungeon wings, depending on drop luck. This set does not use a tier token system. The main farm is split, with the Chest piece dropping in Scholomance while all other major pieces drop from bosses in Stratholme.

### Farming Tips
Use a character with high mobility, such as a Demon Hunter or Monk, to move through the dungeons faster. The items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House; they must also be looted by a mail-wearing class (Hunter or Shaman) to be added to the Wardrobe. For Stratholme, you must run both the main gate entrance ("Live" side) and the service entrance ("Undead" side) to access all necessary bosses. To maximize efficiency, run directly to the bosses that drop the pieces you need, kill them, and then exit to reset the instance.

1167.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of look-alike leather pieces from various Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons. The Fel-Stalker's Headguard and Spaulders drop from The Maker and Keli'dan the Breaker respectively, both located in The Blood Furnace. The Fel-Stalker's Tunic drops from Quagmirran in The Slave Pens, while the Fel-Stalker's Leggings drop from Murmur in Shadow Labyrinth. Warchief Kargath Bladefist in The Shattered Halls drops the Fel-Stalker's Gloves, and the duo Nazan & Vazruden in Hellfire Ramparts drop the Fel-Stalker's Boots. Matching non-set waist and wrist pieces also drop from various bosses within these same Heroic instances.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Burning Crusade Heroic dungeons, which have a daily lockout per character. All required instances can be easily soloed by any character at the current maximum level. With a drop chance of approximately 15-20% per piece, collecting the full six-piece set will likely take 5-10 days of clearing all relevant dungeons. The gear drops directly from bosses and does not use a token system, so it must be farmed on a leather-wearing class: Rogue, Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter. Each piece comes from a different dungeon, requiring you to visit Hellfire Ramparts, The Blood Furnace, The Slave Pens, The Shattered Halls, and Shadow Labyrinth daily for all chances at loot.

### Farming Tips
Ensure your dungeon difficulty is set to Heroic before entering, as the items will not drop on Normal difficulty. Use a character with high mobility and speed buffs, such as a Druid, Monk, or Demon Hunter, to significantly reduce the time spent running through each linear instance. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and therefore cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The five required dungeons are located across three hubs in Outland: Hellfire Citadel, Coilfang Reservoir, and Auchindoun.

1168.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Blackforge Mail (Recolor)," which teaches all eight armor appearances (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist). The ensemble is sold by the vendor Ve'nish, located within Ve'nari's Refuge in The Maw at coordinates 46.8, 41.6. The cost is 15,000 Phantasma. Phantasma is the currency earned by completing runs of the Torghast, Tower of the Damned instance in Shadowlands. A Dark Iron Dwarf character is required to make the purchase.

### Farming Logistics
