Torghast has no weekly or daily lockout and can be farmed repeatedly for Phantasma. The instance is fully soloable at current max level and presents no challenge. Acquiring the necessary 15,000 Phantasma will take approximately 10-15 full clears of a high-level Torghast layer, assuming an average reward of 1,000-1,500 Phantasma per run. This is not a tier token set, and all pieces are learned from the single purchased ensemble. The two available Torghast wings rotate weekly, but this does not affect Phantasma farming.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest Phantasma acquisition, run the highest layer you can clear efficiently; for a max-level character, this is typically Layer 12. Maximize your completion score on each run by killing all enemies, breaking all Ashen Phylacteries, and freeing all Soul Remnants to increase your final currency reward. The ensemble is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or sold on the Auction House. While any character can use the appearance once learned, only a Dark Iron Dwarf character on your account can see and purchase the ensemble from Ve'nish.

1169.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of eight Bind on Equip (BoE) pieces that drop from level 30-40 mobs throughout the world. The items are the Green Iron Helm, Shoulders, Hauberk, Leggings, Gauntlets, Boots, Girdle, and Bracers. While they can drop from any appropriate-level mob in outdoor zones like Arathi Highlands or Stranglethorn Vale, the most efficient farming sources are dungeons. Dungeons with high mob density in this level range, such as Uldaman, Razorfen Kraul, and Razorfen Downs, provide the highest number of chances per hour. All pieces can also be purchased directly from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no instance lockout, as it is a world drop. When farming in dungeons, you are only limited by the cap of 10 instances per hour per account. The farm is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drop BoEs, obtaining a full set through farming can take hundreds of runs; purchasing pieces from the Auction House is a more predictable alternative. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot tables.

### Farming Tips
For dungeon farming, run on Normal difficulty, as there is no benefit to higher difficulties. Use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear trash packs as quickly as possible. Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), check the Auction House frequently, as individual pieces can often be found for a reasonable price. Note that while any class can loot the items, only Plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) will add the appearance to the Wardrobe upon equipping it.

1170.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop obtained from mobs within The Burning Crusade expansion zones. All pieces are random drops primarily from creatures between level 60 and 65. The most efficient farming locations are Hellfire Peninsula and Zangarmarsh. The eight pieces of the set are the Radiant Circlet (Head), Radiant Shoulders, Radiant Breastplate (Chest), Radiant Leggings (Legs), Radiant Gloves (Hands), Radiant Sabatons (Feet), Radiant Belt (Waist), and Radiant Bracers (Wrist). There are no specific bosses or guaranteed sources for any of the items.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout; mobs can be farmed continuously until all pieces are acquired. The entire farm is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and variable drop rates for specific green-quality items, collecting the full set through farming can take anywhere from a few hours to several hundred, depending on luck. There is no tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot tables in the specified level range. The difficulty settings of dungeons or raids do not affect the drop rate of these world items.

### Farming Tips
Check the Auction House for all pieces before beginning to farm, as the items are Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and can often be purchased directly. For farming, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Monk, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. A high-density farming spot is the Pools of Aggonar in Hellfire Peninsula, which contains numerous non-elite demons that respawn rapidly. No bonus rolls or Great Vault rewards can be used to acquire these items.

1171.content:
### How to Obtain
This appearance is a plate recolor of the Fenclaw set known as the Revenant set, a Bind on Equip (BoE) world drop. It is primarily found on level 51-60 mobs in late-game Classic zones and dungeons. While it can drop from mobs in zones like Silithus, Winterspring, or the Plaguelands, the most common source for players is the Auction House. The eight pieces that complete the set are the Revenant Helmet, Revenant Shoulders, Revenant Chestplate, Revenant Leggings, Revenant Gauntlets, Revenant Boots, Revenant Girdle, and Revenant Bracers. No specific boss drops these items; they are random drops from a wide variety of non-boss enemies.

### Farming Logistics
This set is farmed from world drops, so there is no lockout or reset timer to consider. The farm can be fully soloed at maximum level by any class. Because these items are BoE, the most efficient way to acquire them is by purchasing them from the Auction House. Farming them directly is extremely time-consuming due to the low drop chance from a very large loot table, and a full set could take hundreds of hours to obtain this way. No tier tokens are associated with this set, and instance difficulty settings are not relevant to the drop chance of these specific world drops found inside them.

### Farming Tips
The most practical method for acquiring this set is to use the Auction House. Create a wishlist or shopping list and check prices regularly, as individual pieces can appear for a wide range of prices. If you are determined to farm the items yourself, focus on dungeons with a high density of level 51-60 non-boss enemies, such as Dire Maul or Blackrock Spire. Use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect damage to clear the trash packs quickly. Since the items are BoE, you can farm them on any character and mail them to the character you wish to learn the appearance on.

1172.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is created through the Blacksmithing profession and is not a world drop or instance drop. The patterns for all five pieces—Runed Copper Breastplate, Pants, Gauntlets, Belt, and Bracers—are learned directly from any major city's Blacksmithing trainer at low skill levels (25-50). No Head, Shoulder, or Feet pieces exist for this specific set. The primary material required for crafting is Copper Bar, supplemented by Rough Stone and vendor-purchased Weak Flux.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is crafted and its materials can be farmed continuously. All required materials can be farmed solo by a character with the Mining profession. Since all pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE), they can be purchased directly from the Auction House, bypassing the need for farming or crafting entirely. The full set can be acquired instantly if all pieces are available for purchase or if a crafter has the materials on hand.

### Farming Tips
The fastest and most efficient method to obtain this set is to purchase the pieces directly from the Auction House. To craft the set, farm Copper Ore in level 1-10 starting zones like Durotar or Dun Morogh and smelt it into Copper Bars. A character only needs a Blacksmithing skill of 50 to learn and craft all the required patterns. Due to the common nature of the materials, the set is typically inexpensive to either craft or buy.

1173.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from Uncommon-quality quest rewards found in Classic (level 1-30) zones. The pieces are primarily awarded from quests in The Barrens for Horde players, and Darkshore, The Wetlands, Westfall, and Loch Modan for Alliance players. The Head, Blood-Stained Mail Coif, is from "The Thirsty Goblin" (Horde) or "WANTED: Baron Vardus" (Alliance). The Shoulder, Ravager's Mantle, is from "A Vengeful Fate" (Horde) or "The Absent-Minded Prospector" (Alliance). The Chest and Legs, Defender Tunic and Defender Leggings, are from various quests in The Barrens (Horde) or Darkshore (Alliance). The remaining Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist pieces are awarded from separate, individual quests within these same low-level zones for each faction.

### Farming Logistics
This set is acquired via one-time quest completion per character, meaning there is no lockout or reset timer. The entire set can be collected solo by any character powerful enough to complete level 10-25 quests. Because the items are quest rewards, it only takes one "run" through the specific questlines on a single character to collect all pieces. An appearance can only be unlocked by a class that can equip mail (Hunter, Shaman, Warrior, Paladin, Evoker), and you must choose the correct item from the quest reward options if multiple are offered. Once a quest is completed on a character, that character cannot obtain the reward again.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to use a low-level mail-wearing alt to complete the required quests sequentially, as you will not be overleveled for the content. If you have already completed these quests on your main character, you must use an alternate character to acquire the appearances. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House or any vendor. Ensure you select the mail armor piece from the quest reward window, as choosing a different reward will prevent you from obtaining that specific appearance on that character.

1176.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted using the Blacksmithing profession from the Cataclysm expansion. The patterns are learned directly from any Cataclysm Blacksmithing trainer, such as Thundar Longbeard in Stormwind or Kuldar Steeltooth in Orgrimmar, requiring a skill level between 500 and 525. The primary crafting reagent for all pieces is Folded Obsidium, which is created by smelting Obsidium Ore. Secondary reagents include Volatile Earth and Volatile Fire, which are also gathered from Cataclysm zones.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is crafted. All materials can be farmed solo by a character with the Mining profession. The time required to gather all materials is approximately 1-2 hours, depending on farming efficiency and zone population. The best zones for farming Obsidium Ore are Mount Hyjal and Deepholm. Since this is not a raid or dungeon set, there are no tier tokens, lockouts, or different difficulty versions to consider.

### Farming Tips
Before farming, check the Auction House, as both the raw materials and the finished armor pieces are often available for purchase. For those farming the materials, using a character with Mining and Herbalism is efficient, as Volatile Life from herbs can be transmuted into other Volatiles via Alchemy. Players without Blacksmithing can use the public Crafting Order system or ask a guildmate to craft the items if they provide the necessary materials. The set is Bind on Equip, meaning it can be traded or sold.

1177.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of lookalike plate armor pieces that drop from various bosses across four different Heroic-difficulty Cataclysm dungeons. The majority of the set is found within Heroic Stonecore, which contains the Chest, Hands, Legs, and Feet. The Head and Wrists drop from Heroic Shadowfang Keep, while the Shoulder comes from Heroic End Time. The final piece, the Waist, is obtained from a boss in Heroic Hour of Twilight.

*   **Head:** Helm of the Numberless Brood - Lord Godfrey (Heroic Shadowfang Keep)
*   **Shoulder:** Pauldrons of the High Requiem - Echo of Jaina (Heroic End Time)
*   **Chest:** Breastplate of the Risen Land - Ozruk (Heroic Stonecore)
*   **Hands:** Gauntlets of the Disturbed Giant - Slabhide (Heroic Stonecore)
*   **Legs:** Greaves of the Raging Giant - Corborus (Heroic Stonecore)
*   **Feet:** Boots of Sullen Rock - High Priestess Azil (Heroic Stonecore)
*   **Waist:** Belt of the Ferocious Wolf - Asira Dawnslayer (Heroic Hour of Twilight)
*   **Wrist:** Alpha-Worg Bracers - Commander Springvale (Heroic Shadowfang Keep)

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from dungeons subject to a daily reset for Heroic difficulty. Each dungeon can be completed once per day, per character. The entire set can be easily soloed by any max-level character of a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). Assuming a standard 15-25% drop rate per item, collecting all eight pieces could take between 5 to 15 runs of the relevant dungeons, spread across multiple days due to the lockout. This set does not use a tier token system; all items are direct boss drops. The armor pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and only drop on Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
To farm this set, you must set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering each instance. Using a high-mobility class like a Demon Hunter, Druid, or Monk will significantly speed up the process of running through the dungeons. Since four separate dungeons are required, it is efficient to run all of them in a single session each day to maximize your chances. All items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House; they must be looted by a plate-wearing character to be added to your wardrobe.

1178.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted using the Blacksmithing profession from the Wrath of the Lich King expansion. The patterns for all main slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are learned directly from Grand Master Blacksmithing trainers in Northrend, such as those in Dalaran. The primary materials required to craft the full set are Saronite Bars, Titansteel Bars, and various crystallized/eternal elements (Earth, Shadow, Water, Fire). No reputation or specific drops are needed to learn these trainer-taught patterns.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is created via a profession and is not tied to an instance. All materials can be farmed solo at the current max level. The time to collect the set is dependent on the acquisition of materials, either through farming or the Auction House, rather than a percentage-based drop chance. This set does not use a tier token system. All pieces are crafted by Blacksmiths and do not drop from any alternative source.

### Farming Tips
The fastest method to acquire this set is to buy the Bind-on-Equip pieces directly from the Auction House. Alternatively, purchasing the raw materials and commissioning a Blacksmith can be more cost-effective. For self-farming, a character with the Mining profession is required to gather Saronite Ore, which is most abundant in Icecrown and Sholazar Basin. The Titansteel Bars required for crafting have a daily cooldown for Smelting but can also be created by an Alchemist using Transmute.

1179.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted by players with the Blacksmithing profession. All patterns are learned directly from Grand Master Blacksmithing trainers in Northrend, such as Alard Schmied in Valgarde or Onodo in Warsong Hold, requiring a profession skill of at least 425. The primary crafting reagent is Saronite Bar, which is smelted from Saronite Ore found throughout Northrend zones, with high concentrations in Icecrown and Sholazar Basin. Each of the eight pieces also requires one Eternal Earth, which can be obtained from mining deposits or by transmuting Eternal elements with an Alchemist. The complete set requires a total of 88 Saronite Bars and 8 Eternal Earth to craft.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with obtaining this set, as it is crafted and its materials are farmed in the open world. All materials can be farmed solo at maximum level, or a character with the Blacksmithing profession can craft the items if provided with the materials. The time required to gather all materials is approximately 1-2 hours of dedicated mining in zones like Icecrown or Sholazar Basin. Since every piece of the set is Bind-on-Equip (BoE), the entire set can be purchased directly from the Auction House. This avoids the need for farming or a specific profession, making the appearance available for collection by all classes.

### Farming Tips
The most direct method to acquire this set is to purchase all eight Bind-on-Equip pieces from the Auction House. For players who prefer to farm, using a character with the Northrend Mining skill and a fast-flying mount is the most efficient way to gather the required Saronite Ore and Eternal Earth. The add-on "Routes" can be used to create an optimized path for farming ore veins in zones like Icecrown. Check the Auction House prices for both the raw materials (Saronite Ore, Eternal Earth) and the finished armor pieces to determine the most cost-effective acquisition method.

1180.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from level 80-82 creatures in early Cataclysm zones, primarily Mount Hyjal and Vashj'ir. All eight pieces of the "Cobalt Battlegear" set (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) share this same random drop source. As the items are Bind on Equip (BoE), the entire set is frequently available for purchase on the Auction House. This is a plate armor set, but the appearance can be learned by any character class that can equip plate armor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it involves killing mobs in the open world. The set is fully soloable by any max-level character. The time required to farm all the pieces directly is highly variable and dependent on random drop chance; acquiring the set could take anywhere from a few hours to hundreds of kills. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop from the same general loot table. Instance difficulty modes (Normal, Heroic, etc.) are not applicable to this farm.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is to check the Auction House regularly, as all pieces are BoE and can be purchased directly. For players who prefer to farm the items, use a character with high mobility and strong area-of-effect (AoE) damage to clear mobs quickly. Good farming locations include areas with high mob density, such as the Twilight's Hammer encampments in Mount Hyjal. There are no instance skips or specific mechanics to manage for this type of world farm.

1181.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop from trash mobs and bosses within level 80 Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The main pieces are Saronite Warhelm (Head), Saronite Pauldrons (Shoulder), Saronite Warplate (Chest), Saronite Legplates (Legs), Saronite Gauntlets (Hands), and Saronite War-Boots (Feet). A matching Waist (Saronite War-Girdle) and Wrist (Saronite Bracers) also drop from the same sources. No specific boss drops a designated piece; all items have a random chance to drop from any enemy inside the instances, with trash mobs being the primary source.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to a daily Heroic dungeon reset, allowing you to clear each eligible dungeon once per character per day. All dungeons are easily soloable at current max level. Due to the low, random drop chance of each piece, acquiring the full set through farming can take dozens of runs across many different dungeons. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces drop directly from enemies. The lockouts for Normal and Heroic difficulty are separate, but this set only drops on Heroic.

### Farming Tips
Set the dungeon difficulty to Heroic before entering. Since all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), the most efficient method is to check the Auction House first, as you can often buy the entire set for a reasonable amount of gold. If farming directly, use a class with high mobility to clear dungeons with high trash density, such as Utgarde Pinnacle or Halls of Lightning. A common strategy is to run a circuit of all WotLK Heroic dungeons on one character each day to maximize your chances.

1182.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of Uncommon-quality world drops obtained from killing creatures within the level 13-22 range. The pieces are not tied to specific bosses or enemies but are part of a global loot table for mobs in that level bracket. Key farming zones include the Northern Barrens, Silverpine Forest, Loch Modan, and Redridge Mountains. The set consists of the Angro'rosh Breastplate, Legguards, Pauldrons, Gauntlets, Boots, Girdle, and Vambraces. There is no matching helm piece for this specific set.

### Farming Logistics
As a world drop set, there is no lockout; mobs can be farmed continuously. The entire farm is easily soloable at current max level. Due to the very low drop chance of specific green items from large loot tables, acquiring the full set via farming can take many hours and thousands of mob kills. All pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), which means they can be traded or sold on the Auction House. This set does not use a token system, and instance difficulties are not applicable to its acquisition.

### Farming Tips
The most practical method for obtaining this set is to check the Auction House, as all pieces are BoE and are frequently listed by other players. For those who prefer to farm it directly, use a high-mobility class with instant area-of-effect abilities, like a Druid or Monk, to clear mobs efficiently. Focus on areas with high mob density, such as the quilboar encampments in Northern Barrens, to maximize kills per hour. No systems like bonus rolls or the Great Vault can be used to acquire these Classic-era items.

1183.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a world drop found within the level 55-60 Stratholme dungeon from the Classic expansion. The set pieces drop from specific bosses and trash mobs spread across both the "Live" (Crusader's Square) and "Undead" (The Gauntlet) wings of the instance. The Head drops from Darkmaster Gandling, and the Legs drop from Baron Rivendare. The Chest is looted from Baroness Anastari, the Shoulders from Hearthsinger Forresten, the Hands from Ramstein the Gorger, and the Feet from Stonespine. The Waist and Wrist pieces are uncommon zone drops from the various elite undead mobs throughout the instance.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed in a dungeon with no daily or weekly lockout, limited only by the 10 instances per hour cap on a single character. It is fully soloable at the current maximum level, with all bosses being trivial encounters. Assuming an average drop chance of 20% per piece, a full collection will require approximately 35-45 full clears of both wings of the dungeon. This set does not use a tier token system, as all pieces drop directly for the looting player. Note that the Waist and Wrist drop from a different source (trash mobs) than the main armor slots (bosses).

### Farming Tips
For maximum speed, use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to quickly navigate the large instance. You must clear both the Live and Undead sides of Stratholme to acquire all the boss-dependent pieces. The trash-looted Waist and Wrist items are Bind-on-Equip, so check the Auction House to potentially complete the set faster. All other pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and must be farmed directly.

1184.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of uncommon-quality plate armor awarded from quests in the level 80-82 Cataclysm zones of Mount Hyjal and Vashj'ir. A single character cannot obtain the full set from one zone, as the quests offer different pieces. Key pieces from Mount Hyjal include the Breastplate of the Witness, Pauldrons of the Locket, and Greaves of the Saronite Mine. The parallel Vashj'ir questline awards pieces like the Carapace of the Drowned, Spaulders of the Scouring Vapors, and Legguards of the Undrowned. The remaining slots (Head, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are also awarded from main story quests within these two zones.

### Farming Logistics
This set is not farmed in the traditional sense as there is no lockout; the pieces are one-time quest rewards per character. The quests are fully soloable at max level, with the entire acquisition process taking a few hours of focused questing. To acquire the appearances, a character must complete the main questlines in Mount Hyjal and/or Vashj'ir and select these specific items as their reward. Because quest rewards are Bind-on-Pickup, it takes one full "playthrough" of a zone's main story on a single character to acquire the available pieces. There is no tier token system, and no different difficulties apply to this open-world questing content.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to level a new character through Cataclysm Chromie Time, selecting either Mount Hyjal or Vashj'ir as the starting zone. If you have already completed these quests on a character, you must use an alternate character to acquire the appearances. While any class can collect the appearance, the source items are plate, so questing on a Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight is the most direct way to see them offered as rewards. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be found on the Auction House.

1185.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the "Glimmering Mail" set, a world drop from the original game, which shares its model with the "Revenant Plate" set but is mail armor. All pieces are Bind-on-Equip (BoE) and drop from humanoid, demon, and undead mobs in the level 45-55 range. The best zones for farming this set are Tanaris, Feralas, Azshara, and the Western Plaguelands. Because it is a random world drop, no specific boss or location is guaranteed to provide a piece; the items are part of a global loot table for mobs in the designated level range.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this farm, as it involves killing mobs in the open world. The set is fully soloable at the current maximum level, with mobs posing no threat. Due to the extremely large loot tables and low drop chances for specific green items, collecting all eight pieces via farming could take dozens or even hundreds of hours. This set does not use a token system, and all pieces are obtained from the same random drop source. Difficulty modes are not applicable to this type of world farm.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method to acquire this set is by purchasing the pieces from the Auction House, as all items are Bind-on-Equip. For players who prefer to farm it, use a high-mobility class with strong area-of-effect damage, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, to clear large groups of mobs quickly. Running lower-level dungeons with mobs in the 45-55 level range, such as Zul'Farrak or Maraudon, can also be an effective way to farm for the pieces. Check your server's Auction House regularly, as individual pieces may appear for a low price.

1186.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is crafted using the Blacksmithing profession. The plans are trained directly from any Grand Master Blacksmithing trainer in Northrend, such as Alard Schmied in Dalaran's Magus Commerce Exchange, and require a skill level of 400-425. The primary material for all pieces is Saronite Bar, which is smelted from Saronite Ore. Key crafted items include the Spiked Saronite Helm, Spiked Saronite Pauldrons, Saronite War Plate, Saronite War Legguards, Saronite Gauntlets, Saronite War Boots, Spiked Saronite-Reinforced Waistguard, and Spiked Saronite Bracers.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is obtained via crafting rather than instance drops. It can be fully acquired solo by a character with the appropriate level of Northrend Blacksmithing and the necessary materials. All pieces can be crafted in a single session, assuming all reagents have been gathered beforehand. There is no tier token system involved, and all pieces come from the same crafting source. Since this is a crafted set, difficulty modes like Normal or Heroic are not applicable.

### Farming Tips
The most direct way to obtain the set is by purchasing the Bind-on-Equip (BoE) pieces from the Auction House. For players who prefer to craft it themselves, Saronite Ore is best farmed in Icecrown or Sholazar Basin with the Mining profession. The required patterns are not rare drops but are learned directly from trainers, so there is no need to farm for recipes. A character with both Mining and Blacksmithing is the most efficient for self-farming.

1187.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind on Equip world drop items from The Burning Crusade expansion zones. The pieces are most commonly found by defeating mobs between levels 60 and 67, with no specific bosses assigned to any item. The most effective zones for farming are Hellfire Peninsula, Zangarmarsh, and Terokkar Forest. The main set pieces are Bloodscale Belt, Bloodscale Boots, Bloodscale Bracers, Bloodscale Chestpiece, Bloodscale Gauntlets, Bloodscale Helm, Bloodscale Legguards, and Bloodscale Pauldrons. As the items are BoE, the entire set can also be acquired by purchasing it from the Auction House.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, allowing for continuous farming in open-world zones. This is a Plate armor set, and its appearance can only be collected for transmog by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. All farming can be easily soloed at the current maximum level. Due to the extremely low and random nature of world drop chances, acquiring the full set via farming can take an extensive amount of time. All pieces are Bind on Equip, so they do not become soulbound until a character equips them.

### Farming Tips
To maximize efficiency, farm in areas with high mob density, such as the fel orc encampments in Hellfire Peninsula or the ogre areas in Terokkar Forest. Use a character with strong area-of-effect abilities and high mobility to clear large groups of enemies quickly. Regularly check the Auction House, as this is the most reliable and time-efficient method for acquiring specific missing pieces or the entire set. You can farm for these BoE items on any character and mail them to your plate-wearing character to learn the appearance.

1188.content:
### How to Obtain
This mail armor set is assembled from individual pieces that drop from various bosses across different Wrath of the Lich King Heroic dungeons. The Windchanneller's Headguard drops from Loken in Halls of Lightning and the Spaulders drop from Skadi the Ruthless in Utgarde Pinnacle. The Tunic and Bracers are found in Halls of Stone, from Sjonnir The Ironshaper and Maiden of Grief respectively. The Leggings drop from Cyanigosa in The Violet Hold, the Gauntlets from Gortok Palehoof in Utgarde Pinnacle, and the Boots from Ingvar the Plunderer in Utgarde Keep. Finally, the Girdle is obtained from Gal'darah in Gundrak.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from Wrath of the Lich King dungeons on Heroic difficulty, which are on a daily lockout per character. All bosses and instances can be easily soloed by a max-level character. Due to the number of different dungeons involved and standard drop rates, collecting all eight pieces will likely take 1-2 weeks of clearing the relevant dungeons each day. This set does not use a tier token system; each piece is a direct boss drop. Only the Heroic difficulty versions of these items grant the appearance for this set; Normal difficulty versions have a different appearance.

### Farming Tips
Use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Demon Hunter to minimize travel time within the instances. The main obstacle is The Violet Hold, as the final boss is random; you are not guaranteed to fight Cyanigosa on every daily run. In Halls of Stone, you can speed up the Brann Bronzebeard escort by running far ahead and clearing all trash before he reaches it. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1189.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier plate lookalike obtained from bosses in the 10-player difficulty of the Ulduar raid, the Tier 8 instance from Wrath of the Lich King. The specific pieces drop from the following bosses within the instance. The Head, Skystalker's Headpiece, drops from Mimiron. The Shoulders, Skystalker's Pauldrons, drop from Thorim. The Chest, Skystalker's Breastplate, drops from Yogg-Saron. The Legs, Skystalker's Legguards, drop from Freya, and the Hands, Skystalker's Gauntlets, drop from General Vezax. The matching feet, Greaves of the Rockmender, drop from XT-002 Deconstructor; the waist, Girdle of the End-Days, drops from General Vezax; and the wrists, Bracers of the Smothering Inferno, drop from Ignis the Furnace Master.

### Farming Logistics
Ulduar operates on a weekly raid lockout per character. The raid is fully soloable by any class at max level. Because the eight pieces drop from seven different bosses, collecting the full set typically takes 4-6 weekly clears, assuming an average drop rate of 15-25% per piece. This set is a direct drop lookalike and does not use the tier token system. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties drop different colorations of the gear and are on separate weekly lockouts, allowing two full runs per character each week for different-colored appearances.

### Farming Tips
To obtain this specific blue and grey colored set, you must run Ulduar on 10-player difficulty. The 25-player difficulty drops a purple recolor. You can run the instance on multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) each week to increase your chances of obtaining the set. After defeating the initial bosses, use the teleporters that activate within the instance to quickly navigate between the different wings and save time. None of the set pieces are Bind-on-Equip, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1190.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather set is a collection of uncommon-quality items that drop from bosses in the Wrath of the Lich King 5-man dungeons, specifically The Nexus and Utgarde Pinnacle. The Head (Cowl of the Zodar) drops from Commander Stoutbeard/Kolurg, Shoulders (Spaulders of the Careless Thief) from Grand Magus Telestra, Chest (Tunic of the Sarcastic Sprite) from Anomalus, Hands (Handgrips of the Savage Emissary) from Ormorok the Tree-Shaper, and Waist (Belt of the Sleepless) from Keristrasza, all within The Nexus. The Legs (Leggings of the Empty Throne) drop from King Ymiron, Feet (Boots of the Wary Bruin) from Svala Sorrowgrave, and Wrist (Cuffs of Calculated Risk) from Skadi the Ruthless in Utgarde Pinnacle. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup.

### Farming Logistics
The set can be farmed on either Normal or Heroic difficulty, both of which award the same appearance. Normal difficulty has no lockout, but you are limited to 10 instances per hour, while Heroic difficulty has a daily lockout per character. The entire set can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 20% per piece, it may take 5-10 full clears of both dungeons to acquire the full 8-piece set. This is a standard dungeon set with no tier token system, and pieces are class-agnostic beyond the leather armor requirement.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest acquisition, run the dungeons on Normal difficulty to bypass the daily lockout and allow for continuous farming. Use a high-mobility class like a Druid or Monk to speed up transit time between bosses. Since the set is split between two instances, it is most efficient to farm one dungeon repeatedly until you have all of its corresponding pieces before moving on to the next one. All mechanics in both dungeons can be ignored by a max-level character, allowing you to defeat bosses in a few seconds.

1191.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a collection of Bind on Equip world drops found within the Northrend dungeons Utgarde Keep and The Nexus. All eight pieces—Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist—drop from both trash mobs and bosses within these two instances on Normal difficulty. Because the items are random drops from a large loot table, no specific boss is a guaranteed source for any particular piece. The entire set can be farmed by repeatedly clearing the enemies in either dungeon.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to the standard instance lockout limit of 10 instances per hour per account. The dungeons can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. Due to the low, random drop chance of specific green items, collecting the full set via farming could take 20-50+ full instance clears or dozens of trash-only resets. This is not a tier set and does not use a token system. All pieces drop from the same sources, though they may also be found in Satchels of Helpful Goods from the random dungeon finder if your character level is appropriate for the expansion.

### Farming Tips
Run the dungeons on Normal difficulty, as this allows you to exit and manually reset the instance. The most efficient method is to clear the high-density trash packs at the beginning of Utgarde Keep, then exit, reset, and re-enter without killing any bosses. Because all pieces are Bind on Equip (BoE), check the Auction House before farming, as individual items can often be purchased to complete the set more quickly. Classes with high mobility and area-of-effect damage, such as Druids or Monks, are best for clearing the instances rapidly.

1193.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is assembled from tier tokens found in the Wrath of the Lich King raid, Naxxramas, with one piece from the Obsidian Sanctum. The Priest and Warlock versions are purchased with "Lost Conqueror" tokens, while the Mage version uses "Lost Vanquisher" tokens. The non-set Waist, Feet, and Wrist pieces drop directly from bosses within Naxxramas.

*   **Head Token:** Drops from Kel'Thuzad.
*   **Shoulder Token:** Drops from Loatheb and Gluth.
*   **Chest Token:** Drops from The Four Horsemen and Gluth.
*   **Legs Token:** Drops from Thaddius and Gluth.
*   **Hands Token:** Drops from Sartharion in the Obsidian Sanctum raid.
*   **Waist:** Sash of the Grand Necromancer drops from Gluth.
*   **Feet:** Boots of Forlorn Wishes drop from Heigan the Unclean.
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of Unleashed Magic drop from Sapphiron.

### Farming Logistics
The Naxxramas raid has a weekly lockout, which is shared between the 10-player and 25-player difficulties; you can only clear one version per character per week. The entire raid, along with Sartharion in the Obsidian Sanctum, can be easily soloed at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop rate for each token and direct drop, collecting the full 8-piece set typically takes 4-8 weekly resets. The "Conqueror" token for this set is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks. The "Vanquisher" token, used by Mages, is shared with Death Knights, Druids, and Rogues.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, mount up inside the instance and proceed through the four main wings (Plague, Arachnid, Military, Construct) in any order before accessing the final bosses. After clearing a wing, a teleporter at the instance entrance becomes active, allowing you to quickly move between the central hub and any cleared wing's entrance. Tier tokens do not automatically grant the appearance; you must take them to the appropriate armor vendor in Dalaran (Northrend), such as Magistrix Lambriesse, to exchange them for the gear piece. The 10-player and 25-player difficulties award different colorations of the same set, so choose the difficulty that provides the color you prefer.

1194.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate lookalike of the Paladin Tier 16 set, obtainable from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid. All pieces drop directly from bosses within the instance. The Head, Helm of Radiant Glory, drops from Immerseus. The Shoulders, Pauldrons of Radiant Glory, and the matching Feet, Greaves of Unending Vigil, both drop from Malkorok. The Chest, Breastplate of Radiant Glory, is a drop from Garrosh Hellscream. The Legs, Legplates of Radiant Glory, are looted from The Fallen Protectors, while the Hands, Gauntlets of Radiant Glory, and matching Wrist, Bracers of Direct Command, drop from General Nazgrim. The matching Waist, Gleaming-Eye Girdle, drops from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar has a weekly raid lockout, but the LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing four full clears per character each week for more chances at loot. The entire raid is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full set by clearing all four difficulties could take approximately 2-4 weekly resets. These are direct item drops and do not use the tier token system, so they are added to your appearance collection immediately upon being looted. The set can be collected and worn by any plate class: Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, run the raid on Normal or Heroic difficulty, as LFR requires queuing and Mythic has slightly more time-consuming mechanics. After defeating General Nazgrim, a rope appears allowing you to skip to Malkorok, but do not use this skip if you still need the waist piece from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman. You can use Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) on bosses for an extra chance at loot; these can be purchased with Lesser Charms of Good Fortune on the Timeless Isle or in your faction's Pandaria shrine. After killing Malkorok, you can safely jump down to the area for the Spoils of Pandaria encounter, skipping a significant amount of trash.

1195.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike version of the Paladin Tier 15 armor, obtained from the Throne of Thunder (Tier 15) raid on Heroic difficulty. The pieces are direct drops and can be equipped by any plate-wearing class. The specific items drop from the following bosses within the instance: the Crown of the Golden Golem (Head) drops from Dark Animus, the Pauldrons of the Golden Golem (Shoulder) drop from Iron Qon, and the Breastplate of the Golden Golem (Chest) drops from the Twin Consorts. The Legplates of the Golden Golem (Legs) are from Ji-Kun, the Gauntlets of the Golden Golem (Hands) are from the Council of Elders, the Girdle of the Golden Golem (Waist) is from Durumu the Forgotten, and the Bracers of the Golden Golem (Wrist) drop from Horridon. The matching Sabatons of the Unwavering (Feet) are a zone drop from trash mobs within Heroic Throne of Thunder.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid instance operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American realms. The entire raid is fully soloable at the current maximum level on all difficulties, including 25-player Heroic. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces may take between 5 and 10 weekly clears, subject to random luck. This lookalike set drops as direct gear and does not use the tier token system, allowing any plate class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) to farm it directly. The Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout and reward a different color variation of this set, allowing you to clear all three difficulties each week for more transmog options.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of loot, run the instance on 25-player Heroic difficulty, as this setting causes bosses to drop a larger quantity of items. After defeating Horridon (the second boss), you can jump off the side of his arena to skip a significant amount of trash mobs and proceed directly to the Council of Elders. You can use an Elder Charm of Good Fortune for a bonus roll on each boss, providing an extra chance to receive loot. The matching feet, Sabatons of the Unwavering, are a Bind on Equip item that drops from trash mobs and can often be purchased directly from the Auction House to save time.

1196.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder plate armor from the Throne of Thunder raid, the Tier 15 instance from Mists of Pandaria. Each piece drops from a specific boss within the instance when queued for the Raid Finder difficulty. The Head slot drops from the Twin Consorts, Shoulders from Iron Qon, and the Chest from Dark Animus. The Hands drop from the Council of Elders, Legs from Ji-Kun, and the Feet from Tortos. The Waist piece drops from Horridon, and the Wrists drop from Megaera.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder is on a weekly raid lockout per character. The instance is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 weeks per character, depending on luck. Mists of Pandaria LFR does not use a tier token system; the gear drops directly from the bosses, so there are no token-sharing classes. Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the set, allowing you to farm each difficulty once per week.

### Farming Tips
To access the Raid Finder version of Throne of Thunder, you must speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms and select the specific wing you wish to enter. Players can farm Mogu Runes of Fate on the Isle of Thunder to use for bonus rolls, giving an extra chance at loot from a defeated boss. On the Dark Animus encounter, immediately use area-of-effect abilities to destroy all the small anima golems before they can empower the larger ones. After defeating bosses, look for teleportation pads to quickly move between different sections of the raid.

1197.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is a non-tier lookalike found within the Throne of Thunder raid, the Tier 15 instance from Mists of Pandaria. The Head drops from the Twin Consorts, and the Shoulders drop from Iron Qon. The Chest piece is obtained from Dark Animus, while the Legs are looted from Ji-Kun. The Hands drop from the Council of Elders, the Feet from Megaera, the Waist from Tortos, and the Wrist from the first boss, Jin'rokh the Breaker.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder has a weekly raid lockout per character. The instance is fully soloable at max level. As this is a non-tier lookalike set, the pieces drop directly from bosses and do not use the tier token system. You can complete this raid on Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each week on the same character, as they have separate lockouts, providing three chances per week at each boss's loot. Assuming average luck with a 15-25% drop rate per piece, expect to run the raid for 4-8 weeks to collect the full set.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Normal difficulty for the fastest clears and to obtain the blue color variant of this set. After the first boss, use the sewer pipe on the left to skip trash, and after Tortos, use the clickable chains to cross the chasm directly to Megaera. You can obtain Elder Charms of Good Fortune from vendors in your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms and use them on bosses for a bonus roll. The Ji-Kun encounter can knock you off the platform, so position yourself in the center or with your back to the central pillar.

1198.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of non-tier plate lookalikes from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids on Heroic difficulty. The Head, Crown of the Doomed Empress, drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear. The Shoulderguards of the Unflanked drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok in Heart of Fear. The chest piece, Breastplate of the Fallen Striker, drops from Blade Lord Ta'yak in Heart of Fear. The Legplates of the Slain Baron drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, and the Gauntlets of the Disturbed Giant drop from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, both in Heart of Fear. The waist, Girdle of the Mogu's Unwavering Loyalty, drops from the Protectors of the Endless in Terrace of Endless Spring, while the feet, Gore-Soaked Stompers, drop from Garalon in Heart of Fear.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids operate on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday. Normal and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing you to clear each once per week per character for more chances at non-tier pieces. The instances can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance, collecting the full 7-piece set may take an average of 5-10 weeks. While this appearance is a recolor of a tier set, the tier tokens are split across different class groups (Protector for Warrior, Vanquisher for Death Knight, Conqueror for Paladin), making it far more efficient for any plate class to farm the specific lookalike items listed.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance at loot, run both raids on 25-player Heroic difficulty, as this setting drops a larger quantity of items per boss. The fastest way to acquire the set is to run the raids on multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) each week. Players can use Warforged Seals for a bonus roll on bosses for an extra chance at loot; these can be obtained by turning in Lesser Charms of Good Fortune at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. If you receive a tier token instead of a lookalike piece, it must be taken to Commander Oxheart at Niuzao Temple in Townlong Steppes to be exchanged for the gear item; the appearance is not learned from the token itself.

1199.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder recolor of Tier 14 plate armor, dropping from the Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. The Head and Chest pieces drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear LFR. The Legs drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, also in Heart of Fear LFR. The Shoulder piece drops from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring LFR, and the Hands drop from Feng the Accursed in Mogu'shan Vaults LFR. The matching non-set Waist, Wrist, and Feet pieces are lookalikes that drop from various bosses within these LFR wings, such as The Stone Guard and Imperial Vizier Zor'lok.

### Farming Logistics
The Mists of Pandaria Raid Finder wings operate on a weekly loot lockout per character. The entire set can be easily soloed at max level by any of the eligible classes. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 weeks on a single character. This set does not use a tier token system in LFR; gear pieces drop directly for plate-wearing classes. You can run the LFR, Normal, and Heroic versions of these raids each week on the same character, as they have separate lockouts and reward different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
To access Mists of Pandaria LFR, you must speak with Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. You can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, to get an extra chance at loot from each boss. Farming the instances on multiple plate classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) each week will significantly increase your chances of completing the set faster. All pieces are Bind on Pickup, so they cannot be found on the Auction House.

1200.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Mythic difficulty plate armor from the Blackrock Foundry raid, the Tier 17 instance in Warlords of Draenor. The eight pieces of the set are direct drops from specific bosses within the instance. The Helm drops from Blackhand, the Shoulders from The Iron Maidens, the Chest from Flamebender Ka'graz, and the Legs from The Blast Furnace. The Hands drop from Operator Thogar, the Feet from Gruul, the Waist from Oregorger, and the Wrists from Kromog.

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed on a weekly raid lockout, specific to the Mythic difficulty. The entire instance can be fully soloed by a single max-level character. Due to the number of pieces and random drop chances, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 8-12 weeks on average per character. This set does not use a tier token system; the items are direct plate armor drops that can be looted by any Warrior, Paladin, or Death Knight. Note that the LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties are on separate lockouts and reward different color variations of this armor model.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Mythic difficulty, as this is the only source for this set's specific color scheme. To increase your chances of loot per week, purchase Seals of Tempered Fate from vendors in Stormshield or Warspear to use for a bonus roll on each boss. During the Operator Thogar encounter, stand on the far left side of the platform to avoid most of the incoming trains. All set pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House.

1201.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Blackrock Foundry raid instance on Heroic difficulty. The tier pieces are purchased using tokens that drop from specific bosses. The Head token drops from Kromog, the Shoulder token from The Iron Maidens, the Chest token from Flamebender Ka'graz, the Legs token from The Blast Furnace, and the Hands token from Operator Thogar. The non-token matching pieces are the Slag-Covered Footguards from Gruul, the Girdle of the Legion's Vanity from Blackhand, and the Bracers of the Legion's Vanity, also from Blackhand.

### Farming Logistics
Blackrock Foundry has a weekly raid lockout. All bosses are fully soloable at the current maximum level. Collecting the full 8-piece set may take 4-8 weekly resets per character, depending on drop luck. The set uses the "Helm/Spaulders/Chest/Gauntlets/Leggings of the Rylak Stalker" tier token, which is shared by Hunter, Shaman, Monk, and Warrior classes. The feet, waist, and wrist pieces are not part of the token system and drop directly as items. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts and can each be completed once per week.

### Farming Tips
Run the raid on Heroic difficulty to obtain this specific recolor. You can significantly increase your weekly chances by farming on multiple mail-wearing classes (Hunter, Shaman, Evoker) or any class that shares the tier token (Monk, Warrior). Using the Seal of Tempered Fate for a bonus roll is possible if you still have the currency from Warlords of Draenor, though it is no longer obtainable. The raid layout is mostly linear, with a tram available after Operator Thogar to quickly access the final wing. All set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House.

1202.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder plate armor recolor from the Warlords of Draenor raid, Blackrock Foundry. The Headpiece drops from the final boss, Blackhand, while the Shoulder and Feet drop from The Iron Maidens. The Chest is obtained from Flamebender Ka’graz, and the Hands and Waist are sourced from Operator Thogar. The remaining pieces, the Legs and Wrist, both drop from The Blast Furnace encounter.

### Farming Logistics
Blackrock Foundry has a weekly raid lockout per character, per difficulty. Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic lockouts are all separate, allowing a plate-wearing character to clear each one for loot every week. The entire instance is easily soloable by a maximum level character. As this is a non-tier look-alike set, it does not use class tokens and pieces will drop directly for any plate-wearer. Expect to run the instance for 5-10 weeks on a single character, though farming on multiple plate-wearers can significantly speed up collection.

### Farming Tips
To enter the Raid Finder version of Blackrock Foundry, speak to Seer Kazal located in your level 3 Garrison in Draenor. You can use the Warlords of Draenor bonus roll currency, Seal of Tempered Fate, on bosses for an additional chance at loot. Inside the raid, use the mine cart transport system to quickly navigate between the different wings after defeating key bosses. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or bought from the Auction House.

1203.content:
### How to Obtain
This set appearance is obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid, the final raid tier of the Mists of Pandaria expansion. The pieces are direct drops from specific bosses within the instance, not from tier tokens. The Helm of the Seven Sacred Seals drops from Immerseus, and the Spaulders of the Seven Sacred Seals drop from Sha of Pride. The Chestguard of the Seven Sacred Seals drops from Malkorok, while the Gloves of the Seven Sacred Seals are looted from General Nazgrim. Finally, the Legguards of the Seven Sacred Seals drop from The Paragons of the Klaxxi, the Boots of the Seven Sacred Seals from Thok the Bloodthirsty, the Belt of the Seven Sacred Seals from Garrosh Hellscream, and the Bracers of the Seven Sacred Seals from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American realms. The entire instance is fully soloable by a character at the current maximum level. Due to the number of individual pieces required, expect to run the raid for 5-8 weeks to collect the full set, depending on luck. This lookalike set consists of direct drops and does not use the tier token system, meaning any leather-wearing class can loot and learn the appearance. The Looking For Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all on separate weekly lockouts, and each offers a different color variation of the set, allowing for four full clears per character each week.

### Farming Tips
For the quickest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal or Heroic difficulty, as Mythic difficulty has mechanics on bosses like Galakras and Paragons of the Klaxxi that can be cumbersome for a solo player. After defeating the first three bosses, a scroll will appear near the entrance that teleports you past the next several bosses, but do not use this skip until you have looted the shoulders from Sha of Pride. All items in this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House. The Mists of Pandaria bonus roll system using Warforged Seals is no longer available to supplement drops.

1204.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike of the Shaman Tier 16 set, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid on Heroic difficulty. The pieces drop as direct, named items from specific bosses throughout the instance. The Head drops from Malkorok, and the Shoulders drop from Siegecrafter Blackfuse. The Chest is obtained from Garrosh Hellscream, while the Legs are a drop from The Klaxxi council encounter. The Hands drop from General Nazgrim, the Feet from the Sha of Pride, the Waist from Norushen, and the Wrists from Galakras.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar has a weekly raid lockout. Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties share a single lockout, meaning you can only kill each boss once per week on one of those three difficulties; LFR is on a separate lockout. The entire raid is soloable at current maximum level. As this set does not use the tier token system, it consists of direct drops usable by any plate class; therefore, there is no token sharing. Assuming an average drop chance, completing the full 8-piece set may take approximately 5-10 weekly clears.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to Heroic to obtain this specific color variant. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Warforged Seals) can be used for an extra chance at loot from bosses; these can be purchased with Lesser Charms of Good Fortune at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. For the Galakras encounter, you must use the tower cannons to shoot down the drakes before the boss will land. Later in the raid, use the teleporters in the Cleft of Shadow to quickly navigate between the wings leading to Malkorok, Siegecrafter Blackfuse, and The Klaxxi.

1205.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike of the Shaman Tier 16 set and is obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid on Raid Finder difficulty. The Head drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty, and the Shoulders drop from Siegecrafter Blackfuse. The Chest is looted from the Sha of Pride, while the Legs drop from the Paragons of the Klaxxi. The Hands drop from General Nazgrim, and the Feet are from Malkorok. Lastly, the Waist is obtained from Iron Juggernaut and the Wrists from the Kor'kron Dark Shaman.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts, allowing four chances at loot per character per week if you are farming color variants. This set is easily soloed by a maximum-level character. This is a "lookalike" set, meaning the pieces drop directly and do not come from a tier token. Obtaining all eight pieces can take approximately 10-20 runs per character due to RNG.

### Farming Tips
To access the Mists of Pandaria Raid Finder version of Siege of Orgrimmar, speak with Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. Farming the set on multiple plate-wearing characters (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) is the most effective way to speed up acquisition, as each has a separate weekly lockout. You can also use Mogu Runes of Fate, the bonus roll currency for this raid tier, for an extra chance at loot from a boss. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1206.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic set was a promotional item, granted to players who pre-purchased the Heroic or Epic editions of the Dragonflight expansion. It is not obtainable from any in-game source such as a raid, dungeon, or vendor. The set is purely cosmetic and can be used by all classes and armor types. The items granted were the Wyrmstalker's Headpiece, Wyrmstalker's Spaulders, Wyrmstalker's Tunic, Wyrmstalker's Leggings, Wyrmstalker's Handguards, Wyrmstalker's Footguards, Wyrmstalker's Belt, Wyrmstalker's Armguards, and the Wings of Awakening cloak which has five color variants.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout or reset timer associated with this set as it is not farmable. The entire set was unlocked account-wide simultaneously upon the purchase of the appropriate Dragonflight pre-order edition. Because it is a cosmetic set available to all classes, it does not use the tier token system. The number of runs required to collect it is not applicable, as it was never available as a drop. All pieces were granted from this single, external source.

### Farming Tips
This set is no longer obtainable as the Dragonflight pre-purchase promotion has ended. Promotional items of this nature rarely become available again through other means. Players interested in the set should monitor future promotions or the in-game Trading Post, although its return is not guaranteed. As a cosmetic set, all pieces were automatically added to the Wardrobe's Appearances tab upon acquisition and are available for transmog on all characters on the account.

1207.content:
### How to Obtain
This set appearance is obtained from mail armor pieces that drop in the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid on Heroic difficulty. While the set is named after the Hunter tier, the appearance is shared by non-tier mail items, making it collectible by both Hunters and Shamans. The Head drops from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Shoulder is from the Lords of Dread. The Chest piece is looted from Rygelon, Hands from Lihuvim, Principal Architect, and the Legs are on Anduin Wrynn's loot table. The matching Feet drop from The Jailer, the Waist from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and the Wrist from the Vigilant Guardian.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly lockout per character for each difficulty. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic are separate lockouts, and you must run on Heroic for this specific recolor. Due to boss mechanics and health pools, the raid cannot be fully soloed at current max level and requires a small group of 2-5 players for a comfortable clear. The actual Hunter tier set uses the Mystic token (shared with Druid and Mage), while the lookalike pieces drop directly as mail armor. With eight potential bosses to kill for the full set, collection time is highly variable and can take 10-20 weeks per character.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, form a group with other players farming for transmog, as later bosses like Anduin Wrynn and The Jailer remain challenging. The raid is linear, requiring you to defeat bosses in a set order to unlock later wings, so no major skips are possible. Tier set tokens are not automatically added to your wardrobe; a Hunter must receive the token and right-click it to create the item. The non-tier lookalike pieces, however, will grant the appearance as soon as they are soulbound. The Great Vault system from Shadowlands can no longer be used to target pieces from this raid.

1208.content:
### How to Obtain
This set's appearance is obtained from the Raid Finder difficulty of the Vault of the Incarnates raid. The five main tier set pieces are acquired via tokens that drop from specific bosses, which must be used by an Evoker to create the armor and unlock the appearance account-wide. The non-tier waist, feet, and wrist pieces drop directly as mail items from bosses.

*   **Head:** Zenith-Marked Headguard (Token) from Raszageth the Storm-Eater
*   **Shoulder:** Zenith-Marked Spaulders (Token) from Kurog Grimtotem
*   **Chest:** Zenith-Marked Tunic (Token) from Broodkeeper Diurna
*   **Hands:** Zenith-Marked Grips (Token) from Eranog
*   **Legs:** Zenith-Marked Legwraps (Token) from The Primal Council
*   **Waist:** Dathea's Girdle of Coiled Tempests from Dathea, Ascended
*   **Feet:** Ancient Hornswog-Hide Boots from The Primal Council
*   **Wrist:** Tidal Wraps from The Primal Council

### Farming Logistics
The Vault of the Incarnates raid is on a weekly lockout per character, per difficulty. Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts with unique loot tables and colorations. This content is not soloable and requires joining a group by queuing for the specific Raid Finder wings. The primary set pieces use the Zenith tier token, which is shared by Evokers, Monks, Rogues, and Warriors; however, only an Evoker can use it to create the Wyrmstalker set appearance. Collecting all pieces will likely take multiple weekly resets due to the nature of random drops.

### Farming Tips
To queue for past Raid Finder content, speak to the NPC Kazra located in the main plaza of Valdrakken. The most direct way to farm this set is on an Evoker, as they can directly use the Zenith tokens to create the gear. Alternatively, tier pieces can be created using the Revival Catalyst with gear of an appropriate item level from Dragonflight Season 1 sources, including items from the raid itself. The non-tier pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and must be looted on a Mail-wearing character (Hunter, Shaman, or Evoker). The Great Vault can also serve as a supplementary source, potentially offering tier pieces or other raid items you need.

1209.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike version of the Rogue Tier 14 set and is obtained from the Looking for Raid difficulty of the Mogu'shan Vaults and Heart of Fear raids. The specific pieces drop from bosses split between the two instances. From Mogu'shan Vaults, the Hands drop from The Spirit Kings, Legs from Gara'jal the Spiritbinder, Chest from Elegon, and Shoulders from Will of the Emperor. From Heart of Fear, the Head drops from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, Feet from Garalon, and both the Waist and Wrist drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak.

### Farming Logistics
The farm is subject to a weekly raid lockout for each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic), meaning you can clear each version once per character per week. The instances are fully soloable at maximum level. Since the set consists of eight pieces spread across two raids and multiple bosses, collecting the full set can take approximately 5-10 weekly clears, depending on drop luck. This set does not use the tier token system; the items drop directly as gear wearable by any class that uses the same armor type. Pieces are sourced from two different raids, requiring you to clear bosses in both Mogu'shan Vaults and Heart of Fear.

### Farming Tips
To access the specific Looking for Raid version of these instances, speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. You can use Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls, specifically the Elder Charm of Good Fortune, for an extra chance at loot from a boss. The LFR difficulty is divided into wings, allowing you to queue for only the specific sections containing the bosses you need, which can speed up the farm significantly. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1210.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Mists of Pandaria. The entire set is learned from a single ensemble item, "Bindings of the Cursed Protector," which drops from the "Cache of Pandaren Spoils" treasure chest. This chest spawns after defeating the ninth boss, Spoils of Pandaria, on Heroic difficulty. Using the ensemble unlocks the appearances for seven armor slots: Head (Helm of the Cursed Protector), Shoulder (Spaulders of the Cursed Protector), Chest (Tunic of the Cursed Protector), Legs (Leggings of the Cursed Protector), Hands (Handguards of the Cursed Protector), Feet (Boots of the Cursed Protector), and Waist (Belt of the Cursed Protector).

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar has a weekly raid lockout, which resets separately for Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties. The instance can be fully soloed at current max level. Due to the item's low drop rate from the treasure chest, collecting it is highly variable and may take 10-30 weekly runs per character. This set does not use a tier token system; the ensemble item is a direct drop usable by any class. The Normal difficulty version of the set is a different color and drops from the Normal difficulty chest, allowing you to farm both colorations each week on the same character.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Heroic difficulty to obtain this specific color variation. After defeating the first boss, Immerseus, once on a character, a scroll near the raid entrance will allow you to teleport directly to later wings, providing a significant skip to the Spoils of Pandaria encounter. The ensemble item is Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House. During the Spoils of Pandaria fight, focus on opening the larger crates to spawn the required mini-bosses faster and complete the encounter quickly.

1211.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder recolor of Rogue Tier 14 and is obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. Pieces are direct drops from bosses on Raid Finder difficulty. The Head, "Hood of Cursed Dreams," drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Shoulders, "Shoulders of the Cursed Protector," drop from Lei Shi. The Chest, "Tunic of the Cursed Protector," drops from Garalon, and the Legs, "Leggings of the Cursed Protector," drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok. The Hands, "Gloves of the Cursed Protector," drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak, while matching Feet, "Boots of the Still Breath," drop from Amber-Shaper Un'sok. A matching Waist, "Stalker's Cord of Negation," drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, and Wrists, "Pathogenic Bracers," drop from Garalon.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids are on a weekly raid lockout per character. Both instances can be fully soloed at max level. As the Raid Finder lookalike pieces drop directly, there is no tier token system; any class can loot and collect any piece of the set. Because Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, you can farm each one weekly for different color variations without conflict. Collecting the full 8-piece set may take 5-10 weeks per character due to random drop chances.

### Farming Tips
To access Mists of Pandaria Raid Finder difficulties, speak to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above the Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. In Heart of Fear, after defeating the first boss, use the wind gusts on the platform to fly across the chasm, skipping a significant amount of trash. Terrace of Endless Spring is linear and has no major skips. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are no longer functional in these raids, and all pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1212.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid on Normal difficulty. The pieces are cosmetic items that drop in addition to standard loot and can be equipped by any class. The Head, Visage of the Deep Earth, drops from Anduin Wrynn. The Shoulder, Pauldrons of the Deep Earth, drops from Lords of Dread. The Chest, Robes of the Deep Earth, drops from Rygelon. The Legs, Leggings of the Deep Earth, drop from The Jailer. The Hands, Handguards of the Deep Earth, drop from Lihuvim, Principal Architect; the Feet, Footwraps of the Deep Earth, from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle; the Waist, Sash of the Deep Earth, from Halondrus the Reclaimer; and the Wrist, Cuffs of the Deep Earth, from Artificer Xy'mox.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid instance has a weekly raid lockout per character. This specific cosmetic set only drops on Normal difficulty; running LFR, Heroic, or Mythic will not award these appearances. The instance is fully soloable by a character at the current maximum level. This set consists of direct cosmetic item drops and does not use a tier token system. Assuming an average drop chance per piece, collecting the full 8-item set could take approximately 10 to 15 runs per character, subject to random luck.

### Farming Tips
Farming should be done exclusively on Normal difficulty, as it is the only source for this particular color tint. After clearing the raid once, teleporters become available on subsequent runs, allowing you to skip directly to later wings after defeating the initial bosses. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Shadowlands did not use a bonus roll system, and the Great Vault does not award these specific cosmetic items, so your only source is direct boss drops.

1213.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic armor set is a recolor of the Shaman Tier 12 armor and drops from bosses within the Castle Nathria raid on Heroic difficulty. The Head piece drops from Huntsman Altimor, and the Shoulders are looted from Sludgefist. The Chest piece drops from Sire Denathrius, while the Legs can be obtained from the Stone Legion Generals. The Hands drop from Artificer Xy'mox, the Feet from Hungering Destroyer, the Waist from Lady Inerva Darkvein, and the Wrists from The Council of Blood.

### Farming Logistics
Castle Nathria operates on a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can kill each boss for a chance at loot once per week per character. The raid is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Each difficulty (LFR, Normal, Heroic, Mythic) has a separate lockout, but this specific set only drops from Heroic bosses. This set does not use a tier token system; armor pieces drop directly from bosses and are not shared between classes. Given the number of pieces and typical drop rates, it may take 10-20 clears to complete the set.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Heroic difficulty to obtain this specific recolor. After defeating Shriekwing, you can choose which wing to clear first, but all subsequent bosses must be defeated to reach Sire Denathrius. The main armor pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House, though some unrelated BoE items do drop in the instance. The Great Vault system from Shadowlands is no longer a relevant or effective way to farm this specific appearance.

1214.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder recolor of Tier 13 armor and is obtained from the Dragon Soul raid, introduced in the Cataclysm expansion. All pieces are specific boss drops from the Raid Finder difficulty version of the instance.
- **Head:** Drops from Warmaster Blackhorn.
- **Shoulder:** Drops from Warlord Zon'ozz.
- **Chest:** Drops from Ultraxion.
- **Hands:** Drops from Hagara the Stormbinder.
- **Legs:** Drops from Yor'sahj the Unsleeping.
- **Feet:** Drops from the Cache of the Aspects after defeating the Madness of Deathwing.
- **Wrist:** Drops from Morchok.
- **Waist:** A matching belt does not drop from the Dragon Soul Raid Finder instance.

### Farming Logistics
The Dragon Soul raid has a weekly raid lockout, meaning you can loot each boss once per character per week. Raid Finder, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts and distinct loot tables, so you must run the LFR version for this specific color. The entire raid is easily soloed at current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full set may take 5-10 weekly clears, but this is subject to random luck. This set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly from bosses and are not shared between classes.

### Farming Tips
To access the Raid Finder version of Dragon Soul, speak to the NPC Auridormi in the Caverns of Time in Tanaris. You can queue for both wings ("Siege of Wyrmrest Temple" and "Fall of Deathwing") directly from her dialogue options. Farming on multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock) is the most efficient method due to the weekly lockout. All items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. The Spine of Deathwing encounter requires you to stay on the boss's back and kill tentacles, which can be time-consuming but is not mechanically difficult to solo.

1215.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier cloth lookalike of the Warlock Tier 14 armor, obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. The Head, "Hood of the Meticulous Scribe," drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear. The Shoulder, "Shoulders of the Meticulous Scribe," drops from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring. The Chest, Legs, and Hands ("Robes," "Leggings," and "Gloves of the Meticulous Scribe") drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, Amber-Shaper Un'sok, and Blade Lord Ta'yak respectively, all within Heart of Fear. The matching belt, "Klaxxi Lash of the Orator," is purchased from Ambersmith Zikk in Klaxxi'vess after reaching Exalted reputation with The Klaxxi. Matching feet, "Soul-Strider Boots," drop from Tsulong in Terrace of Endless Spring.

### Farming Logistics
Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring operate on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for NA realms and Wednesday for EU realms. Both raids can be easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces spread across two raids and a reputation vendor, expect the full farm to take 4-8 weeks, subject to drop chance. The lookalike pieces drop directly as items for any cloth-wearing class, while the official tier set uses the Vanquisher token shared by Death Knights, Mages, Rogues, and Warlocks. The LFR version of this set is a different color and has a separate lockout, while the Normal and Heroic versions share one weekly loot lockout per character.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raids on 10-player Normal difficulty, as boss health is significantly lower and all loot is on a shared table.
