To obtain the required belt, complete the daily quests for The Klaxxi faction in the Dread Wastes; purchasing a Grand Commendation of the Klaxxi from the quartermaster at Revered reputation will double all future reputation gains for the faction across your entire account. These set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be bought from the Auction House. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are no longer functional, and legacy raids do not contribute to the Great Vault.

1216.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier plate lookalike of the Paladin Tier 16 set, dropping from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid on Heroic difficulty. All pieces are personal loot and must be looted by a plate-wearing class. The Head piece, *Jaw of the Manticore*, drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty. The Shoulder piece, *Shoulderguards of the Manticore*, drops from Siegecrafter Blackfuse. The Chest, *Breastplate of the Manticore*, drops from Garrosh Hellscream. The Legs, *Greaves of the Manticore*, drop from The Sha of Pride, and the Hands, *Gauntlets of the Manticore*, drop from General Nazgrim. The Feet, *Greaves of the Manticore Stinger*, drop from Malkorok; the Waist, *Waistplate of the Manticore*, drops from the Spoils of Pandaria; and the Wrists, *Bracers of the Manticore*, drop from Kor'kron Dark Shaman.

### Farming Logistics
The Siege of Orgrimmar raid has a weekly raid lockout per character per difficulty. The raid is fully soloable at max level. In Mists of Pandaria, the LFR, Flexible (now Normal), Normal (now Heroic), and Heroic (now Mythic) difficulties all had separate lockouts, allowing you to run the raid up to four times a week on one character for different color sets, though only Heroic drops this specific appearance. The set is comprised of non-tier lookalike pieces, so any plate class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) can loot and collect the appearance directly. Paladins may also see tier tokens drop (Conqueror token: Paladin, Priest, Warlock), but the lookalike items are the direct source for all three classes. Expect to complete the set in approximately 4-8 weeks per character due to the large loot tables and number of bosses.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to Heroic and the legacy raid size to 10-player for the fastest clear, as boss health is lower with no change to the loot table. You can use Warforged Seals, the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll currency, for an extra chance at loot from each boss. These are purchased from the Elder Greatworth or Elder Liao at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. After defeating Galakras, a teleporter to Immerseus's room is activated near the raid entrance, allowing you to skip the first four bosses on subsequent runs. The Galakras encounter can be difficult solo, requiring you to use tower cannons to shoot down drakes; a character with high mobility or ranged abilities can simplify this fight.

1217.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Raid Finder difficulty of the Mists of Pandaria Tier 14 raids, Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring. Access these raids by speaking to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. The main set pieces are purchased with tokens that drop from specific bosses, while the non-set lookalike pieces drop directly.

*   **Head:** Token from Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear).
*   **Shoulder:** Token from Lei Shi (Terrace of Endless Spring).
*   **Chest:** Token from Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear).
*   **Legs:** Token from Wind Lord Mel'jarak (Heart of Fear).
*   **Hands:** Token from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok (Heart of Fear).
*   **Feet:** Sandals of the Unbidden (Blade Lord Ta'yak, Heart of Fear).
*   **Waist:** Incineration Belt (Garalon, Heart of Fear).
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of Unkept Promises (Amber-Shaper Un'sok, Heart of Fear).

### Farming Logistics
The Raid Finder instances operate on a weekly raid lockout, with each wing counting as a separate lockout. Heart of Fear is split into two wings ("The Dread Approach" and "Nightmare of Shek'zeer") and Terrace of Endless Spring is a single wing, allowing for three total lockouts per character per week for this set. The entire farm is easily soloable at maximum level. Tier pieces drop as tokens shared by Mage, Priest, and Warlock characters. The LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic versions of these raids all have separate weekly lockouts and drop different color variations of the set. Expect to take between 5-15 weeks to collect the full set due to token and drop randomness.

### Farming Tips
To speed up the farm, you can use multiple cloth-wearing characters (Mage, Priest, Warlock), as the appearances are not class-restricted. Mists of Pandaria raids allow for bonus rolls using Mogu Runes of Fate, which can be purchased for 90 Lesser Charms of Good Fortune from an NPC at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your collection upon looting; you must take the token to a vendor (Commander Oxheart in Townlong Steppes or Commander Lo Ping in Kun-Lai Summit) and exchange it for the actual gear piece to unlock the appearance. The non-tier pieces are bind-on-pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1218.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cloth non-tier lookalike from the Mists of Pandaria, Tier 14 raid, Heart of Fear, and drops exclusively on Looking for Raid (LFR) difficulty. The Helm of the Faceless Shroud drops from Blade Lord Ta'yak, and the Mantle of the Faceless Shroud drops from the final boss, Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Robes of the Faceless Shroud are looted from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, while the Gloves of the Faceless Shroud drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The Leggings of the Faceless Shroud and the Sandals of the Faceless Shroud both drop from Garalon. Lastly, both the Belt of the Faceless Shroud and the Bracers of the Faceless Shroud are on the loot table for the first boss, Imperial Vizier Zor'lok.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear raid has a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for the NA region. This instance can be fully and easily soloed by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting the full 8-piece set may take between 4-8 resets per character. This is a non-tier set, meaning the pieces drop directly and do not use the tier token system. The LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties for Mists of Pandaria raids are all on separate weekly lockouts, but this specific color variation only drops from LFR.

### Farming Tips
To farm this LFR-specific set, you must speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge, located above the Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms, to queue for the "Heart of Fear: The Dread Approach" and "Heart of Fear: Terrace of Endless Spring" scenarios. Since the raid is linear with no major skips, using a character with high movement speed is the most effective way to speed up clears. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Elder Charm of Good Fortune) can be used for an extra chance at loot, though farming the currency is inefficient. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1219.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is composed of cloth lookalike pieces that drop from bosses within the Throne of Thunder raid, which was the Tier 15 instance in Mists of Pandaria. All pieces drop exclusively on Heroic difficulty. The Headpiece of the Tainted-Mind drops from Megaera, and the Soul-Trader's Pauldrons drop from Durumu the Forgotten. The Robes of the Exorcist are looted from Iron Qon, while the Leggings of the Poisoned Soul drop from the first boss, Jin'rokh the Breaker. Gloves of Unliving Fantasies drop from the Council of Elders, the Cord of the Exalted drops from Ji-Kun, the Salt-Stained Treads drop from Tortos, and the Bracers of Constant Implosion drop from Dark Animus.

### Farming Logistics
The Throne of Thunder raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday for North American realms. The instance is fully soloable at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-20% per item, collecting all eight pieces can take approximately 8-12 weekly clears, though this is subject to random luck. This set is a lookalike and does not use the tier token system; the class-specific version of this set comes from the "Vanquisher" token shared by Mages, Priests, and Warlocks. The Looking For Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, but you must be in Heroic difficulty to obtain this specific red recolor.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to Heroic before entering the instance; both 10-player and 25-player Heroic difficulties share the same loot table and appearances. A character with high mobility, such as a Druid or Demon Hunter, can clear the raid faster. After defeating the first boss, you can jump down from the sides of the bridge to skip a short trash section. As you clear each of the raid's four main wings, a teleporter will activate in the central hub, allowing for faster navigation.

1220.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Raid Finder difficulty cloth armor from the Tier 14 raids, Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring. While the tier set is for Priests, identical lookalike pieces are available for all cloth-wearing classes from the same bosses. The Head drops from Sha of Fear and the Legs from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring. The Shoulders and Chest drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, Hands from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, Waist from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, and Feet from Blade Lord Ta'yak, all in Heart of Fear. The matching Wrist piece drops from the Protectors of the Endless in Terrace of Endless Spring.

### Farming Logistics
The raids operate on a weekly loot lockout per character, but Raid Finder, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties are all separate lockouts that can be farmed independently each week. Both Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring are fully soloable by any max-level character. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance, collecting all eight pieces can take between 4-7 weekly clears, subject to luck. Priests can also obtain the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, and Hands via the Vanquisher tier token, which is shared with Rogues, Death Knights, and Mages. Other cloth classes must loot the specific non-token items.

### Farming Tips
To access Mists of Pandaria Raid Finder instances, speak to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. He will allow you to queue directly for the specific LFR wings: "The Dread Approach" and "Nightmare of Shek'zeer" for Heart of Fear, and "The Fallen Protectors" for Terrace of Endless Spring. At max level, all bosses and mechanics can be ignored, allowing for clears of each wing in minutes. There are no major skips in these instances, but you can use your mount to travel between bosses.

1221.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is obtained from the Black Market Auction House (BMAH) by purchasing the "Ensemble: Time Lord's Regalia". This ensemble is a single item that, when used, grants the cosmetic appearances for all eight pieces of the set. This set was originally the reward for completing all Mists of Pandaria dungeons on Challenge Mode with a "Gold" rating, an achievement which is no longer obtainable in the game. The specific items granted by the ensemble are: Time Lord's Hood (Head), Time Lord's Mantle (Shoulder), Time Lord's Robes (Chest), Time Lord's Leggings (Legs), Time Lord's Gloves (Hands), Time Lord's Slippers (Feet), Time Lord's Cinch (Waist), and Time Lord's Cuffs (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
The BMAH does not have a traditional lockout; its inventory of items for auction resets daily. This set is Mage-exclusive and can only be learned and transmogged by Mages. It does not use a tier token system and is not shared with Priests or Warlocks. The acquisition is not a farm based on drop chance but rather a competitive gold bid against other players on your server. Securing the ensemble requires winning a single auction, which often concludes at or near the gold cap (9,999,999 gold).

### Farming Tips
Success requires a significant amount of gold, so focus on gold-making activities prior to attempting to buy the set. The BMAH is located in Valdrakken and can also be accessed via a portable NPC unlocked through the "A Shady Contact" questline in the Dragon Isles. Use third-party websites or Discord servers that track BMAH listings across different realms to receive alerts when the ensemble is posted. To reduce bidding competition, you can transfer a character with sufficient gold to a low-population or medium-population server, where the final bid price may be lower than on high-population servers.

1222.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier lookalike of the Mage Tier 14 set, obtained from the Heart of Fear raid in Heroic difficulty. All pieces are direct boss drops and can be equipped by any class. The Head (Hood of the Enduring Emanation) and Shoulders (Shoulders of the Enduring Emanation) drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Chest (Robes of the Enduring Emanation) and Wrists (Bracers of the Banished) drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The Legs (Leggings of the Enduring Emanation) drop from Garalon, the Hands (Gloves of the Enduring Emanation) drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak, the Feet (Sandals of the Unbidden) drop from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, and the Waist (Klaxxi Lash of the Orator) drops from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly raid lockout, which resets every Tuesday for North American realms. The instance is fully soloable by any class at maximum level, and the entire set can be farmed on a single character. As these are non-tier lookalike items, they drop directly and do not use a token system. Assuming a standard 15-25% drop chance for each item, a full collection will take approximately 4-7 weekly clears. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, but this specific blue recolor is exclusive to Heroic difficulty.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Heroic, as it has lower mob health and drops the same loot as 25-player. Heart of Fear is a linear instance with no available skips, so all six bosses must be defeated in order. On the Amber-Shaper Un'sok encounter, use high burst damage to defeat him before he can cast Reshape Life and trap you in the amber construct phase. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls are no longer available for this content, and all pieces are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1223.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Looking For Raid plate armor from the Tier 14 raids: Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring. The five main set pieces are obtained via tokens that drop from specific bosses, which must be collected on a Paladin. The remaining off-set pieces are direct drops that can be obtained by any plate-wearing class.

*   **Head:** Crown of the Crackling Conqueror (Grand Empress Shek'zeer, Heart of Fear)
*   **Shoulder:** Shoulders of the Crackling Conqueror (Lei Shi, Terrace of Endless Spring)
*   **Chest:** Chest of the Crackling Conqueror (Grand Empress Shek'zeer, Heart of Fear)
*   **Legs:** Leggings of the Crackling Conqueror (The Spirit Kings, Mogu'shan Vaults)
*   **Hands:** Gauntlets of the Crackling Conqueror (Feng the Accursed, Mogu'shan Vaults)
*   **Waist:** Sparkbreath Girdle (Tsulong, Terrace of Endless Spring)
*   **Feet:** Patroller's Gilded Sabatons (Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, Heart of Fear)
*   **Wrist:** Bracers of the Five-Horned Beetle (Garalon, Heart of Fear)

### Farming Logistics
The three raids providing this set (Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, Terrace of Endless Spring) are on a weekly raid lockout. All Mists of Pandaria raids are easily soloable by a max-level character. The Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing you to farm each one every week, but this specific recolor only drops from the LFR versions. The five tier pieces are obtained from the "Crackling Conqueror" token, which is shared by Paladins, Priests, and Warlocks, meaning a Paladin is required to collect the tier appearance. The Waist, Feet, and Wrist are non-token items that drop directly for any plate class. Expect to spend 4-8 weeks collecting the full set due to the number of items spread across three separate raids.

### Farming Tips
To access the Looking for Raid difficulty versions of these raids, speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge, located above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. He provides a dialog option to queue for specific LFR wings from past expansions. Since a Paladin is required to obtain the tier tokens, it is the most efficient class to use for the entire farm. Upon looting a tier token, the appearance is automatically added to your transmog collection, so there is no need to find a vendor to turn it in. There are no significant skips in these raids, so you must clear the bosses in their intended order.

1224.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-elite Season 11 PvP armor from the Cataclysm expansion, purchased with Marks of Honor. The main set pieces are sold by legacy PvP vendors. For the Horde, visit Doris Chiltonius in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar. For the Alliance, visit Captain Dirgehammer in the Champion's Hall in Stormwind. The chest and leg pieces cost 3 Marks of Honor each, while the head, shoulder, hands, feet, and waist pieces cost 2 Marks of Honor each. A full 7-piece set costs 16 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set as it is purchased from a vendor. The primary farm is for the currency, Marks of Honor, which is primarily obtained by participating in PvP activities like random Battlegrounds, Brawls, or Arenas. Farming is done by queuing for these activities, which does not require a pre-formed group. Assuming an average of 2 Marks of Honor per battleground (win or loss), collecting the full set will take approximately 8 battleground runs. All pieces are from the same vendor and this set does not use a token system.

### Farming Tips
The fastest method to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, as it involves fighting easy AI opponents for quick wins. Check the in-game calendar for its availability. Additionally, completing the weekly PvP quests, such as "Observing Battle," rewards a significant number of Marks. This set is Plate-specific, meaning it can only be purchased and its appearance learned by Warriors, Paladins, and Death Knights. The appearance is faction-specific, with a red version for Horde and a blue version for Alliance.

1225.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Elite Tyrannical Gladiator's Plate Armor, which is a red recolor of the Cataclysmic Gladiator's set from Mists of Pandaria PvP Season 13. It is purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Elite Tyrannical Gladiator's Plate Armor," which costs 12 Marks of Honor. The Alliance vendor is Armsmaster Holinka and the Horde vendor is Roo Desvin; both are located on the Serpent's Spine wall that separates the Valley of the Four Winds from Townlong Steppes. Purchasing this ensemble requires the character to have earned the "Tyrannical Gladiator" Feat of Strength achievement during Season 13. The ensemble contains the Head, Shoulders, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, and Waist appearances.

### Farming Logistics
The set is purchased from a vendor and is not subject to a lockout. However, it is only available to players who earned the required Feat of Strength during Mists of Pandaria PvP Season 13; it cannot be farmed or earned today by new players. If you have the achievement, the acquisition is solo and instant once you have 12 Marks of Honor. Marks of Honor are obtained from participating in PvP activities like battlegrounds and arenas, and have no acquisition lockout. This set does not use a token system and all pieces are acquired simultaneously from the ensemble purchase.

### Farming Tips
For players with the required achievement, the only farm is for Marks of Honor. The fastest way to acquire these is by winning PvP matches, with the weekly PvP Brawl often providing a high rate of return, especially during events like "Comp Stomp." Once purchased, the "Ensemble: Elite Tyrannical Gladiator's Plate Armor" is consumed, and the appearances are added to your wardrobe for all plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) on your account. The ensemble and its appearances are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or sold on the Auction House.

1226.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the cosmetic replica of the Season 11 PvP armor and is purchased from a vendor. For Horde players, the vendor is General Trixey in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar. For Alliance players, the vendor is Captain Dirgehammer in the Champion's Hall in Stormwind City. The complete set can be purchased as "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Scaled Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor. This ensemble grants the appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Waist, and Feet slots. Individual pieces can also be purchased for 2-3 Marks of Honor each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for acquiring this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously. The entire set can be obtained in a single transaction once you have 12 Marks of Honor, making the acquisition time dependent on how quickly you can farm the currency. This set does not use a tier token system and is a direct purchase available to Warrior, Paladin, and Death Knight characters. The armor pieces are not drops from any raid or dungeon, so concepts like raid lockouts or different difficulties do not apply. All main armor pieces are acquired from the single ensemble purchase.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly PvP brawl quests, particularly "Comp Stomp" when it is available, as it offers fast wins against AI opponents. Check the PvP vendors in Valdrakken for weekly quests that reward Marks of Honor for participating in battlegrounds or arenas. The ensemble and its individual pieces are soulbound upon purchase and cannot be sold or traded on the Auction House. Winning your first random battleground of the day provides a significant amount of Honor and often a Mark of Honor.

1227.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a PvP vendor for the "Marks of Honor" currency. The relevant vendors are Sergeant Wilson (Alliance, Stormwind Keep) and Blood Guard Zar'shi (Horde, Orgrimmar). The set available is the standard blue and purple Cataclysmic Gladiator's Regalia; the "Elite Recolor" is no longer obtainable in the game. The Warlords of Draenor expansion is not relevant to its acquisition. The cost for the main armor pieces is 18 Marks of Honor: Head (2), Shoulder (2), Chest (3), Legs (3), Hands (2), Feet (2), Waist (2), and Wrist (2).

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as it is dependent on acquiring Marks of Honor. The currency is primarily farmed by participating in PvP battlegrounds, which can be queued for solo at any time. Acquiring the 18 Marks of Honor needed for the full set typically requires 10-20 random battleground matches, which can be completed within a single play session. This set does not use a token system and is purchased directly for the correct class armor type. All pieces are available from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor faster, prioritize winning your first random battleground of the day for bonus rewards. Also, complete the weekly PvP quests offered in Valdrakken (e.g., "Observing War"), which provide a guaranteed quantity of Marks. Joining a pre-made group for random battlegrounds can significantly increase your win rate and currency gain per hour. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and not available on the Auction House, but purchasing a piece unlocks its appearance account-wide for all characters who can wear cloth armor.

1228.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a vendor using Marks of Honor. The Alliance vendor is Armsmaster Holinka, and the Horde vendor is Roo Desin. Both are located on the Serpent's Spine wall that separates the Veiled Stair from Kun-Lai Summit and Townlong Steppes in Pandaria. The entire set can be purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Dreadplate Armor," for 12 Marks of Honor. This ensemble includes appearances for all eight armor slots: Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, and Wrist.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily farmed solo by queuing for PvP content such as random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and weekly PvP brawls. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor needed for the ensemble can take approximately 2-4 hours depending on battleground win rates. This is a cosmetic PvP set, so it does not use a tier token system and all pieces are from the same vendor. While the armor type is Plate, any class can purchase the ensemble to unlock the appearances for all Plate-wearing characters on their account.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to collect this set is to purchase the ensemble rather than individual pieces. Marks of Honor are rewarded from the strongboxes received at the end of a battleground, with wins providing more. Prioritize completing the weekly PvP quests, such as "Observing Victory," which grant a significant number of Marks of Honor. Participating in the weekly rotating PvP brawls can also be a fast source of the currency. The items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1229.content:
### How to Obtain
This appearance is a recolor of the Cataclysmic Gladiator set and is obtained by purchasing the "Prideful Gladiator's Plate Armor" set from a Mists of Pandaria vendor. For Horde, visit Acon Deathwielder, and for Alliance, visit Ethan Natice. Both vendors are located on top of the Serpent's Spine wall that divides Kun-Lai Summit and Townlong Steppes. The currency required is Marks of Honor, which are earned from participating in PvP activities. The total cost for the main set is 20 Marks of Honor: 3 each for the Head, Shoulders, Chest, and Legs, and 2 each for the Hands, Waist, and Feet.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously. The entire process is fully soloable by queueing for random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, or PvP brawls. Assuming a 50% win rate, collecting the required 20 Marks of Honor will take approximately 20-30 battleground matches. This is a cosmetic PvP armor set; the appearance is unlocked for all Plate classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) on your account once purchased by a single character. All pieces of the set are sold by the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the weekly PvP Brawls, particularly "Comp Stomp," which offers fast wins against AI opponents. Check your capital city's PvP weekly quests, as they often reward additional Marks of Honor upon completion. The items are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Purchasing an item immediately and permanently adds its appearance to your collection, so there is no need to equip it or save the physical item.

1230.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a PvP vendor and originates from the Cataclysm expansion. The "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Chain Armor," which contains all core armor pieces, can be bought for 12 Marks of Honor. Alternatively, pieces can be bought individually from legacy PvP vendors. For the Alliance, visit Captain Dirgehammer in the Champion's Hall in Old Town, Stormwind. For the Horde, speak with Blazik Fireclaw in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar's Valley of Strength. Individual costs are 3 Marks of Honor for the Chest and Legs, and 2 Marks for the Head, Shoulders, and Hands.

### Farming Logistics
This set has no lockout, as its currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously. The entire process can be completed solo by queuing for PvP activities such as random battlegrounds, epic battlegrounds, and arenas. A battleground win typically rewards one Mark of Honor, meaning approximately 12 wins are needed to purchase the full ensemble. This is not a tier token set, so there are no class-sharing restrictions beyond it being available to mail-wearing classes (Hunter and Shaman). All pieces are purchased directly from the same vendor.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor efficiently, complete the weekly PvP quests available in Valdrakken, such as "Observing Battle," which often reward Marks. Participating in the weekly rotating PvP brawl can also provide a quest that awards five Marks of Honor upon completion. While Marks can be contained in victory reward chests, focusing on the guaranteed rewards from weekly quests is the fastest method. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be traded or found on the Auction House.

1231.content:
### How to Obtain
This appearance, correctly named the "Elite Primal Gladiator's Chain Armor," was the elite reward for Mail wearers during Warlords of Draenor PvP Season 1. It was awarded to players who achieved a rating of 2000 or higher in 2v2 Arenas, 3v3 Arenas, or Rated Battlegrounds during that specific season. Upon reaching the required rating, players could purchase the individual pieces from the Elite Gear vendor in Ashran for Conquest Points. The set is no longer obtainable through any in-game method, as the season and its associated rewards are retired. The ensemble was a one-time, limited-availability reward for high-rated PvP performance.

### Farming Logistics
This set is not subject to any lockout or drop chance as it was never a farmable reward. It could not be soloed; obtaining it required a dedicated and skilled group for rated Arenas or Rated Battlegrounds. The acquisition was tied to personal rating, not a number of runs or a currency grind available to all players. PvP gear sets do not use a tier token system, and all pieces were purchased from the same vendor after meeting the rating requirement. As a seasonal reward, it was only available during its active season and cannot be acquired now.

### Farming Tips
There are no methods or tips to farm this specific elite set. However, two similar, obtainable appearances exist for players. The original "Cataclysmic Gladiator's Chain Armor" set in its default colors can be purchased from Armorer Nate in the Gadgetzan inn, Tanaris, for 12 Marks of Honor. Additionally, the non-elite "Primal Gladiator's Chain Armor" set, a blue recolor, can be purchased from Amelia Clarke (Alliance, Stormshield) or Claddawar (Horde, Warspear) in Ashran, also for 12 Marks of Honor. Marks of Honor are earned from various PvP activities like winning Battlegrounds and Arena skirmishes.

1232.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor for the currency Mark of Honor. Alliance players can find Captain Dirgehammer in the Old Town of Stormwind City, while Horde players can find Blood Guard Zar'shi in Orgrimmar's Valley of Strength. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands each cost 2 Marks of Honor. The Waist and Feet pieces cost 1 Mark of Honor each, bringing the total cost for the seven-piece set to 12 Marks of Honor. Wrists are not a visible part of this set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as Marks of Honor can be acquired continuously through PvP activities. The currency can be fully farmed solo by queuing for random battlegrounds or by completing certain world quests. Since a battleground win typically awards one Mark of Honor, obtaining the full set requires approximately 12 wins. This set does not use a tier token system and is purchased directly. The items are for the Mail armor type, but any class can purchase them to unlock the appearance account-wide for their mail-wearing characters.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is by winning random battlegrounds, especially during the "Comp Stomp" event which provides quick victories against AI opponents. Alternative sources include PvP world quests in the Legion and Battle for Azeroth zones, which sometimes offer Marks as a reward. The set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. Purchasing an item on any character will permanently add its appearance to your account-wide Wardrobe.

1233.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is no longer obtainable. It was the Elite PvP armor appearance awarded during Shadowlands PvP Season 3 (as the Cosmic Gladiator set) and Shadowlands PvP Season 4 (as the Eternal Gladiator set). To acquire the full appearance, a player had to reach a personal rating of 1800 or higher in rated Arenas or Rated Battlegrounds during those specific seasons. Pieces of the set were unlocked sequentially as a player's rating increased, with the final pieces becoming available at the 1800 rating mark. As these PvP seasons have concluded, the opportunity to earn this specific recolor has been permanently retired.

### Farming Logistics
The set was not subject to a raid or dungeon lockout; its acquisition was tied to personal progression within a limited-time PvP season. It could not be obtained solo, as it required participating in group-based rated PvP (2v2 Arena, 3v3 Arena, or 10-player Rated Battlegrounds). The number of matches required varied based entirely on player skill and win rate to achieve the 1800 rating threshold. This Ringmail (Mail) set was available to Hunters and Shamans who earned the achievement and did not use a shared token system. All pieces were obtained through the same rating-based progression.

### Farming Tips
There are no methods to farm this specific elite set today. Players seeking similar appearances can obtain the non-elite versions. The original Cataclysmic Gladiator's set (red) can be purchased for Marks of Honor from the Cataclysm PvP vendors in Gadgetzan, Tanaris. The non-elite Shadowlands recolors (red/black or blue/silver) can be purchased with Marks of Honor from the Conquest vendors, Zo'sorg and Zo'kuul, in Oribos. These elite appearances are never available from the Great Vault, are not Bind on Equip for sale on the Auction House, and are not obtainable through any other in-game source.

1234.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of Cataclysm Season 11 PvP gear, purchased from vendors introduced in Mists of Pandaria. The vendor is Starlight Trixie (Alliance) or Hex'vex (Horde) on the Serpent's Spine wall between Kun-Lai Summit and Townlong Steppes. The currency required is the Mark of Honor. The main set pieces are purchased individually: Head (2), Shoulder (2), Chest (3), Legs (3), Hands (2), Feet (2), Waist (2), and Wrist (2).

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for this set, as it is purchased with a currency. Farming Marks of Honor can be done entirely solo by participating in PvP activities. A full 8-piece set costs a total of 18 Marks of Honor. The set is purchased directly, and does not use a tier token system; it is a Leather set available to Druids, Rogues, Monks, and Demon Hunters. All pieces are available from the same vendor for the same currency.

### Farming Tips
The fastest method to acquire Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, as it offers quick, guaranteed wins against AI opponents. Participating in random battlegrounds, especially during a bonus event, and completing the PvP World Quests in current expansion zones are also efficient sources. The set pieces are soulbound and cannot be purchased on the Auction House. The original Cataclysm set required a specific PvP rating to purchase, but this replica version has no such requirement.

1235.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is acquired from the Black Market Auction House (BMAH). Individual armor pieces or the complete "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Elite Leather Armor" can appear for auction, though listings are rare and unpredictable. The BMAH is currently located in Valdrakken and is run by Madam Goya. An alternative source is the Unclaimed Black Market Container, which can also be purchased from the BMAH and has a small chance to contain pieces from this set. All purchases are made with gold, and bidding often reaches the gold cap for desirable items.

### Farming Logistics
Farming this set is a matter of gold acquisition and auction monitoring, not instance clearing, so there are no lockouts. The BMAH listings reset daily, but the appearance of any specific item is random. The process is solo, though gold farming methods may involve groups. This set does not use a tier token system; items are purchased directly for gold. Acquiring the full set can take years due to the rarity of listings and the high competition from other players. Bidding takes place on a per-character, per-realm basis.

### Farming Tips
The most effective strategy is to amass a significant amount of gold, ideally the gold cap, to ensure you can win any bid. Monitor the BMAH daily across multiple servers, as listings are realm-specific; you may need to transfer a character with enough gold to a realm where a piece is listed. Items from the BMAH are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be resold on the regular auction house. Be prepared to place your maximum bid in the final minutes of the auction to avoid being outbid.

1236.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a legacy PvP vendor. For Horde, visit Roo Desvin in the Hall of Legends in Orgrimmar's Valley of Strength. For Alliance, visit Lieutenant Jackspring in the Champion's Hall in Stormwind's Old Town. The currency required is Marks of Honor, earned primarily from participating in PvP activities like battlegrounds and arenas. The cost for each piece is as follows: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Dragonhide Helm (3), Chestguard (3), and Legguards (3); Dragonhide Spaulders (2), Gloves (2), and Footguards (2); and Armguards of Alacrity (1) and Waistguard of Alacrity (1). The total cost for all eight pieces is 17 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be acquired continuously. The primary method of farming is through instanced PvP, which is group content that can be queued for solo. The number of battlegrounds required is approximately equal to the number of Marks needed, as a win typically awards one Mark of Honor; therefore, around 17 wins are needed for the full set. This set does not use a token system; each piece is purchased directly. All pieces are available from the same vendor, and there are no separate difficulties or lockouts to consider.

### Farming Tips
To acquire Marks of Honor efficiently, focus on winning Random Battlegrounds, as this is the most consistent source. Check the in-game calendar for the weekly PvP Brawl event, as the first win of the day often awards additional Marks. If you are active in rated PvP, the Great Vault can serve as a supplementary source of Marks if you choose not to take a gear reward. All items purchased from the vendor are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be sold on the Auction House.

1237.content:
### How to Obtain
This set was available during World of Warcraft: Shadowlands PvP Season 4 from the vendor Ko'tul in Oribos. It was purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Elite Cataclysmic Gladiator's Dragonhide Armor," which unlocked all armor appearances simultaneously. The purchase required 10,000 Cosmic Flux and one Cosmic Gladiator's Soul-Eater token. This token was rewarded for earning the "Elite: Shadowlands Season 4" achievement by reaching a 2100+ rating in any rated PvP bracket. This achievement, token, and ensemble are no longer obtainable.

### Farming Logistics
This set was not farmed through traditional means like raids or dungeons and therefore had no lockout. Acquisition was tied to the seasonal "Elite: Shadowlands Season 4" achievement, which required group participation in rated 2v2 Arenas, 3v3 Arenas, or Rated Battlegrounds. Once the achievement was earned and the single-use Cosmic Gladiator's Soul-Eater token was acquired, the entire set was purchased at once. This method of acquisition was only available for the duration of that specific PvP season and has since been removed. The appearance is account-wide for all leather-wearing classes (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter) after the ensemble is used.

### Farming Tips
This specific elite recolor is currently unobtainable, and there is no alternative method to acquire it. Blizzard occasionally reintroduces legacy elite sets through similar vendors in future expansions, so players should monitor end-of-expansion PvP rewards. The non-elite version, "Cataclysmic Gladiator's Dragonhide Armor," shares the same model with a different color scheme and can be purchased for Marks of Honor from legacy PvP vendors like Stone Guard Zarg in Orgrimmar or Agent Malley in Stormwind. No pieces of this set are Bind on Equip, so it cannot be found on the Auction House.

1238.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from Purveyor Zo'kuul in The Enclave in Oribos (coordinates 35.0, 57.6). The primary pieces are acquired by purchasing the "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Silk Armor" for 12 Marks of Honor. This ensemble contains the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Hands, and Legs slots. The matching Waist ("Cataclysmic Gladiator's Cord of Accuracy"), Feet ("Cataclysmic Gladiator's Treads of Alacrity"), and Wrist ("Cataclysmic Gladiator's Cuffs of Prowess") pieces are not in the ensemble and must be purchased separately from the same vendor for 1 Mark of Honor each.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously. Marks are primarily rewarded from participating in and winning PvP activities such as Random Battlegrounds, Arenas, and Brawls, which can be queued for solo. The entire collection (ensemble plus three off-set pieces) costs a total of 15 Marks of Honor, requiring approximately 15 battleground wins to obtain. This set does not use a tier token system, and its acquisition is independent of raid or dungeon lockouts. The set pieces are specific to cloth-wearing classes: Mage, Priest, and Warlock.

### Farming Tips
The fastest way to acquire Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is active, as it offers quick and consistent wins. Completing weekly PvP quests, such as those offered in Valdrakken, also provides a reliable source of Marks. The ensemble item instantly adds all five armor appearances to your account-wide Wardrobe upon use, accessible by any cloth character. While any class can purchase the ensemble to unlock the appearances, only Mages, Priests, and Warlocks can use the transmog.

1239.content:
### How to Obtain
This appearance was purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Silk Armor," from the vendor Ko'tul in Oribos. The vendor was located next to the Great Vault. The ensemble cost 10,000 Cosmic Flux, a currency primarily obtained from activities in Zereth Mortis and other Shadowlands endgame content. A seasonal requirement was mandatory for purchase: players first had to achieve a 2000 rating in any rated PvP bracket during Shadowlands PvP Season 4. This set is no longer obtainable.

### Farming Logistics
There was no lockout associated with this set, as it was a vendor purchase tied to a seasonal PvP achievement. The PvP rating requirement could be pursued solo via Solo Shuffle or in groups for 2v2, 3v3, or Rated Battlegrounds. The Cosmic Flux currency was easily farmed solo with no lockout through world quests, treasures, and rares in Zereth Mortis. This set was not on a token system; purchasing the ensemble unlocked the appearances account-wide for all cloth-wearing classes (Mage, Priest, Warlock).

### Farming Tips
During Shadowlands Season 4, the fastest way to acquire the necessary Cosmic Flux was by completing daily and weekly activities in Zereth Mortis. The primary gate to obtaining this set was the 2000 rating achievement, which varied in difficulty based on player skill and specialization. The ensemble item was soulbound and could not be traded or sold on the Auction House. Both the vendor and the ability to earn the required achievement were removed at the end of Shadowlands.

1240.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a replica of Cataclysm PvP gear purchased from vendors in Warlords of Draenor. The full set is sold by Claddaren Frostbeard in Stormshield, Ashran for the Alliance, and Ingrid Blackingot in Warspear, Ashran for the Horde. The currency used is the Mark of Honor, obtained from participating in various PvP activities. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands pieces each cost 2 Marks of Honor, while the Waist and Feet pieces cost 1 Mark of Honor each, for a total of 12 Marks of Honor for the 7-piece ensemble. A matching Wrist piece is not sold by the vendor.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming Marks of Honor, allowing for continuous acquisition. While the primary sources of this currency are group-based activities like Arenas and Battlegrounds, they can also be earned from soloable PvP World Quests in modern expansions. Assuming a 50% win rate in Random Battlegrounds, where a win typically awards one Mark of Honor, collecting the 12 marks required could take approximately 20-25 matches. This set does not use a tier token system, and all pieces are available from the same vendor. PvP gear acquisition is not tied to difficulty modes like LFR, Normal, Heroic, or Mythic.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl whenever it is available, as it offers fast wins against AI opponents. Check your currency tab before farming to see how many Marks of Honor you already possess from previous activities. The armor pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased or sold on the Auction House. While any class can purchase the set with Marks of Honor, the appearance can only be applied for transmogrification by cloth-wearing classes: Mages, Priests, and Warlocks.

1241.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a Warlords of Draenor PvP vendor. The Horde vendor is Cladd Dawnstrider in Warspear, Ashran, while the Alliance vendor is Amelia Clarke in Stormshield, Ashran. The full 8-piece set can be purchased as a single item, "Ensemble: Wild Gladiator's Ringmail Armor", for 80 Marks of Honor. All individual armor slots (Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, Waist, Wrist) are also sold by this vendor for 12 Marks of Honor per main piece. The set being sold is the red mail "Wild Gladiator" set, which is a recolor of the Cataclysmic Gladiator set.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout associated with farming this set, as the currency, Marks of Honor, can be farmed continuously through PvP activities. Purchasing the set is a solo activity, but farming the currency requires participating in group-based battlegrounds, arenas, or brawls. Obtaining the 80 Marks of Honor required for the full ensemble will take approximately 30-50 random battleground matches, depending on your faction's win rate. There is no tier token system for PvP sets; pieces are purchased directly. All pieces come from the same vendor source.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is by participating in the "Comp Stomp" brawl whenever it is active, as it provides fast, consistent wins against AI opponents. Additionally, completing the weekly PvP quests available in the current capital city (e.g., "Observing Battle" in Valdrakken) often rewards a large amount of Marks. Purchasing the "Ensemble: Wild Gladiator's Ringmail Armor" for 80 Marks of Honor is more convenient than buying each piece individually. These items are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1242.content:
### How to Obtain
This set appearance is purchased as an ensemble from a legacy PvP vendor in Shadowlands. The vendor is Purveyor Zo'kuul, located in The Enclave in Oribos at coordinates (35.8, 57.6). The item is called "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Leather Armor" and it costs 12 Marks of Honor. Purchasing this single item grants the appearances for the Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, Hands, Feet, and Waist slots. There is no matching Wrist appearance in the ensemble.

### Farming Logistics
There is no lockout for farming this set, as the Marks of Honor currency can be acquired continuously. The currency is primarily farmed through PvP activities like random battlegrounds, which can be queued for solo. Acquiring the 12 Marks of Honor needed may take between 4 to 12 battleground wins, depending on daily bonuses. This set does not use a tier token system and the appearances are not shared with other armor types. The entire appearance set is obtained from the single ensemble item, with no pieces dropping from other sources.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to farm Marks of Honor is to participate in the "Comp Stomp" PvP Brawl when it is available, which provides fast and consistent wins. When this brawl is not active, queueing for random battlegrounds and ensuring you get the first win of the day provides bonus currency. Purchasing the ensemble automatically and instantly teaches the appearances to your account's collection, making it available for all eligible classes (Rogue, Druid, Monk, Demon Hunter). The non-elite Horde and Alliance colorations are also available from the same vendor as separate ensembles.

1243.content:
### How to Obtain
This armor set was the elite PvP reward for achieving a 2200+ rating in rated battlegrounds or arenas during Cataclysm Season 11 and is no longer obtainable. The appearance was exclusive to that season and has not been made available again. A non-elite version with a different color scheme, "Cataclysmic Gladiator's Felweave Armor," can be purchased as an ensemble. The "Ensemble: Cataclysmic Gladiator's Felweave Armor" is sold by legacy PvP vendors, such as Zo'sorg in The Enclave in Oribos. The entire ensemble costs 12 Marks of Honor.

### Farming Logistics
As the elite set is unobtainable, there is no associated lockout or farming method. For the non-elite recolor, the currency is Marks of Honor, which has no farming lockout. These marks are primarily earned by winning PvP activities like random battlegrounds, arenas, and brawls. The ensemble teaches every armor slot appearance at once, so there are no individual pieces to farm. Collecting the required 12 Marks of Honor can typically be done in 3-5 hours of dedicated PvP.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method for farming Marks of Honor is to queue for random battlegrounds, especially during a holiday event that encourages participation. Participating in the weekly PvP brawls can also offer fast wins and consistent mark acquisition. The "Comp Stomp" brawl, which pits players against AI opponents, is a source of fast, guaranteed victories for marks. The ensemble is Bind-on-Account, allowing you to farm the marks on your strongest PvP character and send the item to an alt.

1244.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike for the Death Knight Tier 14 set, obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids on Normal difficulty. The majority of the pieces drop within Heart of Fear. The Head and Chest drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, the Legs from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, the Hands from Blade Lord Ta'yak, the Feet from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, the Waist from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, and the Wrists from Garalon. The final piece, the Shoulders, drops from Lei Shi in the Terrace of Endless Spring.

### Farming Logistics
The raids Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring are on a weekly raid lockout, resetting every Tuesday. The instances can be fully soloed by any max-level character with ease. As this is a lookalike set, the items drop directly from bosses and do not use the tier token system. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 5-10 weeks per character. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, and since Normal and Heroic share a loot table under legacy rules, you can clear both difficulties each week to double your chances at the Normal-difficulty appearance.

### Farming Tips
For the quickest clears, set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal, which features enemies with the lowest health pools. Both raids are linear, with no significant skips available for reaching the required bosses faster. When fighting Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear, it is best to defeat her instantly to bypass phase mechanics that can sometimes cause the encounter to bug. All pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House, and the Mists of Pandaria bonus roll system is not a relevant factor for farming this set today.

1245.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-tier plate lookalike of the Normal difficulty Necrotic Boneplate Armor and drops from the Blackrock Foundry raid in Warlords of Draenor. The Head piece drops from The Iron Maidens, and the Shoulder piece drops from Operator Thogar. The Chest is obtained from Flamebender Ka'graz, while the Hands drop from Gruul. The Legs are a drop from the final boss, Blackhand, the Feet from The Blast Furnace, the Waist from Beastlord Darmac, and the Wrist from Hans'gar and Franzok.

### Farming Logistics
Blackrock Foundry operates on a weekly raid lockout, allowing one clear per character per week on Normal difficulty. The instance can be fully soloed at current maximum level by any of the eligible classes. As these pieces are not part of the tier token system, they drop as direct items usable by any plate specialization. With eight pieces to collect, an average farm will take between 5-10 weeks per character, subject to drop chance. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts, but this specific appearance only drops from Normal.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to Normal before entering to ensure you are targeting the correct appearance. After defeating Operator Thogar, you can take the train car as a shortcut to the upper level near the Iron Maidens, saving time. Warlords of Draenor bonus rolls are still functional; use a Seal of Tempered Fate on a boss for an extra chance at loot. The pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1246.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a plate lookalike of Paladin Tier 14 gear, obtained from the three Tier 14 raids in Normal difficulty: Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring. The head, hands, and feet drop in Mogu'shan Vaults: Crown of the Doomed Empress (Feng the Accursed), Gauntlets of the Cursed Vanquisher (The Spirit Kings), and Treads of the Penitent Man (The Stone Guard). The chest, legs, and wrists drop in Heart of Fear: Carapace of the Prime (Grand Empress Shek'zeer), Legplates of the Assembled (Wind Lord Mel'jarak), and Bracers of Inlaid Jade (Imperial Vizier Zor'lok). The shoulders and waist drop in Terrace of Endless Spring: Shoulderguards of the Unflanked (Lei Shi) and Girdle of Unconquered Glory (Lesser Cache of the Aspects, after Tsulong).

### Farming Logistics
The three raids—Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring—are on a weekly raid lockout. All encounters can be fully soloed at maximum level with ease. The pieces are spread across eight different bosses in three instances, so expect to take approximately 4-8 weekly lockouts to collect the full set. The hand slot piece, Gauntlets of the Cursed Vanquisher, is a tier token shared by Paladin, Priest, and Warlock classes; looting this token on any of those three classes will unlock the appearance. All other pieces are direct drops that must be looted on a plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). The Normal and Heroic difficulties share a single lockout, but LFR is a separate lockout.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 10-player Normal for the fastest clears, as boss health is significantly lower than 25-player and the appearances are identical. You can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune for a bonus roll on bosses for an additional chance at loot. These charms can be acquired by trading Lesser Charms of Good Fortune, which drop from mobs throughout Pandaria. While Heart of Fear has a skip to later bosses after the first kill, you must clear all bosses up to the Grand Empress Shek'zeer for this set, rendering the skip unusable for this farm. The "Gauntlets of the Cursed Vanquisher" token must be used on an eligible class to add the appearance to your collection.

1247.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the plate lookalike from the Throne of Thunder raid, available only on Raid Finder (LFR) difficulty. The Head, Crown of Sorrow, drops from the Twin Consorts. The Shoulder, Pauldrons of Sorrow, drops from Iron Qon, and the Chest, Breastplate of Sorrow, drops from Dark Animus. Ji-Kun drops the Legplates of Sorrow, while the Council of Elders drops the Gauntlets of Sorrow. The Feet, Greaves of Sorrow, are from Megaera; the Waist, Girdle of Sorrow, is from Tortos; and the Wrist, Bracers of Sorrow, drops from Horridon.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder is on a weekly raid lockout, but the Raid Finder version has its own separate lockout from the Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties. The entire instance is easily soloable by any class at the current maximum level. This set does not use a tier token system; the pieces are direct drops for any plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight). Assuming average luck with a 15-25% drop chance per item, collecting the full 8-piece set may take 5-10 weekly clears on a single character. All pieces drop from within the Throne of Thunder LFR instance.

### Farming Tips
To farm the LFR-specific appearance, speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge above Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms (Pandaria) and select the "Throne of Thunder" LFR wings. Because the instance is trivial at max level, you can pull entire rooms and kill bosses in seconds, making a full clear take approximately 15-20 minutes. You can use Mogu Runes of Fate for a bonus roll on bosses for an extra chance at loot each week. These pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be bought or sold on the Auction House.

1248.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class leather lookalike obtained from the Raid Finder difficulty of Mists of Pandaria's Tier 14 raids: Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring. The Head slot drops from Ta'yak and the Chest from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, both in Heart of Fear. The Shoulder piece drops from Lei Shi in Terrace of Endless Spring. The Legs are from The Spirit Kings, Hands from Gara'jal the Spiritbinder, and Feet from The Stone Guard, all located in Mogu'shan Vaults. The matching Waist drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak and the Wrist from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, both in Heart of Fear.

### Farming Logistics
The three raids (Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, Terrace of Endless Spring) are on separate weekly raid lockouts per character. This specific color variation of the set only drops in the Raid Finder (LFR) difficulty; the Normal and Heroic versions award different colorations and have their own separate lockouts. The entire set can be fully soloed by any max-level character. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces may take 5-10 weekly clears. These items are direct boss drops and do not use a tier token system.

### Farming Tips
To queue for the LFR versions of these raids, speak to Lorewalker Han in the Seat of Knowledge, which is on the second floor of the building above the Mogu'shan Palace entrance in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. Running multiple leather-wearing alts (Rogue, Monk, Druid, Demon Hunter) through the instances each week will significantly increase your acquisition speed. Some Bind on Equip (BoE) world drops from Mists of Pandaria share appearances with pieces of this set and can occasionally be found on the Auction House. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) are no longer obtainable and cannot be used.

1249.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate set is the Look-for-Raid difficulty recolor of the Death Knight Tier 14 set, obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids in Pandaria. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, Legs, and Hands are tier-lookalike pieces, while the Waist, Feet, and Wrist are non-set items sharing the same model and color scheme. All pieces are personal loot drops from their respective bosses on LFR difficulty.

*   **Head:** Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear)
*   **Shoulder:** Lei Shi (Terrace of Endless Spring)
*   **Chest:** Grand Empress Shek'zeer (Heart of Fear)
*   **Legs:** Imperial Vizier Zor'lok (Heart of Fear)
*   **Hands:** Wind Lord Mel'jarak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Waist:** Garalon (Heart of Fear)
*   **Feet:** Blade Lord Ta'yak (Heart of Fear)
*   **Wrist:** Protectors of the Endless (Terrace of Endless Spring)

### Farming Logistics
The set is farmed from two separate raids, Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring, which are on a weekly raid lockout. You can clear each LFR wing once per character per week. The entire set is easily soloable by any class at maximum level. Due to the number of pieces spread across multiple bosses and two raids, collecting the full set can take approximately 5-10 weekly clears, subject to drop chance. This set is a direct drop for any plate-wearing class (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight); it does not use the tier token system. The Look-for-Raid difficulty has its own separate lockout from the Normal and Heroic versions, allowing you to farm all three color variations in the same week.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR versions of these raids, go to the Seat of Knowledge, located above the Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. Speak to Lorewalker Han to queue for the specific LFR wings: "Heart of Fear: The Dread Approach," "Heart of Fear: Terrace of Endless Spring," and "Terrace of Endless Spring." Running on LFR difficulty is the fastest method. You can use bonus roll seals (Elder Charms of Good Fortune) for an extra chance at loot from each boss. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1250.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier mail lookalike from the Throne of Thunder raid, introduced in Tier 15. The eight main pieces of the set drop from specific bosses within the instance. The Demonsbane Girdle (Waist) drops from Jin'rokh the Breaker, and the Demonsbane Armguards (Wrist) drop from Horridon. The Council of Elders drops the Demonsbane Gauntlets (Hands), while Tortos drops the Demonsbane Sabatons (Feet). Further in, the Demonsbane Legguards drop from Durumu the Forgotten, the Demonsbane Chestguard drops from Ji-Kun, the Demonsbane Shoulderguards drop from Iron Qon, and the Demonsbane Faceguard (Head) drops from Dark Animus.

### Farming Logistics
Throne of Thunder is on a weekly raid lockout. The raid is fully soloable by any class at the current maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 5-10 weekly clears due to the random nature of drops. This set is a direct drop and does not use the tier token system. The Looking for Raid, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout, allowing you to run the instance three times per week on a single character for more chances at loot.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as it has the lowest boss health pools. Players can acquire Mogu Runes of Fate from various Mists of Pandaria content (such as Timeless Isle) to use as bonus rolls on bosses for an additional chance at loot. While most fights are trivial, the Ji-Kun encounter can be slightly delayed for melee classes, as you must wait for the boss to land or use a ranged ability to pull it down from its nest. The pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1251.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the Looking For Raid (LFR) color variant of Hunter Tier 14, obtained from the Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring raids. The tier pieces are acquired via tokens, while the matching non-tier items drop directly from bosses. The Head and Chest tokens drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer in Heart of Fear. The Leg token drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak and the Hands token drops from Blade Lord Ta'yak, both in Heart of Fear. The Shoulder token is the only piece from Terrace of Endless Spring, dropping from Lei Shi. The matching Waist and Feet drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok in Heart of Fear, and the Wrist drops from Blade Lord Ta'yak.

### Farming Logistics
The set is on a weekly raid lockout, with LFR having a separate lockout from Normal or Heroic difficulties. The instances can be fully soloed by any class at maximum level. The main tier pieces use the "Cracked Tiera of the Protector" token, which is shared by Hunters, Monks, Shamans, and Warriors. The shoulder piece drops from a different raid instance than the rest of the set, requiring you to queue for both Heart of Fear and Terrace of Endless Spring LFR wings. Assuming a 15-25% drop chance for each token or direct drop, collecting the full 8-piece set will take approximately 4-8 weekly resets due to loot variance.

### Farming Tips
To access the LFR versions of these raids, speak to Lorewalker Han at the Seat of Knowledge, located above the Mogu'shan Palace in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. Tier tokens are not automatically added to your wardrobe; they are items that must be taken to a vendor to be exchanged for the gear piece. The appropriate vendors are Commander Oxheart (Horde) at Niuzao Temple in Townlong Steppes or Commander Lo Ping (Alliance) at the Temple of the White Tiger in Kun-Lai Summit. No raid skips are available or necessary, as a max-level character can clear each LFR wing in under 10 minutes. The tier set pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1252.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-tier lookalike of the Normal difficulty Hunter tier set from the Vault of the Incarnates raid in the Dragonflight expansion. The pieces are direct drops from bosses within the Normal difficulty version of the instance. The headpiece, Horns of the Incarnate, drops from Raszageth the Storm-Eater. The Shoulderpads of the Triumphant drop from Broodkeeper Diurna, and the Cuirass of the Triumphant drops from Sennarth. The Primal Council drops the Greaves of the Triumphant, Dathea drops the Gauntlets of the Triumphant, and Kurog Grimtotem drops the Boots of the Triumphant. The Cinch of the Triumphant drops from Terros, and the Vambraces of the Triumphant drop from Eranog.

### Farming Logistics
The Vault of the Incarnates raid is on a weekly lockout per difficulty, resetting on Tuesdays for NA realms and Wednesdays for EU realms. At current max level, the raid is not fully soloable and requires a small group of 2-5 players for completion. Assuming a drop chance of 15-25% per boss, it will take an average of 5-10 weekly clears to collect all eight pieces. This lookalike set consists of direct drops and does not use the "Dreadful" tier token system associated with the actual class sets from this raid. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts, allowing you to run each one weekly for different color variants.

### Farming Tips
You must run the raid on Normal difficulty to obtain this specific bronze and brown color tint. The easiest way to complete the raid is by searching for or starting a "transmog run" in the Premade Group Finder. There are no major skips in the instance, so a full clear is required to access all bosses that drop set pieces. The items are Bind-on-Pickup, meaning they cannot be traded or purchased from the Auction House. Drops cannot be supplemented through the Great Vault or bonus rolls, as those systems are either no longer active for this content tier or do not exist in Dragonflight.

1253.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the leather lookalike version of the Shaman Tier 16 armor, obtained from the Siege of Orgrimmar raid in Pandaria. The pieces drop from specific bosses on Normal difficulty. The Head, `Darkfang Mask`, drops from Thok the Bloodthirsty. The Shoulder, `Spaulders of the Unblinking Gaze`, drops from Siegecrafter Blackfuse. The Chest, `Tunic of the Unblinking Gaze`, is from the Sha of Pride. The Legs, `Leggings of the Unblinking Gaze`, are looted from The Paragons of the Klaxxi. The Hands, `Gloves of the Unblinking Gaze`, are a drop from General Nazgrim. The matching Feet, `Quakestomper Footpads`, come from the Iron Juggernaut, and the Waist, `Belt of the Unblinking Gaze`, drops from Malkorok.

### Farming Logistics
Siege of Orgrimmar has a weekly raid lockout per character for each difficulty. The Normal difficulty version of the raid is fully soloable by any class at maximum level. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-20% per item, collecting the full 7-piece set will likely take between 5 to 10 weekly clears. This lookalike set does not use a tier token system; the items drop directly for any Leather-specialization character. The Looking for Raid, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties all have separate lockouts and can be farmed independently each week for different color variations of the set.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as all loot tables are now combined. After defeating the first boss, Immerseus, do not use the sewer pipe skip if you still need the chest piece from the Sha of Pride. Mists of Pandaria bonus rolls, purchased with Lesser Charms of Good Fortune, can be used on bosses for an additional chance at loot. No pieces of this specific set are Bind on Equip (BoE), so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1254.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is purchased from a vendor during the Warlords of Draenor Timewalking event. The Alliance vendor is Kronnus in Stormshield, and the Horde vendor is Tempra in Warspear. The currency used is Timewarped Badges, with the full set costing 675 badges. The Head, Shoulder, Chest, and Legs pieces each cost 100 badges. The Hands, Feet, and Waist pieces each cost 75 badges, and the Wrist costs 50 badges.

### Farming Logistics
The currency is farmed during the Warlords of Draenor Timewalking event, which runs for one week on a rotating calendar schedule. There is no lockout; dungeons can be run repeatedly via the Group Finder tool, but this requires a 5-player group and cannot be soloed. The weekly quest, "A Savage Path Through Time," rewards 500 Timewarped Badges for completing five Timewalking dungeons, which is the primary source of currency. Completing those five dungeons will reward enough additional badges to purchase the entire set within a single event week. This set is purchased directly and does not use a token system.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient method is to accept the weekly quest "A Savage Path Through Time" from the Adventure Guide. The first Warlords of Draenor Timewalking dungeon you complete on your account during the event will also start a quest rewarding 500 Timewarped Badges. Queueing as a tank or healer will result in significantly faster queue times. All pieces are Bind on Pickup and cannot be purchased on the Auction House.

1255.content:
### How to Obtain
This cosmetic plate set is purchased from a vendor during the Warlords of Draenor Timewalking event. The vendor is Tempra in Stormshield for Alliance players and Kronnus in Warspear for Horde players. The currency used is Timewarped Badges, with each armor piece costing 500 badges for a total of 3,500. The set consists of seven pieces with no wrist slot available for purchase. The individual items are the Time-Twisted Faceguard (Head), Spaulders (Shoulder), Tunic (Chest), Gauntlets (Hands), Girdle (Waist), Legguards (Legs), and Sabatons (Feet).

### Farming Logistics
The ability to farm this set is restricted to the Warlords of Draenor Timewalking event, which is active for one week on a rotating calendar schedule. During the event, there is no lockout on farming Timewarped Badges; players can queue for Timewalking dungeons repeatedly via the Group Finder. Acquiring the full 3,500 badges without the weekly quest would take approximately 85-100 dungeon completions. The items are not tied to a token system, nor do they drop from any other source. The appearance is a recolor of the Mythic Tier 17 Warrior set from Blackrock Foundry.

### Farming Tips
The most efficient way to acquire Timewarped Badges is to complete the weekly quest "A Savage Path Through Time" on multiple characters. This quest, available during the event, awards 500 badges for completing five Timewalking dungeons. Since Timewarped Badges are an account-wide currency, you can complete this quest on up to seven different characters to earn the full set in a single event week. While any character can purchase the armor pieces, only plate-wearing classes (Warrior, Paladin, Death Knight) can add the appearances to the wardrobe.

1256.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike of the Rogue Tier 14 set and drops from the Heart of Fear raid, a Mists of Pandaria Tier 14 instance. The individual pieces are direct boss drops within the Normal difficulty of the raid. The Head, "Helmet of the Thousandfold Blades," and Hands, "Gloves of the Thousandfold Blades," both drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak. The Shoulder, "Shoulderguards of the Unflanked," drops from the final boss, Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Chest piece, "Vest of the Desolate Council," is looted from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, while the Legs, "Leggings of the Unrepentant," drop from Garalon. The Feet, "Boots of the Unburdened," drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok; the Waist, "Belt of the Unseen Path," drops from Amber-Shaper Un'sok; and the Wrist, "Bracers of the Banished," is an additional drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak.

### Farming Logistics
The Heart of Fear raid is on a weekly raid lockout. In Mists of Pandaria, the 10-player and 25-player Normal modes have separate lockouts, allowing you to clear the raid twice per week per character for more chances at loot. The raid is fully soloable at the current maximum level. As these are direct drops and not part of a token system, no other classes compete for the items. Assuming a 15-25% drop rate per item, a dedicated player can expect to complete the set within 3-5 lockouts. All listed pieces drop from within Heart of Fear, so no outside farming is required.

### Farming Tips
For the highest chance of loot, clear the raid on both 10-player Normal and 25-player Normal difficulties each week. The 25-player version drops a larger quantity of items per boss, increasing the probability of seeing a desired piece. Players can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune for a bonus roll on each boss, providing an extra chance at loot. These charms can be obtained by trading Lesser Charms of Good Fortune, which drop from Mists of Pandaria mobs, at your faction's shrine in the Vale of Eternal Blossoms. All pieces are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1257.content:
### How to Obtain
This plate armor set is the Normal difficulty, non-tier lookalike of the Shaman Tier 12 set, obtained from the Sepulcher of the First Ones raid in Shadowlands. The pieces drop from specific bosses within the raid on Normal difficulty. The set is only wearable by Paladins, Warriors, and Death Knights. The main armor pieces and their sources are as follows: Head (Crown of the Constellar Protector) from Anduin Wrynn, Shoulder (Pauldrons of the Devourer's Eminence) from Lords of Dread, Chest (Chestplate of Apocryphal Volition) from Rygelon, Legs (Legplates of Reciprocal Reprisal) from Halondrus the Reclaimer, and Hands (Gauntlets of the End-Sent) from Lihuvim, Principal Architect. Matching non-set pieces are Waist (Latticework of the Other Side) from Vigilant Guardian, Feet (Treads of Fractured Realities) from Anduin Wrynn, and Wrist (Bracelets of the Anima-Starved) from Dausegne, the Fallen Oracle.

### Farming Logistics
The Sepulcher of the First Ones raid has a weekly raid lockout, meaning each boss can be looted once per character per week on a specific difficulty. The Normal difficulty version of this raid is fully soloable by a max-level character in current gear. LFR, Normal, Heroic, and Mythic difficulties have separate lockouts and reward different color variations of this set, so you must clear it on Normal for these specific items. Assuming an average drop chance of 15-25% per boss, collecting all eight pieces will likely take between 6 and 12 weekly clears. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use a tier token system for acquisition.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clear, run the instance on Normal difficulty. After defeating the first boss, Vigilant Guardian, you can use the teleporters to navigate between the different wings of the raid to reach the bosses you need more efficiently. All pieces for this set are Bind-on-Pickup, so they cannot be purchased from the Auction House. No bonus roll system exists for Shadowlands raids, and the Great Vault is no longer a source for this gear. The Halondrus encounter requires significant movement across a large chasm, so use a class with high mobility to speed up this specific fight.

1258.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the non-class-specific lookalike of the Rogue Tier 14 set, obtained from the Heart of Fear raid introduced in Tier 14 of Mists of Pandaria. The pieces drop as individual, named items from specific bosses within the instance. The Helm of the Thousandfold Blades and Shoulders of the Thousandfold Blades both drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Chest of the Thousandfold Blades and the lookalike Ghost-Forged Treads drop from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The Leggings of the Thousandfold Blades drop from Ta'yak, the Gloves of the Thousandfold Blades drop from Garalon, the Bracers of the Thousandfold Blades drop from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, and the lookalike Klaxxi Lash of the Orator drops from Amber-Shaper Un'sok.

### Farming Logistics
Heart of Fear has a weekly raid lockout, meaning each character can loot each boss once per week per difficulty. The instance is easily soloed by any class at maximum level. Assuming a standard 15-25% drop chance for each piece, collecting the full 8-piece set will take approximately 5-12 weekly resets. This set consists of direct item drops and does not use the tier token system, so no class sharing is relevant for these specific pieces. LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties each have a separate weekly lockout and drop different color variations of the set, allowing you to clear all three difficulties on the same character each week.

### Farming Tips
For the fastest clears, run the raid on 10-player Normal difficulty, as this provides the lowest enemy health pools. There are no major skips or shortcuts available in Heart of Fear; you must defeat the bosses in a linear order. The Mists of Pandaria bonus roll system, using Elder Charms of Good Fortune, can be used for an extra chance at loot, but acquiring the currency is inefficient for modern farming.
