All pieces from this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House.

1259.content:
### How to Obtain
This leather armor set is a non-tier recolor of the Druid Tier 14 set and is obtained from bosses within the Mists of Pandaria raids: Mogu'shan Vaults, Heart of Fear, and Terrace of Endless Spring. The Head drops from Grand Empress Shek'zeer, Legs from Wind Lord Mel'jarak, and Hands from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, all within Heart of Fear. The Shoulders drop from Lei Shi and the Chest from Tsulong, both located in the Terrace of Endless Spring. Finally, the Waist drops from The Stone Guard, the Feet from The Spirit Kings, and the Wrist from Feng the Accursed in Mogu'shan Vaults.

### Farming Logistics
The three source raids all use a weekly raid lockout system. At current max level, all difficulties are fully soloable by any leather-wearing class (Druid, Rogue, Monk, Demon Hunter). LFR, Normal, and Heroic difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing for three attempts per boss each week; however, 10-player and 25-player modes for Normal and Heroic share a single lockout. This set consists of direct boss drops and does not use a tier token system, so a specific leather-wearing character must be used to loot the items and collect their appearance. Assuming an average drop rate, collecting all eight pieces will likely take 3-6 weeks if running all available difficulties.

### Farming Tips
Set the raid difficulty to 25-player Normal mode, as bosses drop a higher quantity of items, which increases the probability of your desired piece appearing. You can use Elder Charms of Good Fortune, primarily obtained on the Timeless Isle, for a bonus roll on each boss kill for an additional chance at loot. The armor pieces for this set are Bind-on-Pickup and cannot be purchased from the Auction House. In Mogu'shan Vaults, you can use the teleportation device near the entrance after your first clear to go directly to Elegon's platform, skipping the first four bosses on subsequent runs if you no longer need their loot.

1260.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is a non-class-specific lookalike ensemble found within the Mists of Pandaria raid, Heart of Fear. All pieces drop from bosses on Normal difficulty. The Head, Crown of the Doomed Empress, and Waist, Klaxxi Lash of the Orator, drop from Grand Empress Shek'zeer. The Shoulders, Shoulders of the Unflinching, drop from Amber-Shaper Un'sok, and the Chest, Robes of the Waking Nightmare, drops from Wind Lord Mel'jarak. The Hands, Gloves of Inevitable Ruin, are from Imperial Vizier Zor'lok, while the Legs, Leggings of the Cursed Vanquisher, drop from Garalon. Lastly, the Feet, Boots of the Still Breath, and Wrist, Bracers of Passing Attack, both drop from Blade Lord Ta'yak.

### Farming Logistics
The raid operates on a weekly raid lockout. In Mists of Pandaria, the 10-player and 25-player Normal difficulties have separate lockouts, allowing two full clears per character each week for twice the opportunity at loot. The raid is fully soloable at maximum level with trivial difficulty. As this is a lookalike set, the items drop directly from bosses and do not use the tier token system. Assuming an average drop chance, collecting all eight pieces may take approximately 5-10 resets. The LFR and Heroic versions of the raid also have their own separate weekly lockouts, but they reward different colorations of the set.

### Farming Tips
For maximum efficiency, run the raid on 25-player Normal difficulty, as this setting causes bosses to drop a larger quantity of loot compared to the 10-player version. Since 10-player and 25-player difficulties have separate lockouts, you can clear both each week on the same character to double your chances. The instance is linear with no significant skips; movement speed enhancements will be the primary way to reduce clear time. On the Garalon encounter, you must attack his legs first to make the boss vulnerable, though at high levels this phase ends almost instantly.

1262.content:
### How to Obtain
This set is the starting equipment for Death Knight characters. The items are Bind on Equip, so they can be traded or purchased from the Auction House. Alternatively, the pieces can be obtained by opening a "Big Crate of Salvage," which is a reward from completing Warlords of Draenor Garrison missions if the character has a Level 3 Salvage Yard building. The set consists of the Acherus Knight's Hood (Head), Pauldrons (Shoulder), Tunic (Chest), Legplates (Legs), Gauntlets (Hands), Greaves (Feet), Girdle (Waist), and Vambraces (Wrist).

### Farming Logistics
This farm is not subject to a raid or dungeon lockout; it is gated by the real-time cooldown of Garrison missions. The entire process is soloable. The number of missions required to obtain the set from salvage crates is entirely random and can be very high. Because all pieces are Bind on Equip, the farm can be bypassed entirely by purchasing them from the Auction House. This set does not use a token system and has no class restrictions on who can loot it from the salvage crates.

### Farming Tips
The most direct method to acquire this set is to create a new Death Knight character, which automatically starts with the full set equipped. Mail the gear to another character on your account, then delete the new Death Knight. Alternatively, check the Auction House frequently, as the pieces are often available for purchase due to their BoE status. If farming via the Garrison, using multiple characters with active Level 3 Salvage Yards will increase the quantity of salvage crates you can earn per day.
